n the beginning od created the heaven and the earth
nd the earth was without form and void and darkness was upon the face of the deep
nd the pirit of od moved upon the face of the waters
nd od said et there be light and there was light
nd od saw the light that it was good and od divided the light from the darkness
nd od called the light ay and the darkness he called ight
nd the evening and the morning were the first day
nd od said et there be a firmament in the midst of the waters and let it divide the waters from the waters
nd od made the firmament and divided the waters which were under the firmament from the waters which were above the firmament and it was so
nd od called the firmament eaven
nd the evening and the morning were the second day
nd od said et the waters under the heaven be gathered together unto one place and let the dry land appear and it was so
nd od called the dry land arth and the gathering together of the waters called he eas and od saw that it was good
nd od said et the earth bring forth grass the herb yielding seed and the fruit tree yielding fruit after his kind whose seed is in itself upon the earth and it was so
nd the earth brought forth grass and herb yielding seed after his kind and the tree yielding fruit whose seed was in itself after his kind and od saw that it was good
nd the evening and the morning were the third day
nd od said et there be lights in the firmament of the heaven to divide the day from the night and let them be for signs and for seasons and for days and years and let them be for lights in the firmament of the heaven to give light upon the earth and it was so
nd od made two great lights the greater light to rule the day and the lesser light to rule the night he made the stars also
nd od set them in the firmament of the heaven to give light upon the earth and to rule over the day and over the night and to divide the light from the darkness and od saw that it was good
nd the evening and the morning were the fourth day
nd od said et the waters bring forth abundantly the moving creature that hath life and fowl that may fly above the earth in the open firmament of heaven
nd od created great whales and every living creature that moveth which the waters brought forth abundantly after their kind and every winged fowl after his kind and od saw that it was good
nd od blessed them saying e fruitful and multiply and fill the waters in the seas and let fowl multiply in the earth
nd the evening and the morning were the fifth day
nd od said et the earth bring forth the living creature after his kind cattle and creeping thing and beast of the earth after his kind and it was so
nd od made the beast of the earth after his kind and cattle after their kind and every thing that creepeth upon the earth after his kind and od saw that it was good
nd od said et us make man in our image after our likeness and let them have dominion over the fish of the sea and over the fowl of the air and over the cattle and over all the earth and over every creeping thing that creepeth upon the earth
o od created man in his own image in the image of od created he him male and female created he them
nd od blessed them and od said unto them e fruitful and multiply and replenish the earth and subdue it and have dominion over the fish of the sea and over the fowl of the air and over every living thing that moveth upon the earth
nd od said ehold  have given you every herb bearing seed which is upon the face of all the earth and every tree in the which is the fruit of a tree yielding seed to you it shall be for meat
nd to every beast of the earth and to every fowl of the air and to every thing that creepeth upon the earth wherein there is life  have given every green herb for meat and it was so
nd od saw every thing that he had made and behold it was very good
nd the evening and the morning were the sixth day
hus the heavens and the earth were finished and all the host of them
nd on the seventh day od ended his work which he had made and he rested on the seventh day from all his work which he had made
nd od blessed the seventh day and sanctified it because that in it he had rested from all his work which od created and made
hese are the generations of the heavens and of the earth when they were created in the day that the  od made the earth and the heavens and every plant of the field before it was in the earth and every herb of the field before it grew for the  od had not caused it to rain upon the earth and there was not a man to till the ground
ut there went up a mist from the earth and watered the whole face of the ground
nd the  od formed man of the dust of the ground and breathed into his nostrils the breath of life and man became a living soul
nd the  od planted a garden eastward in den and there he put the man whom he had formed
nd out of the ground made the  od to grow every tree that is pleasant to the sight and good for food the tree of life also in the midst of the garden and the tree of knowledge of good and evil
nd a river went out of den to water the garden and from thence it was parted and became into four heads
he name of the first is ison that is it which compasseth the whole land of avilah where there is gold and the gold of that land is good there is bdellium and the onyx stone
nd the name of the second river is ihon the same is it that compasseth the whole land of thiopia
nd the name of the third river is iddekel that is it which goeth toward the east of ssyria
nd the fourth river is uphrates
nd the  od took the man and put him into the garden of den to dress it and to keep it
nd the  od commanded the man saying f every tree of the garden thou mayest freely eat but of the tree of the knowledge of good and evil thou shalt not eat of it for in the day that thou eatest thereof thou shalt surely die
nd the  od said t is not good that the man should be alone  will make him an help meet for him
nd out of the ground the  od formed every beast of the field and every fowl of the air and brought them unto dam to see what he would call them and whatsoever dam called every living creature that was the name thereof
nd dam gave names to all cattle and to the fowl of the air and to every beast of the field but for dam there was not found an help meet for him
nd the  od caused a deep sleep to fall upon dam and he slept and he took one of his ribs and closed up the flesh instead thereof and the rib which the  od had taken from man made he a woman and brought her unto the man
nd dam said his is now bone of my bones and flesh of my flesh she shall be called oman because she was taken out of an
herefore shall a man leave his father and his mother and shall cleave unto his wife and they shall be one flesh
nd they were both naked the man and his wife and were not ashamed
ow the serpent was more subtil than any beast of the field which the  od had made
nd he said unto the woman ea hath od said e shall not eat of every tree of the garden
nd the woman said unto the serpent e may eat of the fruit of the trees of the garden but of the fruit of the tree which is in the midst of the garden od hath said e shall not eat of it neither shall ye touch it lest ye die
nd the serpent said unto the woman e shall not surely die for od doth know that in the day ye eat thereof then your eyes shall be opened and ye shall be as gods knowing good and evil
nd when the woman saw that the tree was good for food and that it was pleasant to the eyes and a tree to be desired to make one wise she took of the fruit thereof and did eat and gave also unto her husband with her and he did eat
nd the eyes of them both were opened and they knew that they were naked and they sewed fig leaves together and made themselves aprons
nd they heard the voice of the  od walking in the garden in the cool of the day and dam and his wife hid themselves from the presence of the  od amongst the trees of the garden
nd the  od called unto dam and said unto him here art thou
nd he said  heard thy voice in the garden and  was afraid because  was naked and  hid myself
nd he said ho told thee that thou wast naked
ast thou eaten of the tree whereof  commanded thee that thou shouldest not eat
nd the man said he woman whom thou gavest to be with me she gave me of the tree and  did eat
nd the  od said unto the woman hat is this that thou hast done
nd the woman said he serpent beguiled me and  did eat
nd the  od said unto the serpent ecause thou hast done this thou art cursed above all cattle and above every beast of the field upon thy belly shalt thou go and dust shalt thou eat all the days of thy life and  will put enmity between thee and the woman and between thy seed and her seed it shall bruise thy head and thou shalt bruise his heel
nto the woman he said  will greatly multiply thy sorrow and thy conception in sorrow thou shalt bring forth children and thy desire shall be to thy husband and he shall rule over thee
nd unto dam he said ecause thou hast hearkened unto the voice of thy wife and hast eaten of the tree of which  commanded thee saying hou shalt not eat of it cursed is the ground for thy sake in sorrow shalt thou eat of it all the days of thy life thorns also and thistles shall it bring forth to thee and thou shalt eat the herb of the field in the sweat of thy face shalt thou eat bread till thou return unto the ground for out of it wast thou taken for dust thou art and unto dust shalt thou return
nd dam called his wifes name ve because she was the mother of all living
nto dam also and to his wife did the  od make coats of skins and clothed them
nd the  od said ehold the man is become as one of us to know good and evil and now lest he put forth his hand and take also of the tree of life and eat and live for ever therefore the  od sent him forth from the garden of den to till the ground from whence he was taken
o he drove out the man and he placed at the east of the garden of den herubims and a flaming sword which turned every way to keep the way of the tree of life
nd dam knew ve his wife and she conceived and bare ain and said  have gotten a man from the 
nd she again bare his brother bel
nd bel was a keeper of sheep but ain was a tiller of the ground
nd in process of time it came to pass that ain brought of the fruit of the ground an offering unto the 
nd bel he also brought of the firstlings of his flock and of the fat thereof
nd the  had respect unto bel and to his offering but unto ain and to his offering he had not respect
nd ain was very wroth and his countenance fell
nd the  said unto ain hy art thou wroth and why is thy countenance fallen
f thou doest well shalt thou not be accepted and if thou doest not well sin lieth at the door
nd unto thee shall be his desire and thou shalt rule over him
nd ain talked with bel his brother and it came to pass when they were in the field that ain rose up against bel his brother and slew him
nd the  said unto ain here is bel thy brother
nd he said  know not m  my brothers keeper
nd he said hat hast thou done the voice of thy brothers blood crieth unto me from the ground
nd now art thou cursed from the earth which hath opened her mouth to receive thy brothers blood from thy hand when thou tillest the ground it shall not henceforth yield unto thee her strength a fugitive and a vagabond shalt thou be in the earth
nd ain said unto the  y punishment is greater than  can bear
ehold thou hast driven me out this day from the face of the earth and from thy face shall  be hid and  shall be a fugitive and a vagabond in the earth and it shall come to pass that every one that findeth me shall slay me
nd the  said unto him herefore whosoever slayeth ain vengeance shall be taken on him sevenfold
nd the  set a mark upon ain lest any finding him should kill him
nd ain went out from the presence of the  and dwelt in the land of od on the east of den
nd ain knew his wife and she conceived and bare noch and he builded a city and called the name of the city after the name of his son noch
nd unto noch was born rad and rad begat ehujael and ehujael begat ethusael and ethusael begat amech
nd amech took unto him two wives the name of the one was dah and the name of the other illah
nd dah bare abal he was the father of such as dwell in tents and of such as have cattle
nd his brothers name was ubal he was the father of all such as handle the harp and organ
nd illah she also bare ubalcain an instructer of every artificer in brass and iron and the sister of ubalcain was aamah

here were giants in the earth in those days and also after that when the sons of od came in unto the daughters of men and they bare children to them the same became mighty men which were of old men of renown
nd  saw that the wickedness of man was great in the earth and that every imagination of the thoughts of his heart was only evil continually

 window shalt thou make to the ark and in a cubit shalt thou finish it above and the door of the ark shalt thou set in the side thereof with lower second and third stories shalt thou make it

nd every living substance was destroyed which was upon the face of the ground both man and cattle and the creeping things and the fowl of the heaven and they were destroyed from the earth and oah only remained alive and they that were with him in the ark
nd the waters prevailed upon the earth an hundred and fifty days
nd od remembered oah and every living thing and all the cattle that was with him in the ark and od made a wind to pass over the earth and the waters asswaged the fountains also of the deep and the windows of heaven were stopped and the rain from heaven was restrained and the waters returned from off the earth continually and after the end of the hundred and fifty days the waters were abated
nd the ark rested in the seventh month on the seventeenth day of the month upon the mountains of rarat
nd the waters decreased continually until the tenth month in the tenth month on the first day of the month were the tops of the mountains seen
nd it came to pass at the end of forty days that oah opened the window of the ark which he had made and he sent forth a raven which went forth to and fro until the waters were dried up from off the earth
lso he sent forth a dove from him to see if the waters were abated from off the face of the ground but the dove found no rest for the sole of her foot and she returned unto him into the ark for the waters were on the face of the whole earth then he put forth his hand and took her and pulled her in unto him into the ark
nd he stayed yet other seven days and again he sent forth the dove out of the ark and the dove came in to him in the evening and lo in her mouth was an olive leaf pluckt off so oah knew that the waters were abated from off the earth

nd it came to pass in the six hundredth and first year in the first month the first day of the month the waters were dried up from off the earth and oah removed the covering of the ark and looked and behold the face of the ground was dry

nd the angel of the  called unto braham out of heaven the second time and said y myself have  sworn saith the  for because thou hast done this thing and hast not withheld thy son thine only son that in blessing  will bless thee and in multiplying  will multiply thy seed as the stars of the heaven and as the sand which is upon the sea shore and thy seed shall possess the gate of his enemies and in thy seed shall all the nations of the earth be blessed because thou hast obeyed my voice
o braham returned unto his young men and they rose up and went together to eersheba and braham dwelt at eersheba
nd it came to pass after these things that it was told braham saying ehold ilcah she hath also born children unto thy brother ahor uz his firstborn and uz his brother and emuel the father of ram and hesed and azo and ildash and idlaph and ethuel
nd ethuel begat ebekah these eight ilcah did bear to ahor brahams brother
nd his concubine whose name was eumah she bare also ebah and aham and hahash and aachah
nd arah was an hundred and seven and twenty years old these were the years of the life of arah
nd arah died in irjatharba the same is ebron in the land of anaan and braham came to mourn for arah and to weep for her
nd braham stood up from before his dead and spake unto the sons of eth saying  am a stranger and a sojourner with you give me a possession of a buryingplace with you that  may bury my dead out of my sight
nd the children of eth answered braham saying unto him ear us my lord thou art a mighty prince among us in the choice of our sepulchres bury thy dead none of us shall withhold from thee his sepulchre but that thou mayest bury thy dead
nd braham stood up and bowed himself to the people of the land even to the children of eth
nd he communed with them saying f it be your mind that  should bury my dead out of my sight hear me and intreat for me to phron the son of ohar that he may give me the cave of achpelah which he hath which is in the end of his field for as much money as it is worth he shall give it me for a possession of a buryingplace amongst you
nd phron dwelt among the children of eth and phron the ittite answered braham in the audience of the children of eth even of all that went in at the gate of his city saying ay my lord hear me the field give  thee and the cave that is therein  give it thee in the presence of the sons of my people give  it thee bury thy dead
nd braham bowed down himself before the people of the land
nd he spake unto phron in the audience of the people of the land saying ut if thou wilt give it  pray thee hear me  will give thee money for the field take it of me and  will bury my dead there
nd phron answered braham saying unto him y lord hearken unto me the land is worth four hundred shekels of silver what is that betwixt me and thee bury therefore thy dead
nd braham hearkened unto phron and braham weighed to phron the silver which he had named in the audience of the sons of eth four hundred shekels of silver current money with the merchant
nd the field of phron which was in achpelah which was before amre the field and the cave which was therein and all the trees that were in the field that were in all the borders round about were made sure unto braham for a possession in the presence of the children of eth before all that went in at the gate of his city
nd after this braham buried arah his wife in the cave of the field of achpelah before amre the same is ebron in the land of anaan
nd the field and the cave that is therein were made sure unto braham for a possession of a buryingplace by the sons of eth
nd braham was old and well stricken in age and the  had blessed braham in all things
nd braham said unto his eldest servant of his house that ruled over all that he had ut  pray thee thy hand under my thigh and  will make thee swear by the  the od of heaven and the od of the earth that thou shalt not take a wife unto my son of the daughters of the anaanites among whom  dwell but thou shalt go unto my country and to my kindred and take a wife unto my son saac
nd the servant said unto him eradventure the woman will not be willing to follow me unto this land must  needs bring thy son again unto the land from whence thou camest
nd braham said unto him eware thou that thou bring not my son thither again
he  od of heaven which took me from my fathers house and from the land of my kindred and which spake unto me and that sware unto me saying nto thy seed will  give this land he shall send his angel before thee and thou shalt take a wife unto my son from thence
nd if the woman will not be willing to follow thee then thou shalt be clear from this my oath only bring not my son thither again
nd the servant put his hand under the thigh of braham his master and sware to him concerning that matter
nd the servant took ten camels of the camels of his master and departed for all the goods of his master were in his hand and he arose and went to esopotamia unto the city of ahor
nd he made his camels to kneel down without the city by a well of water at the time of the evening even the time that women go out to draw water
nd he said   od of my master braham  pray thee send me good speed this day and shew kindness unto my master braham
ehold  stand here by the well of water and the daughters of the men of the city come out to draw water and let it come to pass that the damsel to whom  shall say et down thy pitcher  pray thee that  may drink and she shall say rink and  will give thy camels drink also let the same be she that thou hast appointed for thy servant saac and thereby shall  know that thou hast shewed kindness unto my master
nd it came to pass before he had done speaking that behold ebekah came out who was born to ethuel son of ilcah the wife of ahor brahams brother with her pitcher upon her shoulder
nd the damsel was very fair to look upon a virgin neither had any man known her and she went down to the well and filled her pitcher and came up
nd the servant ran to meet her and said et me  pray thee drink a little water of thy pitcher
nd she said rink my lord and she hasted and let down her pitcher upon her hand and gave him drink
nd when she had done giving him drink she said  will draw water for thy camels also until they have done drinking
nd she hasted and emptied her pitcher into the trough and ran again unto the well to draw water and drew for all his camels
nd the man wondering at her held his peace to wit whether the  had made his journey prosperous or not
nd it came to pass as the camels had done drinking that the man took a golden earring of half a shekel weight and two bracelets for her hands of ten shekels weight of gold and said hose daughter art thou tell me  pray thee is there room in thy fathers house for us to lodge in
nd she said unto him  am the daughter of ethuel the son of ilcah which she bare unto ahor
he said moreover unto him e have both straw and provender enough and room to lodge in

nd  came this day unto the well and said   od of my master braham if now thou do prosper my way which  go behold  stand by the well of water and it shall come to pass that when the virgin cometh forth to draw water and  say to her ive me  pray thee a little water of thy pitcher to drink and she say to me oth drink thou and  will also draw for thy camels let the same be the woman whom the  hath appointed out for my masters son

hen aban and ethuel answered and said he thing proceedeth from the  we cannot speak unto thee bad or good

nd ebekah arose and her damsels and they rode upon the camels and followed the man and the servant took ebekah and went his way
nd saac came from the way of the well ahairoi for he dwelt in the south country
nd saac went out to meditate in the field at the eventide and he lifted up his eyes and saw and behold the camels were coming
nd ebekah lifted up her eyes and when she saw saac she lighted off the camel

nd the servant had said t is my master therefore she took a vail and covered herself
nd the servant told saac all things that he had done
nd saac brought her into his mother arahs tent and took ebekah and she became his wife and he loved her and saac was comforted after his mothers death
hen again braham took a wife and her name was eturah
nd she bare him imran and okshan and edan and idian and shbak and huah
nd okshan begat heba and edan
nd the sons of edan were sshurim and etushim and eummim

nd braham gave all that he had unto saac

nd these are the days of the years of brahams life which he lived an hundred threescore and fifteen years
hen braham gave up the ghost and died in a good old age an old man and full of years and was gathered to his people
nd his sons saac and shmael buried him in the cave of achpelah in the field of phron the son of ohar the ittite which is before amre the field which braham purchased of the sons of eth there was braham buried and arah his wife
nd it came to pass after the death of braham that od blessed his son saac and saac dwelt by the well ahairoi
ow these are the generations of shmael brahams son whom agar the gyptian arahs handmaid bare unto braham and these are the names of the sons of shmael by their names according to their generations the firstborn of shmael ebajoth and edar and dbeel and ibsam and ishma and umah and assa adar and ema etur aphish and edemah these are the sons of shmael and these are their names by their towns and by their castles twelve princes according to their nations
nd these are the years of the life of shmael an hundred and thirty and seven years and he gave up the ghost and died and was gathered unto his people
nd they dwelt from avilah unto hur that is before gypt as thou goest toward ssyria and he died in the presence of all his brethren
nd these are the generations of saac brahams son braham begat saac and saac was forty years old when he took ebekah to wife the daughter of ethuel the yrian of adanaram the sister to aban the yrian
nd saac intreated the  for his wife because she was barren and the  was intreated of him and ebekah his wife conceived
nd the children struggled together within her and she said f it be so why am  thus
nd she went to enquire of the 
nd the  said unto her wo nations are in thy womb nd two manner of people shall be separated from thy bowels nd the one people shall be stronger than the other people nd the elder shall serve the younger
nd when her days to be delivered were fulfilled behold there were twins in her womb
nd the first came out red all over like an hairy garment and they called his name sau
nd after that came his brother out and his hand took hold on saus heel and his name was called acob and saac was threescore years old when she bare them
nd the boys grew and sau was a cunning hunter a man of the field and acob was a plain man dwelling in tents
nd saac loved sau because he did eat of his venison but ebekah loved acob
nd acob sod pottage and sau came from the field and he was faint and sau said to acob eed me  pray thee with that same red pottage for  am faint therefore was his name called dom
nd acob said ell me this day thy birthright
nd sau said ehold  am at the point to die and what profit shall this birthright do to me
nd acob said wear to me this day and he sware unto him and he sold his birthright unto acob
hen acob gave sau bread and pottage of lentiles and he did eat and drink and rose up and went his way thus sau despised his birthright
nd there was a famine in the land beside the first famine that was in the days of braham
nd saac went unto bimelech king of the hilistines unto erar
nd the  appeared unto him and said o not down into gypt dwell in the land which  shall tell thee of sojourn in this land and  will be with thee and will bless thee for unto thee and unto thy seed  will give all these countries and  will perform the oath which  sware unto braham thy father and  will make thy seed to multiply as the stars of heaven and will give unto thy seed all these countries and in thy seed shall all the nations of the earth be blessed because that braham obeyed my voice and kept my charge my commandments my statutes and my laws
nd saac dwelt in erar nd the men of the place asked him of his wife and he said he is my sister for he feared to say he is my wife lest said he the men of the place should kill me for ebekah because she was fair to look upon
nd it came to pass when he had been there a long time that bimelech king of the hilistines looked out at a window and saw and behold saac was sporting with ebekah his wife
nd bimelech called saac and said ehold of a surety she is thy wife and how saidst thou he is my sister
nd saac said unto him ecause  said est  die for her
nd bimelech said hat is this thou hast done unto us one of the people might lightly have lien with thy wife and thou shouldest have brought guiltiness upon us
nd bimelech charged all his people saying e that toucheth this man or his wife shall surely be put to death
hen saac sowed in that land and received in the same year an hundredfold and the  blessed him
nd the man waxed great and went forward and grew until he became very great for he had possession of flocks and possession of herds and great store of servants and the hilistines envied him
or all the wells which his fathers servants had digged in the days of braham his father the hilistines had stopped them and filled them with earth
nd bimelech said unto saac o from us for thou art much mightier than we
nd saac departed thence and pitched his tent in the valley of erar and dwelt there
nd saac digged again the wells of water which they had digged in the days of braham his father for the hilistines had stopped them after the death of braham and he called their names after the names by which his father had called them
nd saacs servants digged in the valley and found there a well of springing water
nd the herdmen of erar did strive with saacs herdmen saying he water is ours and he called the name of the well sek because they strove with him
nd they digged another well and strove for that also and he called the name of it itnah
nd he removed from thence and digged another well and for that they strove not and he called the name of it ehoboth and he said or now the  hath made room for us and we shall be fruitful in the land
nd he went up from thence to eersheba
nd the  appeared unto him the same night and said  am the od of braham thy father fear not for  am with thee and will bless thee and multiply thy seed for my servant brahams sake

nd they said e saw certainly that the  was with thee and we said et there be now an oath betwixt us even betwixt us and thee and let us make a covenant with thee that thou wilt do us no hurt as we have not touched thee and as we have done unto thee nothing but good and have sent thee away in peace thou art now the blessed of the 
nd he made them a feast and they did eat and drink

nd it came to pass the same day that saacs servants came and told him concerning the well which they had digged and said unto him e have found water
nd he called it hebah therefore the name of the city is eersheba unto this day
nd sau was forty years old when he took to wife udith the daughter of eeri the ittite and ashemath the daughter of lon the ittite which were a grief of mind unto saac and to ebekah
nd it came to pass that when saac was old and his eyes were dim so that he could not see he called sau his eldest son and said unto him y son and he said unto him ehold here am 
nd he said ehold now  am old  know not the day of my death now therefore take  pray thee thy weapons thy quiver and thy bow and go out to the field and take me some venison and make me savoury meat such as  love and bring it to me that  may eat that my soul may bless thee before  die
nd ebekah heard when saac spake to sau his son
nd sau went to the field to hunt for venison and to bring it
nd ebekah spake unto acob her son saying ehold  heard thy father speak unto sau thy brother saying ring me venison and make me savoury meat that  may eat and bless thee before the  before my death
ow therefore my son obey my voice according to that which  command thee
o now to the flock and fetch me from thence two good kids of the goats and  will make them savoury meat for thy father such as he loveth and thou shalt bring it to thy father that he may eat and that he may bless thee before his death
nd acob said to ebekah his mother ehold sau my brother is a hairy man and  am a smooth man my father peradventure will feel me and  shall seem to him as a deceiver and  shall bring a curse upon me and not a blessing
nd his mother said unto him pon me be thy curse my son only obey my voice and go fetch me them
nd he went and fetched and brought them to his mother and his mother made savoury meat such as his father loved
nd ebekah took goodly raiment of her eldest son sau which were with her in the house and put them upon acob her younger son and she put the skins of the kids of the goats upon his hands and upon the smooth of his neck and she gave the savoury meat and the bread which she had prepared into the hand of her son acob
nd he came unto his father and said y father and he said ere am  who art thou my son

nd it came to pass as soon as saac had made an end of blessing acob and acob was yet scarce gone out from the presence of saac his father that sau his brother came in from his hunting

nd sau hated acob because of the blessing wherewith his father blessed him and sau said in his heart he days of mourning for my father are at hand then will  slay my brother acob
nd these words of sau her elder son were told to ebekah and she sent and called acob her younger son and said unto him ehold thy brother sau as touching thee doth comfort himself purposing to kill thee

nd ebekah said to saac  am weary of my life because of the daughters of eth if acob take a wife of the daughters of eth such as these which are of the daughters of the land what good shall my life do me

nd od lmighty bless thee and make thee fruitful and multiply thee that thou mayest be a multitude of people and give thee the blessing of braham to thee and to thy seed with thee that thou mayest inherit the land wherein thou art a stranger which od gave unto braham
