hen sau saw that saac had blessed acob and sent him away to adanaram to take him a wife from thence and that as he blessed him he gave him a charge saying hou shalt not take a wife of the daughters of anaan and that acob obeyed his father and his mother and was gone to adanaram and sau seeing that the daughters of anaan pleased not saac his father then went sau unto shmael and took unto the wives which he had ahalath the daughter of shmael brahams son the sister of ebajoth to be his wife
nd acob went out from eersheba and went toward aran
nd he lighted upon a certain place and tarried there all night because the sun was set and he took of the stones of that place and put them for his pillows and lay down in that place to sleep
nd he dreamed and behold a ladder set up on the earth and the top of it reached to heaven and behold the angels of od ascending and descending on it
nd behold the  stood above it and said  am the  od of braham thy father and the od of saac the land whereon thou liest to thee will  give it and to thy seed and thy seed shall be as the dust of the earth and thou shalt spread abroad to the west and to the east and to the north and to the south and in thee and in thy seed shall all the families of the earth be blessed
nd behold  am with thee and will keep thee in all places whither thou goest and will bring thee again into this land for  will not leave thee until  have done that which  have spoken to thee of
nd acob awaked out of his sleep and he said urely the  is in this place and  knew it not
nd he was afraid and said ow dreadful is this place this is none other but the house of od and this is the gate of heaven
nd acob rose up early in the morning and took the stone that he had put for his pillows and set it up for a pillar and poured oil upon the top of it
nd he called the name of that place ethel but the name of that city was called uz at the first
nd acob vowed a vow saying f od will be with me and will keep me in this way that  go and will give me bread to eat and raiment to put on so that  come again to my fathers house in peace then shall the  be my od and this stone which  have set for a pillar shall be ods house and of all that thou shalt give me  will surely give the tenth unto thee
hen acob went on his journey and came into the land of the people of the east
nd he looked and behold a well in the field and lo there were three flocks of sheep lying by it for out of that well they watered the flocks and a great stone was upon the wells mouth
nd thither were all the flocks gathered and they rolled the stone from the wells mouth and watered the sheep and put the stone again upon the wells mouth in his place
nd acob said unto them y brethren whence be ye
nd they said f aran are we
nd he said unto them now ye aban the son of ahor
nd they said e know him
nd he said unto them s he well
nd they said e is well and behold achel his daughter cometh with the sheep
nd he said o it is yet high day neither is it time that the cattle should be gathered together water ye the sheep and go and feed them
nd they said e cannot until all the flocks be gathered together and till they roll the stone from the wells mouth then we water the sheep
nd while he yet spake with them achel came with her fathers sheep for she kept them
nd it came to pass when acob saw achel the daughter of aban his mothers brother and the sheep of aban his mothers brother that acob went near and rolled the stone from the wells mouth and watered the flock of aban his mothers brother
nd acob kissed achel and lifted up his voice and wept
nd acob told achel that he was her fathers brother and that he was ebekahs son and she ran and told her father
nd it came to pass when aban heard the tidings of acob his sisters son that he ran to meet him and embraced him and kissed him and brought him to his house
nd he told aban all these things
nd aban said to him urely thou art my bone and my flesh
nd he abode with him the space of a month
nd aban said unto acob ecause thou art my brother shouldest thou therefore serve me for nought tell me what shall thy wages be
nd aban had two daughters the name of the elder was eah and the name of the younger was achel
eah was tender eyed but achel was beautiful and well favoured
nd acob loved achel and said  will serve thee seven years for achel thy younger daughter
nd aban said t is better that  give her to thee than that  should give her to another man abide with me
nd acob served seven years for achel and they seemed unto him but a few days for the love he had to her
nd acob said unto aban ive me my wife for my days are fulfilled that  may go in unto her
nd aban gathered together all the men of the place and made a feast
nd it came to pass in the evening that he took eah his daughter and brought her to him and he went in unto her
nd aban gave unto his daughter eah ilpah his maid for an handmaid
nd it came to pass that in the morning behold it was eah and he said to aban hat is this thou hast done unto me did not  serve with thee for achel wherefore then hast thou beguiled me
nd aban said t must not be so done in our country to give the younger before the firstborn
ulfil her week and we will give thee this also for the service which thou shalt serve with me yet seven other years
nd acob did so and fulfilled her week and he gave him achel his daughter to wife also
nd aban gave to achel his daughter ilhah his handmaid to be her maid
nd he went in also unto achel and he loved also achel more than eah and served with him yet seven other years
nd when the  saw that eah was hated he opened her womb but achel was barren
nd eah conceived and bare a son and she called his name euben for she said urely the  hath looked upon my affliction now therefore my husband will love me
nd she conceived again and bare a son and said ecause the  hath heard that  was hated he hath therefore given me this son also and she called his name imeon
nd she conceived again and bare a son and said ow this time will my husband be joined unto me because  have born him three sons therefore was his name called evi
nd she conceived again and bare a son and she said ow will  praise the  therefore she called his name udah and left bearing
nd when achel saw that she bare acob no children achel envied her sister and said unto acob ive me children or else  die
nd acobs anger was kindled against achel and he said m  in ods stead who hath withheld from thee the fruit of the womb
nd she said ehold my maid ilhah go in unto her and she shall bear upon my knees that  may also have children by her
nd she gave him ilhah her handmaid to wife and acob went in unto her
nd ilhah conceived and bare acob a son
nd achel said od hath judged me and hath also heard my voice and hath given me a son therefore called she his name an
nd ilhah achels maid conceived again and bare acob a second son
nd achel said ith great wrestlings have  wrestled with my sister and  have prevailed and she called his name aphtali
hen eah saw that she had left bearing she took ilpah her maid and gave her acob to wife
nd ilpah eahs maid bare acob a son
nd eah said  troop cometh and she called his name ad
nd ilpah eahs maid bare acob a second son
nd eah said appy am  for the daughters will call me blessed and she called his name sher
nd euben went in the days of wheat harvest and found mandrakes in the field and brought them unto his mother eah
hen achel said to eah ive me  pray thee of thy sons mandrakes
nd she said unto her s it a small matter that thou hast taken my husband and wouldest thou take away my sons mandrakes also
nd achel said herefore he shall lie with thee to night for thy sons mandrakes
nd acob came out of the field in the evening and eah went out to meet him and said hou must come in unto me for surely  have hired thee with my sons mandrakes
nd he lay with her that night
nd od hearkened unto eah and she conceived and bare acob the fifth son
nd eah said od hath given me my hire because  have given my maiden to my husband and she called his name ssachar
nd eah conceived again and bare acob the sixth son
nd eah said od hath endued me with a good dowry now will my husband dwell with me because  have born him six sons and she called his name ebulun
nd afterwards she bare a daughter and called her name inah
nd od remembered achel and od hearkened to her and opened her womb
nd she conceived and bare a son and said od hath taken away my reproach and she called his name oseph and said he  shall add to me another son
nd it came to pass when achel had born oseph that acob said unto aban end me away that  may go unto mine own place and to my country
ive me my wives and my children for whom  have served thee and let me go for thou knowest my service which  have done thee
nd aban said unto him  pray thee if  have found favour in thine eyes tarry for  have learned by experience that the  hath blessed me for thy sake
nd he said ppoint me thy wages and  will give it
nd he said unto him hou knowest how  have served thee and how thy cattle was with me
or it was little which thou hadst before  came and it is now increased unto a multitude and the  hath blessed thee since my coming and now when shall  provide for mine own house also
nd he said hat shall  give thee
nd acob said hou shalt not give me any thing if thou wilt do this thing for me  will again feed and keep thy flock
 will pass through all thy flock to day removing from thence all the speckled and spotted cattle and all the brown cattle among the sheep and the spotted and speckled among the goats and of such shall be my hire
o shall my righteousness answer for me in time to come when it shall come for my hire before thy face every one that is not speckled and spotted among the goats and brown among the sheep that shall be counted stolen with me
nd aban said ehold  would it might be according to thy word
nd he removed that day the he goats that were ringstraked and spotted and all the she goats that were speckled and spotted and every one that had some white in it and all the brown among the sheep and gave them into the hand of his sons
nd he set three days journey betwixt himself and acob and acob fed the rest of abans flocks
nd acob took him rods of green poplar and of the hazel and chesnut tree and pilled white strakes in them and made the white appear which was in the rods
nd he set the rods which he had pilled before the flocks in the gutters in the watering troughs when the flocks came to drink that they should conceive when they came to drink
nd the flocks conceived before the rods and brought forth cattle ringstraked speckled and spotted
nd acob did separate the lambs and set the faces of the flocks toward the ringstraked and all the brown in the flock of aban and he put his own flocks by themselves and put them not unto abans cattle
nd it came to pass whensoever the stronger cattle did conceive that acob laid the rods before the eyes of the cattle in the gutters that they might conceive among the rods
ut when the cattle were feeble he put them not in so the feebler were abans and the stronger acobs
nd the man increased exceedingly and had much cattle and maidservants and menservants and camels and asses
nd he heard the words of abans sons saying acob hath taken away all that was our fathers and of that which was our fathers hath he gotten all this glory
nd acob beheld the countenance of aban and behold it was not toward him as before
nd the  said unto acob eturn unto the land of thy fathers and to thy kindred and  will be with thee
nd acob sent and called achel and eah to the field unto his flock and said unto them  see your fathers countenance that it is not toward me as before but the od of my father hath been with me
nd ye know that with all my power  have served your father
nd your father hath deceived me and changed my wages ten times but od suffered him not to hurt me
f he said thus he speckled shall be thy wages then all the cattle bare speckled and if he said thus he ringstraked shall be thy hire then bare all the cattle ringstraked
hus od hath taken away the cattle of your father and given them to me
nd it came to pass at the time that the cattle conceived that  lifted up mine eyes and saw in a dream and behold the rams which leaped upon the cattle were ringstraked speckled and grisled
nd the angel of od spake unto me in a dream saying acob nd  said ere am 
nd he said ift up now thine eyes and see all the rams which leap upon the cattle are ringstraked speckled and grisled for  have seen all that aban doeth unto thee
 am the od of ethel where thou anointedst the pillar and where thou vowedst a vow unto me now arise get thee out from this land and return unto the land of thy kindred
nd achel and eah answered and said unto him s there yet any portion or inheritance for us in our fathers house
re we not counted of him strangers for he hath sold us and hath quite devoured also our money
or all the riches which od hath taken from our father that is ours and our childrens now then whatsoever od hath said unto thee do
hen acob rose up and set his sons and his wives upon camels and he carried away all his cattle and all his goods which he had gotten the cattle of his getting which he had gotten in adanaram for to go to saac his father in the land of anaan
nd aban went to shear his sheep and achel had stolen the images that were her fathers
nd acob stole away unawares to aban the yrian in that he told him not that he fled
o he fled with all that he had and he rose up and passed over the river and set his face toward the mount ilead
nd it was told aban on the third day that acob was fled
nd he took his brethren with him and pursued after him seven days journey and they overtook him in the mount ilead
nd od came to aban the yrian in a dream by night and said unto him ake heed that thou speak not to acob either good or bad
hen aban overtook acob
ow acob had pitched his tent in the mount and aban with his brethren pitched in the mount of ilead
nd aban said to acob hat hast thou done that thou hast stolen away unawares to me and carried away my daughters as captives taken with the sword
herefore didst thou flee away secretly and steal away from me and didst not tell me that  might have sent thee away with mirth and with songs with tabret and with harp
nd hast not suffered me to kiss my sons and my daughters thou hast now done foolishly in so doing
t is in the power of my hand to do you hurt but the od of your father spake unto me yesternight saying ake thou heed that thou speak not to acob either good or bad
nd now though thou wouldest needs be gone because thou sore longedst after thy fathers house yet wherefore hast thou stolen my gods
nd acob answered and said to aban ecause  was afraid for  said eradventure thou wouldest take by force thy daughters from me
ith whomsoever thou findest thy gods let him not live before our brethren discern thou what is thine with me and take it to thee
or acob knew not that achel had stolen them
nd aban went into acobs tent and into eahs tent and into the two maidservants tents but he found them not
hen went he out of eahs tent and entered into achels tent
ow achel had taken the images and put them in the camels furniture and sat upon them
nd aban searched all the tent but found them not
nd she said to her father et it not displease my lord that  cannot rise up before thee for the custom of women is upon me
nd he searched but found not the images
nd acob was wroth and chode with aban and acob answered and said to aban hat is my trespass what is my sin that thou hast so hotly pursued after me
hereas thou hast searched all my stuff what hast thou found of all thy household stuff set it here before my brethren and thy brethren that they may judge betwixt us both
his twenty years have  been with thee thy ewes and thy she goats have not cast their young and the rams of thy flock have  not eaten
hat which was torn of beasts  brought not unto thee  bare the loss of it of my hand didst thou require it whether stolen by day or stolen by night
hus  was in the day the drought consumed me and the frost by night and my sleep departed from mine eyes
hus have  been twenty years in thy house  served thee fourteen years for thy two daughters and six years for thy cattle and thou hast changed my wages ten times
xcept the od of my father the od of braham and the fear of saac had been with me surely thou hadst sent me away now empty
od hath seen mine affliction and the labour of my hands and rebuked thee yesternight
nd aban answered and said unto acob hese daughters are my daughters and these children are my children and these cattle are my cattle and all that thou seest is mine and what can  do this day unto these my daughters or unto their children which they have born
ow therefore come thou let us make a covenant  and thou and let it be for a witness between me and thee
nd acob took a stone and set it up for a pillar
nd acob said unto his brethren ather stones and they took stones and made an heap and they did eat there upon the heap
nd aban called it egarsahadutha but acob called it aleed
nd aban said his heap is a witness between me and thee this day
herefore was the name of it called aleed and izpah for he said he  watch between me and thee when we are absent one from another
f thou shalt afflict my daughters or if thou shalt take other wives beside my daughters no man is with us see od is witness betwixt me and thee
nd aban said to acob ehold this heap and behold this pillar which  have cast betwixt me and thee this heap be witness and this pillar be witness that  will not pass over this heap to thee and that thou shalt not pass over this heap and this pillar unto me for harm
he od of braham and the od of ahor the od of their father judge betwixt us
nd acob sware by the fear of his father saac
hen acob offered sacrifice upon the mount and called his brethren to eat bread and they did eat bread and tarried all night in the mount
nd early in the morning aban rose up and kissed his sons and his daughters and blessed them and aban departed and returned unto his place
nd acob went on his way and the angels of od met him
nd when acob saw them he said his is ods host and he called the name of that place ahanaim
nd acob sent messengers before him to sau his brother unto the land of eir the country of dom
nd he commanded them saying hus shall ye speak unto my lord sau hy servant acob saith thus  have sojourned with aban and stayed there until now and  have oxen and asses flocks and menservants and womenservants and  have sent to tell my lord that  may find grace in thy sight
nd the messengers returned to acob saying e came to thy brother sau and also he cometh to meet thee and four hundred men with him
hen acob was greatly afraid and distressed and he divided the people that was with him and the flocks and herds and the camels into two bands and said f sau come to the one company and smite it then the other company which is left shall escape
nd acob said  od of my father braham and od of my father saac the  which saidst unto me eturn unto thy country and to thy kindred and  will deal well with thee  am not worthy of the least of all the mercies and of all the truth which thou hast shewed unto thy servant for with my staff  passed over this ordan and now  am become two bands
eliver me  pray thee from the hand of my brother from the hand of sau for  fear him lest he will come and smite me and the mother with the children
nd thou saidst  will surely do thee good and make thy seed as the sand of the sea which cannot be numbered for multitude
nd he lodged there that same night and took of that which came to his hand a present for sau his brother two hundred she goats and twenty he goats two hundred ewes and twenty rams thirty milch camels with their colts forty kine and ten bulls twenty she asses and ten foals
nd he delivered them into the hand of his servants every drove by themselves and said unto his servants ass over before me and put a space betwixt drove and drove
nd he commanded the foremost saying hen sau my brother meeteth thee and asketh thee saying hose art thou and whither goest thou and whose are these before thee
hen thou shalt say hey be thy servant acobs it is a present sent unto my lord sau and behold also he is behind us
nd so commanded he the second and the third and all that followed the droves saying n this manner shall ye speak unto sau when ye find him
nd say ye moreover ehold thy servant acob is behind us
or he said  will appease him with the present that goeth before me and afterward  will see his face peradventure he will accept of me
o went the present over before him and himself lodged that night in the company
nd he rose up that night and took his two wives and his two womenservants and his eleven sons and passed over the ford abbok
nd he took them and sent them over the brook and sent over that he had
nd acob was left alone and there wrestled a man with him until the breaking of the day
nd when he saw that he prevailed not against him he touched the hollow of his thigh and the hollow of acobs thigh was out of joint as he wrestled with him
nd he said et me go for the day breaketh
nd he said  will not let thee go except thou bless me
nd he said unto him hat is thy name
nd he said acob
nd he said hy name shall be called no more acob but srael for as a prince hast thou power with od and with men and hast prevailed
nd acob asked him and said ell me  pray thee thy name
nd he said herefore is it that thou dost ask after my name
nd he blessed him there
nd acob called the name of the place eniel for  have seen od face to face and my life is preserved
nd as he passed over enuel the sun rose upon him and he halted upon his thigh
herefore the children of srael eat not of the sinew which shrank which is upon the hollow of the thigh unto this day because he touched the hollow of acobs thigh in the sinew that shrank
nd acob lifted up his eyes and looked and behold sau came and with him four hundred men
nd he divided the children unto eah and unto achel and unto the two handmaids
nd he put the handmaids and their children foremost and eah and her children after and achel and oseph hindermost
nd he passed over before them and bowed himself to the ground seven times until he came near to his brother
nd sau ran to meet him and embraced him and fell on his neck and kissed him and they wept
nd he lifted up his eyes and saw the women and the children and said ho are those with thee
nd he said he children which od hath graciously given thy servant
hen the handmaidens came near they and their children and they bowed themselves
nd eah also with her children came near and bowed themselves and after came oseph near and achel and they bowed themselves
nd he said hat meanest thou by all this drove which  met
nd he said hese are to find grace in the sight of my lord
nd sau said  have enough my brother keep that thou hast unto thyself
nd acob said ay  pray thee if now  have found grace in thy sight then receive my present at my hand for therefore  have seen thy face as though  had seen the face of od and thou wast pleased with me
ake  pray thee my blessing that is brought to thee because od hath dealt graciously with me and because  have enough
nd he urged him and he took it
nd he said et us take our journey and let us go and  will go before thee
nd he said unto him y lord knoweth that the children are tender and the flocks and herds with young are with me and if men should overdrive them one day all the flock will die
et my lord  pray thee pass over before his servant and  will lead on softly according as the cattle that goeth before me and the children be able to endure until  come unto my lord unto eir
nd sau said et me now leave with thee some of the folk that are with me
nd he said hat needeth it let me find grace in the sight of my lord
o sau returned that day on his way unto eir
nd acob journeyed to uccoth and built him an house and made booths for his cattle therefore the name of the place is called uccoth
nd acob came to halem a city of hechem which is in the land of anaan when he came from adanaram and pitched his tent before the city
nd he bought a parcel of a field where he had spread his tent at the hand of the children of amor hechems father for an hundred pieces of money
nd he erected there an altar and called it lelohesrael
nd inah the daughter of eah which she bare unto acob went out to see the daughters of the land
nd when hechem the son of amor the ivite prince of the country saw her he took her and lay with her and defiled her
nd his soul clave unto inah the daughter of acob and he loved the damsel and spake kindly unto the damsel
nd hechem spake unto his father amor saying et me this damsel to wife
nd acob heard that he had defiled inah his daughter now his sons were with his cattle in the field and acob held his peace until they were come
nd amor the father of hechem went out unto acob to commune with him
nd the sons of acob came out of the field when they heard it and the men were grieved and they were very wroth because he had wrought folly in srael in lying with acobs daughter which thing ought not to be done
nd amor communed with them saying he soul of my son hechem longeth for your daughter  pray you give her him to wife
nd make ye marriages with us and give your daughters unto us and take our daughters unto you
nd ye shall dwell with us and the land shall be before you dwell and trade ye therein and get you possessions therein
nd hechem said unto her father and unto her brethren et me find grace in your eyes and what ye shall say unto me  will give
sk me never so much dowry and gift and  will give according as ye shall say unto me but give me the damsel to wife
nd the sons of acob answered hechem and amor his father deceitfully and said because he had defiled inah their sister and they said unto them e cannot do this thing to give our sister to one that is uncircumcised for that were a reproach unto us but in this will we consent unto you f ye will be as we be that every male of you be circumcised then will we give our daughters unto you and we will take your daughters to us and we will dwell with you and we will become one people
ut if ye will not hearken unto us to be circumcised then will we take our daughter and we will be gone
nd their words pleased amor and hechem amors son
nd the young man deferred not to do the thing because he had delight in acobs daughter and he was more honourable than all the house of his father
nd amor and hechem his son came unto the gate of their city and communed with the men of their city saying hese men are peaceable with us therefore let them dwell in the land and trade therein for the land behold it is large enough for them let us take their daughters to us for wives and let us give them our daughters
nly herein will the men consent unto us for to dwell with us to be one people if every male among us be circumcised as they are circumcised
hall not their cattle and their substance and every beast of theirs be ours only let us consent unto them and they will dwell with us
nd unto amor and unto hechem his son hearkened all that went out of the gate of his city and every male was circumcised all that went out of the gate of his city
nd it came to pass on the third day when they were sore that two of the sons of acob imeon and evi inahs brethren took each man his sword and came upon the city boldly and slew all the males
nd they slew amor and hechem his son with the edge of the sword and took inah out of hechems house and went out
he sons of acob came upon the slain and spoiled the city because they had defiled their sister
hey took their sheep and their oxen and their asses and that which was in the city and that which was in the field and all their wealth and all their little ones and their wives took they captive and spoiled even all that was in the house
nd acob said to imeon and evi e have troubled me to make me to stink among the inhabitants of the land among the anaanites and the erizzites and  being few in number they shall gather themselves together against me and slay me and  shall be destroyed  and my house
nd they said hould he deal with our sister as with an harlot
nd od said unto acob rise go up to ethel and dwell there and make there an altar unto od that appeared unto thee when thou fleddest from the face of sau thy brother
hen acob said unto his household and to all that were with him ut away the strange gods that are among you and be clean and change your garments and let us arise and go up to ethel and  will make there an altar unto od who answered me in the day of my distress and was with me in the way which  went
nd they gave unto acob all the strange gods which were in their hand and all their earrings which were in their ears and acob hid them under the oak which was by hechem
nd they journeyed and the terror of od was upon the cities that were round about them and they did not pursue after the sons of acob
o acob came to uz which is in the land of anaan that is ethel he and all the people that were with him
nd he built there an altar and called the place lbethel because there od appeared unto him when he fled from the face of his brother
ut eborah ebekahs nurse died and she was buried beneath ethel under an oak and the name of it was called llonbachuth
nd od appeared unto acob again when he came out of adanaram and blessed him
nd od said unto him hy name is acob thy name shall not be called any more acob but srael shall be thy name and he called his name srael
nd od said unto him  am od lmighty be fruitful and multiply a nation and a company of nations shall be of thee and kings shall come out of thy loins and the land which  gave braham and saac to thee  will give it and to thy seed after thee will  give the land
nd od went up from him in the place where he talked with him
nd acob set up a pillar in the place where he talked with him even a pillar of stone and he poured a drink offering thereon and he poured oil thereon
nd acob called the name of the place where od spake with him ethel
nd they journeyed from ethel and there was but a little way to come to phrath and achel travailed and she had hard labour
nd it came to pass when she was in hard labour that the midwife said unto her ear not thou shalt have this son also
nd it came to pass as her soul was in departing for she died that she called his name enoni but his father called him enjamin
nd achel died and was buried in the way to phrath which is ethlehem
nd acob set a pillar upon her grave that is the pillar of achels grave unto this day
nd srael journeyed and spread his tent beyond the tower of dar
nd it came to pass when srael dwelt in that land that euben went and lay with ilhah his fathers concubine and srael heard it
ow the sons of acob were twelve the sons of eah euben acobs firstborn and imeon and evi and udah and ssachar and ebulun the sons of achel oseph and enjamin and the sons of ilhah achels handmaid an and aphtali and the sons of ilpah eahs handmaid ad and sher these are the sons of acob which were born to him in adanaram
nd acob came unto saac his father unto amre unto the city of rbah which is ebron where braham and saac sojourned
nd the days of saac were an hundred and fourscore years
nd saac gave up the ghost and died and was gathered unto his people being old and full of days and his sons sau and acob buried him
ow these are the generations of sau who is dom
sau took his wives of the daughters of anaan dah the daughter of lon the ittite and holibamah the daughter of nah the daughter of ibeon the ivite and ashemath shmaels daughter sister of ebajoth
nd dah bare to sau liphaz and ashemath bare euel and holibamah bare eush and aalam and orah these are the sons of sau which were born unto him in the land of anaan
nd sau took his wives and his sons and his daughters and all the persons of his house and his cattle and all his beasts and all his substance which he had got in the land of anaan and went into the country from the face of his brother acob
or their riches were more than that they might dwell together and the land wherein they were strangers could not bear them because of their cattle
hus dwelt sau in mount eir sau is dom
nd these are the generations of sau the father of the domites in mount eir these are the names of saus sons liphaz the son of dah the wife of sau euel the son of ashemath the wife of sau
nd the sons of liphaz were eman mar epho and atam and enaz
nd imna was concubine to liphaz saus son and she bare to liphaz malek these were the sons of dah saus wife
nd these are the sons of euel ahath and erah hammah and izzah these were the sons of ashemath saus wife
nd these were the sons of holibamah the daughter of nah the daughter of ibeon saus wife and she bare to sau eush and aalam and orah
hese were dukes of the sons of sau the sons of liphaz the firstborn son of sau duke eman duke mar duke epho duke enaz duke orah duke atam and duke malek these are the dukes that came of liphaz in the land of dom these were the sons of dah
nd these are the sons of euel saus son duke ahath duke erah duke hammah duke izzah these are the dukes that came of euel in the land of dom these are the sons of ashemath saus wife
nd these are the sons of holibamah saus wife duke eush duke aalam duke orah these were the dukes that came of holibamah the daughter of nah saus wife
hese are the sons of sau who is dom and these are their dukes
hese are the sons of eir the orite who inhabited the land otan and hobal and ibeon and nah and ishon and zer and ishan these are the dukes of the orites the children of eir in the land of dom
nd the children of otan were ori and emam and otans sister was imna
nd the children of hobal were these lvan and anahath and bal hepho and nam
nd these are the children of ibeon both jah and nah this was that nah that found the mules in the wilderness as he fed the asses of ibeon his father
nd the children of nah were these ishon and holibamah the daughter of nah
nd these are the children of ishon emdan and shban and thran and heran
he children of zer are these ilhan and aavan and kan
he children of ishan are these z and ran
hese are the dukes that came of the orites duke otan duke hobal duke ibeon duke nah duke ishon duke zer duke ishan these are the dukes that came of ori among their dukes in the land of eir
nd these are the kings that reigned in the land of dom before there reigned any king over the children of srael
nd ela the son of eor reigned in dom and the name of his city was inhabah
nd ela died and obab the son of erah of ozrah reigned in his stead
nd obab died and usham of the land of emani reigned in his stead
nd usham died and adad the son of edad who smote idian in the field of oab reigned in his stead and the name of his city was vith
nd adad died and amlah of asrekah reigned in his stead
nd amlah died and aul of ehoboth by the river reigned in his stead
nd aul died and aalhanan the son of chbor reigned in his stead
nd aalhanan the son of chbor died and adar reigned in his stead and the name of his city was au and his wifes name was ehetabel the daughter of atred the daughter of ezahab
nd these are the names of the dukes that came of sau according to their families after their places by their names duke imnah duke lvah duke etheth duke holibamah duke lah duke inon duke enaz duke eman duke ibzar duke agdiel duke ram these be the dukes of dom according to their habitations in the land of their possession he is sau the father of the domites
nd acob dwelt in the land wherein his father was a stranger in the land of anaan
hese are the generations of acob
oseph being seventeen years old was feeding the flock with his brethren and the lad was with the sons of ilhah and with the sons of ilpah his fathers wives and oseph brought unto his father their evil report
ow srael loved oseph more than all his children because he was the son of his old age and he made him a coat of many colours
nd when his brethren saw that their father loved him more than all his brethren they hated him and could not speak peaceably unto him
nd oseph dreamed a dream and he told it his brethren and they hated him yet the more
nd he said unto them ear  pray you this dream which  have dreamed for behold we were binding sheaves in the field and lo my sheaf arose and also stood upright and behold your sheaves stood round about and made obeisance to my sheaf
nd his brethren said to him halt thou indeed reign over us or shalt thou indeed have dominion over us
nd they hated him yet the more for his dreams and for his words
nd he dreamed yet another dream and told it his brethren and said ehold  have dreamed a dream more and behold the sun and the moon and the eleven stars made obeisance to me
nd he told it to his father and to his brethren and his father rebuked him and said unto him hat is this dream that thou hast dreamed
hall  and thy mother and thy brethren indeed come to bow down ourselves to thee to the earth
nd his brethren envied him but his father observed the saying
nd his brethren went to feed their fathers flock in hechem
nd srael said unto oseph o not thy brethren feed the flock in hechem come and  will send thee unto them
nd he said to him ere am 
nd he said to him o  pray thee see whether it be well with thy brethren and well with the flocks and bring me word again
o he sent him out of the vale of ebron and he came to hechem
nd a certain man found him and behold he was wandering in the field and the man asked him saying hat seekest thou
nd he said  seek my brethren tell me  pray thee where they feed their flocks
nd the man said hey are departed hence for  heard them say et us go to othan
nd oseph went after his brethren and found them in othan
nd when they saw him afar off even before he came near unto them they conspired against him to slay him
nd they said one to another ehold this dreamer cometh
ome now therefore and let us slay him and cast him into some pit and we will say ome evil beast hath devoured him and we shall see what will become of his dreams
nd euben heard it and he delivered him out of their hands and said et us not kill him
nd euben said unto them hed no blood but cast him into this pit that is in the wilderness and lay no hand upon him that he might rid him out of their hands to deliver him to his father again
nd it came to pass when oseph was come unto his brethren that they stript oseph out of his coat his coat of many colours that was on him and they took him and cast him into a pit and the pit was empty there was no water in it
nd they sat down to eat bread and they lifted up their eyes and looked and behold a company of shmeelites came from ilead with their camels bearing spicery and balm and myrrh going to carry it down to gypt
nd udah said unto his brethren hat profit is it if we slay our brother and conceal his blood
ome and let us sell him to the shmeelites and let not our hand be upon him for he is our brother and our flesh
nd his brethren were content
hen there passed by idianites merchantmen and they drew and lifted up oseph out of the pit and sold oseph to the shmeelites for twenty pieces of silver and they brought oseph into gypt
nd euben returned unto the pit and behold oseph was not in the pit and he rent his clothes
nd he returned unto his brethren and said he child is not and  whither shall  go
nd they took osephs coat and killed a kid of the goats and dipped the coat in the blood and they sent the coat of many colours and they brought it to their father and said his have we found know now whether it be thy sons coat or no
nd he knew it and said t is my sons coat an evil beast hath devoured him oseph is without doubt rent in pieces
nd acob rent his clothes and put sackcloth upon his loins and mourned for his son many days
nd all his sons and all his daughters rose up to comfort him but he refused to be comforted and he said or  will go down into the grave unto my son mourning
hus his father wept for him
nd the idianites sold him into gypt unto otiphar an officer of haraohs and captain of the guard
nd it came to pass at that time that udah went down from his brethren and turned in to a certain dullamite whose name was irah
nd udah saw there a daughter of a certain anaanite whose name was huah and he took her and went in unto her
nd she conceived and bare a son and he called his name r
nd she conceived again and bare a son and she called his name nan
nd she yet again conceived and bare a son and called his name helah and he was at hezib when she bare him
nd udah took a wife for r his firstborn whose name was amar
nd r udahs firstborn was wicked in the sight of the  and the  slew him
nd udah said unto nan o in unto thy brothers wife and marry her and raise up seed to thy brother
nd nan knew that the seed should not be his and it came to pass when he went in unto his brothers wife that he spilled it on the ground lest that he should give seed to his brother
nd the thing which he did displeased the  wherefore he slew him also
hen said udah to amar his daughter in law emain a widow at thy fathers house till helah my son be grown for he said est peradventure he die also as his brethren did
nd amar went and dwelt in her fathers house
nd in process of time the daughter of huah udahs wife died and udah was comforted and went up unto his sheepshearers to imnath he and his friend irah the dullamite
nd it was told amar saying ehold thy father in law goeth up to imnath to shear his sheep
nd she put her widows garments off from her and covered her with a vail and wrapped herself and sat in an open place which is by the way to imnath for she saw that helah was grown and she was not given unto him to wife
hen udah saw her he thought her to be an harlot because she had covered her face
nd he turned unto her by the way and said o to  pray thee let me come in unto thee for he knew not that she was his daughter in law nd she said hat wilt thou give me that thou mayest come in unto me
nd he said  will send thee a kid from the flock
nd she said ilt thou give me a pledge till thou send it
nd he said hat pledge shall  give thee
nd she said hy signet and thy bracelets and thy staff that is in thine hand
nd he gave it her and came in unto her and she conceived by him
nd she arose and went away and laid by her vail from her and put on the garments of her widowhood
nd udah sent the kid by the hand of his friend the dullamite to receive his pledge from the womans hand but he found her not
hen he asked the men of that place saying here is the harlot that was openly by the way side
nd they said here was no harlot in this place
nd he returned to udah and said  cannot find her and also the men of the place said that there was no harlot in this place
nd udah said et her take it to her lest we be shamed behold  sent this kid and thou hast not found her
nd it came to pass about three months after that it was told udah saying amar thy daughter in law hath played the harlot and also behold she is with child by whoredom
nd udah said ring her forth and let her be burnt
hen she was brought forth she sent to her father in law saying y the man whose these are am  with child and she said iscern  pray thee whose are these the signet and bracelets and staff
nd udah acknowledged them and said he hath been more righteous than  because that  gave her not to helah my son
nd he knew her again no more
nd it came to pass in the time of her travail that behold twins were in her womb
nd it came to pass when she travailed that the one put out his hand and the midwife took and bound upon his hand a scarlet thread saying his came out first
nd it came to pass as he drew back his hand that behold his brother came out and she said ow hast thou broken forth this breach be upon thee therefore his name was called harez
nd afterward came out his brother that had the scarlet thread upon his hand and his name was called arah
nd oseph was brought down to gypt and otiphar an officer of haraoh captain of the guard an gyptian bought him of the hands of the shmeelites which had brought him down thither
nd the  was with oseph and he was a prosperous man and he was in the house of his master the gyptian
nd his master saw that the  was with him and that the  made all that he did to prosper in his hand
nd oseph found grace in his sight and he served him and he made him overseer over his house and all that he had he put into his hand
nd it came to pass from the time that he had made him overseer in his house and over all that he had that the  blessed the gyptians house for osephs sake and the blessing of the  was upon all that he had in the house and in the field
nd he left all that he had in osephs hand and he knew not ought he had save the bread which he did eat
nd oseph was a goodly person and well favoured
nd it came to pass after these things that his masters wife cast her eyes upon oseph and she said ie with me
ut he refused and said unto his masters wife ehold my master wotteth not what is with me in the house and he hath committed all that he hath to my hand there is none greater in this house than  neither hath he kept back any thing from me but thee because thou art his wife how then can  do this great wickedness and sin against od
nd it came to pass as she spake to oseph day by day that he hearkened not unto her to lie by her or to be with her
nd it came to pass about this time that oseph went into the house to do his business and there was none of the men of the house there within
nd she caught him by his garment saying ie with me and he left his garment in her hand and fled and got him out
nd it came to pass when she saw that he had left his garment in her hand and was fled forth that she called unto the men of her house and spake unto them saying ee he hath brought in an ebrew unto us to mock us he came in unto me to lie with me and  cried with a loud voice and it came to pass when he heard that  lifted up my voice and cried that he left his garment with me and fled and got him out
nd she laid up his garment by her until his lord came home
nd she spake unto him according to these words saying he ebrew servant which thou hast brought unto us came in unto me to mock me and it came to pass as  lifted up my voice and cried that he left his garment with me and fled out
nd it came to pass when his master heard the words of his wife which she spake unto him saying fter this manner did thy servant to me that his wrath was kindled
nd osephs master took him and put him into the prison a place where the kings prisoners were bound and he was there in the prison
ut the  was with oseph and shewed him mercy and gave him favour in the sight of the keeper of the prison
nd the keeper of the prison committed to osephs hand all the prisoners that were in the prison and whatsoever they did there he was the doer of it
he keeper of the prison looked not to any thing that was under his hand because the  was with him and that which he did the  made it to prosper
nd it came to pass after these things that the butler of the king of gypt and his baker had offended their lord the king of gypt
nd haraoh was wroth against two of his officers against the chief of the butlers and against the chief of the bakers
nd he put them in ward in the house of the captain of the guard into the prison the place where oseph was bound
nd the captain of the guard charged oseph with them and he served them and they continued a season in ward
nd they dreamed a dream both of them each man his dream in one night each man according to the interpretation of his dream the butler and the baker of the king of gypt which were bound in the prison
nd oseph came in unto them in the morning and looked upon them and behold they were sad
nd he asked haraohs officers that were with him in the ward of his lords house saying herefore look ye so sadly to day
nd they said unto him e have dreamed a dream and there is no interpreter of it
nd oseph said unto them o not interpretations belong to od tell me them  pray you
nd the chief butler told his dream to oseph and said to him n my dream behold a vine was before me and in the vine were three branches and it was as though it budded and her blossoms shot forth and the clusters thereof brought forth ripe grapes and haraohs cup was in my hand and  took the grapes and pressed them into haraohs cup and  gave the cup into haraohs hand
nd oseph said unto him his is the interpretation of it he three branches are three days yet within three days shall haraoh lift up thine head and restore thee unto thy place and thou shalt deliver haraohs cup into his hand after the former manner when thou wast his butler
ut think on me when it shall be well with thee and shew kindness  pray thee unto me and make mention of me unto haraoh and bring me out of this house for indeed  was stolen away out of the land of the ebrews and here also have  done nothing that they should put me into the dungeon
hen the chief baker saw that the interpretation was good he said unto oseph  also was in my dream and behold  had three white baskets on my head and in the uppermost basket there was of all manner of bakemeats for haraoh and the birds did eat them out of the basket upon my head
nd oseph answered and said his is the interpretation thereof he three baskets are three days yet within three days shall haraoh lift up thy head from off thee and shall hang thee on a tree and the birds shall eat thy flesh from off thee
nd it came to pass the third day which was haraohs birthday that he made a feast unto all his servants and he lifted up the head of the chief butler and of the chief baker among his servants
nd he restored the chief butler unto his butlership again and he gave the cup into haraohs hand but he hanged the chief baker as oseph had interpreted to them
et did not the chief butler remember oseph but forgat him
nd it came to pass at the end of two full years that haraoh dreamed and behold he stood by the river
nd behold there came up out of the river seven well favoured kine and fatfleshed and they fed in a meadow
nd behold seven other kine came up after them out of the river ill favoured and leanfleshed and stood by the other kine upon the brink of the river
nd the ill favoured and leanfleshed kine did eat up the seven well favoured and fat kine
o haraoh awoke
nd he slept and dreamed the second time and behold seven ears of corn came up upon one stalk rank and good
nd behold seven thin ears and blasted with the east wind sprung up after them
nd the seven thin ears devoured the seven rank and full ears
nd haraoh awoke and behold it was a dream
nd it came to pass in the morning that his spirit was troubled and he sent and called for all the magicians of gypt and all the wise men thereof and haraoh told them his dream but there was none that could interpret them unto haraoh
hen spake the chief butler unto haraoh saying  do remember my faults this day haraoh was wroth with his servants and put me in ward in the captain of the guards house both me and the chief baker and we dreamed a dream in one night  and he we dreamed each man according to the interpretation of his dream
nd there was there with us a young man an ebrew servant to the captain of the guard and we told him and he interpreted to us our dreams to each man according to his dream he did interpret
nd it came to pass as he interpreted to us so it was me he restored unto mine office and him he hanged
hen haraoh sent and called oseph and they brought him hastily out of the dungeon and he shaved himself and changed his raiment and came in unto haraoh
nd haraoh said unto oseph  have dreamed a dream and there is none that can interpret it and  have heard say of thee that thou canst understand a dream to interpret it
nd oseph answered haraoh saying t is not in me od shall give haraoh an answer of peace
nd haraoh said unto oseph n my dream behold  stood upon the bank of the river and behold there came up out of the river seven kine fatfleshed and well favoured and they fed in a meadow and behold seven other kine came up after them poor and very ill favoured and leanfleshed such as  never saw in all the land of gypt for badness and the lean and the ill favoured kine did eat up the first seven fat kine and when they had eaten them up it could not be known that they had eaten them but they were still ill favoured as at the beginning
o  awoke
nd  saw in my dream and behold seven ears came up in one stalk full and good and behold seven ears withered thin and blasted with the east wind sprung up after them and the thin ears devoured the seven good ears and  told this unto the magicians but there was none that could declare it to me
nd oseph said unto haraoh he dream of haraoh is one od hath shewed haraoh what he is about to do
he seven good kine are seven years and the seven good ears are seven years the dream is one
nd the seven thin and ill favoured kine that came up after them are seven years and the seven empty ears blasted with the east wind shall be seven years of famine
his is the thing which  have spoken unto haraoh hat od is about to do he sheweth unto haraoh
ehold there come seven years of great plenty throughout all the land of gypt and there shall arise after them seven years of famine and all the plenty shall be forgotten in the land of gypt and the famine shall consume the land and the plenty shall not be known in the land by reason of that famine following for it shall be very grievous
nd for that the dream was doubled unto haraoh twice it is because the thing is established by od and od will shortly bring it to pass
ow therefore let haraoh look out a man discreet and wise and set him over the land of gypt
et haraoh do this and let him appoint officers over the land and take up the fifth part of the land of gypt in the seven plenteous years
nd let them gather all the food of those good years that come and lay up corn under the hand of haraoh and let them keep food in the cities
nd that food shall be for store to the land against the seven years of famine which shall be in the land of gypt that the land perish not through the famine
nd the thing was good in the eyes of haraoh and in the eyes of all his servants
nd haraoh said unto his servants an we find such a one as this is a man in whom the pirit of od is
nd haraoh said unto oseph orasmuch as od hath shewed thee all this there is none so discreet and wise as thou art thou shalt be over my house and according unto thy word shall all my people be ruled only in the throne will  be greater than thou
nd haraoh said unto oseph ee  have set thee over all the land of gypt
nd haraoh took off his ring from his hand and put it upon osephs hand and arrayed him in vestures of fine linen and put a gold chain about his neck and he made him to ride in the second chariot which he had and they cried before him ow the knee and he made him ruler over all the land of gypt
nd haraoh said unto oseph  am haraoh and without thee shall no man lift up his hand or foot in all the land of gypt
nd haraoh called osephs name aphnathpaaneah and he gave him to wife senath the daughter of otipherah priest of n
nd oseph went out over all the land of gypt
nd oseph was thirty years old when he stood before haraoh king of gypt
nd oseph went out from the presence of haraoh and went throughout all the land of gypt
nd in the seven plenteous years the earth brought forth by handfuls
nd he gathered up all the food of the seven years which were in the land of gypt and laid up the food in the cities the food of the field which was round about every city laid he up in the same
nd oseph gathered corn as the sand of the sea very much until he left numbering for it was without number
nd unto oseph were born two sons before the years of famine came which senath the daughter of otipherah priest of n bare unto him
nd oseph called the name of the firstborn anasseh or od said he hath made me forget all my toil and all my fathers house
nd the name of the second called he phraim or od hath caused me to be fruitful in the land of my affliction
nd the seven years of plenteousness that was in the land of gypt were ended
nd the seven years of dearth began to come according as oseph had said and the dearth was in all lands but in all the land of gypt there was bread
nd when all the land of gypt was famished the people cried to haraoh for bread and haraoh said unto all the gyptians o unto oseph what he saith to you do
nd the famine was over all the face of the earth nd oseph opened all the storehouses and sold unto the gyptians and the famine waxed sore in the land of gypt
nd all countries came into gypt to oseph for to buy corn because that the famine was so sore in all lands
ow when acob saw that there was corn in gypt acob said unto his sons hy do ye look one upon another
nd he said ehold  have heard that there is corn in gypt get you down thither and buy for us from thence that we may live and not die
nd osephs ten brethren went down to buy corn in gypt
ut enjamin osephs brother acob sent not with his brethren for he said est peradventure mischief befall him
nd the sons of srael came to buy corn among those that came for the famine was in the land of anaan
nd oseph was the governor over the land and he it was that sold to all the people of the land and osephs brethren came and bowed down themselves before him with their faces to the earth
nd oseph saw his brethren and he knew them but made himself strange unto them and spake roughly unto them and he said unto them hence come ye
nd they said rom the land of anaan to buy food
nd oseph knew his brethren but they knew not him
nd oseph remembered the dreams which he dreamed of them and said unto them e are spies to see the nakedness of the land ye are come
nd they said unto him ay my lord but to buy food are thy servants come
e are all one mans sons we are true men thy servants are no spies
nd he said unto them ay but to see the nakedness of the land ye are come
nd they said hy servants are twelve brethren the sons of one man in the land of anaan and behold the youngest is this day with our father and one is not
nd oseph said unto them hat is it that  spake unto you saying e are spies hereby ye shall be proved y the life of haraoh ye shall not go forth hence except your youngest brother come hither
end one of you and let him fetch your brother and ye shall be kept in prison that your words may be proved whether there be any truth in you or else by the life of haraoh surely ye are spies
nd he put them all together into ward three days
nd oseph said unto them the third day his do and live for  fear od if ye be true men let one of your brethren be bound in the house of your prison go ye carry corn for the famine of your houses but bring your youngest brother unto me so shall your words be verified and ye shall not die
nd they did so
nd they said one to another e are verily guilty concerning our brother in that we saw the anguish of his soul when he besought us and we would not hear therefore is this distress come upon us
nd euben answered them saying pake  not unto you saying o not sin against the child and ye would not hear therefore behold also his blood is required
nd they knew not that oseph understood them for he spake unto them by an interpreter
nd he turned himself about from them and wept and returned to them again and communed with them and took from them imeon and bound him before their eyes
hen oseph commanded to fill their sacks with corn and to restore every mans money into his sack and to give them provision for the way and thus did he unto them
nd they laded their asses with the corn and departed thence
nd as one of them opened his sack to give his ass provender in the inn he espied his money for behold it was in his sacks mouth
nd he said unto his brethren y money is restored and lo it is even in my sack and their heart failed them and they were afraid saying one to another hat is this that od hath done unto us
nd they came unto acob their father unto the land of anaan and told him all that befell unto them saying he man who is the lord of the land spake roughly to us and took us for spies of the country
nd we said unto him e are true men we are no spies we be twelve brethren sons of our father one is not and the youngest is this day with our father in the land of anaan
nd the man the lord of the country said unto us ereby shall  know that ye are true men leave one of your brethren here with me and take food for the famine of your households and be gone and bring your youngest brother unto me then shall  know that ye are no spies but that ye are true men so will  deliver you your brother and ye shall traffick in the land
nd it came to pass as they emptied their sacks that behold every mans bundle of money was in his sack and when both they and their father saw the bundles of money they were afraid
nd acob their father said unto them e have ye bereaved of my children oseph is not and imeon is not and ye will take enjamin away all these things are against me
nd euben spake unto his father saying lay my two sons if  bring him not to thee deliver him into my hand and  will bring him to thee again
nd he said y son shall not go down with you for his brother is dead and he is left alone if mischief befall him by the way in the which ye go then shall ye bring down my gray hairs with sorrow to the grave
nd the famine was sore in the land
nd it came to pass when they had eaten up the corn which they had brought out of gypt their father said unto them o again buy us a little food
nd udah spake unto him saying he man did solemnly protest unto us saying e shall not see my face except your brother be with you
f thou wilt send our brother with us we will go down and buy thee food but if thou wilt not send him we will not go down for the man said unto us e shall not see my face except your brother be with you
nd srael said herefore dealt ye so ill with me as to tell the man whether ye had yet a brother
nd they said he man asked us straitly of our state and of our kindred saying s your father yet alive have ye another brother and we told him according to the tenor of these words could we certainly know that he would say ring your brother down
nd udah said unto srael his father end the lad with me and we will arise and go that we may live and not die both we and thou and also our little ones
 will be surety for him of my hand shalt thou require him if  bring him not unto thee and set him before thee then let me bear the blame for ever for except we had lingered surely now we had returned this second time
nd their father srael said unto them f it must be so now do this take of the best fruits in the land in your vessels and carry down the man a present a little balm and a little honey spices and myrrh nuts and almonds and take double money in your hand and the money that was brought again in the mouth of your sacks carry it again in your hand peradventure it was an oversight take also your brother and arise go again unto the man and od lmighty give you mercy before the man that he may send away your other brother and enjamin
f  be bereaved of my children  am bereaved
nd the men took that present and they took double money in their hand and enjamin and rose up and went down to gypt and stood before oseph
nd when oseph saw enjamin with them he said to the ruler of his house ring these men home and slay and make ready for these men shall dine with me at noon
nd the man did as oseph bade and the man brought the men into osephs house
nd the men were afraid because they were brought into osephs house and they said ecause of the money that was returned in our sacks at the first time are we brought in that he may seek occasion against us and fall upon us and take us for bondmen and our asses
nd they came near to the steward of osephs house and they communed with him at the door of the house and said  sir we came indeed down at the first time to buy food and it came to pass when we came to the inn that we opened our sacks and behold every mans money was in the mouth of his sack our money in full weight and we have brought it again in our hand
nd other money have we brought down in our hands to buy food we cannot tell who put our money in our sacks
nd he said eace be to you fear not your od and the od of your father hath given you treasure in your sacks  had your money
nd he brought imeon out unto them
nd the man brought the men into osephs house and gave them water and they washed their feet and he gave their asses provender
nd they made ready the present against oseph came at noon for they heard that they should eat bread there
nd when oseph came home they brought him the present which was in their hand into the house and bowed themselves to him to the earth
nd he asked them of their welfare and said s your father well the old man of whom ye spake
s he yet alive
nd they answered hy servant our father is in good health he is yet alive
nd they bowed down their heads and made obeisance
nd he lifted up his eyes and saw his brother enjamin his mothers son and said s this your younger brother of whom ye spake unto me
nd he said od be gracious unto thee my son
nd oseph made haste for his bowels did yearn upon his brother and he sought where to weep and he entered into his chamber and wept there
nd he washed his face and went out and refrained himself and said et on bread
nd they set on for him by himself and for them by themselves and for the gyptians which did eat with him by themselves because the gyptians might not eat bread with the ebrews for that is an abomination unto the gyptians
nd they sat before him the firstborn according to his birthright and the youngest according to his youth and the men marvelled one at another
nd he took and sent messes unto them from before him but enjamins mess was five times so much as any of theirs
nd they drank and were merry with him
nd he commanded the steward of his house saying ill the mens sacks with food as much as they can carry and put every mans money in his sacks mouth
nd put my cup the silver cup in the sacks mouth of the youngest and his corn money
nd he did according to the word that oseph had spoken
s soon as the morning was light the men were sent away they and their asses
nd when they were gone out of the city and not yet far off oseph said unto his steward p follow after the men and when thou dost overtake them say unto them herefore have ye rewarded evil for good
s not this it in which my lord drinketh and whereby indeed he divineth ye have done evil in so doing
nd he overtook them and he spake unto them these same words
nd they said unto him herefore saith my lord these words
od forbid that thy servants should do according to this thing behold the money which we found in our sacks mouths we brought again unto thee out of the land of anaan how then should we steal out of thy lords house silver or gold
ith whomsoever of thy servants it be found both let him die and we also will be my lords bondmen
nd he said ow also let it be according unto your words he with whom it is found shall be my servant and ye shall be blameless
hen they speedily took down every man his sack to the ground and opened every man his sack
nd he searched and began at the eldest and left at the youngest and the cup was found in enjamins sack
hen they rent their clothes and laded every man his ass and returned to the city
nd udah and his brethren came to osephs house for he was yet there and they fell before him on the ground
nd oseph said unto them hat deed is this that ye have done wot ye not that such a man as  can certainly divine
nd udah said hat shall we say unto my lord what shall we speak or how shall we clear ourselves
od hath found out the iniquity of thy servants behold we are my lords servants both we and he also with whom the cup is found
nd he said od forbid that  should do so but the man in whose hand the cup is found he shall be my servant and as for you get you up in peace unto your father
hen udah came near unto him and said h my lord let thy servant  pray thee speak a word in my lords ears and let not thine anger burn against thy servant for thou art even as haraoh
y lord asked his servants saying ave ye a father or a brother
nd we said unto my lord e have a father an old man and a child of his old age a little one and his brother is dead and he alone is left of his mother and his father loveth him
nd thou saidst unto thy servants ring him down unto me that  may set mine eyes upon him
nd we said unto my lord he lad cannot leave his father for if he should leave his father his father would die
nd thou saidst unto thy servants xcept your youngest brother come down with you ye shall see my face no more
nd it came to pass when we came up unto thy servant my father we told him the words of my lord
nd our father said o again and buy us a little food
nd we said e cannot go down if our youngest brother be with us then will we go down for we may not see the mans face except our youngest brother be with us
nd thy servant my father said unto us e know that my wife bare me two sons and the one went out from me and  said urely he is torn in pieces and  saw him not since and if ye take this also from me and mischief befall him ye shall bring down my gray hairs with sorrow to the grave
ow therefore when  come to thy servant my father and the lad be not with us seeing that his life is bound up in the lads life it shall come to pass when he seeth that the lad is not with us that he will die and thy servants shall bring down the gray hairs of thy servant our father with sorrow to the grave
or thy servant became surety for the lad unto my father saying f  bring him not unto thee then  shall bear the blame to my father for ever
ow therefore  pray thee let thy servant abide instead of the lad a bondman to my lord and let the lad go up with his brethren
or how shall  go up to my father and the lad be not with me lest peradventure  see the evil that shall come on my father
hen oseph could not refrain himself before all them that stood by him and he cried ause every man to go out from me
nd there stood no man with him while oseph made himself known unto his brethren
nd he wept aloud and the gyptians and the house of haraoh heard
nd oseph said unto his brethren  am oseph doth my father yet live
nd his brethren could not answer him for they were troubled at his presence
nd oseph said unto his brethren ome near to me  pray you
nd they came near
nd he said  am oseph your brother whom ye sold into gypt
ow therefore be not grieved nor angry with yourselves that ye sold me hither for od did send me before you to preserve life
or these two years hath the famine been in the land and yet there are five years in the which there shall neither be earing nor harvest
nd od sent me before you to preserve you a posterity in the earth and to save your lives by a great deliverance
o now it was not you that sent me hither but od and he hath made me a father to haraoh and lord of all his house and a ruler throughout all the land of gypt
aste ye and go up to my father and say unto him hus saith thy son oseph od hath made me lord of all gypt come down unto me tarry not and thou shalt dwell in the land of oshen and thou shalt be near unto me thou and thy children and thy childrens children and thy flocks and thy herds and all that thou hast and there will  nourish thee for yet there are five years of famine lest thou and thy household and all that thou hast come to poverty
nd behold your eyes see and the eyes of my brother enjamin that it is my mouth that speaketh unto you
nd ye shall tell my father of all my glory in gypt and of all that ye have seen and ye shall haste and bring down my father hither
nd he fell upon his brother enjamins neck and wept and enjamin wept upon his neck
oreover he kissed all his brethren and wept upon them and after that his brethren talked with him
nd the fame thereof was heard in haraohs house saying osephs brethren are come and it pleased haraoh well and his servants
nd haraoh said unto oseph ay unto thy brethren his do ye lade your beasts and go get you unto the land of anaan and take your father and your households and come unto me and  will give you the good of the land of gypt and ye shall eat the fat of the land
ow thou art commanded this do ye take you wagons out of the land of gypt for your little ones and for your wives and bring your father and come
lso regard not your stuff for the good of all the land of gypt is yours
nd the children of srael did so and oseph gave them wagons according to the commandment of haraoh and gave them provision for the way
o all of them he gave each man changes of raiment but to enjamin he gave three hundred pieces of silver and five changes of raiment
nd to his father he sent after this manner ten asses laden with the good things of gypt and ten she asses laden with corn and bread and meat for his father by the way
o he sent his brethren away and they departed and he said unto them ee that ye fall not out by the way
nd they went up out of gypt and came into the land of anaan unto acob their father and told him saying oseph is yet alive and he is governor over all the land of gypt
nd acobs heart fainted for he believed them not
nd they told him all the words of oseph which he had said unto them and when he saw the wagons which oseph had sent to carry him the spirit of acob their father revived and srael said t is enough oseph my son is yet alive  will go and see him before  die
nd srael took his journey with all that he had and came to eersheba and offered sacrifices unto the od of his father saac
nd od spake unto srael in the visions of the night and said acob acob
nd he said ere am 
nd he said  am od the od of thy father fear not to go down into gypt for  will there make of thee a great nation  will go down with thee into gypt and  will also surely bring thee up again and oseph shall put his hand upon thine eyes
nd acob rose up from eersheba and the sons of srael carried acob their father and their little ones and their wives in the wagons which haraoh had sent to carry him
nd they took their cattle and their goods which they had gotten in the land of anaan and came into gypt acob and all his seed with him his sons and his sons sons with him his daughters and his sons daughters and all his seed brought he with him into gypt
nd these are the names of the children of srael which came into gypt acob and his sons euben acobs firstborn

he sons of achel acobs wife oseph and enjamin
