nd he sent udah before him unto oseph to direct his face unto oshen and they came into the land of oshen
nd oseph made ready his chariot and went up to meet srael his father to oshen and presented himself unto him and he fell on his neck and wept on his neck a good while
nd srael said unto oseph ow let me die since  have seen thy face because thou art yet alive
nd oseph said unto his brethren and unto his fathers house  will go up and shew haraoh and say unto him y brethren and my fathers house which were in the land of anaan are come unto me and the men are shepherds for their trade hath been to feed cattle and they have brought their flocks and their herds and all that they have
nd it shall come to pass when haraoh shall call you and shall say hat is your occupation
hat ye shall say hy servants trade hath been about cattle from our youth even until now both we and also our fathers that ye may dwell in the land of oshen for every shepherd is an abomination unto the gyptians
hen oseph came and told haraoh and said y father and my brethren and their flocks and their herds and all that they have are come out of the land of anaan and behold they are in the land of oshen
nd he took some of his brethren even five men and presented them unto haraoh
nd haraoh said unto his brethren hat is your occupation
nd they said unto haraoh hy servants are shepherds both we and also our fathers
hey said moreover unto haraoh or to sojourn in the land are we come for thy servants have no pasture for their flocks for the famine is sore in the land of anaan now therefore we pray thee let thy servants dwell in the land of oshen
nd haraoh spake unto oseph saying hy father and thy brethren are come unto thee the land of gypt is before thee in the best of the land make thy father and brethren to dwell in the land of oshen let them dwell and if thou knowest any men of activity among them then make them rulers over my cattle
nd oseph brought in acob his father and set him before haraoh and acob blessed haraoh
nd haraoh said unto acob ow old art thou
nd acob said unto haraoh he days of the years of my pilgrimage are an hundred and thirty years few and evil have the days of the years of my life been and have not attained unto the days of the years of the life of my fathers in the days of their pilgrimage
nd acob blessed haraoh and went out from before haraoh
nd oseph placed his father and his brethren and gave them a possession in the land of gypt in the best of the land in the land of ameses as haraoh had commanded
nd oseph nourished his father and his brethren and all his fathers household with bread according to their families
nd there was no bread in all the land for the famine was very sore so that the land of gypt and all the land of anaan fainted by reason of the famine
nd oseph gathered up all the money that was found in the land of gypt and in the land of anaan for the corn which they bought and oseph brought the money into haraohs house
nd when money failed in the land of gypt and in the land of anaan all the gyptians came unto oseph and said ive us bread for why should we die in thy presence for the money faileth
nd oseph said ive your cattle and  will give you for your cattle if money fail
nd they brought their cattle unto oseph and oseph gave them bread in exchange for horses and for the flocks and for the cattle of the herds and for the asses and he fed them with bread for all their cattle for that year
hen that year was ended they came unto him the second year and said unto him e will not hide it from my lord how that our money is spent my lord also hath our herds of cattle there is not ought left in the sight of my lord but our bodies and our lands wherefore shall we die before thine eyes both we and our land buy us and our land for bread and we and our land will be servants unto haraoh and give us seed that we may live and not die that the land be not desolate
nd oseph bought all the land of gypt for haraoh for the gyptians sold every man his field because the famine prevailed over them so the land became haraohs
nd as for the people he removed them to cities from one end of the borders of gypt even to the other end thereof
nly the land of the priests bought he not for the priests had a portion assigned them of haraoh and did eat their portion which haraoh gave them wherefore they sold not their lands
hen oseph said unto the people ehold  have bought you this day and your land for haraoh lo here is seed for you and ye shall sow the land
nd it shall come to pass in the increase that ye shall give the fifth part unto haraoh and four parts shall be your own for seed of the field and for your food and for them of your households and for food for your little ones
nd they said hou hast saved our lives let us find grace in the sight of my lord and we will be haraohs servants
nd oseph made it a law over the land of gypt unto this day that haraoh should have the fifth part except the land of the priests only which became not haraohs
nd srael dwelt in the land of gypt in the country of oshen and they had possessions therein and grew and multiplied exceedingly
nd acob lived in the land of gypt seventeen years so the whole age of acob was an hundred forty and seven years
nd the time drew nigh that srael must die and he called his son oseph and said unto him f now  have found grace in thy sight put  pray thee thy hand under my thigh and deal kindly and truly with me bury me not  pray thee in gypt but  will lie with my fathers and thou shalt carry me out of gypt and bury me in their buryingplace
nd he said  will do as thou hast said
nd he said wear unto me
nd he sware unto him
nd srael bowed himself upon the beds head
nd it came to pass after these things that one told oseph ehold thy father is sick and he took with him his two sons anasseh and phraim
nd one told acob and said ehold thy son oseph cometh unto thee and srael strengthened himself and sat upon the bed
nd acob said unto oseph od lmighty appeared unto me at uz in the land of anaan and blessed me and said unto me ehold  will make thee fruitful and multiply thee and  will make of thee a multitude of people and will give this land to thy seed after thee for an everlasting possession
nd now thy two sons phraim and anasseh which were born unto thee in the land of gypt before  came unto thee into gypt are mine as euben and imeon they shall be mine
nd thy issue which thou begettest after them shall be thine and shall be called after the name of their brethren in their inheritance
nd as for me when  came from adan achel died by me in the land of anaan in the way when yet there was but a little way to come unto phrath and  buried her there in the way of phrath the same is ethlehem
nd srael beheld osephs sons and said ho are these
nd oseph said unto his father hey are my sons whom od hath given me in this place
nd he said ring them  pray thee unto me and  will bless them
ow the eyes of srael were dim for age so that he could not see
nd he brought them near unto him and he kissed them and embraced them
nd srael said unto oseph  had not thought to see thy face and lo od hath shewed me also thy seed
nd oseph brought them out from between his knees and he bowed himself with his face to the earth
nd oseph took them both phraim in his right hand toward sraels left hand and anasseh in his left hand toward sraels right hand and brought them near unto him
nd srael stretched out his right hand and laid it upon phraims head who was the younger and his left hand upon anassehs head guiding his hands wittingly for anasseh was the firstborn
nd he blessed oseph and said od before whom my fathers braham and saac did walk the od which fed me all my life long unto this day the ngel which redeemed me from all evil bless the lads and let my name be named on them and the name of my fathers braham and saac and let them grow into a multitude in the midst of the earth
nd when oseph saw that his father laid his right hand upon the head of phraim it displeased him and he held up his fathers hand to remove it from phraims head unto anassehs head
nd oseph said unto his father ot so my father for this is the firstborn put thy right hand upon his head
nd his father refused and said  know it my son  know it he also shall become a people and he also shall be great but truly his younger brother shall be greater than he and his seed shall become a multitude of nations
nd he blessed them that day saying n thee shall srael bless saying od make thee as phraim and as anasseh and he set phraim before anasseh
nd srael said unto oseph ehold  die but od shall be with you and bring you again unto the land of your fathers
oreover  have given to thee one portion above thy brethren which  took out of the hand of the morite with my sword and with my bow
nd acob called unto his sons and said ather yourselves together that  may tell you that which shall befall you in the last days
ather yourselves together and hear ye sons of acob nd hearken unto srael your father
euben thou art my firstborn my might and the beginning of my strength he excellency of dignity and the excellency of power nstable as water thou shalt not excel ecause thou wentest up to thy fathers bed hen defiledst thou it he went up to my couch

 my soul come not thou into their secret nto their assembly mine honour be not thou united or in their anger they slew a man nd in their selfwill they digged down a wall

udah thou art he whom thy brethren shall praise hy hand shall be in the neck of thine enemies hy fathers children shall bow down before thee
udah is a lions whelp rom the prey my son thou art gone up e stooped down he couched as a lion nd as an old lion who shall rouse him up
he sceptre shall not depart from udah or a lawgiver from between his feet ntil hiloh come nd unto him shall the gathering of the people be
inding his foal unto the vine nd his asss colt unto the choice vine e washed his garments in wine nd his clothes in the blood of grapes is eyes shall be red with wine nd his teeth white with milk

 have waited for thy salvation

ll these are the twelve tribes of srael and this is it that their father spake unto them and blessed them every one according to his blessing he blessed them
nd he charged them and said unto them  am to be gathered unto my people bury me with my fathers in the cave that is in the field of phron the ittite in the cave that is in the field of achpelah which is before amre in the land of anaan which braham bought with the field of phron the ittite for a possession of a buryingplace
here they buried braham and arah his wife there they buried saac and ebekah his wife and there  buried eah
he purchase of the field and of the cave that is therein was from the children of eth
nd when acob had made an end of commanding his sons he gathered up his feet into the bed and yielded up the ghost and was gathered unto his people
nd oseph fell upon his fathers face and wept upon him and kissed him
nd oseph commanded his servants the physicians to embalm his father and the physicians embalmed srael
nd forty days were fulfilled for him for so are fulfilled the days of those which are embalmed and the gyptians mourned for him threescore and ten days
nd when the days of his mourning were past oseph spake unto the house of haraoh saying f now  have found grace in your eyes speak  pray you in the ears of haraoh saying y father made me swear saying o  die in my grave which  have digged for me in the land of anaan there shalt thou bury me
ow therefore let me go up  pray thee and bury my father and  will come again
nd haraoh said o up and bury thy father according as he made thee swear
nd oseph went up to bury his father and with him went up all the servants of haraoh the elders of his house and all the elders of the land of gypt and all the house of oseph and his brethren and his fathers house only their little ones and their flocks and their herds they left in the land of oshen
nd there went up with him both chariots and horsemen and it was a very great company
nd they came to the threshingfloor of tad which is beyond ordan and there they mourned with a great and very sore lamentation and he made a mourning for his father seven days
nd when the inhabitants of the land the anaanites saw the mourning in the floor of tad they said his is a grievous mourning to the gyptians wherefore the name of it was called belmizraim which is beyond ordan
nd his sons did unto him according as he commanded them for his sons carried him into the land of anaan and buried him in the cave of the field of achpelah which braham bought with the field for a possession of a buryingplace of phron the ittite before amre
nd oseph returned into gypt he and his brethren and all that went up with him to bury his father after he had buried his father
nd when osephs brethren saw that their father was dead they said oseph will peradventure hate us and will certainly requite us all the evil which we did unto him
nd they sent a messenger unto oseph saying hy father did command before he died saying o shall ye say unto oseph orgive  pray thee now the trespass of thy brethren and their sin for they did unto thee evil and now we pray thee forgive the trespass of the servants of the od of thy father
nd oseph wept when they spake unto him
nd his brethren also went and fell down before his face and they said ehold we be thy servants
nd oseph said unto them ear not for am  in the place of od
ut as for you ye thought evil against me but od meant it unto good to bring to pass as it is this day to save much people alive
ow therefore fear ye not  will nourish you and your little ones
nd he comforted them and spake kindly unto them
nd oseph dwelt in gypt he and his fathers house and oseph lived an hundred and ten years
nd oseph saw phraims children of the third generation the children also of achir the son of anasseh were brought up upon osephs knees
nd oseph said unto his brethren  die and od will surely visit you and bring you out of this land unto the land which he sware to braham to saac and to acob
nd oseph took an oath of the children of srael saying od will surely visit you and ye shall carry up my bones from hence
o oseph died being an hundred and ten years old and they embalmed him and he was put in a coffin in gypt
ow these are the names of the children of srael which came into gypt every man and his household came with acob
euben imeon evi and udah ssachar ebulun and enjamin an and aphtali ad and sher
nd all the souls that came out of the loins of acob were seventy souls for oseph was in gypt already
nd oseph died and all his brethren and all that generation
nd the children of srael were fruitful and increased abundantly and multiplied and waxed exceeding mighty and the land was filled with them
ow there arose up a new king over gypt which knew not oseph
nd he said unto his people ehold the people of the children of srael are more and mightier than we come on let us deal wisely with them lest they multiply and it come to pass that when there falleth out any war they join also unto our enemies and fight against us and so get them up out of the land
herefore they did set over them taskmasters to afflict them with their burdens
nd they built for haraoh treasure cities ithom and aamses
ut the more they afflicted them the more they multiplied and grew
nd they were grieved because of the children of srael
nd the gyptians made the children of srael to serve with rigour and they made their lives bitter with hard bondage in morter and in brick and in all manner of service in the field all their service wherein they made them serve was with rigour
nd the king of gypt spake to the ebrew midwives of which the name of the one was hiphrah and the name of the other uah and he said hen ye do the office of a midwife to the ebrew women and see them upon the stools if it be a son then ye shall kill him but if it be a daughter then she shall live
ut the midwives feared od and did not as the king of gypt commanded them but saved the men children alive
nd the king of gypt called for the midwives and said unto them hy have ye done this thing and have saved the men children alive
nd the midwives said unto haraoh ecause the ebrew women are not as the gyptian women for they are lively and are delivered ere the midwives come in unto them
herefore od dealt well with the midwives and the people multiplied and waxed very mighty
nd it came to pass because the midwives feared od that he made them houses
nd haraoh charged all his people saying very son that is born ye shall cast into the river and every daughter ye shall save alive
nd there went a man of the house of evi and took to wife a daughter of evi
nd the woman conceived and bare a son and when she saw him that he was a goodly child she hid him three months
nd when she could not longer hide him she took for him an ark of bulrushes and daubed it with slime and with pitch and put the child therein and she laid it in the flags by the rivers brink
nd his sister stood afar off to wit what would be done to him
nd the daughter of haraoh came down to wash herself at the river and her maidens walked along by the rivers side and when she saw the ark among the flags she sent her maid to fetch it
nd when she had opened it she saw the child and behold the babe wept
nd she had compassion on him and said his is one of the ebrews children
hen said his sister to haraohs daughter hall  go and call to thee a nurse of the ebrew women that she may nurse the child for thee
nd haraohs daughter said to her o
nd the maid went and called the childs mother
nd haraohs daughter said unto her ake this child away and nurse it for me and  will give thee thy wages
nd the woman took the child and nursed it
nd the child grew and she brought him unto haraohs daughter and he became her son
nd she called his name oses and she said ecause  drew him out of the water
nd it came to pass in those days when oses was grown that he went out unto his brethren and looked on their burdens and he spied an gyptian smiting an ebrew one of his brethren
nd he looked this way and that way and when he saw that there was no man he slew the gyptian and hid him in the sand
nd when he went out the second day behold two men of the ebrews strove together and he said to him that did the wrong herefore smitest thou thy fellow
nd he said ho made thee a prince and a judge over us intendest thou to kill me as thou killedst the gyptian
nd oses feared and said urely this thing is known
ow when haraoh heard this thing he sought to slay oses
ut oses fled from the face of haraoh and dwelt in the land of idian and he sat down by a well
ow the priest of idian had seven daughters and they came and drew water and filled the troughs to water their fathers flock
nd the shepherds came and drove them away but oses stood up and helped them and watered their flock
nd when they came to euel their father he said ow is it that ye are come so soon to day
nd they said n gyptian delivered us out of the hand of the shepherds and also drew water enough for us and watered the flock
nd he said unto his daughters nd where is he why is it that ye have left the man call him that he may eat bread
nd oses was content to dwell with the man and he gave oses ipporah his daughter
nd she bare him a son and he called his name ershom for he said  have been a stranger in a strange land
nd it came to pass in process of time that the king of gypt died and the children of srael sighed by reason of the bondage and they cried and their cry came up unto od by reason of the bondage
nd od heard their groaning and od remembered his covenant with braham with saac and with acob
nd od looked upon the children of srael and od had respect unto them
ow oses kept the flock of ethro his father in law the priest of idian and he led the flock to the backside of the desert and came to the mountain of od even to oreb
nd the angel of the  appeared unto him in a flame of fire out of the midst of a bush and he looked and behold the bush burned with fire and the bush was not consumed
nd oses said  will now turn aside and see this great sight why the bush is not burnt
nd when the  saw that he turned aside to see od called unto him out of the midst of the bush and said oses oses
nd he said ere am 
nd he said raw not nigh hither put off thy shoes from off thy feet for the place whereon thou standest is holy ground
oreover he said  am the od of thy father the od of braham the od of saac and the od of acob
nd oses hid his face for he was afraid to look upon od
nd the  said  have surely seen the affliction of my people which are in gypt and have heard their cry by reason of their taskmasters for  know their sorrows and  am come down to deliver them out of the hand of the gyptians and to bring them up out of that land unto a good land and a large unto a land flowing with milk and honey unto the place of the anaanites and the ittites and the morites and the erizzites and the ivites and the ebusites
ow therefore behold the cry of the children of srael is come unto me and  have also seen the oppression wherewith the gyptians oppress them
ome now therefore and  will send thee unto haraoh that thou mayest bring forth my people the children of srael out of gypt
nd oses said unto od ho am  that  should go unto haraoh and that  should bring forth the children of srael out of gypt
nd he said ertainly  will be with thee and this shall be a token unto thee that  have sent thee hen thou hast brought forth the people out of gypt ye shall serve od upon this mountain
nd oses said unto od ehold when  come unto the children of srael and shall say unto them he od of your fathers hath sent me unto you and they shall say to me hat is his name what shall  say unto them
nd od said unto oses      and he said hus shalt thou say unto the children of srael   hath sent me unto you
nd od said moreover unto oses hus shalt thou say unto the children of srael he  od of your fathers the od of braham the od of saac and the od of acob hath sent me unto you this is my name for ever and this is my memorial unto all generations
o and gather the elders of srael together and say unto them he  od of your fathers the od of braham of saac and of acob appeared unto me saying  have surely visited you and seen that which is done to you in gypt and  have said  will bring you up out of the affliction of gypt unto the land of the anaanites and the ittites and the morites and the erizzites and the ivites and the ebusites unto a land flowing with milk and honey
nd they shall hearken to thy voice and thou shalt come thou and the elders of srael unto the king of gypt and ye shall say unto him he  od of the ebrews hath met with us and now let us go we beseech thee three days journey into the wilderness that we may sacrifice to the  our od
nd  am sure that the king of gypt will not let you go no not by a mighty hand
nd  will stretch out my hand and smite gypt with all my wonders which  will do in the midst thereof and after that he will let you go
nd  will give this people favour in the sight of the gyptians and it shall come to pass that when ye go ye shall not go empty but every woman shall borrow of her neighbour and of her that sojourneth in her house jewels of silver and jewels of gold and raiment and ye shall put them upon your sons and upon your daughters and ye shall spoil the gyptians
nd oses answered and said ut behold they will not believe me nor hearken unto my voice for they will say he  hath not appeared unto thee

nd he said ast it on the ground

our lamb shall be without blemish a male of the first year ye shall take it out from the sheep or from the goats and ye shall keep it up until the fourteenth day of the same month and the whole assembly of the congregation of srael shall kill it in the evening

nd in the first day there shall be an holy convocation and in the seventh day there shall be an holy convocation to you no manner of work shall be done in them save that which every man must eat that only may be done of you

nd if the servant shall plainly say  love my master my wife and my children  will not go out free then his master shall bring him unto the judges he shall also bring him to the door or unto the door post and his master shall bore his ear through with an aul and he shall serve him for ever
nd if a man sell his daughter to be a maidservant she shall not go out as the menservants do
f she please not her master who hath betrothed her to himself then shall he let her be redeemed to sell her unto a strange nation he shall have no power seeing he hath dealt deceitfully with her
nd if he have betrothed her unto his son he shall deal with her after the manner of daughters
f he take him another wife her food her raiment and her duty of marriage shall he not diminish
nd if he do not these three unto her then shall she go out free without money
e that smiteth a man so that he die shall be surely put to death
nd if a man lie not in wait but od deliver him into his hand then  will appoint thee a place whither he shall flee
ut if a man come presumptuously upon his neighbour to slay him with guile thou shalt take him from mine altar that he may die
nd he that smiteth his father or his mother shall be surely put to death
nd he that stealeth a man and selleth him or if he be found in his hand he shall surely be put to death
nd he that curseth his father or his mother shall surely be put to death
nd if men strive together and one smite another with a stone or with his fist and he die not but keepeth his bed if he rise again and walk abroad upon his staff then shall he that smote him be quit only he shall pay for the loss of his time and shall cause him to be thoroughly healed
nd if a man smite his servant or his maid with a rod and he die under his hand he shall be surely punished
otwithstanding if he continue a day or two he shall not be punished for he is his money
f men strive and hurt a woman with child so that her fruit depart from her and yet no mischief follow he shall be surely punished according as the womans husband will lay upon him and he shall pay as the judges determine
nd if any mischief follow then thou shalt give life for life eye for eye tooth for tooth hand for hand foot for foot burning for burning wound for wound stripe for stripe
nd if a man smite the eye of his servant or the eye of his maid that it perish he shall let him go free for his eyes sake
nd if he smite out his manservants tooth or his maidservants tooth he shall let him go free for his tooths sake
f an ox gore a man or a woman that they die then the ox shall be surely stoned and his flesh shall not be eaten but the owner of the ox shall be quit
ut if the ox were wont to push with his horn in time past and it hath been testified to his owner and he hath not kept him in but that he hath killed a man or a woman the ox shall be stoned and his owner also shall be put to death
f there be laid on him a sum of money then he shall give for the ransom of his life whatsoever is laid upon him
hether he have gored a son or have gored a daughter according to this judgment shall it be done unto him
f the ox shall push a manservant or a maidservant he shall give unto their master thirty shekels of silver and the ox shall be stoned
nd if a man shall open a pit or if a man shall dig a pit and not cover it and an ox or an ass fall therein the owner of the pit shall make it good and give money unto the owner of them and the dead beast shall be his
nd if one mans ox hurt anothers that he die then they shall sell the live ox and divide the money of it and the dead ox also they shall divide
r if it be known that the ox hath used to push in time past and his owner hath not kept him in he shall surely pay ox for ox and the dead shall be his own
f a man shall steal an ox or a sheep and kill it or sell it he shall restore five oxen for an ox and four sheep for a sheep
f a thief be found breaking up and be smitten that he die there shall no blood be shed for him
f the sun be risen upon him there shall be blood shed for him for he should make full restitution if he have nothing then he shall be sold for his theft
f the theft be certainly found in his hand alive whether it be ox or ass or sheep he shall restore double
f a man shall cause a field or vineyard to be eaten and shall put in his beast and shall feed in another mans field of the best of his own field and of the best of his own vineyard shall he make restitution
f fire break out and catch in thorns so that the stacks of corn or the standing corn or the field be consumed therewith he that kindled the fire shall surely make restitution
f a man shall deliver unto his neighbour money or stuff to keep and it be stolen out of the mans house if the thief be found let him pay double
f the thief be not found then the master of the house shall be brought unto the judges to see whether he have put his hand unto his neighbours goods
or all manner of trespass whether it be for ox for ass for sheep for raiment or for any manner of lost thing which another challengeth to be his the cause of both parties shall come before the judges and whom the judges shall condemn he shall pay double unto his neighbour
f a man deliver unto his neighbour an ass or an ox or a sheep or any beast to keep and it die or be hurt or driven away no man seeing it then shall an oath of the  be between them both that he hath not put his hand unto his neighbours goods and the owner of it shall accept thereof and he shall not make it good
nd if it be stolen from him he shall make restitution unto the owner thereof
f it be torn in pieces then let him bring it for witness and he shall not make good that which was torn
nd if a man borrow ought of his neighbour and it be hurt or die the owner thereof being not with it he shall surely make it good
ut if the owner thereof be with it he shall not make it good if it be an hired thing it came for his hire
nd if a man entice a maid that is not betrothed and lie with her he shall surely endow her to be his wife
f her father utterly refuse to give her unto him he shall pay money according to the dowry of virgins
hou shalt not suffer a witch to live
hosoever lieth with a beast shall surely be put to death
e that sacrificeth unto any god save unto the  only he shall be utterly destroyed
hou shalt neither vex a stranger nor oppress him for ye were strangers in the land of gypt
e shall not afflict any widow or fatherless child
f thou afflict them in any wise and they cry at all unto me  will surely hear their cry and my wrath shall wax hot and  will kill you with the sword and your wives shall be widows and your children fatherless
f thou lend money to any of my people that is poor by thee thou shalt not be to him as an usurer neither shalt thou lay upon him usury
f thou at all take thy neighbours raiment to pledge thou shalt deliver it unto him by that the sun goeth down for that is his covering only it is his raiment for his skin wherein shall he sleep and it shall come to pass when he crieth unto me that  will hear for  am gracious
hou shalt not revile the gods nor curse the ruler of thy people
hou shalt not delay to offer the first of thy ripe fruits and of thy liquors the firstborn of thy sons shalt thou give unto me
ikewise shalt thou do with thine oxen and with thy sheep seven days it shall be with his dam on the eighth day thou shalt give it me
nd ye shall be holy men unto me neither shall ye eat any flesh that is torn of beasts in the field ye shall cast it to the dogs
hou shalt not raise a false report put not thine hand with the wicked to be an unrighteous witness
hou shalt not follow a multitude to do evil neither shalt thou speak in a cause to decline after many to wrest judgment neither shalt thou countenance a poor man in his cause
f thou meet thine enemys ox or his ass going astray thou shalt surely bring it back to him again
f thou see the ass of him that hateth thee lying under his burden and wouldest forbear to help him thou shalt surely help with him
hou shalt not wrest the judgment of thy poor in his cause
eep thee far from a false matter and the innocent and righteous slay thou not for  will not justify the wicked
nd thou shalt take no gift for the gift blindeth the wise and perverteth the words of the righteous
lso thou shalt not oppress a stranger for ye know the heart of a stranger seeing ye were strangers in the land of gypt
nd six years thou shalt sow thy land and shalt gather in the fruits thereof but the seventh year thou shalt let it rest and lie still that the poor of thy people may eat and what they leave the beasts of the field shall eat
n like manner thou shalt deal with thy vineyard and with thy oliveyard
ix days thou shalt do thy work and on the seventh day thou shalt rest that thine ox and thine ass may rest and the son of thy handmaid and the stranger may be refreshed
nd in all things that  have said unto you be circumspect and make no mention of the name of other gods neither let it be heard out of thy mouth
hree times thou shalt keep a feast unto me in the year
hou shalt keep the feast of unleavened bread thou shalt eat unleavened bread seven days as  commanded thee in the time appointed of the month bib for in it thou camest out from gypt and none shall appear before me empty and the feast of harvest the firstfruits of thy labours which thou hast sown in the field and the feast of ingathering which is in the end of the year when thou hast gathered in thy labours out of the field
hree times in the year all thy males shall appear before the ord 
hou shalt not offer the blood of my sacrifice with leavened bread neither shall the fat of my sacrifice remain until the morning
he first of the firstfruits of thy land thou shalt bring into the house of the  thy od
hou shalt not seethe a kid in his mothers milk
ehold  send an ngel before thee to keep thee in the way and to bring thee into the place which  have prepared
eware of him and obey his voice provoke him not for he will not pardon your transgressions for my name is in him
ut if thou shalt indeed obey his voice and do all that  speak then  will be an enemy unto thine enemies and an adversary unto thine adversaries
or mine ngel shall go before thee and bring thee in unto the morites and the ittites and the erizzites and the anaanites the ivites and the ebusites and  will cut them off
hou shalt not bow down to their gods nor serve them nor do after their works but thou shalt utterly overthrow them and quite break down their images
nd ye shall serve the  your od and he shall bless thy bread and thy water and  will take sickness away from the midst of thee
here shall nothing cast their young nor be barren in thy land the number of thy days  will fulfil
 will send my fear before thee and will destroy all the people to whom thou shalt come and  will make all thine enemies turn their backs unto thee
nd  will send hornets before thee which shall drive out the ivite the anaanite and the ittite from before thee
 will not drive them out from before thee in one year lest the land become desolate and the beast of the field multiply against thee
y little and little  will drive them out from before thee until thou be increased and inherit the land
nd  will set thy bounds from the ed sea even unto the sea of the hilistines and from the desert unto the river for  will deliver the inhabitants of the land into your hand and thou shalt drive them out before thee
hou shalt make no covenant with them nor with their gods
hey shall not dwell in thy land lest they make thee sin against me for if thou serve their gods it will surely be a snare unto thee
nd he said unto oses ome up unto the  thou and aron adab and bihu and seventy of the elders of srael and worship ye afar off
nd oses alone shall come near the  but they shall not come nigh neither shall the people go up with him
nd oses came and told the people all the words of the  and all the judgments and all the people answered with one voice and said ll the words which the  hath said will we do
nd oses wrote all the words of the  and rose up early in the morning and builded an altar under the hill and twelve pillars according to the twelve tribes of srael
nd he sent young men of the children of srael which offered burnt offerings and sacrificed peace offerings of oxen unto the 
nd oses took half of the blood and put it in basons and half of the blood he sprinkled on the altar
nd he took the book of the covenant and read in the audience of the people and they said ll that the  hath said will we do and be obedient
nd oses took the blood and sprinkled it on the people and said ehold the blood of the covenant which the  hath made with you concerning all these words
hen went up oses and aron adab and bihu and seventy of the elders of srael and they saw the od of srael and there was under his feet as it were a paved work of a sapphire stone and as it were the body of heaven in his clearness
nd upon the nobles of the children of srael he laid not his hand also they saw od and did eat and drink
nd the  said unto oses ome up to me into the mount and be there and  will give thee tables of stone and a law and commandments which  have written that thou mayest teach them
nd oses rose up and his minister oshua and oses went up into the mount of od
nd he said unto the elders arry ye here for us until we come again unto you and behold aron and ur are with you if any man have any matters to do let him come unto them
nd oses went up into the mount and a cloud covered the mount
nd the glory of the  abode upon mount inai and the cloud covered it six days and the seventh day he called unto oses out of the midst of the cloud
nd the sight of the glory of the  was like devouring fire on the top of the mount in the eyes of the children of srael
nd oses went into the midst of the cloud and gat him up into the mount and oses was in the mount forty days and forty nights
nd the  spake unto oses saying peak unto the children of srael that they bring me an offering of every man that giveth it willingly with his heart ye shall take my offering
nd this is the offering which ye shall take of them gold and silver and brass and blue and purple and scarlet and fine linen and goats hair and rams skins dyed red and badgers skins and shittim wood oil for the light spices for anointing oil and for sweet incense onyx stones and stones to be set in the ephod and in the breastplate
nd let them make me a sanctuary that  may dwell among them
ccording to all that  shew thee after the pattern of the tabernacle and the pattern of all the instruments thereof even so shall ye make it
nd they shall make an ark of shittim wood two cubits and a half shall be the length thereof and a cubit and a half the breadth thereof and a cubit and a half the height thereof
nd thou shalt overlay it with pure gold within and without shalt thou overlay it and shalt make upon it a crown of gold round about
nd thou shalt cast four rings of gold for it and put them in the four corners thereof and two rings shall be in the one side of it and two rings in the other side of it
nd thou shalt make staves of shittim wood and overlay them with gold
nd thou shalt put the staves into the rings by the sides of the ark that the ark may be borne with them
he staves shall be in the rings of the ark they shall not be taken from it
nd thou shalt put into the ark the testimony which  shall give thee
nd thou shalt make a mercy seat of pure gold two cubits and a half shall be the length thereof and a cubit and a half the breadth thereof
nd thou shalt make two cherubims of gold of beaten work shalt thou make them in the two ends of the mercy seat
nd make one cherub on the one end and the other cherub on the other end even of the mercy seat shall ye make the cherubims on the two ends thereof
nd the cherubims shall stretch forth their wings on high covering the mercy seat with their wings and their faces shall look one to another toward the mercy seat shall the faces of the cherubims be
nd thou shalt put the mercy seat above upon the ark and in the ark thou shalt put the testimony that  shall give thee
nd there  will meet with thee and  will commune with thee from above the mercy seat from between the two cherubims which are upon the ark of the testimony of all things which  will give thee in commandment unto the children of srael
hou shalt also make a table of shittim wood two cubits shall be the length thereof and a cubit the breadth thereof and a cubit and a half the height thereof
nd thou shalt overlay it with pure gold and make thereto a crown of gold round about
nd thou shalt make unto it a border of an hand breadth round about and thou shalt make a golden crown to the border thereof round about
nd thou shalt make for it four rings of gold and put the rings in the four corners that are on the four feet thereof
ver against the border shall the rings be for places of the staves to bear the table
nd thou shalt make the staves of shittim wood and overlay them with gold that the table may be borne with them
nd thou shalt make the dishes thereof and spoons thereof and covers thereof and bowls thereof to cover withal of pure gold shalt thou make them
nd thou shalt set upon the table shewbread before me alway
nd thou shalt make a candlestick of pure gold of beaten work shall the candlestick be made his shaft and his branches his bowls his knops and his flowers shall be of the same
nd six branches shall come out of the sides of it three branches of the candlestick out of the one side and three branches of the candlestick out of the other side three bowls made like unto almonds with a knop and a flower in one branch and three bowls made like almonds in the other branch with a knop and a flower so in the six branches that come out of the candlestick
nd in the candlestick shall be four bowls made like unto almonds with their knops and their flowers
nd there shall be a knop under two branches of the same and a knop under two branches of the same and a knop under two branches of the same according to the six branches that proceed out of the candlestick
heir knops and their branches shall be of the same all it shall be one beaten work of pure gold
nd thou shalt make the seven lamps thereof and they shall light the lamps thereof that they may give light over against it
nd the tongs thereof and the snuffdishes thereof shall be of pure gold
f a talent of pure gold shall he make it with all these vessels
nd look that thou make them after their pattern which was shewed thee in the mount
oreover thou shalt make the tabernacle with ten curtains of fine twined linen and blue and purple and scarlet with cherubims of cunning work shalt thou make them
he length of one curtain shall be eight and twenty cubits and the breadth of one curtain four cubits and every one of the curtains shall have one measure
he five curtains shall be coupled together one to another and other five curtains shall be coupled one to another
nd thou shalt make loops of blue upon the edge of the one curtain from the selvedge in the coupling and likewise shalt thou make in the uttermost edge of another curtain in the coupling of the second
ifty loops shalt thou make in the one curtain and fifty loops shalt thou make in the edge of the curtain that is in the coupling of the second that the loops may take hold one of another
nd thou shalt make fifty taches of gold and couple the curtains together with the taches and it shall be one tabernacle
nd thou shalt make curtains of goats hair to be a covering upon the tabernacle eleven curtains shalt thou make
he length of one curtain shall be thirty cubits and the breadth of one curtain four cubits and the eleven curtains shall be all of one measure
nd thou shalt couple five curtains by themselves and six curtains by themselves and shalt double the sixth curtain in the forefront of the tabernacle
nd thou shalt make fifty loops on the edge of the one curtain that is outmost in the coupling and fifty loops in the edge of the curtain which coupleth the second
nd thou shalt make fifty taches of brass and put the taches into the loops and couple the tent together that it may be one
nd the remnant that remaineth of the curtains of the tent the half curtain that remaineth shall hang over the backside of the tabernacle
nd a cubit on the one side and a cubit on the other side of that which remaineth in the length of the curtains of the tent it shall hang over the sides of the tabernacle on this side and on that side to cover it
nd thou shalt make a covering for the tent of rams skins dyed red and a covering above of badgers skins
nd thou shalt make boards for the tabernacle of shittim wood standing up
en cubits shall be the length of a board and a cubit and a half shall be the breadth of one board
wo tenons shall there be in one board set in order one against another thus shalt thou make for all the boards of the tabernacle
nd thou shalt make the boards for the tabernacle twenty boards on the south side southward
nd thou shalt make forty sockets of silver under the twenty boards two sockets under one board for his two tenons and two sockets under another board for his two tenons
nd for the second side of the tabernacle on the north side there shall be twenty boards and their forty sockets of silver two sockets under one board and two sockets under another board
nd for the sides of the tabernacle westward thou shalt make six boards
nd two boards shalt thou make for the corners of the tabernacle in the two sides
nd they shall be coupled together beneath and they shall be coupled together above the head of it unto one ring thus shall it be for them both they shall be for the two corners
nd they shall be eight boards and their sockets of silver sixteen sockets two sockets under one board and two sockets under another board
nd thou shalt make bars of shittim wood five for the boards of the one side of the tabernacle and five bars for the boards of the other side of the tabernacle and five bars for the boards of the side of the tabernacle for the two sides westward
nd the middle bar in the midst of the boards shall reach from end to end
nd thou shalt overlay the boards with gold and make their rings of gold for places for the bars and thou shalt overlay the bars with gold

nd thou shalt make a vail of blue and purple and scarlet and fine twined linen of cunning work with cherubims shall it be made and thou shalt hang it upon four pillars of shittim wood overlaid with gold their hooks shall be of gold upon the four sockets of silver

nd thou shalt take of the blood of the bullock and put it upon the horns of the altar with thy finger and pour all the blood beside the bottom of the altar
nd thou shalt take all the fat that covereth the inwards and the caul that is above the liver and the two kidneys and the fat that is upon them and burn them upon the altar
ut the flesh of the bullock and his skin and his dung shalt thou burn with fire without the camp it is a sin offering
hou shalt also take one ram and aron and his sons shall put their hands upon the head of the ram
nd thou shalt slay the ram and thou shalt take his blood and sprinkle it round about upon the altar
nd thou shalt cut the ram in pieces and wash the inwards of him and his legs and put them unto his pieces and unto his head
nd thou shalt burn the whole ram upon the altar it is a burnt offering unto the  it is a sweet savour an offering made by fire unto the 
nd thou shalt take the other ram and aron and his sons shall put their hands upon the head of the ram
hen shalt thou kill the ram and take of his blood and put it upon the tip of the right ear of aron and upon the tip of the right ear of his sons and upon the thumb of their right hand and upon the great toe of their right foot and sprinkle the blood upon the altar round about
nd thou shalt take of the blood that is upon the altar and of the anointing oil and sprinkle it upon aron and upon his garments and upon his sons and upon the garments of his sons with him and he shall be hallowed and his garments and his sons and his sons garments with him
lso thou shalt take of the ram the fat and the rump and the fat that covereth the inwards and the caul above the liver and the two kidneys and the fat that is upon them and the right shoulder for it is a ram of consecration and one loaf of bread and one cake of oiled bread and one wafer out of the basket of the unleavened bread that is before the  and thou shalt put all in the hands of aron and in the hands of his sons and shalt wave them for a wave offering before the 
nd thou shalt receive them of their hands and burn them upon the altar for a burnt offering for a sweet savour before the  it is an offering made by fire unto the 
nd thou shalt take the breast of the ram of arons consecration and wave it for a wave offering before the  and it shall be thy part
nd thou shalt sanctify the breast of the wave offering and the shoulder of the heave offering which is waved and which is heaved up of the ram of the consecration even of that which is for aron and of that which is for his sons and it shall be arons and his sons by a statute for ever from the children of srael for it is an heave offering and it shall be an heave offering from the children of srael of the sacrifice of their peace offerings even their heave offering unto the 
nd the holy garments of aron shall be his sons after him to be anointed therein and to be consecrated in them
nd that son that is priest in his stead shall put them on seven days when he cometh into the tabernacle of the congregation to minister in the holy place
nd thou shalt take the ram of the consecration and seethe his flesh in the holy place
nd aron and his sons shall eat the flesh of the ram and the bread that is in the basket by the door of the tabernacle of the congregation
nd they shall eat those things wherewith the atonement was made to consecrate and to sanctify them but a stranger shall not eat thereof because they are holy
nd if ought of the flesh of the consecrations or of the bread remain unto the morning then thou shalt burn the remainder with fire it shall not be eaten because it is holy
nd thus shalt thou do unto aron and to his sons according to all things which  have commanded thee seven days shalt thou consecrate them

even days thou shalt make an atonement for the altar and sanctify it and it shall be an altar most holy whatsoever toucheth the altar shall be holy
ow this is that which thou shalt offer upon the altar two lambs of the first year day by day continually

nd the  said unto oses ake unto thee sweet spices stacte and onycha and galbanum these sweet spices with pure frankincense of each shall there be a like weight and thou shalt make it a perfume a confection after the art of the apothecary tempered together pure and holy and thou shalt beat some of it very small and put of it before the testimony in the tabernacle of the congregation where  will meet with thee it shall be unto you most holy

nd every wise hearted man among them that wrought the work of the tabernacle made ten curtains of fine twined linen and blue and purple and scarlet with cherubims of cunning work made he them
he length of one curtain was twenty and eight cubits and the breadth of one curtain four cubits the curtains were all of one size
nd he coupled the five curtains one unto another and the other five curtains he coupled one unto another
nd he made loops of blue on the edge of one curtain from the selvedge in the coupling likewise he made in the uttermost side of another curtain in the coupling of the second

nd he made fifty taches of gold and coupled the curtains one unto another with the taches so it became one tabernacle
nd he made curtains of goats hair for the tent over the tabernacle eleven curtains he made them

nd he made boards for the tabernacle twenty boards for the south side southward and forty sockets of silver he made under the twenty boards two sockets under one board for his two tenons and two sockets under another board for his two tenons

nd he made bars of shittim wood five for the boards of the one side of the tabernacle and five bars for the boards of the other side of the tabernacle and five bars for the boards of the tabernacle for the sides westward
nd he made the middle bar to shoot through the boards from the one end to the other
nd he overlaid the boards with gold and made their rings of gold to be places for the bars and overlaid the bars with gold
nd he made a vail of blue and purple and scarlet and fine twined linen with cherubims made he it of cunning work
nd he made thereunto four pillars of shittim wood and overlaid them with gold their hooks were of gold and he cast for them four sockets of silver
nd he made an hanging for the tabernacle door of blue and purple and scarlet and fine twined linen of needlework and the five pillars of it with their hooks and he overlaid their chapiters and their fillets with gold but their five sockets were of brass
nd ezaleel made the ark of shittim wood two cubits and a half was the length of it and a cubit and a half the breadth of it and a cubit and a half the height of it and he overlaid it with pure gold within and without and made a crown of gold to it round about
nd he cast for it four rings of gold to be set by the four corners of it even two rings upon the one side of it and two rings upon the other side of it
nd he made staves of shittim wood and overlaid them with gold
nd he put the staves into the rings by the sides of the ark to bear the ark
nd he made the mercy seat of pure gold two cubits and a half was the length thereof and one cubit and a half the breadth thereof
nd he made two cherubims of gold beaten out of one piece made he them on the two ends of the mercy seat one cherub on the end on this side and another cherub on the other end on that side out of the mercy seat made he the cherubims on the two ends thereof
nd the cherubims spread out their wings on high and covered with their wings over the mercy seat with their faces one to another even to the mercy seatward were the faces of the cherubims
nd he made the table of shittim wood two cubits was the length thereof and a cubit the breadth thereof and a cubit and a half the height thereof and he overlaid it with pure gold and made thereunto a crown of gold round about
lso he made thereunto a border of an handbreadth round about and made a crown of gold for the border thereof round about
nd he cast for it four rings of gold and put the rings upon the four corners that were in the four feet thereof
ver against the border were the rings the places for the staves to bear the table
nd he made the staves of shittim wood and overlaid them with gold to bear the table
nd he made the vessels which were upon the table his dishes and his spoons and his bowls and his covers to cover withal of pure gold
nd he made the candlestick of pure gold of beaten work made he the candlestick his shaft and his branch his bowls his knops and his flowers were of the same and six branches going out of the sides thereof three branches of the candlestick out of the one side thereof and three branches of the candlestick out of the other side thereof three bowls made after the fashion of almonds in one branch a knop and a flower and three bowls made like almonds in another branch a knop and a flower so throughout the six branches going out of the candlestick
nd in the candlestick were four bowls made like almonds his knops and his flowers and a knop under two branches of the same and a knop under two branches of the same and a knop under two branches of the same according to the six branches going out of it
heir knops and their branches were of the same all of it was one beaten work of pure gold
nd he made his seven lamps and his snuffers and his snuffdishes of pure gold
f a talent of pure gold made he it and all the vessels thereof
nd he made the incense altar of shittim wood the length of it was a cubit and the breadth of it a cubit it was foursquare and two cubits was the height of it the horns thereof were of the same
nd he overlaid it with pure gold both the top of it and the sides thereof round about and the horns of it also he made unto it a crown of gold round about
nd he made two rings of gold for it under the crown thereof by the two corners of it upon the two sides thereof to be places for the staves to bear it withal
nd he made the staves of shittim wood and overlaid them with gold
nd he made the holy anointing oil and the pure incense of sweet spices according to the work of the apothecary
nd he made the altar of burnt offering of shittim wood five cubits was the length thereof and five cubits the breadth thereof it was foursquare and three cubits the height thereof
nd he made the horns thereof on the four corners of it the horns thereof were of the same and he overlaid it with brass
nd he made all the vessels of the altar the pots and the shovels and the basons and the fleshhooks and the firepans all the vessels thereof made he of brass
nd he made for the altar a brasen grate of network under the compass thereof beneath unto the midst of it
nd he cast four rings for the four ends of the grate of brass to be places for the staves
nd he made the staves of shittim wood and overlaid them with brass
nd he put the staves into the rings on the sides of the altar to bear it withal he made the altar hollow with boards
nd he made the laver of brass and the foot of it of brass of the lookingglasses of the women assembling which assembled at the door of the tabernacle of the congregation
nd he made the court on the south side southward the hangings of the court were of fine twined linen an hundred cubits their pillars were twenty and their brasen sockets twenty the hooks of the pillars and their fillets were of silver
nd for the north side the hangings were an hundred cubits their pillars were twenty and their sockets of brass twenty the hooks of the pillars and their fillets of silver
nd for the west side were hangings of fifty cubits their pillars ten and their sockets ten the hooks of the pillars and their fillets of silver
nd for the east side eastward fifty cubits
he hangings of the one side of the gate were fifteen cubits their pillars three and their sockets three
nd for the other side of the court gate on this hand and that hand were hangings of fifteen cubits their pillars three and their sockets three
ll the hangings of the court round about were of fine twined linen
nd the sockets for the pillars were of brass the hooks of the pillars and their fillets of silver and the overlaying of their chapiters of silver and all the pillars of the court were filleted with silver
nd the hanging for the gate of the court was needlework of blue and purple and scarlet and fine twined linen and twenty cubits was the length and the height in the breadth was five cubits answerable to the hangings of the court
nd their pillars were four and their sockets of brass four their hooks of silver and the overlaying of their chapiters and their fillets of silver
nd all the pins of the tabernacle and of the court round about were of brass
his is the sum of the tabernacle even of the tabernacle of testimony as it was counted according to the commandment of oses for the service of the evites by the hand of thamar son to aron the priest
nd ezaleel the son of ri the son of ur of the tribe of udah made all that the  commanded oses
nd with him was holiab son of hisamach of the tribe of an an engraver and a cunning workman and an embroiderer in blue and in purple and in scarlet and fine linen
ll the gold that was occupied for the work in all the work of the holy place even the gold of the offering was twenty and nine talents and seven hundred and thirty shekels after the shekel of the sanctuary
nd the silver of them that were numbered of the congregation was an hundred talents and a thousand seven hundred and threescore and fifteen shekels after the shekel of the sanctuary a bekah for every man that is half a shekel after the shekel of the sanctuary for every one that went to be numbered from twenty years old and upward for six hundred thousand and three thousand and five hundred and fifty men
nd of the hundred talents of silver were cast the sockets of the sanctuary and the sockets of the vail an hundred sockets of the hundred talents a talent for a socket
nd of the thousand seven hundred seventy and five shekels he made hooks for the pillars and overlaid their chapiters and filleted them
nd the brass of the offering was seventy talents and two thousand and four hundred shekels
nd therewith he made the sockets to the door of the tabernacle of the congregation and the brasen altar and the brasen grate for it and all the vessels of the altar and the sockets of the court round about and the sockets of the court gate and all the pins of the tabernacle and all the pins of the court round about
nd of the blue and purple and scarlet they made cloths of service to do service in the holy place and made the holy garments for aron as the  commanded oses
nd he made the ephod of gold blue and purple and scarlet and fine twined linen
nd they did beat the gold into thin plates and cut it into wires to work it in the blue and in the purple and in the scarlet and in the fine linen with cunning work
hey made shoulderpieces for it to couple it together by the two edges was it coupled together
nd the curious girdle of his ephod that was upon it was of the same according to the work thereof of gold blue and purple and scarlet and fine twined linen as the  commanded oses
nd they wrought onyx stones inclosed in ouches of gold graven as signets are graven with the names of the children of srael
nd he put them on the shoulders of the ephod that they should be stones for a memorial to the children of srael as the  commanded oses
nd he made the breastplate of cunning work like the work of the ephod of gold blue and purple and scarlet and fine twined linen
t was foursquare they made the breastplate double a span was the length thereof and a span the breadth thereof being doubled
nd they set in it four rows of stones the first row was a sardius a topaz and a carbuncle this was the first row
nd the second row an emerald a sapphire and a diamond
nd the third row a ligure an agate and an amethyst
nd the fourth row a beryl an onyx and a jasper they were inclosed in ouches of gold in their inclosings
nd the stones were according to the names of the children of srael twelve according to their names like the engravings of a signet every one with his name according to the twelve tribes
nd they made upon the breastplate chains at the ends of wreathen work of pure gold
nd they made two ouches of gold and two gold rings and put the two rings in the two ends of the breastplate
nd they put the two wreathen chains of gold in the two rings on the ends of the breastplate
nd the two ends of the two wreathen chains they fastened in the two ouches and put them on the shoulderpieces of the ephod before it
nd they made two rings of gold and put them on the two ends of the breastplate upon the border of it which was on the side of the ephod inward
nd they made two other golden rings and put them on the two sides of the ephod underneath toward the forepart of it over against the other coupling thereof above the curious girdle of the ephod
nd they did bind the breastplate by his rings unto the rings of the ephod with a lace of blue that it might be above the curious girdle of the ephod and that the breastplate might not be loosed from the ephod as the  commanded oses
nd he made the robe of the ephod of woven work all of blue
nd there was an hole in the midst of the robe as the hole of an habergeon with a band round about the hole that it should not rend
nd they made upon the hems of the robe pomegranates of blue and purple and scarlet and twined linen
nd they made bells of pure gold and put the bells between the pomegranates upon the hem of the robe round about between the pomegranates a bell and a pomegranate a bell and a pomegranate round about the hem of the robe to minister in as the  commanded oses
nd they made coats of fine linen of woven work for aron and for his sons and a mitre of fine linen and goodly bonnets of fine linen and linen breeches of fine twined linen and a girdle of fine twined linen and blue and purple and scarlet of needlework as the  commanded oses
nd they made the plate of the holy crown of pure gold and wrote upon it a writing like to the engravings of a signet    ord

hus was all the work of the tabernacle of the tent of the congregation finished and the children of srael did according to all that the  commanded oses so did they
nd they brought the tabernacle unto oses the tent and all his furniture his taches his boards his bars and his pillars and his sockets and the covering of rams skins dyed red and the covering of badgers skins and the vail of the covering the ark of the testimony and the staves thereof and the mercy seat the table and all the vessels thereof and the shewbread the pure candlestick with the lamps thereof even with the lamps to be set in order and all the vessels thereof and the oil for light and the golden altar and the anointing oil and the sweet incense and the hanging for the tabernacle door the brasen altar and his grate of brass his staves and all his vessels the laver and his foot the hangings of the court his pillars and his sockets and the hanging for the court gate his cords and his pins and all the vessels of the service of the tabernacle for the tent of the congregation the cloths of service to do service in the holy place and the holy garments for aron the priest and his sons garments to minister in the priests office
ccording to all that the  commanded oses so the children of srael made all the work
nd oses did look upon all the work and behold they had done it as the  had commanded even so had they done it and oses blessed them
nd the  spake unto oses saying n the first day of the first month shalt thou set up the tabernacle of the tent of the congregation
nd thou shalt put therein the ark of the testimony and cover the ark with the vail
nd thou shalt bring in the table and set in order the things that are to be set in order upon it and thou shalt bring in the candlestick and light the lamps thereof
nd thou shalt set the altar of gold for the incense before the ark of the testimony and put the hanging of the door to the tabernacle
nd thou shalt set the altar of the burnt offering before the door of the tabernacle of the tent of the congregation
nd thou shalt set the laver between the tent of the congregation and the altar and shalt put water therein
nd thou shalt set up the court round about and hang up the hanging at the court gate
nd thou shalt take the anointing oil and anoint the tabernacle and all that is therein and shalt hallow it and all the vessels thereof and it shall be holy
nd thou shalt anoint the altar of the burnt offering and all his vessels and sanctify the altar and it shall be an altar most holy
nd thou shalt anoint the laver and his foot and sanctify it
nd thou shalt bring aron and his sons unto the door of the tabernacle of the congregation and wash them with water
nd thou shalt put upon aron the holy garments and anoint him and sanctify him that he may minister unto me in the priests office
nd thou shalt bring his sons and clothe them with coats and thou shalt anoint them as thou didst anoint their father that they may minister unto me in the priests office for their anointing shall surely be an everlasting priesthood throughout their generations
hus did oses according to all that the  commanded him so did he
nd it came to pass in the first month in the second year on the first day of the month that the tabernacle was reared up
nd oses reared up the tabernacle and fastened his sockets and set up the boards thereof and put in the bars thereof and reared up his pillars
nd he spread abroad the tent over the tabernacle and put the covering of the tent above upon it as the  commanded oses
nd he took and put the testimony into the ark and set the staves on the ark and put the mercy seat above upon the ark and he brought the ark into the tabernacle and set up the vail of the covering and covered the ark of the testimony as the  commanded oses
nd he put the table in the tent of the congregation upon the side of the tabernacle northward without the vail

nd he put the golden altar in the tent of the congregation before the vail and he burnt sweet incense thereon as the  commanded oses
nd he set up the hanging at the door of the tabernacle
nd he put the altar of burnt offering by the door of the tabernacle of the tent of the congregation and offered upon it the burnt offering and the meat offering as the  commanded oses
nd he set the laver between the tent of the congregation and the altar and put water there to wash withal
nd oses and aron and his sons washed their hands and their feet thereat when they went into the tent of the congregation and when they came near unto the altar they washed as the  commanded oses
nd he reared up the court round about the tabernacle and the altar and set up the hanging of the court gate
o oses finished the work
hen a cloud covered the tent of the congregation and the glory of the  filled the tabernacle
nd oses was not able to enter into the tent of the congregation because the cloud abode thereon and the glory of the  filled the tabernacle
nd when the cloud was taken up from over the tabernacle the children of srael went onward in all their journeys but if the cloud were not taken up then they journeyed not till the day that it was taken up

nd if the burnt sacrifice for his offering to the  be of fowls then he shall bring his offering of turtledoves or of young pigeons

nd when any will offer a meat offering unto the  his offering shall be of fine flour and he shall pour oil upon it and put frankincense thereon and he shall bring it to arons sons the priests and he shall take thereout his handful of the flour thereof and of the oil thereof with all the frankincense thereof and the priest shall burn the memorial of it upon the altar to be an offering made by fire of a sweet savour unto the  and the remnant of the meat offering shall be arons and his sons it is a thing most holy of the offerings of the  made by fire
nd if thou bring an oblation of a meat offering baken in the oven it shall be unleavened cakes of fine flour mingled with oil or unleavened wafers anointed with oil
nd if thy oblation be a meat offering baken in a pan it shall be of fine flour unleavened mingled with oil
hou shalt part it in pieces and pour oil thereon it is a meat offering
nd if thy oblation be a meat offering baken in the fryingpan it shall be made of fine flour with oil
nd thou shalt bring the meat offering that is made of these things unto the  and when it is presented unto the priest he shall bring it unto the altar
nd the priest shall take from the meat offering a memorial thereof and shall burn it upon the altar it is an offering made by fire of a sweet savour unto the 
nd that which is left of the meat offering shall be arons and his sons it is a thing most holy of the offerings of the  made by fire
o meat offering which ye shall bring unto the  shall be made with leaven for ye shall burn no leaven nor any honey in any offering of the  made by fire
s for the oblation of the firstfruits ye shall offer them unto the  but they shall not be burnt on the altar for a sweet savour
nd every oblation of thy meat offering shalt thou season with salt neither shalt thou suffer the salt of the covenant of thy od to be lacking from thy meat offering with all thine offerings thou shalt offer salt
nd if thou offer a meat offering of thy firstfruits unto the  thou shalt offer for the meat offering of thy firstfruits green ears of corn dried by the fire even corn beaten out of full ears
