he  shall send upon thee cursing vexation and rebuke in all that thou settest thine hand unto for to do until thou be destroyed and until thou perish quickly because of the wickedness of thy doings whereby thou hast forsaken me
he  shall make the pestilence cleave unto thee until he have consumed thee from off the land whither thou goest to possess it
he  shall smite thee with a consumption and with a fever and with an inflammation and with an extreme burning and with the sword and with blasting and with mildew and they shall pursue thee until thou perish
nd thy heaven that is over thy head shall be brass and the earth that is under thee shall be iron
he  shall make the rain of thy land powder and dust from heaven shall it come down upon thee until thou be destroyed
he  shall cause thee to be smitten before thine enemies thou shalt go out one way against them and flee seven ways before them and shalt be removed into all the kingdoms of the earth
nd thy carcase shall be meat unto all fowls of the air and unto the beasts of the earth and no man shall fray them away
he  will smite thee with the botch of gypt and with the emerods and with the scab and with the itch whereof thou canst not be healed
he  shall smite thee with madness and blindness and astonishment of heart and thou shalt grope at noonday as the blind gropeth in darkness and thou shalt not prosper in thy ways and thou shalt be only oppressed and spoiled evermore and no man shall save thee
hou shalt betroth a wife and another man shall lie with her thou shalt build an house and thou shalt not dwell therein thou shalt plant a vineyard and shalt not gather the grapes thereof
hine ox shall be slain before thine eyes and thou shalt not eat thereof thine ass shall be violently taken away from before thy face and shall not be restored to thee thy sheep shall be given unto thine enemies and thou shalt have none to rescue them
hy sons and thy daughters shall be given unto another people and thine eyes shall look and fail with longing for them all the day long and there shall be no might in thine hand
he fruit of thy land and all thy labours shall a nation which thou knowest not eat up and thou shalt be only oppressed and crushed alway so that thou shalt be mad for the sight of thine eyes which thou shalt see
he  shall smite thee in the knees and in the legs with a sore botch that cannot be healed from the sole of thy foot unto the top of thy head
he  shall bring thee and thy king which thou shalt set over thee unto a nation which neither thou nor thy fathers have known and there shalt thou serve other gods wood and stone
nd thou shalt become an astonishment a proverb and a byword among all nations whither the  shall lead thee
hou shalt carry much seed out into the field and shalt gather but little in for the locust shall consume it
hou shalt plant vineyards and dress them but shalt neither drink of the wine nor gather the grapes for the worms shall eat them
hou shalt have olive trees throughout all thy coasts but thou shalt not anoint thyself with the oil for thine olive shall cast his fruit
hou shalt beget sons and daughters but thou shalt not enjoy them for they shall go into captivity
ll thy trees and fruit of thy land shall the locust consume
he stranger that is within thee shall get up above thee very high and thou shalt come down very low
e shall lend to thee and thou shalt not lend to him he shall be the head and thou shalt be the tail
oreover all these curses shall come upon thee and shall pursue thee and overtake thee till thou be destroyed because thou hearkenedst not unto the voice of the  thy od to keep his commandments and his statutes which he commanded thee and they shall be upon thee for a sign and for a wonder and upon thy seed for ever
ecause thou servedst not the  thy od with joyfulness and with gladness of heart for the abundance of all things therefore shalt thou serve thine enemies which the  shall send against thee in hunger and in thirst and in nakedness and in want of all things and he shall put a yoke of iron upon thy neck until he have destroyed thee
he  shall bring a nation against thee from far from the end of the earth as swift as the eagle flieth a nation whose tongue thou shalt not understand a nation of fierce countenance which shall not regard the person of the old nor shew favour to the young and he shall eat the fruit of thy cattle and the fruit of thy land until thou be destroyed which also shall not leave thee either corn wine or oil or the increase of thy kine or flocks of thy sheep until he have destroyed thee
nd he shall besiege thee in all thy gates until thy high and fenced walls come down wherein thou trustedst throughout all thy land and he shall besiege thee in all thy gates throughout all thy land which the  thy od hath given thee
nd thou shalt eat the fruit of thine own body the flesh of thy sons and of thy daughters which the  thy od hath given thee in the siege and in the straitness wherewith thine enemies shall distress thee so that the man that is tender among you and very delicate his eye shall be evil toward his brother and toward the wife of his bosom and toward the remnant of his children which he shall leave so that he will not give to any of them of the flesh of his children whom he shall eat because he hath nothing left him in the siege and in the straitness wherewith thine enemies shall distress thee in all thy gates
he tender and delicate woman among you which would not adventure to set the sole of her foot upon the ground for delicateness and tenderness her eye shall be evil toward the husband of her bosom and toward her son and toward her daughter and toward her young one that cometh out from between her feet and toward her children which she shall bear for she shall eat them for want of all things secretly in the siege and straitness wherewith thine enemy shall distress thee in thy gates
f thou wilt not observe to do all the words of this law that are written in this book that thou mayest fear this glorious and fearful name  ord   then the  will make thy plagues wonderful and the plagues of thy seed even great plagues and of long continuance and sore sicknesses and of long continuance
oreover he will bring upon thee all the diseases of gypt which thou wast afraid of and they shall cleave unto thee
lso every sickness and every plague which is not written in the book of this law them will the  bring upon thee until thou be destroyed
nd ye shall be left few in number whereas ye were as the stars of heaven for multitude because thou wouldest not obey the voice of the  thy od
nd it shall come to pass that as the  rejoiced over you to do you good and to multiply you so the  will rejoice over you to destroy you and to bring you to nought and ye shall be plucked from off the land whither thou goest to possess it
nd the  shall scatter thee among all people from the one end of the earth even unto the other and there thou shalt serve other gods which neither thou nor thy fathers have known even wood and stone
nd among these nations shalt thou find no ease neither shall the sole of thy foot have rest but the  shall give thee there a trembling heart and failing of eyes and sorrow of mind and thy life shall hang in doubt before thee and thou shalt fear day and night and shalt have none assurance of thy life in the morning thou shalt say ould od it were even and at even thou shalt say ould od it were morning for the fear of thine heart wherewith thou shalt fear and for the sight of thine eyes which thou shalt see
nd the  shall bring thee into gypt again with ships by the way whereof  spake unto thee hou shalt see it no more again and there ye shall be sold unto your enemies for bondmen and bondwomen and no man shall buy you
hese are the words of the covenant which the  commanded oses to make with the children of srael in the land of oab beside the covenant which he made with them in oreb
nd oses called unto all srael and said unto them e have seen all that the  did before your eyes in the land of gypt unto haraoh and unto all his servants and unto all his land the great temptations which thine eyes have seen the signs and those great miracles yet the  hath not given you an heart to perceive and eyes to see and ears to hear unto this day
nd  have led you forty years in the wilderness your clothes are not waxen old upon you and thy shoe is not waxen old upon thy foot
e have not eaten bread neither have ye drunk wine or strong drink that ye might know that  am the  your od
nd when ye came unto this place ihon the king of eshbon and g the king of ashan came out against us unto battle and we smote them and we took their land and gave it for an inheritance unto the eubenites and to the adites and to the half tribe of anasseh
eep therefore the words of this covenant and do them that ye may prosper in all that ye do
e stand this day all of you before the  your od your captains of your tribes your elders and your officers with all the men of srael your little ones your wives and thy stranger that is in thy camp from the hewer of thy wood unto the drawer of thy water that thou shouldest enter into covenant with the  thy od and into his oath which the  thy od maketh with thee this day that he may establish thee to day for a people unto himself and that he may be unto thee a od as he hath said unto thee and as he hath sworn unto thy fathers to braham to saac and to acob
either with you only do  make this covenant and this oath but with him that standeth here with us this day before the  our od and also with him that is not here with us this day for ye know how we have dwelt in the land of gypt and how we came through the nations which ye passed by and ye have seen their abominations and their idols wood and stone silver and gold which were among them lest there should be among you man or woman or family or tribe whose heart turneth away this day from the  our od to go and serve the gods of these nations lest there should be among you a root that beareth gall and wormwood and it come to pass when he heareth the words of this curse that he bless himself in his heart saying  shall have peace though  walk in the imagination of mine heart to add drunkenness to thirst the  will not spare him but then the anger of the  and his jealousy shall smoke against that man and all the curses that are written in this book shall lie upon him and the  shall blot out his name from under heaven
nd the  shall separate him unto evil out of all the tribes of srael according to all the curses of the covenant that are written in this book of the law so that the generation to come of your children that shall rise up after you and the stranger that shall come from a far land shall say when they see the plagues of that land and the sicknesses which the  hath laid upon it and that the whole land thereof is brimstone and salt and burning that it is not sown nor beareth nor any grass groweth therein like the overthrow of odom and omorrah dmah and eboim which the  overthrew in his anger and in his wrath even all nations shall say herefore hath the  done thus unto this land what meaneth the heat of this great anger
hen men shall say ecause they have forsaken the covenant of the  od of their fathers which he made with them when he brought them forth out of the land of gypt for they went and served other gods and worshipped them gods whom they knew not and whom he had not given unto them and the anger of the  was kindled against this land to bring upon it all the curses that are written in this book and the  rooted them out of their land in anger and in wrath and in great indignation and cast them into another land as it is this day
he secret things belong unto the  our od but those things which are revealed belong unto us and to our children for ever that we may do all the words of this law
nd it shall come to pass when all these things are come upon thee the blessing and the curse which  have set before thee and thou shalt call them to mind among all the nations whither the  thy od hath driven thee and shalt return unto the  thy od and shalt obey his voice according to all that  command thee this day thou and thy children with all thine heart and with all thy soul that then the  thy od will turn thy captivity and have compassion upon thee and will return and gather thee from all the nations whither the  thy od hath scattered thee

nd oshua said unto them ho are ye and from whence come ye

herefore our elders and all the inhabitants of our country spake to us saying ake victuals with you for the journey and go to meet them and say unto them e are your servants therefore now make ye a league with us
his our bread we took hot for our provision out of our houses on the day we came forth to go unto you but now behold it is dry and it is mouldy and these bottles of wine which we filled were new and behold they be rent and these our garments and our shoes are become old by reason of the very long journey
nd the men took of their victuals and asked not counsel at the mouth of the 
nd oshua made peace with them and made a league with them to let them live and the princes of the congregation sware unto them
nd it came to pass at the end of three days after they had made a league with them that they heard that they were their neighbours and that they dwelt among them
nd the children of srael journeyed and came unto their cities on the third day
ow their cities were ibeon and hephirah and eeroth and irjathjearim
nd the children of srael smote them not because the princes of the congregation had sworn unto them by the  od of srael
nd all the congregation murmured against the princes
ut all the princes said unto all the congregation e have sworn unto them by the  od of srael now therefore we may not touch them
his we will do to them we will even let them live lest wrath be upon us because of the oath which we sware unto them
nd the princes said unto them et them live but let them be hewers of wood and drawers of water unto all the congregation as the princes had promised them
nd oshua called for them and he spake unto them saying herefore have ye beguiled us saying e are very far from you when ye dwell among us
ow therefore ye are cursed and there shall none of you be freed from being bondmen and hewers of wood and drawers of water for the house of my od
nd they answered oshua and said ecause it was certainly told thy servants how that the  thy od commanded his servant oses to give you all the land and to destroy all the inhabitants of the land from before you therefore we were sore afraid of our lives because of you and have done this thing
nd now behold we are in thine hand as it seemeth good and right unto thee to do unto us do
nd so did he unto them and delivered them out of the hand of the children of srael that they slew them not
nd oshua made them that day hewers of wood and drawers of water for the congregation and for the altar of the  even unto this day in the place which he should choose
ow it came to pass when donizedec king of erusalem had heard how oshua had taken i and had utterly destroyed it as he had done to ericho and her king so he had done to i and her king and how the inhabitants of ibeon had made peace with srael and were among them that they feared greatly because ibeon was a great city as one of the royal cities and because it was greater than i and all the men thereof were mighty
herefore donizedec king of erusalem sent unto oham king of ebron and unto iram king of armuth and unto aphia king of achish and unto ebir king of glon saying ome up unto me and help me that we may smite ibeon for it hath made peace with oshua and with the children of srael
herefore the five kings of the morites the king of erusalem the king of ebron the king of armuth the king of achish the king of glon gathered themselves together and went up they and all their hosts and encamped before ibeon and made war against it
nd the men of ibeon sent unto oshua to the camp to ilgal saying lack not thy hand from thy servants come up to us quickly and save us and help us for all the kings of the morites that dwell in the mountains are gathered together against us
o oshua ascended from ilgal he and all the people of war with him and all the mighty men of valour
nd the  said unto oshua ear them not for  have delivered them into thine hand there shall not a man of them stand before thee
oshua therefore came unto them suddenly and went up from ilgal all night
nd the  discomfited them before srael and slew them with a great slaughter at ibeon and chased them along the way that goeth up to ethhoron and smote them to zekah and unto akkedah
nd it came to pass as they fled from before srael and were in the going down to ethhoron that the  cast down great stones from heaven upon them unto zekah and they died they were more which died with hailstones than they whom the children of srael slew with the sword
hen spake oshua to the  in the day when the  delivered up the morites before the children of srael and he said in the sight of srael un stand thou still upon ibeon nd thou oon in the valley of jalon
nd the sun stood still and the moon stayed ntil the people had avenged themselves upon their enemies
s not this written in the book of asher
o the sun stood still in the midst of heaven and hasted not to go down about a whole day
nd there was no day like that before it or after it that the  hearkened unto the voice of a man for the  fought for srael
nd oshua returned and all srael with him unto the camp to ilgal
ut these five kings fled and hid themselves in a cave at akkedah
nd it was told oshua saying he five kings are found hid in a cave at akkedah
nd oshua said oll great stones upon the mouth of the cave and set men by it for to keep them and stay ye not but pursue after your enemies and smite the hindmost of them suffer them not to enter into their cities for the  your od hath delivered them into your hand
nd it came to pass when oshua and the children of srael had made an end of slaying them with a very great slaughter till they were consumed that the rest which remained of them entered into fenced cities
nd all the people returned to the camp to oshua at akkedah in peace none moved his tongue against any of the children of srael
hen said oshua pen the mouth of the cave and bring out those five kings unto me out of the cave
nd they did so and brought forth those five kings unto him out of the cave the king of erusalem the king of ebron the king of armuth the king of achish and the king of glon
nd it came to pass when they brought out those kings unto oshua that oshua called for all the men of srael and said unto the captains of the men of war which went with him ome near put your feet upon the necks of these kings
nd they came near and put their feet upon the necks of them
nd oshua said unto them ear not nor be dismayed be strong and of good courage for thus shall the  do to all your enemies against whom ye fight
nd afterward oshua smote them and slew them and hanged them on five trees and they were hanging upon the trees until the evening
nd it came to pass at the time of the going down of the sun that oshua commanded and they took them down off the trees and cast them into the cave wherein they had been hid and laid great stones in the caves mouth which remain until this very day
nd that day oshua took akkedah and smote it with the edge of the sword and the king thereof he utterly destroyed them and all the souls that were therein he let none remain and he did to the king of akkedah as he did unto the king of ericho
hen oshua passed from akkedah and all srael with him unto ibnah and fought against ibnah and the  delivered it also and the king thereof into the hand of srael and he smote it with the edge of the sword and all the souls that were therein he let none remain in it but did unto the king thereof as he did unto the king of ericho
nd oshua passed from ibnah and all srael with him unto achish and encamped against it and fought against it and the  delivered achish into the hand of srael which took it on the second day and smote it with the edge of the sword and all the souls that were therein according to all that he had done to ibnah
hen oram king of ezer came up to help achish and oshua smote him and his people until he had left him none remaining
nd from achish oshua passed unto glon and all srael with him and they encamped against it and fought against it and they took it on that day and smote it with the edge of the sword and all the souls that were therein he utterly destroyed that day according to all that he had done to achish
nd oshua went up from glon and all srael with him unto ebron and they fought against it and they took it and smote it with the edge of the sword and the king thereof and all the cities thereof and all the souls that were therein he left none remaining according to all that he had done to glon but destroyed it utterly and all the souls that were therein
nd oshua returned and all srael with him to ebir and fought against it and he took it and the king thereof and all the cities thereof and they smote them with the edge of the sword and utterly destroyed all the souls that were therein he left none remaining as he had done to ebron so he did to ebir and to the king thereof as he had done also to ibnah and to her king
o oshua smote all the country of the hills and of the south and of the vale and of the springs and all their kings he left none remaining but utterly destroyed all that breathed as the  od of srael commanded
nd oshua smote them from adeshbarnea even unto aza and all the country of oshen even unto ibeon
nd all these kings and their land did oshua take at one time because the  od of srael fought for srael
nd oshua returned and all srael with him unto the camp to ilgal
nd it came to pass when abin king of azor had heard those things that he sent to obab king of adon and to the king of himron and to the king of chshaph and to the kings that were on the north of the mountains and of the plains south of hinneroth and in the valley and in the borders of or on the west and to the anaanite on the east and on the west and to the morite and the ittite and the erizzite and the ebusite in the mountains and to the ivite under ermon in the land of izpeh
nd they went out they and all their hosts with them much people even as the sand that is upon the sea shore in multitude with horses and chariots very many
nd when all these kings were met together they came and pitched together at the waters of erom to fight against srael
nd the  said unto oshua e not afraid because of them for to morrow about this time will  deliver them up all slain before srael thou shalt hough their horses and burn their chariots with fire
o oshua came and all the people of war with him against them by the waters of erom suddenly and they fell upon them
nd the  delivered them into the hand of srael who smote them and chased them unto great idon and unto isrephothmaim and unto the valley of izpeh eastward and they smote them until they left them none remaining
nd oshua did unto them as the  bade him he houghed their horses and burnt their chariots with fire
nd oshua at that time turned back and took azor and smote the king thereof with the sword for azor beforetime was the head of all those kingdoms
nd they smote all the souls that were therein with the edge of the sword utterly destroying them there was not any left to breathe and he burnt azor with fire
nd all the cities of those kings and all the kings of them did oshua take and smote them with the edge of the sword and he utterly destroyed them as oses the servant of the  commanded
ut as for the cities that stood still in their strength srael burned none of them save azor only that did oshua burn
nd all the spoil of these cities and the cattle the children of srael took for a prey unto themselves but every man they smote with the edge of the sword until they had destroyed them neither left they any to breathe
s the  commanded oses his servant so did oses command oshua and so did oshua he left nothing undone of all that the  commanded oses
o oshua took all that land the hills and all the south country and all the land of oshen and the valley and the plain and the mountain of srael and the valley of the same even from the mount alak that goeth up to eir even unto aalgad in the valley of ebanon under mount ermon and all their kings he took and smote them and slew them
oshua made war a long time with all those kings
here was not a city that made peace with the children of srael save the ivites the inhabitants of ibeon all other they took in battle
or it was of the  to harden their hearts that they should come against srael in battle that he might destroy them utterly and that they might have no favour but that he might destroy them as the  commanded oses
nd at that time came oshua and cut off the nakims from the mountains from ebron from ebir from nab and from all the mountains of udah and from all the mountains of srael oshua destroyed them utterly with their cities
here was none of the nakims left in the land of the children of srael only in aza in ath and in shdod there remained
o oshua took the whole land according to all that the  said unto oses and oshua gave it for an inheritance unto srael according to their divisions by their tribes
nd the land rested from war
ow these are the kings of the land which the children of srael smote and possessed their land on the other side ordan toward the rising of the sun from the river rnon unto mount ermon and all the plain on the east ihon king of the morites who dwelt in eshbon and ruled from roer which is upon the bank of the river rnon and from the middle of the river and from half ilead even unto the river abbok which is the border of the children of mmon and from the plain to the sea of hinneroth on the east and unto the sea of the plain even the salt sea on the east the way to ethjeshimoth and from the south under shdothpisgah and the coast of g king of ashan which was of the remnant of the giants that dwelt at shtaroth and at drei and reigned in mount ermon and in alcah and in all ashan unto the border of the eshurites and the aachathites and half ilead the border of ihon king of eshbon

ow oshua was old and stricken in years and the  said unto him hou art old and stricken in years and there remaineth yet very much land to be possessed
his is the land that yet remaineth all the borders of the hilistines and all eshuri from ihor which is before gypt even unto the borders of kron northward which is counted to the anaanite five lords of the hilistines the azathites and the shdothites the shkalonites the ittites and the kronites also the vites from the south all the land of the anaanites and earah that is beside the idonians unto phek to the borders of the morites and the land of the iblites and all ebanon toward the sunrising from aalgad under mount ermon unto the entering into amath
ll the inhabitants of the hill country from ebanon unto isrephothmaim and all the idonians them will  drive out from before the children of srael only divide thou it by lot unto the sraelites for an inheritance as  have commanded thee
ow therefore divide this land for an inheritance unto the nine tribes and the half tribe of anasseh with whom the eubenites and the adites have received their inheritance which oses gave them beyond ordan eastward even as oses the servant of the  gave them from roer that is upon the bank of the river rnon and the city that is in the midst of the river and all the plain of edeba unto ibon and all the cities of ihon king of the morites which reigned in eshbon unto the border of the children of mmon and ilead and the border of the eshurites and aachathites and all mount ermon and all ashan unto alcah all the kingdom of g in ashan which reigned in shtaroth and in drei who remained of the remnant of the giants for these did oses smite and cast them out
evertheless the children of srael expelled not the eshurites nor the aachathites but the eshurites and the aachathites dwell among the sraelites until this day
nly unto the tribe of evi he gave none inheritance the sacrifices of the  od of srael made by fire are their inheritance as he said unto them
nd oses gave unto the tribe of the children of euben inheritance according to their families
nd their coast was from roer that is on the bank of the river rnon and the city that is in the midst of the river and all the plain by edeba eshbon and all her cities that are in the plain ibon and amothbaal and ethbaalmeon and ahaza and edemoth and ephaath and irjathaim and ibmah and arethshahar in the mount of the valley and ethpeor and shdothpisgah and ethjeshimoth and all the cities of the plain and all the kingdom of ihon king of the morites which reigned in eshbon whom oses smote with the princes of idian vi and ekem and ur and ur and eba which were dukes of ihon dwelling in the country
alaam also the son of eor the soothsayer did the children of srael slay with the sword among them that were slain by them
nd the border of the children of euben was ordan and the border thereof
his was the inheritance of the children of euben after their families the cities and the villages thereof
nd oses gave inheritance unto the tribe of ad even unto the children of ad according to their families
nd their coast was azer and all the cities of ilead and half the land of the children of mmon unto roer that is before abbah and from eshbon unto amathmizpeh and etonim and from ahanaim unto the border of ebir and in the valley etharam and ethnimrah and uccoth and aphon the rest of the kingdom of ihon king of eshbon ordan and his border even unto the edge of the sea of hinnereth on the other side ordan eastward
his is the inheritance of the children of ad after their families the cities and their villages
nd oses gave inheritance unto the half tribe of anasseh and this was the possession of the half tribe of the children of anasseh by their families
nd their coast was from ahanaim all ashan all the kingdom of g king of ashan and all the towns of air which are in ashan threescore cities and half ilead and shtaroth and drei cities of the kingdom of g in ashan were pertaining unto the children of achir the son of anasseh even to the one half of the children of achir by their families
hese are the countries which oses did distribute for inheritance in the plains of oab on the other side ordan by ericho eastward
ut unto the tribe of evi oses gave not any inheritance the  od of srael was their inheritance as he said unto them
nd these are the countries which the children of srael inherited in the land of anaan which leazar the priest and oshua the son of un and the heads of the fathers of the tribes of the children of srael distributed for inheritance to them
y lot was their inheritance as the  commanded by the hand of oses for the nine tribes and for the half tribe
or oses had given the inheritance of two tribes and an half tribe on the other side ordan but unto the evites he gave none inheritance among them
or the children of oseph were two tribes anasseh and phraim therefore they gave no part unto the evites in the land save cities to dwell in with their suburbs for their cattle and for their substance
s the  commanded oses so the children of srael did and they divided the land
hen the children of udah came unto oshua in ilgal and aleb the son of ephunneh the enezite said unto him hou knowest the thing that the  said unto oses the man of od concerning me and thee in adeshbarnea
orty years old was  when oses the servant of the  sent me from adeshbarnea to espy out the land and  brought him word again as it was in mine heart
evertheless my brethren that went up with me made the heart of the people melt but  wholly followed the  my od
nd oses sware on that day saying urely the land whereon thy feet have trodden shall be thine inheritance and thy childrens for ever because thou hast wholly followed the  my od
nd now behold the  hath kept me alive as he said these forty and five years even since the  spake this word unto oses while the children of srael wandered in the wilderness and now lo  am this day fourscore and five years old
s yet  am as strong this day as  was in the day that oses sent me as my strength was then even so is my strength now for war both to go out and to come in
ow therefore give me this mountain whereof the  spake in that day for thou heardest in that day how the nakims were there and that the cities were great and fenced if so be the  will be with me then  shall be able to drive them out as the  said
nd oshua blessed him and gave unto aleb the son of ephunneh ebron for an inheritance
ebron therefore became the inheritance of aleb the son of ephunneh the enezite unto this day because that he wholly followed the  od of srael
nd the name of ebron before was irjatharba which rba was a great man among the nakims
nd the land had rest from war
his then was the lot of the tribe of the children of udah by their families even to the border of dom the wilderness of in southward was the uttermost part of the south coast
nd their south border was from the shore of the salt sea from the bay that looketh southward and it went out to the south side to aalehacrabbim and passed along to in and ascended up on the south side unto adeshbarnea and passed along to ezron and went up to dar and fetched a compass to arkaa from thence it passed toward zmon and went out unto the river of gypt and the goings out of that coast were at the sea this shall be your south coast
nd the east border was the salt sea even unto the end of ordan
nd their border in the north quarter was from the bay of the sea at the uttermost part of ordan and the border went up to ethhogla and passed along by the north of etharabah and the border went up to the stone of ohan the son of euben and the border went up toward ebir from the valley of chor and so northward looking toward ilgal that is before the going up to dummim which is on the south side of the river and the border passed toward the waters of nshemesh and the goings out thereof were at nrogel and the border went up by the valley of the son of innom unto the south side of the ebusite the same is erusalem and the border went up to the top of the mountain that lieth before the valley of innom westward which is at the end of the valley of the giants northward and the border was drawn from the top of the hill unto the fountain of the water of ephtoah and went out to the cities of mount phron and the border was drawn to aalah which is irjathjearim and the border compassed from aalah westward unto mount eir and passed along unto the side of mount earim which is hesalon on the north side and went down to ethshemesh and passed on to imnah and the border went out unto the side of kron northward and the border was drawn to hicron and passed along to mount aalah and went out unto abneel and the goings out of the border were at the sea
nd the west border was to the great sea and the coast thereof
his is the coast of the children of udah round about according to their families
nd unto aleb the son of ephunneh he gave a part among the children of udah according to the commandment of the  to oshua even the city of rba the father of nak which city is ebron
nd aleb drove thence the three sons of nak heshai and himan and almai the children of nak

ho answered ive me a blessing for thou hast given me a south land give me also springs of water

nd the lot of the children of oseph fell from ordan by ericho unto the water of ericho on the east to the wilderness that goeth up from ericho throughout mount ethel and goeth out from ethel to uz and passeth along unto the borders of rchi to taroth and goeth down westward to the coast of aphleti unto the coast of ethhoron the nether and to ezer and the goings out thereof are at the sea
o the children of oseph anasseh and phraim took their inheritance
nd the border of the children of phraim according to their families was thus even the border of their inheritance on the east side was tarothaddar unto ethhoron the upper and the border went out toward the sea to ichmethah on the north side and the border went about eastward unto aanathshiloh and passed by it on the east to anohah and it went down from anohah to taroth and to aarath and came to ericho and went out at ordan
he border went out from appuah westward unto the river anah and the goings out thereof were at the sea

nd the separate cities for the children of phraim were among the inheritance of the children of anasseh all the cities with their villages
nd they drave not out the anaanites that dwelt in ezer but the anaanites dwell among the phraimites unto this day and serve under tribute
here was also a lot for the tribe of anasseh for he was the firstborn of oseph to wit for achir the firstborn of anasseh the father of ilead because he was a man of war therefore he had ilead and ashan
here was also a lot for the rest of the children of anasseh by their families for the children of biezer and for the children of elek and for the children of sriel and for the children of hechem and for the children of epher and for the children of hemida these were the male children of anasseh the son of oseph by their families
ut elophehad the son of epher the son of ilead the son of achir the son of anasseh had no sons but daughters and these are the names of his daughters ahlah and oah oglah ilcah and irzah
nd they came near before leazar the priest and before oshua the son of un and before the princes saying he  commanded oses to give us an inheritance among our brethren
herefore according to the commandment of the  he gave them an inheritance among the brethren of their father
nd there fell ten portions to anasseh beside the land of ilead and ashan which were on the other side ordan because the daughters of anasseh had an inheritance among his sons and the rest of anassehs sons had the land of ilead
nd the coast of anasseh was from sher to ichmethah that lieth before hechem and the border went along on the right hand unto the inhabitants of ntappuah
ow anasseh had the land of appuah but appuah on the border of anasseh belonged to the children of phraim and the coast descended unto the river anah southward of the river these cities of phraim are among the cities of anasseh the coast of anasseh also was on the north side of the river and the outgoings of it were at the sea southward it was phraims and northward it was anassehs and the sea is his border and they met together in sher on the north and in ssachar on the east
nd anasseh had in ssachar and in sher ethshean and her towns and bleam and her towns and the inhabitants of or and her towns and the inhabitants of ndor and her towns and the inhabitants of aanach and her towns and the inhabitants of egiddo and her towns even three countries
et the children of anasseh could not drive out the inhabitants of those cities but the anaanites would dwell in that land
et it came to pass when the children of srael were waxen strong that they put the anaanites to tribute but did not utterly drive them out
nd the children of oseph spake unto oshua saying hy hast thou given me but one lot and one portion to inherit seeing  am a great people forasmuch as the  hath blessed me hitherto
nd oshua answered them f thou be a great people then get thee up to the wood country and cut down for thyself there in the land of the erizzites and of the giants if mount phraim be too narrow for thee
nd the children of oseph said he hill is not enough for us and all the anaanites that dwell in the land of the valley have chariots of iron both they who are of ethshean and her towns and they who are of the valley of ezreel
nd oshua spake unto the house of oseph even to phraim and to anasseh saying hou art a great people and hast great power thou shalt not have one lot only but the mountain shall be thine for it is a wood and thou shalt cut it down and the outgoings of it shall be thine for thou shalt drive out the anaanites though they have iron chariots and though they be strong
nd the whole congregation of the children of srael assembled together at hiloh and set up the tabernacle of the congregation there
nd the land was subdued before them
nd there remained among the children of srael seven tribes which had not yet received their inheritance

nd the lot of the tribe of the children of enjamin came up according to their families and the coast of their lot came forth between the children of udah and the children of oseph
nd their border on the north side was from ordan and the border went up to the side of ericho on the north side and went up through the mountains westward and the goings out thereof were at the wilderness of ethaven
nd the border went over from thence toward uz to the side of uz which is ethel southward and the border descended to tarothaddar near the hill that lieth on the south side of the nether ethhoron
nd the border was drawn thence and compassed the corner of the sea southward from the hill that lieth before ethhoron southward and the goings out thereof were at irjathbaal which is irjathjearim a city of the children of udah this was the west quarter
nd the south quarter was from the end of irjathjearim and the border went out on the west and went out to the well of waters of ephtoah and the border came down to the end of the mountain that lieth before the valley of the son of innom and which is in the valley of the giants on the north and descended to the valley of innom to the side of ebusi on the south and descended to nrogel and was drawn from the north and went forth to nshemesh and went forth toward eliloth which is over against the going up of dummim and descended to the stone of ohan the son of euben and passed along toward the side over against rabah northward and went down unto rabah and the border passed along to the side of ethhoglah northward and the outgoings of the border were at the north bay of the salt sea at the south end of ordan this was the south coast
nd ordan was the border of it on the east side

nd when he that doth flee unto one of those cities shall stand at the entering of the gate of the city and shall declare his cause in the ears of the elders of that city they shall take him into the city unto them and give him a place that he may dwell among them
nd if the avenger of blood pursue after him then they shall not deliver the slayer up into his hand because he smote his neighbour unwittingly and hated him not beforetime
nd he shall dwell in that city until he stand before the congregation for judgment and until the death of the high priest that shall be in those days then shall the slayer return and come unto his own city and unto his own house unto the city from whence he fled

nd it came to pass when srael was strong that they put the anaanites to tribute and did not utterly drive them out

either did ebulun drive out the inhabitants of itron nor the inhabitants of ahalol but the anaanites dwelt among them and became tributaries
either did sher drive out the inhabitants of ccho nor the inhabitants of idon nor of hlab nor of chzib nor of elbah nor of phik nor of ehob but the sherites dwelt among the anaanites the inhabitants of the land for they did not drive them out
either did aphtali drive out the inhabitants of ethshemesh nor the inhabitants of ethanath but he dwelt among the anaanites the inhabitants of the land nevertheless the inhabitants of ethshemesh and of ethanath became tributaries unto them
nd the morites forced the children of an into the mountain for they would not suffer them to come down to the valley but the morites would dwell in mount eres in ijalon and in haalbim yet the hand of the house of oseph prevailed so that they became tributaries

hus was idian subdued before the children of srael so that they lifted up their heads no more
nd the country was in quietness forty years in the days of ideon
nd erubbaal the son of oash went and dwelt in his own house
nd ideon had threescore and ten sons of his body begotten for he had many wives
nd his concubine that was in hechem she also bare him a son whose name he called bimelech
nd ideon the son of oash died in a good old age and was buried in the sepulchre of oash his father in phrah of the biezrites
nd it came to pass as soon as ideon was dead that the children of srael turned again and went a whoring after aalim and made aalberith their god
nd the children of srael remembered not the  their od who had delivered them out of the hands of all their enemies on every side neither shewed they kindness to the house of erubbaal namely ideon according to all the goodness which he had shewed unto srael
nd bimelech the son of erubbaal went to hechem unto his mothers brethren and communed with them and with all the family of the house of his mothers father saying peak  pray you in the ears of all the men of hechem hether is better for you either that all the sons of erubbaal which are threescore and ten persons reign over you or that one reign over you remember also that  am your bone and your flesh
nd his mothers brethren spake of him in the ears of all the men of hechem all these words and their hearts inclined to follow bimelech for they said e is our brother
nd they gave him threescore and ten pieces of silver out of the house of aalberith wherewith bimelech hired vain and light persons which followed him
nd he went unto his fathers house at phrah and slew his brethren the sons of erubbaal being threescore and ten persons upon one stone notwithstanding yet otham the youngest son of erubbaal was left for he hid himself
nd all the men of hechem gathered together and all the house of illo and went and made bimelech king by the plain of the pillar that was in hechem
nd when they told it to otham he went and stood in the top of mount erizim and lifted up his voice and cried and said unto them earken unto me ye men of hechem that od may hearken unto you
he trees went forth on a time to anoint a king over them and they said unto the olive tree eign thou over us
ut the olive tree said unto them hould  leave my fatness wherewith by me they honour od and man and go to be promoted over the trees
nd the trees said to the fig tree ome thou and reign over us
ut the fig tree said unto them hould  forsake my sweetness and my good fruit and go to be promoted over the trees
hen said the trees unto the vine ome thou and reign over us
nd the vine said unto them hould  leave my wine which cheereth od and man and go to be promoted over the trees
hen said all the trees unto the bramble ome thou and reign over us
nd the bramble said unto the trees f in truth ye anoint me king over you then come and put your trust in my shadow and if not let fire come out of the bramble and devour the cedars of ebanon
ow therefore if ye have done truly and sincerely in that ye have made bimelech king and if ye have dealt well with erubbaal and his house and have done unto him according to the deserving of his hands for my father fought for you and adventured his life far and delivered you out of the hand of idian and ye are risen up against my fathers house this day and have slain his sons threescore and ten persons upon one stone and have made bimelech the son of his maidservant king over the men of hechem because he is your brother if ye then have dealt truly and sincerely with erubbaal and with his house this day then rejoice ye in bimelech and let him also rejoice in you but if not let fire come out from bimelech and devour the men of hechem and the house of illo and let fire come out from the men of hechem and from the house of illo and devour bimelech
nd otham ran away and fled and went to eer and dwelt there for fear of bimelech his brother
hen bimelech had reigned three years over srael then od sent an evil spirit between bimelech and the men of hechem and the men of hechem dealt treacherously with bimelech that the cruelty done to the threescore and ten sons of erubbaal might come and their blood be laid upon bimelech their brother which slew them and upon the men of hechem which aided him in the killing of his brethren
nd the men of hechem set liers in wait for him in the top of the mountains and they robbed all that came along that way by them and it was told bimelech
nd aal the son of bed came with his brethren and went over to hechem and the men of hechem put their confidence in him
nd they went out into the fields and gathered their vineyards and trode the grapes and made merry and went into the house of their god and did eat and drink and cursed bimelech
nd aal the son of bed said ho is bimelech and who is hechem that we should serve him is not he the son of erubbaal and ebul his officer serve the men of amor the father of hechem for why should we serve him
nd would to od this people were under my hand then would  remove bimelech
nd he said to bimelech ncrease thine army and come out
nd when ebul the ruler of the city heard the words of aal the son of bed his anger was kindled
nd he sent messengers unto bimelech privily saying ehold aal the son of bed and his brethren be come to hechem and behold they fortify the city against thee
ow therefore up by night thou and the people that is with thee and lie in wait in the field and it shall be that in the morning as soon as the sun is up thou shalt rise early and set upon the city and behold when he and the people that is with him come out against thee then mayest thou do to them as thou shalt find occasion
nd bimelech rose up and all the people that were with him by night and they laid wait against hechem in four companies
nd aal the son of bed went out and stood in the entering of the gate of the city and bimelech rose up and the people that were with him from lying in wait
nd when aal saw the people he said to ebul ehold there come people down from the top of the mountains
nd ebul said unto him hou seest the shadow of the mountains as if they were men
nd aal spake again and said ee there come people down by the middle of the land and another company come along by the plain of eonenim
hen said ebul unto him here is now thy mouth wherewith thou saidst ho is bimelech that we should serve him is not this the people that thou hast despised go out  pray now and fight with them
nd aal went out before the men of hechem and fought with bimelech
nd bimelech chased him and he fled before him and many were overthrown and wounded even unto the entering of the gate
nd bimelech dwelt at rumah and ebul thrust out aal and his brethren that they should not dwell in hechem
nd it came to pass on the morrow that the people went out into the field and they told bimelech
nd he took the people and divided them into three companies and laid wait in the field and looked and behold the people were come forth out of the city and he rose up against them and smote them
nd bimelech and the company that was with him rushed forward and stood in the entering of the gate of the city and the two other companies ran upon all the people that were in the fields and slew them
nd bimelech fought against the city all that day and he took the city and slew the people that was therein and beat down the city and sowed it with salt
nd when all the men of the tower of hechem heard that they entered into an hold of the house of the god erith
nd it was told bimelech that all the men of the tower of hechem were gathered together
nd bimelech gat him up to mount almon he and all the people that were with him and bimelech took an axe in his hand and cut down a bough from the trees and took it and laid it on his shoulder and said unto the people that were with him hat ye have seen me do make haste and do as  have done
nd all the people likewise cut down every man his bough and followed bimelech and put them to the hold and set the hold on fire upon them so that all the men of the tower of hechem died also about a thousand men and women
hen went bimelech to hebez and encamped against hebez and took it
ut there was a strong tower within the city and thither fled all the men and women and all they of the city and shut it to them and gat them up to the top of the tower
nd bimelech came unto the tower and fought against it and went hard unto the door of the tower to burn it with fire
nd a certain woman cast a piece of a millstone upon bimelechs head and all to brake his skull
hen he called hastily unto the young man his armourbearer and said unto him raw thy sword and slay me that men say not of me  woman slew him
nd his young man thrust him through and he died
nd when the men of srael saw that bimelech was dead they departed every man unto his place
hus od rendered the wickedness of bimelech which he did unto his father in slaying his seventy brethren and all the evil of the men of hechem did od render upon their heads and upon them came the curse of otham the son of erubbaal
nd after bimelech there arose to defend srael ola the son of uah the son of odo a man of ssachar and he dwelt in hamir in mount phraim
nd he judged srael twenty and three years and died and was buried in hamir
nd after him arose air a ileadite and judged srael twenty and two years
nd he had thirty sons that rode on thirty ass colts and they had thirty cities which are called avothjair unto this day which are in the land of ilead
nd air died and was buried in amon
nd the children of srael did evil again in the sight of the  and served aalim and shtaroth and the gods of yria and the gods of idon and the gods of oab and the gods of the children of mmon and the gods of the hilistines and forsook the  and served not him
nd the anger of the  was hot against srael and he sold them into the hands of the hilistines and into the hands of the children of mmon
nd that year they vexed and oppressed the children of srael eighteen years all the children of srael that were on the other side ordan in the land of the morites which is in ilead
oreover the children of mmon passed over ordan to fight also against udah and against enjamin and against the house of phraim so that srael was sore distressed
nd the children of srael cried unto the  saying e have sinned against thee both because we have forsaken our od and also served aalim
nd the  said unto the children of srael id not  deliver you from the gyptians and from the morites from the children of mmon and from the hilistines
he idonians also and the malekites and the aonites did oppress you and ye cried to me and  delivered you out of their hand
et ye have forsaken me and served other gods wherefore  will deliver you no more
o and cry unto the gods which ye have chosen let them deliver you in the time of your tribulation
nd the children of srael said unto the  e have sinned do thou unto us whatsoever seemeth good unto thee deliver us only we pray thee this day
nd they put away the strange gods from among them and served the  and his soul was grieved for the misery of srael
hen the children of mmon were gathered together and encamped in ilead
nd the children of srael assembled themselves together and encamped in izpeh
nd the people and princes of ilead said one to another hat man is he that will begin to fight against the children of mmon he shall be head over all the inhabitants of ilead
ow ephthah the ileadite was a mighty man of valour and he was the son of an harlot and ilead begat ephthah
nd ileads wife bare him sons and his wifes sons grew up and they thrust out ephthah and said unto him hou shalt not inherit in our fathers house for thou art the son of a strange woman
hen ephthah fled from his brethren and dwelt in the land of ob and there were gathered vain men to ephthah and went out with him
nd it came to pass in process of time that the children of mmon made war against srael
nd it was so that when the children of mmon made war against srael the elders of ilead went to fetch ephthah out of the land of ob and they said unto ephthah ome and be our captain that we may fight with the children of mmon
nd ephthah said unto the elders of ilead id not ye hate me and expel me out of my fathers house and why are ye come unto me now when ye are in distress
nd the elders of ilead said unto ephthah herefore we turn again to thee now that thou mayest go with us and fight against the children of mmon and be our head over all the inhabitants of ilead
nd ephthah said unto the elders of ilead f ye bring me home again to fight against the children of mmon and the  deliver them before me shall  be your head
nd the elders of ilead said unto ephthah he  be witness between us if we do not so according to thy words
hen ephthah went with the elders of ilead and the people made him head and captain over them and ephthah uttered all his words before the  in izpeh
nd ephthah sent messengers unto the king of the children of mmon saying hat hast thou to do with me that thou art come against me to fight in my land
nd the king of the children of mmon answered unto the messengers of ephthah ecause srael took away my land when they came up out of gypt from rnon even unto abbok and unto ordan now therefore restore those lands again peaceably
nd ephthah sent messengers again unto the king of the children of mmon and said unto him hus saith ephthah srael took not away the land of oab nor the land of the children of mmon but when srael came up from gypt and walked through the wilderness unto the ed sea and came to adesh then srael sent messengers unto the king of dom saying et me  pray thee pass through thy land but the king of dom would not hearken thereto
nd in like manner they sent unto the king of oab but he would not consent and srael abode in adesh
hen they went along through the wilderness and compassed the land of dom and the land of oab and came by the east side of the land of oab and pitched on the other side of rnon but came not within the border of oab for rnon was the border of oab
nd srael sent messengers unto ihon king of the morites the king of eshbon and srael said unto him et us pass we pray thee through thy land into my place
ut ihon trusted not srael to pass through his coast but ihon gathered all his people together and pitched in ahaz and fought against srael
nd the  od of srael delivered ihon and all his people into the hand of srael and they smote them so srael possessed all the land of the morites the inhabitants of that country
nd they possessed all the coasts of the morites from rnon even unto abbok and from the wilderness even unto ordan
o now the  od of srael hath dispossessed the morites from before his people srael and shouldest thou possess it
ilt not thou possess that which hemosh thy god giveth thee to possess
o whomsoever the  our od shall drive out from before us them will we possess
nd now art thou any thing better than alak the son of ippor king of oab did he ever strive against srael or did he ever fight against them while srael dwelt in eshbon and her towns and in roer and her towns and in all the cities that be along by the coasts of rnon three hundred years why therefore did ye not recover them within that time
herefore  have not sinned against thee but thou doest me wrong to war against me the  the udge be judge this day between the children of srael and the children of mmon
owbeit the king of the children of mmon hearkened not unto the words of ephthah which he sent him
hen the pirit of the  came upon ephthah and he passed over ilead and anasseh and passed over izpeh of ilead and from izpeh of ilead he passed over unto the children of mmon
nd ephthah vowed a vow unto the  and said f thou shalt without fail deliver the children of mmon into mine hands then it shall be that whatsoever cometh forth of the doors of my house to meet me when  return in peace from the children of mmon shall surely be the s and  will offer it up for a burnt offering
o ephthah passed over unto the children of mmon to fight against them and the  delivered them into his hands
nd he smote them from roer even till thou come to innith even twenty cities and unto the plain of the vineyards with a very great slaughter
hus the children of mmon were subdued before the children of srael
nd ephthah came to izpeh unto his house and behold his daughter came out to meet him with timbrels and with dances and she was his only child beside her he had neither son nor daughter
nd it came to pass when he saw her that he rent his clothes and said las my daughter thou hast brought me very low and thou art one of them that trouble me for  have opened my mouth unto the  and  cannot go back
nd she said unto him y father if thou hast opened thy mouth unto the  do to me according to that which hath proceeded out of thy mouth forasmuch as the  hath taken vengeance for thee of thine enemies even of the children of mmon
nd she said unto her father et this thing be done for me let me alone two months that  may go up and down upon the mountains and bewail my virginity  and my fellows
nd he said o
nd he sent her away for two months and she went with her companions and bewailed her virginity upon the mountains
nd it came to pass at the end of two months that she returned unto her father who did with her according to his vow which he had vowed and she knew no man
nd it was a custom in srael that the daughters of srael went yearly to lament the daughter of ephthah the ileadite four days in a year
nd the men of phraim gathered themselves together and went northward and said unto ephthah herefore passedst thou over to fight against the children of mmon and didst not call us to go with thee we will burn thine house upon thee with fire
nd ephthah said unto them  and my people were at great strife with the children of mmon and when  called you ye delivered me not out of their hands
nd when  saw that ye delivered me not  put my life in my hands and passed over against the children of mmon and the  delivered them into my hand wherefore then are ye come up unto me this day to fight against me
hen ephthah gathered together all the men of ilead and fought with phraim and the men of ilead smote phraim because they said e ileadites are fugitives of phraim among the phraimites and among the anassites
nd the ileadites took the passages of ordan before the phraimites and it was so that when those phraimites which were escaped said et me go over that the men of ilead said unto him rt thou an phraimite
f he said ay then said they unto him ay now hibboleth and he said ibboleth for he could not frame to pronounce it right
hen they took him and slew him at the passages of ordan and there fell at that time of the phraimites forty and two thousand
nd ephthah judged srael six years
hen died ephthah the ileadite and was buried in one of the cities of ilead
nd after him bzan of ethlehem judged srael
nd he had thirty sons and thirty daughters whom he sent abroad and took in thirty daughters from abroad for his sons
nd he judged srael seven years
hen died bzan and was buried at ethlehem
nd after him lon a ebulonite judged srael and he judged srael ten years
nd lon the ebulonite died and was buried in ijalon in the country of ebulun
nd after him bdon the son of illel a irathonite judged srael
nd he had forty sons and thirty nephews that rode on threescore and ten ass colts and he judged srael eight years
nd bdon the son of illel the irathonite died and was buried in irathon in the land of phraim in the mount of the malekites
nd the children of srael did evil again in the sight of the  and the  delivered them into the hand of the hilistines forty years
nd there was a certain man of orah of the family of the anites whose name was anoah and his wife was barren and bare not
nd the angel of the  appeared unto the woman and said unto her ehold now thou art barren and bearest not but thou shalt conceive and bear a son
ow therefore beware  pray thee and drink not wine nor strong drink and eat not any unclean thing for lo thou shalt conceive and bear a son and no razor shall come on his head for the child shall be a azarite unto od from the womb and he shall begin to deliver srael out of the hand of the hilistines
hen the woman came and told her husband saying  man of od came unto me and his countenance was like the countenance of an angel of od very terrible but  asked him not whence he was neither told he me his name but he said unto me ehold thou shalt conceive and bear a son and now drink no wine nor strong drink neither eat any unclean thing for the child shall be a azarite to od from the womb to the day of his death
hen anoah intreated the  and said  my ord let the man of od which thou didst send come again unto us and teach us what we shall do unto the child that shall be born
nd od hearkened to the voice of anoah and the angel of od came again unto the woman as she sat in the field but anoah her husband was not with her
nd the woman made haste and ran and shewed her husband and said unto him ehold the man hath appeared unto me that came unto me the other day
nd anoah arose and went after his wife and came to the man and said unto him rt thou the man that spakest unto the woman
nd he said  am
nd anoah said ow let thy words come to pass
ow shall we order the child and how shall we do unto him
nd the angel of the  said unto anoah f all that  said unto the woman let her beware
he may not eat of any thing that cometh of the vine neither let her drink wine or strong drink nor eat any unclean thing all that  commanded her let her observe
nd anoah said unto the angel of the   pray thee let us detain thee until we shall have made ready a kid for thee
nd the angel of the  said unto anoah hough thou detain me  will not eat of thy bread and if thou wilt offer a burnt offering thou must offer it unto the 
or anoah knew not that he was an angel of the 
nd anoah said unto the angel of the  hat is thy name that when thy sayings come to pass we may do thee honour
