o anoah took a kid with a meat offering and offered it upon a rock unto the  and the angel did wondrously and anoah and his wife looked on
or it came to pass when the flame went up toward heaven from off the altar that the angel of the  ascended in the flame of the altar
nd anoah and his wife looked on it and fell on their faces to the ground
ut the angel of the  did no more appear to anoah and to his wife
hen anoah knew that he was an angel of the 
nd anoah said unto his wife e shall surely die because we have seen od
ut his wife said unto him f the  were pleased to kill us he would not have received a burnt offering and a meat offering at our hands neither would he have shewed us all these things nor would as at this time have told us such things as these
nd the woman bare a son and called his name amson and the child grew and the  blessed him
nd the pirit of the  began to move him at times in the camp of an between orah and shtaol
nd amson went down to imnath and saw a woman in imnath of the daughters of the hilistines
nd he came up and told his father and his mother and said  have seen a woman in imnath of the daughters of the hilistines now therefore get her for me to wife
hen his father and his mother said unto him s there never a woman among the daughters of thy brethren or among all my people that thou goest to take a wife of the uncircumcised hilistines
nd amson said unto his father et her for me for she pleaseth me well
ut his father and his mother knew not that it was of the  that he sought an occasion against the hilistines for at that time the hilistines had dominion over srael
hen went amson down and his father and his mother to imnath and came to the vineyards of imnath and behold a young lion roared against him
nd the pirit of the  came mightily upon him and he rent him as he would have rent a kid and he had nothing in his hand but he told not his father or his mother what he had done
nd he went down and talked with the woman and she pleased amson well
nd after a time he returned to take her and he turned aside to see the carcase of the lion and behold there was a swarm of bees and honey in the carcase of the lion
nd he took thereof in his hands and went on eating and came to his father and mother and he gave them and they did eat but he told not them that he had taken the honey out of the carcase of the lion
o his father went down unto the woman and amson made there a feast for so used the young men to do
nd it came to pass when they saw him that they brought thirty companions to be with him
nd amson said unto them  will now put forth a riddle unto you if ye can certainly declare it me within the seven days of the feast and find it out then  will give you thirty sheets and thirty change of garments but if ye cannot declare it me then shall ye give me thirty sheets and thirty change of garments
nd they said unto him ut forth thy riddle that we may hear it
nd he said unto them ut of the eater came forth meat nd out of the strong came forth sweetness
nd they could not in three days expound the riddle
nd it came to pass on the seventh day that they said unto amsons wife ntice thy husband that he may declare unto us the riddle lest we burn thee and thy fathers house with fire have ye called us to take that we have is it not so
nd amsons wife wept before him and said hou dost but hate me and lovest me not thou hast put forth a riddle unto the children of my people and hast not told it me
nd he said unto her ehold  have not told it my father nor my mother and shall  tell it thee
nd she wept before him the seven days while their feast lasted and it came to pass on the seventh day that he told her because she lay sore upon him and she told the riddle to the children of her people
nd the men of the city said unto him on the seventh day before the sun went down hat is sweeter than honey
nd what is stronger than a lion
nd he said unto them f ye had not plowed with my heifer e had not found out my riddle
nd the pirit of the  came upon him and he went down to shkelon and slew thirty men of them and took their spoil and gave change of garments unto them which expounded the riddle
nd his anger was kindled and he went up to his fathers house
ut amsons wife was given to his companion whom he had used as his friend
ut it came to pass within a while after in the time of wheat harvest that amson visited his wife with a kid and he said  will go in to my wife into the chamber
ut her father would not suffer him to go in
nd her father said  verily thought that thou hadst utterly hated her therefore  gave her to thy companion is not her younger sister fairer than she take her  pray thee instead of her
nd amson said concerning them ow shall  be more blameless than the hilistines though  do them a displeasure
nd amson went and caught three hundred foxes and took firebrands and turned tail to tail and put a firebrand in the midst between two tails
nd when he had set the brands on fire he let them go into the standing corn of the hilistines and burnt up both the shocks and also the standing corn with the vineyards and olives
hen the hilistines said ho hath done this
nd they answered amson the son in law of the imnite because he had taken his wife and given her to his companion
nd the hilistines came up and burnt her and her father with fire
nd amson said unto them hough ye have done this yet will  be avenged of you and after that  will cease
nd he smote them hip and thigh with a great slaughter and he went down and dwelt in the top of the rock tam
hen the hilistines went up and pitched in udah and spread themselves in ehi
nd the men of udah said hy are ye come up against us
nd they answered o bind amson are we come up to do to him as he hath done to us
hen three thousand men of udah went to the top of the rock tam and said to amson nowest thou not that the hilistines are rulers over us what is this that thou hast done unto us
nd he said unto them s they did unto me so have  done unto them
nd they said unto him e are come down to bind thee that we may deliver thee into the hand of the hilistines
nd amson said unto them wear unto me that ye will not fall upon me yourselves
nd they spake unto him saying o but we will bind thee fast and deliver thee into their hand but surely we will not kill thee
nd they bound him with two new cords and brought him up from the rock
nd when he came unto ehi the hilistines shouted against him and the pirit of the  came mightily upon him and the cords that were upon his arms became as flax that was burnt with fire and his bands loosed from off his hands
nd he found a new jawbone of an ass and put forth his hand and took it and slew a thousand men therewith
nd amson said ith the jawbone of an ass heaps upon heaps ith the jaw of an ass have  slain a thousand men
nd it came to pass when he had made an end of speaking that he cast away the jawbone out of his hand and called that place amathlehi
nd he was sore athirst and called on the  and said hou hast given this great deliverance into the hand of thy servant and now shall  die for thirst and fall into the hand of the uncircumcised
ut od clave an hollow place that was in the jaw and there came water thereout and when he had drunk his spirit came again and he revived wherefore he called the name thereof nhakkore which is in ehi unto this day
nd he judged srael in the days of the hilistines twenty years
hen went amson to aza and saw there an harlot and went in unto her
nd it was told the azites saying amson is come hither
nd they compassed him in and laid wait for him all night in the gate of the city and were quiet all the night saying n the morning when it is day we shall kill him
nd amson lay till midnight and arose at midnight and took the doors of the gate of the city and the two posts and went away with them bar and all and put them upon his shoulders and carried them up to the top of an hill that is before ebron
nd it came to pass afterward that he loved a woman in the valley of orek whose name was elilah
nd the lords of the hilistines came up unto her and said unto her ntice him and see wherein his great strength lieth and by what means we may prevail against him that we may bind him to afflict him and we will give thee every one of us eleven hundred pieces of silver
nd elilah said to amson ell me  pray thee wherein thy great strength lieth and wherewith thou mightest be bound to afflict thee
nd amson said unto her f they bind me with seven green withs that were never dried then shall  be weak and be as another man
hen the lords of the hilistines brought up to her seven green withs which had not been dried and she bound him with them
ow there were men lying in wait abiding with her in the chamber
nd she said unto him he hilistines be upon thee amson
nd he brake the withs as a thread of tow is broken when it toucheth the fire
o his strength was not known
nd elilah said unto amson ehold thou hast mocked me and told me lies now tell me  pray thee wherewith thou mightest be bound
nd he said unto her f they bind me fast with new ropes that never were occupied then shall  be weak and be as another man
elilah therefore took new ropes and bound him therewith and said unto him he hilistines be upon thee amson
nd there were liers in wait abiding in the chamber
nd he brake them from off his arms like a thread
nd elilah said unto amson itherto thou hast mocked me and told me lies tell me wherewith thou mightest be bound
nd he said unto her f thou weavest the seven locks of my head with the web
nd she fastened it with the pin and said unto him he hilistines be upon thee amson
nd he awaked out of his sleep and went away with the pin of the beam and with the web
nd she said unto him ow canst thou say  love thee when thine heart is not with me
hou hast mocked me these three times and hast not told me wherein thy great strength lieth
nd it came to pass when she pressed him daily with her words and urged him so that his soul was vexed unto death that he told her all his heart and said unto her here hath not come a razor upon mine head for  have been a azarite unto od from my mothers womb if  be shaven then my strength will go from me and  shall become weak and be like any other man
nd when elilah saw that he had told her all his heart she sent and called for the lords of the hilistines saying ome up this once for he hath shewed me all his heart
hen the lords of the hilistines came up unto her and brought money in their hand
nd she made him sleep upon her knees and she called for a man and she caused him to shave off the seven locks of his head and she began to afflict him and his strength went from him
nd she said he hilistines be upon thee amson
nd he awoke out of his sleep and said  will go out as at other times before and shake myself
nd he wist not that the  was departed from him
ut the hilistines took him and put out his eyes and brought him down to aza and bound him with fetters of brass and he did grind in the prison house
owbeit the hair of his head began to grow again after he was shaven
hen the lords of the hilistines gathered them together for to offer a great sacrifice unto agon their god and to rejoice for they said ur god hath delivered amson our enemy into our hand
nd when the people saw him they praised their god for they said ur god hath delivered into our hands our enemy and the destroyer of our country which slew many of us
nd it came to pass when their hearts were merry that they said all for amson that he may make us sport
nd they called for amson out of the prison house and he made them sport and they set him between the pillars
nd amson said unto the lad that held him by the hand uffer me that  may feel the pillars whereupon the house standeth that  may lean upon them
ow the house was full of men and women and all the lords of the hilistines were there and there were upon the roof about three thousand men and women that beheld while amson made sport
nd amson called unto the  and said  ord  remember me  pray thee and strengthen me  pray thee only this once  od that  may be at once avenged of the hilistines for my two eyes
nd amson took hold of the two middle pillars upon which the house stood and on which it was borne up of the one with his right hand and of the other with his left
nd amson said et me die with the hilistines
nd he bowed himself with all his might and the house fell upon the lords and upon all the people that were therein
o the dead which he slew at his death were more than they which he slew in his life
hen his brethren and all the house of his father came down and took him and brought him up and buried him between orah and shtaol in the buryingplace of anoah his father
nd he judged srael twenty years
nd there was a man of mount phraim whose name was icah
nd he said unto his mother he eleven hundred shekels of silver that were taken from thee about which thou cursedst and spakest of also in mine ears behold the silver is with me  took it
nd his mother said lessed be thou of the  my son
nd when he had restored the eleven hundred shekels of silver to his mother his mother said  had wholly dedicated the silver unto the  from my hand for my son to make a graven image and a molten image now therefore  will restore it unto thee
et he restored the money unto his mother and his mother took two hundred shekels of silver and gave them to the founder who made thereof a graven image and a molten image and they were in the house of icah
nd the man icah had an house of gods and made an ephod and teraphim and consecrated one of his sons who became his priest
n those days there was no king in srael but every man did that which was right in his own eyes
nd there was a young man out of ethlehemjudah of the family of udah who was a evite and he sojourned there
nd the man departed out of the city from ethlehemjudah to sojourn where he could find a place and he came to mount phraim to the house of icah as he journeyed
nd icah said unto him hence comest thou
nd he said unto him  am a evite of ethlehemjudah and  go to sojourn where  may find a place
nd icah said unto him well with me and be unto me a father and a priest and  will give thee ten shekels of silver by the year and a suit of apparel and thy victuals
o the evite went in
nd the evite was content to dwell with the man and the young man was unto him as one of his sons
nd icah consecrated the evite and the young man became his priest and was in the house of icah
hen said icah ow know  that the  will do me good seeing  have a evite to my priest
n those days there was no king in srael and in those days the tribe of the anites sought them an inheritance to dwell in for unto that day all their inheritance had not fallen unto them among the tribes of srael
nd the children of an sent of their family five men from their coasts men of valour from orah and from shtaol to spy out the land and to search it and they said unto them o search the land who when they came to mount phraim to the house of icah they lodged there
hen they were by the house of icah they knew the voice of the young man the evite and they turned in thither and said unto him ho brought thee hither and what makest thou in this place and what hast thou here
nd he said unto them hus and thus dealeth icah with me and hath hired me and  am his priest
nd they said unto him sk counsel we pray thee of od that we may know whether our way which we go shall be prosperous
nd the priest said unto them o in peace before the  is your way wherein ye go
hen the five men departed and came to aish and saw the people that were therein how they dwelt careless after the manner of the idonians quiet and secure and there was no magistrate in the land that might put them to shame in any thing and they were far from the idonians and had no business with any man
nd they came unto their brethren to orah and shtaol and their brethren said unto them hat say ye
nd they said rise that we may go up against them for we have seen the land and behold it is very good and are ye still be not slothful to go and to enter to possess the land
hen ye go ye shall come unto a people secure and to a large land for od hath given it into your hands a place where there is no want of any thing that is in the earth
nd there went from thence of the family of the anites out of orah and out of shtaol six hundred men appointed with weapons of war
nd they went up and pitched in irjathjearim in udah wherefore they called that place ahanehdan unto this day behold it is behind irjathjearim
nd they passed thence unto mount phraim and came unto the house of icah
hen answered the five men that went to spy out the country of aish and said unto their brethren o ye know that there is in these houses an ephod and teraphim and a graven image and a molten image now therefore consider what ye have to do
nd they turned thitherward and came to the house of the young man the evite even unto the house of icah and saluted him
nd the six hundred men appointed with their weapons of war which were of the children of an stood by the entering of the gate
nd the five men that went to spy out the land went up and came in thither and took the graven image and the ephod and the teraphim and the molten image and the priest stood in the entering of the gate with the six hundred men that were appointed with weapons of war
nd these went into icahs house and fetched the carved image the ephod and the teraphim and the molten image
hen said the priest unto them hat do ye
nd they said unto him old thy peace lay thine hand upon thy mouth and go with us and be to us a father and a priest is it better for thee to be a priest unto the house of one man or that thou be a priest unto a tribe and a family in srael
nd the priests heart was glad and he took the ephod and the teraphim and the graven image and went in the midst of the people
o they turned and departed and put the little ones and the cattle and the carriage before them
nd when they were a good way from the house of icah the men that were in the houses near to icahs house were gathered together and overtook the children of an
nd they cried unto the children of an
nd they turned their faces and said unto icah hat aileth thee that thou comest with such a company
nd he said e have taken away my gods which  made and the priest and ye are gone away and what have  more and what is this that ye say unto me hat aileth thee
nd the children of an said unto him et not thy voice be heard among us lest angry fellows run upon thee and thou lose thy life with the lives of thy household
nd the children of an went their way and when icah saw that they were too strong for him he turned and went back unto his house
nd they took the things which icah had made and the priest which he had and came unto aish unto a people that were at quiet and secure and they smote them with the edge of the sword and burnt the city with fire
nd there was no deliverer because it was far from idon and they had no business with any man and it was in the valley that lieth by ethrehob
nd they built a city and dwelt therein
nd they called the name of the city an after the name of an their father who was born unto srael howbeit the name of the city was aish at the first
nd the children of an set up the graven image and onathan the son of ershom the son of anasseh he and his sons were priests to the tribe of an until the day of the captivity of the land
nd they set them up icahs graven image which he made all the time that the house of od was in hiloh
nd it came to pass in those days when there was no king in srael that there was a certain evite sojourning on the side of mount phraim who took to him a concubine out of ethlehemjudah
nd his concubine played the whore against him and went away from him unto her fathers house to ethlehemjudah and was there four whole months
nd her husband arose and went after her to speak friendly unto her and to bring her again having his servant with him and a couple of asses and she brought him into her fathers house and when the father of the damsel saw him he rejoiced to meet him
nd his father in law the damsels father retained him and he abode with him three days so they did eat and drink and lodged there
nd it came to pass on the fourth day when they arose early in the morning that he rose up to depart and the damsels father said unto his son in law omfort thine heart with a morsel of bread and afterward go your way
nd they sat down and did eat and drink both of them together for the damsels father had said unto the man e content  pray thee and tarry all night and let thine heart be merry
nd when the man rose up to depart his father in law urged him therefore he lodged there again
nd he arose early in the morning on the fifth day to depart and the damsels father said omfort thine heart  pray thee
nd they tarried until afternoon and they did eat both of them
nd when the man rose up to depart he and his concubine and his servant his father in law the damsels father said unto him ehold now the day draweth toward evening  pray you tarry all night behold the day groweth to an end lodge here that thine heart may be merry and to morrow get you early on your way that thou mayest go home
ut the man would not tarry that night but he rose up and departed and came over against ebus which is erusalem and there were with him two asses saddled his concubine also was with him
nd when they were by ebus the day was far spent and the servant said unto his master ome  pray thee and let us turn in into this city of the ebusites and lodge in it
nd his master said unto him e will not turn aside hither into the city of a stranger that is not of the children of srael we will pass over to ibeah
nd he said unto his servant ome and let us draw near to one of these places to lodge all night in ibeah or in amah
nd they passed on and went their way and the sun went down upon them when they were by ibeah which belongeth to enjamin
nd they turned aside thither to go in and to lodge in ibeah and when he went in he sat him down in a street of the city for there was no man that took them into his house to lodging
nd behold there came an old man from his work out of the field at even which was also of mount phraim and he sojourned in ibeah but the men of the place were enjamites
nd when he had lifted up his eyes he saw a wayfaring man in the street of the city and the old man said hither goest thou and whence comest thou

et there is both straw and provender for our asses and there is bread and wine also for me and for thy handmaid and for the young man which is with thy servants there is no want of any thing
nd the old man said eace be with thee howsoever let all thy wants lie upon me only lodge not in the street
o he brought him into his house and gave provender unto the asses and they washed their feet and did eat and drink
ow as they were making their hearts merry behold the men of the city certain sons of elial beset the house round about and beat at the door and spake to the master of the house the old man saying ring forth the man that came into thine house that we may know him

ehold here is my daughter a maiden and his concubine them  will bring out now and humble ye them and do with them what seemeth good unto you but unto this man do not so vile a thing
ut the men would not hearken to him so the man took his concubine and brought her forth unto them and they knew her and abused her all the night until the morning and when the day began to spring they let her go
hen came the woman in the dawning of the day and fell down at the door of the mans house where her lord was till it was light
nd her lord rose up in the morning and opened the doors of the house and went out to go his way and behold the woman his concubine was fallen down at the door of the house and her hands were upon the threshold
nd he said unto her p and let us be going

nd it was so that all that saw it said here was no such deed done nor seen from the day that the children of srael came up out of the land of gypt unto this day consider of it take advice and speak your minds

o all the men of srael were gathered against the city knit together as one man

nd it shall be when their fathers or their brethren come unto us to complain that we will say unto them e favourable unto them for our sakes because we reserved not to each man his wife in the war for ye did not give unto them at this time that ye should be guilty
nd the children of enjamin did so and took them wives according to their number of them that danced whom they caught and they went and returned unto their inheritance and repaired the cities and dwelt in them

n those days there was no king in srael every man did that which was right in his own eyes
ow it came to pass in the days when the judges ruled that there was a famine in the land
nd a certain man of ethlehemjudah went to sojourn in the country of oab he and his wife and his two sons
nd the name of the man was limelech and the name of his wife aomi and the name of his two sons ahlon and hilion phrathites of ethlehemjudah
nd they came into the country of oab and continued there
nd limelech aomis husband died and she was left and her two sons
nd they took them wives of the women of oab the name of the one was rpah and the name of the other uth and they dwelled there about ten years

herefore she went forth out of the place where she was and her two daughters in law with her and they went on the way to return unto the land of udah

urn again my daughters go your way for  am too old to have an husband
f  should say  have hope if  should have an husband also to night and should also bear sons would ye tarry for them till they were grown would ye stay for them from having husbands nay my daughters for it grieveth me much for your sakes that the hand of the  is gone out against me
nd they lifted up their voice and wept again and rpah kissed her mother in law but uth clave unto her

hen she saw that she was stedfastly minded to go with her then she left speaking unto her

 went out full and the  hath brought me home again empty why then call ye me aomi seeing the  hath testified against me and the lmighty hath afflicted me
o aomi returned and uth the oabitess her daughter in law with her which returned out of the country of oab and they came to ethlehem in the beginning of barley harvest
nd aomi had a kinsman of her husbands a mighty man of wealth of the family of limelech and his name was oaz

nd she went and came and gleaned in the field after the reapers and her hap was to light on a part of the field belonging unto oaz who was of the kindred of limelech

nd they answered him he  bless thee
hen said oaz unto his servant that was set over the reapers hose damsel is this
nd the servant that was set over the reapers answered and said t is the oabitish damsel that came back with aomi out of the country of oab and she said  pray you let me glean and gather after the reapers among the sheaves so she came and hath continued even from the morning until now that she tarried a little in the house
hen said oaz unto uth earest thou not my daughter
o not to glean in another field neither go from hence but abide here fast by my maidens let thine eyes be on the field that they do reap and go thou after them have  not charged the young men that they shall not touch thee and when thou art athirst go unto the vessels and drink of that which the young men have drawn
hen she fell on her face and bowed herself to the ground and said unto him hy have  found grace in thine eyes that thou shouldest take knowledge of me seeing  am a stranger
nd oaz answered and said unto her t hath fully been shewed me all that thou hast done unto thy mother in law since the death of thine husband and how thou hast left thy father and thy mother and the land of thy nativity and art come unto a people which thou knewest not heretofore
he  recompense thy work and a full reward be given thee of the  od of srael under whose wings thou art come to trust
hen she said et me find favour in thy sight my lord for that thou hast comforted me and for that thou hast spoken friendly unto thine handmaid though  be not like unto one of thine handmaidens
nd oaz said unto her t mealtime come thou hither and eat of the bread and dip thy morsel in the vinegar
nd she sat beside the reapers and he reached her parched corn and she did eat and was sufficed and left
nd when she was risen up to glean oaz commanded his young men saying et her glean even among the sheaves and reproach her not and let fall also some of the handfuls of purpose for her and leave them that she may glean them and rebuke her not
o she gleaned in the field until even and beat out that she had gleaned and it was about an ephah of barley
nd she took it up and went into the city and her mother in law saw what she had gleaned and she brought forth and gave to her that she had reserved after she was sufficed
nd her mother in law said unto her here hast thou gleaned to day and where wroughtest thou blessed be he that did take knowledge of thee
nd she shewed her mother in law with whom she had wrought and said he mans name with whom  wrought to day is oaz

nd aomi said unto uth her daughter in law t is good my daughter that thou go out with his maidens that they meet thee not in any other field
o she kept fast by the maidens of oaz to glean unto the end of barley harvest and of wheat harvest and dwelt with her mother in law
hen aomi her mother in law said unto her y daughter shall  not seek rest for thee that it may be well with thee

nd when they were come to the land of uph aul said to his servant that was with him ome and let us return lest my father leave caring for the asses and take thought for us
nd he said unto him ehold now there is in this city a man of od and he is an honourable man all that he saith cometh surely to pass now let us go thither peradventure he can shew us our way that we should go
hen said aul to his servant ut behold if we go what shall we bring the man for the bread is spent in our vessels and there is not a present to bring to the man of od what have we
nd the servant answered aul again and said ehold  have here at hand the fourth part of a shekel of silver that will  give to the man of od to tell us our way
eforetime in srael when a man went to enquire of od thus he spake ome and let us go to the seer for he that is now called a rophet was beforetime called a eer hen said aul to his servant ell said come let us go
o they went unto the city where the man of od was
nd as they went up the hill to the city they found young maidens going out to draw water and said unto them s the seer here
nd they answered them and said e is behold he is before you make haste now for he came to day to the city for there is a sacrifice of the people to day in the high place as soon as ye be come into the city ye shall straightway find him before he go up to the high place to eat for the people will not eat until he come because he doth bless the sacrifice and afterwards they eat that be bidden
ow therefore get you up for about this time ye shall find him
nd they went up into the city and when they were come into the city behold amuel came out against them for to go up to the high place
ow the  had told amuel in his ear a day before aul came saying o morrow about this time  will send thee a man out of the land of enjamin and thou shalt anoint him to be captain over my people srael that he may save my people out of the hand of the hilistines for  have looked upon my people because their cry is come unto me
nd when amuel saw aul the  said unto him ehold the man whom  spake to thee of this same shall reign over my people
hen aul drew near to amuel in the gate and said ell me  pray thee where the seers house is
nd amuel answered aul and said  am the seer go up before me unto the high place for ye shall eat with me to day and to morrow  will let thee go and will tell thee all that is in thine heart
nd as for thine asses that were lost three days ago set not thy mind on them for they are found
nd on whom is all the desire of srael
s it not on thee and on all thy fathers house
nd aul answered and said m not  a enjamite of the smallest of the tribes of srael and my family the least of all the families of the tribe of enjamin wherefore then speakest thou so to me
nd amuel took aul and his servant and brought them into the parlour and made them sit in the chiefest place among them that were bidden which were about thirty persons
nd amuel said unto the cook ring the portion which  gave thee of which  said unto thee et it by thee
nd the cook took up the shoulder and that which was upon it and set it before aul
nd amuel said ehold that which is left set it before thee and eat for unto this time hath it been kept for thee since  said  have invited the people
o aul did eat with amuel that day
nd when they were come down from the high place into the city amuel communed with aul upon the top of the house
nd they arose early and it came to pass about the spring of the day that amuel called aul to the top of the house saying p that  may send thee away
nd aul arose and they went out both of them he and amuel abroad
nd as they were going down to the end of the city amuel said to aul id the servant pass on before us and he passed on  but stand thou still a while that  may shew thee the word of od
hen amuel took a vial of oil and poured it upon his head and kissed him and said s it not because the  hath anointed thee to be captain over his inheritance
hen thou art departed from me to day then thou shalt find two men by achels sepulchre in the border of enjamin at elzah and they will say unto thee he asses which thou wentest to seek are found and lo thy father hath left the care of the asses and sorroweth for you saying hat shall  do for my son
hen shalt thou go on forward from thence and thou shalt come to the plain of abor and there shall meet thee three men going up to od to ethel one carrying three kids and another carrying three loaves of bread and another carrying a bottle of wine and they will salute thee and give thee two loaves of bread which thou shalt receive of their hands
fter that thou shalt come to the hill of od where is the garrison of the hilistines and it shall come to pass when thou art come thither to the city that thou shalt meet a company of prophets coming down from the high place with a psaltery and a tabret and a pipe and a harp before them and they shall prophesy and the pirit of the  will come upon thee and thou shalt prophesy with them and shalt be turned into another man
nd let it be when these signs are come unto thee that thou do as occasion serve thee for od is with thee
nd thou shalt go down before me to ilgal and behold  will come down unto thee to offer burnt offerings and to sacrifice sacrifices of peace offerings seven days shalt thou tarry till  come to thee and shew thee what thou shalt do
nd it was so that when he had turned his back to go from amuel od gave him another heart and all those signs came to pass that day
nd when they came thither to the hill behold a company of prophets met him and the pirit of od came upon him and he prophesied among them
nd it came to pass when all that knew him beforetime saw that behold he prophesied among the prophets then the people said one to another hat is this that is come unto the son of ish
s aul also among the prophets
nd one of the same place answered and said ut who is their father
herefore it became a proverb s aul also among the prophets
nd when he had made an end of prophesying he came to the high place
nd auls uncle said unto him and to his servant hither went ye
nd he said o seek the asses and when we saw that they were no where we came to amuel

ut of the matter of the kingdom whereof amuel spake he told him not
nd amuel called the people together unto the  to izpeh and said unto the children of srael hus saith the  od of srael  brought up srael out of gypt and delivered you out of the hand of the gyptians and out of the hand of all kingdoms and of them that oppressed you and ye have this day rejected your od who himself saved you out of all your adversities and your tribulations and ye have said unto him ay but set a king over us
ow therefore present yourselves before the  by your tribes and by your thousands
nd when amuel had caused all the tribes of srael to come near the tribe of enjamin was taken

herefore they enquired of the  further if the man should yet come thither
nd the  answered ehold he hath hid himself among the stuff

hen ahash the mmonite came up and encamped against abeshgilead and all the men of abesh said unto ahash ake a covenant with us and we will serve thee
nd ahash the mmonite answered them n this condition will  make a covenant with you that  may thrust out all your right eyes and lay it for a reproach upon all srael
nd the elders of abesh said unto him ive us seven days respite that we may send messengers unto all the coasts of srael and then if there be no man to save us we will come out to thee
hen came the messengers to ibeah of aul and told the tidings in the ears of the people and all the people lifted up their voices and wept
nd behold aul came after the herd out of the field and aul said hat aileth the people that they weep
nd they told him the tidings of the men of abesh
nd the pirit of od came upon aul when he heard those tidings and his anger was kindled greatly
nd he took a yoke of oxen and hewed them in pieces and sent them throughout all the coasts of srael by the hands of messengers saying hosoever cometh not forth after aul and after amuel so shall it be done unto his oxen

nd when he numbered them in ezek the children of srael were three hundred thousand and the men of udah thirty thousand
nd they said unto the messengers that came hus shall ye say unto the men of abeshgilead o morrow by that time the sun be hot ye shall have help
nd the messengers came and shewed it to the men of abesh and they were glad
herefore the men of abesh said o morrow we will come out unto you and ye shall do with us all that seemeth good unto you
nd it was so on the morrow that aul put the people in three companies and they came into the midst of the host in the morning watch and slew the mmonites until the heat of the day and it came to pass that they which remained were scattered so that two of them were not left together
nd the people said unto amuel ho is he that said hall aul reign over us bring the men that we may put them to death
nd aul said here shall not a man be put to death this day for to day the  hath wrought salvation in srael
hen said amuel to the people ome and let us go to ilgal and renew the kingdom there

nd amuel said unto all srael ehold  have hearkened unto your voice in all that ye said unto me and have made a king over you
nd now behold the king walketh before you and  am old and grayheaded and behold my sons are with you and  have walked before you from my childhood unto this day
ehold here  am witness against me before the  and before his anointed whose ox have  taken or whose ass have  taken or whom have  defrauded whom have  oppressed or of whose hand have  received any bribe to blind mine eyes therewith and  will restore it you
nd they said hou hast not defrauded us nor oppressed us neither hast thou taken ought of any mans hand

nd they answered e is witness
nd amuel said unto the people t is the  that advanced oses and aron and that brought your fathers up out of the land of gypt

hen acob was come into gypt and your fathers cried unto the  then the  sent oses and aron which brought forth your fathers out of gypt and made them dwell in this place

nd when ye saw that ahash the king of the children of mmon came against you ye said unto me ay but a king shall reign over us when the  your od was your king
ow therefore behold the king whom ye have chosen and whom ye have desired and behold the  hath set a king over you
f ye will fear the  and serve him and obey his voice and not rebel against the commandment of the  then shall both ye and also the king that reigneth over you continue following the  your od but if ye will not obey the voice of the  but rebel against the commandment of the  then shall the hand of the  be against you as it was against your fathers
ow therefore stand and see this great thing which the  will do before your eyes
s it not wheat harvest to day
 will call unto the  and he shall send thunder and rain that ye may perceive and see that your wickedness is great which ye have done in the sight of the  in asking you a king
o amuel called unto the  and the  sent thunder and rain that day and all the people greatly feared the  and amuel
nd all the people said unto amuel ray for thy servants unto the  thy od that we die not for we have added unto all our sins this evil to ask us a king
nd amuel said unto the people ear not ye have done all this wickedness yet turn not aside from following the  but serve the  with all your heart and turn ye not aside for then should ye go after vain things which cannot profit nor deliver for they are vain
or the  will not forsake his people for his great names sake because it hath pleased the  to make you his people
oreover as for me od forbid that  should sin against the  in ceasing to pray for you but  will teach you the good and the right way only fear the  and serve him in truth with all your heart for consider how great things he hath done for you
ut if ye shall still do wickedly ye shall be consumed both ye and your king
aul reigned one year and when he had reigned two years over srael aul chose him three thousand men of srael whereof two thousand were with aul in ichmash and in mount ethel and a thousand were with onathan in ibeah of enjamin and the rest of the people he sent every man to his tent
nd onathan smote the garrison of the hilistines that was in eba and the hilistines heard of it
nd aul blew the trumpet throughout all the land saying et the ebrews hear
nd all srael heard say that aul had smitten a garrison of the hilistines and that srael also was had in abomination with the hilistines
nd the people were called together after aul to ilgal
nd the hilistines gathered themselves together to fight with srael thirty thousand chariots and six thousand horsemen and people as the sand which is on the sea shore in multitude and they came up and pitched in ichmash eastward from ethaven
hen the men of srael saw that they were in a strait for the people were distressed  then the people did hide themselves in caves and in thickets and in rocks and in high places and in pits
nd some of the ebrews went over ordan to the land of ad and ilead
s for aul he was yet in ilgal and all the people followed him trembling
nd he tarried seven days according to the set time that amuel had appointed but amuel came not to ilgal and the people were scattered from him
nd aul said ring hither a burnt offering to me and peace offerings
nd he offered the burnt offering
nd it came to pass that as soon as he had made an end of offering the burnt offering behold amuel came and aul went out to meet him that he might salute him
nd amuel said hat hast thou done
nd aul said ecause  saw that the people were scattered from me and that thou camest not within the days appointed and that the hilistines gathered themselves together at ichmash therefore said  he hilistines will come down now upon me to ilgal and  have not made supplication unto the   forced myself therefore and offered a burnt offering
nd amuel said to aul hou hast done foolishly thou hast not kept the commandment of the  thy od which he commanded thee for now would the  have established thy kingdom upon srael for ever
ut now thy kingdom shall not continue the  hath sought him a man after his own heart and the  hath commanded him to be captain over his people because thou hast not kept that which the  commanded thee
nd amuel arose and gat him up from ilgal unto ibeah of enjamin
nd aul numbered the people that were present with him about six hundred men
nd aul and onathan his son and the people that were present with them abode in ibeah of enjamin but the hilistines encamped in ichmash
nd the spoilers came out of the camp of the hilistines in three companies one company turned unto the way that leadeth to phrah unto the land of hual and another company turned the way to ethhoron and another company turned to the way of the border that looketh to the valley of eboim toward the wilderness
ow there was no smith found throughout all the land of srael for the hilistines said est the ebrews make them swords or spears but all the sraelites went down to the hilistines to sharpen every man his share and his coulter and his axe and his mattock
et they had a file for the mattocks and for the coulters and for the forks and for the axes and to sharpen the goads
o it came to pass in the day of battle that there was neither sword nor spear found in the hand of any of the people that were with aul and onathan but with aul and with onathan his son was there found
nd the garrison of the hilistines went out to the passage of ichmash
ow it came to pass upon a day that onathan the son of aul said unto the young man that bare his armour ome and let us go over to the hilistines garrison that is on the other side
ut he told not his father
nd aul tarried in the uttermost part of ibeah under a pomegranate tree which is in igron and the people that were with him were about six hundred men and hiah the son of hitub chabods brother the son of hinehas the son of li the s priest in hiloh wearing an ephod
nd the people knew not that onathan was gone
nd between the passages by which onathan sought to go over unto the hilistines garrison there was a sharp rock on the one side and a sharp rock on the other side and the name of the one was ozez and the name of the other eneh
he forefront of the one was situate northward over against ichmash and the other southward over against ibeah
nd onathan said to the young man that bare his armour ome and let us go over unto the garrison of these uncircumcised it may be that the  will work for us for there is no restraint to the  to save by many or by few

hen said onathan ehold we will pass over unto these men and we will discover ourselves unto them
f they say thus unto us arry until we come to you then we will stand still in our place and will not go up unto them
ut if they say thus ome up unto us then we will go up for the  hath delivered them into our hand and this shall be a sign unto us
nd both of them discovered themselves unto the garrison of the hilistines and the hilistines said ehold the ebrews come forth out of the holes where they had hid themselves
nd the men of the garrison answered onathan and his armourbearer and said ome up to us and we will shew you a thing
nd onathan said unto his armourbearer ome up after me for the  hath delivered them into the hand of srael
nd onathan climbed up upon his hands and upon his feet and his armourbearer after him and they fell before onathan and his armourbearer slew after him
nd that first slaughter which onathan and his armourbearer made was about twenty men within as it were an half acre of land which a yoke of oxen might plow
nd there was trembling in the host in the field and among all the people the garrison and the spoilers they also trembled and the earth quaked so it was a very great trembling
nd the watchmen of aul in ibeah of enjamin looked and behold the multitude melted away and they went on beating down one another

nd aul and all the people that were with him assembled themselves and they came to the battle and behold every mans sword was against his fellow and there was a very great discomfiture
oreover the ebrews that were with the hilistines before that time which went up with them into the camp from the country round about even they also turned to be with the sraelites that were with aul and onathan
ikewise all the men of srael which had hid themselves in mount phraim when they heard that the hilistines fled even they also followed hard after them in the battle
o the  saved srael that day and the battle passed over unto ethaven
nd the men of srael were distressed that day for aul had adjured the people saying ursed be the man that eateth any food until evening that  may be avenged on mine enemies
o none of the people tasted any food
nd all they of the land came to a wood and there was honey upon the ground
nd when the people were come into the wood behold the honey dropped but no man put his hand to his mouth for the people feared the oath
ut onathan heard not when his father charged the people with the oath wherefore he put forth the end of the rod that was in his hand and dipped it in an honeycomb and put his hand to his mouth and his eyes were enlightened
hen answered one of the people and said hy father straitly charged the people with an oath saying ursed be the man that eateth any food this day
nd the people were faint
hen said onathan y father hath troubled the land see  pray you how mine eyes have been enlightened because  tasted a little of this honey
ow much more if haply the people had eaten freely to day of the spoil of their enemies which they found for had there not been now a much greater slaughter among the hilistines
nd they smote the hilistines that day from ichmash to ijalon and the people were very faint
nd the people flew upon the spoil and took sheep and oxen and calves and slew them on the ground and the people did eat them with the blood

nd ish was the father of aul and er the father of bner was the son of biel

nd he sent and brought him in
ow he was ruddy and withal of a beautiful countenance and goodly to look to
nd the  said rise anoint him for this is he
hen amuel took the horn of oil and anointed him in the midst of his brethren and the pirit of the  came upon avid from that day forward
o amuel rose up and went to amah
ut the pirit of the  departed from aul and an evil spirit from the  troubled him
nd auls servants said unto him ehold now an evil spirit from od troubleth thee
et our lord now command thy servants which are before thee to seek out a man who is a cunning player on an harp and it shall come to pass when the evil spirit from od is upon thee that he shall play with his hand and thou shalt be well
nd aul said unto his servants rovide me now a man that can play well and bring him to me
hen answered one of the servants and said ehold  have seen a son of esse the ethlehemite that is cunning in playing and a mighty valiant man and a man of war and prudent in matters and a comely person and the  is with him
herefore aul sent messengers unto esse and said end me avid thy son which is with the sheep
nd esse took an ass laden with bread and a bottle of wine and a kid and sent them by avid his son unto aul
nd avid came to aul and stood before him and he loved him greatly and he became his armourbearer
nd aul sent to esse saying et avid  pray thee stand before me for he hath found favour in my sight
nd it came to pass when the evil spirit from od was upon aul that avid took an harp and played with his hand so aul was refreshed and was well and the evil spirit departed from him
ow the hilistines gathered together their armies to battle and were gathered together at hochoh which belongeth to udah and pitched between hochoh and zekah in phesdammim
nd aul and the men of srael were gathered together and pitched by the valley of lah and set the battle in array against the hilistines
nd the hilistines stood on a mountain on the one side and srael stood on a mountain on the other side and there was a valley between them
nd there went out a champion out of the camp of the hilistines named oliath of ath whose height was six cubits and a span
nd he had an helmet of brass upon his head and he was armed with a coat of mail and the weight of the coat was five thousand shekels of brass
nd he had greaves of brass upon his legs and a target of brass between his shoulders
nd the staff of his spear was like a weavers beam and his spears head weighed six hundred shekels of iron and one bearing a shield went before him
nd he stood and cried unto the armies of srael and said unto them hy are ye come out to set your battle in array am not  a hilistine and ye servants to aul choose you a man for you and let him come down to me
f he be able to fight with me and to kill me then will we be your servants but if  prevail against him and kill him then shall ye be our servants and serve us
nd the hilistine said  defy the armies of srael this day give me a man that we may fight together
hen aul and all srael heard those words of the hilistine they were dismayed and greatly afraid
ow avid was the son of that phrathite of ethlehemjudah whose name was esse and he had eight sons and the man went among men for an old man in the days of aul
nd the three eldest sons of esse went and followed aul to the battle and the names of his three sons that went to the battle were liab the firstborn and next unto him binadab and the third hammah
nd avid was the youngest and the three eldest followed aul
ut avid went and returned from aul to feed his fathers sheep at ethlehem
nd the hilistine drew near morning and evening and presented himself forty days
nd esse said unto avid his son ake now for thy brethren an ephah of this parched corn and these ten loaves and run to the camp to thy brethren and carry these ten cheeses unto the captain of their thousand and look how thy brethren fare and take their pledge
ow aul and they and all the men of srael were in the valley of lah fighting with the hilistines
nd avid rose up early in the morning and left the sheep with a keeper and took and went as esse had commanded him and he came to the trench as the host was going forth to the fight and shouted for the battle
or srael and the hilistines had put the battle in array army against army
nd avid left his carriage in the hand of the keeper of the carriage and ran into the army and came and saluted his brethren
nd as he talked with them behold there came up the champion the hilistine of ath oliath by name out of the armies of the hilistines and spake according to the same words and avid heard them
nd all the men of srael when they saw the man fled from him and were sore afraid
nd the men of srael said ave ye seen this man that is come up surely to defy srael is he come up and it shall be that the man who killeth him the king will enrich him with great riches and will give him his daughter and make his fathers house free in srael
nd avid spake to the men that stood by him saying hat shall be done to the man that killeth this hilistine and taketh away the reproach from srael for who is this uncircumcised hilistine that he should defy the armies of the living od
nd the people answered him after this manner saying o shall it be done to the man that killeth him
nd liab his eldest brother heard when he spake unto the men and liabs anger was kindled against avid and he said hy camest thou down hither and with whom hast thou left those few sheep in the wilderness
 know thy pride and the naughtiness of thine heart for thou art come down that thou mightest see the battle
nd avid said hat have  now done
s there not a cause
nd he turned from him toward another and spake after the same manner and the people answered him again after the former manner

nd avid said to aul et no mans heart fail because of him thy servant will go and fight with this hilistine
nd aul said to avid hou art not able to go against this hilistine to fight with him for thou art but a youth and he a man of war from his youth
nd avid said unto aul hy servant kept his fathers sheep and there came a lion and a bear and took a lamb out of the flock and  went out after him and smote him and delivered it out of his mouth and when he arose against me  caught him by his beard and smote him and slew him
hy servant slew both the lion and the bear and this uncircumcised hilistine shall be as one of them seeing he hath defied the armies of the living od
avid said moreover he  that delivered me out of the paw of the lion and out of the paw of the bear he will deliver me out of the hand of this hilistine
nd aul said unto avid o and the  be with thee
nd aul armed avid with his armour and he put an helmet of brass upon his head also he armed him with a coat of mail
nd avid girded his sword upon his armour and he assayed to go for he had not proved it
nd avid said unto aul  cannot go with these for  have not proved them
nd avid put them off him
nd he took his staff in his hand and chose him five smooth stones out of the brook and put them in a shepherds bag which he had even in a scrip and his sling was in his hand and he drew near to the hilistine
nd the hilistine came on and drew near unto avid and the man that bare the shield went before him
nd when the hilistine looked about and saw avid he disdained him for he was but a youth and ruddy and of a fair countenance

nd the hilistine cursed avid by his gods

nd avid put his hand in his bag and took thence a stone and slang it and smote the hilistine in his forehead that the stone sunk into his forehead and he fell upon his face to the earth
o avid prevailed over the hilistine with a sling and with a stone and smote the hilistine and slew him but there was no sword in the hand of avid
herefore avid ran and stood upon the hilistine and took his sword and drew it out of the sheath thereof and slew him and cut off his head therewith
nd when the hilistines saw their champion was dead they fled
nd the men of srael and of udah arose and shouted and pursued the hilistines until thou come to the valley and to the gates of kron

hen onathan and avid made a covenant because he loved him as his own soul
nd onathan stripped himself of the robe that was upon him and gave it to avid and his garments even to his sword and to his bow and to his girdle
nd avid went out whithersoever aul sent him and behaved himself wisely and aul set him over the men of war and he was accepted in the sight of all the people and also in the sight of auls servants
nd it came to pass as they came when avid was returned from the slaughter of the hilistine that the women came out of all cities of srael singing and dancing to meet king aul with tabrets with joy and with instruments of musick
nd the women answered one another as they played and said aul hath slain his thousands nd avid his ten thousands
nd aul was very wroth and the saying displeased him and he said hey have ascribed unto avid ten thousands and to me they have ascribed but thousands and what can he have more but the kingdom
nd aul eyed avid from that day and forward

nd aul said lessed be ye of the  for ye have compassion on me
o  pray you prepare yet and know and see his place where his haunt is and who hath seen him there for it is told me that he dealeth very subtilly
ee therefore and take knowledge of all the lurking places where he hideth himself and come ye again to me with the certainty and  will go with you and it shall come to pass if he be in the land that  will search him out throughout all the thousands of udah
nd they arose and went to iph before aul but avid and his men were in the wilderness of aon in the plain on the south of eshimon
aul also and his men went to seek him
nd they told avid wherefore he came down into a rock and abode in the wilderness of aon
nd when aul heard that he pursued after avid in the wilderness of aon
nd aul went on this side of the mountain and avid and his men on that side of the mountain and avid made haste to get away for fear of aul for aul and his men compassed avid and his men round about to take them
ut there came a messenger unto aul saying aste thee and come for the hilistines have invaded the land
herefore aul returned from pursuing after avid and went against the hilistines therefore they called that place elahammahlekoth
nd avid went up from thence and dwelt in strong holds at ngedi
nd it came to pass when aul was returned from following the hilistines that it was told him saying ehold avid is in the wilderness of ngedi
hen aul took three thousand chosen men out of all srael and went to seek avid and his men upon the rocks of the wild goats
nd he came to the sheepcotes by the way where was a cave and aul went in to cover his feet and avid and his men remained in the sides of the cave
nd the men of avid said unto him ehold the day of which the  said unto thee ehold  will deliver thine enemy into thine hand that thou mayest do to him as it shall seem good unto thee
hen avid arose and cut off the skirt of auls robe privily

o avid stayed his servants with these words and suffered them not to rise against aul

nd when aul looked behind him avid stooped with his face to the earth and bowed himself

ehold this day thine eyes have seen how that the  had delivered thee to day into mine hand in the cave and some bade me kill thee but mine eye spared thee and  said  will not put forth mine hand against my lord for he is the s anointed
oreover my father see yea see the skirt of thy robe in my hand for in that  cut off the skirt of thy robe and killed thee not know thou and see that there is neither evil nor transgression in mine hand and  have not sinned against thee yet thou huntest my soul to take it
he  judge between me and thee and the  avenge me of thee but mine hand shall not be upon thee
s saith the proverb of the ancients ickedness proceedeth from the wicked but mine hand shall not be upon thee
fter whom is the king of srael come out after whom dost thou pursue after a dead dog after a flea
he  therefore be judge and judge between me and thee and see and plead my cause and deliver me out of thine hand

nd now behold  know well that thou shalt surely be king and that the kingdom of srael shall be established in thine hand

nd amuel died and all the sraelites were gathered together and lamented him and buried him in his house at amah
nd avid arose and went down to the wilderness of aran
nd there was a man in aon whose possessions were in armel and the man was very great and he had three thousand sheep and a thousand goats and he was shearing his sheep in armel
ow the name of the man was abal and the name of his wife bigail and she was a woman of good understanding and of a beautiful countenance but the man was churlish and evil in his doings and he was of the house of aleb
nd avid heard in the wilderness that abal did shear his sheep
nd avid sent out ten young men and avid said unto the young men et you up to armel and go to abal and greet him in my name and thus shall ye say to him that liveth in prosperity eace be both to thee and peace be to thine house and peace be unto all that thou hast

ut the men were very good unto us and we were not hurt neither missed we any thing as long as we were conversant with them when we were in the fields they were a wall unto us both by night and day all the while we were with them keeping the sheep
ow therefore know and consider what thou wilt do for evil is determined against our master and against all his household for he is such a son of elial that a man cannot speak to him

o avid and his men came to the city and behold it was burned with fire and their wives and their sons and their daughters were taken captives

nd avid recovered all that the malekites had carried away and avid rescued his two wives

herefore aul took a sword and fell upon it

nd when the men of srael that were on the other side of the valley and they that were on the other side ordan saw that the men of srael fled and that aul and his sons were dead they forsook the cities and fled and the hilistines came and dwelt in them
nd it came to pass on the morrow when the hilistines came to strip the slain that they found aul and his three sons fallen in mount ilboa
nd they cut off his head and stripped off his armour and sent into the land of the hilistines round about to publish it in the house of their idols and among the people

nd they took their bones and buried them under a tree at abesh and fasted seven days
ow it came to pass after the death of aul when avid was returned from the slaughter of the malekites and avid had abode two days in iklag it came even to pass on the third day that behold a man came out of the camp from aul with his clothes rent and earth upon his head and so it was when he came to avid that he fell to the earth and did obeisance

nd avid sent and enquired after the woman

nd avid sent messengers and took her and she came in unto him and he lay with her for she was purified from her uncleanness and she returned unto her house
nd the woman conceived and sent and told avid and said  am with child
nd avid sent to oab saying end me riah the ittite
nd oab sent riah to avid
nd when riah was come unto him avid demanded of him how oab did and how the people did and how the war prospered
nd avid said to riah o down to thy house and wash thy feet
nd riah departed out of the kings house and there followed him a mess of meat from the king
ut riah slept at the door of the kings house with all the servants of his lord and went not down to his house
nd when they had told avid saying riah went not down unto his house avid said unto riah amest thou not from thy journey why then didst thou not go down unto thine house
nd riah said unto avid he ark and srael and udah abide in tents and my lord oab and the servants of my lord are encamped in the open fields shall  then go into mine house to eat and to drink and to lie with my wife as thou livest and as thy soul liveth  will not do this thing
nd avid said to riah arry here to day also and to morrow  will let thee depart
o riah abode in erusalem that day and the morrow
nd when avid had called him he did eat and drink before him and he made him drunk and at even he went out to lie on his bed with the servants of his lord but went not down to his house
nd it came to pass in the morning that avid wrote a letter to oab and sent it by the hand of riah
nd he wrote in the letter saying et ye riah in the forefront of the hottest battle and retire ye from him that he may be smitten and die
nd it came to pass when oab observed the city that he assigned riah unto a place where he knew that valiant men were
nd the men of the city went out and fought with oab and there fell some of the people of the servants of avid and riah the ittite died also
hen oab sent and told avid all the things concerning the war and charged the messenger saying hen thou hast made an end of telling the matters of the war unto the king and if so be that the kings wrath arise and he say unto thee herefore approached ye so nigh unto the city when ye did fight knew ye not that they would shoot from the wall
ho smote bimelech the son of erubbesheth did not a woman cast a piece of a millstone upon him from the wall that he died in hebez why went ye nigh the wall then say thou hy servant riah the ittite is dead also
o the messenger went and came and shewed avid all that oab had sent him for
nd the messenger said unto avid urely the men prevailed against us and came out unto us into the field and we were upon them even unto the entering of the gate
nd the shooters shot from off the wall upon thy servants and some of the kings servants be dead and thy servant riah the ittite is dead also
hen avid said unto the messenger hus shalt thou say unto oab et not this thing displease thee for the sword devoureth one as well as another make thy battle more strong against the city and overthrow it and encourage thou him
nd when the wife of riah heard that riah her husband was dead she mourned for her husband
nd when the mourning was past avid sent and fetched her to his house and she became his wife and bare him a son
ut the thing that avid had done displeased the 
nd the  sent athan unto avid
nd he came unto him and said unto him here were two men in one city the one rich and the other poor
he rich man had exceeding many flocks and herds but the poor man had nothing save one little ewe lamb which he had bought and nourished up and it grew up together with him and with his children it did eat of his own meat and drank of his own cup and lay in his bosom and was unto him as a daughter
nd there came a traveller unto the rich man and he spared to take of his own flock and of his own herd to dress for the wayfaring man that was come unto him but took the poor mans lamb and dressed it for the man that was come to him
nd avids anger was greatly kindled against the man and he said to athan s the  liveth the man that hath done this thing shall surely die and he shall restore the lamb fourfold because he did this thing and because he had no pity
nd athan said to avid hou art the man
hus saith the  od of srael  anointed thee king over srael and  delivered thee out of the hand of aul and  gave thee thy masters house and thy masters wives into thy bosom and gave thee the house of srael and of udah and if that had been too little  would moreover have given unto thee such and such things
herefore hast thou despised the commandment of the  to do evil in his sight thou hast killed riah the ittite with the sword and hast taken his wife to be thy wife and hast slain him with the sword of the children of mmon
ow therefore the sword shall never depart from thine house because thou hast despised me and hast taken the wife of riah the ittite to be thy wife
hus saith the  ehold  will raise up evil against thee out of thine own house and  will take thy wives before thine eyes and give them unto thy neighbour and he shall lie with thy wives in the sight of this sun
or thou didst it secretly but  will do this thing before all srael and before the sun
nd avid said unto athan  have sinned against the 
nd athan said unto avid he  also hath put away thy sin thou shalt not die
owbeit because by this deed thou hast given great occasion to the enemies of the  to blaspheme the child also that is born unto thee shall surely die

nd the  struck the child that riahs wife bare unto avid and it was very sick
avid therefore besought od for the child and avid fasted and went in and lay all night upon the earth
nd the elders of his house arose and went to him to raise him up from the earth but he would not neither did he eat bread with them
nd it came to pass on the seventh day that the child died
nd the servants of avid feared to tell him that the child was dead for they said ehold while the child was yet alive we spake unto him and he would not hearken unto our voice how will he then vex himself if we tell him that the child is dead
ut when avid saw that his servants whispered avid perceived that the child was dead therefore avid said unto his servants s the child dead
nd they said e is dead
hen avid arose from the earth and washed and anointed himself and changed his apparel and came into the house of the  and worshipped then he came to his own house and when he required they set bread before him and he did eat
hen said his servants unto him hat thing is this that thou hast done thou didst fast and weep for the child while it was alive but when the child was dead thou didst rise and eat bread
nd he said hile the child was yet alive  fasted and wept for  said ho can tell whether  will be gracious to me that the child may live
ut now he is dead wherefore should  fast can  bring him back again

nd oab fought against abbah of the children of mmon and took the royal city
