ow therefore gather the rest of the people together and encamp against the city and take it lest  take the city and it be called after my name
nd avid gathered all the people together and went to abbah and fought against it and took it
nd he took their kings crown from off his head the weight whereof was a talent of gold with the precious stones and it was set on avids head
nd he brought forth the spoil of the city in great abundance
nd he brought forth the people that were therein and put them under saws and under harrows of iron and under axes of iron and made them pass through the brickkiln and thus did he unto all the cities of the children of mmon
o avid and all the people returned unto erusalem
nd it came to pass after this that bsalom the son of avid had a fair sister whose name was amar and mnon the son of avid loved her
nd mnon was so vexed that he fell sick for his sister amar for she was a virgin and mnon thought it hard for him to do any thing to her
ut mnon had a friend whose name was onadab the son of himeah avids brother and onadab was a very subtil man
nd he said unto him hy art thou being the kings son lean from day to day wilt thou not tell me
nd mnon said unto him  love amar my brother bsaloms sister
nd onadab said unto him ay thee down on thy bed and make thyself sick and when thy father cometh to see thee say unto him  pray thee let my sister amar come and give me meat and dress the meat in my sight that  may see it and eat it at her hand
o mnon lay down and made himself sick and when the king was come to see him mnon said unto the king  pray thee let amar my sister come and make me a couple of cakes in my sight that  may eat at her hand
hen avid sent home to amar saying o now to thy brother mnons house and dress him meat
o amar went to her brother mnons house and he was laid down
nd she took flour and kneaded it and made cakes in his sight and did bake the cakes
nd she took a pan and poured them out before him but he refused to eat
nd mnon said ave out all men from me
nd they went out every man from him
nd mnon said unto amar ring the meat into the chamber that  may eat of thine hand
nd amar took the cakes which she had made and brought them into the chamber to mnon her brother
nd when she had brought them unto him to eat he took hold of her and said unto her ome lie with me my sister
nd she answered him ay my brother do not force me for no such thing ought to be done in srael do not thou this folly
nd  whither shall  cause my shame to go and as for thee thou shalt be as one of the fools in srael
ow therefore  pray thee speak unto the king for he will not withhold me from thee
owbeit he would not hearken unto her voice but being stronger than she forced her and lay with her
hen mnon hated her exceedingly so that the hatred wherewith he hated her was greater than the love wherewith he had loved her
nd mnon said unto her rise be gone
nd she said unto him here is no cause this evil in sending me away is greater than the other that thou didst unto me
ut he would not hearken unto her
hen he called his servant that ministered unto him and said ut now this woman out from me and bolt the door after her
nd she had a garment of divers colours upon her for with such robes were the kings daughters that were virgins apparelled
hen his servant brought her out and bolted the door after her
nd amar put ashes on her head and rent her garment of divers colours that was on her and laid her hand on her head and went on crying
nd bsalom her brother said unto her ath mnon thy brother been with thee but hold now thy peace my sister he is thy brother regard not this thing
o amar remained desolate in her brother bsaloms house
ut when king avid heard of all these things he was very wroth
nd bsalom spake unto his brother mnon neither good nor bad for bsalom hated mnon because he had forced his sister amar
nd it came to pass after two full years that bsalom had sheepshearers in aalhazor which is beside phraim and bsalom invited all the kings sons
nd bsalom came to the king and said ehold now thy servant hath sheepshearers let the king  beseech thee and his servants go with thy servant
nd the king said to bsalom ay my son let us not all now go lest we be chargeable unto thee
nd he pressed him howbeit he would not go but blessed him
hen said bsalom f not  pray thee let my brother mnon go with us
nd the king said unto him hy should he go with thee
ut bsalom pressed him that he let mnon and all the kings sons go with him
ow bsalom had commanded his servants saying ark ye now when mnons heart is merry with wine and when  say unto you mite mnon then kill him fear not have not  commanded you be courageous and be valiant
nd the servants of bsalom did unto mnon as bsalom had commanded
hen all the kings sons arose and every man gat him up upon his mule and fled
nd it came to pass while they were in the way that tidings came to avid saying bsalom hath slain all the kings sons and there is not one of them left
hen the king arose and tare his garments and lay on the earth and all his servants stood by with their clothes rent
nd onadab the son of himeah avids brother answered and said et not my lord suppose that they have slain all the young men the kings sons for mnon only is dead for by the appointment of bsalom this hath been determined from the day that he forced his sister amar
ow therefore let not my lord the king take the thing to his heart to think that all the kings sons are dead for mnon only is dead
ut bsalom fled
nd the young man that kept the watch lifted up his eyes and looked and behold there came much people by the way of the hill side behind him
nd onadab said unto the king ehold the kings sons come as thy servant said so it is
nd it came to pass as soon as he had made an end of speaking that behold the kings sons came and lifted up their voice and wept and the king also and all his servants wept very sore
ut bsalom fled and went to almai the son of mmihud king of eshur
nd avid mourned for his son every day
o bsalom fled and went to eshur and was there three years
nd the soul of king avid longed to go forth unto bsalom for he was comforted concerning mnon seeing he was dead
ow oab the son of eruiah perceived that the kings heart was toward bsalom
nd oab sent to ekoah and fetched thence a wise woman and said unto her  pray thee feign thyself to be a mourner and put on now mourning apparel and anoint not thyself with oil but be as a woman that had a long time mourned for the dead and come to the king and speak on this manner unto him
o oab put the words in her mouth
nd when the woman of ekoah spake to the king she fell on her face to the ground and did obeisance and said elp  king
nd the king said unto her hat aileth thee
nd she answered  am indeed a widow woman and mine husband is dead
nd thy handmaid had two sons and they two strove together in the field and there was none to part them but the one smote the other and slew him
nd behold the whole family is risen against thine handmaid and they said eliver him that smote his brother that we may kill him for the life of his brother whom he slew and we will destroy the heir also and so they shall quench my coal which is left and shall not leave to my husband neither name nor remainder upon the earth
nd the king said unto the woman o to thine house and  will give charge concerning thee
nd the woman of ekoah said unto the king y lord  king the iniquity be on me and on my fathers house and the king and his throne be guiltless
nd the king said hosoever saith ought unto thee bring him to me and he shall not touch thee any more
hen said she  pray thee let the king remember the  thy od that thou wouldest not suffer the revengers of blood to destroy any more lest they destroy my son
nd he said s the  liveth there shall not one hair of thy son fall to the earth
hen the woman said et thine handmaid  pray thee speak one word unto my lord the king
nd he said ay on
nd the woman said herefore then hast thou thought such a thing against the people of od for the king doth speak this thing as one which is faulty in that the king doth not fetch home again his banished
or we must needs die and are as water spilt on the ground which cannot be gathered up again neither doth od respect any person yet doth he devise means that his banished be not expelled from him
ow therefore that  am come to speak of this thing unto my lord the king it is because the people have made me afraid and thy handmaid said  will now speak unto the king it may be that the king will perform the request of his handmaid
or the king will hear to deliver his handmaid out of the hand of the man that would destroy me and my son together out of the inheritance of od
hen thine handmaid said he word of my lord the king shall now be comfortable for as an angel of od so is my lord the king to discern good and bad therefore the  thy od will be with thee
hen the king answered and said unto the woman ide not from me  pray thee the thing that  shall ask thee
nd the woman said et my lord the king now speak
nd the king said s not the hand of oab with thee in all this
nd the woman answered and said s thy soul liveth my lord the king none can turn to the right hand or to the left from ought that my lord the king hath spoken for thy servant oab he bade me and he put all these words in the mouth of thine handmaid to fetch about this form of speech hath thy servant oab done this thing and my lord is wise according to the wisdom of an angel of od to know all things that are in the earth
nd the king said unto oab ehold now  have done this thing go therefore bring the young man bsalom again
nd oab fell to the ground on his face and bowed himself and thanked the king and oab said o day thy servant knoweth that  have found grace in thy sight my lord  king in that the king hath fulfilled the request of his servant
o oab arose and went to eshur and brought bsalom to erusalem
nd the king said et him turn to his own house and let him not see my face
o bsalom returned to his own house and saw not the kings face
ut in all srael there was none to be so much praised as bsalom for his beauty from the sole of his foot even to the crown of his head there was no blemish in him
nd when he polled his head for it was at every years end that he polled it because the hair was heavy on him therefore he polled it he weighed the hair of his head at two hundred shekels after the kings weight
nd unto bsalom there were born three sons and one daughter whose name was amar she was a woman of a fair countenance
o bsalom dwelt two full years in erusalem and saw not the kings face
herefore bsalom sent for oab to have sent him to the king but he would not come to him and when he sent again the second time he would not come
herefore he said unto his servants ee oabs field is near mine and he hath barley there go and set it on fire
nd bsaloms servants set the field on fire
hen oab arose and came to bsalom unto his house and said unto him herefore have thy servants set my field on fire
nd bsalom answered oab ehold  sent unto thee saying ome hither that  may send thee to the king to say herefore am  come from eshur it had been good for me to have been there still now therefore let me see the kings face and if there be any iniquity in me let him kill me
o oab came to the king and told him and when he had called for bsalom he came to the king and bowed himself on his face to the ground before the king and the king kissed bsalom
nd it came to pass after this that bsalom prepared him chariots and horses and fifty men to run before him
nd bsalom rose up early and stood beside the way of the gate and it was so that when any man that had a controversy came to the king for judgment then bsalom called unto him and said f what city art thou
nd he said hy servant is of one of the tribes of srael
nd bsalom said unto him ee thy matters are good and right but there is no man deputed of the king to hear thee
bsalom said moreover h that  were made judge in the land that every man which hath any suit or cause might come unto me and  would do him justice
nd it was so that when any man came nigh to him to do him obeisance he put forth his hand and took him and kissed him
nd on this manner did bsalom to all srael that came to the king for judgment so bsalom stole the hearts of the men of srael
nd it came to pass after forty years that bsalom said unto the king  pray thee let me go and pay my vow which  have vowed unto the  in ebron
or thy servant vowed a vow while  abode at eshur in yria saying f the  shall bring me again indeed to erusalem then  will serve the 
nd the king said unto him o in peace
o he arose and went to ebron
ut bsalom sent spies throughout all the tribes of srael saying s soon as ye hear the sound of the trumpet then ye shall say bsalom reigneth in ebron
nd with bsalom went two hundred men out of erusalem that were called and they went in their simplicity and they knew not any thing
nd bsalom sent for hithophel the ilonite avids counsellor from his city even from iloh while he offered sacrifices
nd the conspiracy was strong for the people increased continually with bsalom
nd there came a messenger to avid saying he hearts of the men of srael are after bsalom
nd avid said unto all his servants that were with him at erusalem rise and let us flee for we shall not else escape from bsalom make speed to depart lest he overtake us suddenly and bring evil upon us and smite the city with the edge of the sword
nd the kings servants said unto the king ehold thy servants are ready to do whatsoever my lord the king shall appoint
nd the king went forth and all his household after him
nd the king left ten women which were concubines to keep the house
nd the king went forth and all the people after him and tarried in a place that was far off
nd all his servants passed on beside him and all the herethites and all the elethites and all the ittites six hundred men which came after him from ath passed on before the king
hen said the king to ttai the ittite herefore goest thou also with us return to thy place and abide with the king for thou art a stranger and also an exile
hereas thou camest but yesterday should  this day make thee go up and down with us seeing  go whither  may return thou and take back thy brethren mercy and truth be with thee
nd ttai answered the king and said s the  liveth and as my lord the king liveth surely in what place my lord the king shall be whether in death or life even there also will thy servant be
nd avid said to ttai o and pass over
nd ttai the ittite passed over and all his men and all the little ones that were with him
nd all the country wept with a loud voice and all the people passed over the king also himself passed over the brook idron and all the people passed over toward the way of the wilderness
nd lo adok also and all the evites were with him bearing the ark of the covenant of od and they set down the ark of od and biathar went up until all the people had done passing out of the city

he king said also unto adok the priest rt not thou a seer return into the city in peace and your two sons with you himaaz thy son and onathan the son of biathar
ee  will tarry in the plain of the wilderness until there come word from you to certify me

nd avid went up by the ascent of mount livet and wept as he went up and had his head covered and he went barefoot and all the people that was with him covered every man his head and they went up weeping as they went up

nd it came to pass that when avid was come to the top of the mount where he worshipped od behold ushai the rchite came to meet him with his coat rent and earth upon his head unto whom avid said f thou passest on with me then thou shalt be a burden unto me but if thou return to the city and say unto bsalom  will be thy servant  king as  have been thy fathers servant hitherto so will  now also be thy servant then mayest thou for me defeat the counsel of hithophel
nd hast thou not there with thee adok and biathar the priests therefore it shall be that what thing soever thou shalt hear out of the kings house thou shalt tell it to adok and biathar the priests
ehold they have there with them their two sons himaaz adoks son and onathan biathars son and by them ye shall send unto me every thing that ye can hear
o ushai avids friend came into the city and bsalom came into erusalem
nd when avid was a little past the top of the hill behold iba the servant of ephibosheth met him with a couple of asses saddled and upon them two hundred loaves of bread and an hundred bunches of raisins and an hundred of summer fruits and a bottle of wine
nd the king said unto iba hat meanest thou by these
nd iba said he asses be for the kings household to ride on and the bread and summer fruit for the young men to eat and the wine that such as be faint in the wilderness may drink

nd when king avid came to ahurim behold thence came out a man of the family of the house of aul whose name was himei the son of era he came forth and cursed still as he came
nd he cast stones at avid and at all the servants of king avid and all the people and all the mighty men were on his right hand and on his left
nd thus said himei when he cursed ome out come out thou bloody man and thou man of elial the  hath returned upon thee all the blood of the house of aul in whose stead thou hast reigned and the  hath delivered the kingdom into the hand of bsalom thy son and behold thou art taken in thy mischief because thou art a bloody man
hen said bishai the son of eruiah unto the king hy should this dead dog curse my lord the king let me go over  pray thee and take off his head
nd the king said hat have  to do with you ye sons of eruiah so let him curse because the  hath said unto him urse avid
ho shall then say herefore hast thou done so
nd avid said to bishai and to all his servants ehold my son which came forth of my bowels seeketh my life how much more now may this enjamite do it let him alone and let him curse for the  hath bidden him
t may be that the  will look on mine affliction and that the  will requite me good for his cursing this day
nd as avid and his men went by the way himei went along on the hills side over against him and cursed as he went and threw stones at him and cast dust

nd again whom should  serve should  not serve in the presence of his son as  have served in thy fathers presence so will  be in thy presence
hen said bsalom to hithophel ive counsel among you what we shall do
nd hithophel said unto bsalom o in unto thy fathers concubines which he hath left to keep the house and all srael shall hear that thou art abhorred of thy father then shall the hands of all that are with thee be strong
o they spread bsalom a tent upon the top of the house and bsalom went in unto his fathers concubines in the sight of all srael
nd the counsel of hithophel which he counselled in those days was as if a man had enquired at the oracle of od so was all the counsel of hithophel both with avid and with bsalom
oreover hithophel said unto bsalom et me now choose out twelve thousand men and  will arise and pursue after avid this night and  will come upon him while he is weary and weak handed and will make him afraid and all the people that are with him shall flee and  will smite the king only and  will bring back all the people unto thee the man whom thou seekest is as if all returned so all the people shall be in peace
nd the saying pleased bsalom well and all the elders of srael
hen said bsalom all now ushai the rchite also and let us hear likewise what he saith
nd when ushai was come to bsalom bsalom spake unto him saying hithophel hath spoken after this manner shall we do after his saying if not speak thou
nd ushai said unto bsalom he counsel that hithophel hath given is not good at this time
or said ushai thou knowest thy father and his men that they be mighty men and they be chafed in their minds as a bear robbed of her whelps in the field and thy father is a man of war and will not lodge with the people
ehold he is hid now in some pit or in some other place and it will come to pass when some of them be overthrown at the first that whosoever heareth it will say here is a slaughter among the people that follow bsalom
nd he also that is valiant whose heart is as the heart of a lion shall utterly melt for all srael knoweth that thy father is a mighty man and they which be with him are valiant men
herefore  counsel that all srael be generally gathered unto thee from an even to eersheba as the sand that is by the sea for multitude and that thou go to battle in thine own person
o shall we come upon him in some place where he shall be found and we will light upon him as the dew falleth on the ground and of him and of all the men that are with him there shall not be left so much as one
oreover if he be gotten into a city then shall all srael bring ropes to that city and we will draw it into the river until there be not one small stone found there
nd bsalom and all the men of srael said he counsel of ushai the rchite is better than the counsel of hithophel
or the  had appointed to defeat the good counsel of hithophel to the intent that the  might bring evil upon bsalom
hen said ushai unto adok and to biathar the priests hus and thus did hithophel counsel bsalom and the elders of srael and thus and thus have  counselled
ow therefore send quickly and tell avid saying odge not this night in the plains of the wilderness but speedily pass over lest the king be swallowed up and all the people that are with him
ow onathan and himaaz stayed by nrogel for they might not be seen to come into the city and a wench went and told them and they went and told king avid
evertheless a lad saw them and told bsalom but they went both of them away quickly and came to a mans house in ahurim which had a well in his court whither they went down
nd the woman took and spread a covering over the wells mouth and spread ground corn thereon and the thing was not known
nd when bsaloms servants came to the woman to the house they said here is himaaz and onathan
nd the woman said unto them hey be gone over the brook of water
nd when they had sought and could not find them they returned to erusalem
nd it came to pass after they were departed that they came up out of the well and went and told king avid and said unto avid rise and pass quickly over the water for thus hath hithophel counselled against you
hen avid arose and all the people that were with him and they passed over ordan by the morning light there lacked not one of them that was not gone over ordan
nd when hithophel saw that his counsel was not followed he saddled his ass and arose and gat him home to his house to his city and put his household in order and hanged himself and died and was buried in the sepulchre of his father
hen avid came to ahanaim
nd bsalom passed over ordan he and all the men of srael with him
nd bsalom made masa captain of the host instead of oab which masa was a mans son whose name was thra an sraelite that went in to bigail the daughter of ahash sister to eruiah oabs mother

o the people went out into the field against srael and the battle was in the wood of phraim where the people of srael were slain before the servants of avid and there was there a great slaughter that day of twenty thousand men

nd bsalom met the servants of avid
nd bsalom rode upon a mule and the mule went under the thick boughs of a great oak and his head caught hold of the oak and he was taken up between the heaven and the earth and the mule that was under him went away
nd a certain man saw it and told oab and said ehold  saw bsalom hanged in an oak

nd olomons provision for one day was thirty measures of fine flour and threescore measures of meal ten fat oxen and twenty oxen out of the pastures and an hundred sheep beside harts and roebucks and fallow deer and fatted fowl
or he had dominion over all the region on this side the river from iphsah even to zzah over all the kings on this side the river and he had peace on all sides round about him

nd olomon had forty thousand stalls of horses for his chariots and twelve thousand horsemen

arley also and straw for the horses and dromedaries brought they unto the place where the officers were every man according to his charge
nd od gave olomon wisdom and understanding exceeding much and largeness of heart even as the sand that is on the sea shore
nd olomons wisdom excelled the wisdom of all the children of the east country and all the wisdom of gypt
or he was wiser than all men than than the zrahite and eman and halcol and arda the sons of ahol and his fame was in all nations round about
nd he spake three thousand proverbs and his songs were a thousand and five
nd he spake of trees from the cedar tree that is in ebanon even unto the hyssop that springeth out of the wall he spake also of beasts and of fowl and of creeping things and of fishes
nd there came of all people to hear the wisdom of olomon from all kings of the earth which had heard of his wisdom

ut now the  my od hath given me rest on every side so that there is neither adversary nor evil occurrent

ow therefore command thou that they hew me cedar trees out of ebanon and my servants shall be with thy servants and unto thee will  give hire for thy servants according to all that thou shalt appoint for thou knowest that there is not among us any that can skill to hew timber like unto the idonians
nd it came to pass when iram heard the words of olomon that he rejoiced greatly and said lessed be the  this day which hath given unto avid a wise son over this great people

o iram gave olomon cedar trees and fir trees according to all his desire
nd olomon gave iram twenty thousand measures of wheat for food to his household and twenty measures of pure oil thus gave olomon to iram year by year
nd the  gave olomon wisdom as he promised him and there was peace between iram and olomon and they two made a league together
nd king olomon raised a levy out of all srael and the levy was thirty thousand men
nd he sent them to ebanon ten thousand a month by courses a month they were in ebanon and two months at home and doniram was over the levy
nd olomon had threescore and ten thousand that bare burdens and fourscore thousand hewers in the mountains beside the chief of olomons officers which were over the work three thousand and three hundred which ruled over the people that wrought in the work
nd the king commanded and they brought great stones costly stones and hewed stones to lay the foundation of the house
nd olomons builders and irams builders did hew them and the stonesquarers so they prepared timber and stones to build the house
nd it came to pass in the four hundred and eightieth year after the children of srael were come out of the land of gypt in the fourth year of olomons reign over srael in the month if which is the second month that he began to build the house of the 
nd the house which king olomon built for the  the length thereof was threescore cubits and the breadth thereof twenty cubits and the height thereof thirty cubits
nd the porch before the temple of the house twenty cubits was the length thereof according to the breadth of the house and ten cubits was the breadth thereof before the house
nd for the house he made windows of narrow lights
nd against the wall of the house he built chambers round about against the walls of the house round about both of the temple and of the oracle and he made chambers round about the nethermost chamber was five cubits broad and the middle was six cubits broad and the third was seven cubits broad for without in the wall of the house he made narrowed rests round about that the beams should not be fastened in the walls of the house
nd the house when it was in building was built of stone made ready before it was brought thither so that there was neither hammer nor axe nor any tool of iron heard in the house while it was in building
he door for the middle chamber was in the right side of the house and they went up with winding stairs into the middle chamber and out of the middle into the third
o he built the house and finished it and covered the house with beams and boards of cedar
nd then he built chambers against all the house five cubits high and they rested on the house with timber of cedar
nd the word of the  came to olomon saying oncerning this house which thou art in building if thou wilt walk in my statutes and execute my judgments and keep all my commandments to walk in them then will  perform my word with thee which  spake unto avid thy father and  will dwell among the children of srael and will not forsake my people srael
o olomon built the house and finished it
nd he built the walls of the house within with boards of cedar both the floor of the house and the walls of the cieling and he covered them on the inside with wood and covered the floor of the house with planks of fir
nd he built twenty cubits on the sides of the house both the floor and the walls with boards of cedar he even built them for it within even for the oracle even for the most holy place
nd the house that is the temple before it was forty cubits long
nd the cedar of the house within was carved with knops and open flowers all was cedar there was no stone seen
nd the oracle he prepared in the house within to set there the ark of the covenant of the 
nd the oracle in the forepart was twenty cubits in length and twenty cubits in breadth and twenty cubits in the height thereof and he overlaid it with pure gold and so covered the altar which was of cedar
o olomon overlaid the house within with pure gold and he made a partition by the chains of gold before the oracle and he overlaid it with gold
nd the whole house he overlaid with gold until he had finished all the house also the whole altar that was by the oracle he overlaid with gold
nd within the oracle he made two cherubims of olive tree each ten cubits high
nd five cubits was the one wing of the cherub and five cubits the other wing of the cherub from the uttermost part of the one wing unto the uttermost part of the other were ten cubits
nd the other cherub was ten cubits both the cherubims were of one measure and one size
he height of the one cherub was ten cubits and so was it of the other cherub
nd he set the cherubims within the inner house and they stretched forth the wings of the cherubims so that the wing of the one touched the one wall and the wing of the other cherub touched the other wall and their wings touched one another in the midst of the house
nd he overlaid the cherubims with gold
nd he carved all the walls of the house round about with carved figures of cherubims and palm trees and open flowers within and without
nd the floor of the house he overlaid with gold within and without
nd for the entering of the oracle he made doors of olive tree the lintel and side posts were a fifth part of the wall
he two doors also were of olive tree and he carved upon them carvings of cherubims and palm trees and open flowers and overlaid them with gold and spread gold upon the cherubims and upon the palm trees
o also made he for the door of the temple posts of olive tree a fourth part of the wall
nd the two doors were of fir tree the two leaves of the one door were folding and the two leaves of the other door were folding
nd he carved thereon cherubims and palm trees and open flowers and covered them with gold fitted upon the carved work
nd he built the inner court with three rows of hewed stone and a row of cedar beams
n the fourth year was the foundation of the house of the  laid in the month if and in the eleventh year in the month ul which is the eighth month was the house finished throughout all the parts thereof and according to all the fashion of it
o was he seven years in building it
ut olomon was building his own house thirteen years and he finished all his house
e built also the house of the forest of ebanon the length thereof was an hundred cubits and the breadth thereof fifty cubits and the height thereof thirty cubits upon four rows of cedar pillars with cedar beams upon the pillars
nd it was covered with cedar above upon the beams that lay on forty five pillars fifteen in a row
nd there were windows in three rows and light was against light in three ranks
nd all the doors and posts were square with the windows and light was against light in three ranks
nd he made a porch of pillars the length thereof was fifty cubits and the breadth thereof thirty cubits and the porch was before them and the other pillars and the thick beam were before them
hen he made a porch for the throne where he might judge even the porch of judgment and it was covered with cedar from one side of the floor to the other
nd his house where he dwelt had another court within the porch which was of the like work
olomon made also an house for haraohs daughter whom he had taken to wife like unto this porch
ll these were of costly stones according to the measures of hewed stones sawed with saws within and without even from the foundation unto the coping and so on the outside toward the great court
nd the foundation was of costly stones even great stones stones of ten cubits and stones of eight cubits
nd above were costly stones after the measures of hewed stones and cedars
nd the great court round about was with three rows of hewed stones and a row of cedar beams both for the inner court of the house of the  and for the porch of the house
nd king olomon sent and fetched iram out of yre
e was a widows son of the tribe of aphtali and his father was a man of yre a worker in brass and he was filled with wisdom and understanding and cunning to work all works in brass
nd he came to king olomon and wrought all his work
or he cast two pillars of brass of eighteen cubits high apiece and a line of twelve cubits did compass either of them about
nd he made two chapiters of molten brass to set upon the tops of the pillars the height of the one chapiter was five cubits and the height of the other chapiter was five cubits and nets of checker work and wreaths of chain work for the chapiters which were upon the top of the pillars seven for the one chapiter and seven for the other chapiter
nd he made the pillars and two rows round about upon the one network to cover the chapiters that were upon the top with pomegranates and so did he for the other chapiter
nd the chapiters that were upon the top of the pillars were of lily work in the porch four cubits
nd the chapiters upon the two pillars had pomegranates also above over against the belly which was by the network and the pomegranates were two hundred in rows round about upon the other chapiter
nd he set up the pillars in the porch of the temple and he set up the right pillar and called the name thereof achin and he set up the left pillar and called the name thereof oaz
nd upon the top of the pillars was lily work so was the work of the pillars finished
nd he made a molten sea ten cubits from the one brim to the other it was round all about and his height was five cubits and a line of thirty cubits did compass it round about
nd under the brim of it round about there were knops compassing it ten in a cubit compassing the sea round about the knops were cast in two rows when it was cast
t stood upon twelve oxen three looking toward the north and three looking toward the west and three looking toward the south and three looking toward the east and the sea was set above upon them and all their hinder parts were inward
nd it was an hand breadth thick and the brim thereof was wrought like the brim of a cup with flowers of lilies it contained two thousand baths
nd he made ten bases of brass four cubits was the length of one base and four cubits the breadth thereof and three cubits the height of it
nd the work of the bases was on this manner they had borders and the borders were between the ledges and on the borders that were between the ledges were lions oxen and cherubims and upon the ledges there was a base above and beneath the lions and oxen were certain additions made of thin work
nd every base had four brasen wheels and plates of brass and the four corners thereof had undersetters under the laver were undersetters molten at the side of every addition
nd the mouth of it within the chapiter and above was a cubit but the mouth thereof was round after the work of the base a cubit and an half and also upon the mouth of it were gravings with their borders foursquare not round
nd under the borders were four wheels and the axletrees of the wheels were joined to the base and the height of a wheel was a cubit and half a cubit
nd the work of the wheels was like the work of a chariot wheel their axletrees and their naves and their felloes and their spokes were all molten
nd there were four undersetters to the four corners of one base and the undersetters were of the very base itself
nd in the top of the base was there a round compass of half a cubit high and on the top of the base the ledges thereof and the borders thereof were of the same
or on the plates of the ledges thereof and on the borders thereof he graved cherubims lions and palm trees according to the proportion of every one and additions round about
fter this manner he made the ten bases all of them had one casting one measure and one size
hen made he ten lavers of brass one laver contained forty baths and every laver was four cubits and upon every one of the ten bases one laver
nd he put five bases on the right side of the house and five on the left side of the house and he set the sea on the right side of the house eastward over against the south
nd iram made the lavers and the shovels and the basons
o iram made an end of doing all the work that he made king olomon for the house of the  the two pillars and the two bowls of the chapiters that were on the top of the two pillars and the two networks to cover the two bowls of the chapiters which were upon the top of the pillars and four hundred pomegranates for the two networks even two rows of pomegranates for one network to cover the two bowls of the chapiters that were upon the pillars and the ten bases and ten lavers on the bases and one sea and twelve oxen under the sea and the pots and the shovels and the basons and all these vessels which iram made to king olomon for the house of the  were of bright brass
n the plain of ordan did the king cast them in the clay ground between uccoth and arthan
nd olomon left all the vessels unweighed because they were exceeding many neither was the weight of the brass found out
nd olomon made all the vessels that pertained unto the house of the  the altar of gold and the table of gold whereupon the shewbread was and the candlesticks of pure gold five on the right side and five on the left before the oracle with the flowers and the lamps and the tongs of gold and the bowls and the snuffers and the basons and the spoons and the censers of pure gold and the hinges of gold both for the doors of the inner house the most holy place and for the doors of the house to wit of the temple
o was ended all the work that king olomon made for the house of the 
nd olomon brought in the things which avid his father had dedicated even the silver and the gold and the vessels did he put among the treasures of the house of the 
hen olomon assembled the elders of srael and all the heads of the tribes the chief of the fathers of the children of srael unto king olomon in erusalem that they might bring up the ark of the covenant of the  out of the city of avid which is ion
nd all the men of srael assembled themselves unto king olomon at the feast in the month thanim which is the seventh month
nd all the elders of srael came and the priests took up the ark
nd they brought up the ark of the  and the tabernacle of the congregation and all the holy vessels that were in the tabernacle even those did the priests and the evites bring up
nd king olomon and all the congregation of srael that were assembled unto him were with him before the ark sacrificing sheep and oxen that could not be told nor numbered for multitude
nd the priests brought in the ark of the covenant of the  unto his place into the oracle of the house to the most holy place even under the wings of the cherubims
or the cherubims spread forth their two wings over the place of the ark and the cherubims covered the ark and the staves thereof above
nd they drew out the staves that the ends of the staves were seen out in the holy place before the oracle and they were not seen without and there they are unto this day
here was nothing in the ark save the two tables of stone which oses put there at oreb when the  made a covenant with the children of srael when they came out of the land of gypt
nd it came to pass when the priests were come out of the holy place that the cloud filled the house of the  so that the priests could not stand to minister because of the cloud for the glory of the  had filled the house of the 
hen spake olomon he  said that he would dwell in the thick darkness
 have surely built thee an house to dwell in a settled place for thee to abide in for ever
nd the king turned his face about and blessed all the congregation of srael and all the congregation of srael stood and he said lessed be the  od of srael which spake with his mouth unto avid my father and hath with his hand fulfilled it saying ince the day that  brought forth my people srael out of gypt  chose no city out of all the tribes of srael to build an house that my name might be therein but  chose avid to be over my people srael
nd it was in the heart of avid my father to build an house for the name of the  od of srael
nd the  said unto avid my father hereas it was in thine heart to build an house unto my name thou didst well that it was in thine heart
evertheless thou shalt not build the house but thy son that shall come forth out of thy loins he shall build the house unto my name
nd the  hath performed his word that he spake and  am risen up in the room of avid my father and sit on the throne of srael as the  promised and have built an house for the name of the  od of srael
nd  have set there a place for the ark wherein is the covenant of the  which he made with our fathers when he brought them out of the land of gypt
nd olomon stood before the altar of the  in the presence of all the congregation of srael and spread forth his hands toward heaven and he said  od of srael there is no od like thee in heaven above or on earth beneath who keepest covenant and mercy with thy servants that walk before thee with all their heart who hast kept with thy servant avid my father that thou promisedst him thou spakest also with thy mouth and hast fulfilled it with thine hand as it is this day
herefore now  od of srael keep with thy servant avid my father that thou promisedst him saying here shall not fail thee a man in my sight to sit on the throne of srael so that thy children take heed to their way that they walk before me as thou hast walked before me
nd now  od of srael let thy word  pray thee be verified which thou spakest unto thy servant avid my father
ut will od indeed dwell on the earth behold the heaven and heaven of heavens cannot contain thee how much less this house that  have builded
et have thou respect unto the prayer of thy servant and to his supplication   my od to hearken unto the cry and to the prayer which thy servant prayeth before thee to day that thine eyes may be open toward this house night and day even toward the place of which thou hast said y name shall be there that thou mayest hearken unto the prayer which thy servant shall make toward this place
nd hearken thou to the supplication of thy servant and of thy people srael when they shall pray toward this place and hear thou in heaven thy dwelling place and when thou hearest forgive
f any man trespass against his neighbour and an oath be laid upon him to cause him to swear and the oath come before thine altar in this house then hear thou in heaven and do and judge thy servants condemning the wicked to bring his way upon his head and justifying the righteous to give him according to his righteousness
hen thy people srael be smitten down before the enemy because they have sinned against thee and shall turn again to thee and confess thy name and pray and make supplication unto thee in this house then hear thou in heaven and forgive the sin of thy people srael and bring them again unto the land which thou gavest unto their fathers
hen heaven is shut up and there is no rain because they have sinned against thee if they pray toward this place and confess thy name and turn from their sin when thou afflictest them then hear thou in heaven and forgive the sin of thy servants and of thy people srael that thou teach them the good way wherein they should walk and give rain upon thy land which thou hast given to thy people for an inheritance
f there be in the land famine if there be pestilence blasting mildew locust or if there be caterpiller if their enemy besiege them in the land of their cities whatsoever plague whatsoever sickness there be what prayer and supplication soever be made by any man or by all thy people srael which shall know every man the plague of his own heart and spread forth his hands toward this house then hear thou in heaven thy dwelling place and forgive and do and give to every man according to his ways whose heart thou knowest for thou even thou only knowest the hearts of all the children of men that they may fear thee all the days that they live in the land which thou gavest unto our fathers
oreover concerning a stranger that is not of thy people srael but cometh out of a far country for thy names sake for they shall hear of thy great name and of thy strong hand and of thy stretched out arm when he shall come and pray toward this house hear thou in heaven thy dwelling place and do according to all that the stranger calleth to thee for that all people of the earth may know thy name to fear thee as do thy people srael and that they may know that this house which  have builded is called by thy name
f thy people go out to battle against their enemy whithersoever thou shalt send them and shall pray unto the  toward the city which thou hast chosen and toward the house that  have built for thy name then hear thou in heaven their prayer and their supplication and maintain their cause
f they sin against thee for there is no man that sinneth not  and thou be angry with them and deliver them to the enemy so that they carry them away captives unto the land of the enemy far or near yet if they shall bethink themselves in the land whither they were carried captives and repent and make supplication unto thee in the land of them that carried them captives saying e have sinned and have done perversely we have committed wickedness and so return unto thee with all their heart and with all their soul in the land of their enemies which led them away captive and pray unto thee toward their land which thou gavest unto their fathers the city which thou hast chosen and the house which  have built for thy name then hear thou their prayer and their supplication in heaven thy dwelling place and maintain their cause and forgive thy people that have sinned against thee and all their transgressions wherein they have transgressed against thee and give them compassion before them who carried them captive that they may have compassion on them for they be thy people and thine inheritance which thou broughtest forth out of gypt from the midst of the furnace of iron that thine eyes may be open unto the supplication of thy servant and unto the supplication of thy people srael to hearken unto them in all that they call for unto thee
or thou didst separate them from among all the people of the earth to be thine inheritance as thou spakest by the hand of oses thy servant when thou broughtest our fathers out of gypt  ord 
nd it was so that when olomon had made an end of praying all this prayer and supplication unto the  he arose from before the altar of the  from kneeling on his knees with his hands spread up to heaven
nd he stood and blessed all the congregation of srael with a loud voice saying lessed be the  that hath given rest unto his people srael according to all that he promised there hath not failed one word of all his good promise which he promised by the hand of oses his servant
he  our od be with us as he was with our fathers let him not leave us nor forsake us that he may incline our hearts unto him to walk in all his ways and to keep his commandments and his statutes and his judgments which he commanded our fathers
nd let these my words wherewith  have made supplication before the  be nigh unto the  our od day and night that he maintain the cause of his servant and the cause of his people srael at all times as the matter shall require that all the people of the earth may know that the  is od and that there is none else
et your heart therefore be perfect with the  our od to walk in his statutes and to keep his commandments as at this day
nd the king and all srael with him offered sacrifice before the 
nd olomon offered a sacrifice of peace offerings which he offered unto the  two and twenty thousand oxen and an hundred and twenty thousand sheep
o the king and all the children of srael dedicated the house of the 
he same day did the king hallow the middle of the court that was before the house of the  for there he offered burnt offerings and meat offerings and the fat of the peace offerings because the brasen altar that was before the  was too little to receive the burnt offerings and meat offerings and the fat of the peace offerings
nd at that time olomon held a feast and all srael with him a great congregation from the entering in of amath unto the river of gypt before the  our od seven days and seven days even fourteen days
n the eighth day he sent the people away and they blessed the king and went unto their tents joyful and glad of heart for all the goodness that the  had done for avid his servant and for srael his people
nd it came to pass when olomon had finished the building of the house of the  and the kings house and all olomons desire which he was pleased to do that the  appeared to olomon the second time as he had appeared unto him at ibeon
nd the  said unto him  have heard thy prayer and thy supplication that thou hast made before me  have hallowed this house which thou hast built to put my name there for ever and mine eyes and mine heart shall be there perpetually
nd if thou wilt walk before me as avid thy father walked in integrity of heart and in uprightness to do according to all that  have commanded thee and wilt keep my statutes and my judgments then  will establish the throne of thy kingdom upon srael for ever as  promised to avid thy father saying here shall not fail thee a man upon the throne of srael
ut if ye shall at all turn from following me ye or your children and will not keep my commandments and my statutes which  have set before you but go and serve other gods and worship them then will  cut off srael out of the land which  have given them and this house which  have hallowed for my name will  cast out of my sight and srael shall be a proverb and a byword among all people and at this house which is high every one that passeth by it shall be astonished and shall hiss and they shall say hy hath the  done thus unto this land and to this house
nd they shall answer ecause they forsook the  their od who brought forth their fathers out of the land of gypt and have taken hold upon other gods and have worshipped them and served them therefore hath the  brought upon them all this evil
nd it came to pass at the end of twenty years when olomon had built the two houses the house of the  and the kings house now iram the king of yre had furnished olomon with cedar trees and fir trees and with gold according to all his desire  that then king olomon gave iram twenty cities in the land of alilee
nd iram came out from yre to see the cities which olomon had given him and they pleased him not
nd he said hat cities are these which thou hast given me my brother
nd he called them the land of abul unto this day
nd iram sent to the king sixscore talents of gold
nd this is the reason of the levy which king olomon raised for to build the house of the  and his own house and illo and the wall of erusalem and azor and egiddo and ezer
or haraoh king of gypt had gone up and taken ezer and burnt it with fire and slain the anaanites that dwelt in the city and given it for a present unto his daughter olomons wife
nd olomon built ezer and ethhoron the nether and aalath and admor in the wilderness in the land and all the cities of store that olomon had and cities for his chariots and cities for his horsemen and that which olomon desired to build in erusalem and in ebanon and in all the land of his dominion
nd all the people that were left of the morites ittites erizzites ivites and ebusites which were not of the children of srael their children that were left after them in the land whom the children of srael also were not able utterly to destroy upon those did olomon levy a tribute of bondservice unto this day
ut of the children of srael did olomon make no bondmen but they were men of war and his servants and his princes and his captains and rulers of his chariots and his horsemen
hese were the chief of the officers that were over olomons work five hundred and fifty which bare rule over the people that wrought in the work
ut haraohs daughter came up out of the city of avid unto her house which olomon had built for her then did he build illo

o he finished the house
nd king olomon made a navy of ships in ziongeber which is beside loth on the shore of the ed sea in the land of dom
nd iram sent in the navy his servants shipmen that had knowledge of the sea with the servants of olomon
nd they came to phir and fetched from thence gold four hundred and twenty talents and brought it to king olomon
nd when the queen of heba heard of the fame of olomon concerning the name of the  she came to prove him with hard questions
nd she came to erusalem with a very great train with camels that bare spices and very much gold and precious stones and when she was come to olomon she communed with him of all that was in her heart
nd olomon told her all her questions there was not any thing hid from the king which he told her not
nd when the queen of heba had seen all olomons wisdom and the house that he had built and the meat of his table and the sitting of his servants and the attendance of his ministers and their apparel and his cupbearers and his ascent by which he went up unto the house of the  there was no more spirit in her
nd she said to the king t was a true report that  heard in mine own land of thy acts and of thy wisdom
owbeit  believed not the words until  came and mine eyes had seen it and behold the half was not told me thy wisdom and prosperity exceedeth the fame which  heard
appy are thy men happy are these thy servants which stand continually before thee and that hear thy wisdom
lessed be the  thy od which delighted in thee to set thee on the throne of srael because the  loved srael for ever therefore made he thee king to do judgment and justice
nd she gave the king an hundred and twenty talents of gold and of spices very great store and precious stones there came no more such abundance of spices as these which the queen of heba gave to king olomon
nd the navy also of iram that brought gold from phir brought in from phir great plenty of almug trees and precious stones
nd the king made of the almug trees pillars for the house of the  and for the kings house harps also and psalteries for singers there came no such almug trees nor were seen unto this day
nd king olomon gave unto the queen of heba all her desire whatsoever she asked beside that which olomon gave her of his royal bounty
o she turned and went to her own country she and her servants
ow the weight of gold that came to olomon in one year was six hundred threescore and six talents of gold beside that he had of the merchantmen and of the traffick of the spice merchants and of all the kings of rabia and of the governors of the country
nd king olomon made two hundred targets of beaten gold six hundred shekels of gold went to one target
nd he made three hundred shields of beaten gold three pound of gold went to one shield and the king put them in the house of the forest of ebanon
oreover the king made a great throne of ivory and overlaid it with the best gold
he throne had six steps and the top of the throne was round behind and there were stays on either side on the place of the seat and two lions stood beside the stays
nd twelve lions stood there on the one side and on the other upon the six steps there was not the like made in any kingdom
nd all king olomons drinking vessels were of gold and all the vessels of the house of the forest of ebanon were of pure gold none were of silver it was nothing accounted of in the days of olomon
or the king had at sea a navy of harshish with the navy of iram once in three years came the navy of harshish bringing gold and silver ivory and apes and peacocks
o king olomon exceeded all the kings of the earth for riches and for wisdom
nd all the earth sought to olomon to hear his wisdom which od had put in his heart
nd they brought every man his present vessels of silver and vessels of gold and garments and armour and spices horses and mules a rate year by year
nd olomon gathered together chariots and horsemen and he had a thousand and four hundred chariots and twelve thousand horsemen whom he bestowed in the cities for chariots and with the king at erusalem
nd the king made silver to be in erusalem as stones and cedars made he to be as the sycomore trees that are in the vale for abundance
nd olomon had horses brought out of gypt and linen yarn the kings merchants received the linen yarn at a price
nd a chariot came up and went out of gypt for six hundred shekels of silver and an horse for an hundred and fifty and so for all the kings of the ittites and for the kings of yria did they bring them out by their means
ut king olomon loved many strange women together with the daughter of haraoh women of the oabites mmonites domites idonians and ittites of the nations concerning which the  said unto the children of srael e shall not go in to them neither shall they come in unto you for surely they will turn away your heart after their gods olomon clave unto these in love
nd he had seven hundred wives princesses and three hundred concubines and his wives turned away his heart
or it came to pass when olomon was old that his wives turned away his heart after other gods and his heart was not perfect with the  his od as was the heart of avid his father
or olomon went after shtoreth the goddess of the idonians and after ilcom the abomination of the mmonites
nd olomon did evil in the sight of the  and went not fully after the  as did avid his father
hen did olomon build an high place for hemosh the abomination of oab in the hill that is before erusalem and for olech the abomination of the children of mmon
nd likewise did he for all his strange wives which burnt incense and sacrificed unto their gods
nd the  was angry with olomon because his heart was turned from the  od of srael which had appeared unto him twice and had commanded him concerning this thing that he should not go after other gods but he kept not that which the  commanded
herefore the  said unto olomon orasmuch as this is done of thee and thou hast not kept my covenant and my statutes which  have commanded thee  will surely rend the kingdom from thee and will give it to thy servant
otwithstanding in thy days  will not do it for avid thy fathers sake but  will rend it out of the hand of thy son
owbeit  will not rend away all the kingdom but will give one tribe to thy son for avid my servants sake and for erusalems sake which  have chosen
nd the  stirred up an adversary unto olomon adad the domite he was of the kings seed in dom
or it came to pass when avid was in dom and oab the captain of the host was gone up to bury the slain after he had smitten every male in dom for six months did oab remain there with all srael until he had cut off every male in dom that adad fled he and certain domites of his fathers servants with him to go into gypt adad being yet a little child
nd they arose out of idian and came to aran and they took men with them out of aran and they came to gypt unto haraoh king of gypt which gave him an house and appointed him victuals and gave him land
nd adad found great favour in the sight of haraoh so that he gave him to wife the sister of his own wife the sister of ahpenes the queen
nd the sister of ahpenes bare him enubath his son whom ahpenes weaned in haraohs house and enubath was in haraohs household among the sons of haraoh
nd when adad heard in gypt that avid slept with his fathers and that oab the captain of the host was dead adad said to haraoh et me depart that  may go to mine own country
hen haraoh said unto him ut what hast thou lacked with me that behold thou seekest to go to thine own country
nd he answered othing howbeit let me go in any wise
nd od stirred him up another adversary ezon the son of liadah which fled from his lord adadezer king of obah and he gathered men unto him and became captain over a band when avid slew them of obah and they went to amascus and dwelt therein and reigned in amascus
nd he was an adversary to srael all the days of olomon beside the mischief that adad did and he abhorred srael and reigned over yria
nd eroboam the son of ebat an phrathite of ereda olomons servant whose mothers name was eruah a widow woman even he lifted up his hand against the king
nd this was the cause that he lifted up his hand against the king olomon built illo and repaired the breaches of the city of avid his father
nd the man eroboam was a mighty man of valour and olomon seeing the young man that he was industrious he made him ruler over all the charge of the house of oseph
nd it came to pass at that time when eroboam went out of erusalem that the prophet hijah the hilonite found him in the way and he had clad himself with a new garment and they two were alone in the field and hijah caught the new garment that was on him and rent it in twelve pieces and he said to eroboam ake thee ten pieces for thus saith the  the od of srael ehold  will rend the kingdom out of the hand of olomon and will give ten tribes to thee but he shall have one tribe for my servant avids sake and for erusalems sake the city which  have chosen out of all the tribes of srael because that they have forsaken me and have worshipped shtoreth the goddess of the idonians hemosh the god of the oabites and ilcom the god of the children of mmon and have not walked in my ways to do that which is right in mine eyes and to keep my statutes and my judgments as did avid his father
owbeit  will not take the whole kingdom out of his hand but  will make him prince all the days of his life for avid my servants sake whom  chose because he kept my commandments and my statutes but  will take the kingdom out of his sons hand and will give it unto thee even ten tribes
nd unto his son will  give one tribe that avid my servant may have a light alway before me in erusalem the city which  have chosen me to put my name there
nd  will take thee and thou shalt reign according to all that thy soul desireth and shalt be king over srael
nd it shall be if thou wilt hearken unto all that  command thee and wilt walk in my ways and do that is right in my sight to keep my statutes and my commandments as avid my servant did that  will be with thee and build thee a sure house as  built for avid and will give srael unto thee
nd  will for this afflict the seed of avid but not for ever
olomon sought therefore to kill eroboam
nd eroboam arose and fled into gypt unto hishak king of gypt and was in gypt until the death of olomon
nd the rest of the acts of olomon and all that he did and his wisdom are they not written in the book of the acts of olomon
nd the time that olomon reigned in erusalem over all srael was forty years
nd olomon slept with his fathers and was buried in the city of avid his father and ehoboam his son reigned in his stead
nd ehoboam went to hechem for all srael were come to hechem to make him king
nd it came to pass when eroboam the son of ebat who was yet in gypt heard of it for he was fled from the presence of king olomon and eroboam dwelt in gypt that they sent and called him
nd eroboam and all the congregation of srael came and spake unto ehoboam saying hy father made our yoke grievous now therefore make thou the grievous service of thy father and his heavy yoke which he put upon us lighter and we will serve thee
nd he said unto them epart yet for three days then come again to me
nd the people departed
nd king ehoboam consulted with the old men that stood before olomon his father while he yet lived and said ow do ye advise that  may answer this people
nd they spake unto him saying f thou wilt be a servant unto this people this day and wilt serve them and answer them and speak good words to them then they will be thy servants for ever
ut he forsook the counsel of the old men which they had given him and consulted with the young men that were grown up with him and which stood before him and he said unto them hat counsel give ye that we may answer this people who have spoken to me saying ake the yoke which thy father did put upon us lighter
nd the young men that were grown up with him spake unto him saying hus shalt thou speak unto this people that spake unto thee saying hy father made our yoke heavy but make thou it lighter unto us thus shalt thou say unto them y little finger shall be thicker than my fathers loins
nd now whereas my father did lade you with a heavy yoke  will add to your yoke my father hath chastised you with whips but  will chastise you with scorpions
o eroboam and all the people came to ehoboam the third day as the king had appointed saying ome to me again the third day
nd the king answered the people roughly and forsook the old mens counsel that they gave him and spake to them after the counsel of the young men saying y father made your yoke heavy and  will add to your yoke my father also chastised you with whips but  will chastise you with scorpions
herefore the king hearkened not unto the people for the cause was from the  that he might perform his saying which the  spake by hijah the hilonite unto eroboam the son of ebat

o srael departed unto their tents
ut as for the children of srael which dwelt in the cities of udah ehoboam reigned over them

herefore king ehoboam made speed to get him up to his chariot to flee to erusalem
o srael rebelled against the house of avid unto this day
nd it came to pass when all srael heard that eroboam was come again that they sent and called him unto the congregation and made him king over all srael there was none that followed the house of avid but the tribe of udah only
nd when ehoboam was come to erusalem he assembled all the house of udah with the tribe of enjamin an hundred and fourscore thousand chosen men which were warriors to fight against the house of srael to bring the kingdom again to ehoboam the son of olomon

nd he set the one in ethel and the other put he in an
nd this thing became a sin for the people went to worship before the one even unto an

o he offered upon the altar which he had made in ethel the fifteenth day of the eighth month even in the month which he had devised of his own heart and ordained a feast unto the children of srael and he offered upon the altar and burnt incense

nd he went up and looked and said here is nothing

nd it came to pass at the seventh time that he said ehold there ariseth a little cloud out of the sea like a mans hand
nd he said o up say unto hab repare thy chariot and get thee down that the rain stop thee not
nd it came to pass in the mean while that the heaven was black with clouds and wind and there was a great rain
nd hab rode and went to ezreel
nd the hand of the  was on lijah and he girded up his loins and ran before hab to the entrance of ezreel
nd hab told ezebel all that lijah had done and withal how he had slain all the prophets with the sword
hen ezebel sent a messenger unto lijah saying o let the gods do to me and more also if  make not thy life as the life of one of them by to morrow about this time
nd when he saw that he arose and went for his life and came to eersheba which belongeth to udah and left his servant there
ut he himself went a days journey into the wilderness and came and sat down under a juniper tree and he requested for himself that he might die and said t is enough now   take away my life for  am not better than my fathers
nd as he lay and slept under a juniper tree behold then an angel touched him and said unto him rise and eat
nd he looked and behold there was a cake baken on the coals and a cruse of water at his head
nd he did eat and drink and laid him down again
nd the angel of the  came again the second time and touched him and said rise and eat because the journey is too great for thee
nd he arose and did eat and drink and went in the strength of that meat forty days and forty nights unto oreb the mount of od
nd he came thither unto a cave and lodged there and behold the word of the  came to him and he said unto him hat doest thou here lijah
nd he said  have been very jealous for the  od of hosts for the children of srael have forsaken thy covenant thrown down thine altars and slain thy prophets with the sword and  even  only am left and they seek my life to take it away
nd he said o forth and stand upon the mount before the 
nd behold the  passed by and a great and strong wind rent the mountains and brake in pieces the rocks before the  but the  was not in the wind and after the wind an earthquake but the  was not in the earthquake and after the earthquake a fire but the  was not in the fire and after the fire a still small voice
nd it was so when lijah heard it that he wrapped his face in his mantle and went out and stood in the entering in of the cave
nd behold there came a voice unto him and said hat doest thou here lijah
nd he said  have been very jealous for the  od of hosts because the children of srael have forsaken thy covenant thrown down thine altars and slain thy prophets with the sword and  even  only am left and they seek my life to take it away
nd the  said unto him o return on thy way to the wilderness of amascus and when thou comest anoint azael to be king over yria and ehu the son of imshi shalt thou anoint to be king over srael and lisha the son of haphat of belmeholah shalt thou anoint to be prophet in thy room
nd it shall come to pass that him that escapeth the sword of azael shall ehu slay and him that escapeth from the sword of ehu shall lisha slay
et  have left me seven thousand in srael all the knees which have not bowed unto aal and every mouth which hath not kissed him
o he departed thence and found lisha the son of haphat who was plowing with twelve yoke of oxen before him and he with the twelfth and lijah passed by him and cast his mantle upon him
nd he left the oxen and ran after lijah and said et me  pray thee kiss my father and my mother and then  will follow thee
nd he said unto him o back again for what have  done to thee
nd he returned back from him and took a yoke of oxen and slew them and boiled their flesh with the instruments of the oxen and gave unto the people and they did eat
hen he arose and went after lijah and ministered unto him
nd enhadad the king of yria gathered all his host together and there were thirty and two kings with him and horses and chariots and he went up and besieged amaria and warred against it
nd he sent messengers to hab king of srael into the city and said unto him hus saith enhadad hy silver and thy gold is mine thy wives also and thy children even the goodliest are mine
nd the king of srael answered and said y lord  king according to thy saying  am thine and all that  have
nd the messengers came again and said hus speaketh enhadad saying lthough  have sent unto thee saying hou shalt deliver me thy silver and thy gold and thy wives and thy children yet  will send my servants unto thee to morrow about this time and they shall search thine house and the houses of thy servants and it shall be that whatsoever is pleasant in thine eyes they shall put it in their hand and take it away
hen the king of srael called all the elders of the land and said ark  pray you and see how this man seeketh mischief for he sent unto me for my wives and for my children and for my silver and for my gold and  denied him not
nd all the elders and all the people said unto him earken not unto him nor consent

nd he said hether they be come out for peace take them alive or whether they be come out for war take them alive
o these young men of the princes of the provinces came out of the city and the army which followed them

nd the king of srael went out and smote the horses and chariots and slew the yrians with a great slaughter
nd the prophet came to the king of srael and said unto him o strengthen thyself and mark and see what thou doest for at the return of the year the king of yria will come up against thee
nd the servants of the king of yria said unto him heir gods are gods of the hills therefore they were stronger than we but let us fight against them in the plain and surely we shall be stronger than they
nd do this thing ake the kings away every man out of his place and put captains in their rooms and number thee an army like the army that thou hast lost horse for horse and chariot for chariot and we will fight against them in the plain and surely we shall be stronger than they
nd he hearkened unto their voice and did so
nd it came to pass at the return of the year that enhadad numbered the yrians and went up to phek to fight against srael
nd the children of srael were numbered and were all present and went against them and the children of srael pitched before them like two little flocks of kids but the yrians filled the country

nd they pitched one over against the other seven days
