nd lisha said s the  of hosts liveth before whom  stand surely were it not that  regard the presence of ehoshaphat the king of udah  would not look toward thee nor see thee
ut now bring me a minstrel
nd it came to pass when the minstrel played that the hand of the  came upon him
nd he said hus saith the  ake this valley full of ditches
or thus saith the  e shall not see wind neither shall ye see rain yet that valley shall be filled with water that ye may drink both ye and your cattle and your beasts
nd this is but a light thing in the sight of the  he will deliver the oabites also into your hand
nd ye shall smite every fenced city and every choice city and shall fell every good tree and stop all wells of water and mar every good piece of land with stones
nd it came to pass in the morning when the meat offering was offered that behold there came water by the way of dom and the country was filled with water
nd when all the oabites heard that the kings were come up to fight against them they gathered all that were able to put on armour and upward and stood in the border
nd they rose up early in the morning and the sun shone upon the water and the oabites saw the water on the other side as red as blood and they said his is blood the kings are surely slain and they have smitten one another now therefore oab to the spoil
nd when they came to the camp of srael the sraelites rose up and smote the oabites so that they fled before them but they went forward smiting the oabites even in their country
nd they beat down the cities and on every good piece of land cast every man his stone and filled it and they stopped all the wells of water and felled all the good trees only in irhareseth left they the stones thereof howbeit the slingers went about it and smote it
nd when the king of oab saw that the battle was too sore for him he took with him seven hundred men that drew swords to break through even unto the king of dom but they could not
hen he took his eldest son that should have reigned in his stead and offered him for a burnt offering upon the wall
nd there was great indignation against srael and they departed from him and returned to their own land
ow there cried a certain woman of the wives of the sons of the prophets unto lisha saying hy servant my husband is dead and thou knowest that thy servant did fear the  and the creditor is come to take unto him my two sons to be bondmen
nd lisha said unto her hat shall  do for thee tell me what hast thou in the house
nd she said hine handmaid hath not any thing in the house save a pot of oil
hen he said o borrow thee vessels abroad of all thy neighbours even empty vessels borrow not a few
nd when thou art come in thou shalt shut the door upon thee and upon thy sons and shalt pour out into all those vessels and thou shalt set aside that which is full
o she went from him and shut the door upon her and upon her sons who brought the vessels to her and she poured out
nd it came to pass when the vessels were full that she said unto her son ring me yet a vessel
nd he said unto her here is not a vessel more
nd the oil stayed
hen she came and told the man of od
nd he said o sell the oil and pay thy debt and live thou and thy children of the rest
nd it fell on a day that lisha passed to hunem where was a great woman and she constrained him to eat bread
nd so it was that as oft as he passed by he turned in thither to eat bread
nd she said unto her husband ehold now  perceive that this is an holy man of od which passeth by us continually
et us make a little chamber  pray thee on the wall and let us set for him there a bed and a table and a stool and a candlestick and it shall be when he cometh to us that he shall turn in thither
nd it fell on a day that he came thither and he turned into the chamber and lay there
nd he said to ehazi his servant all this hunammite
nd when he had called her she stood before him
nd he said unto him ay now unto her ehold thou hast been careful for us with all this care what is to be done for thee wouldest thou be spoken for to the king or to the captain of the host
nd she answered  dwell among mine own people
nd he said hat then is to be done for her
nd ehazi answered erily she hath no child and her husband is old
nd he said all her
nd when he had called her she stood in the door
nd he said bout this season according to the time of life thou shalt embrace a son
nd she said ay my lord thou man of od do not lie unto thine handmaid
nd the woman conceived and bare a son at that season that lisha had said unto her according to the time of life
nd when the child was grown it fell on a day that he went out to his father to the reapers
nd he said unto his father y head my head
nd he said to a lad arry him to his mother
nd when he had taken him and brought him to his mother he sat on her knees till noon and then died
nd she went up and laid him on the bed of the man of od and shut the door upon him and went out
nd she called unto her husband and said end me  pray thee one of the young men and one of the asses that  may run to the man of od and come again
nd he said herefore wilt thou go to him to day it is neither new moon nor sabbath
nd she said t shall be well
hen she saddled an ass and said to her servant rive and go forward slack not thy riding for me except  bid thee
o she went and came unto the man of od to mount armel
nd it came to pass when the man of od saw her afar off that he said to ehazi his servant ehold yonder is that hunammite run now  pray thee to meet her and say unto her s it well with thee is it well with thy husband is it well with the child
nd she answered t is well
nd when she came to the man of od to the hill she caught him by the feet but ehazi came near to thrust her away
nd the man of od said et her alone for her soul is vexed within her and the  hath hid it from me and hath not told me
hen she said id  desire a son of my lord did  not say o not deceive me
hen he said to ehazi ird up thy loins and take my staff in thine hand and go thy way if thou meet any man salute him not and if any salute thee answer him not again and lay my staff upon the face of the child
nd the mother of the child said s the  liveth and as thy soul liveth  will not leave thee
nd he arose and followed her
nd ehazi passed on before them and laid the staff upon the face of the child but there was neither voice nor hearing
herefore he went again to meet him and told him saying he child is not awaked
nd when lisha was come into the house behold the child was dead and laid upon his bed
e went in therefore and shut the door upon them twain and prayed unto the 
nd he went up and lay upon the child and put his mouth upon his mouth and his eyes upon his eyes and his hands upon his hands and he stretched himself upon the child and the flesh of the child waxed warm
hen he returned and walked in the house to and fro and went up and stretched himself upon him and the child sneezed seven times and the child opened his eyes
nd he called ehazi and said all this hunammite
o he called her
nd when she was come in unto him he said ake up thy son
hen she went in and fell at his feet and bowed herself to the ground and took up her son and went out
nd lisha came again to ilgal and there was a dearth in the land and the sons of the prophets were sitting before him and he said unto his servant et on the great pot and seethe pottage for the sons of the prophets
nd one went out into the field to gather herbs and found a wild vine and gathered thereof wild gourds his lap full and came and shred them into the pot of pottage for they knew them not
o they poured out for the men to eat
nd it came to pass as they were eating of the pottage that they cried out and said  thou man of od there is death in the pot
nd they could not eat thereof
ut he said hen bring meal
nd he cast it into the pot and he said our out for the people that they may eat
nd there was no harm in the pot
nd there came a man from aalshalisha and brought the man of od bread of the firstfruits twenty loaves of barley and full ears of corn in the husk thereof
nd he said ive unto the people that they may eat
nd his servitor said hat should  set this before an hundred men
e said again ive the people that they may eat for thus saith the  hey shall eat and shall leave thereof
o he set it before them and they did eat and left thereof according to the word of the 
ow aaman captain of the host of the king of yria was a great man with his master and honourable because by him the  had given deliverance unto yria he was also a mighty man in valour but he was a leper
nd the yrians had gone out by companies and had brought away captive out of the land of srael a little maid and she waited on aamans wife
nd she said unto her mistress ould od my lord were with the prophet that is in amaria for he would recover him of his leprosy
nd one went in and told his lord saying hus and thus said the maid that is of the land of srael
nd the king of yria said o to go and  will send a letter unto the king of srael
nd he departed and took with him ten talents of silver and six thousand pieces of gold and ten changes of raiment
nd he brought the letter to the king of srael saying ow when this letter is come unto thee behold  have therewith sent aaman my servant to thee that thou mayest recover him of his leprosy
nd it came to pass when the king of srael had read the letter that he rent his clothes and said m  od to kill and to make alive that this man doth send unto me to recover a man of his leprosy
herefore consider  pray you and see how he seeketh a quarrel against me
nd it was so when lisha the man of od had heard that the king of srael had rent his clothes that he sent to the king saying herefore hast thou rent thy clothes let him come now to me and he shall know that there is a prophet in srael
o aaman came with his horses and with his chariot and stood at the door of the house of lisha
nd lisha sent a messenger unto him saying o and wash in ordan seven times and thy flesh shall come again to thee and thou shalt be clean
ut aaman was wroth and went away and said ehold  thought e will surely come out to me and stand and call on the name of the  his od and strike his hand over the place and recover the leper
re not bana and harpar rivers of amascus better than all the waters of srael may  not wash in them and be clean
o he turned and went away in a rage
nd his servants came near and spake unto him and said y father if the prophet had bid thee do some great thing wouldest thou not have done it how much rather then when he saith to thee ash and be clean
hen went he down and dipped himself seven times in ordan according to the saying of the man of od and his flesh came again like unto the flesh of a little child and he was clean
nd he returned to the man of od he and all his company and came and stood before him and he said ehold now  know that there is no od in all the earth but in srael now therefore  pray thee take a blessing of thy servant
ut he said s the  liveth before whom  stand  will receive none
nd he urged him to take it but he refused
nd aaman said hall there not then  pray thee be given to thy servant two mules burden of earth for thy servant will henceforth offer neither burnt offering nor sacrifice unto other gods but unto the 
n this thing the  pardon thy servant that when my master goeth into the house of immon to worship there and he leaneth on my hand and  bow myself in the house of immon when  bow down myself in the house of immon the  pardon thy servant in this thing
nd he said unto him o in peace
o he departed from him a little way
ut ehazi the servant of lisha the man of od said ehold my master hath spared aaman this yrian in not receiving at his hands that which he brought but as the  liveth  will run after him and take somewhat of him
o ehazi followed after aaman
nd when aaman saw him running after him he lighted down from the chariot to meet him and said s all well
nd he said ll is well
y master hath sent me saying ehold even now there be come to me from mount phraim two young men of the sons of the prophets give them  pray thee a talent of silver and two changes of garments
nd aaman said e content take two talents
nd he urged him and bound two talents of silver in two bags with two changes of garments and laid them upon two of his servants and they bare them before him
nd when he came to the tower he took them from their hand and bestowed them in the house and he let the men go and they departed
ut he went in and stood before his master
nd lisha said unto him hence comest thou ehazi
nd he said hy servant went no whither
nd he said unto him ent not mine heart with thee when the man turned again from his chariot to meet thee
s it a time to receive money and to receive garments and oliveyards and vineyards and sheep and oxen and menservants and maidservants
he leprosy therefore of aaman shall cleave unto thee and unto thy seed for ever
nd he went out from his presence a leper as white as snow
nd the sons of the prophets said unto lisha ehold now the place where we dwell with thee is too strait for us
et us go we pray thee unto ordan and take thence every man a beam and let us make us a place there where we may dwell
nd he answered o ye
nd one said e content  pray thee and go with thy servants
nd he answered  will go
o he went with them
nd when they came to ordan they cut down wood
ut as one was felling a beam the axe head fell into the water and he cried and said las master for it was borrowed
nd the man of od said here fell it
nd he shewed him the place
nd he cut down a stick and cast it in thither and the iron did swim
herefore said he ake it up to thee
nd he put out his hand and took it
hen the king of yria warred against srael and took counsel with his servants saying n such and such a place shall be my camp
nd the man of od sent unto the king of srael saying eware that thou pass not such a place for thither the yrians are come down
nd the king of srael sent to the place which the man of od told him and warned him of and saved himself there not once nor twice
herefore the heart of the king of yria was sore troubled for this thing and he called his servants and said unto them ill ye not shew me which of us is for the king of srael
nd one of his servants said one my lord  king but lisha the prophet that is in srael telleth the king of srael the words that thou speakest in thy bedchamber
nd he said o and spy where he is that  may send and fetch him
nd it was told him saying ehold he is in othan
herefore sent he thither horses and chariots and a great host and they came by night and compassed the city about
nd when the servant of the man of od was risen early and gone forth behold an host compassed the city both with horses and chariots
nd his servant said unto him las my master how shall we do
nd he answered ear not for they that be with us are more than they that be with them
nd lisha prayed and said   pray thee open his eyes that he may see
nd the  opened the eyes of the young man and he saw and behold the mountain was full of horses and chariots of fire round about lisha
nd when they came down to him lisha prayed unto the  and said mite this people  pray thee with blindness
nd he smote them with blindness according to the word of lisha
nd lisha said unto them his is not the way neither is this the city follow me and  will bring you to the man whom ye seek
ut he led them to amaria
nd it came to pass when they were come into amaria that lisha said  open the eyes of these men that they may see
nd the  opened their eyes and they saw and behold they were in the midst of amaria
nd the king of srael said unto lisha when he saw them y father shall  smite them shall  smite them
nd he answered hou shalt not smite them wouldest thou smite those whom thou hast taken captive with thy sword and with thy bow set bread and water before them that they may eat and drink and go to their master
nd he prepared great provision for them and when they had eaten and drunk he sent them away and they went to their master
o the bands of yria came no more into the land of srael
nd it came to pass after this that enhadad king of yria gathered all his host and went up and besieged amaria
nd there was a great famine in amaria and behold they besieged it until an asss head was sold for fourscore pieces of silver and the fourth part of a cab of doves dung for five pieces of silver
nd as the king of srael was passing by upon the wall there cried a woman unto him saying elp my lord  king
nd he said f the  do not help thee whence shall  help thee out of the barnfloor or out of the winepress
nd the king said unto her hat aileth thee
nd she answered his woman said unto me ive thy son that we may eat him to day and we will eat my son to morrow
o we boiled my son and did eat him and  said unto her on the next day ive thy son that we may eat him and she hath hid her son
nd it came to pass when the king heard the words of the woman that he rent his clothes and he passed by upon the wall and the people looked and behold he had sackcloth within upon his flesh
hen he said od do so and more also to me if the head of lisha the son of haphat shall stand on him this day
ut lisha sat in his house and the elders sat with him and the king sent a man from before him but ere the messenger came to him he said to the elders ee ye how this son of a murderer hath sent to take away mine head look when the messenger cometh shut the door and hold him fast at the door is not the sound of his masters feet behind him
nd while he yet talked with them behold the messenger came down unto him and he said ehold this evil is of the  what should  wait for the  any longer
hen lisha said ear ye the word of the  hus saith the  o morrow about this time shall a measure of fine flour be sold for a shekel and two measures of barley for a shekel in the gate of amaria
hen a lord on whose hand the king leaned answered the man of od and said ehold if the  would make windows in heaven might this thing be
nd he said ehold thou shalt see it with thine eyes but shalt not eat thereof
nd there were four leprous men at the entering in of the gate and they said one to another hy sit we here until we die
f we say e will enter into the city then the famine is in the city and we shall die there and if we sit still here we die also
ow therefore come and let us fall unto the host of the yrians if they save us alive we shall live and if they kill us we shall but die
nd they rose up in the twilight to go unto the camp of the yrians and when they were come to the uttermost part of the camp of yria behold there was no man there
or the  had made the host of the yrians to hear a noise of chariots and a noise of horses even the noise of a great host and they said one to another o the king of srael hath hired against us the kings of the ittites and the kings of the gyptians to come upon us
herefore they arose and fled in the twilight and left their tents and their horses and their asses even the camp as it was and fled for their life
nd when these lepers came to the uttermost part of the camp they went into one tent and did eat and drink and carried thence silver and gold and raiment and went and hid it and came again and entered into another tent and carried thence also and went and hid it
hen they said one to another e do not well this day is a day of good tidings and we hold our peace if we tarry till the morning light some mischief will come upon us now therefore come that we may go and tell the kings household
o they came and called unto the porter of the city and they told them saying e came to the camp of the yrians and behold there was no man there neither voice of man but horses tied and asses tied and the tents as they were
nd he called the porters and they told it to the kings house within
nd the king arose in the night and said unto his servants  will now shew you what the yrians have done to us
hey know that we be hungry therefore are they gone out of the camp to hide themselves in the field saying hen they come out of the city we shall catch them alive and get into the city
nd one of his servants answered and said et some take  pray thee five of the horses that remain which are left in the city behold they are as all the multitude of srael that are left in it behold  say they are even as all the multitude of the sraelites that are consumed and let us send and see
hey took therefore two chariot horses and the king sent after the host of the yrians saying o and see
nd they went after them unto ordan and lo all the way was full of garments and vessels which the yrians had cast away in their haste
nd the messengers returned and told the king
nd the people went out and spoiled the tents of the yrians

hen spake lisha unto the woman whose son he had restored to life saying rise and go thou and thine household and sojourn wheresoever thou canst sojourn for the  hath called for a famine and it shall also come upon the land seven years
nd the woman arose and did after the saying of the man of od and she went with her household and sojourned in the land of the hilistines seven years
nd it came to pass at the seven years end that the woman returned out of the land of the hilistines and she went forth to cry unto the king for her house and for her land
nd the king talked with ehazi the servant of the man of od saying ell me  pray thee all the great things that lisha hath done

nd ehazi said y lord  king this is the woman and this is her son whom lisha restored to life
nd when the king asked the woman she told him
o the king appointed unto her a certain officer saying estore all that was hers and all the fruits of the field since the day that she left the land even until now
nd lisha came to amascus and enhadad the king of yria was sick and it was told him saying he man of od is come hither
nd the king said unto azael ake a present in thine hand and go meet the man of od and enquire of the  by him saying hall  recover of this disease
o azael went to meet him and took a present with him even of every good thing of amascus forty camels burden and came and stood before him and said hy son enhadad king of yria hath sent me to thee saying hall  recover of this disease
nd lisha said unto him o say unto him hou mayest certainly recover howbeit the  hath shewed me that he shall surely die
nd he settled his countenance stedfastly until he was ashamed and the man of od wept
nd azael said hy weepeth my lord
nd he answered ecause  know the evil that thou wilt do unto the children of srael their strong holds wilt thou set on fire and their young men wilt thou slay with the sword and wilt dash their children and rip up their women with child
nd azael said ut what is thy servant a dog that he should do this great thing
nd lisha answered he  hath shewed me that thou shalt be king over yria
o he departed from lisha and came to his master who said to him hat said lisha to thee
nd he answered e told me that thou shouldest surely recover
nd it came to pass on the morrow that he took a thick cloth and dipped it in water and spread it on his face so that he died and azael reigned in his stead
nd in the fifth year of oram the son of hab king of srael ehoshaphat being then king of udah ehoram the son of ehoshaphat king of udah began to reign
hirty and two years old was he when he began to reign and he reigned eight years in erusalem
nd he walked in the way of the kings of srael as did the house of hab for the daughter of hab was his wife and he did evil in the sight of the 
et the  would not destroy udah for avid his servants sake as he promised him to give him alway a light and to his children
n his days dom revolted from under the hand of udah and made a king over themselves
o oram went over to air and all the chariots with him and he rose by night and smote the domites which compassed him about and the captains of the chariots and the people fled into their tents
et dom revolted from under the hand of udah unto this day
hen ibnah revolted at the same time
nd the rest of the acts of oram and all that he did are they not written in the book of the chronicles of the kings of udah
nd oram slept with his fathers and was buried with his fathers in the city of avid and haziah his son reigned in his stead
n the twelfth year of oram the son of hab king of srael did haziah the son of ehoram king of udah begin to reign
wo and twenty years old was haziah when he began to reign and he reigned one year in erusalem
nd his mothers name was thaliah the daughter of mri king of srael
nd he walked in the way of the house of hab and did evil in the sight of the  as did the house of hab for he was the son in law of the house of hab
nd he went with oram the son of hab to the war against azael king of yria in amothgilead and the yrians wounded oram
nd king oram went back to be healed in ezreel of the wounds which the yrians had given him at amah when he fought against azael king of yria
nd haziah the son of ehoram king of udah went down to see oram the son of hab in ezreel because he was sick
nd lisha the prophet called one of the children of the prophets and said unto him ird up thy loins and take this box of oil in thine hand and go to amothgilead and when thou comest thither look out there ehu the son of ehoshaphat the son of imshi and go in and make him arise up from among his brethren and carry him to an inner chamber then take the box of oil and pour it on his head and say hus saith the   have anointed thee king over srael
hen open the door and flee and tarry not

or the whole house of hab shall perish and  will cut off from hab him that pisseth against the wall and him that is shut up and left in srael and  will make the house of hab like the house of eroboam the son of ebat and like the house of aasha the son of hijah and the dogs shall eat ezebel in the portion of ezreel and there shall be none to bury her

hen ehu came forth to the servants of his lord and one said unto him s all well wherefore came this mad fellow to thee

ow oram had kept amothgilead he and all srael because of azael king of yria
ut king oram was returned to be healed in ezreel of the wounds which the yrians had given him when he fought with azael king of yria nd ehu said f it be your minds then let none go forth nor escape out of the city to go to tell it in ezreel
o ehu rode in a chariot and went to ezreel for oram lay there
nd haziah king of udah was come down to see oram

o there went one on horseback to meet him and said hus saith the king s it peace
nd ehu said hat hast thou to do with peace turn thee behind me

hen he sent out a second on horseback which came to them and said hus saith the king s it peace
nd ehu answered hat hast thou to do with peace turn thee behind me
nd the watchman told saying e came even unto them and cometh not again and the driving is like the driving of ehu the son of imshi for he driveth furiously
nd oram said ake ready
nd his chariot was made ready
nd oram king of srael and haziah king of udah went out each in his chariot and they went out against ehu and met him in the portion of aboth the ezreelite
nd it came to pass when oram saw ehu that he said s it peace ehu
nd he answered hat peace so long as the whoredoms of thy mother ezebel and her witchcrafts are so many
nd oram turned his hands and fled and said to haziah here is treachery  haziah
nd ehu drew a bow with his full strength and smote ehoram between his arms and the arrow went out at his heart and he sunk down in his chariot

ow therefore take and cast him into the plat of ground according to the word of the 
ut when haziah the king of udah saw this he fled by the way of the garden house
nd ehu followed after him and said mite him also in the chariot
nd they did so at the going up to ur which is by bleam
nd he fled to egiddo and died there
nd his servants carried him in a chariot to erusalem and buried him in his sepulchre with his fathers in the city of avid
nd in the eleventh year of oram the son of hab began haziah to reign over udah
nd when ehu was come to ezreel ezebel heard of it and she painted her face and tired her head and looked out at a window
nd as ehu entered in at the gate she said ad imri peace who slew his master
nd he lifted up his face to the window and said ho is on my side who
nd there looked out to him two or three eunuchs
nd he said hrow her down
o they threw her down and some of her blood was sprinkled on the wall and on the horses and he trode her under foot
nd when he was come in he did eat and drink and said o see now this cursed woman and bury her for she is a kings daughter

nd the children of srael did secretly those things that were not right against the  their od and they built them high places in all their cities from the tower of the watchmen to the fenced city
nd they set them up images and groves in every high hill and under every green tree and there they burnt incense in all the high places as did the heathen whom the  carried away before them and wrought wicked things to provoke the  to anger for they served idols whereof the  had said unto them e shall not do this thing
et the  testified against srael and against udah by all the prophets and by all the seers saying urn ye from your evil ways and keep my commandments and my statutes according to all the law which  commanded your fathers and which  sent to you by my servants the prophets

nd ehoiakim gave the silver and the gold to haraoh but he taxed the land to give the money according to the commandment of haraoh he exacted the silver and the gold of the people of the land of every one according to his taxation to give it unto haraohnechoh
ehoiakim was twenty and five years old when he began to reign and he reigned eleven years in erusalem
nd his mothers name was ebudah the daughter of edaiah of umah
nd he did that which was evil in the sight of the  according to all that his fathers had done
n his days ebuchadnezzar king of abylon came up and ehoiakim became his servant three years then he turned and rebelled against him
nd the  sent against him bands of the haldees and bands of the yrians and bands of the oabites and bands of the children of mmon and sent them against udah to destroy it according to the word of the  which he spake by his servants the prophets
urely at the commandment of the  came this upon udah to remove them out of his sight for the sins of anasseh according to all that he did and also for the innocent blood that he shed for he filled erusalem with innocent blood which the  would not pardon
ow the rest of the acts of ehoiakim and all that he did are they not written in the book of the chronicles of the kings of udah
o ehoiakim slept with his fathers and ehoiachin his son reigned in his stead
nd the king of gypt came not again any more out of his land for the king of abylon had taken from the river of gypt unto the river uphrates all that pertained to the king of gypt
ehoiachin was eighteen years old when he began to reign and he reigned in erusalem three months
nd his mothers name was ehushta the daughter of lnathan of erusalem
nd he did that which was evil in the sight of the  according to all that his father had done
t that time the servants of ebuchadnezzar king of abylon came up against erusalem and the city was besieged
nd ebuchadnezzar king of abylon came against the city and his servants did besiege it
nd ehoiachin the king of udah went out to the king of abylon he and his mother and his servants and his princes and his officers and the king of abylon took him in the eighth year of his reign
nd he carried out thence all the treasures of the house of the  and the treasures of the kings house and cut in pieces all the vessels of gold which olomon king of srael had made in the temple of the  as the  had said
nd he carried away all erusalem and all the princes and all the mighty men of valour even ten thousand captives and all the craftsmen and smiths none remained save the poorest sort of the people of the land
nd he carried away ehoiachin to abylon and the kings mother and the kings wives and his officers and the mighty of the land those carried he into captivity from erusalem to abylon
nd all the men of might even seven thousand and craftsmen and smiths a thousand all that were strong and apt for war even them the king of abylon brought captive to abylon
nd the king of abylon made attaniah his fathers brother king in his stead and changed his name to edekiah
edekiah was twenty and one years old when he began to reign and he reigned eleven years in erusalem
nd his mothers name was amutal the daughter of eremiah of ibnah
nd he did that which was evil in the sight of the  according to all that ehoiakim had done
or through the anger of the  it came to pass in erusalem and udah until he had cast them out from his presence that edekiah rebelled against the king of abylon
nd it came to pass in the ninth year of his reign in the tenth month in the tenth day of the month that ebuchadnezzar king of abylon came he and all his host against erusalem and pitched against it and they built forts against it round about
nd the city was besieged unto the eleventh year of king edekiah
nd on the ninth day of the fourth month the famine prevailed in the city and there was no bread for the people of the land
nd the city was broken up and all the men of war fled by night by the way of the gate between two walls which is by the kings garden now the haldees were against the city round about and the king went the way toward the plain
nd the army of the haldees pursued after the king and overtook him in the plains of ericho and all his army were scattered from him
o they took the king and brought him up to the king of abylon to iblah and they gave judgment upon him
nd they slew the sons of edekiah before his eyes and put out the eyes of edekiah and bound him with fetters of brass and carried him to abylon
nd in the fifth month on the seventh day of the month which is the nineteenth year of king ebuchadnezzar king of abylon came ebuzaradan captain of the guard a servant of the king of abylon unto erusalem and he burnt the house of the  and the kings house and all the houses of erusalem and every great mans house burnt he with fire
nd all the army of the haldees that were with the captain of the guard brake down the walls of erusalem round about
ow the rest of the people that were left in the city and the fugitives that fell away to the king of abylon with the remnant of the multitude did ebuzaradan the captain of the guard carry away
ut the captain of the guard left of the poor of the land to be vinedressers and husbandmen
nd the pillars of brass that were in the house of the  and the bases and the brasen sea that was in the house of the  did the haldees break in pieces and carried the brass of them to abylon
nd the pots and the shovels and the snuffers and the spoons and all the vessels of brass wherewith they ministered took they away
nd the firepans and the bowls and such things as were of gold in gold and of silver in silver the captain of the guard took away
he two pillars one sea and the bases which olomon had made for the house of the  the brass of all these vessels was without weight
he height of the one pillar was eighteen cubits and the chapiter upon it was brass and the height of the chapiter three cubits and the wreathen work and pomegranates upon the chapiter round about all of brass and like unto these had the second pillar with wreathen work
nd the captain of the guard took eraiah the chief priest and ephaniah the second priest and the three keepers of the door and out of the city he took an officer that was set over the men of war and five men of them that were in the kings presence which were found in the city and the principal scribe of the host which mustered the people of the land and threescore men of the people of the land that were found in the city and ebuzaradan captain of the guard took these and brought them to the king of abylon to iblah and the king of abylon smote them and slew them at iblah in the land of amath
o udah was carried away out of their land
nd as for the people that remained in the land of udah whom ebuchadnezzar king of abylon had left even over them he made edaliah the son of hikam the son of haphan ruler
nd when all the captains of the armies they and their men heard that the king of abylon had made edaliah governor there came to edaliah to izpah even shmael the son of ethaniah and ohanan the son of areah and eraiah the son of anhumeth the etophathite and aazaniah the son of a aachathite they and their men
nd edaliah sware to them and to their men and said unto them ear not to be the servants of the haldees dwell in the land and serve the king of abylon and it shall be well with you
ut it came to pass in the seventh month that shmael the son of ethaniah the son of lishama of the seed royal came and ten men with him and smote edaliah that he died and the ews and the haldees that were with him at izpah
nd all the people both small and great and the captains of the armies arose and came to gypt for they were afraid of the haldees
nd it came to pass in the seven and thirtieth year of the captivity of ehoiachin king of udah in the twelfth month on the seven and twentieth day of the month that vilmerodach king of abylon in the year that he began to reign did lift up the head of ehoiachin king of udah out of prison and he spake kindly to him and set his throne above the throne of the kings that were with him in abylon and changed his prison garments and he did eat bread continually before him all the days of his life
nd his allowance was a continual allowance given him of the king a daily rate for every day all the days of his life

ow the hilistines fought against srael and the men of srael fled from before the hilistines and fell down slain in mount ilboa
nd the hilistines followed hard after aul and after his sons and the hilistines slew onathan and binadab and alchishua the sons of aul
nd the battle went sore against aul and the archers hit him and he was wounded of the archers
hen said aul to his armourbearer raw thy sword and thrust me through therewith lest these uncircumcised come and abuse me
ut his armourbearer would not for he was sore afraid
o aul took a sword and fell upon it
nd when his armourbearer saw that aul was dead he fell likewise on the sword and died
o aul died and his three sons and all his house died together
nd when all the men of srael that were in the valley saw that they fled and that aul and his sons were dead then they forsook their cities and fled and the hilistines came and dwelt in them
nd it came to pass on the morrow when the hilistines came to strip the slain that they found aul and his sons fallen in mount ilboa
nd when they had stripped him they took his head and his armour and sent into the land of the hilistines round about to carry tidings unto their idols and to the people
nd they put his armour in the house of their gods and fastened his head in the temple of agon
nd when all abeshgilead heard all that the hilistines had done to aul they arose all the valiant men and took away the body of aul and the bodies of his sons and brought them to abesh and buried their bones under the oak in abesh and fasted seven days
o aul died for his transgression which he committed against the  even against the word of the  which he kept not and also for asking counsel of one that had a familiar spirit to enquire of it and enquired not of the  therefore he slew him and turned the kingdom unto avid the son of esse
hen all srael gathered themselves to avid unto ebron saying ehold we are thy bone and thy flesh

nd avid said hosoever smiteth the ebusites first shall be chief and captain
o oab the son of eruiah went first up and was chief

nd they set themselves in the midst of that parcel and delivered it and slew the hilistines and the  saved them by a great deliverance
ow three of the thirty captains went down to the rock to avid into the cave of dullam and the host of the hilistines encamped in the valley of ephaim
nd avid was then in the hold and the hilistines garrison was then at ethlehem
nd avid longed and said h that one would give me drink of the water of the well of ethlehem that is at the gate
nd the three brake through the host of the hilistines and drew water out of the well of ethlehem that was by the gate and took it and brought it to avid but avid would not drink of it but poured it out to the  and said y od forbid it me that  should do this thing shall  drink the blood of these men that have put their lives in jeopardy for with the jeopardy of their lives they brought it
herefore he would not drink it
hese things did these three mightiest
nd bishai the brother of oab he was chief of the three for lifting up his spear against three hundred he slew them and had a name among the three
f the three he was more honourable than the two for he was their captain howbeit he attained not to the first three
enaiah the son of ehoiada the son of a valiant man of abzeel who had done many acts he slew two lionlike men of oab also he went down and slew a lion in a pit in a snowy day
nd he slew an gyptian a man of great stature five cubits high and in the gyptians hand was a spear like a weavers beam and he went down to him with a staff and plucked the spear out of the gyptians hand and slew him with his own spear
hese things did enaiah the son of ehoiada and had the name among the three mighties
ehold he was honourable among the thirty but attained not to the first three and avid set him over his guard
lso the valiant men of the armies were sahel the brother of oab lhanan the son of odo of ethlehem hammoth the arorite elez the elonite ra the son of kkesh the ekoite biezer the ntothite ibbecai the ushathite lai the hohite aharai the etophathite eled the son of aanah the etophathite thai the son of ibai of ibeah that pertained to the children of enjamin enaiah the irathonite urai of the brooks of aash biel the rbathite zmaveth the aharumite liahba the haalbonite the sons of ashem the izonite onathan the son of hage the ararite hiam the son of acar the ararite liphal the son of r epher the echerathite hijah the elonite ezro the armelite aarai the son of zbai oel the brother of athan ibhar the son of aggeri elek the mmonite aharai the erothite the armourbearer of oab the son of eruiah ra the thrite areb the thrite riah the ittite abad the son of hlai dina the son of hiza the eubenite a captain of the eubenites and thirty with him anan the son of aachah and oshaphat the ithnite zzia the shterathite hama and ehiel the sons of othan the roerite ediael the son of himri and oha his brother the izite liel the ahavite and eribai and oshaviah the sons of lnaam and thmah the oabite liel and bed and asiel the esobaite
ow these are they that came to avid to iklag while he yet kept himself close because of aul the son of ish and they were among the mighty men helpers of the war

nd of the adites there separated themselves unto avid into the hold to the wilderness men of might and men of war fit for the battle that could handle shield and buckler whose faces were like the faces of lions and were as swift as the roes upon the mountains zer the first badiah the second liab the third ishmannah the fourth eremiah the fifth ttai the sixth liel the seventh ohanan the eighth lzabad the ninth eremiah the tenth achbanai the eleventh
hese were of the sons of ad captains of the host one of the least was over an hundred and the greatest over a thousand
hese are they that went over ordan in the first month when it had overflown all his banks and they put to flight all them of the valleys both toward the east and toward the west
nd there came of the children of enjamin and udah to the hold unto avid
nd avid went out to meet them and answered and said unto them f ye be come peaceably unto me to help me mine heart shall be knit unto you but if ye be come to betray me to mine enemies seeing there is no wrong in mine hands the od of our fathers look thereon and rebuke it
hen the spirit came upon masai who was chief of the captains and he said hine are we avid and on thy side thou son of esse peace peace be unto thee and peace be to thine helpers for thy od helpeth thee
hen avid received them and made them captains of the band
nd there fell some of anasseh to avid when he came with the hilistines against aul to battle but they helped them not for the lords of the hilistines upon advisement sent him away saying e will fall to his master aul to the jeopardy of our heads

or at that time day by day there came to avid to help him until it was a great host like the host of od
nd these are the numbers of the bands that were ready armed to the war and came to avid to ebron to turn the kingdom of aul to him according to the word of the 
he children of udah that bare shield and spear were six thousand and eight hundred ready armed to the war
f the children of imeon mighty men of valour for the war seven thousand and one hundred
f the children of evi four thousand and six hundred
nd ehoiada was the leader of the aronites and with him were three thousand and seven hundred and adok a young man mighty of valour and of his fathers house twenty and two captains
nd of the children of enjamin the kindred of aul three thousand for hitherto the greatest part of them had kept the ward of the house of aul
nd of the children of phraim twenty thousand and eight hundred mighty men of valour famous throughout the house of their fathers
nd of the half tribe of anasseh eighteen thousand which were expressed by name to come and make avid king
nd of the children of ssachar which were men that had understanding of the times to know what srael ought to do the heads of them were two hundred and all their brethren were at their commandment
f ebulun such as went forth to battle expert in war with all instruments of war fifty thousand which could keep rank they were not of double heart
nd of aphtali a thousand captains and with them with shield and spear thirty and seven thousand
nd of the anites expert in war twenty and eight thousand and six hundred
nd of sher such as went forth to battle expert in war forty thousand
nd on the other side of ordan of the eubenites and the adites and of the half tribe of anasseh with all manner of instruments of war for the battle an hundred and twenty thousand
ll these men of war that could keep rank came with a perfect heart to ebron to make avid king over all srael and all the rest also of srael were of one heart to make avid king
nd there they were with avid three days eating and drinking for their brethren had prepared for them
oreover they that were nigh them even unto ssachar and ebulun and aphtali brought bread on asses and on camels and on mules and on oxen and meat meal cakes of figs and bunches of raisins and wine and oil and oxen and sheep abundantly for there was joy in srael
nd avid consulted with the captains of thousands and hundreds and with every leader
nd avid said unto all the congregation of srael f it seem good unto you and that it be of the  our od let us send abroad unto our brethren every where that are left in all the land of srael and with them also to the priests and evites which are in their cities and suburbs that they may gather themselves unto us and let us bring again the ark of our od to us for we enquired not at it in the days of aul
nd all the congregation said that they would do so for the thing was right in the eyes of all the people
o avid gathered all srael together from hihor of gypt even unto the entering of emath to bring the ark of od from irjathjearim
nd avid went up and all srael to aalah that is to irjathjearim which belonged to udah to bring up thence the ark of od the  that dwelleth between the cherubims whose name is called on it
nd they carried the ark of od in a new cart out of the house of binadab and zza and hio drave the cart
nd avid and all srael played before od with all their might and with singing and with harps and with psalteries and with timbrels and with cymbals and with trumpets
nd when they came unto the threshingfloor of hidon zza put forth his hand to hold the ark for the oxen stumbled
nd the anger of the  was kindled against zza and he smote him because he put his hand to the ark and there he died before od
nd avid was displeased because the  had made a breach upon zza wherefore that place is called erezuzza to this day
nd avid was afraid of od that day saying ow shall  bring the ark of od home to me
o avid brought not the ark home to himself to the city of avid but carried it aside into the house of bededom the ittite
nd the ark of od remained with the family of bededom in his house three months
nd the  blessed the house of bededom and all that he had
ow iram king of yre sent messengers to avid and timber of cedars with masons and carpenters to build him an house
nd avid perceived that the  had confirmed him king over srael for his kingdom was lifted up on high because of his people srael
nd avid took more wives at erusalem and avid begat more sons and daughters
ow these are the names of his children which he had in erusalem hammua and hobab athan and olomon and bhar and lishua and lpalet and ogah and epheg and aphia and lishama and eeliada and liphalet
nd when the hilistines heard that avid was anointed king over all srael all the hilistines went up to seek avid
nd avid heard of it and went out against them
nd the hilistines came and spread themselves in the valley of ephaim

hen avid the king stood up upon his feet and said ear me my brethren and my people s for me  had in mine heart to build an house of rest for the ark of the covenant of the  and for the footstool of our od and had made ready for the building but od said unto me hou shalt not build an house for my name because thou hast been a man of war and hast shed blood

nd od said to olomon ecause this was in thine heart and thou hast not asked riches wealth or honour nor the life of thine enemies neither yet hast asked long life but hast asked wisdom and knowledge for thyself that thou mayest judge my people over whom  have made thee king wisdom and knowledge is granted unto thee and  will give thee riches and wealth and honour such as none of the kings have had that have been before thee neither shall there any after thee have the like
hen olomon came from his journey to the high place that was at ibeon to erusalem from before the tabernacle of the congregation and reigned over srael
nd olomon gathered chariots and horsemen and he had a thousand and four hundred chariots and twelve thousand horsemen which he placed in the chariot cities and with the king at erusalem

nd olomon had horses brought out of gypt and linen yarn the kings merchants received the linen yarn at a price

his is an ordinance for ever to srael
nd the house which  build is great for great is our od above all gods
ut who is able to build him an house seeing the heaven and heaven of heavens cannot contain him who am  then that  should build him an house save only to burn sacrifice before him
end me now therefore a man cunning to work in gold and in silver and in brass and in iron and in purple and crimson and blue and that can skill to grave with the cunning men that are with me in udah and in erusalem whom avid my father did provide

nd behold  will give to thy servants the hewers that cut timber twenty thousand measures of beaten wheat and twenty thousand measures of barley and twenty thousand baths of wine and twenty thousand baths of oil
hen uram the king of yre answered in writing which he sent to olomon ecause the  hath loved his people he hath made thee king over them

nd now  have sent a cunning man endued with understanding of uram my fathers the son of a woman of the daughters of an and his father was a man of yre skilful to work in gold and in silver in brass in iron in stone and in timber in purple in blue and in fine linen and in crimson also to grave any manner of graving and to find out every device which shall be put to him with thy cunning men and with the cunning men of my lord avid thy father
ow therefore the wheat and the barley the oil and the wine which my lord hath spoken of let him send unto his servants and we will cut wood out of ebanon as much as thou shalt need and we will bring it to thee in floats by sea to oppa and thou shalt carry it up to erusalem
nd olomon numbered all the strangers that were in the land of srael after the numbering wherewith avid his father had numbered them and they were found an hundred and fifty thousand and three thousand and six hundred
nd he set threescore and ten thousand of them to be bearers of burdens and fourscore thousand to be hewers in the mountain and three thousand and six hundred overseers to set the people a work
hen olomon began to build the house of the  at erusalem in mount oriah where the  appeared unto avid his father in the place that avid had prepared in the threshingfloor of rnan the ebusite
nd he began to build in the second day of the second month in the fourth year of his reign
ow these are the things wherein olomon was instructed for the building of the house of od
he length by cubits after the first measure was threescore cubits and the breadth twenty cubits
nd the porch that was in the front of the house the length of it was according to the breadth of the house twenty cubits and the height was an hundred and twenty and he overlaid it within with pure gold
nd the greater house he cieled with fir tree which he overlaid with fine gold and set thereon palm trees and chains
nd he garnished the house with precious stones for beauty and the gold was gold of arvaim
e overlaid also the house the beams the posts and the walls thereof and the doors thereof with gold and graved cherubims on the walls
nd he made the most holy house the length whereof was according to the breadth of the house twenty cubits and the breadth thereof twenty cubits and he overlaid it with fine gold amounting to six hundred talents
nd the weight of the nails was fifty shekels of gold
nd he overlaid the upper chambers with gold
nd in the most holy house he made two cherubims of image work and overlaid them with gold
nd the wings of the cherubims were twenty cubits long one wing of the one cherub was five cubits reaching to the wall of the house and the other wing was likewise five cubits reaching to the wing of the other cherub
nd one wing of the other cherub was five cubits reaching to the wall of the house and the other wing was five cubits also joining to the wing of the other cherub
he wings of these cherubims spread themselves forth twenty cubits and they stood on their feet and their faces were inward
nd he made the vail of blue and purple and crimson and fine linen and wrought cherubims thereon
lso he made before the house two pillars of thirty and five cubits high and the chapiter that was on the top of each of them was five cubits
nd he made chains as in the oracle and put them on the heads of the pillars and made an hundred pomegranates and put them on the chains
nd he reared up the pillars before the temple one on the right hand and the other on the left and called the name of that on the right hand achin and the name of that on the left oaz
oreover he made an altar of brass twenty cubits the length thereof and twenty cubits the breadth thereof and ten cubits the height thereof
lso he made a molten sea of ten cubits from brim to brim round in compass and five cubits the height thereof and a line of thirty cubits did compass it round about
nd under it was the similitude of oxen which did compass it round about ten in a cubit compassing the sea round about
wo rows of oxen were cast when it was cast
t stood upon twelve oxen three looking toward the north and three looking toward the west and three looking toward the south and three looking toward the east and the sea was set above upon them and all their hinder parts were inward
nd the thickness of it was an handbreadth and the brim of it like the work of the brim of a cup with flowers of lilies and it received and held three thousand baths
e made also ten lavers and put five on the right hand and five on the left to wash in them such things as they offered for the burnt offering they washed in them but the sea was for the priests to wash in
nd he made ten candlesticks of gold according to their form and set them in the temple five on the right hand and five on the left
e made also ten tables and placed them in the temple five on the right side and five on the left
nd he made an hundred basons of gold
urthermore he made the court of the priests and the great court and doors for the court and overlaid the doors of them with brass
nd he set the sea on the right side of the east end over against the south
nd uram made the pots and the shovels and the basons
nd uram finished the work that he was to make for king olomon for the house of od to wit the two pillars and the pommels and the chapiters which were on the top of the two pillars and the two wreaths to cover the two pommels of the chapiters which were on the top of the pillars and four hundred pomegranates on the two wreaths two rows of pomegranates on each wreath to cover the two pommels of the chapiters which were upon the pillars
e made also bases and lavers made he upon the bases one sea and twelve oxen under it
he pots also and the shovels and the fleshhooks and all their instruments did uram his father make to king olomon for the house of the  of bright brass
n the plain of ordan did the king cast them in the clay ground between uccoth and eredathah
hus olomon made all these vessels in great abundance for the weight of the brass could not be found out
nd olomon made all the vessels that were for the house of od the golden altar also and the tables whereon the shewbread was set moreover the candlesticks with their lamps that they should burn after the manner before the oracle of pure gold and the flowers and the lamps and the tongs made he of gold and that perfect gold and the snuffers and the basons and the spoons and the censers of pure gold and the entry of the house the inner doors thereof for the most holy place and the doors of the house of the temple were of gold
hus all the work that olomon made for the house of the  was finished and olomon brought in all the things that avid his father had dedicated and the silver and the gold and all the instruments put he among the treasures of the house of od
hen olomon assembled the elders of srael and all the heads of the tribes the chief of the fathers of the children of srael unto erusalem to bring up the ark of the covenant of the  out of the city of avid which is ion
herefore all the men of srael assembled themselves unto the king in the feast which was in the seventh month
nd all the elders of srael came and the evites took up the ark
nd they brought up the ark and the tabernacle of the congregation and all the holy vessels that were in the tabernacle these did the priests and the evites bring up
lso king olomon and all the congregation of srael that were assembled unto him before the ark sacrificed sheep and oxen which could not be told nor numbered for multitude
nd the priests brought in the ark of the covenant of the  unto his place to the oracle of the house into the most holy place even under the wings of the cherubims for the cherubims spread forth their wings over the place of the ark and the cherubims covered the ark and the staves thereof above
nd they drew out the staves of the ark that the ends of the staves were seen from the ark before the oracle but they were not seen without
nd there it is unto this day
here was nothing in the ark save the two tables which oses put therein at oreb when the  made a covenant with the children of srael when they came out of gypt
nd it came to pass when the priests were come out of the holy place for all the priests that were present were sanctified and did not then wait by course also the evites which were the singers all of them of saph of eman of eduthun with their sons and their brethren being arrayed in white linen having cymbals and psalteries and harps stood at the east end of the altar and with them an hundred and twenty priests sounding with trumpets it came even to pass as the trumpeters and singers were as one to make one sound to be heard in praising and thanking the  and when they lifted up their voice with the trumpets and cymbals and instruments of musick and praised the  saying or he is good for his mercy endureth for ever that then the house was filled with a cloud even the house of the  so that the priests could not stand to minister by reason of the cloud for the glory of the  had filled the house of od

ow it was in the heart of avid my father to build an house for the name of the  od of srael
ut the  said to avid my father orasmuch as it was in thine heart to build an house for my name thou didst well in that it was in thine heart notwithstanding thou shalt not build the house but thy son which shall come forth out of thy loins he shall build the house for my name
he  therefore hath performed his word that he hath spoken for  am risen up in the room of avid my father and am set on the throne of srael as the  promised and have built the house for the name of the  od of srael

ow therefore   od of srael keep with thy servant avid my father that which thou hast promised him saying here shall not fail thee a man in my sight to sit upon the throne of srael yet so that thy children take heed to their way to walk in my law as thou hast walked before me

ut will od in very deed dwell with men on the earth

ave respect therefore to the prayer of thy servant and to his supplication   my od to hearken unto the cry and the prayer which thy servant prayeth before thee that thine eyes may be open upon this house day and night upon the place whereof thou hast said that thou wouldest put thy name there to hearken unto the prayer which thy servant prayeth toward this place
earken therefore unto the supplications of thy servant and of thy people srael which they shall make toward this place hear thou from thy dwelling place even from heaven and when thou hearest forgive
f a man sin against his neighbour and an oath be laid upon him to make him swear and the oath come before thine altar in this house then hear thou from heaven and do and judge thy servants by requiting the wicked by recompensing his way upon his own head and by justifying the righteous by giving him according to his righteousness
nd if thy people srael be put to the worse before the enemy because they have sinned against thee and shall return and confess thy name and pray and make supplication before thee in this house then hear thou from the heavens and forgive the sin of thy people srael and bring them again unto the land which thou gavest to them and to their fathers
hen the heaven is shut up and there is no rain because they have sinned against thee yet if they pray toward this place and confess thy name and turn from their sin when thou dost afflict them then hear thou from heaven and forgive the sin of thy servants and of thy people srael when thou hast taught them the good way wherein they should walk and send rain upon thy land which thou hast given unto thy people for an inheritance
f there be dearth in the land if there be pestilence if there be blasting or mildew locusts or caterpillers if their enemies besiege them in the cities of their land whatsoever sore or whatsoever sickness there be then what prayer or what supplication soever shall be made of any man or of all thy people srael when every one shall know his own sore and his own grief and shall spread forth his hands in this house then hear thou from heaven thy dwelling place and forgive and render unto every man according unto all his ways whose heart thou knowest for thou only knowest the hearts of the children of men that they may fear thee to walk in thy ways so long as they live in the land which thou gavest unto our fathers
oreover concerning the stranger which is not of thy people srael but is come from a far country for thy great names sake and thy mighty hand and thy stretched out arm if they come and pray in this house then hear thou from the heavens even from thy dwelling place and do according to all that the stranger calleth to thee for that all people of the earth may know thy name and fear thee as doth thy people srael and may know that this house which  have built is called by thy name
f thy people go out to war against their enemies by the way that thou shalt send them and they pray unto thee toward this city which thou hast chosen and the house which  have built for thy name then hear thou from the heavens their prayer and their supplication and maintain their cause

ow because we have maintenance from the kings palace and it was not meet for us to see the kings dishonour therefore have we sent and certified the king that search may be made in the book of the records of thy fathers so shalt thou find in the book of the records and know that this city is a rebellious city and hurtful unto kings and provinces and that they have moved sedition within the same of old time for which cause was this city destroyed
e certify the king that if this city be builded again and the walls thereof set up by this means thou shalt have no portion on this side the river
hen sent the king an answer unto ehum the chancellor and to himshai the scribe and to the rest of their companions that dwell in amaria and unto the rest beyond the river eace and at such a time
he letter which ye sent unto us hath been plainly read before me
nd  commanded and search hath been made and it is found that this city of old time hath made insurrection against kings and that rebellion and sedition have been made therein
here have been mighty kings also over erusalem which have ruled over all countries beyond the river and toll tribute and custom was paid unto them
ive ye now commandment to cause these men to cease and that this city be not builded until another commandment shall be given from me
ake heed now that ye fail not to do this why should damage grow to the hurt of the kings
ow when the copy of king rtaxerxes letter was read before ehum and himshai the scribe and their companions they went up in haste to erusalem unto the ews and made them to cease by force and power

nd thus they returned us answer saying e are the servants of the od of heaven and earth and build the house that was builded these many years ago which a great king of srael builded and set up
ut after that our fathers had provoked the od of heaven unto wrath he gave them into the hand of ebuchadnezzar the king of abylon the haldean who destroyed this house and carried the people away into abylon
ut in the first year of yrus the king of abylon the same king yrus made a decree to build this house of od
nd the vessels also of gold and silver of the house of od which ebuchadnezzar took out of the temple that was in erusalem and brought them into the temple of abylon those did yrus the king take out of the temple of abylon and they were delivered unto one whose name was heshbazzar whom he had made governor and said unto him ake these vessels go carry them into the temple that is in erusalem and let the house of od be builded in his place
hen came the same heshbazzar and laid the foundation of the house of od which is in erusalem and since that time even until now hath it been in building and yet it is not finished
ow therefore if it seem good to the king let there be search made in the kings treasure house which is there at abylon whether it be so that a decree was made of yrus the king to build this house of od at erusalem and let the king send his pleasure to us concerning this matter
hen arius the king made a decree and search was made in the house of the rolls where the treasures were laid up in abylon
nd there was found at chmetha in the palace that is in the province of the edes a roll and therein was a record thus written in the first year of yrus the king the same yrus the king made a decree concerning the house of od at erusalem et the house be builded the place where they offered sacrifices and let the foundations thereof be strongly laid the height thereof threescore cubits and the breadth thereof threescore cubits with three rows of great stones and a row of new timber and let the expenses be given out of the kings house and also let the golden and silver vessels of the house of od which ebuchadnezzar took forth out of the temple which is at erusalem and brought unto abylon be restored and brought again unto the temple which is at erusalem every one to his place and place them in the house of od
ow therefore atnai governor beyond the river hetharboznai and your companions the pharsachites which are beyond the river be ye far from thence let the work of this house of od alone let the governor of the ews and the elders of the ews build this house of od in his place
oreover  make a decree what ye shall do to the elders of these ews for the building of this house of od that of the kings goods even of the tribute beyond the river forthwith expenses be given unto these men that they be not hindered
nd that which they have need of both young bullocks and rams and lambs for the burnt offerings of the od of heaven wheat salt wine and oil according to the appointment of the priests which are at erusalem let it be given them day by day without fail that they may offer sacrifices of sweet savours unto the od of heaven and pray for the life of the king and of his sons
lso  have made a decree that whosoever shall alter this word let timber be pulled down from his house and being set up let him be hanged thereon and let his house be made a dunghill for this
nd the od that hath caused his name to dwell there destroy all kings and people that shall put to their hand to alter and to destroy this house of od which is at erusalem
 arius have made a decree let it be done with speed
hen atnai governor on this side the river hetharboznai and their companions according to that which arius the king had sent so they did speedily
nd the elders of the ews builded and they prospered through the prophesying of aggai the prophet and echariah the son of ddo
nd they builded and finished it according to the commandment of the od of srael and according to the commandment of yrus and arius and rtaxerxes king of ersia
nd this house was finished on the third day of the month dar which was in the sixth year of the reign of arius the king
nd the children of srael the priests and the evites and the rest of the children of the captivity kept the dedication of this house of od with joy and offered at the dedication of this house of od an hundred bullocks two hundred rams four hundred lambs and for a sin offering for all srael twelve he goats according to the number of the tribes of srael
nd they set the priests in their divisions and the evites in their courses for the service of od which is at erusalem as it is written in the book of oses
nd the children of the captivity kept the passover upon the fourteenth day of the first month
or the priests and the evites were purified together all of them were pure and killed the passover for all the children of the captivity and for their brethren the priests and for themselves
nd the children of srael which were come again out of captivity and all such as had separated themselves unto them from the filthiness of the heathen of the land to seek the  od of srael did eat and kept the feast of unleavened bread seven days with joy for the  had made them joyful and turned the heart of the king of ssyria unto them to strengthen their hands in the work of the house of od the od of srael
ow after these things in the reign of rtaxerxes king of ersia zra the son of eraiah the son of zariah the son of ilkiah the son of hallum the son of adok the son of hitub the son of mariah the son of zariah the son of eraioth the son of erahiah the son of zzi the son of ukki the son of bishua the son of hinehas the son of leazar the son of aron the chief priest this zra went up from abylon and he was a ready scribe in the law of oses which the  od of srael had given and the king granted him all his request according to the hand of the  his od upon him
nd there went up some of the children of srael and of the priests and the evites and the singers and the porters and the ethinims unto erusalem in the seventh year of rtaxerxes the king
nd he came to erusalem in the fifth month which was in the seventh year of the king
or upon the first day of the first month began he to go up from abylon and on the first day of the fifth month came he to erusalem according to the good hand of his od upon him
or zra had prepared his heart to seek the law of the  and to do it and to teach in srael statutes and judgments
ow this is the copy of the letter that the king rtaxerxes gave unto zra the priest the scribe even a scribe of the words of the commandments of the  and of his statutes to srael
rtaxerxes king of kings unto zra the priest a scribe of the law of the od of heaven perfect peace and at such a time
 make a decree that all they of the people of srael and of his priests and evites in my realm which are minded of their own freewill to go up to erusalem go with thee
orasmuch as thou art sent of the king and of his seven counsellors to enquire concerning udah and erusalem according to the law of thy od which is in thine hand and to carry the silver and gold which the king and his counsellors have freely offered unto the od of srael whose habitation is in erusalem and all the silver and gold that thou canst find in all the province of abylon with the freewill offering of the people and of the priests offering willingly for the house of their od which is in erusalem that thou mayest buy speedily with this money bullocks rams lambs with their meat offerings and their drink offerings and offer them upon the altar of the house of your od which is in erusalem
nd whatsoever shall seem good to thee and to thy brethren to do with the rest of the silver and the gold that do after the will of your od
he vessels also that are given thee for the service of the house of thy od those deliver thou before the od of erusalem
nd whatsoever more shall be needful for the house of thy od which thou shalt have occasion to bestow bestow it out of the kings treasure house
nd  even  rtaxerxes the king do make a decree to all the treasurers which are beyond the river that whatsoever zra the priest the scribe of the law of the od of heaven shall require of you it be done speedily unto an hundred talents of silver and to an hundred measures of wheat and to an hundred baths of wine and to an hundred baths of oil and salt without prescribing how much
hatsoever is commanded by the od of heaven let it be diligently done for the house of the od of heaven for why should there be wrath against the realm of the king and his sons
lso we certify you that touching any of the priests and evites singers porters ethinims or ministers of this house of od it shall not be lawful to impose toll tribute or custom upon them
nd thou zra after the wisdom of thy od that is in thine hand set magistrates and judges which may judge all the people that are beyond the river all such as know the laws of thy od and teach ye them that know them not
nd whosoever will not do the law of thy od and the law of the king let judgment be executed speedily upon him whether it be unto death or to banishment or to confiscation of goods or to imprisonment
lessed be the  od of our fathers which hath put such a thing as this in the kings heart to beautify the house of the  which is in erusalem and hath extended mercy unto me before the king and his counsellors and before all the kings mighty princes
nd  was strengthened as the hand of the  my od was upon me and  gathered together out of srael chief men to go up with me
hese are now the chief of their fathers and this is the genealogy of them that went up with me from abylon in the reign of rtaxerxes the king

nd  gathered them together to the river that runneth to hava and there abode we in tents three days and  viewed the people and the priests and found there none of the sons of evi
hen sent  for liezer for riel for hemaiah and for lnathan and for arib and for lnathan and for athan and for echariah and for eshullam chief men also for oiarib and for lnathan men of understanding
