nd by the good hand of our od upon us they brought us a man of understanding of the sons of ahli the son of evi the son of srael and herebiah with his sons and his brethren eighteen and ashabiah and with him eshaiah of the sons of erari his brethren and their sons twenty also of the ethinims whom avid and the princes had appointed for the service of the evites two hundred and twenty ethinims all of them were expressed by name
hen  proclaimed a fast there at the river of hava that we might afflict ourselves before our od to seek of him a right way for us and for our little ones and for all our substance
or  was ashamed to require of the king a band of soldiers and horsemen to help us against the enemy in the way because we had spoken unto the king saying he hand of our od is upon all them for good that seek him but his power and his wrath is against all them that forsake him
o we fasted and besought our od for this and he was intreated of us
hen  separated twelve of the chief of the priests herebiah ashabiah and ten of their brethren with them and weighed unto them the silver and the gold and the vessels even the offering of the house of our od which the king and his counsellors and his lords and all srael there present had offered  even weighed unto their hand six hundred and fifty talents of silver and silver vessels an hundred talents and of gold an hundred talents also twenty basons of gold of a thousand drams and two vessels of fine copper precious as gold
nd  said unto them e are holy unto the  the vessels are holy also and the silver and the gold are a freewill offering unto the  od of your fathers
atch ye and keep them until ye weigh them before the chief of the priests and the evites and chief of the fathers of srael at erusalem in the chambers of the house of the 
o took the priests and the evites the weight of the silver and the gold and the vessels to bring them to erusalem unto the house of our od
hen we departed from the river of hava on the twelfth day of the first month to go unto erusalem and the hand of our od was upon us and he delivered us from the hand of the enemy and of such as lay in wait by the way
nd we came to erusalem and abode there three days
ow on the fourth day was the silver and the gold and the vessels weighed in the house of our od by the hand of eremoth the son of riah the priest and with him was leazar the son of hinehas and with them was ozabad the son of eshua and oadiah the son of innui evites by number and by weight of every one and all the weight was written at that time
lso the children of those that had been carried away which were come out of the captivity offered burnt offerings unto the od of srael twelve bullocks for all srael ninety and six rams seventy and seven lambs twelve he goats for a sin offering all this was a burnt offering unto the 
nd they delivered the kings commissions unto the kings lieutenants and to the governors on this side the river and they furthered the people and the house of od
ow when these things were done the princes came to me saying he people of srael and the priests and the evites have not separated themselves from the people of the lands doing according to their abominations even of the anaanites the ittites the erizzites the ebusites the mmonites the oabites the gyptians and the morites
or they have taken of their daughters for themselves and for their sons so that the holy seed have mingled themselves with the people of those lands yea the hand of the princes and rulers hath been chief in this trespass
nd when  heard this thing  rent my garment and my mantle and plucked off the hair of my head and of my beard and sat down astonied
hen were assembled unto me every one that trembled at the words of the od of srael because of the transgression of those that had been carried away and  sat astonied until the evening sacrifice
nd at the evening sacrifice  arose up from my heaviness and having rent my garment and my mantle  fell upon my knees and spread out my hands unto the  my od and said  my od  am ashamed and blush to lift up my face to thee my od for our iniquities are increased over our head and our trespass is grown up unto the heavens
ince the days of our fathers have we been in a great trespass unto this day and for our iniquities have we our kings and our priests been delivered into the hand of the kings of the lands to the sword to captivity and to a spoil and to confusion of face as it is this day
nd now for a little space grace hath been shewed from the  our od to leave us a remnant to escape and to give us a nail in his holy place that our od may lighten our eyes and give us a little reviving in our bondage
or we were bondmen yet our od hath not forsaken us in our bondage but hath extended mercy unto us in the sight of the kings of ersia to give us a reviving to set up the house of our od and to repair the desolations thereof and to give us a wall in udah and in erusalem
nd now  our od what shall we say after this for we have forsaken thy commandments which thou hast commanded by thy servants the prophets saying he land unto which ye go to possess it is an unclean land with the filthiness of the people of the lands with their abominations which have filled it from one end to another with their uncleanness
ow therefore give not your daughters unto their sons neither take their daughters unto your sons nor seek their peace or their wealth for ever that ye may be strong and eat the good of the land and leave it for an inheritance to your children for ever
nd after all that is come upon us for our evil deeds and for our great trespass seeing that thou our od hast punished us less than our iniquities deserve and hast given us such deliverance as this should we again break thy commandments and join in affinity with the people of these abominations wouldest not thou be angry with us till thou hadst consumed us so that there should be no remnant nor escaping
  od of srael thou art righteous for we remain yet escaped as it is this day behold we are before thee in our trespasses for we cannot stand before thee because of this
ow when zra had prayed and when he had confessed weeping and casting himself down before the house of od there assembled unto him out of srael a very great congregation of men and women and children for the people wept very sore
nd hechaniah the son of ehiel one of the sons of lam answered and said unto zra e have trespassed against our od and have taken strange wives of the people of the land yet now there is hope in srael concerning this thing
ow therefore let us make a covenant with our od to put away all the wives and such as are born of them according to the counsel of my lord and of those that tremble at the commandment of our od and let it be done according to the law
rise for this matter belongeth unto thee we also will be with thee be of good courage and do it
hen arose zra and made the chief priests the evites and all srael to swear that they should do according to this word
nd they sware
hen zra rose up from before the house of od and went into the chamber of ohanan the son of liashib and when he came thither he did eat no bread nor drink water for he mourned because of the transgression of them that had been carried away
nd they made proclamation throughout udah and erusalem unto all the children of the captivity that they should gather themselves together unto erusalem and that whosoever would not come within three days according to the counsel of the princes and the elders all his substance should be forfeited and himself separated from the congregation of those that had been carried away
hen all the men of udah and enjamin gathered themselves together unto erusalem within three days
t was the ninth month on the twentieth day of the month and all the people sat in the street of the house of od trembling because of this matter and for the great rain
nd zra the priest stood up and said unto them e have transgressed and have taken strange wives to increase the trespass of srael
ow therefore make confession unto the  od of your fathers and do his pleasure and separate yourselves from the people of the land and from the strange wives
hen all the congregation answered and said with a loud voice s thou hast said so must we do
ut the people are many and it is a time of much rain and we are not able to stand without neither is this a work of one day or two for we are many that have transgressed in this thing
et now our rulers of all the congregation stand and let all them which have taken strange wives in our cities come at appointed times and with them the elders of every city and the judges thereof until the fierce wrath of our od for this matter be turned from us
nly onathan the son of sahel and ahaziah the son of ikvah were employed about this matter and eshullam and habbethai the evite helped them
nd the children of the captivity did so
nd zra the priest with certain chief of the fathers after the house of their fathers and all of them by their names were separated and sat down in the first day of the tenth month to examine the matter
nd they made an end with all the men that had taken strange wives by the first day of the first month
nd among the sons of the priests there were found that had taken strange wives namely of the sons of eshua the son of ozadak and his brethren aaseiah and liezer and arib and edaliah
nd they gave their hands that they would put away their wives and being guilty they offered a ram of the flock for their trespass

nd it came to pass in the month hisleu in the twentieth year as  was in hushan the palace that anani one of my brethren came he and certain men of udah and  asked them concerning the ews that had escaped which were left of the captivity and concerning erusalem
nd they said unto me he remnant that are left of the captivity there in the province are in great affliction and reproach the wall of erusalem also is broken down and the gates thereof are burned with fire
nd it came to pass when  heard these words that  sat down and wept and mourned certain days and fasted and prayed before the od of heaven and said  beseech thee   od of heaven the great and terrible od that keepeth covenant and mercy for them that love him and observe his commandments let thine ear now be attentive and thine eyes open that thou mayest hear the prayer of thy servant which  pray before thee now day and night for the children of srael thy servants and confess the sins of the children of srael which we have sinned against thee both  and my fathers house have sinned
e have dealt very corruptly against thee and have not kept the commandments nor the statutes nor the judgments which thou commandedst thy servant oses
emember  beseech thee the word that thou commandedst thy servant oses saying f ye transgress  will scatter you abroad among the nations but if ye turn unto me and keep my commandments and do them though there were of you cast out unto the uttermost part of the heaven yet will  gather them from thence and will bring them unto the place that  have chosen to set my name there
ow these are thy servants and thy people whom thou hast redeemed by thy great power and by thy strong hand
 ord  beseech thee let now thine ear be attentive to the prayer of thy servant and to the prayer of thy servants who desire to fear thy name and prosper  pray thee thy servant this day and grant him mercy in the sight of this man
or  was the kings cupbearer
nd it came to pass in the month isan in the twentieth year of rtaxerxes the king that wine was before him and  took up the wine and gave it unto the king
ow  had not been beforetime sad in his presence
herefore the king said unto me hy is thy countenance sad seeing thou art not sick this is nothing else but sorrow of heart
hen  was very sore afraid and said unto the king et the king live for ever why should not my countenance be sad when the city the place of my fathers sepulchres lieth waste and the gates thereof are consumed with fire
hen the king said unto me or what dost thou make request
o  prayed to the od of heaven
nd  said unto the king f it please the king and if thy servant have found favour in thy sight that thou wouldest send me unto udah unto the city of my fathers sepulchres that  may build it
nd the king said unto me the queen also sitting by him  or how long shall thy journey be and when wilt thou return
o it pleased the king to send me and  set him a time
oreover  said unto the king f it please the king let letters be given me to the governors beyond the river that they may convey me over till  come into udah and a letter unto saph the keeper of the kings forest that he may give me timber to make beams for the gates of the palace which appertained to the house and for the wall of the city and for the house that  shall enter into
nd the king granted me according to the good hand of my od upon me
hen  came to the governors beyond the river and gave them the kings letters
ow the king had sent captains of the army and horsemen with me
hen anballat the oronite and obiah the servant the mmonite heard of it grieved them exceedingly that there was come a man to seek the welfare of the children of srael
o  came to erusalem and was there three days
nd  arose in the night  and some few men with me neither told  any man what my od had put in my heart to do at erusalem neither was there any beast with me save the beast that  rode upon
nd  went out by night by the gate of the valley even before the dragon well and to the dung port and viewed the walls of erusalem which were broken down and the gates thereof were consumed with fire
hen  went on to the gate of the fountain and to the kings pool but there was no place for the beast that was under me to pass
hen went  up in the night by the brook and viewed the wall and turned back and entered by the gate of the valley and so returned
nd the rulers knew not whither  went or what  did neither had  as yet told it to the ews nor to the priests nor to the nobles nor to the rulers nor to the rest that did the work
hen said  unto them e see the distress that we are in how erusalem lieth waste and the gates thereof are burned with fire come and let us build up the wall of erusalem that we be no more a reproach
hen  told them of the hand of my od which was good upon me as also the kings words that he had spoken unto me
nd they said et us rise up and build
o they strengthened their hands for this good work
ut when anballat the oronite and obiah the servant the mmonite and eshem the rabian heard it they laughed us to scorn and despised us and said hat is this thing that ye do will ye rebel against the king
hen answered  them and said unto them he od of heaven he will prosper us therefore we his servants will arise and build but ye have no portion nor right nor memorial in erusalem
hen liashib the high priest rose up with his brethren the priests and they builded the sheep gate they sanctified it and set up the doors of it even unto the tower of eah they sanctified it unto the tower of ananeel
nd next unto him builded the men of ericho
nd next to them builded accur the son of mri
ut the fish gate did the sons of assenaah build who also laid the beams thereof and set up the doors thereof the locks thereof and the bars thereof
nd next unto them repaired eremoth the son of rijah the son of oz
nd next unto them repaired eshullam the son of erechiah the son of eshezabeel
nd next unto them repaired adok the son of aana
nd next unto them the ekoites repaired but their nobles put not their necks to the work of their ord
oreover the old gate repaired ehoiada the son of aseah and eshullam the son of esodeiah they laid the beams thereof and set up the doors thereof and the locks thereof and the bars thereof
nd next unto them repaired elatiah the ibeonite and adon the eronothite the men of ibeon and of izpah unto the throne of the governor on this side the river
ext unto him repaired zziel the son of arhaiah of the goldsmiths

nd next unto them repaired ephaiah the son of ur the ruler of the half part of erusalem

nd next unto him repaired hallum the son of alohesh the ruler of the half part of erusalem he and his daughters
he valley gate repaired anun and the inhabitants of anoah they built it and set up the doors thereof the locks thereof and the bars thereof and a thousand cubits on the wall unto the dung gate
ut the dung gate repaired alchiah the son of echab the ruler of part of ethhaccerem he built it and set up the doors thereof the locks thereof and the bars thereof
ut the gate of the fountain repaired hallun the son of olhozeh the ruler of part of izpah he built it and covered it and set up the doors thereof the locks thereof and the bars thereof and the wall of the pool of iloah by the kings garden and unto the stairs that go down from the city of avid
fter him repaired ehemiah the son of zbuk the ruler of the half part of ethzur unto the place over against the sepulchres of avid and to the pool that was made and unto the house of the mighty

ext unto him repaired ashabiah the ruler of the half part of eilah in his part

nd next to him repaired zer the son of eshua the ruler of izpah another piece over against the going up to the armoury at the turning of the wall

nd after him repaired the priests the men of the plain

alal the son of zai over against the turning of the wall and the tower which lieth out from the kings high house that was by the court of the prison
fter him edaiah the son of arosh
oreover the ethinims dwelt in phel unto the place over against the water gate toward the east and the tower that lieth out

rom above the horse gate repaired the priests every one over against his house

fter him repaired also hemaiah the son of hechaniah the keeper of the east gate
fter him repaired ananiah the son of helemiah and anun the sixth son of alaph another piece
fter him repaired eshullam the son of erechiah over against his chamber
fter him repaired alchiah the goldsmiths son unto the place of the ethinims and of the merchants over against the gate iphkad and to the going up of the corner
nd between the going up of the corner unto the sheep gate repaired the goldsmiths and the merchants
ut it came to pass that when anballat heard that we builded the wall he was wroth and took great indignation and mocked the ews
nd he spake before his brethren and the army of amaria and said hat do these feeble ews will they fortify themselves will they sacrifice will they make an end in a day will they revive the stones out of the heaps of the rubbish which are burned
ow obiah the mmonite was by him and he said ven that which they build if a fox go up he shall even break down their stone wall
ear  our od for we are despised and turn their reproach upon their own head and give them for a prey in the land of captivity and cover not their iniquity and let not their sin be blotted out from before thee for they have provoked thee to anger before the builders
o built we the wall and all the wall was joined together unto the half thereof for the people had a mind to work
ut it came to pass that when anballat and obiah and the rabians and the mmonites and the shdodites heard that the walls of erusalem were made up and that the breaches began to be stopped then they were very wroth and conspired all of them together to come and to fight against erusalem and to hinder it
evertheless we made our prayer unto our od and set a watch against them day and night because of them
nd udah said he strength of the bearers of burdens is decayed and there is much rubbish so that we are not able to build the wall
nd our adversaries said hey shall not know neither see till we come in the midst among them and slay them and cause the work to cease
nd it came to pass that when the ews which dwelt by them came they said unto us ten times rom all places whence ye shall return unto us they will be upon you
herefore set  in the lower places behind the wall and on the higher places  even set the people after their families with their swords their spears and their bows
nd  looked and rose up and said unto the nobles and to the rulers and to the rest of the people e not ye afraid of them remember the ord which is great and terrible and fight for your brethren your sons and your daughters your wives and your houses
nd it came to pass when our enemies heard that it was known unto us and od had brought their counsel to nought that we returned all of us to the wall every one unto his work
nd it came to pass from that time forth that the half of my servants wrought in the work and the other half of them held both the spears the shields and the bows and the habergeons and the rulers were behind all the house of udah
hey which builded on the wall and they that bare burdens with those that laded every one with one of his hands wrought in the work and with the other hand held a weapon
or the builders every one had his sword girded by his side and so builded
nd he that sounded the trumpet was by me
nd  said unto the nobles and to the rulers and to the rest of the people he work is great and large and we are separated upon the wall one far from another
n what place therefore ye hear the sound of the trumpet resort ye thither unto us our od shall fight for us
o we laboured in the work and half of them held the spears from the rising of the morning till the stars appeared
ikewise at the same time said  unto the people et every one with his servant lodge within erusalem that in the night they may be a guard to us and labour on the day
o neither  nor my brethren nor my servants nor the men of the guard which followed me none of us put off our clothes saving that every one put them off for washing
nd there was a great cry of the people and of their wives against their brethren the ews
or there were that said e our sons and our daughters are many therefore we take up corn for them that we may eat and live
ome also there were that said e have mortgaged our lands vineyards and houses that we might buy corn because of the dearth
here were also that said e have borrowed money for the kings tribute and that upon our lands and vineyards
et now our flesh is as the flesh of our brethren our children as their children and lo we bring into bondage our sons and our daughters to be servants and some of our daughters are brought unto bondage already neither is it in our power to redeem them for other men have our lands and vineyards
nd  was very angry when  heard their cry and these words
hen  consulted with myself and  rebuked the nobles and the rulers and said unto them e exact usury every one of his brother
nd  set a great assembly against them
nd  said unto them e after our ability have redeemed our brethren the ews which were sold unto the heathen and will ye even sell your brethren or shall they be sold unto us
hen held they their peace and found nothing to answer
lso  said t is not good that ye do ought ye not to walk in the fear of our od because of the reproach of the heathen our enemies
 likewise and my brethren and my servants might exact of them money and corn  pray you let us leave off this usury
estore  pray you to them even this day their lands their vineyards their oliveyards and their houses also the hundredth part of the money and of the corn the wine and the oil that ye exact of them
hen said they e will restore them and will require nothing of them so will we do as thou sayest
hen  called the priests and took an oath of them that they should do according to this promise
lso  shook my lap and said o od shake out every man from his house and from his labour that performeth not this promise even thus be he shaken out and emptied
nd all the congregation said men and praised the 
nd the people did according to this promise
oreover from the time that  was appointed to be their governor in the land of udah from the twentieth year even unto the two and thirtieth year of rtaxerxes the king that is twelve years  and my brethren have not eaten the bread of the governor
ut the former governors that had been before me were chargeable unto the people and had taken of them bread and wine beside forty shekels of silver yea even their servants bare rule over the people but so did not  because of the fear of od
ea also  continued in the work of this wall neither bought we any land and all my servants were gathered thither unto the work
oreover there were at my table an hundred and fifty of the ews and rulers beside those that came unto us from among the heathen that are about us
ow that which was prepared for me daily was one ox and six choice sheep also fowls were prepared for me and once in ten days store of all sorts of wine yet for all this required not  the bread of the governor because the bondage was heavy upon this people
hink upon me my od for good according to all that  have done for this people
ow it came to pass when anballat and obiah and eshem the rabian and the rest of our enemies heard that  had builded the wall and that there was no breach left therein though at that time  had not set up the doors upon the gates that anballat and eshem sent unto me saying ome let us meet together in some one of the villages in the plain of no
ut they thought to do me mischief
nd  sent messengers unto them saying  am doing a great work so that  cannot come down why should the work cease whilst  leave it and come down to you
et they sent unto me four times after this sort and  answered them after the same manner
hen sent anballat his servant unto me in like manner the fifth time with an open letter in his hand wherein was written t is reported among the heathen and ashmu saith it that thou and the ews think to rebel for which cause thou buildest the wall that thou mayest be their king according to these words
nd thou hast also appointed prophets to preach of thee at erusalem saying here is a king in udah and now shall it be reported to the king according to these words
ome now therefore and let us take counsel together
hen  sent unto him saying here are no such things done as thou sayest but thou feignest them out of thine own heart
or they all made us afraid saying heir hands shall be weakened from the work that it be not done
ow therefore  od strengthen my hands
fterward  came unto the house of hemaiah the son of elaiah the son of ehetabeel who was shut up and he said et us meet together in the house of od within the temple and let us shut the doors of the temple for they will come to slay thee yea in the night will they come to slay thee
nd  said hould such a man as  flee and who is there that being as  am would go into the temple to save his life
 will not go in
nd lo  perceived that od had not sent him but that he pronounced this prophecy against me for obiah and anballat had hired him
herefore was he hired that  should be afraid and do so and sin and that they might have matter for an evil report that they might reproach me
y od think thou upon obiah and anballat according to these their works and on the prophetess oadiah and the rest of the prophets that would have put me in fear
o the wall was finished in the twenty and fifth day of the month lul in fifty and two days
nd it came to pass that when all our enemies heard thereof and all the heathen that were about us saw these things they were much cast down in their own eyes for they perceived that this work was wrought of our od
oreover in those days the nobles of udah sent many letters unto obiah and the letters of obiah came unto them
or there were many in udah sworn unto him because he was the son in law of hechaniah the son of rah and his son ohanan had taken the daughter of eshullam the son of erechiah
lso they reported his good deeds before me and uttered my words to him
nd obiah sent letters to put me in fear
ow it came to pass when the wall was built and  had set up the doors and the porters and the singers and the evites were appointed that  gave my brother anani and ananiah the ruler of the palace charge over erusalem for he was a faithful man and feared od above many
nd  said unto them et not the gates of erusalem be opened until the sun be hot and while they stand by let them shut the doors and bar them and appoint watches of the inhabitants of erusalem every one in his watch and every one to be over against his house
ow the city was large and great but the people were few therein and the houses were not builded
nd my od put into mine heart to gather together the nobles and the rulers and the people that they might be reckoned by genealogy
nd  found a register of the genealogy of them which came up at the first and found written therein hese are the children of the province that went up out of the captivity of those that had been carried away whom ebuchadnezzar the king of abylon had carried away and came again to erusalem and to udah every one unto his city who came with erubbabel eshua ehemiah zariah aamiah ahamani ordecai ilshan ispereth igvai ehum aanah

nd these were they which went up also from elmelah elharesha herub ddon and mmer but they could not shew their fathers house nor their seed whether they were of srael
he children of elaiah the children of obiah the children of ekoda six hundred forty and two
nd of the priests the children of abaiah the children of oz the children of arzillai which took one of the daughters of arzillai the ileadite to wife and was called after their name
hese sought their register among those that were reckoned by genealogy but it was not found therefore were they as polluted put from the priesthood
nd the irshatha said unto them that they should not eat of the most holy things till there stood up a priest with rim and hummim
he whole congregation together was forty and two thousand three hundred and threescore beside their manservants and their maidservants of whom there were seven thousand three hundred thirty and seven and they had two hundred forty and five singing men and singing women
heir horses seven hundred thirty and six their mules two hundred forty and five their camels four hundred thirty and five six thousand seven hundred and twenty asses
nd some of the chief of the fathers gave unto the work
he irshatha gave to the treasure a thousand drams of gold fifty basons five hundred and thirty priests garments
nd some of the chief of the fathers gave to the treasure of the work twenty thousand drams of gold and two thousand and two hundred pound of silver
nd that which the rest of the people gave was twenty thousand drams of gold and two thousand pound of silver and threescore and seven priests garments
o the priests and the evites and the porters and the singers and some of the people and the ethinims and all srael dwelt in their cities and when the seventh month came the children of srael were in their cities
nd all the people gathered themselves together as one man into the street that was before the water gate and they spake unto zra the scribe to bring the book of the law of oses which the  had commanded to srael
nd zra the priest brought the law before the congregation both of men and women and all that could hear with understanding upon the first day of the seventh month
nd he read therein before the street that was before the water gate from the morning until midday before the men and the women and those that could understand and the ears of all the people were attentive unto the book of the law
nd zra the scribe stood upon a pulpit of wood which they had made for the purpose and beside him stood attithiah and hema and naiah and rijah and ilkiah and aaseiah on his right hand and on his left hand edaiah and ishael and alchiah and ashum and ashbadana echariah and eshullam
nd zra opened the book in the sight of all the people for he was above all the people and when he opened it all the people stood up and zra blessed the  the great od
nd all the people answered men men with lifting up their hands and they bowed their heads and worshipped the  with their faces to the ground
lso eshua and ani and herebiah amin kkub habbethai odijah aaseiah elita zariah ozabad anan elaiah and the evites caused the people to understand the law and the people stood in their place
o they read in the book in the law of od distinctly and gave the sense and caused them to understand the reading
nd ehemiah which is the irshatha and zra the priest the scribe and the evites that taught the people said unto all the people his day is holy unto the  your od mourn not nor weep
or all the people wept when they heard the words of the law
hen he said unto them o your way eat the fat and drink the sweet and send portions unto them for whom nothing is prepared for this day is holy unto our ord neither be ye sorry for the joy of the  is your strength
o the evites stilled all the people saying old your peace for the day is holy neither be ye grieved
nd all the people went their way to eat and to drink and to send portions and to make great mirth because they had understood the words that were declared unto them
nd on the second day were gathered together the chief of the fathers of all the people the priests and the evites unto zra the scribe even to understand the words of the law
nd they found written in the law which the  had commanded by oses that the children of srael should dwell in booths in the feast of the seventh month and that they should publish and proclaim in all their cities and in erusalem saying o forth unto the mount and fetch olive branches and pine branches and myrtle branches and palm branches and branches of thick trees to make booths as it is written
o the people went forth and brought them and made themselves booths every one upon the roof of his house and in their courts and in the courts of the house of od and in the street of the water gate and in the street of the gate of phraim
nd all the congregation of them that were come again out of the captivity made booths and sat under the booths for since the days of eshua the son of un unto that day had not the children of srael done so
nd there was very great gladness
lso day by day from the first day unto the last day he read in the book of the law of od
nd they kept the feast seven days and on the eighth day was a solemn assembly according unto the manner
ow in the twenty and fourth day of this month the children of srael were assembled with fasting and with sackclothes and earth upon them
nd the seed of srael separated themselves from all strangers and stood and confessed their sins and the iniquities of their fathers
nd they stood up in their place and read in the book of the law of the  their od one fourth part of the day and another fourth part they confessed and worshipped the  their od
hen stood up upon the stairs of the evites eshua and ani admiel hebaniah unni herebiah ani and henani and cried with a loud voice unto the  their od
hen the evites eshua and admiel ani ashabniah herebiah odijah hebaniah and ethahiah said tand up and bless the  your od for ever and ever and blessed be thy glorious name which is exalted above all blessing and praise
hou even thou art  alone thou hast made heaven the heaven of heavens with all their host the earth and all things that are therein the seas and all that is therein and thou preservest them all and the host of heaven worshippeth thee
hou art the  the od who didst choose bram and broughtest him forth out of r of the haldees and gavest him the name of braham and foundest his heart faithful before thee and madest a covenant with him to give the land of the anaanites the ittites the morites and the erizzites and the ebusites and the irgashites to give it  say to his seed and hast performed thy words for thou art righteous and didst see the affliction of our fathers in gypt and heardest their cry by the ed sea and shewedst signs and wonders upon haraoh and on all his servants and on all the people of his land for thou knewest that they dealt proudly against them
o didst thou get thee a name as it is this day
nd thou didst divide the sea before them so that they went through the midst of the sea on the dry land and their persecutors thou threwest into the deeps as a stone into the mighty waters
oreover thou leddest them in the day by a cloudy pillar and in the night by a pillar of fire to give them light in the way wherein they should go
hou camest down also upon mount inai and spakest with them from heaven and gavest them right judgments and true laws good statutes and commandments and madest known unto them thy holy sabbath and commandedst them precepts statutes and laws by the hand of oses thy servant and gavest them bread from heaven for their hunger and broughtest forth water for them out of the rock for their thirst and promisedst them that they should go in to possess the land which thou hadst sworn to give them
ut they and our fathers dealt proudly and hardened their necks and hearkened not to thy commandments and refused to obey neither were mindful of thy wonders that thou didst among them but hardened their necks and in their rebellion appointed a captain to return to their bondage but thou art a od ready to pardon gracious and merciful slow to anger and of great kindness and forsookest them not
ea when they had made them a molten calf and said his is thy od that brought thee up out of gypt and had wrought great provocations yet thou in thy manifold mercies forsookest them not in the wilderness the pillar of the cloud departed not from them by day to lead them in the way neither the pillar of fire by night to shew them light and the way wherein they should go
hou gavest also thy good spirit to instruct them and withheldest not thy manna from their mouth and gavest them water for their thirst
ea forty years didst thou sustain them in the wilderness so that they lacked nothing their clothes waxed not old and their feet swelled not
oreover thou gavest them kingdoms and nations and didst divide them into corners so they possessed the land of ihon and the land of the king of eshbon and the land of g king of ashan
heir children also multipliedst thou as the stars of heaven and broughtest them into the land concerning which thou hadst promised to their fathers that they should go in to possess it
o the children went in and possessed the land and thou subduedst before them the inhabitants of the land the anaanites and gavest them into their hands with their kings and the people of the land that they might do with them as they would
nd they took strong cities and a fat land and possessed houses full of all goods wells digged vineyards and oliveyards and fruit trees in abundance so they did eat and were filled and became fat and delighted themselves in thy great goodness
evertheless they were disobedient and rebelled against thee and cast thy law behind their backs and slew thy prophets which testified against them to turn them to thee and they wrought great provocations
herefore thou deliveredst them into the hand of their enemies who vexed them and in the time of their trouble when they cried unto thee thou heardest them from heaven and according to thy manifold mercies thou gavest them saviours who saved them out of the hand of their enemies
ut after they had rest they did evil again before thee therefore leftest thou them in the hand of their enemies so that they had the dominion over them yet when they returned and cried unto thee thou heardest them from heaven and many times didst thou deliver them according to thy mercies and testifiedst against them that thou mightest bring them again unto thy law yet they dealt proudly and hearkened not unto thy commandments but sinned against thy judgments which if a man do he shall live in them and withdrew the shoulder and hardened their neck and would not hear
et many years didst thou forbear them and testifiedst against them by thy spirit in thy prophets yet would they not give ear therefore gavest thou them into the hand of the people of the lands
evertheless for thy great mercies sake thou didst not utterly consume them nor forsake them for thou art a gracious and merciful od
ow therefore our od the great the mighty and the terrible od who keepest covenant and mercy let not all the trouble seem little before thee that hath come upon us on our kings on our princes and on our priests and on our prophets and on our fathers and on all thy people since the time of the kings of ssyria unto this day
owbeit thou art just in all that is brought upon us for thou hast done right but we have done wickedly neither have our kings our princes our priests nor our fathers kept thy law nor hearkened unto thy commandments and thy testimonies wherewith thou didst testify against them
or they have not served thee in their kingdom and in thy great goodness that thou gavest them and in the large and fat land which thou gavest before them neither turned they from their wicked works
ehold we are servants this day and for the land that thou gavest unto our fathers to eat the fruit thereof and the good thereof behold we are servants in it and it yieldeth much increase unto the kings whom thou hast set over us because of our sins also they have dominion over our bodies and over our cattle at their pleasure and we are in great distress
nd because of all this we make a sure covenant and write it and our princes evites and priests seal unto it
ow those that sealed were ehemiah the irshatha the son of achaliah and idkijah eraiah zariah eremiah ashur mariah alchijah attush hebaniah alluch arim eremoth badiah aniel innethon aruch eshullam bijah ijamin aaziah ilgai hemaiah these were the priests
nd the evites both eshua the son of zaniah innui of the sons of enadad admiel and their brethren hebaniah odijah elita elaiah anan icha ehob ashabiah accur herebiah hebaniah odijah ani eninu
he chief of the people arosh ahathmoab lam atthu ani unni zgad ebai donijah igvai din ter izkijah zzur odijah ashum ezai ariph nathoth ebai agpiash eshullam ezir eshezabeel adok addua elatiah anan naiah oshea ananiah ashub allohesh ileha hobek ehum ashabnah aaseiah and hijah anan nan alluch arim aanah
nd the rest of the people the priests the evites the porters the singers the ethinims and all they that had separated themselves from the people of the lands unto the law of od their wives their sons and their daughters every one having knowledge and having understanding they clave to their brethren their nobles and entered into a curse and into an oath to walk in ods law which was given by oses the servant of od and to observe and do all the commandments of the  our ord and his judgments and his statutes and that we would not give our daughters unto the people of the land nor take their daughters for our sons and if the people of the land bring ware or any victuals on the sabbath day to sell that we would not buy it of them on the sabbath or on the holy day and that we would leave the seventh year and the exaction of every debt
lso we made ordinances for us to charge ourselves yearly with the third part of a shekel for the service of the house of our od for the shewbread and for the continual meat offering and for the continual burnt offering of the sabbaths of the new moons for the set feasts and for the holy things and for the sin offerings to make an atonement for srael and for all the work of the house of our od
nd we cast the lots among the priests the evites and the people for the wood offering to bring it into the house of our od after the houses of our fathers at times appointed year by year to burn upon the altar of the  our od as it is written in the law and to bring the firstfruits of our ground and the firstfruits of all fruit of all trees year by year unto the house of the  also the firstborn of our sons and of our cattle as it is written in the law and the firstlings of our herds and of our flocks to bring to the house of our od unto the priests that minister in the house of our od and that we should bring the firstfruits of our dough and our offerings and the fruit of all manner of trees of wine and of oil unto the priests to the chambers of the house of our od and the tithes of our ground unto the evites that the same evites might have the tithes in all the cities of our tillage
nd the priest the son of aron shall be with the evites when the evites take tithes and the evites shall bring up the tithe of the tithes unto the house of our od to the chambers into the treasure house
or the children of srael and the children of evi shall bring the offering of the corn of the new wine and the oil unto the chambers where are the vessels of the sanctuary and the priests that minister and the porters and the singers and we will not forsake the house of our od
nd the rulers of the people dwelt at erusalem the rest of the people also cast lots to bring one of ten to dwell in erusalem the holy city and nine parts to dwell in other cities
nd the people blessed all the men that willingly offered themselves to dwell at erusalem
ow these are the chief of the province that dwelt in erusalem but in the cities of udah dwelt every one in his possession in their cities to wit srael the priests and the evites and the ethinims and the children of olomons servants
nd at erusalem dwelt certain of the children of udah and of the children of enjamin
f the children of udah thaiah the son of zziah the son of echariah the son of mariah the son of hephatiah the son of ahalaleel of the children of erez and aaseiah the son of aruch the son of olhozeh the son of azaiah the son of daiah the son of oiarib the son of echariah the son of hiloni
ll the sons of erez that dwelt at erusalem were four hundred threescore and eight valiant men
nd these are the sons of enjamin allu the son of eshullam the son of oed the son of edaiah the son of olaiah the son of aaseiah the son of thiel the son of esaiah

nd their brethren that did the work of the house were eight hundred twenty and two and daiah the son of eroham the son of elaliah the son of mzi the son of echariah the son of ashur the son of alchiah and his brethren chief of the fathers two hundred forty and two and mashai the son of zareel the son of hasai the son of eshillemoth the son of mmer and their brethren mighty men of valour an hundred twenty and eight and their overseer was abdiel the son of one of the great men
lso of the evites hemaiah the son of ashub the son of zrikam the son of ashabiah the son of unni and habbethai and ozabad of the chief of the evites had the oversight of the outward business of the house of od

oreover the porters kkub almon and their brethren that kept the gates were an hundred seventy and two
