f the sons of saph the singers were over the business of the house of od
or it was the kings commandment concerning them that a certain portion should be for the singers due for every day
nd ethahiah the son of eshezabeel of the children of erah the son of udah was at the kings hand in all matters concerning the people
nd for the villages with their fields some of the children of udah dwelt at irjatharba and in the villages thereof and at ibon and in the villages thereof and at ekabzeel and in the villages thereof and at eshua and at oladah and at ethphelet and at azarshual and at eersheba and in the villages thereof and at iklag and at ekonah and in the villages thereof and at nrimmon and at areah and at armuth anoah dullam and in their villages at achish and the fields thereof at zekah and in the villages thereof
nd they dwelt from eersheba unto the valley of innom
he children also of enjamin from eba dwelt at ichmash and ija and ethel and in their villages and at nathoth ob naniah azor amah ittaim adid eboim eballat od and no the valley of craftsmen
nd of the evites were divisions in udah and in enjamin
ow these are the priests and the evites that went up with erubbabel the son of healtiel and eshua eraiah eremiah zra mariah alluch attush hechaniah ehum eremoth ddo innetho bijah iamin aadiah ilgah hemaiah and oiarib edaiah allu mok ilkiah edaiah
hese were the chief of the priests and of their brethren in the days of eshua
oreover the evites eshua innui admiel herebiah udah and attaniah which was over the thanksgiving he and his brethren
lso akbukiah and nni their brethren were over against them in the watches
nd eshua begat oiakim oiakim also begat liashib and liashib begat oiada and oiada begat onathan and onathan begat addua
nd in the days of oiakim were priests the chief of the fathers of eraiah eraiah of eremiah ananiah of zra eshullam of mariah ehohanan of elicu onathan of hebaniah oseph of arim dna of eraioth elkai of ddo echariah of innethon eshullam of bijah ichri of iniamin of oadiah iltai of ilgah hammua of hemaiah ehonathan and of oiarib attenai of edaiah zzi of allai allai of mok ber of ilkiah ashabiah of edaiah ethaneel
he evites in the days of liashib oiada and ohanan and addua were recorded chief of the fathers also the priests to the reign of arius the ersian
he sons of evi the chief of the fathers were written in the book of the chronicles even until the days of ohanan the son of liashib
nd the chief of the evites ashabiah herebiah and eshua the son of admiel with their brethren over against them to praise and to give thanks according to the commandment of avid the man of od ward over against ward
attaniah and akbukiah badiah eshullam almon kkub were porters keeping the ward at the thresholds of the gates
hese were in the days of oiakim the son of eshua the son of ozadak and in the days of ehemiah the governor and of zra the priest the scribe
nd at the dedication of the wall of erusalem they sought the evites out of all their places to bring them to erusalem to keep the dedication with gladness both with thanksgivings and with singing with cymbals psalteries and with harps
nd the sons of the singers gathered themselves together both out of the plain country round about erusalem and from the villages of etophathi also from the house of ilgal and out of the fields of eba and zmaveth for the singers had builded them villages round about erusalem
nd the priests and the evites purified themselves and purified the people and the gates and the wall
hen  brought up the princes of udah upon the wall and appointed two great companies of them that gave thanks whereof one went on the right hand upon the wall toward the dung gate and after them went oshaiah and half of the princes of udah and zariah zra and eshullam udah and enjamin and hemaiah and eremiah and certain of the priests sons with trumpets namely echariah the son of onathan the son of hemaiah the son of attaniah the son of ichaiah the son of accur the son of saph and his brethren hemaiah and zarael ilalai ilalai aai ethaneel and udah anani with the musical instruments of avid the man of od and zra the scribe before them
nd at the fountain gate which was over against them they went up by the stairs of the city of avid at the going up of the wall above the house of avid even unto the water gate eastward
nd the other company of them that gave thanks went over against them and  after them and the half of the people upon the wall from beyond the tower of the furnaces even unto the broad wall and from above the gate of phraim and above the old gate and above the fish gate and the tower of ananeel and the tower of eah even unto the sheep gate and they stood still in the prison gate
o stood the two companies of them that gave thanks in the house of od and  and the half of the rulers with me and the priests liakim aaseiah iniamin ichaiah lioenai echariah and ananiah with trumpets and aaseiah and hemaiah and leazar and zzi and ehohanan and alchijah and lam and zer
nd the singers sang loud with ezrahiah their overseer
lso that day they offered great sacrifices and rejoiced for od had made them rejoice with great joy the wives also and the children rejoiced so that the joy of erusalem was heard even afar off
nd at that time were some appointed over the chambers for the treasures for the offerings for the firstfruits and for the tithes to gather into them out of the fields of the cities the portions of the law for the priests and evites for udah rejoiced for the priests and for the evites that waited
nd both the singers and the porters kept the ward of their od and the ward of the purification according to the commandment of avid and of olomon his son
or in the days of avid and saph of old there were chief of the singers and songs of praise and thanksgiving unto od
nd all srael in the days of erubbabel and in the days of ehemiah gave the portions of the singers and the porters every day his portion and they sanctified holy things unto the evites and the evites sanctified them unto the children of aron
n that day they read in the book of oses in the audience of the people and therein was found written that the mmonite and the oabite should not come into the congregation of od for ever because they met not the children of srael with bread and with water but hired alaam against them that he should curse them howbeit our od turned the curse into a blessing
ow it came to pass when they had heard the law that they separated from srael all the mixed multitude
nd before this liashib the priest having the oversight of the chamber of the house of our od was allied unto obiah and he had prepared for him a great chamber where aforetime they laid the meat offerings the frankincense and the vessels and the tithes of the corn the new wine and the oil which was commanded to be given to the evites and the singers and the porters and the offerings of the priests
ut in all this time was not  at erusalem for in the two and thirtieth year of rtaxerxes king of abylon came  unto the king and after certain days obtained  leave of the king and  came to erusalem and understood of the evil that liashib did for obiah in preparing him a chamber in the courts of the house of od
nd it grieved me sore therefore  cast forth all the household stuff of obiah out of the chamber
hen  commanded and they cleansed the chambers and thither brought  again the vessels of the house of od with the meat offering and the frankincense
nd  perceived that the portions of the evites had not been given them for the evites and the singers that did the work were fled every one to his field
hen contended  with the rulers and said hy is the house of od forsaken
nd  gathered them together and set them in their place
hen brought all udah the tithe of the corn and the new wine and the oil unto the treasuries
nd  made treasurers over the treasuries helemiah the priest and adok the scribe and of the evites edaiah and next to them was anan the son of accur the son of attaniah for they were counted faithful and their office was to distribute unto their brethren
emember me  my od concerning this and wipe not out my good deeds that  have done for the house of my od and for the offices thereof
n those days saw  in udah some treading wine presses on the sabbath and bringing in sheaves and lading asses as also wine grapes and figs and all manner of burdens which they brought into erusalem on the sabbath day and  testified against them in the day wherein they sold victuals
here dwelt men of yre also therein which brought fish and all manner of ware and sold on the sabbath unto the children of udah and in erusalem
hen  contended with the nobles of udah and said unto them hat evil thing is this that ye do and profane the sabbath day
id not your fathers thus and did not our od bring all this evil upon us and upon this city yet ye bring more wrath upon srael by profaning the sabbath
nd it came to pass that when the gates of erusalem began to be dark before the sabbath  commanded that the gates should be shut and charged that they should not be opened till after the sabbath and some of my servants set  at the gates that there should no burden be brought in on the sabbath day
o the merchants and sellers of all kind of ware lodged without erusalem once or twice
hen  testified against them and said unto them hy lodge ye about the wall if ye do so again  will lay hands on you
rom that time forth came they no more on the sabbath
nd  commanded the evites that they should cleanse themselves and that they should come and keep the gates to sanctify the sabbath day
emember me  my od concerning this also and spare me according to the greatness of thy mercy
n those days also saw  ews that had married wives of shdod of mmon and of oab and their children spake half in the speech of shdod and could not speak in the ews language but according to the language of each people
nd  contended with them and cursed them and smote certain of them and plucked off their hair and made them swear by od saying e shall not give your daughters unto their sons nor take their daughters unto your sons or for yourselves
id not olomon king of srael sin by these things yet among many nations was there no king like him who was beloved of his od and od made him king over all srael nevertheless even him did outlandish women cause to sin
hall we then hearken unto you to do all this great evil to transgress against our od in marrying strange wives
nd one of the sons of oiada the son of liashib the high priest was son in law to anballat the oronite therefore  chased him from me
emember them  my od because they have defiled the priesthood and the covenant of the priesthood and of the evites
hus cleansed  them from all strangers and appointed the wards of the priests and the evites every one in his business and for the wood offering at times appointed and for the firstfruits
emember me  my od for good
ow it came to pass in the days of hasuerus this is hasuerus which reigned from ndia even unto thiopia over an hundred and seven and twenty provinces that in those days when the king hasuerus sat on the throne of his kingdom which was in hushan the palace in the third year of his reign he made a feast unto all his princes and his servants the power of ersia and edia the nobles and princes of the provinces being before him when he shewed the riches of his glorious kingdom and the honour of his excellent majesty many days even an hundred and fourscore days
nd when these days were expired the king made a feast unto all the people that were present in hushan the palace both unto great and small seven days in the court of the garden of the kings palace where were white green and blue hangings fastened with cords of fine linen and purple to silver rings and pillars of marble the beds were of gold and silver upon a pavement of red and blue and white and black marble
nd they gave them drink in vessels of gold the vessels being diverse one from another  and royal wine in abundance according to the state of the king
nd the drinking was according to the law none did compel for so the king had appointed to all the officers of his house that they should do according to every mans pleasure
lso ashti the queen made a feast for the women in the royal house which belonged to king hasuerus
n the seventh day when the heart of the king was merry with wine he commanded ehuman iztha arbona igtha and bagtha ethar and arcas the seven chamberlains that served in the presence of hasuerus the king to bring ashti the queen before the king with the crown royal to shew the people and the princes her beauty for she was fair to look on
ut the queen ashti refused to come at the kings commandment by his chamberlains therefore was the king very wroth and his anger burned in him
hen the king said to the wise men which knew the times for so was the kings manner toward all that knew law and judgment and the next unto him was arshena hethar dmatha arshish eres arsena and emucan the seven princes of ersia and edia which saw the kings face and which sat the first in the kingdom what shall we do unto the queen ashti according to law because she hath not performed the commandment of the king hasuerus by the chamberlains
nd emucan answered before the king and the princes ashti the queen hath not done wrong to the king only but also to all the princes and to all the people that are in all the provinces of the king hasuerus
or this deed of the queen shall come abroad unto all women so that they shall despise their husbands in their eyes when it shall be reported he king hasuerus commanded ashti the queen to be brought in before him but she came not
ikewise shall the ladies of ersia and edia say this day unto all the kings princes which have heard of the deed of the queen
hus shall there arise too much contempt and wrath
f it please the king let there go a royal commandment from him and let it be written among the laws of the ersians and the edes that it be not altered hat ashti come no more before king hasuerus and let the king give her royal estate unto another that is better than she
nd when the kings decree which he shall make shall be published throughout all his empire for it is great  all the wives shall give to their husbands honour both to great and small
nd the saying pleased the king and the princes and the king did according to the word of emucan for he sent letters into all the kings provinces into every province according to the writing thereof and to every people after their language that every man should bear rule in his own house and that it should be published according to the language of every people
fter these things when the wrath of king hasuerus was appeased he remembered ashti and what she had done and what was decreed against her
hen said the kings servants that ministered unto him et there be fair young virgins sought for the king and let the king appoint officers in all the provinces of his kingdom that they may gather together all the fair young virgins unto hushan the palace to the house of the women unto the custody of ege the kings chamberlain keeper of the women and let their things for purification be given them and let the maiden which pleaseth the king be queen instead of ashti
nd the thing pleased the king and he did so
ow in hushan the palace there was a certain ew whose name was ordecai the son of air the son of himei the son of ish a enjamite who had been carried away from erusalem with the captivity which had been carried away with econiah king of udah whom ebuchadnezzar the king of abylon had carried away
nd he brought up adassah that is sther his uncles daughter for she had neither father nor mother and the maid was fair and beautiful whom ordecai when her father and mother were dead took for his own daughter
o it came to pass when the kings commandment and his decree was heard and when many maidens were gathered together unto hushan the palace to the custody of egai that sther was brought also unto the kings house to the custody of egai keeper of the women
nd the maiden pleased him and she obtained kindness of him and he speedily gave her her things for purification with such things as belonged to her and seven maidens which were meet to be given her out of the kings house and he preferred her and her maids unto the best place of the house of the women
sther had not shewed her people nor her kindred for ordecai had charged her that she should not shew it
nd ordecai walked every day before the court of the womens house to know how sther did and what should become of her
ow when every maids turn was come to go in to king hasuerus after that she had been twelve months according to the manner of the women for so were the days of their purifications accomplished to wit six months with oil of myrrh and six months with sweet odours and with other things for the purifying of the women then thus came every maiden unto the king whatsoever she desired was given her to go with her out of the house of the women unto the kings house
n the evening she went and on the morrow she returned into the second house of the women to the custody of haashgaz the kings chamberlain which kept the concubines she came in unto the king no more except the king delighted in her and that she were called by name
ow when the turn of sther the daughter of bihail the uncle of ordecai who had taken her for his daughter was come to go in unto the king she required nothing but what egai the kings chamberlain the keeper of the women appointed
nd sther obtained favour in the sight of all them that looked upon her
o sther was taken unto king hasuerus into his house royal in the tenth month which is the month ebeth in the seventh year of his reign
nd the king loved sther above all the women and she obtained grace and favour in his sight more than all the virgins so that he set the royal crown upon her head and made her queen instead of ashti
hen the king made a great feast unto all his princes and his servants even sthers feast and he made a release to the provinces and gave gifts according to the state of the king
nd when the virgins were gathered together the second time then ordecai sat in the kings gate
sther had not yet shewed her kindred nor her people as ordecai had charged her for sther did the commandment of ordecai like as when she was brought up with him
n those days while ordecai sat in the kings gate two of the kings chamberlains igthan and eresh of those which kept the door were wroth and sought to lay hand on the king hasuerus
nd the thing was known to ordecai who told it unto sther the queen and sther certified the king thereof in ordecais name
nd when inquisition was made of the matter it was found out therefore they were both hanged on a tree and it was written in the book of the chronicles before the king
fter these things did king hasuerus promote aman the son of ammedatha the gagite and advanced him and set his seat above all the princes that were with him
nd all the kings servants that were in the kings gate bowed and reverenced aman for the king had so commanded concerning him
ut ordecai bowed not nor did him reverence
hen the kings servants which were in the kings gate said unto ordecai hy transgressest thou the kings commandment
ow it came to pass when they spake daily unto him and he hearkened not unto them that they told aman to see whether ordecais matters would stand for he had told them that he was a ew
nd when aman saw that ordecai bowed not nor did him reverence then was aman full of wrath
nd he thought scorn to lay hands on ordecai alone for they had shewed him the people of ordecai wherefore aman sought to destroy all the ews that were throughout the whole kingdom of hasuerus even the people of ordecai
n the first month that is the month isan in the twelfth year of king hasuerus they cast ur that is the lot before aman from day to day and from month to month to the twelfth month that is the month dar
nd aman said unto king hasuerus here is a certain people scattered abroad and dispersed among the people in all the provinces of thy kingdom and their laws are diverse from all people neither keep they the kings laws therefore it is not for the kings profit to suffer them
f it please the king let it be written that they may be destroyed and  will pay ten thousand talents of silver to the hands of those that have the charge of the business to bring it into the kings treasuries
nd the king took his ring from his hand and gave it unto aman the son of ammedatha the gagite the ews enemy
nd the king said unto aman he silver is given to thee the people also to do with them as it seemeth good to thee
hen were the kings scribes called on the thirteenth day of the first month and there was written according to all that aman had commanded unto the kings lieutenants and to the governors that were over every province and to the rulers of every people of every province according to the writing thereof and to every people after their language in the name of king hasuerus was it written and sealed with the kings ring
nd the letters were sent by posts into all the kings provinces to destroy to kill and to cause to perish all ews both young and old little children and women in one day even upon the thirteenth day of the twelfth month which is the month dar and to take the spoil of them for a prey
he copy of the writing for a commandment to be given in every province was published unto all people that they should be ready against that day
he posts went out being hastened by the kings commandment and the decree was given in hushan the palace
nd the king and aman sat down to drink but the city hushan was perplexed
hen ordecai perceived all that was done ordecai rent his clothes and put on sackcloth with ashes and went out into the midst of the city and cried with a loud and a bitter cry and came even before the kings gate for none might enter into the kings gate clothed with sackcloth
nd in every province whithersoever the kings commandment and his decree came there was great mourning among the ews and fasting and weeping and wailing and many lay in sackcloth and ashes
o sthers maids and her chamberlains came and told it her
hen was the queen exceedingly grieved and she sent raiment to clothe ordecai and to take away his sackcloth from him but he received it not
hen called sther for atach one of the kings chamberlains whom he had appointed to attend upon her and gave him a commandment to ordecai to know what it was and why it was
o atach went forth to ordecai unto the street of the city which was before the kings gate
nd ordecai told him of all that had happened unto him and of the sum of the money that aman had promised to pay to the kings treasuries for the ews to destroy them
lso he gave him the copy of the writing of the decree that was given at hushan to destroy them to shew it unto sther and to declare it unto her and to charge her that she should go in unto the king to make supplication unto him and to make request before him for her people
nd atach came and told sther the words of ordecai
gain sther spake unto atach and gave him commandment unto ordecai all the kings servants and the people of the kings provinces do know that whosoever whether man or woman shall come unto the king into the inner court who is not called there is one law of his to put him to death except such to whom the king shall hold out the golden sceptre that he may live but  have not been called to come in unto the king these thirty days
nd they told to ordecai sthers words
hen ordecai commanded to answer sther hink not with thyself that thou shalt escape in the kings house more than all the ews
or if thou altogether holdest thy peace at this time then shall there enlargement and deliverance arise to the ews from another place but thou and thy fathers house shall be destroyed and who knoweth whether thou art come to the kingdom for such a time as this
hen sther bade them return ordecai this answer o gather together all the ews that are present in hushan and fast ye for me and neither eat nor drink three days night or day  also and my maidens will fast likewise and so will  go in unto the king which is not according to the law and if  perish  perish
o ordecai went his way and did according to all that sther had commanded him
ow it came to pass on the third day that sther put on her royal apparel and stood in the inner court of the kings house over against the kings house and the king sat upon his royal throne in the royal house over against the gate of the house
nd it was so when the king saw sther the queen standing in the court that she obtained favour in his sight and the king held out to sther the golden sceptre that was in his hand
o sther drew near and touched the top of the sceptre
hen said the king unto her hat wilt thou queen sther and what is thy request it shall be even given thee to the half of the kingdom
nd sther answered f it seem good unto the king let the king and aman come this day unto the banquet that  have prepared for him
hen the king said ause aman to make haste that he may do as sther hath said
o the king and aman came to the banquet that sther had prepared
nd the king said unto sther at the banquet of wine hat is thy petition and it shall be granted thee and what is thy request even to the half of the kingdom it shall be performed
hen answered sther and said y petition and my request is if  have found favour in the sight of the king and if it please the king to grant my petition and to perform my request let the king and aman come to the banquet that  shall prepare for them and  will do to morrow as the king hath said
hen went aman forth that day joyful and with a glad heart but when aman saw ordecai in the kings gate that he stood not up nor moved for him he was full of indignation against ordecai
evertheless aman refrained himself and when he came home he sent and called for his friends and eresh his wife
nd aman told them of the glory of his riches and the multitude of his children and all the things wherein the king had promoted him and how he had advanced him above the princes and servants of the king
aman said moreover ea sther the queen did let no man come in with the king unto the banquet that she had prepared but myself and to morrow am  invited unto her also with the king
et all this availeth me nothing so long as  see ordecai the ew sitting at the kings gate
hen said eresh his wife and all his friends unto him et a gallows be made of fifty cubits high and to morrow speak thou unto the king that ordecai may be hanged thereon then go thou in merrily with the king unto the banquet
nd the thing pleased aman and he caused the gallows to be made
n that night could not the king sleep and he commanded to bring the book of records of the chronicles and they were read before the king
nd it was found written that ordecai had told of igthana and eresh two of the kings chamberlains the keepers of the door who sought to lay hand on the king hasuerus
nd the king said hat honour and dignity hath been done to ordecai for this
hen said the kings servants that ministered unto him here is nothing done for him
nd the king said ho is in the court
ow aman was come into the outward court of the kings house to speak unto the king to hang ordecai on the gallows that he had prepared for him
nd the kings servants said unto him ehold aman standeth in the court
nd the king said et him come in
o aman came in
nd the king said unto him hat shall be done unto the man whom the king delighteth to honour
ow aman thought in his heart o whom would the king delight to do honour more than to myself
nd aman answered the king or the man whom the king delighteth to honour let the royal apparel be brought which the king useth to wear and the horse that the king rideth upon and the crown royal which is set upon his head and let this apparel and horse be delivered to the hand of one of the kings most noble princes that they may array the man withal whom the king delighteth to honour and bring him on horseback through the street of the city and proclaim before him hus shall it be done to the man whom the king delighteth to honour
hen the king said to aman ake haste and take the apparel and the horse as thou hast said and do even so to ordecai the ew that sitteth at the kings gate let nothing fail of all that thou hast spoken
hen took aman the apparel and the horse and arrayed ordecai and brought him on horseback through the street of the city and proclaimed before him hus shall it be done unto the man whom the king delighteth to honour
nd ordecai came again to the kings gate
ut aman hasted to his house mourning and having his head covered
nd aman told eresh his wife and all his friends every thing that had befallen him
hen said his wise men and eresh his wife unto him f ordecai be of the seed of the ews before whom thou hast begun to fall thou shalt not prevail against him but shalt surely fall before him
nd while they were yet talking with him came the kings chamberlains and hasted to bring aman unto the banquet that sther had prepared
o the king and aman came to banquet with sther the queen
nd the king said again unto sther on the second day at the banquet of wine hat is thy petition queen sther and it shall be granted thee and what is thy request and it shall be performed even to the half of the kingdom
hen sther the queen answered and said f  have found favour in thy sight  king and if it please the king let my life be given me at my petition and my people at my request for we are sold  and my people to be destroyed to be slain and to perish
ut if we had been sold for bondmen and bondwomen  had held my tongue although the enemy could not countervail the kings damage
hen the king hasuerus answered and said unto sther the queen ho is he and where is he that durst presume in his heart to do so
nd sther said he adversary and enemy is this wicked aman
hen aman was afraid before the king and the queen
nd the king arising from the banquet of wine in his wrath went into the palace garden and aman stood up to make request for his life to sther the queen for he saw that there was evil determined against him by the king
hen the king returned out of the palace garden into the place of the banquet of wine and aman was fallen upon the bed whereon sther was
hen said the king ill he force the queen also before me in the house
s the word went out of the kings mouth they covered amans face
nd arbonah one of the chamberlains said before the king ehold also the gallows fifty cubits high which aman had made for ordecai who had spoken good for the king standeth in the house of aman
hen the king said ang him thereon
o they hanged aman on the gallows that he had prepared for ordecai
hen was the kings wrath pacified
n that day did the king hasuerus give the house of aman the ews enemy unto sther the queen
nd ordecai came before the king for sther had told what he was unto her
nd the king took off his ring which he had taken from aman and gave it unto ordecai
nd sther set ordecai over the house of aman
nd sther spake yet again before the king and fell down at his feet and besought him with tears to put away the mischief of aman the gagite and his device that he had devised against the ews
hen the king held out the golden sceptre toward sther
o sther arose and stood before the king and said f it please the king and if  have found favour in his sight and the thing seem right before the king and  be pleasing in his eyes let it be written to reverse the letters devised by aman the son of ammedatha the gagite which he wrote to destroy the ews which are in all the kings provinces for how can  endure to see the evil that shall come unto my people or how can  endure to see the destruction of my kindred
hen the king hasuerus said unto sther the queen and to ordecai the ew ehold  have given sther the house of aman and him they have hanged upon the gallows because he laid his hand upon the ews
rite ye also for the ews as it liketh you in the kings name and seal it with the kings ring for the writing which is written in the kings name and sealed with the kings ring may no man reverse
hen were the kings scribes called at that time in the third month that is the month ivan on the three and twentieth day thereof and it was written according to all that ordecai commanded unto the ews and to the lieutenants and the deputies and rulers of the provinces which are from ndia unto thiopia an hundred twenty and seven provinces unto every province according to the writing thereof and unto every people after their language and to the ews according to their writing and according to their language
nd he wrote in the king hasuerus name and sealed it with the kings ring and sent letters by posts on horseback and riders on mules camels and young dromedaries wherein the king granted the ews which were in every city to gather themselves together and to stand for their life to destroy to slay and to cause to perish all the power of the people and province that would assault them both little ones and women and to take the spoil of them for a prey upon one day in all the provinces of king hasuerus namely upon the thirteenth day of the twelfth month which is the month dar
he copy of the writing for a commandment to be given in every province was published unto all people and that the ews should be ready against that day to avenge themselves on their enemies
o the posts that rode upon mules and camels went out being hastened and pressed on by the kings commandment
nd the decree was given at hushan the palace
nd ordecai went out from the presence of the king in royal apparel of blue and white and with a great crown of gold and with a garment of fine linen and purple and the city of hushan rejoiced and was glad
he ews had light and gladness and joy and honour
nd in every province and in every city whithersoever the kings commandment and his decree came the ews had joy and gladness a feast and a good day
nd many of the people of the land became ews for the fear of the ews fell upon them
ow in the twelfth month that is the month dar on the thirteenth day of the same when the kings commandment and his decree drew near to be put in execution in the day that the enemies of the ews hoped to have power over them though it was turned to the contrary that the ews had rule over them that hated them the ews gathered themselves together in their cities throughout all the provinces of the king hasuerus to lay hand on such as sought their hurt and no man could withstand them for the fear of them fell upon all people
nd all the rulers of the provinces and the lieutenants and the deputies and officers of the king helped the ews because the fear of ordecai fell upon them
or ordecai was great in the kings house and his fame went out throughout all the provinces for this man ordecai waxed greater and greater
hus the ews smote all their enemies with the stroke of the sword and slaughter and destruction and did what they would unto those that hated them
nd in hushan the palace the ews slew and destroyed five hundred men
nd arshandatha and alphon and spatha and oratha and dalia and ridatha and armashta and risai and ridai and ajezatha the ten sons of aman the son of ammedatha the enemy of the ews slew they but on the spoil laid they not their hand
n that day the number of those that were slain in hushan the palace was brought before the king
nd the king said unto sther the queen he ews have slain and destroyed five hundred men in hushan the palace and the ten sons of aman what have they done in the rest of the kings provinces now what is thy petition and it shall be granted thee or what is thy request further and it shall be done
hen said sther f it please the king let it be granted to the ews which are in hushan to do to morrow also according unto this days decree and let amans ten sons be hanged upon the gallows
nd the king commanded it so to be done and the decree was given at hushan and they hanged amans ten sons
or the ews that were in hushan gathered themselves together on the fourteenth day also of the month dar and slew three hundred men at hushan but on the prey they laid not their hand
ut the other ews that were in the kings provinces gathered themselves together and stood for their lives and had rest from their enemies and slew of their foes seventy and five thousand but they laid not their hands on the prey on the thirteenth day of the month dar and on the fourteenth day of the same rested they and made it a day of feasting and gladness
ut the ews that were at hushan assembled together on the thirteenth day thereof and on the fourteenth thereof and on the fifteenth day of the same they rested and made it a day of feasting and gladness
herefore the ews of the villages that dwelt in the unwalled towns made the fourteenth day of the month dar a day of gladness and feasting and a good day and of sending portions one to another
nd ordecai wrote these things and sent letters unto all the ews that were in all the provinces of the king hasuerus both nigh and far to stablish this among them that they should keep the fourteenth day of the month dar and the fifteenth day of the same yearly as the days wherein the ews rested from their enemies and the month which was turned unto them from sorrow to joy and from mourning into a good day that they should make them days of feasting and joy and of sending portions one to another and gifts to the poor
nd the ews undertook to do as they had begun and as ordecai had written unto them because aman the son of ammedatha the gagite the enemy of all the ews had devised against the ews to destroy them and had cast ur that is the lot to consume them and to destroy them but when sther came before the king he commanded by letters that his wicked device which he devised against the ews should return upon his own head and that he and his sons should be hanged on the gallows
herefore they called these days urim after the name of ur
herefore for all the words of this letter and of that which they had seen concerning this matter and which had come unto them the ews ordained and took upon them and upon their seed and upon all such as joined themselves unto them so as it should not fail that they would keep these two days according to their writing and according to their appointed time every year and that these days should be remembered and kept throughout every generation every family every province and every city and that these days of urim should not fail from among the ews nor the memorial of them perish from their seed
hen sther the queen the daughter of bihail and ordecai the ew wrote with all authority to confirm this second letter of urim
nd he sent the letters unto all the ews to the hundred twenty and seven provinces of the kingdom of hasuerus with words of peace and truth to confirm these days of urim in their times appointed according as ordecai the ew and sther the queen had enjoined them and as they had decreed for themselves and for their seed the matters of the fastings and their cry
nd the decree of sther confirmed these matters of urim and it was written in the book
nd the king hasuerus laid a tribute upon the land and upon the isles of the sea
nd all the acts of his power and of his might and the declaration of the greatness of ordecai whereunto the king advanced him are they not written in the book of the chronicles of the kings of edia and ersia
or ordecai the ew was next unto king hasuerus and great among the ews and accepted of the multitude of his brethren seeking the wealth of his people and speaking peace to all his seed
here was a man in the land of z whose name was ob and that man was perfect and upright and one that feared od and eschewed evil
nd there were born unto him seven sons and three daughters
is substance also was seven thousand sheep and three thousand camels and five hundred yoke of oxen and five hundred she asses and a very great household so that this man was the greatest of all the men of the east
nd his sons went and feasted in their houses every one his day and sent and called for their three sisters to eat and to drink with them
nd it was so when the days of their feasting were gone about that ob sent and sanctified them and rose up early in the morning and offered burnt offerings according to the number of them all for ob said t may be that my sons have sinned and cursed od in their hearts
hus did ob continually
ow there was a day when the sons of od came to present themselves before the  and atan came also among them
nd the  said unto atan hence comest thou
hen atan answered the  and said rom going to and fro in the earth and from walking up and down in it
nd the  said unto atan ast thou considered my servant ob that there is none like him in the earth a perfect and an upright man one that feareth od and escheweth evil
hen atan answered the  and said oth ob fear od for nought
ast not thou made an hedge about him and about his house and about all that he hath on every side thou hast blessed the work of his hands and his substance is increased in the land
ut put forth thine hand now and touch all that he hath and he will curse thee to thy face
nd the  said unto atan ehold all that he hath is in thy power only upon himself put not forth thine hand
o atan went forth from the presence of the 
nd there was a day when his sons and his daughters were eating and drinking wine in their eldest brothers house and there came a messenger unto ob and said he oxen were plowing and the asses feeding beside them and the abeans fell upon them and took them away yea they have slain the servants with the edge of the sword and  only am escaped alone to tell thee
hile he was yet speaking there came also another and said he fire of od is fallen from heaven and hath burned up the sheep and the servants and consumed them and  only am escaped alone to tell thee
hile he was yet speaking there came also another and said he haldeans made out three bands and fell upon the camels and have carried them away yea and slain the servants with the edge of the sword and  only am escaped alone to tell thee
hile he was yet speaking there came also another and said hy sons and thy daughters were eating and drinking wine in their eldest brothers house and behold there came a great wind from the wilderness and smote the four corners of the house and it fell upon the young men and they are dead and  only am escaped alone to tell thee
hen ob arose and rent his mantle and shaved his head and fell down upon the ground and worshipped and said aked came  out of my mothers womb and naked shall  return thither the  gave and the  hath taken away blessed be the name of the 
n all this ob sinned not nor charged od foolishly
gain there was a day when the sons of od came to present themselves before the  and atan came also among them to present himself before the 
nd the  said unto atan rom whence comest thou

nd the  said unto atan ast thou considered my servant ob that there is none like him in the earth a perfect and an upright man one that feareth od and escheweth evil and still he holdeth fast his integrity although thou movedst me against him to destroy him without cause
nd atan answered the  and said kin for skin yea all that a man hath will he give for his life
ut put forth thine hand now and touch his bone and his flesh and he will curse thee to thy face
nd the  said unto atan ehold he is in thine hand but save his life
o went atan forth from the presence of the  and smote ob with sore boils from the sole of his foot unto his crown
nd he took him a potsherd to scrape himself withal and he sat down among the ashes

ut he said unto her hou speakest as one of the foolish women speaketh
hat shall we receive good at the hand of od and shall we not receive evil
n all this did not ob sin with his lips
ow when obs three friends heard of all this evil that was come upon him they came every one from his own place liphaz the emanite and ildad the huhite and ophar the aamathite for they had made an appointment together to come to mourn with him and to comfort him
nd when they lifted up their eyes afar off and knew him not they lifted up their voice and wept and they rent every one his mantle and sprinkled dust upon their heads toward heaven
o they sat down with him upon the ground seven days and seven nights and none spake a word unto him for they saw that his grief was very great
fter this opened ob his mouth and cursed his day
nd ob spake and said et the day perish wherein  was born nd the night in which it was said here is a man child conceived
et that day be darkness et not od regard it from above either let the light shine upon it
et darkness and the shadow of death stain it et a cloud dwell upon it et the blackness of the day terrify it
s for that night let darkness seize upon it et it not be joined unto the days of the year et it not come into the number of the months
o let that night be solitary et no joyful voice come therein
et them curse it that curse the day ho are ready to raise up their mourning
et the stars of the twilight thereof be dark et it look for light but have none either let it see the dawning of the day ecause it shut not up the doors of my mothers womb or hid sorrow from mine eyes

here the wicked cease from troubling nd there the weary be at rest
here the prisoners rest together hey hear not the voice of the oppressor
he small and great are there nd the servant is free from his master
