nd they have turned unto me the back and not the face though  taught them rising up early and teaching them yet they have not hearkened to receive instruction
ut they set their abominations in the house which is called by my name to defile it
nd they built the high places of aal which are in the valley of the son of innom to cause their sons and their daughters to pass through the fire unto olech which  commanded them not neither came it into my mind that they should do this abomination to cause udah to sin
nd now therefore thus saith the  the od of srael concerning this city whereof ye say t shall be delivered into the hand of the king of abylon by the sword and by the famine and by the pestilence behold  will gather them out of all countries whither  have driven them in mine anger and in my fury and in great wrath and  will bring them again unto this place and  will cause them to dwell safely and they shall be my people and  will be their od and  will give them one heart and one way that they may fear me for ever for the good of them and of their children after them and  will make an everlasting covenant with them that  will not turn away from them to do them good but  will put my fear in their hearts that they shall not depart from me
ea  will rejoice over them to do them good and  will plant them in this land assuredly with my whole heart and with my whole soul
or thus saith the  ike as  have brought all this great evil upon this people so will  bring upon them all the good that  have promised them
nd fields shall be bought in this land whereof ye say t is desolate without man or beast it is given into the hand of the haldeans
en shall buy fields for money and subscribe evidences and seal them and take witnesses in the land of enjamin and in the places about erusalem and in the cities of udah and in the cities of the mountains and in the cities of the valley and in the cities of the south for  will cause their captivity to return saith the 
oreover the word of the  came unto eremiah the second time while he was yet shut up in the court of the prison saying hus saith the  the maker thereof the  that formed it to establish it the  is his name all unto me and  will answer thee and shew thee great and mighty things which thou knowest not
or thus saith the  the od of srael concerning the houses of this city and concerning the houses of the kings of udah which are thrown down by the mounts and by the sword hey come to fight with the haldeans but it is to fill them with the dead bodies of men whom  have slain in mine anger and in my fury and for all whose wickedness  have hid my face from this city
ehold  will bring it health and cure and  will cure them and will reveal unto them the abundance of peace and truth
nd  will cause the captivity of udah and the captivity of srael to return and will build them as at the first
nd  will cleanse them from all their iniquity whereby they have sinned against me and  will pardon all their iniquities whereby they have sinned and whereby they have transgressed against me
nd it shall be to me a name of joy a praise and an honour before all the nations of the earth which shall hear all the good that  do unto them and they shall fear and tremble for all the goodness and for all the prosperity that  procure unto it
hus saith the  gain there shall be heard in this place which ye say shall be desolate without man and without beast even in the cities of udah and in the streets of erusalem that are desolate without man and without inhabitant and without beast the voice of joy and the voice of gladness the voice of the bridegroom and the voice of the bride the voice of them that shall say raise the  of hosts for the  is good for his mercy endureth for ever and of them that shall bring the sacrifice of praise into the house of the 
or  will cause to return the captivity of the land as at first saith the 
hus saith the  of hosts gain in this place which is desolate without man and without beast and in all the cities thereof shall be an habitation of shepherds causing their flocks to lie down
n the cities of the mountains in the cities of the vale and in the cities of the south and in the land of enjamin and in the places about erusalem and in the cities of udah shall the flocks pass again under the hands of him that telleth them saith the 
ehold the days come saith the  that  will perform that good thing which  have promised unto the house of srael and to the house of udah
n those days and at that time will  cause the ranch of righteousness to grow up unto avid and he shall execute judgment and righteousness in the land
n those days shall udah be saved and erusalem shall dwell safely and this is the name wherewith she shall be called he  our righteousness
or thus saith the  avid shall never want a man to sit upon the throne of the house of srael neither shall the priests the evites want a man before me to offer burnt offerings and to kindle meat offerings and to do sacrifice continually
nd the word of the  came unto eremiah saying hus saith the  f ye can break my covenant of the day and my covenant of the night and that there should not be day and night in their season then may also my covenant be broken with avid my servant that he should not have a son to reign upon his throne and with the evites the priests my ministers
s the host of heaven cannot be numbered neither the sand of the sea measured so will  multiply the seed of avid my servant and the evites that minister unto me
oreover the word of the  came to eremiah saying onsiderest thou not what this people have spoken saying he two families which the  hath chosen he hath even cast them off thus they have despised my people that they should be no more a nation before them
hus saith the  f my covenant be not with day and night and if  have not appointed the ordinances of heaven and earth then will  cast away the seed of acob and avid my servant so that  will not take any of his seed to be rulers over the seed of braham saac and acob for  will cause their captivity to return and have mercy on them
he word which came unto eremiah from the  when ebuchadnezzar king of abylon and all his army and all the kingdoms of the earth of his dominion and all the people fought against erusalem and against all the cities thereof saying hus saith the  the od of srael o and speak to edekiah king of udah and tell him hus saith the  ehold  will give this city into the hand of the king of abylon and he shall burn it with fire and thou shalt not escape out of his hand but shalt surely be taken and delivered into his hand and thine eyes shall behold the eyes of the king of abylon and he shall speak with thee mouth to mouth and thou shalt go to abylon
et hear the word of the   edekiah king of udah hus saith the  of thee hou shalt not die by the sword but thou shalt die in peace and with the burnings of thy fathers the former kings which were before thee so shall they burn odours for thee and they will lament thee saying h lord for  have pronounced the word saith the 
hen eremiah the prophet spake all these words unto edekiah king of udah in erusalem when the king of abylons army fought against erusalem and against all the cities of udah that were left against achish and against zekah for these defenced cities remained of the cities of udah
his is the word that came unto eremiah from the  after that the king edekiah had made a covenant with all the people which were at erusalem to proclaim liberty unto them that every man should let his manservant and every man his maidservant being an ebrew or an ebrewess go free that none should serve himself of them to wit of a ew his brother
ow when all the princes and all the people which had entered into the covenant heard that every one should let his manservant and every one his maidservant go free that none should serve themselves of them any more then they obeyed and let them go
ut afterward they turned and caused the servants and the handmaids whom they had let go free to return and brought them into subjection for servants and for handmaids
herefore the word of the  came to eremiah from the  saying hus saith the  the od of srael  made a covenant with your fathers in the day that  brought them forth out of the land of gypt out of the house of bondmen saying at the end of seven years let ye go every man his brother an ebrew which hath been sold unto thee and when he hath served thee six years thou shalt let him go free from thee but your fathers hearkened not unto me neither inclined their ear
nd ye were now turned and had done right in my sight in proclaiming liberty every man to his neighbour and ye had made a covenant before me in the house which is called by my name but ye turned and polluted my name and caused every man his servant and every man his handmaid whom he had set at liberty at their pleasure to return and brought them into subjection to be unto you for servants and for handmaids
herefore thus saith the  e have not hearkened unto me in proclaiming liberty every one to his brother and every man to his neighbour behold  proclaim a liberty for you saith the  to the sword to the pestilence and to the famine and  will make you to be removed into all the kingdoms of the earth
nd  will give the men that have transgressed my covenant which have not performed the words of the covenant which they had made before me when they cut the calf in twain and passed between the parts thereof the princes of udah and the princes of erusalem the eunuchs and the priests and all the people of the land which passed between the parts of the calf  will even give them into the hand of their enemies and into the hand of them that seek their life and their dead bodies shall be for meat unto the fowls of the heaven and to the beasts of the earth
nd edekiah king of udah and his princes will  give into the hand of their enemies and into the hand of them that seek their life and into the hand of the king of abylons army which are gone up from you
ehold  will command saith the  and cause them to return to this city and they shall fight against it and take it and burn it with fire and  will make the cities of udah a desolation without an inhabitant
he word which came unto eremiah from the  in the days of ehoiakim the son of osiah king of udah saying o unto the house of the echabites and speak unto them and bring them into the house of the  into one of the chambers and give them wine to drink
hen  took aazaniah the son of eremiah the son of abaziniah and his brethren and all his sons and the whole house of the echabites and  brought them into the house of the  into the chamber of the sons of anan the son of gdaliah a man of od which was by the chamber of the princes which was above the chamber of aaseiah the son of hallum the keeper of the door and  set before the sons of the house of the echabites pots full of wine and cups and  said unto them rink ye wine
ut they said e will drink no wine for onadab the son of echab our father commanded us saying e shall drink no wine neither ye nor your sons for ever neither shall ye build house nor sow seed nor plant vineyard nor have any but all your days ye shall dwell in tents that ye may live many days in the land where ye be strangers
hus have we obeyed the voice of onadab the son of echab our father in all that he hath charged us to drink no wine all our days we our wives our sons nor our daughters nor to build houses for us to dwell in neither have we vineyard nor field nor seed but we have dwelt in tents and have obeyed and done according to all that onadab our father commanded us
ut it came to pass when ebuchadrezzar king of abylon came up into the land that we said ome and let us go to erusalem for fear of the army of the haldeans and for fear of the army of the yrians so we dwell at erusalem
hen came the word of the  unto eremiah saying hus saith the  of hosts the od of srael o and tell the men of udah and the inhabitants of erusalem ill ye not receive instruction to hearken to my words saith the 
he words of onadab the son of echab that he commanded his sons not to drink wine are performed for unto this day they drink none but obey their fathers commandment notwithstanding  have spoken unto you rising early and speaking but ye hearkened not unto me
 have sent also unto you all my servants the prophets rising up early and sending them saying eturn ye now every man from his evil way and amend your doings and go not after other gods to serve them and ye shall dwell in the land which  have given to you and to your fathers but ye have not inclined your ear nor hearkened unto me
ecause the sons of onadab the son of echab have performed the commandment of their father which he commanded them but this people hath not hearkened unto me therefore thus saith the  od of hosts the od of srael ehold  will bring upon udah and upon all the inhabitants of erusalem all the evil that  have pronounced against them because  have spoken unto them but they have not heard and  have called unto them but they have not answered
nd eremiah said unto the house of the echabites hus saith the  of hosts the od of srael ecause ye have obeyed the commandment of onadab your father and kept all his precepts and done according unto all that he hath commanded you therefore thus saith the  of hosts the od of srael onadab the son of echab shall not want a man to stand before me for ever
nd it came to pass in the fourth year of ehoiakim the son of osiah king of udah that this word came unto eremiah from the  saying ake thee a roll of a book and write therein all the words that  have spoken unto thee against srael and against udah and against all the nations from the day  spake unto thee from the days of osiah even unto this day
t may be that the house of udah will hear all the evil which  purpose to do unto them that they may return every man from his evil way that  may forgive their iniquity and their sin
hen eremiah called aruch the son of eriah and aruch wrote from the mouth of eremiah all the words of the  which he had spoken unto him upon a roll of a book
nd eremiah commanded aruch saying  am shut up  cannot go into the house of the  therefore go thou and read in the roll which thou hast written from my mouth the words of the  in the ears of the people in the s house upon the fasting day and also thou shalt read them in the ears of all udah that come out of their cities
t may be they will present their supplication before the  and will return every one from his evil way for great is the anger and the fury that the  hath pronounced against this people
nd aruch the son of eriah did according to all that eremiah the prophet commanded him reading in the book the words of the  in the s house
nd it came to pass in the fifth year of ehoiakim the son of osiah king of udah in the ninth month that they proclaimed a fast before the  to all the people in erusalem and to all the people that came from the cities of udah unto erusalem
hen read aruch in the book the words of eremiah in the house of the  in the chamber of emariah the son of haphan the scribe in the higher court at the entry of the new gate of the s house in the ears of all the people
hen ichaiah the son of emariah the son of haphan had heard out of the book all the words of the  then he went down into the kings house into the scribes chamber and lo all the princes sat there even lishama the scribe and elaiah the son of hemaiah and lnathan the son of chbor and emariah the son of haphan and edekiah the son of ananiah and all the princes
hen ichaiah declared unto them all the words that he had heard when aruch read the book in the ears of the people
herefore all the princes sent ehudi the son of ethaniah the son of helemiah the son of ushi unto aruch saying ake in thine hand the roll wherein thou hast read in the ears of the people and come
o aruch the son of eriah took the roll in his hand and came unto them
nd they said unto him it down now and read it in our ears
o aruch read it in their ears
ow it came to pass when they had heard all the words they were afraid both one and other and said unto aruch e will surely tell the king of all these words
nd they asked aruch saying ell us now how didst thou write all these words at his mouth
hen aruch answered them e pronounced all these words unto me with his mouth and  wrote them with ink in the book
hen said the princes unto aruch o hide thee thou and eremiah and let no man know where ye be
nd they went in to the king into the court but they laid up the roll in the chamber of lishama the scribe and told all the words in the ears of the king
o the king sent ehudi to fetch the roll and he took it out of lishama the scribes chamber
nd ehudi read it in the ears of the king and in the ears of all the princes which stood beside the king
ow the king sat in the winterhouse in the ninth month and there was a fire on the hearth burning before him
nd it came to pass that when ehudi had read three or four leaves he cut it with the penknife and cast it into the fire that was on the hearth until all the roll was consumed in the fire that was on the hearth
et they were not afraid nor rent their garments neither the king nor any of his servants that heard all these words
evertheless lnathan and elaiah and emariah had made intercession to the king that he would not burn the roll but he would not hear them
ut the king commanded erahmeel the son of ammelech and eraiah the son of zriel and helemiah the son of bdeel to take aruch the scribe and eremiah the prophet but the  hid them
hen the word of the  came to eremiah after that the king had burned the roll and the words which aruch wrote at the mouth of eremiah saying ake thee again another roll and write in it all the former words that were in the first roll which ehoiakim the king of udah hath burned
nd thou shalt say to ehoiakim king of udah hus saith the  hou hast burned this roll saying hy hast thou written therein saying he king of abylon shall certainly come and destroy this land and shall cause to cease from thence man and beast
herefore thus saith the  of ehoiakim king of udah e shall have none to sit upon the throne of avid and his dead body shall be cast out in the day to the heat and in the night to the frost
nd  will punish him and his seed and his servants for their iniquity and  will bring upon them and upon the inhabitants of erusalem and upon the men of udah all the evil that  have pronounced against them but they hearkened not
hen took eremiah another roll and gave it to aruch the scribe the son of eriah who wrote therein from the mouth of eremiah all the words of the book which ehoiakim king of udah had burned in the fire and there were added besides unto them many like words
nd king edekiah the son of osiah reigned instead of oniah the son of ehoiakim whom ebuchadrezzar king of abylon made king in the land of udah
ut neither he nor his servants nor the people of the land did hearken unto the words of the  which he spake by the prophet eremiah
nd edekiah the king sent ehucal the son of helemiah and ephaniah the son of aaseiah the priest to the prophet eremiah saying ray now unto the  our od for us
ow eremiah came in and went out among the people for they had not put him into prison
hen haraohs army was come forth out of gypt and when the haldeans that besieged erusalem heard tidings of them they departed from erusalem
hen came the word of the  unto the prophet eremiah saying hus saith the  the od of srael hus shall ye say to the king of udah that sent you unto me to enquire of me ehold haraohs army which is come forth to help you shall return to gypt into their own land
nd the haldeans shall come again and fight against this city and take it and burn it with fire
hus saith the  eceive not yourselves saying he haldeans shall surely depart from us for they shall not depart
or though ye had smitten the whole army of the haldeans that fight against you and there remained but wounded men among them yet should they rise up every man in his tent and burn this city with fire
nd it came to pass that when the army of the haldeans was broken up from erusalem for fear of haraohs army then eremiah went forth out of erusalem to go into the land of enjamin to separate himself thence in the midst of the people
nd when he was in the gate of enjamin a captain of the ward was there whose name was rijah the son of helemiah the son of ananiah and he took eremiah the prophet saying hou fallest away to the haldeans
hen said eremiah t is false  fall not away to the haldeans
ut he hearkened not to him so rijah took eremiah and brought him to the princes
herefore the princes were wroth with eremiah and smote him and put him in prison in the house of onathan the scribe for they had made that the prison
hen eremiah was entered into the dungeon and into the cabins and eremiah had remained there many days then edekiah the king sent and took him out and the king asked him secretly in his house and said s there any word from the 
nd eremiah said here is for said he thou shalt be delivered into the hand of the king of abylon
oreover eremiah said unto king edekiah hat have  offended against thee or against thy servants or against this people that ye have put me in prison
here are now your prophets which prophesied unto you saying he king of abylon shall not come against you nor against this land
herefore hear now  pray thee  my lord the king let my supplication  pray thee be accepted before thee that thou cause me not to return to the house of onathan the scribe lest  die there
hen edekiah the king commanded that they should commit eremiah into the court of the prison and that they should give him daily a piece of bread out of the bakers street until all the bread in the city were spent
hus eremiah remained in the court of the prison
hen hephatiah the son of attan and edaliah the son of ashur and ucal the son of helemiah and ashur the son of alchiah heard the words that eremiah had spoken unto all the people saying hus saith the  e that remaineth in this city shall die by the sword by the famine and by the pestilence but he that goeth forth to the haldeans shall live for he shall have his life for a prey and shall live
hus saith the  his city shall surely be given into the hand of the king of abylons army which shall take it
herefore the princes said unto the king e beseech thee let this man be put to death for thus he weakeneth the hands of the men of war that remain in this city and the hands of all the people in speaking such words unto them for this man seeketh not the welfare of this people but the hurt
hen edekiah the king said ehold he is in your hand for the king is not he that can do any thing against you
hen took they eremiah and cast him into the dungeon of alchiah the son of ammelech that was in the court of the prison and they let down eremiah with cords
nd in the dungeon there was no water but mire so eremiah sunk in the mire
ow when bedmelech the thiopian one of the eunuchs which was in the kings house heard that they had put eremiah in the dungeon the king then sitting in the gate of enjamin bedmelech went forth out of the kings house and spake to the king saying y lord the king these men have done evil in all that they have done to eremiah the prophet whom they have cast into the dungeon and he is like to die for hunger in the place where he is for there is no more bread in the city
hen the king commanded bedmelech the thiopian saying ake from hence thirty men with thee and take up eremiah the prophet out of the dungeon before he die
o bedmelech took the men with him and went into the house of the king under the treasury and took thence old cast clouts and old rotten rags and let them down by cords into the dungeon to eremiah
nd bedmelech the thiopian said unto eremiah ut now these old cast clouts and rotten rags under thine armholes under the cords
nd eremiah did so
o they drew up eremiah with cords and took him up out of the dungeon and eremiah remained in the court of the prison
hen edekiah the king sent and took eremiah the prophet unto him into the third entry that is in the house of the  and the king said unto eremiah  will ask thee a thing hide nothing from me
hen eremiah said unto edekiah f  declare it unto thee wilt thou not surely put me to death and if  give thee counsel wilt thou not hearken unto me
o edekiah the king sware secretly unto eremiah saying s the  liveth that made us this soul  will not put thee to death neither will  give thee into the hand of these men that seek thy life
hen said eremiah unto edekiah hus saith the  the od of hosts the od of srael f thou wilt assuredly go forth unto the king of abylons princes then thy soul shall live and this city shall not be burned with fire and thou shalt live and thine house but if thou wilt not go forth to the king of abylons princes then shall this city be given into the hand of the haldeans and they shall burn it with fire and thou shalt not escape out of their hand
nd edekiah the king said unto eremiah  am afraid of the ews that are fallen to the haldeans lest they deliver me into their hand and they mock me
ut eremiah said hey shall not deliver thee
bey  beseech thee the voice of the  which  speak unto thee so it shall be well unto thee and thy soul shall live
ut if thou refuse to go forth this is the word that the  hath shewed me and behold all the women that are left in the king of udahs house shall be brought forth to the king of abylons princes and those women shall say hy friends have set thee on and have prevailed against thee thy feet are sunk in the mire and they are turned away back
o they shall bring out all thy wives and thy children to the haldeans and thou shalt not escape out of their hand but shalt be taken by the hand of the king of abylon and thou shalt cause this city to be burned with fire
hen said edekiah unto eremiah et no man know of these words and thou shalt not die
ut if the princes hear that  have talked with thee and they come unto thee and say unto thee eclare unto us now what thou hast said unto the king hide it not from us and we will not put thee to death also what the king said unto thee then thou shalt say unto them  presented my supplication before the king that he would not cause me to return to onathans house to die there
hen came all the princes unto eremiah and asked him and he told them according to all these words that the king had commanded
o they left off speaking with him for the matter was not perceived
o eremiah abode in the court of the prison until the day that erusalem was taken and he was there when erusalem was taken
n the ninth year of edekiah king of udah in the tenth month came ebuchadrezzar king of abylon and all his army against erusalem and they besieged it
nd in the eleventh year of edekiah in the fourth month the ninth day of the month the city was broken up
nd all the princes of the king of abylon came in and sat in the middle gate even ergalsharezer amgarnebo arsechim absaris ergalsharezer abmag with all the residue of the princes of the king of abylon
nd it came to pass that when edekiah the king of udah saw them and all the men of war then they fled and went forth out of the city by night by the way of the kings garden by the gate betwixt the two walls and he went out the way of the plain
ut the haldeans army pursued after them and overtook edekiah in the plains of ericho and when they had taken him they brought him up to ebuchadnezzar king of abylon to iblah in the land of amath where he gave judgment upon him
hen the king of abylon slew the sons of edekiah in iblah before his eyes also the king of abylon slew all the nobles of udah
oreover he put out edekiahs eyes and bound him with chains to carry him to abylon
nd the haldeans burned the kings house and the houses of the people with fire and brake down the walls of erusalem
hen ebuzaradan the captain of the guard carried away captive into abylon the remnant of the people that remained in the city and those that fell away that fell to him with the rest of the people that remained
ut ebuzaradan the captain of the guard left of the poor of the people which had nothing in the land of udah and gave them vineyards and fields at the same time
ow ebuchadrezzar king of abylon gave charge concerning eremiah to ebuzaradan the captain of the guard saying ake him and look well to him and do him no harm but do unto him even as he shall say unto thee
o ebuzaradan the captain of the guard sent and ebushasban absaris and ergalsharezer abmag and all the king of abylons princes even they sent and took eremiah out of the court of the prison and committed him unto edaliah the son of hikam the son of haphan that he should carry him home so he dwelt among the people
ow the word of the  came unto eremiah while he was shut up in the court of the prison saying o and speak to bedmelech the thiopian saying hus saith the  of hosts the od of srael ehold  will bring my words upon this city for evil and not for good and they shall be accomplished in that day before thee
ut  will deliver thee in that day saith the  and thou shalt not be given into the hand of the men of whom thou art afraid
or  will surely deliver thee and thou shalt not fall by the sword but thy life shall be for a prey unto thee because thou hast put thy trust in me saith the 
he word that came to eremiah from the  after that ebuzaradan the captain of the guard had let him go from amah when he had taken him being bound in chains among all that were carried away captive of erusalem and udah which were carried away captive unto abylon
nd the captain of the guard took eremiah and said unto him he  thy od hath pronounced this evil upon this place
ow the  hath brought it and done according as he hath said because ye have sinned against the  and have not obeyed his voice therefore this thing is come upon you
nd now behold  loose thee this day from the chains which were upon thine hand
f it seem good unto thee to come with me into abylon come and  will look well unto thee but if it seem ill unto thee to come with me into abylon forbear behold all the land is before thee whither it seemeth good and convenient for thee to go thither go
ow while he was not yet gone back he said o back also to edaliah the son of hikam the son of haphan whom the king of abylon hath made governor over the cities of udah and dwell with him among the people or go wheresoever it seemeth convenient unto thee to go
o the captain of the guard gave him victuals and a reward and let him go
hen went eremiah unto edaliah the son of hikam to izpah and dwelt with him among the people that were left in the land
ow when all the captains of the forces which were in the fields even they and their men heard that the king of abylon had made edaliah the son of hikam governor in the land and had committed unto him men and women and children and of the poor of the land of them that were not carried away captive to abylon then they came to edaliah to izpah even shmael the son of ethaniah and ohanan and onathan the sons of areah and eraiah the son of anhumeth and the sons of phai the etophathite and ezaniah the son of a aachathite they and their men
nd edaliah the son of hikam the son of haphan sware unto them and to their men saying ear not to serve the haldeans dwell in the land and serve the king of abylon and it shall be well with you
s for me behold  will dwell at izpah to serve the haldeans which will come unto us but ye gather ye wine and summer fruits and oil and put them in your vessels and dwell in your cities that ye have taken
ikewise when all the ews that were in oab and among the mmonites and in dom and that were in all the countries heard that the king of abylon had left a remnant of udah and that he had set over them edaliah the son of hikam the son of haphan even all the ews returned out of all places whither they were driven and came to the land of udah to edaliah unto izpah and gathered wine and summer fruits very much
oreover ohanan the son of areah and all the captains of the forces that were in the fields came to edaliah to izpah and said unto him ost thou certainly know that aalis the king of the mmonites hath sent shmael the son of ethaniah to slay thee
ut edaliah the son of hikam believed them not
hen ohanan the son of areah spake to edaliah in izpah secretly saying et me go  pray thee and  will slay shmael the son of ethaniah and no man shall know it wherefore should he slay thee that all the ews which are gathered unto thee should be scattered and the remnant in udah perish
ut edaliah the son of hikam said unto ohanan the son of areah hou shalt not do this thing for thou speakest falsely of shmael
ow it came to pass in the seventh month that shmael the son of ethaniah the son of lishama of the seed royal and the princes of the king even ten men with him came unto edaliah the son of hikam to izpah and there they did eat bread together in izpah
hen arose shmael the son of ethaniah and the ten men that were with him and smote edaliah the son of hikam the son of haphan with the sword and slew him whom the king of abylon had made governor over the land

nd shmael the son of ethaniah went forth from izpah to meet them weeping all along as he went and it came to pass as he met them he said unto them ome to edaliah the son of hikam

o he forbare and slew them not among their brethren
ow the pit wherein shmael had cast all the dead bodies of the men whom he had slain because of edaliah was it which sa the king had made for fear of aasha king of srael and shmael the son of ethaniah filled it with them that were slain

ut when ohanan the son of areah and all the captains of the forces that were with him heard of all the evil that shmael the son of ethaniah had done then they took all the men and went to fight with shmael the son of ethaniah and found him by the great waters that are in ibeon
ow it came to pass that when all the people which were with shmael saw ohanan the son of areah and all the captains of the forces that were with him then they were glad
o all the people that shmael had carried away captive from izpah cast about and returned and went unto ohanan the son of areah
ut shmael the son of ethaniah escaped from ohanan with eight men and went to the mmonites
hen took ohanan the son of areah and all the captains of the forces that were with him all the remnant of the people whom he had recovered from shmael the son of ethaniah from izpah after that he had slain edaliah the son of hikam even mighty men of war and the women and the children and the eunuchs whom he had brought again from ibeon and they departed and dwelt in the habitation of himham which is by ethlehem to go to enter into gypt because of the haldeans for they were afraid of them because shmael the son of ethaniah had slain edaliah the son of hikam whom the king of abylon made governor in the land
hen all the captains of the forces and ohanan the son of areah and ezaniah the son of oshaiah and all the people from the least even unto the greatest came near and said unto eremiah the prophet et we beseech thee our supplication be accepted before thee and pray for us unto the  thy od even for all this remnant for we are left but a few of many as thine eyes do behold us that the  thy od may shew us the way wherein we may walk and the thing that we may do

o shall it be with all the men that set their faces to go into gypt to sojourn there they shall die by the sword by the famine and by the pestilence and none of them shall remain or escape from the evil that  will bring upon them

herefore now thus saith the  the od of hosts the od of srael herefore commit ye this great evil against your souls to cut off from you man and woman child and suckling out of udah to leave you none to remain in that ye provoke me unto wrath with the works of your hands burning incense unto other gods in the land of gypt whither ye be gone to dwell that ye might cut yourselves off and that ye might be a curse and a reproach among all the nations of the earth

ow it came to pass in the thirtieth year in the fourth month in the fifth day of the month as  was among the captives by the river of hebar that the heavens were opened and  saw visions of od
n the fifth day of the month which was the fifth year of king ehoiachins captivity the word of the  came expressly unto zekiel the priest the son of uzi in the land of the haldeans by the river hebar and the hand of the  was there upon him
nd  looked and behold a whirlwind came out of the north a great cloud and a fire infolding itself and a brightness was about it and out of the midst thereof as the colour of amber out of the midst of the fire
lso out of the midst thereof came the likeness of four living creatures
nd this was their appearance they had the likeness of a man
nd every one had four faces and every one had four wings
nd their feet were straight feet and the sole of their feet was like the sole of a calfs foot and they sparkled like the colour of burnished brass
nd they had the hands of a man under their wings on their four sides and they four had their faces and their wings
heir wings were joined one to another they turned not when they went they went every one straight forward
s for the likeness of their faces they four had the face of a man and the face of a lion on the right side and they four had the face of an ox on the left side they four also had the face of an eagle
hus were their faces and their wings were stretched upward two wings of every one were joined one to another and two covered their bodies
nd they went every one straight forward whither the spirit was to go they went and they turned not when they went
s for the likeness of the living creatures their appearance was like burning coals of fire and like the appearance of lamps it went up and down among the living creatures and the fire was bright and out of the fire went forth lightning
nd the living creatures ran and returned as the appearance of a flash of lightning
ow as  beheld the living creatures behold one wheel upon the earth by the living creatures with his four faces
he appearance of the wheels and their work was like unto the colour of a beryl and they four had one likeness and their appearance and their work was as it were a wheel in the middle of a wheel
hen they went they went upon their four sides and they turned not when they went
s for their rings they were so high that they were dreadful and their rings were full of eyes round about them four
nd when the living creatures went the wheels went by them and when the living creatures were lifted up from the earth the wheels were lifted up
hithersoever the spirit was to go they went thither was their spirit to go and the wheels were lifted up over against them for the spirit of the living creature was in the wheels
hen those went these went and when those stood these stood and when those were lifted up from the earth the wheels were lifted up over against them for the spirit of the living creature was in the wheels
nd the likeness of the firmament upon the heads of the living creature was as the colour of the terrible crystal stretched forth over their heads above
nd under the firmament were their wings straight the one toward the other every one had two which covered on this side and every one had two which covered on that side their bodies
nd when they went  heard the noise of their wings like the noise of great waters as the voice of the lmighty the voice of speech as the noise of an host when they stood they let down their wings
nd there was a voice from the firmament that was over their heads when they stood and had let down their wings
nd above the firmament that was over their heads was the likeness of a throne as the appearance of a sapphire stone and upon the likeness of the throne was the likeness as the appearance of a man above upon it

nd it came to pass at the end of seven days that the word of the  came unto me saying on of man  have made thee a watchman unto the house of srael therefore hear the word at my mouth and give them warning from me
hen  say unto the wicked hou shalt surely die and thou givest him not warning nor speakest to warn the wicked from his wicked way to save his life the same wicked man shall die in his iniquity but his blood will  require at thine hand
et if thou warn the wicked and he turn not from his wickedness nor from his wicked way he shall die in his iniquity but thou hast delivered thy soul
gain hen a righteous man doth turn from his righteousness and commit iniquity and  lay a stumblingblock before him he shall die because thou hast not given him warning he shall die in his sin and his righteousness which he hath done shall not be remembered but his blood will  require at thine hand
evertheless if thou warn the righteous man that the righteous sin not and he doth not sin he shall surely live because he is warned also thou hast delivered thy soul
nd the hand of the  was there upon me and he said unto me rise go forth into the plain and  will there talk with thee
hen  arose and went forth into the plain and behold the glory of the  stood there as the glory which  saw by the river of hebar and  fell on my face

his shall be a sign to the house of srael

or  have laid upon thee the years of their iniquity according to the number of the days three hundred and ninety days so shalt thou bear the iniquity of the house of srael
nd when thou hast accomplished them lie again on thy right side and thou shalt bear the iniquity of the house of udah forty days  have appointed thee each day for a year
herefore thou shalt set thy face toward the siege of erusalem and thine arm shall be uncovered and thou shalt prophesy against it
nd behold  will lay bands upon thee and thou shalt not turn thee from one side to another till thou hast ended the days of thy siege

nd thy meat which thou shalt eat shall be by weight twenty shekels a day from time to time shalt thou eat it
hou shalt drink also water by measure the sixth part of an hin from time to time shalt thou drink

nd the  said ven thus shall the children of srael eat their defiled bread among the entiles whither  will drive them
hen said  h ord  behold my soul hath not been polluted for from my youth up even till now have  not eaten of that which dieth of itself or is torn in pieces neither came there abominable flesh into my mouth
hen he said unto me o  have given thee cows dung for mans dung and thou shalt prepare thy bread therewith

nd thou son of man take thee a sharp knife take thee a barbers razor and cause it to pass upon thine head and upon thy beard then take thee balances to weigh and divide the hair
hou shalt burn with fire a third part in the midst of the city when the days of the siege are fulfilled and thou shalt take a third part and smite about it with a knife and a third part thou shalt scatter in the wind and  will draw out a sword after them
hou shalt also take thereof a few in number and bind them in thy skirts
hen take of them again and cast them into the midst of the fire and burn them in the fire for thereof shall a fire come forth into all the house of srael
hus saith the ord  his is erusalem  have set it in the midst of the nations and countries that are round about her
nd she hath changed my judgments into wickedness more than the nations and my statutes more than the countries that are round about her for they have refused my judgments and my statutes they have not walked in them
herefore thus saith the ord  ecause ye multiplied more than the nations that are round about you and have not walked in my statutes neither have kept my judgments neither have done according to the judgments of the nations that are round about you therefore thus saith the ord  ehold  even  am against thee and will execute judgments in the midst of thee in the sight of the nations
nd  will do in thee that which  have not done and whereunto  will not do any more the like because of all thine abominations
herefore the fathers shall eat the sons in the midst of thee and the sons shall eat their fathers and  will execute judgments in thee and the whole remnant of thee will  scatter into all the winds
herefore as  live saith the ord  urely because thou hast defiled my sanctuary with all thy detestable things and with all thine abominations therefore will  also diminish thee neither shall mine eye spare neither will  have any pity
 third part of thee shall die with the pestilence and with famine shall they be consumed in the midst of thee and a third part shall fall by the sword round about thee and  will scatter a third part into all the winds and  will draw out a sword after them
hus shall mine anger be accomplished and  will cause my fury to rest upon them and  will be comforted and they shall know that  the  have spoken it in my zeal when  have accomplished my fury in them

hey have blown the trumpet even to make all ready but none goeth to the battle for my wrath is upon all the multitude thereof
he sword is without and the pestilence and the famine within he that is in the field shall die with the sword and he that is in the city famine and pestilence shall devour him

nd it came to pass in the sixth year in the sixth month in the fifth day of the month as  sat in mine house and the elders of udah sat before me that the hand of the ord  fell there upon me

nd behold the glory of the od of srael was there according to the vision that  saw in the plain

his is the living creature that  saw by the river of hebar
nd when the cherubims went the wheels went by them and when the cherubims lifted up their wings to mount up from the earth the same wheels also turned not from beside them

his is the living creature that  saw under the od of srael by the river of hebar and  knew that they were the cherubims
very one had four faces apiece and every one four wings and the likeness of the hands of a man was under their wings
nd the likeness of their faces was the same faces which  saw by the river of hebar their appearances and themselves they went every one straight forward
oreover the spirit lifted me up and brought me unto the east gate of the s house which looketh eastward and behold at the door of the gate five and twenty men among whom  saw aazaniah the son of zur and elatiah the son of enaiah princes of the people
hen said he unto me on of man these are the men that devise mischief and give wicked counsel in this city which say t is not near let us build houses this city is the caldron and we be the flesh
herefore prophesy against them prophesy  son of man
nd the pirit of the  fell upon me and said unto me peak hus saith the  hus have ye said  house of srael for  know the things that come into your mind every one of them
e have multiplied your slain in this city and ye have filled the streets thereof with the slain
herefore thus saith the ord  our slain whom ye have laid in the midst of it they are the flesh and this city is the caldron but  will bring you forth out of the midst of it
e have feared the sword and  will bring a sword upon you saith the ord 
nd  will bring you out of the midst thereof and deliver you into the hands of strangers and will execute judgments among you
e shall fall by the sword  will judge you in the border of srael and ye shall know that  am the 
his city shall not be your caldron neither shall ye be the flesh in the midst thereof but  will judge you in the border of srael and ye shall know that  am the  for ye have not walked in my statutes neither executed my judgments but have done after the manners of the heathen that are round about you
nd it came to pass when  prophesied that elatiah the son of enaiah died
hen fell  down upon my face and cried with a loud voice and said h ord  wilt thou make a full end of the remnant of srael
gain the word of the  came unto me saying on of man thy brethren even thy brethren the men of thy kindred and all the house of srael wholly are they unto whom the inhabitants of erusalem have said et you far from the  unto us is this land given in possession
herefore say hus saith the ord  lthough  have cast them far off among the heathen and although  have scattered them among the countries yet will  be to them as a little sanctuary in the countries where they shall come
herefore say hus saith the ord   will even gather you from the people and assemble you out of the countries where ye have been scattered and  will give you the land of srael
nd they shall come thither and they shall take away all the detestable things thereof and all the abominations thereof from thence
nd  will give them one heart and  will put a new spirit within you and  will take the stony heart out of their flesh and will give them an heart of flesh that they may walk in my statutes and keep mine ordinances and do them and they shall be my people and  will be their od
ut as for them whose heart walketh after the heart of their detestable things and their abominations  will recompense their way upon their own heads saith the ord 
hen did the cherubims lift up their wings and the wheels beside them and the glory of the od of srael was over them above
nd the glory of the  went up from the midst of the city and stood upon the mountain which is on the east side of the city
fterwards the spirit took me up and brought me in a vision by the pirit of od into haldea to them of the captivity
o the vision that  had seen went up from me
hen  spake unto them of the captivity all the things that the  had shewed me
he word of the  also came unto me saying on of man thou dwellest in the midst of a rebellious house which have eyes to see and see not they have ears to hear and hear not for they are a rebellious house
herefore thou son of man prepare thee stuff for removing and remove by day in their sight and thou shalt remove from thy place to another place in their sight it may be they will consider though they be a rebellious house
hen shalt thou bring forth thy stuff by day in their sight as stuff for removing and thou shalt go forth at even in their sight as they that go forth into captivity
ig thou through the wall in their sight and carry out thereby
n their sight shalt thou bear it upon thy shoulders and carry it forth in the twilight thou shalt cover thy face that thou see not the ground for  have set thee for a sign unto the house of srael
nd  did so as  was commanded  brought forth my stuff by day as stuff for captivity and in the even  digged through the wall with mine hand  brought it forth in the twilight and  bare it upon my shoulder in their sight
nd in the morning came the word of the  unto me saying on of man hath not the house of srael the rebellious house said unto thee hat doest thou
ay thou unto them hus saith the ord  his burden concerneth the prince in erusalem and all the house of srael that are among them
ay  am your sign like as  have done so shall it be done unto them they shall remove and go into captivity
nd the prince that is among them shall bear upon his shoulder in the twilight and shall go forth they shall dig through the wall to carry out thereby he shall cover his face that he see not the ground with his eyes
y net also will  spread upon him and he shall be taken in my snare and  will bring him to abylon to the land of the haldeans yet shall he not see it though he shall die there
nd  will scatter toward every wind all that are about him to help him and all his bands and  will draw out the sword after them
nd they shall know that  am the  when  shall scatter them among the nations and disperse them in the countries
ut  will leave a few men of them from the sword from the famine and from the pestilence that they may declare all their abominations among the heathen whither they come and they shall know that  am the 
oreover the word of the  came to me saying on of man eat thy bread with quaking and drink thy water with trembling and with carefulness and say unto the people of the land hus saith the ord  of the inhabitants of erusalem and of the land of srael hey shall eat their bread with carefulness and drink their water with astonishment that her land may be desolate from all that is therein because of the violence of all them that dwell therein
nd the cities that are inhabited shall be laid waste and the land shall be desolate and ye shall know that  am the 
nd the word of the  came unto me saying on of man what is that proverb that ye have in the land of srael saying he days are prolonged and every vision faileth
ell them therefore hus saith the ord   will make this proverb to cease and they shall no more use it as a proverb in srael but say unto them he days are at hand and the effect of every vision
or there shall be no more any vain vision nor flattering divination within the house of srael
or  am the   will speak and the word that  shall speak shall come to pass it shall be no more prolonged for in your days  rebellious house will  say the word and will perform it saith the ord 
gain the word of the  came to me saying on of man behold they of the house of srael say he vision that he seeth is for many days to come and he prophesieth of the times that are far off
herefore say unto them hus saith the ord  here shall none of my words be prolonged any more but the word which  have spoken shall be done saith the ord 
nd the word of the  came unto me saying on of man prophesy against the prophets of srael that prophesy and say thou unto them that prophesy out of their own hearts ear ye the word of the  hus saith the ord  oe unto the foolish prophets that follow their own spirit and have seen nothing
 srael thy prophets are like the foxes in the deserts
e have not gone up into the gaps neither made up the hedge for the house of srael to stand in the battle in the day of the 
hey have seen vanity and lying divination saying he  saith and the  hath not sent them and they have made others to hope that they would confirm the word
ave ye not seen a vain vision and have ye not spoken a lying divination whereas ye say he  saith it albeit  have not spoken
herefore thus saith the ord  ecause ye have spoken vanity and seen lies therefore behold  am against you saith the ord 
nd mine hand shall be upon the prophets that see vanity and that divine lies they shall not be in the assembly of my people neither shall they be written in the writing of the house of srael neither shall they enter into the land of srael and ye shall know that  am the ord 
ecause even because they have seduced my people saying eace and there was no peace and one built up a wall and lo others daubed it with untempered morter say unto them which daub it with untempered morter that it shall fall there shall be an overflowing shower and ye  great hailstones shall fall and a stormy wind shall rend it
o when the wall is fallen shall it not be said unto you here is the daubing wherewith ye have daubed it
herefore thus saith the ord   will even rend it with a stormy wind in my fury and there shall be an overflowing shower in mine anger and great hailstones in my fury to consume it
o will  break down the wall that ye have daubed with untempered morter and bring it down to the ground so that the foundation thereof shall be discovered and it shall fall and ye shall be consumed in the midst thereof and ye shall know that  am the 
hus will  accomplish my wrath upon the wall and upon them that have daubed it with untempered morter and will say unto you he wall is no more neither they that daubed it to wit the prophets of srael which prophesy concerning erusalem and which see visions of peace for her and there is no peace saith the ord 
ikewise thou son of man set thy face against the daughters of thy people which prophesy out of their own heart and prophesy thou against them and say hus saith the ord  oe to the women that sew pillows to all armholes and make kerchiefs upon the head of every stature to hunt souls
ill ye hunt the souls of my people and will ye save the souls alive that come unto you
nd will ye pollute me among my people for handfuls of barley and for pieces of bread to slay the souls that should not die and to save the souls alive that should not live by your lying to my people that hear your lies
herefore thus saith the ord  ehold  am against your pillows wherewith ye there hunt the souls to make them fly and  will tear them from your arms and will let the souls go even the souls that ye hunt to make them fly
our kerchiefs also will  tear and deliver my people out of your hand and they shall be no more in your hand to be hunted and ye shall know that  am the 
ecause with lies ye have made the heart of the righteous sad whom  have not made sad and strengthened the hands of the wicked that he should not return from his wicked way by promising him life therefore ye shall see no more vanity nor divine divinations for  will deliver my people out of your hand and ye shall know that  am the 
hen came certain of the elders of srael unto me and sat before me
nd the word of the  came unto me saying on of man these men have set up their idols in their heart and put the stumblingblock of their iniquity before their face should  be enquired of at all by them
herefore speak unto them and say unto them hus saith the ord  very man of the house of srael that setteth up his idols in his heart and putteth the stumblingblock of his iniquity before his face and cometh to the prophet  the  will answer him that cometh according to the multitude of his idols that  may take the house of srael in their own heart because they are all estranged from me through their idols
herefore say unto the house of srael hus saith the ord  epent and turn yourselves from your idols and turn away your faces from all your abominations
or every one of the house of srael or of the stranger that sojourneth in srael which separateth himself from me and setteth up his idols in his heart and putteth the stumblingblock of his iniquity before his face and cometh to a prophet to enquire of him concerning me  the  will answer him by myself and  will set my face against that man and will make him a sign and a proverb and  will cut him off from the midst of my people and ye shall know that  am the 
nd if the prophet be deceived when he hath spoken a thing  the  have deceived that prophet and  will stretch out my hand upon him and will destroy him from the midst of my people srael
nd they shall bear the punishment of their iniquity the punishment of the prophet shall be even as the punishment of him that seeketh unto him that the house of srael may go no more astray from me neither be polluted any more with all their transgressions but that they may be my people and  may be their od saith the ord 
he word of the  came again to me saying on of man when the land sinneth against me by trespassing grievously then will  stretch out mine hand upon it and will break the staff of the bread thereof and will send famine upon it and will cut off man and beast from it though these three men oah aniel and ob were in it they should deliver but their own souls by their righteousness saith the ord 
f  cause noisome beasts to pass through the land and they spoil it so that it be desolate that no man may pass through because of the beasts though these three men were in it as  live saith the ord  they shall deliver neither sons nor daughters they only shall be delivered but the land shall be desolate
r if  bring a sword upon that land and say word go through the land so that  cut off man and beast from it though these three men were in it as  live saith the ord  they shall deliver neither sons nor daughters but they only shall be delivered themselves
r if  send a pestilence into that land and pour out my fury upon it in blood to cut off from it man and beast though oah aniel and ob were in it as  live saith the ord  they shall deliver neither son nor daughter they shall but deliver their own souls by their righteousness
or thus saith the ord  ow much more when  send my four sore judgments upon erusalem the sword and the famine and the noisome beast and the pestilence to cut off from it man and beast
et behold therein shall be left a remnant that shall be brought forth both sons and daughters behold they shall come forth unto you and ye shall see their way and their doings and ye shall be comforted concerning the evil that  have brought upon erusalem even concerning all that  have brought upon it
nd they shall comfort you when ye see their ways and their doings and ye shall know that  have not done without cause all that  have done in it saith the ord 
nd the word of the  came unto me saying on of man hat is the vine tree more than any tree or than a branch which is among the trees of the forest
hall wood be taken thereof to do any work or will men take a pin of it to hang any vessel thereon
ehold it is cast into the fire for fuel the fire devoureth both the ends of it and the midst of it is burned
s it meet for any work
ehold when it was whole it was meet for no work how much less shall it be meet yet for any work when the fire hath devoured it and it is burned
herefore thus saith the ord  s the vine tree among the trees of the forest which  have given to the fire for fuel so will  give the inhabitants of erusalem
nd  will set my face against them they shall go out from one fire and another fire shall devour them and ye shall know that  am the  when  set my face against them
nd  will make the land desolate because they have committed a trespass saith the ord 
gain the word of the  came unto me saying on of man cause erusalem to know her abominations and say hus saith the ord  unto erusalem hy birth and thy nativity is of the land of anaan thy father was an morite and thy mother an ittite
nd as for thy nativity in the day thou wast born thy navel was not cut neither wast thou washed in water to supple thee thou wast not salted at all nor swaddled at all
one eye pitied thee to do any of these unto thee to have compassion upon thee but thou wast cast out in the open field to the lothing of thy person in the day that thou wast born
nd when  passed by thee and saw thee polluted in thine own blood  said unto thee when thou wast in thy blood ive yea  said unto thee when thou wast in thy blood ive
 have caused thee to multiply as the bud of the field and thou hast increased and waxen great and thou art come to excellent ornaments thy breasts are fashioned and thine hair is grown whereas thou wast naked and bare
ow when  passed by thee and looked upon thee behold thy time was the time of love and  spread my skirt over thee and covered thy nakedness yea  sware unto thee and entered into a covenant with thee saith the ord  and thou becamest mine
hen washed  thee with water yea  throughly washed away thy blood from thee and  anointed thee with oil
 clothed thee also with broidered work and shod thee with badgers skin and  girded thee about with fine linen and  covered thee with silk
 decked thee also with ornaments and  put bracelets upon thy hands and a chain on thy neck
nd  put a jewel on thy forehead and earrings in thine ears and a beautiful crown upon thine head
hus wast thou decked with gold and silver and thy raiment was of fine linen and silk and broidered work thou didst eat fine flour and honey and oil and thou wast exceeding beautiful and thou didst prosper into a kingdom
nd thy renown went forth among the heathen for thy beauty for it was perfect through my comeliness which  had put upon thee saith the ord 
ut thou didst trust in thine own beauty and playedst the harlot because of thy renown and pouredst out thy fornications on every one that passed by his it was
nd of thy garments thou didst take and deckedst thy high places with divers colours and playedst the harlot thereupon the like things shall not come neither shall it be so
hou hast also taken thy fair jewels of my gold and of my silver which  had given thee and madest to thyself images of men and didst commit whoredom with them and tookest thy broidered garments and coveredst them and thou hast set mine oil and mine incense before them
y meat also which  gave thee fine flour and oil and honey wherewith  fed thee thou hast even set it before them for a sweet savour and thus it was saith the ord 
oreover thou hast taken thy sons and thy daughters whom thou hast borne unto me and these hast thou sacrificed unto them to be devoured
s this of thy whoredoms a small matter that thou hast slain my children and delivered them to cause them to pass through the fire for them
nd in all thine abominations and thy whoredoms thou hast not remembered the days of thy youth when thou wast naked and bare and wast polluted in thy blood
nd it came to pass after all thy wickedness woe woe unto thee saith the ord  that thou hast also built unto thee an eminent place and hast made thee an high place in every street
hou hast built thy high place at every head of the way and hast made thy beauty to be abhorred and hast opened thy feet to every one that passed by and multiplied thy whoredoms
hou hast also committed fornication with the gyptians thy neighbours great of flesh and hast increased thy whoredoms to provoke me to anger
ehold therefore  have stretched out my hand over thee and have diminished thine ordinary food and delivered thee unto the will of them that hate thee the daughters of the hilistines which are ashamed of thy lewd way
hou hast played the whore also with the ssyrians because thou wast unsatiable yea thou hast played the harlot with them and yet couldest not be satisfied
hou hast moreover multiplied thy fornication in the land of anaan unto haldea and yet thou wast not satisfied herewith
ow weak is thine heart saith the ord  seeing thou doest all these things the work of an imperious whorish woman in that thou buildest thine eminent place in the head of every way and makest thine high place in every street and hast not been as an harlot in that thou scornest hire but as a wife that committeth adultery which taketh strangers instead of her husband
hey give gifts to all whores but thou givest thy gifts to all thy lovers and hirest them that they may come unto thee on every side for thy whoredom
nd the contrary is in thee from other women in thy whoredoms whereas none followeth thee to commit whoredoms and in that thou givest a reward and no reward is given unto thee therefore thou art contrary
herefore  harlot hear the word of the  hus saith the ord  ecause thy filthiness was poured out and thy nakedness discovered through thy whoredoms with thy lovers and with all the idols of thy abominations and by the blood of thy children which thou didst give unto them behold therefore  will gather all thy lovers with whom thou hast taken pleasure and all them that thou hast loved with all them that thou hast hated  will even gather them round about against thee and will discover thy nakedness unto them that they may see all thy nakedness
nd  will judge thee as women that break wedlock and shed blood are judged and  will give thee blood in fury and jealousy
nd  will also give thee into their hand and they shall throw down thine eminent place and shall break down thy high places they shall strip thee also of thy clothes and shall take thy fair jewels and leave thee naked and bare
hey shall also bring up a company against thee and they shall stone thee with stones and thrust thee through with their swords
nd they shall burn thine houses with fire and execute judgments upon thee in the sight of many women and  will cause thee to cease from playing the harlot and thou also shalt give no hire any more
o will  make my fury toward thee to rest and my jealousy shall depart from thee and  will be quiet and will be no more angry
ecause thou hast not remembered the days of thy youth but hast fretted me in all these things behold therefore  also will recompense thy way upon thine head saith the ord  and thou shalt not commit this lewdness above all thine abominations
ehold every one that useth proverbs shall use this proverb against thee saying s is the mother so is her daughter
hou art thy mothers daughter that lotheth her husband and her children and thou art the sister of thy sisters which lothed their husbands and their children your mother was an ittite and your father an morite
nd thine elder sister is amaria she and her daughters that dwell at thy left hand and thy younger sister that dwelleth at thy right hand is odom and her daughters
et hast thou not walked after their ways nor done after their abominations but as if that were a very little thing thou wast corrupted more than they in all thy ways
s  live saith the ord  odom thy sister hath not done she nor her daughters as thou hast done thou and thy daughters
ehold this was the iniquity of thy sister odom pride fulness of bread and abundance of idleness was in her and in her daughters neither did she strengthen the hand of the poor and needy
nd they were haughty and committed abomination before me therefore  took them away as  saw good
either hath amaria committed half of thy sins but thou hast multiplied thine abominations more than they and hast justified thy sisters in all thine abominations which thou hast done
hou also which hast judged thy sisters bear thine own shame for thy sins that thou hast committed more abominable than they they are more righteous than thou yea be thou confounded also and bear thy shame in that thou hast justified thy sisters
hen  shall bring again their captivity the captivity of odom and her daughters and the captivity of amaria and her daughters then will  bring again the captivity of thy captives in the midst of them that thou mayest bear thine own shame and mayest be confounded in all that thou hast done in that thou art a comfort unto them
hen thy sisters odom and her daughters shall return to their former estate and amaria and her daughters shall return to their former estate then thou and thy daughters shall return to your former estate
or thy sister odom was not mentioned by thy mouth in the day of thy pride before thy wickedness was discovered as at the time of thy reproach of the daughters of yria and all that are round about her the daughters of the hilistines which despise thee round about
hou hast borne thy lewdness and thine abominations saith the 
or thus saith the ord   will even deal with thee as thou hast done which hast despised the oath in breaking the covenant
evertheless  will remember my covenant with thee in the days of thy youth and  will establish unto thee an everlasting covenant
hen thou shalt remember thy ways and be ashamed when thou shalt receive thy sisters thine elder and thy younger and  will give them unto thee for daughters but not by thy covenant
nd  will establish my covenant with thee and thou shalt know that  am the  that thou mayest remember and be confounded and never open thy mouth any more because of thy shame when  am pacified toward thee for all that thou hast done saith the ord 
nd the word of the  came unto me saying on of man put forth a riddle and speak a parable unto the house of srael and say hus saith the ord   great eagle with great wings longwinged full of feathers which had divers colours came unto ebanon and took the highest branch of the cedar he cropped off the top of his young twigs and carried it into a land of traffick he set it in a city of merchants
e took also of the seed of the land and planted it in a fruitful field he placed it by great waters and set it as a willow tree
nd it grew and became a spreading vine of low stature whose branches turned toward him and the roots thereof were under him so it became a vine and brought forth branches and shot forth sprigs
here was also another great eagle with great wings and many feathers and behold this vine did bend her roots toward him and shot forth her branches toward him that he might water it by the furrows of her plantation
t was planted in a good soil by great waters that it might bring forth branches and that it might bear fruit that it might be a goodly vine
ay thou hus saith the ord  hall it prosper shall he not pull up the roots thereof and cut off the fruit thereof that it wither it shall wither in all the leaves of her spring even without great power or many people to pluck it up by the roots thereof
ea behold being planted shall it prosper shall it not utterly wither when the east wind toucheth it it shall wither in the furrows where it grew
oreover the word of the  came unto me saying ay now to the rebellious house now ye not what these things mean tell them ehold the king of abylon is come to erusalem and hath taken the king thereof and the princes thereof and led them with him to abylon and hath taken of the kings seed and made a covenant with him and hath taken an oath of him he hath also taken the mighty of the land that the kingdom might be base that it might not lift itself up but that by keeping of his covenant it might stand
ut he rebelled against him in sending his ambassadors into gypt that they might give him horses and much people
hall he prosper shall he escape that doeth such things or shall he break the covenant and be delivered
s  live saith the ord  surely in the place where the king dwelleth that made him king whose oath he despised and whose covenant he brake even with him in the midst of abylon he shall die
either shall haraoh with his mighty army and great company make for him in the war by casting up mounts and building forts to cut off many persons seeing he despised the oath by breaking the covenant when lo he had given his hand and hath done all these things he shall not escape
herefore thus saith the ord  s  live surely mine oath that he hath despised and my covenant that he hath broken even it will  recompense upon his own head
nd  will spread my net upon him and he shall be taken in my snare and  will bring him to abylon and will plead with him there for his trespass that he hath trespassed against me
nd all his fugitives with all his bands shall fall by the sword and they that remain shall be scattered toward all winds and ye shall know that  the  have spoken it
hus saith the ord   will also take of the highest branch of the high cedar and will set it  will crop off from the top of his young twigs a tender one and will plant it upon an high mountain and eminent in the mountain of the height of srael will  plant it and it shall bring forth boughs and bear fruit and be a goodly cedar and under it shall dwell all fowl of every wing in the shadow of the branches thereof shall they dwell
nd all the trees of the field shall know that  the  have brought down the high tree have exalted the low tree have dried up the green tree and have made the dry tree to flourish  the  have spoken and have done it
he word of the  came unto me again saying hat mean ye that ye use this proverb concerning the land of srael saying he fathers have eaten sour grapes and the childrens teeth are set on edge
s  live saith the ord  ye shall not have occasion any more to use this proverb in srael
ehold all souls are mine as the soul of the father so also the soul of the son is mine the soul that sinneth it shall die
ut if a man be just and do that which is lawful and right and hath not eaten upon the mountains neither hath lifted up his eyes to the idols of the house of srael neither hath defiled his neighbours wife neither hath come near to a menstruous woman and hath not oppressed any but hath restored to the debtor his pledge hath spoiled none by violence hath given his bread to the hungry and hath covered the naked with a garment he that hath not given forth upon usury neither hath taken any increase that hath withdrawn his hand from iniquity hath executed true judgment between man and man hath walked in my statutes and hath kept my judgments to deal truly he is just he shall surely live saith the ord 
f he beget a son that is a robber a shedder of blood and that doeth the like to any one of these things and that doeth not any of those duties but even hath eaten upon the mountains and defiled his neighbours wife hath oppressed the poor and needy hath spoiled by violence hath not restored the pledge and hath lifted up his eyes to the idols hath committed abomination hath given forth upon usury and hath taken increase shall he then live he shall not live he hath done all these abominations he shall surely die his blood shall be upon him
ow lo if he beget a son that seeth all his fathers sins which he hath done and considereth and doeth not such like that hath not eaten upon the mountains neither hath lifted up his eyes to the idols of the house of srael hath not defiled his neighbours wife neither hath oppressed any hath not withholden the pledge neither hath spoiled by violence but hath given his bread to the hungry and hath covered the naked with a garment that hath taken off his hand from the poor that hath not received usury nor increase hath executed my judgments hath walked in my statutes he shall not die for the iniquity of his father he shall surely live
s for his father because he cruelly oppressed spoiled his brother by violence and did that which is not good among his people lo even he shall die in his iniquity
et say ye hy doth not the son bear the iniquity of the father
hen the son hath done that which is lawful and right and hath kept all my statutes and hath done them he shall surely live
he soul that sinneth it shall die
he son shall not bear the iniquity of the father neither shall the father bear the iniquity of the son the righteousness of the righteous shall be upon him and the wickedness of the wicked shall be upon him
ut if the wicked will turn from all his sins that he hath committed and keep all my statutes and do that which is lawful and right he shall surely live he shall not die
ll his transgressions that he hath committed they shall not be mentioned unto him in his righteousness that he hath done he shall live
ave  any pleasure at all that the wicked should die saith the ord  and not that he should return from his ways and live
ut when the righteous turneth away from his righteousness and committeth iniquity and doeth according to all the abominations that the wicked man doeth shall he live
ll his righteousness that he hath done shall not be mentioned in his trespass that he hath trespassed and in his sin that he hath sinned in them shall he die
et ye say he way of the ord is not equal
ear now  house of srael s not my way equal are not your ways unequal
hen a righteous man turneth away from his righteousness and committeth iniquity and dieth in them for his iniquity that he hath done shall he die
gain when the wicked man turneth away from his wickedness that he hath committed and doeth that which is lawful and right he shall save his soul alive
ecause he considereth and turneth away from all his transgressions that he hath committed he shall surely live he shall not die
et saith the house of srael he way of the ord is not equal
 house of srael are not my ways equal are not your ways unequal
herefore  will judge you  house of srael every one according to his ways saith the ord 
epent and turn yourselves from all your transgressions so iniquity shall not be your ruin
ast away from you all your transgressions whereby ye have transgressed and make you a new heart and a new spirit for why will ye die  house of srael
or  have no pleasure in the death of him that dieth saith the ord  wherefore turn yourselves and live ye
oreover take thou up a lamentation for the princes of srael and say hat is thy mother
 lioness she lay down among lions she nourished her whelps among young lions
nd she brought up one of her whelps it became a young lion and it learned to catch the prey it devoured men
he nations also heard of him he was taken in their pit and they brought him with chains unto the land of gypt
