ow when she saw that she had waited and her hope was lost then she took another of her whelps and made him a young lion
nd he went up and down among the lions he became a young lion and learned to catch the prey and devoured men
nd he knew their desolate palaces and he laid waste their cities and the land was desolate and the fulness thereof by the noise of his roaring
hen the nations set against him on every side from the provinces and spread their net over him he was taken in their pit
nd they put him in ward in chains and brought him to the king of abylon they brought him into holds that his voice should no more be heard upon the mountains of srael
hy mother is like a vine in thy blood planted by the waters she was fruitful and full of branches by reason of many waters
nd she had strong rods for the sceptres of them that bare rule and her stature was exalted among the thick branches and she appeared in her height with the multitude of her branches
ut she was plucked up in fury she was cast down to the ground and the east wind dried up her fruit her strong rods were broken and withered the fire consumed them
nd now she is planted in the wilderness in a dry and thirsty ground
nd fire is gone out of a rod of her branches which hath devoured her fruit so that she hath no strong rod to be a sceptre to rule
his is a lamentation and shall be for a lamentation
nd it came to pass in the seventh year in the fifth month the tenth day of the month that certain of the elders of srael came to enquire of the  and sat before me
hen came the word of the  unto me saying on of man speak unto the elders of srael and say unto them hus saith the ord  re ye come to enquire of me
s  live saith the ord   will not be enquired of by you
ilt thou judge them son of man wilt thou judge them cause them to know the abominations of their fathers and say unto them hus saith the ord  n the day when  chose srael and lifted up mine hand unto the seed of the house of acob and made myself known unto them in the land of gypt when  lifted up mine hand unto them saying  am the  your od in the day that  lifted up mine hand unto them to bring them forth of the land of gypt into a land that  had espied for them flowing with milk and honey which is the glory of all lands then said  unto them ast ye away every man the abominations of his eyes and defile not yourselves with the idols of gypt  am the  your od
ut they rebelled against me and would not hearken unto me they did not every man cast away the abominations of their eyes neither did they forsake the idols of gypt then  said  will pour out my fury upon them to accomplish my anger against them in the midst of the land of gypt
ut  wrought for my names sake that it should not be polluted before the heathen among whom they were in whose sight  made myself known unto them in bringing them forth out of the land of gypt
herefore  caused them to go forth out of the land of gypt and brought them into the wilderness
nd  gave them my statutes and shewed them my judgments which if a man do he shall even live in them
oreover also  gave them my sabbaths to be a sign between me and them that they might know that  am the  that sanctify them
ut the house of srael rebelled against me in the wilderness they walked not in my statutes and they despised my judgments which if a man do he shall even live in them and my sabbaths they greatly polluted then  said  would pour out my fury upon them in the wilderness to consume them
ut  wrought for my names sake that it should not be polluted before the heathen in whose sight  brought them out
et also  lifted up my hand unto them in the wilderness that  would not bring them into the land which  had given them flowing with milk and honey which is the glory of all lands because they despised my judgments and walked not in my statutes but polluted my sabbaths for their heart went after their idols
evertheless mine eye spared them from destroying them neither did  make an end of them in the wilderness
ut  said unto their children in the wilderness alk ye not in the statutes of your fathers neither observe their judgments nor defile yourselves with their idols  am the  your od walk in my statutes and keep my judgments and do them and hallow my sabbaths and they shall be a sign between me and you that ye may know that  am the  your od
otwithstanding the children rebelled against me they walked not in my statutes neither kept my judgments to do them which if a man do he shall even live in them they polluted my sabbaths then  said  would pour out my fury upon them to accomplish my anger against them in the wilderness
evertheless  withdrew mine hand and wrought for my names sake that it should not be polluted in the sight of the heathen in whose sight  brought them forth
 lifted up mine hand unto them also in the wilderness that  would scatter them among the heathen and disperse them through the countries because they had not executed my judgments but had despised my statutes and had polluted my sabbaths and their eyes were after their fathers idols
herefore  gave them also statutes that were not good and judgments whereby they should not live and  polluted them in their own gifts in that they caused to pass through the fire all that openeth the womb that  might make them desolate to the end that they might know that  am the 
herefore son of man speak unto the house of srael and say unto them hus saith the ord  et in this your fathers have blasphemed me in that they have committed a trespass against me
or when  had brought them into the land for the which  lifted up mine hand to give it to them then they saw every high hill and all the thick trees and they offered there their sacrifices and there they presented the provocation of their offering there also they made their sweet savour and poured out there their drink offerings
hen  said unto them hat is the high place whereunto ye go
nd the name thereof is called amah unto this day
herefore say unto the house of srael hus saith the ord  re ye polluted after the manner of your fathers and commit ye whoredom after their abominations
or when ye offer your gifts when ye make your sons to pass through the fire ye pollute yourselves with all your idols even unto this day and shall  be enquired of by you  house of srael
s  live saith the ord   will not be enquired of by you
nd that which cometh into your mind shall not be at all that ye say e will be as the heathen as the families of the countries to serve wood and stone
s  live saith the ord  surely with a mighty hand and with a stretched out arm and with fury poured out will  rule over you and  will bring you out from the people and will gather you out of the countries wherein ye are scattered with a mighty hand and with a stretched out arm and with fury poured out
nd  will bring you into the wilderness of the people and there will  plead with you face to face
ike as  pleaded with your fathers in the wilderness of the land of gypt so will  plead with you saith the ord 
nd  will cause you to pass under the rod and  will bring you into the bond of the covenant and  will purge out from among you the rebels and them that transgress against me  will bring them forth out of the country where they sojourn and they shall not enter into the land of srael and ye shall know that  am the 
s for you  house of srael thus saith the ord  o ye serve ye every one his idols and hereafter also if ye will not hearken unto me but pollute ye my holy name no more with your gifts and with your idols
or in mine holy mountain in the mountain of the height of srael saith the ord  there shall all the house of srael all of them in the land serve me there will  accept them and there will  require your offerings and the firstfruits of your oblations with all your holy things
 will accept you with your sweet savour when  bring you out from the people and gather you out of the countries wherein ye have been scattered and  will be sanctified in you before the heathen
nd ye shall know that  am the  when  shall bring you into the land of srael into the country for the which  lifted up mine hand to give it to your fathers
nd there shall ye remember your ways and all your doings wherein ye have been defiled and ye shall lothe yourselves in your own sight for all your evils that ye have committed
nd ye shall know that  am the  when  have wrought with you for my names sake not according to your wicked ways nor according to your corrupt doings  ye house of srael saith the ord 
oreover the word of the  came unto me saying on of man set thy face toward the south and drop thy word toward the south and prophesy against the forest of the south field and say to the forest of the south ear the word of the  hus saith the ord  ehold  will kindle a fire in thee and it shall devour every green tree in thee and every dry tree the flaming flame shall not be quenched and all faces from the south to the north shall be burned therein
nd all flesh shall see that  the  have kindled it it shall not be quenched
hen said  h ord  they say of me oth he not speak parables
nd the word of the  came unto me saying on of man set thy face toward erusalem and drop thy word toward the holy places and prophesy against the land of srael and say to the land of srael hus saith the  ehold  am against thee and will draw forth my sword out of his sheath and will cut off from thee the righteous and the wicked
eeing then that  will cut off from thee the righteous and the wicked therefore shall my sword go forth out of his sheath against all flesh from the south to the north that all flesh may know that  the  have drawn forth my sword out of his sheath it shall not return any more
igh therefore thou son of man with the breaking of thy loins and with bitterness sigh before their eyes
nd it shall be when they say unto thee herefore sighest thou that thou shalt answer or the tidings because it cometh and every heart shall melt and all hands shall be feeble and every spirit shall faint and all knees shall be weak as water behold it cometh and shall be brought to pass saith the ord 
gain the word of the  came unto me saying on of man prophesy and say hus saith the  ay  sword a sword is sharpened and also furbished it is sharpened to make a sore slaughter it is furbished that it may glitter should we then make mirth it contemneth the rod of my son as every tree
nd he hath given it to be furbished that it may be handled this sword is sharpened and it is furbished to give it into the hand of the slayer
ry and howl son of man for it shall be upon my people it shall be upon all the princes of srael terrors by reason of the sword shall be upon my people smite therefore upon thy thigh
ecause it is a trial and what if the sword contemn even the rod it shall be no more saith the ord 
hou therefore son of man prophesy and smite thine hands together and let the sword be doubled the third time the sword of the slain it is the sword of the great men that are slain which entereth into their privy chambers
 have set the point of the sword against all their gates that their heart may faint and their ruins be multiplied ah it is made bright it is wrapped up for the slaughter
o thee one way or other either on the right hand or on the left whithersoever thy face is set
 will also smite mine hands together and  will cause my fury to rest  the  have said it
he word of the  came unto me again saying lso thou son of man appoint thee two ways that the sword of the king of abylon may come both twain shall come forth out of one land and choose thou a place choose it at the head of the way to the city
ppoint a way that the sword may come to abbath of the mmonites and to udah in erusalem the defenced
or the king of abylon stood at the parting of the way at the head of the two ways to use divination he made his arrows bright he consulted with images he looked in the liver
t his right hand was the divination for erusalem to appoint captains to open the mouth in the slaughter to lift up the voice with shouting to appoint battering rams against the gates to cast a mount and to build a fort
nd it shall be unto them as a false divination in their sight to them that have sworn oaths but he will call to remembrance the iniquity that they may be taken
herefore thus saith the ord  ecause ye have made your iniquity to be remembered in that your transgressions are discovered so that in all your doings your sins do appear because  say that ye are come to remembrance ye shall be taken with the hand
nd thou profane wicked prince of srael whose day is come when iniquity shall have an end thus saith the ord  emove the diadem and take off the crown this shall not be the same exalt him that is low and abase him that is high
 will overturn overturn overturn it and it shall be no more until he come whose right it is and  will give it him
nd thou son of man prophesy and say hus saith the ord  concerning the mmonites and concerning their reproach even say thou he sword the sword is drawn for the slaughter it is furbished to consume because of the glittering whiles they see vanity unto thee whiles they divine a lie unto thee to bring thee upon the necks of them that are slain of the wicked whose day is come when their iniquity shall have an end
hall  cause it to return into his sheath
 will judge thee in the place where thou wast created in the land of thy nativity
nd  will pour out mine indignation upon thee  will blow against thee in the fire of my wrath and deliver thee into the hand of brutish men and skilful to destroy
hou shalt be for fuel to the fire thy blood shall be in the midst of the land thou shalt be no more remembered for  the  have spoken it
oreover the word of the  came unto me saying ow thou son of man wilt thou judge wilt thou judge the bloody city yea thou shalt shew her all her abominations
hen say thou hus saith the ord  he city sheddeth blood in the midst of it that her time may come and maketh idols against herself to defile herself
hou art become guilty in thy blood that thou hast shed and hast defiled thyself in thine idols which thou hast made and thou hast caused thy days to draw near and art come even unto thy years therefore have  made thee a reproach unto the heathen and a mocking to all countries
hose that be near and those that be far from thee shall mock thee which art infamous and much vexed
ehold the princes of srael every one were in thee to their power to shed blood
n thee have they set light by father and mother in the midst of thee have they dealt by oppression with the stranger in thee have they vexed the fatherless and the widow
hou hast despised mine holy things and hast profaned my sabbaths
n thee are men that carry tales to shed blood and in thee they eat upon the mountains in the midst of thee they commit lewdness
n thee have they discovered their fathers nakedness in thee have they humbled her that was set apart for pollution
nd one hath committed abomination with his neighbours wife and another hath lewdly defiled his daughter in law and another in thee hath humbled his sister his fathers daughter
n thee have they taken gifts to shed blood thou hast taken usury and increase and thou hast greedily gained of thy neighbours by extortion and hast forgotten me saith the ord 
ehold therefore  have smitten mine hand at thy dishonest gain which thou hast made and at thy blood which hath been in the midst of thee
an thine heart endure or can thine hands be strong in the days that  shall deal with thee
 the  have spoken it and will do it
nd  will scatter thee among the heathen and disperse thee in the countries and will consume thy filthiness out of thee
nd thou shalt take thine inheritance in thyself in the sight of the heathen and thou shalt know that  am the 
nd the word of the  came unto me saying on of man the house of srael is to me become dross all they are brass and tin and iron and lead in the midst of the furnace they are even the dross of silver
herefore thus saith the ord  ecause ye are all become dross behold therefore  will gather you into the midst of erusalem
s they gather silver and brass and iron and lead and tin into the midst of the furnace to blow the fire upon it to melt it so will  gather you in mine anger and in my fury and  will leave you there and melt you
ea  will gather you and blow upon you in the fire of my wrath and ye shall be melted in the midst thereof
s silver is melted in the midst of the furnace so shall ye be melted in the midst thereof and ye shall know that  the  have poured out my fury upon you
nd the word of the  came unto me saying on of man say unto her hou art the land that is not cleansed nor rained upon in the day of indignation
here is a conspiracy of her prophets in the midst thereof like a roaring lion ravening the prey they have devoured souls they have taken the treasure and precious things they have made her many widows in the midst thereof
er priests have violated my law and have profaned mine holy things they have put no difference between the holy and profane neither have they shewed difference between the unclean and the clean and have hid their eyes from my sabbaths and  am profaned among them
er princes in the midst thereof are like wolves ravening the prey to shed blood and to destroy souls to get dishonest gain
nd her prophets have daubed them with untempered morter seeing vanity and divining lies unto them saying hus saith the ord  when the  hath not spoken
he people of the land have used oppression and exercised robbery and have vexed the poor and needy yea they have oppressed the stranger wrongfully
nd  sought for a man among them that should make up the hedge and stand in the gap before me for the land that  should not destroy it but  found none
herefore have  poured out mine indignation upon them  have consumed them with the fire of my wrath their own way have  recompensed upon their heads saith the ord 
he word of the  came again unto me saying on of man there were two women the daughters of one mother and they committed whoredoms in gypt they committed whoredoms in their youth there were their breasts pressed and there they bruised the teats of their virginity
nd the names of them were holah the elder and holibah her sister and they were mine and they bare sons and daughters
hus were their names amaria is holah and erusalem holibah
nd holah played the harlot when she was mine and she doted on her lovers on the ssyrians her neighbours which were clothed with blue captains and rulers all of them desirable young men horsemen riding upon horses
hus she committed her whoredoms with them with all them that were the chosen men of ssyria and with all on whom she doted with all their idols she defiled herself
either left she her whoredoms brought from gypt for in her youth they lay with her and they bruised the breasts of her virginity and poured their whoredom upon her
herefore  have delivered her into the hand of her lovers into the hand of the ssyrians upon whom she doted
hese discovered her nakedness they took her sons and her daughters and slew her with the sword and she became famous among women for they had executed judgment upon her
nd when her sister holibah saw this she was more corrupt in her inordinate love than she and in her whoredoms more than her sister in her whoredoms
he doted upon the ssyrians her neighbours captains and rulers clothed most gorgeously horsemen riding upon horses all of them desirable young men
hen  saw that she was defiled that they took both one way and that she increased her whoredoms for when she saw men pourtrayed upon the wall the images of the haldeans pourtrayed with vermilion girded with girdles upon their loins exceeding in dyed attire upon their heads all of them princes to look to after the manner of the abylonians of haldea the land of their nativity and as soon as she saw them with her eyes she doted upon them and sent messengers unto them into haldea
nd the abylonians came to her into the bed of love and they defiled her with their whoredom and she was polluted with them and her mind was alienated from them
o she discovered her whoredoms and discovered her nakedness then my mind was alienated from her like as my mind was alienated from her sister
et she multiplied her whoredoms in calling to remembrance the days of her youth wherein she had played the harlot in the land of gypt
or she doted upon their paramours whose flesh is as the flesh of asses and whose issue is like the issue of horses
hus thou calledst to remembrance the lewdness of thy youth in bruising thy teats by the gyptians for the paps of thy youth
herefore  holibah thus saith the ord  ehold  will raise up thy lovers against thee from whom thy mind is alienated and  will bring them against thee on every side the abylonians and all the haldeans ekod and hoa and oa and all the ssyrians with them all of them desirable young men captains and rulers great lords and renowned all of them riding upon horses
nd they shall come against thee with chariots wagons and wheels and with an assembly of people which shall set against thee buckler and shield and helmet round about and  will set judgment before them and they shall judge thee according to their judgments
nd  will set my jealousy against thee and they shall deal furiously with thee they shall take away thy nose and thine ears and thy remnant shall fall by the sword they shall take thy sons and thy daughters and thy residue shall be devoured by the fire
hey shall also strip thee out of thy clothes and take away thy fair jewels
hus will  make thy lewdness to cease from thee and thy whoredom brought from the land of gypt so that thou shalt not lift up thine eyes unto them nor remember gypt any more
or thus saith the ord  ehold  will deliver thee into the hand of them whom thou hatest into the hand of them from whom thy mind is alienated and they shall deal with thee hatefully and shall take away all thy labour and shall leave thee naked and bare and the nakedness of thy whoredoms shall be discovered both thy lewdness and thy whoredoms
 will do these things unto thee because thou hast gone a whoring after the heathen and because thou art polluted with their idols
hou hast walked in the way of thy sister therefore will  give her cup into thine hand
hus saith the ord  hou shalt drink of thy sisters cup deep and large thou shalt be laughed to scorn and had in derision it containeth much
hou shalt be filled with drunkenness and sorrow with the cup of astonishment and desolation with the cup of thy sister amaria
hou shalt even drink it and suck it out and thou shalt break the sherds thereof and pluck off thine own breasts for  have spoken it saith the ord 
herefore thus saith the ord  ecause thou hast forgotten me and cast me behind thy back therefore bear thou also thy lewdness and thy whoredoms
he  said moreover unto me on of man wilt thou judge holah and holibah yea declare unto them their abominations that they have committed adultery and blood is in their hands and with their idols have they committed adultery and have also caused their sons whom they bare unto me to pass for them through the fire to devour them
oreover this they have done unto me they have defiled my sanctuary in the same day and have profaned my sabbaths
or when they had slain their children to their idols then they came the same day into my sanctuary to profane it and lo thus have they done in the midst of mine house
nd furthermore that ye have sent for men to come from far unto whom a messenger was sent and lo they came for whom thou didst wash thyself paintedst thy eyes and deckedst thyself with ornaments and satest upon a stately bed and a table prepared before it whereupon thou hast set mine incense and mine oil
nd a voice of a multitude being at ease was with her and with the men of the common sort were brought abeans from the wilderness which put bracelets upon their hands and beautiful crowns upon their heads
hen said  unto her that was old in adulteries ill they now commit whoredoms with her and she with them
et they went in unto her as they go in unto a woman that playeth the harlot so went they in unto holah and unto holibah the lewd women
nd the righteous men they shall judge them after the manner of adulteresses and after the manner of women that shed blood because they are adulteresses and blood is in their hands
or thus saith the ord   will bring up a company upon them and will give them to be removed and spoiled
nd the company shall stone them with stones and dispatch them with their swords they shall slay their sons and their daughters and burn up their houses with fire
hus will  cause lewdness to cease out of the land that all women may be taught not to do after your lewdness
nd they shall recompense your lewdness upon you and ye shall bear the sins of your idols and ye shall know that  am the ord 
gain in the ninth year in the tenth month in the tenth day of the month the word of the  came unto me saying on of man write thee the name of the day even of this same day the king of abylon set himself against erusalem this same day
nd utter a parable unto the rebellious house and say unto them hus saith the ord  et on a pot set it on and also pour water into it gather the pieces thereof into it even every good piece the thigh and the shoulder fill it with the choice bones
ake the choice of the flock and burn also the bones under it and make it boil well and let them seethe the bones of it therein
herefore thus saith the ord  oe to the bloody city to the pot whose scum is therein and whose scum is not gone out of it bring it out piece by piece let no lot fall upon it
or her blood is in the midst of her she set it upon the top of a rock she poured it not upon the ground to cover it with dust that it might cause fury to come up to take vengeance  have set her blood upon the top of a rock that it should not be covered
herefore thus saith the ord  oe to the bloody city
 will even make the pile for fire great
eap on wood kindle the fire consume the flesh and spice it well and let the bones be burned
hen set it empty upon the coals thereof that the brass of it may be hot and may burn and that the filthiness of it may be molten in it that the scum of it may be consumed
he hath wearied herself with lies and her great scum went not forth out of her her scum shall be in the fire
n thy filthiness is lewdness because  have purged thee and thou wast not purged thou shalt not be purged from thy filthiness any more till  have caused my fury to rest upon thee
 the  have spoken it it shall come to pass and  will do it  will not go back neither will  spare neither will  repent according to thy ways and according to thy doings shall they judge thee saith the ord 
lso the word of the  came unto me saying on of man behold  take away from thee the desire of thine eyes with a stroke yet neither shalt thou mourn nor weep neither shall thy tears run down
orbear to cry make no mourning for the dead bind the tire of thine head upon thee and put on thy shoes upon thy feet and cover not thy lips and eat not the bread of men
o  spake unto the people in the morning and at even my wife died and  did in the morning as  was commanded
nd the people said unto me ilt thou not tell us what these things are to us that thou doest so
hen  answered them he word of the  came unto me saying peak unto the house of srael hus saith the ord  ehold  will profane my sanctuary the excellency of your strength the desire of your eyes and that which your soul pitieth and your sons and your daughters whom ye have left shall fall by the sword
nd ye shall do as  have done ye shall not cover your lips nor eat the bread of men
nd your tires shall be upon your heads and your shoes upon your feet ye shall not mourn nor weep but ye shall pine away for your iniquities and mourn one toward another
hus zekiel is unto you a sign according to all that he hath done shall ye do and when this cometh ye shall know that  am the ord 
lso thou son of man shall it not be in the day when  take from them their strength the joy of their glory the desire of their eyes and that whereupon they set their minds their sons and their daughters that he that escapeth in that day shall come unto thee to cause thee to hear it with thine ears
n that day shall thy mouth be opened to him which is escaped and thou shalt speak and be no more dumb and thou shalt be a sign unto them and they shall know that  am the 
he word of the  came again unto me saying on of man set thy face against the mmonites and prophesy against them and say unto the mmonites ear the word of the ord  hus saith the ord  ecause thou saidst ha against my sanctuary when it was profaned and against the land of srael when it was desolate and against the house of udah when they went into captivity behold therefore  will deliver thee to the men of the east for a possession and they shall set their palaces in thee and make their dwellings in thee they shall eat thy fruit and they shall drink thy milk
nd  will make abbah a stable for camels and the mmonites a couchingplace for flocks and ye shall know that  am the 
or thus saith the ord  ecause thou hast clapped thine hands and stamped with the feet and rejoiced in heart with all thy despite against the land of srael behold therefore  will stretch out mine hand upon thee and will deliver thee for a spoil to the heathen and  will cut thee off from the people and  will cause thee to perish out of the countries  will destroy thee and thou shalt know that  am the 
hus saith the ord  ecause that oab and eir do say ehold the house of udah is like unto all the heathen therefore behold  will open the side of oab from the cities from his cities which are on his frontiers the glory of the country ethjeshimoth aalmeon and iriathaim unto the men of the east with the mmonites and will give them in possession that the mmonites may not be remembered among the nations
nd  will execute judgments upon oab and they shall know that  am the 
hus saith the ord  ecause that dom hath dealt against the house of udah by taking vengeance and hath greatly offended and revenged himself upon them therefore thus saith the ord   will also stretch out mine hand upon dom and will cut off man and beast from it and  will make it desolate from eman and they of edan shall fall by the sword
nd  will lay my vengeance upon dom by the hand of my people srael and they shall do in dom according to mine anger and according to my fury and they shall know my vengeance saith the ord 
hus saith the ord  ecause the hilistines have dealt by revenge and have taken vengeance with a despiteful heart to destroy it for the old hatred therefore thus saith the ord  ehold  will stretch out mine hand upon the hilistines and  will cut off the herethims and destroy the remnant of the sea coast
nd  will execute great vengeance upon them with furious rebukes and they shall know that  am the  when  shall lay my vengeance upon them
nd it came to pass in the eleventh year in the first day of the month that the word of the  came unto me saying on of man because that yrus hath said against erusalem ha she is broken that was the gates of the people she is turned unto me  shall be replenished now she is laid waste therefore thus saith the ord  ehold  am against thee  yrus and will cause many nations to come up against thee as the sea causeth his waves to come up
nd they shall destroy the walls of yrus and break down her towers  will also scrape her dust from her and make her like the top of a rock
t shall be a place for the spreading of nets in the midst of the sea for  have spoken it saith the ord  and it shall become a spoil to the nations
nd her daughters which are in the field shall be slain by the sword and they shall know that  am the 
or thus saith the ord  ehold  will bring upon yrus ebuchadrezzar king of abylon a king of kings from the north with horses and with chariots and with horsemen and companies and much people
e shall slay with the sword thy daughters in the field and he shall make a fort against thee and cast a mount against thee and lift up the buckler against thee
nd he shall set engines of war against thy walls and with his axes he shall break down thy towers
y reason of the abundance of his horses their dust shall cover thee thy walls shall shake at the noise of the horsemen and of the wheels and of the chariots when he shall enter into thy gates as men enter into a city wherein is made a breach
ith the hoofs of his horses shall he tread down all thy streets he shall slay thy people by the sword and thy strong garrisons shall go down to the ground
nd they shall make a spoil of thy riches and make a prey of thy merchandise and they shall break down thy walls and destroy thy pleasant houses and they shall lay thy stones and thy timber and thy dust in the midst of the water
nd  will cause the noise of thy songs to cease and the sound of thy harps shall be no more heard
nd  will make thee like the top of a rock thou shalt be a place to spread nets upon thou shalt be built no more for  the  have spoken it saith the ord 
hus saith the ord  to yrus hall not the isles shake at the sound of thy fall when the wounded cry when the slaughter is made in the midst of thee
hen all the princes of the sea shall come down from their thrones and lay away their robes and put off their broidered garments they shall clothe themselves with trembling they shall sit upon the ground and shall tremble at every moment and be astonished at thee
nd they shall take up a lamentation for thee and say to thee ow art thou destroyed that wast inhabited of seafaring men the renowned city which wast strong in the sea she and her inhabitants which cause their terror to be on all that haunt it
ow shall the isles tremble in the day of thy fall yea the isles that are in the sea shall be troubled at thy departure
or thus saith the ord  hen  shall make thee a desolate city like the cities that are not inhabited when  shall bring up the deep upon thee and great waters shall cover thee when  shall bring thee down with them that descend into the pit with the people of old time and shall set thee in the low parts of the earth in places desolate of old with them that go down to the pit that thou be not inhabited and  shall set glory in the land of the living  will make thee a terror and thou shalt be no more though thou be sought for yet shalt thou never be found again saith the ord 
he word of the  came again unto me saying ow thou son of man take up a lamentation for yrus and say unto yrus  thou that art situate at the entry of the sea which art a merchant of the people for many isles hus saith the ord   yrus thou hast said  am of perfect beauty
hy borders are in the midst of the seas thy builders have perfected thy beauty
hey have made all thy ship boards of fir trees of enir they have taken cedars from ebanon to make masts for thee
f the oaks of ashan have they made thine oars the company of the shurites have made thy benches of ivory brought out of the isles of hittim
ine linen with broidered work from gypt was that which thou spreadest forth to be thy sail blue and purple from the isles of lishah was that which covered thee
he inhabitants of idon and rvad were thy mariners thy wise men  yrus that were in thee were thy pilots
he ancients of ebal and the wise men thereof were in thee thy calkers all the ships of the sea with their mariners were in thee to occupy thy merchandise
hey of ersia and of ud and of hut were in thine army thy men of war they hanged the shield and helmet in thee they set forth thy comeliness
he men of rvad with thine army were upon thy walls round about and the ammadims were in thy towers they hanged their shields upon thy walls round about they have made thy beauty perfect
arshish was thy merchant by reason of the multitude of all kind of riches with silver iron tin and lead they traded in thy fairs
avan ubal and eshech they were thy merchants they traded the persons of men and vessels of brass in thy market
hey of the house of ogarmah traded in thy fairs with horses and horsemen and mules
he men of edan were thy merchants many isles were the merchandise of thine hand they brought thee for a present horns of ivory and ebony
yria was thy merchant by reason of the multitude of the wares of thy making they occupied in thy fairs with emeralds purple and broidered work and fine linen and coral and agate
udah and the land of srael they were thy merchants they traded in thy market wheat of innith and annag and honey and oil and balm
amascus was thy merchant in the multitude of the wares of thy making for the multitude of all riches in the wine of elbon and white wool
an also and avan going to and fro occupied in thy fairs bright iron cassia and calamus were in thy market
edan was thy merchant in precious clothes for chariots
rabia and all the princes of edar they occupied with thee in lambs and rams and goats in these were they thy merchants
he merchants of heba and aamah they were thy merchants they occupied in thy fairs with chief of all spices and with all precious stones and gold
aran and anneh and den the merchants of heba sshur and hilmad were thy merchants
hese were thy merchants in all sorts of things in blue clothes and broidered work and in chests of rich apparel bound with cords and made of cedar among thy merchandise
he ships of arshish did sing of thee in thy market and thou wast replenished and made very glorious in the midst of the seas
hy rowers have brought thee into great waters the east wind hath broken thee in the midst of the seas
hy riches and thy fairs thy merchandise thy mariners and thy pilots thy calkers and the occupiers of thy merchandise and all thy men of war that are in thee and in all thy company which is in the midst of thee shall fall into the midst of the seas in the day of thy ruin
he suburbs shall shake at the sound of the cry of thy pilots
nd all that handle the oar the mariners and all the pilots of the sea shall come down from their ships they shall stand upon the land and shall cause their voice to be heard against thee and shall cry bitterly and shall cast up dust upon their heads they shall wallow themselves in the ashes and they shall make themselves utterly bald for thee and gird them with sackcloth and they shall weep for thee with bitterness of heart and bitter wailing
nd in their wailing they shall take up a lamentation for thee and lament over thee saying hat city is like yrus like the destroyed in the midst of the sea
hen thy wares went forth out of the seas thou filledst many people thou didst enrich the kings of the earth with the multitude of thy riches and of thy merchandise
n the time when thou shalt be broken by the seas in the depths of the waters thy merchandise and all thy company in the midst of thee shall fall
ll the inhabitants of the isles shall be astonished at thee and their kings shall be sore afraid they shall be troubled in their countenance
he merchants among the people shall hiss at thee thou shalt be a terror and never shalt be any more
he word of the  came again unto me saying on of man say unto the prince of yrus hus saith the ord  ecause thine heart is lifted up and thou hast said  am a od  sit in the seat of od in the midst of the seas yet thou art a man and not od though thou set thine heart as the heart of od behold thou art wiser than aniel there is no secret that they can hide from thee with thy wisdom and with thine understanding thou hast gotten thee riches and hast gotten gold and silver into thy treasures by thy great wisdom and by thy traffick hast thou increased thy riches and thine heart is lifted up because of thy riches herefore thus saith the ord  ecause thou hast set thine heart as the heart of od behold therefore  will bring strangers upon thee the terrible of the nations and they shall draw their swords against the beauty of thy wisdom and they shall defile thy brightness
hey shall bring thee down to the pit and thou shalt die the deaths of them that are slain in the midst of the seas
ilt thou yet say before him that slayeth thee  am od but thou shalt be a man and no od in the hand of him that slayeth thee
hou shalt die the deaths of the uncircumcised by the hand of strangers for  have spoken it saith the ord 
oreover the word of the  came unto me saying on of man take up a lamentation upon the king of yrus and say unto him hus saith the ord  hou sealest up the sum full of wisdom and perfect in beauty
hou hast been in den the garden of od every precious stone was thy covering the sardius topaz and the diamond the beryl the onyx and the jasper the sapphire the emerald and the carbuncle and gold the workmanship of thy tabrets and of thy pipes was prepared in thee in the day that thou wast created
hou art the anointed cherub that covereth and  have set thee so thou wast upon the holy mountain of od thou hast walked up and down in the midst of the stones of fire
hou wast perfect in thy ways from the day that thou wast created till iniquity was found in thee
y the multitude of thy merchandise they have filled the midst of thee with violence and thou hast sinned therefore  will cast thee as profane out of the mountain of od and  will destroy thee  covering cherub from the midst of the stones of fire
hine heart was lifted up because of thy beauty thou hast corrupted thy wisdom by reason of thy brightness  will cast thee to the ground  will lay thee before kings that they may behold thee
hou hast defiled thy sanctuaries by the multitude of thine iniquities by the iniquity of thy traffick therefore will  bring forth a fire from the midst of thee it shall devour thee and  will bring thee to ashes upon the earth in the sight of all them that behold thee
ll they that know thee among the people shall be astonished at thee thou shalt be a terror and never shalt thou be any more
gain the word of the  came unto me saying on of man set thy face against idon and prophesy against it and say hus saith the ord  ehold  am against thee  idon and  will be glorified in the midst of thee and they shall know that  am the  when  shall have executed judgments in her and shall be sanctified in her
or  will send into her pestilence and blood into her streets and the wounded shall be judged in the midst of her by the sword upon her on every side and they shall know that  am the 
nd there shall be no more a pricking brier unto the house of srael nor any grieving thorn of all that are round about them that despised them and they shall know that  am the ord 
hus saith the ord  hen  shall have gathered the house of srael from the people among whom they are scattered and shall be sanctified in them in the sight of the heathen then shall they dwell in their land that  have given to my servant acob
nd they shall dwell safely therein and shall build houses and plant vineyards yea they shall dwell with confidence when  have executed judgments upon all those that despise them round about them and they shall know that  am the  their od
n the tenth year in the tenth month in the twelfth day of the month the word of the  came unto me saying on of man set thy face against haraoh king of gypt and prophesy against him and against all gypt speak and say hus saith the ord  ehold  am against thee haraoh king of gypt the great dragon that lieth in the midst of his rivers which hath said y river is mine own and  have made it for myself
ut  will put hooks in thy jaws and  will cause the fish of thy rivers to stick unto thy scales and  will bring thee up out of the midst of thy rivers and all the fish of thy rivers shall stick unto thy scales
nd  will leave thee thrown into the wilderness thee and all the fish of thy rivers thou shalt fall upon the open fields thou shalt not be brought together nor gathered  have given thee for meat to the beasts of the field and to the fowls of the heaven
nd all the inhabitants of gypt shall know that  am the  because they have been a staff of reed to the house of srael
hen they took hold of thee by thy hand thou didst break and rend all their shoulder and when they leaned upon thee thou brakest and madest all their loins to be at a stand
herefore thus saith the ord  ehold  will bring a sword upon thee and cut off man and beast out of thee
nd the land of gypt shall be desolate and waste and they shall know that  am the  because he hath said he river is mine and  have made it
ehold therefore  am against thee and against thy rivers and  will make the land of gypt utterly waste and desolate from the tower of yene even unto the border of thiopia
o foot of man shall pass through it nor foot of beast shall pass through it neither shall it be inhabited forty years
nd  will make the land of gypt desolate in the midst of the countries that are desolate and her cities among the cities that are laid waste shall be desolate forty years and  will scatter the gyptians among the nations and will disperse them through the countries
et thus saith the ord  t the end of forty years will  gather the gyptians from the people whither they were scattered and  will bring again the captivity of gypt and will cause them to return into the land of athros into the land of their habitation and they shall be there a base kingdom
t shall be the basest of the kingdoms neither shall it exalt itself any more above the nations for  will diminish them that they shall no more rule over the nations
nd it shall be no more the confidence of the house of srael which bringeth their iniquity to remembrance when they shall look after them but they shall know that  am the ord 
nd it came to pass in the seven and twentieth year in the first month in the first day of the month the word of the  came unto me saying on of man ebuchadrezzar king of abylon caused his army to serve a great service against yrus every head was made bald and every shoulder was peeled yet had he no wages nor his army for yrus for the service that he had served against it therefore thus saith the ord  ehold  will give the land of gypt unto ebuchadrezzar king of abylon and he shall take her multitude and take her spoil and take her prey and it shall be the wages for his army
 have given him the land of gypt for his labour wherewith he served against it because they wrought for me saith the ord 
n that day will  cause the horn of the house of srael to bud forth and  will give thee the opening of the mouth in the midst of them and they shall know that  am the 
he word of the  came again unto me saying on of man prophesy and say hus saith the ord  owl ye oe worth the day
or the day is near even the day of the  is near a cloudy day it shall be the time of the heathen
nd the sword shall come upon gypt and great pain shall be in thiopia when the slain shall fall in gypt and they shall take away her multitude and her foundations shall be broken down
thiopia and ibya and ydia and all the mingled people and hub and the men of the land that is in league shall fall with them by the sword
hus saith the  hey also that uphold gypt shall fall and the pride of her power shall come down from the tower of yene shall they fall in it by the sword saith the ord 
nd they shall be desolate in the midst of the countries that are desolate and her cities shall be in the midst of the cities that are wasted
nd they shall know that  am the  when  have set a fire in gypt and when all her helpers shall be destroyed
n that day shall messengers go forth from me in ships to make the careless thiopians afraid and great pain shall come upon them as in the day of gypt for lo it cometh
hus saith the ord   will also make the multitude of gypt to cease by the hand of ebuchadrezzar king of abylon
e and his people with him the terrible of the nations shall be brought to destroy the land and they shall draw their swords against gypt and fill the land with the slain
nd  will make the rivers dry and sell the land into the hand of the wicked and  will make the land waste and all that is therein by the hand of strangers  the  have spoken it
hus saith the ord   will also destroy the idols and  will cause their images to cease out of oph and there shall be no more a prince of the land of gypt and  will put a fear in the land of gypt
nd  will make athros desolate and will set fire in oan and will execute judgments in o
nd  will pour my fury upon in the strength of gypt and  will cut off the multitude of o
nd  will set fire in gypt in shall have great pain and o shall be rent asunder and oph shall have distresses daily
he young men of ven and of ibeseth shall fall by the sword and these cities shall go into captivity
t ehaphnehes also the day shall be darkened when  shall break there the yokes of gypt and the pomp of her strength shall cease in her as for her a cloud shall cover her and her daughters shall go into captivity
hus will  execute judgments in gypt and they shall know that  am the 
nd it came to pass in the eleventh year in the first month in the seventh day of the month that the word of the  came unto me saying on of man  have broken the arm of haraoh king of gypt and lo it shall not be bound up to be healed to put a roller to bind it to make it strong to hold the sword
herefore thus saith the ord  ehold  am against haraoh king of gypt and will break his arms the strong and that which was broken and  will cause the sword to fall out of his hand
nd  will scatter the gyptians among the nations and will disperse them through the countries
nd  will strengthen the arms of the king of abylon and put my sword in his hand but  will break haraohs arms and he shall groan before him with the groanings of a deadly wounded man
ut  will strengthen the arms of the king of abylon and the arms of haraoh shall fall down and they shall know that  am the  when  shall put my sword into the hand of the king of abylon and he shall stretch it out upon the land of gypt
nd  will scatter the gyptians among the nations and disperse them among the countries and they shall know that  am the 
nd it came to pass in the eleventh year in the third month in the first day of the month that the word of the  came unto me saying on of man speak unto haraoh king of gypt and to his multitude hom art thou like in thy greatness
ehold the ssyrian was a cedar in ebanon with fair branches and with a shadowing shroud and of an high stature and his top was among the thick boughs
he waters made him great the deep set him up on high with her rivers running round about his plants and sent out her little rivers unto all the trees of the field
herefore his height was exalted above all the trees of the field and his boughs were multiplied and his branches became long because of the multitude of waters when he shot forth
ll the fowls of heaven made their nests in his boughs and under his branches did all the beasts of the field bring forth their young and under his shadow dwelt all great nations
hus was he fair in his greatness in the length of his branches for his root was by great waters
he cedars in the garden of od could not hide him the fir trees were not like his boughs and the chesnut trees were not like his branches nor any tree in the garden of od was like unto him in his beauty
 have made him fair by the multitude of his branches so that all the trees of den that were in the garden of od envied him
herefore thus saith the ord  ecause thou hast lifted up thyself in height and he hath shot up his top among the thick boughs and his heart is lifted up in his height  have therefore delivered him into the hand of the mighty one of the heathen he shall surely deal with him  have driven him out for his wickedness
nd strangers the terrible of the nations have cut him off and have left him upon the mountains and in all the valleys his branches are fallen and his boughs are broken by all the rivers of the land and all the people of the earth are gone down from his shadow and have left him
pon his ruin shall all the fowls of the heaven remain and all the beasts of the field shall be upon his branches to the end that none of all the trees by the waters exalt themselves for their height neither shoot up their top among the thick boughs neither their trees stand up in their height all that drink water for they are all delivered unto death to the nether parts of the earth in the midst of the children of men with them that go down to the pit
hus saith the ord  n the day when he went down to the grave  caused a mourning  covered the deep for him and  restrained the floods thereof and the great waters were stayed and  caused ebanon to mourn for him and all the trees of the field fainted for him
 made the nations to shake at the sound of his fall when  cast him down to hell with them that descend into the pit and all the trees of den the choice and best of ebanon all that drink water shall be comforted in the nether parts of the earth
hey also went down into hell with him unto them that be slain with the sword and they that were his arm that dwelt under his shadow in the midst of the heathen
o whom art thou thus like in glory and in greatness among the trees of den yet shalt thou be brought down with the trees of den unto the nether parts of the earth thou shalt lie in the midst of the uncircumcised with them that be slain by the sword
his is haraoh and all his multitude saith the ord 
nd it came to pass in the twelfth year in the twelfth month in the first day of the month that the word of the  came unto me saying on of man take up a lamentation for haraoh king of gypt and say unto him hou art like a young lion of the nations and thou art as a whale in the seas and thou camest forth with thy rivers and troubledst the waters with thy feet and fouledst their rivers
hus saith the ord   will therefore spread out my net over thee with a company of many people and they shall bring thee up in my net
hen will  leave thee upon the land  will cast thee forth upon the open field and will cause all the fowls of the heaven to remain upon thee and  will fill the beasts of the whole earth with thee
nd  will lay thy flesh upon the mountains and fill the valleys with thy height
 will also water with thy blood the land wherein thou swimmest even to the mountains and the rivers shall be full of thee
nd when  shall put thee out  will cover the heaven and make the stars thereof dark  will cover the sun with a cloud and the moon shall not give her light
ll the bright lights of heaven will  make dark over thee and set darkness upon thy land saith the ord 
 will also vex the hearts of many people when  shall bring thy destruction among the nations into the countries which thou hast not known
ea  will make many people amazed at thee and their kings shall be horribly afraid for thee when  shall brandish my sword before them and they shall tremble at every moment every man for his own life in the day of thy fall
or thus saith the ord  he sword of the king of abylon shall come upon thee
y the swords of the mighty will  cause thy multitude to fall the terrible of the nations all of them and they shall spoil the pomp of gypt and all the multitude thereof shall be destroyed
 will destroy also all the beasts thereof from beside the great waters neither shall the foot of man trouble them any more nor the hoofs of beasts trouble them
hen will  make their waters deep and cause their rivers to run like oil saith the ord 
hen  shall make the land of gypt desolate and the country shall be destitute of that whereof it was full when  shall smite all them that dwell therein then shall they know that  am the 
his is the lamentation wherewith they shall lament her the daughters of the nations shall lament her they shall lament for her even for gypt and for all her multitude saith the ord 
t came to pass also in the twelfth year in the fifteenth day of the month that the word of the  came unto me saying on of man wail for the multitude of gypt and cast them down even her and the daughters of the famous nations unto the nether parts of the earth with them that go down into the pit
hom dost thou pass in beauty go down and be thou laid with the uncircumcised
hey shall fall in the midst of them that are slain by the sword she is delivered to the sword draw her and all her multitudes
he strong among the mighty shall speak to him out of the midst of hell with them that help him they are gone down they lie uncircumcised slain by the sword
sshur is there and all her company his graves are about him all of them slain fallen by the sword whose graves are set in the sides of the pit and her company is round about her grave all of them slain fallen by the sword which caused terror in the land of the living
here is lam and all her multitude round about her grave all of them slain fallen by the sword which are gone down uncircumcised into the nether parts of the earth which caused their terror in the land of the living yet have they borne their shame with them that go down to the pit
hey have set her a bed in the midst of the slain with all her multitude her graves are round about him all of them uncircumcised slain by the sword though their terror was caused in the land of the living yet have they borne their shame with them that go down to the pit he is put in the midst of them that be slain
here is eshech ubal and all her multitude her graves are round about him all of them uncircumcised slain by the sword though they caused their terror in the land of the living
nd they shall not lie with the mighty that are fallen of the uncircumcised which are gone down to hell with their weapons of war and they have laid their swords under their heads but their iniquities shall be upon their bones though they were the terror of the mighty in the land of the living
ea thou shalt be broken in the midst of the uncircumcised and shalt lie with them that are slain with the sword
here is dom her kings and all her princes which with their might are laid by them that were slain by the sword they shall lie with the uncircumcised and with them that go down to the pit
here be the princes of the north all of them and all the idonians which are gone down with the slain with their terror they are ashamed of their might and they lie uncircumcised with them that be slain by the sword and bear their shame with them that go down to the pit
haraoh shall see them and shall be comforted over all his multitude even haraoh and all his army slain by the sword saith the ord 
or  have caused my terror in the land of the living and he shall be laid in the midst of the uncircumcised with them that are slain with the sword even haraoh and all his multitude saith the ord 
gain the word of the  came unto me saying on of man speak to the children of thy people and say unto them hen  bring the sword upon a land if the people of the land take a man of their coasts and set him for their watchman if when he seeth the sword come upon the land he blow the trumpet and warn the people then whosoever heareth the sound of the trumpet and taketh not warning if the sword come and take him away his blood shall be upon his own head
e heard the sound of the trumpet and took not warning his blood shall be upon him
ut he that taketh warning shall deliver his soul
ut if the watchman see the sword come and blow not the trumpet and the people be not warned if the sword come and take any person from among them he is taken away in his iniquity but his blood will  require at the watchmans hand
o thou  son of man  have set thee a watchman unto the house of srael therefore thou shalt hear the word at my mouth and warn them from me
hen  say unto the wicked  wicked man thou shalt surely die if thou dost not speak to warn the wicked from his way that wicked man shall die in his iniquity but his blood will  require at thine hand
evertheless if thou warn the wicked of his way to turn from it if he do not turn from his way he shall die in his iniquity but thou hast delivered thy soul
herefore  thou son of man speak unto the house of srael hus ye speak saying f our transgressions and our sins be upon us and we pine away in them how should we then live
ay unto them s  live saith the ord   have no pleasure in the death of the wicked but that the wicked turn from his way and live turn ye turn ye from your evil ways for why will ye die  house of srael
herefore thou son of man say unto the children of thy people he righteousness of the righteous shall not deliver him in the day of his transgression as for the wickedness of the wicked he shall not fall thereby in the day that he turneth from his wickedness neither shall the righteous be able to live for his righteousness in the day that he sinneth
hen  shall say to the righteous that he shall surely live if he trust to his own righteousness and commit iniquity all his righteousnesses shall not be remembered but for his iniquity that he hath committed he shall die for it
gain when  say unto the wicked hou shalt surely die if he turn from his sin and do that which is lawful and right if the wicked restore the pledge give again that he had robbed walk in the statutes of life without committing iniquity he shall surely live he shall not die
one of his sins that he hath committed shall be mentioned unto him he hath done that which is lawful and right he shall surely live
et the children of thy people say he way of the ord is not equal but as for them their way is not equal
hen the righteous turneth from his righteousness and committeth iniquity he shall even die thereby
ut if the wicked turn from his wickedness and do that which is lawful and right he shall live thereby
et ye say he way of the ord is not equal
 ye house of srael  will judge you every one after his ways
nd it came to pass in the twelfth year of our captivity in the tenth month in the fifth day of the month that one that had escaped out of erusalem came unto me saying he city is smitten
ow the hand of the  was upon me in the evening afore he that was escaped came and had opened my mouth until he came to me in the morning and my mouth was opened and  was no more dumb
hen the word of the  came unto me saying on of man they that inhabit those wastes of the land of srael speak saying braham was one and he inherited the land but we are many the land is given us for inheritance
herefore say unto them hus saith the ord  e eat with the blood and lift up your eyes toward your idols and shed blood and shall ye possess the land
e stand upon your sword ye work abomination and ye defile every one his neighbours wife and shall ye possess the land
ay thou thus unto them hus saith the ord  s  live surely they that are in the wastes shall fall by the sword and him that is in the open field will  give to the beasts to be devoured and they that be in the forts and in the caves shall die of the pestilence
or  will lay the land most desolate and the pomp of her strength shall cease and the mountains of srael shall be desolate that none shall pass through
hen shall they know that  am the  when  have laid the land most desolate because of all their abominations which they have committed
lso thou son of man the children of thy people still are talking against thee by the walls and in the doors of the houses and speak one to another every one to his brother saying ome  pray you and hear what is the word that cometh forth from the 
nd they come unto thee as the people cometh and they sit before thee as my people and they hear thy words but they will not do them for with their mouth they shew much love but their heart goeth after their covetousness
nd lo thou art unto them as a very lovely song of one that hath a pleasant voice and can play well on an instrument for they hear thy words but they do them not
nd when this cometh to pass lo it will come  then shall they know that a prophet hath been among them
nd the word of the  came unto me saying on of man prophesy against the shepherds of srael prophesy and say unto them hus saith the ord  unto the shepherds oe be to the shepherds of srael that do feed themselves should not the shepherds feed the flocks
e eat the fat and ye clothe you with the wool ye kill them that are fed but ye feed not the flock
he diseased have ye not strengthened neither have ye healed that which was sick neither have ye bound up that which was broken neither have ye brought again that which was driven away neither have ye sought that which was lost but with force and with cruelty have ye ruled them
nd they were scattered because there is no shepherd and they became meat to all the beasts of the field when they were scattered
y sheep wandered through all the mountains and upon every high hill yea my flock was scattered upon all the face of the earth and none did search or seek after them
herefore ye shepherds hear the word of the  s  live saith the ord  surely because my flock became a prey and my flock became meat to every beast of the field because there was no shepherd neither did my shepherds search for my flock but the shepherds fed themselves and fed not my flock therefore  ye shepherds hear the word of the  thus saith the ord  ehold  am against the shepherds and  will require my flock at their hand and cause them to cease from feeding the flock neither shall the shepherds feed themselves any more for  will deliver my flock from their mouth that they may not be meat for them
or thus saith the ord  ehold  even  will both search my sheep and seek them out
s a shepherd seeketh out his flock in the day that he is among his sheep that are scattered so will  seek out my sheep and will deliver them out of all places where they have been scattered in the cloudy and dark day
nd  will bring them out from the people and gather them from the countries and will bring them to their own land and feed them upon the mountains of srael by the rivers and in all the inhabited places of the country
 will feed them in a good pasture and upon the high mountains of srael shall their fold be there shall they lie in a good fold and in a fat pasture shall they feed upon the mountains of srael
 will feed my flock and  will cause them to lie down saith the ord 
 will seek that which was lost and bring again that which was driven away and will bind up that which was broken and will strengthen that which was sick but  will destroy the fat and the strong  will feed them with judgment
nd as for you  my flock thus saith the ord  ehold  judge between cattle and cattle between the rams and the he goats
eemeth it a small thing unto you to have eaten up the good pasture but ye must tread down with your feet the residue of your pastures and to have drunk of the deep waters but ye must foul the residue with your feet
nd as for my flock they eat that which ye have trodden with your feet and they drink that which ye have fouled with your feet
herefore thus saith the ord  unto them ehold  even  will judge between the fat cattle and between the lean cattle
ecause ye have thrust with side and with shoulder and pushed all the diseased with your horns till ye have scattered them abroad therefore will  save my flock and they shall no more be a prey and  will judge between cattle and cattle
nd  will set up one shepherd over them and he shall feed them even my servant avid he shall feed them and he shall be their shepherd
nd  the  will be their od and my servant avid a prince among them  the  have spoken it
nd  will make with them a covenant of peace and will cause the evil beasts to cease out of the land and they shall dwell safely in the wilderness and sleep in the woods
nd  will make them and the places round about my hill a blessing and  will cause the shower to come down in his season there shall be showers of blessing
nd the tree of the field shall yield her fruit and the earth shall yield her increase and they shall be safe in their land and shall know that  am the  when  have broken the bands of their yoke and delivered them out of the hand of those that served themselves of them
nd they shall no more be a prey to the heathen neither shall the beast of the land devour them but they shall dwell safely and none shall make them afraid
nd  will raise up for them a plant of renown and they shall be no more consumed with hunger in the land neither bear the shame of the heathen any more
hus shall they know that  the  their od am with them and that they even the house of srael are my people saith the ord 
nd ye my flock the flock of my pasture are men and  am your od saith the ord 
oreover the word of the  came unto me saying on of man set thy face against mount eir and prophesy against it and say unto it hus saith the ord  ehold  mount eir  am against thee and  will stretch out mine hand against thee and  will make thee most desolate
 will lay thy cities waste and thou shalt be desolate and thou shalt know that  am the 
ecause thou hast had a perpetual hatred and hast shed the blood of the children of srael by the force of the sword in the time of their calamity in the time that their iniquity had an end therefore as  live saith the ord   will prepare thee unto blood and blood shall pursue thee sith thou hast not hated blood even blood shall pursue thee
hus will  make mount eir most desolate and cut off from it him that passeth out and him that returneth
nd  will fill his mountains with his slain men in thy hills and in thy valleys and in all thy rivers shall they fall that are slain with the sword
 will make thee perpetual desolations and thy cities shall not return and ye shall know that  am the 
ecause thou hast said hese two nations and these two countries shall be mine and we will possess it whereas the  was there therefore as  live saith the ord   will even do according to thine anger and according to thine envy which thou hast used out of thy hatred against them and  will make myself known among them when  have judged thee
nd thou shalt know that  am the  and that  have heard all thy blasphemies which thou hast spoken against the mountains of srael saying hey are laid desolate they are given us to consume
hus with your mouth ye have boasted against me and have multiplied your words against me  have heard them
hus saith the ord  hen the whole earth rejoiceth  will make thee desolate
s thou didst rejoice at the inheritance of the house of srael because it was desolate so will  do unto thee thou shalt be desolate  mount eir and all dumea even all of it and they shall know that  am the 
lso thou son of man prophesy unto the mountains of srael and say e mountains of srael hear the word of the  hus saith the ord  ecause the enemy hath said against you ha even the ancient high places are ours in possession therefore prophesy and say hus saith the ord  ecause they have made you desolate and swallowed you up on every side that ye might be a possession unto the residue of the heathen and ye are taken up in the lips of talkers and are an infamy of the people therefore ye mountains of srael hear the word of the ord  hus saith the ord  to the mountains and to the hills to the rivers and to the valleys to the desolate wastes and to the cities that are forsaken which became a prey and derision to the residue of the heathen that are round about therefore thus saith the ord  urely in the fire of my jealousy have  spoken against the residue of the heathen and against all dumea which have appointed my land into their possession with the joy of all their heart with despiteful minds to cast it out for a prey
rophesy therefore concerning the land of srael and say unto the mountains and to the hills to the rivers and to the valleys hus saith the ord  ehold  have spoken in my jealousy and in my fury because ye have borne the shame of the heathen therefore thus saith the ord   have lifted up mine hand urely the heathen that are about you they shall bear their shame
ut ye  mountains of srael ye shall shoot forth your branches and yield your fruit to my people of srael for they are at hand to come
or behold  am for you and  will turn unto you and ye shall be tilled and sown and  will multiply men upon you all the house of srael even all of it and the cities shall be inhabited and the wastes shall be builded and  will multiply upon you man and beast and they shall increase and bring fruit and  will settle you after your old estates and will do better unto you than at your beginnings and ye shall know that  am the 
ea  will cause men to walk upon you even my people srael and they shall possess thee and thou shalt be their inheritance and thou shalt no more henceforth bereave them of men
hus saith the ord  ecause they say unto you hou land devourest up men and hast bereaved thy nations therefore thou shalt devour men no more neither bereave thy nations any more saith the ord 
either will  cause men to hear in thee the shame of the heathen any more neither shalt thou bear the reproach of the people any more neither shalt thou cause thy nations to fall any more saith the ord 
oreover the word of the  came unto me saying on of man when the house of srael dwelt in their own land they defiled it by their own way and by their doings their way was before me as the uncleanness of a removed woman
herefore  poured my fury upon them for the blood that they had shed upon the land and for their idols wherewith they had polluted it and  scattered them among the heathen and they were dispersed through the countries according to their way and according to their doings  judged them
nd when they entered unto the heathen whither they went they profaned my holy name when they said to them hese are the people of the  and are gone forth out of his land
ut  had pity for mine holy name which the house of srael had profaned among the heathen whither they went
herefore say unto the house of srael hus saith the ord   do not this for your sakes  house of srael but for mine holy names sake which ye have profaned among the heathen whither ye went
nd  will sanctify my great name which was profaned among the heathen which ye have profaned in the midst of them and the heathen shall know that  am the  saith the ord  when  shall be sanctified in you before their eyes
or  will take you from among the heathen and gather you out of all countries and will bring you into your own land
hen will  sprinkle clean water upon you and ye shall be clean from all your filthiness and from all your idols will  cleanse you
 new heart also will  give you and a new spirit will  put within you and  will take away the stony heart out of your flesh and  will give you an heart of flesh
nd  will put my spirit within you and cause you to walk in my statutes and ye shall keep my judgments and do them
nd ye shall dwell in the land that  gave to your fathers and ye shall be my people and  will be your od
 will also save you from all your uncleannesses and  will call for the corn and will increase it and lay no famine upon you
nd  will multiply the fruit of the tree and the increase of the field that ye shall receive no more reproach of famine among the heathen
hen shall ye remember your own evil ways and your doings that were not good and shall lothe yourselves in your own sight for your iniquities and for your abominations
ot for your sakes do  this saith the ord  be it known unto you be ashamed and confounded for your own ways  house of srael
hus saith the ord  n the day that  shall have cleansed you from all your iniquities  will also cause you to dwell in the cities and the wastes shall be builded
nd the desolate land shall be tilled whereas it lay desolate in the sight of all that passed by
nd they shall say his land that was desolate is become like the garden of den and the waste and desolate and ruined cities are become fenced and are inhabited
hen the heathen that are left round about you shall know that  the  build the ruined places and plant that that was desolate  the  have spoken it and  will do it
hus saith the ord   will yet for this be enquired of by the house of srael to do it for them  will increase them with men like a flock
s the holy flock as the flock of erusalem in her solemn feasts so shall the waste cities be filled with flocks of men and they shall know that  am the 
he hand of the  was upon me and carried me out in the spirit of the  and set me down in the midst of the valley which was full of bones and caused me to pass by them round about and behold there were very many in the open valley and lo they were very dry
nd he said unto me on of man can these bones live
nd  answered  ord  thou knowest
gain he said unto me rophesy upon these bones and say unto them  ye dry bones hear the word of the 
hus saith the ord  unto these bones ehold  will cause breath to enter into you and ye shall live and  will lay sinews upon you and will bring up flesh upon you and cover you with skin and put breath in you and ye shall live and ye shall know that  am the 
o  prophesied as  was commanded and as  prophesied there was a noise and behold a shaking and the bones came together bone to his bone
nd when  beheld lo the sinews and the flesh came up upon them and the skin covered them above but there was no breath in them
hen said he unto me rophesy unto the wind prophesy son of man and say to the wind hus saith the ord  ome from the four winds  breath and breathe upon these slain that they may live
o  prophesied as he commanded me and the breath came into them and they lived and stood up upon their feet an exceeding great army
hen he said unto me on of man these bones are the whole house of srael behold they say ur bones are dried and our hope is lost we are cut off for our parts
herefore prophesy and say unto them hus saith the ord  ehold  my people  will open your graves and cause you to come up out of your graves and bring you into the land of srael
nd ye shall know that  am the  when  have opened your graves  my people and brought you up out of your graves and shall put my spirit in you and ye shall live and  shall place you in your own land then shall ye know that  the  have spoken it and performed it saith the 
he word of the  came again unto me saying oreover thou son of man take thee one stick and write upon it or udah and for the children of srael his companions then take another stick and write upon it or oseph the stick of phraim and for all the house of srael his companions and join them one to another into one stick and they shall become one in thine hand
nd when the children of thy people shall speak unto thee saying ilt thou not shew us what thou meanest by these
ay unto them hus saith the ord  ehold  will take the stick of oseph which is in the hand of phraim and the tribes of srael his fellows and will put them with him even with the stick of udah and make them one stick and they shall be one in mine hand
nd the sticks whereon thou writest shall be in thine hand before their eyes
nd say unto them hus saith the ord  ehold  will take the children of srael from among the heathen whither they be gone and will gather them on every side and bring them into their own land and  will make them one nation in the land upon the mountains of srael and one king shall be king to them all and they shall be no more two nations neither shall they be divided into two kingdoms any more at all neither shall they defile themselves any more with their idols nor with their detestable things nor with any of their transgressions but  will save them out of all their dwellingplaces wherein they have sinned and will cleanse them so shall they be my people and  will be their od
nd avid my servant shall be king over them and they all shall have one shepherd they shall also walk in my judgments and observe my statutes and do them
nd they shall dwell in the land that  have given unto acob my servant wherein your fathers have dwelt and they shall dwell therein even they and their children and their childrens children for ever and my servant avid shall be their prince for ever
