n the third year of the reign of ehoiakim king of udah came ebuchadnezzar king of abylon unto erusalem and besieged it
nd the ord gave ehoiakim king of udah into his hand with part of the vessels of the house of od which he carried into the land of hinar to the house of his god and he brought the vessels into the treasure house of his god
nd the king spake unto shpenaz the master of his eunuchs that he should bring certain of the children of srael and of the kings seed and of the princes children in whom was no blemish but well favoured and skilful in all wisdom and cunning in knowledge and understanding science and such as had ability in them to stand in the kings palace and whom they might teach the learning and the tongue of the haldeans
nd the king appointed them a daily provision of the kings meat and of the wine which he drank so nourishing them three years that at the end thereof they might stand before the king
ow among these were of the children of udah aniel ananiah ishael and zariah unto whom the prince of the eunuchs gave names for he gave unto aniel the name of elteshazzar and to ananiah of hadrach and to ishael of eshach and to zariah of bednego
ut aniel purposed in his heart that he would not defile himself with the portion of the kings meat nor with the wine which he drank therefore he requested of the prince of the eunuchs that he might not defile himself
ow od had brought aniel into favour and tender love with the prince of the eunuchs
nd the prince of the eunuchs said unto aniel  fear my lord the king who hath appointed your meat and your drink for why should he see your faces worse liking than the children which are of your sort then shall ye make me endanger my head to the king
hen said aniel to elzar whom the prince of the eunuchs had set over aniel ananiah ishael and zariah rove thy servants  beseech thee ten days and let them give us pulse to eat and water to drink
hen let our countenances be looked upon before thee and the countenance of the children that eat of the portion of the kings meat and as thou seest deal with thy servants
o he consented to them in this matter and proved them ten days
nd at the end of ten days their countenances appeared fairer and fatter in flesh than all the children which did eat the portion of the kings meat
hus elzar took away the portion of their meat and the wine that they should drink and gave them pulse
s for these four children od gave them knowledge and skill in all learning and wisdom and aniel had understanding in all visions and dreams
ow at the end of the days that the king had said he should bring them in then the prince of the eunuchs brought them in before ebuchadnezzar
nd the king communed with them and among them all was found none like aniel ananiah ishael and zariah therefore stood they before the king
nd in all matters of wisdom and understanding that the king enquired of them he found them ten times better than all the magicians and astrologers that were in all his realm
nd aniel continued even unto the first year of king yrus
nd in the second year of the reign of ebuchadnezzar ebuchadnezzar dreamed dreams wherewith his spirit was troubled and his sleep brake from him
hen the king commanded to call the magicians and the astrologers and the sorcerers and the haldeans for to shew the king his dreams
o they came and stood before the king
nd the king said unto them  have dreamed a dream and my spirit was troubled to know the dream
hen spake the haldeans to the king in yriack  king live for ever tell thy servants the dream and we will shew the interpretation
he king answered and said to the haldeans he thing is gone from me if ye will not make known unto me the dream with the interpretation thereof ye shall be cut in pieces and your houses shall be made a dunghill
ut if ye shew the dream and the interpretation thereof ye shall receive of me gifts and rewards and great honour therefore shew me the dream and the interpretation thereof
hey answered again and said et the king tell his servants the dream and we will shew the interpretation of it
he king answered and said  know of certainty that ye would gain the time because ye see the thing is gone from me
ut if ye will not make known unto me the dream there is but one decree for you for ye have prepared lying and corrupt words to speak before me till the time be changed therefore tell me the dream and  shall know that ye can shew me the interpretation thereof
he haldeans answered before the king and said here is not a man upon the earth that can shew the kings matter therefore there is no king lord nor ruler that asked such things at any magician or astrologer or haldean
nd it is a rare thing that the king requireth and there is none other that can shew it before the king except the gods whose dwelling is not with flesh
or this cause the king was angry and very furious and commanded to destroy all the wise men of abylon
nd the decree went forth that the wise men should be slain and they sought aniel and his fellows to be slain
hen aniel answered with counsel and wisdom to rioch the captain of the kings guard which was gone forth to slay the wise men of abylon he answered and said to rioch the kings captain hy is the decree so hasty from the king
hen rioch made the thing known to aniel
hen aniel went in and desired of the king that he would give him time and that he would shew the king the interpretation
hen aniel went to his house and made the thing known to ananiah ishael and zariah his companions that they would desire mercies of the od of heaven concerning this secret that aniel and his fellows should not perish with the rest of the wise men of abylon
hen was the secret revealed unto aniel in a night vision
hen aniel blessed the od of heaven
aniel answered and said lessed be the name of od for ever and ever for wisdom and might are his and he changeth the times and the seasons he removeth kings and setteth up kings he giveth wisdom unto the wise and knowledge to them that know understanding he revealeth the deep and secret things he knoweth what is in the darkness and the light dwelleth with him
 thank thee and praise thee  thou od of my fathers who hast given me wisdom and might and hast made known unto me now what we desired of thee for thou hast now made known unto us the kings matter
herefore aniel went in unto rioch whom the king had ordained to destroy the wise men of abylon he went and said thus unto him estroy not the wise men of abylon bring me in before the king and  will shew unto the king the interpretation
hen rioch brought in aniel before the king in haste and said thus unto him  have found a man of the captives of udah that will make known unto the king the interpretation
he king answered and said to aniel whose name was elteshazzar rt thou able to make known unto me the dream which  have seen and the interpretation thereof
aniel answered in the presence of the king and said he secret which the king hath demanded cannot the wise men the astrologers the magicians the soothsayers shew unto the king but there is a od in heaven that revealeth secrets and maketh known to the king ebuchadnezzar what shall be in the latter days
hy dream and the visions of thy head upon thy bed are these s for thee  king thy thoughts came into thy mind upon thy bed what should come to pass hereafter and he that revealeth secrets maketh known to thee what shall come to pass
ut as for me this secret is not revealed to me for any wisdom that  have more than any living but for their sakes that shall make known the interpretation to the king and that thou mightest know the thoughts of thy heart
hou  king sawest and behold a great image
his great image whose brightness was excellent stood before thee and the form thereof was terrible
his images head was of fine gold his breast and his arms of silver his belly and his thighs of brass his legs of iron his feet part of iron and part of clay
hou sawest till that a stone was cut out without hands which smote the image upon his feet that were of iron and clay and brake them to pieces
hen was the iron the clay the brass the silver and the gold broken to pieces together and became like the chaff of the summer threshingfloors and the wind carried them away that no place was found for them and the stone that smote the image became a great mountain and filled the whole earth
his is the dream and we will tell the interpretation thereof before the king
hou  king art a king of kings for the od of heaven hath given thee a kingdom power and strength and glory
nd wheresoever the children of men dwell the beasts of the field and the fowls of the heaven hath he given into thine hand and hath made thee ruler over them all
hou art this head of gold
nd after thee shall arise another kingdom inferior to thee and another third kingdom of brass which shall bear rule over all the earth
nd the fourth kingdom shall be strong as iron forasmuch as iron breaketh in pieces and subdueth all things and as iron that breaketh all these shall it break in pieces and bruise
nd whereas thou sawest the feet and toes part of potters clay and part of iron the kingdom shall be divided but there shall be in it of the strength of the iron forasmuch as thou sawest the iron mixed with miry clay
nd as the toes of the feet were part of iron and part of clay so the kingdom shall be partly strong and partly broken
nd whereas thou sawest iron mixed with miry clay they shall mingle themselves with the seed of men but they shall not cleave one to another even as iron is not mixed with clay
nd in the days of these kings shall the od of heaven set up a kingdom which shall never be destroyed and the kingdom shall not be left to other people but it shall break in pieces and consume all these kingdoms and it shall stand for ever
orasmuch as thou sawest that the stone was cut out of the mountain without hands and that it brake in pieces the iron the brass the clay the silver and the gold the great od hath made known to the king what shall come to pass hereafter and the dream is certain and the interpretation thereof sure
hen the king ebuchadnezzar fell upon his face and worshipped aniel and commanded that they should offer an oblation and sweet odours unto him
he king answered unto aniel and said f a truth it is that your od is a od of gods and a ord of kings and a revealer of secrets seeing thou couldest reveal this secret
hen the king made aniel a great man and gave him many great gifts and made him ruler over the whole province of abylon and chief of the governors over all the wise men of abylon
hen aniel requested of the king and he set hadrach eshach and bednego over the affairs of the province of abylon but aniel sat in the gate of the king
ebuchadnezzar the king made an image of gold whose height was threescore cubits and the breadth thereof six cubits he set it up in the plain of ura in the province of abylon
hen ebuchadnezzar the king sent to gather together the princes the governors and the captains the judges the treasurers the counsellors the sheriffs and all the rulers of the provinces to come to the dedication of the image which ebuchadnezzar the king had set up
hen the princes the governors and captains the judges the treasurers the counsellors the sheriffs and all the rulers of the provinces were gathered together unto the dedication of the image that ebuchadnezzar the king had set up and they stood before the image that ebuchadnezzar had set up
hen an herald cried aloud o you it is commanded  people nations and languages that at what time ye hear the sound of the cornet flute harp sackbut psaltery dulcimer and all kinds of musick ye fall down and worship the golden image that ebuchadnezzar the king hath set up and whoso falleth not down and worshippeth shall the same hour be cast into the midst of a burning fiery furnace
herefore at that time when all the people heard the sound of the cornet flute harp sackbut psaltery and all kinds of musick all the people the nations and the languages fell down and worshipped the golden image that ebuchadnezzar the king had set up
herefore at that time certain haldeans came near and accused the ews
hey spake and said to the king ebuchadnezzar  king live for ever
hou  king hast made a decree that every man that shall hear the sound of the cornet flute harp sackbut psaltery and dulcimer and all kinds of musick shall fall down and worship the golden image and whoso falleth not down and worshippeth that he should be cast into the midst of a burning fiery furnace
here are certain ews whom thou hast set over the affairs of the province of abylon hadrach eshach and bednego these men  king have not regarded thee they serve not thy gods nor worship the golden image which thou hast set up
hen ebuchadnezzar in his rage and fury commanded to bring hadrach eshach and bednego
hen they brought these men before the king
ebuchadnezzar spake and said unto them s it true  hadrach eshach and bednego do not ye serve my gods nor worship the golden image which  have set up
ow if ye be ready that at what time ye hear the sound of the cornet flute harp sackbut psaltery and dulcimer and all kinds of musick ye fall down and worship the image which  have made well but if ye worship not ye shall be cast the same hour into the midst of a burning fiery furnace and who is that od that shall deliver you out of my hands
hadrach eshach and bednego answered and said to the king  ebuchadnezzar we are not careful to answer thee in this matter
f it be so our od whom we serve is able to deliver us from the burning fiery furnace and he will deliver us out of thine hand  king
ut if not be it known unto thee  king that we will not serve thy gods nor worship the golden image which thou hast set up
hen was ebuchadnezzar full of fury and the form of his visage was changed against hadrach eshach and bednego therefore he spake and commanded that they should heat the furnace one seven times more than it was wont to be heated
nd he commanded the most mighty men that were in his army to bind hadrach eshach and bednego and to cast them into the burning fiery furnace
hen these men were bound in their coats their hosen and their hats and their other garments and were cast into the midst of the burning fiery furnace
herefore because the kings commandment was urgent and the furnace exceeding hot the flame of the fire slew those men that took up hadrach eshach and bednego
nd these three men hadrach eshach and bednego fell down bound into the midst of the burning fiery furnace
hen ebuchadnezzar the king was astonied and rose up in haste and spake and said unto his counsellors id not we cast three men bound into the midst of the fire
hey answered and said unto the king rue  king
e answered and said o  see four men loose walking in the midst of the fire and they have no hurt and the form of the fourth is like the on of od
hen ebuchadnezzar came near to the mouth of the burning fiery furnace and spake and said hadrach eshach and bednego ye servants of the most high od come forth and come hither
hen hadrach eshach and bednego came forth of the midst of the fire
nd the princes governors and captains and the kings counsellors being gathered together saw these men upon whose bodies the fire had no power nor was an hair of their head singed neither were their coats changed nor the smell of fire had passed on them
hen ebuchadnezzar spake and said lessed be the od of hadrach eshach and bednego who hath sent his angel and delivered his servants that trusted in him and have changed the kings word and yielded their bodies that they might not serve nor worship any god except their own od
herefore  make a decree hat every people nation and language which speak any thing amiss against the od of hadrach eshach and bednego shall be cut in pieces and their houses shall be made a dunghill because there is no other od that can deliver after this sort
hen the king promoted hadrach eshach and bednego in the province of abylon
ebuchadnezzar the king unto all people nations and languages that dwell in all the earth eace be multiplied unto you
 thought it good to shew the signs and wonders that the high od hath wrought toward me
ow great are his signs and how mighty are his wonders his kingdom is an everlasting kingdom and his dominion is from generation to generation
 ebuchadnezzar was at rest in mine house and flourishing in my palace  saw a dream which made me afraid and the thoughts upon my bed and the visions of my head troubled me
herefore made  a decree to bring in all the wise men of abylon before me that they might make known unto me the interpretation of the dream
hen came in the magicians the astrologers the haldeans and the soothsayers and  told the dream before them but they did not make known unto me the interpretation thereof
ut at the last aniel came in before me whose name was elteshazzar according to the name of my god and in whom is the spirit of the holy gods and before him  told the dream saying  elteshazzar master of the magicians because  know that the spirit of the holy gods is in thee and no secret troubleth thee tell me the visions of my dream that  have seen and the interpretation thereof
hus were the visions of mine head in my bed  saw and behold a tree in the midst of the earth and the height thereof was great
he tree grew and was strong and the height thereof reached unto heaven and the sight thereof to the end of all the earth the leaves thereof were fair and the fruit thereof much and in it was meat for all the beasts of the field had shadow under it and the fowls of the heaven dwelt in the boughs thereof and all flesh was fed of it
 saw in the visions of my head upon my bed and behold a watcher and an holy one came down from heaven he cried aloud and said thus ew down the tree and cut off his branches shake off his leaves and scatter his fruit let the beasts get away from under it and the fowls from his branches nevertheless leave the stump of his roots in the earth even with a band of iron and brass in the tender grass of the field and let it be wet with the dew of heaven and let his portion be with the beasts in the grass of the earth let his heart be changed from mans and let a beasts heart be given unto him and let seven times pass over him
his matter is by the decree of the watchers and the demand by the word of the holy ones to the intent that the living may know that the most igh ruleth in the kingdom of men and giveth it to whomsoever he will and setteth up over it the basest of men
his dream  king ebuchadnezzar have seen
ow thou  elteshazzar declare the interpretation thereof forasmuch as all the wise men of my kingdom are not able to make known unto me the interpretation but thou art able for the spirit of the holy gods is in thee
hen aniel whose name was elteshazzar was astonied for one hour and his thoughts troubled him
he king spake and said elteshazzar let not the dream or the interpretation thereof trouble thee
elteshazzar answered and said y lord the dream be to them that hate thee and the interpretation thereof to thine enemies
he tree that thou sawest which grew and was strong whose height reached unto the heaven and the sight thereof to all the earth whose leaves were fair and the fruit thereof much and in it was meat for all under which the beasts of the field dwelt and upon whose branches the fowls of the heaven had their habitation it is thou  king that art grown and become strong for thy greatness is grown and reacheth unto heaven and thy dominion to the end of the earth
nd whereas the king saw a watcher and an holy one coming down from heaven and saying ew the tree down and destroy it yet leave the stump of the roots thereof in the earth even with a band of iron and brass in the tender grass of the field and let it be wet with the dew of heaven and let his portion be with the beasts of the field till seven times pass over him this is the interpretation  king and this is the decree of the most igh which is come upon my lord the king that they shall drive thee from men and thy dwelling shall be with the beasts of the field and they shall make thee to eat grass as oxen and they shall wet thee with the dew of heaven and seven times shall pass over thee till thou know that the most igh ruleth in the kingdom of men and giveth it to whomsoever he will
nd whereas they commanded to leave the stump of the tree roots thy kingdom shall be sure unto thee after that thou shalt have known that the heavens do rule
herefore  king let my counsel be acceptable unto thee and break off thy sins by righteousness and thine iniquities by shewing mercy to the poor if it may be a lengthening of thy tranquillity
ll this came upon the king ebuchadnezzar
t the end of twelve months he walked in the palace of the kingdom of abylon
he king spake and said s not this great abylon that  have built for the house of the kingdom by the might of my power and for the honour of my majesty
hile the word was in the kings mouth there fell a voice from heaven saying  king ebuchadnezzar to thee it is spoken he kingdom is departed from thee
nd they shall drive thee from men and thy dwelling shall be with the beasts of the field they shall make thee to eat grass as oxen and seven times shall pass over thee until thou know that the most igh ruleth in the kingdom of men and giveth it to whomsoever he will
he same hour was the thing fulfilled upon ebuchadnezzar and he was driven from men and did eat grass as oxen and his body was wet with the dew of heaven till his hairs were grown like eagles feathers and his nails like birds claws
nd at the end of the days  ebuchadnezzar lifted up mine eyes unto heaven and mine understanding returned unto me and  blessed the most igh and  praised and honoured him that liveth for ever whose dominion is an everlasting dominion and his kingdom is from generation to generation and all the inhabitants of the earth are reputed as nothing and he doeth according to his will in the army of heaven and among the inhabitants of the earth and none can stay his hand or say unto him hat doest thou
t the same time my reason returned unto me and for the glory of my kingdom mine honour and brightness returned unto me and my counsellors and my lords sought unto me and  was established in my kingdom and excellent majesty was added unto me
ow  ebuchadnezzar praise and extol and honour the ing of heaven all whose works are truth and his ways judgment and those that walk in pride he is able to abase
elshazzar the king made a great feast to a thousand of his lords and drank wine before the thousand
elshazzar whiles he tasted the wine commanded to bring the golden and silver vessels which his father ebuchadnezzar had taken out of the temple which was in erusalem that the king and his princes his wives and his concubines might drink therein
hen they brought the golden vessels that were taken out of the temple of the house of od which was at erusalem and the king and his princes his wives and his concubines drank in them
hey drank wine and praised the gods of gold and of silver of brass of iron of wood and of stone
n the same hour came forth fingers of a mans hand and wrote over against the candlestick upon the plaister of the wall of the kings palace and the king saw the part of the hand that wrote
hen the kings countenance was changed and his thoughts troubled him so that the joints of his loins were loosed and his knees smote one against another
he king cried aloud to bring in the astrologers the haldeans and the soothsayers
nd the king spake and said to the wise men of abylon hosoever shall read this writing and shew me the interpretation thereof shall be clothed with scarlet and have a chain of gold about his neck and shall be the third ruler in the kingdom
hen came in all the kings wise men but they could not read the writing nor make known to the king the interpretation thereof
hen was king elshazzar greatly troubled and his countenance was changed in him and his lords were astonied
ow the queen by reason of the words of the king and his lords came into the banquet house and the queen spake and said  king live for ever let not thy thoughts trouble thee nor let thy countenance be changed there is a man in thy kingdom in whom is the spirit of the holy gods and in the days of thy father light and understanding and wisdom like the wisdom of the gods was found in him whom the king ebuchadnezzar thy father the king  say thy father made master of the magicians astrologers haldeans and soothsayers forasmuch as an excellent spirit and knowledge and understanding interpreting of dreams and shewing of hard sentences and dissolving of doubts were found in the same aniel whom the king named elteshazzar now let aniel be called and he will shew the interpretation
hen was aniel brought in before the king
nd the king spake and said unto aniel rt thou that aniel which art of the children of the captivity of udah whom the king my father brought out of ewry
 have even heard of thee that the spirit of the gods is in thee and that light and understanding and excellent wisdom is found in thee
nd now the wise men the astrologers have been brought in before me that they should read this writing and make known unto me the interpretation thereof but they could not shew the interpretation of the thing and  have heard of thee that thou canst make interpretations and dissolve doubts now if thou canst read the writing and make known to me the interpretation thereof thou shalt be clothed with scarlet and have a chain of gold about thy neck and shalt be the third ruler in the kingdom
hen aniel answered and said before the king et thy gifts be to thyself and give thy rewards to another yet  will read the writing unto the king and make known to him the interpretation
 thou king the most high od gave ebuchadnezzar thy father a kingdom and majesty and glory and honour and for the majesty that he gave him all people nations and languages trembled and feared before him whom he would he slew and whom he would he kept alive and whom he would he set up and whom he would he put down
ut when his heart was lifted up and his mind hardened in pride he was deposed from his kingly throne and they took his glory from him and he was driven from the sons of men and his heart was made like the beasts and his dwelling was with the wild asses they fed him with grass like oxen and his body was wet with the dew of heaven till he knew that the most high od ruled in the kingdom of men and that he appointeth over it whomsoever he will
nd thou his son  elshazzar hast not humbled thine heart though thou knewest all this but hast lifted up thyself against the ord of heaven and they have brought the vessels of his house before thee and thou and thy lords thy wives and thy concubines have drunk wine in them and thou hast praised the gods of silver and gold of brass iron wood and stone which see not nor hear nor know and the od in whose hand thy breath is and whose are all thy ways hast thou not glorified then was the part of the hand sent from him and this writing was written
nd this is the writing that was written    
his is the interpretation of the thing  od hath numbered thy kingdom and finished it
 hou art weighed in the balances and art found wanting
 hy kingdom is divided and given to the edes and ersians
hen commanded elshazzar and they clothed aniel with scarlet and put a chain of gold about his neck and made a proclamation concerning him that he should be the third ruler in the kingdom
n that night was elshazzar the king of the haldeans slain
nd arius the edian took the kingdom being about threescore and two years old
t pleased arius to set over the kingdom an hundred and twenty princes which should be over the whole kingdom and over these three presidents of whom aniel was first that the princes might give accounts unto them and the king should have no damage
hen this aniel was preferred above the presidents and princes because an excellent spirit was in him and the king thought to set him over the whole realm
hen the presidents and princes sought to find occasion against aniel concerning the kingdom but they could find none occasion nor fault forasmuch as he was faithful neither was there any error or fault found in him
hen said these men e shall not find any occasion against this aniel except we find it against him concerning the law of his od
hen these presidents and princes assembled together to the king and said thus unto him ing arius live for ever
ll the presidents of the kingdom the governors and the princes the counsellors and the captains have consulted together to establish a royal statute and to make a firm decree that whosoever shall ask a petition of any od or man for thirty days save of thee  king he shall be cast into the den of lions
ow  king establish the decree and sign the writing that it be not changed according to the law of the edes and ersians which altereth not
herefore king arius signed the writing and the decree
ow when aniel knew that the writing was signed he went into his house and his windows being open in his chamber toward erusalem he kneeled upon his knees three times a day and prayed and gave thanks before his od as he did aforetime
hen these men assembled and found aniel praying and making supplication before his od
hen they came near and spake before the king concerning the kings decree ast thou not signed a decree that every man that shall ask a petition of any od or man within thirty days save of thee  king shall be cast into the den of lions
he king answered and said he thing is true according to the law of the edes and ersians which altereth not
hen answered they and said before the king hat aniel which is of the children of the captivity of udah regardeth not thee  king nor the decree that thou hast signed but maketh his petition three times a day
hen the king when he heard these words was sore displeased with himself and set his heart on aniel to deliver him and he laboured till the going down of the sun to deliver him
hen these men assembled unto the king and said unto the king now  king that the law of the edes and ersians is hat no decree nor statute which the king establisheth may be changed
hen the king commanded and they brought aniel and cast him into the den of lions
ow the king spake and said unto aniel hy od whom thou servest continually he will deliver thee
nd a stone was brought and laid upon the mouth of the den and the king sealed it with his own signet and with the signet of his lords that the purpose might not be changed concerning aniel
hen the king went to his palace and passed the night fasting neither were instruments of musick brought before him and his sleep went from him
hen the king arose very early in the morning and went in haste unto the den of lions
nd when he came to the den he cried with a lamentable voice unto aniel and the king spake and said to aniel  aniel servant of the living od is thy od whom thou servest continually able to deliver thee from the lions
hen said aniel unto the king  king live for ever
y od hath sent his angel and hath shut the lions mouths that they have not hurt me forasmuch as before him innocency was found in me and also before thee  king have  done no hurt
hen was the king exceeding glad for him and commanded that they should take aniel up out of the den
o aniel was taken up out of the den and no manner of hurt was found upon him because he believed in his od
nd the king commanded and they brought those men which had accused aniel and they cast them into the den of lions them their children and their wives and the lions had the mastery of them and brake all their bones in pieces or ever they came at the bottom of the den
hen king arius wrote unto all people nations and languages that dwell in all the earth eace be multiplied unto you
 make a decree hat in every dominion of my kingdom men tremble and fear before the od of aniel for he is the living od and stedfast for ever and his kingdom that which shall not be destroyed and his dominion shall be even unto the end
e delivereth and rescueth and he worketh signs and wonders in heaven and in earth who hath delivered aniel from the power of the lions
o this aniel prospered in the reign of arius and in the reign of yrus the ersian
n the first year of elshazzar king of abylon aniel had a dream and visions of his head upon his bed then he wrote the dream and told the sum of the matters
aniel spake and said  saw in my vision by night and behold the four winds of the heaven strove upon the great sea
nd four great beasts came up from the sea diverse one from another
he first was like a lion and had eagles wings  beheld till the wings thereof were plucked and it was lifted up from the earth and made stand upon the feet as a man and a mans heart was given to it
nd behold another beast a second like to a bear and it raised up itself on one side and it had three ribs in the mouth of it between the teeth of it and they said thus unto it rise devour much flesh
fter this  beheld and lo another like a leopard which had upon the back of it four wings of a fowl the beast had also four heads and dominion was given to it
fter this  saw in the night visions and behold a fourth beast dreadful and terrible and strong exceedingly and it had great iron teeth it devoured and brake in pieces and stamped the residue with the feet of it and it was diverse from all the beasts that were before it and it had ten horns
 considered the horns and behold there came up among them another little horn before whom there were three of the first horns plucked up by the roots and behold in this horn were eyes like the eyes of man and a mouth speaking great things
 beheld till the thrones were cast down and the ncient of days did sit whose garment was white as snow and the hair of his head like the pure wool his throne was like the fiery flame and his wheels as burning fire
 fiery stream issued and came forth from before him thousand thousands ministered unto him and ten thousand times ten thousand stood before him the judgment was set and the books were opened
 beheld then because of the voice of the great words which the horn spake  beheld even till the beast was slain and his body destroyed and given to the burning flame
s concerning the rest of the beasts they had their dominion taken away yet their lives were prolonged for a season and time
 saw in the night visions and behold one like the on of man came with the clouds of heaven and came to the ncient of days and they brought him near before him
nd there was given him dominion and glory and a kingdom that all people nations and languages should serve him his dominion is an everlasting dominion which shall not pass away and his kingdom that which shall not be destroyed
 aniel was grieved in my spirit in the midst of my body and the visions of my head troubled me
 came near unto one of them that stood by and asked him the truth of all this
o he told me and made me know the interpretation of the things
hese great beasts which are four are four kings which shall arise out of the earth
ut the saints of the most igh shall take the kingdom and possess the kingdom for ever even for ever and ever
hen  would know the truth of the fourth beast which was diverse from all the others exceeding dreadful whose teeth were of iron and his nails of brass which devoured brake in pieces and stamped the residue with his feet and of the ten horns that were in his head and of the other which came up and before whom three fell even of that horn that had eyes and a mouth that spake very great things whose look was more stout than his fellows
 beheld and the same horn made war with the saints and prevailed against them until the ncient of days came and judgment was given to the saints of the most igh and the time came that the saints possessed the kingdom
hus he said he fourth beast shall be the fourth kingdom upon earth which shall be diverse from all kingdoms and shall devour the whole earth and shall tread it down and break it in pieces
nd the ten horns out of this kingdom are ten kings that shall arise and another shall rise after them and he shall be diverse from the first and he shall subdue three kings
nd he shall speak great words against the most igh and shall wear out the saints of the most igh and think to change times and laws and they shall be given into his hand until a time and times and the dividing of time
ut the judgment shall sit and they shall take away his dominion to consume and to destroy it unto the end
nd the kingdom and dominion and the greatness of the kingdom under the whole heaven shall be given to the people of the saints of the most igh whose kingdom is an everlasting kingdom and all dominions shall serve and obey him
itherto is the end of the matter
s for me aniel my cogitations much troubled me and my countenance changed in me but  kept the matter in my heart
n the third year of the reign of king elshazzar a vision appeared unto me even unto me aniel after that which appeared unto me at the first
nd  saw in a vision and it came to pass when  saw that  was at hushan in the palace which is in the province of lam and  saw in a vision and  was by the river of lai
hen  lifted up mine eyes and saw and behold there stood before the river a ram which had two horns and the two horns were high but one was higher than the other and the higher came up last
 saw the ram pushing westward and northward and southward so that no beasts might stand before him neither was there any that could deliver out of his hand but he did according to his will and became great
nd as  was considering behold an he goat came from the west on the face of the whole earth and touched not the ground and the goat had a notable horn between his eyes
nd he came to the ram that had two horns which  had seen standing before the river and ran unto him in the fury of his power
nd  saw him come close unto the ram and he was moved with choler against him and smote the ram and brake his two horns and there was no power in the ram to stand before him but he cast him down to the ground and stamped upon him and there was none that could deliver the ram out of his hand
herefore the he goat waxed very great and when he was strong the great horn was broken and for it came up four notable ones toward the four winds of heaven
nd out of one of them came forth a little horn which waxed exceeding great toward the south and toward the east and toward the pleasant land
nd it waxed great even to the host of heaven and it cast down some of the host and of the stars to the ground and stamped upon them
ea he magnified himself even to the prince of the host and by him the daily sacrifice was taken away and the place of his sanctuary was cast down
nd an host was given him against the daily sacrifice by reason of transgression and it cast down the truth to the ground and it practised and prospered
hen  heard one saint speaking and another saint said unto that certain saint which spake ow long shall be the vision concerning the daily sacrifice and the transgression of desolation to give both the sanctuary and the host to be trodden under foot
nd he said unto me nto two thousand and three hundred days then shall the sanctuary be cleansed
nd it came to pass when  even  aniel had seen the vision and sought for the meaning then behold there stood before me as the appearance of a man
nd  heard a mans voice between the banks of lai which called and said abriel make this man to understand the vision
o he came near where  stood and when he came  was afraid and fell upon my face but he said unto me nderstand  son of man for at the time of the end shall be the vision
ow as he was speaking with me  was in a deep sleep on my face toward the ground but he touched me and set me upright
nd he said ehold  will make thee know what shall be in the last end of the indignation for at the time appointed the end shall be
he ram which thou sawest having two horns are the kings of edia and ersia
nd the rough goat is the king of recia and the great horn that is between his eyes is the first king
ow that being broken whereas four stood up for it four kingdoms shall stand up out of the nation but not in his power
nd in the latter time of their kingdom when the transgressors are come to the full a king of fierce countenance and understanding dark sentences shall stand up
nd his power shall be mighty but not by his own power and he shall destroy wonderfully and shall prosper and practise and shall destroy the mighty and the holy people
nd through his policy also he shall cause craft to prosper in his hand and he shall magnify himself in his heart and by peace shall destroy many he shall also stand up against the rince of princes but he shall be broken without hand
nd the vision of the evening and the morning which was told is true wherefore shut thou up the vision for it shall be for many days
nd  aniel fainted and was sick certain days afterward  rose up and did the kings business and  was astonished at the vision but none understood it
n the first year of arius the son of hasuerus of the seed of the edes which was made king over the realm of the haldeans in the first year of his reign  aniel understood by books the number of the years whereof the word of the  came to eremiah the prophet that he would accomplish seventy years in the desolations of erusalem
nd  set my face unto the ord od to seek by prayer and supplications with fasting and sackcloth and ashes and  prayed unto the  my od and made my confession and said  ord the great and dreadful od keeping the covenant and mercy to them that love him and to them that keep his commandments we have sinned and have committed iniquity and have done wickedly and have rebelled even by departing from thy precepts and from thy judgments neither have we hearkened unto thy servants the prophets which spake in thy name to our kings our princes and our fathers and to all the people of the land
 ord righteousness belongeth unto thee but unto us confusion of faces as at this day to the men of udah and to the inhabitants of erusalem and unto all srael that are near and that are far off through all the countries whither thou hast driven them because of their trespass that they have trespassed against thee
 ord to us belongeth confusion of face to our kings to our princes and to our fathers because we have sinned against thee
o the ord our od belong mercies and forgivenesses though we have rebelled against him neither have we obeyed the voice of the  our od to walk in his laws which he set before us by his servants the prophets
ea all srael have transgressed thy law even by departing that they might not obey thy voice therefore the curse is poured upon us and the oath that is written in the law of oses the servant of od because we have sinned against him
nd he hath confirmed his words which he spake against us and against our judges that judged us by bringing upon us a great evil for under the whole heaven hath not been done as hath been done upon erusalem
s it is written in the law of oses all this evil is come upon us yet made we not our prayer before the  our od that we might turn from our iniquities and understand thy truth
herefore hath the  watched upon the evil and brought it upon us for the  our od is righteous in all his works which he doeth for we obeyed not his voice
nd now  ord our od that hast brought thy people forth out of the land of gypt with a mighty hand and hast gotten thee renown as at this day we have sinned we have done wickedly
 ord according to all thy righteousness  beseech thee let thine anger and thy fury be turned away from thy city erusalem thy holy mountain because for our sins and for the iniquities of our fathers erusalem and thy people are become a reproach to all that are about us
ow therefore  our od hear the prayer of thy servant and his supplications and cause thy face to shine upon thy sanctuary that is desolate for the ords sake
 my od incline thine ear and hear open thine eyes and behold our desolations and the city which is called by thy name for we do not present our supplications before thee for our righteousnesses but for thy great mercies
 ord hear  ord forgive  ord hearken and do defer not for thine own sake  my od for thy city and thy people are called by thy name
nd whiles  was speaking and praying and confessing my sin and the sin of my people srael and presenting my supplication before the  my od for the holy mountain of my od yea whiles  was speaking in prayer even the man abriel whom  had seen in the vision at the beginning being caused to fly swiftly touched me about the time of the evening oblation
nd he informed me and talked with me and said  aniel  am now come forth to give thee skill and understanding
t the beginning of thy supplications the commandment came forth and  am come to shew thee for thou art greatly beloved therefore understand the matter and consider the vision
eventy weeks are determined upon thy people and upon thy holy city to finish the transgression and to make an end of sins and to make reconciliation for iniquity and to bring in everlasting righteousness and to seal up the vision and prophecy and to anoint the most oly
now therefore and understand that from the going forth of the commandment to restore and to build erusalem unto the essiah the rince shall be seven weeks and threescore and two weeks the street shall be built again and the wall even in troublous times
nd after threescore and two weeks shall essiah be cut off but not for himself and the people of the prince that shall come shall destroy the city and the sanctuary and the end thereof shall be with a flood and unto the end of the war desolations are determined
nd he shall confirm the covenant with many for one week and in the midst of the week he shall cause the sacrifice and the oblation to cease and for the overspreading of abominations he shall make it desolate even until the consummation and that determined shall be poured upon the desolate
n the third year of yrus king of ersia a thing was revealed unto aniel whose name was called elteshazzar and the thing was true but the time appointed was long and he understood the thing and had understanding of the vision
n those days  aniel was mourning three full weeks
 ate no pleasant bread neither came flesh nor wine in my mouth neither did  anoint myself at all till three whole weeks were fulfilled
nd in the four and twentieth day of the first month as  was by the side of the great river which is iddekel then  lifted up mine eyes and looked and behold a certain man clothed in linen whose loins were girded with fine gold of phaz his body also was like the beryl and his face as the appearance of lightning and his eyes as lamps of fire and his arms and his feet like in colour to polished brass and the voice of his words like the voice of a multitude
nd  aniel alone saw the vision for the men that were with me saw not the vision but a great quaking fell upon them so that they fled to hide themselves
herefore  was left alone and saw this great vision and there remained no strength in me for my comeliness was turned in me into corruption and  retained no strength
et heard  the voice of his words and when  heard the voice of his words then was  in a deep sleep on my face and my face toward the ground
nd behold an hand touched me which set me upon my knees and upon the palms of my hands
nd he said unto me  aniel a man greatly beloved understand the words that  speak unto thee and stand upright for unto thee am  now sent
nd when he had spoken this word unto me  stood trembling
hen said he unto me ear not aniel for from the first day that thou didst set thine heart to understand and to chasten thyself before thy od thy words were heard and  am come for thy words
ut the prince of the kingdom of ersia withstood me one and twenty days but lo ichael one of the chief princes came to help me and  remained there with the kings of ersia
ow  am come to make thee understand what shall befall thy people in the latter days for yet the vision is for many days
nd when he had spoken such words unto me  set my face toward the ground and  became dumb
nd behold one like the similitude of the sons of men touched my lips then  opened my mouth and spake and said unto him that stood before me  my lord by the vision my sorrows are turned upon me and  have retained no strength
or how can the servant of this my lord talk with this my lord for as for me straightway there remained no strength in me neither is there breath left in me
hen there came again and touched me one like the appearance of a man and he strengthened me and said  man greatly beloved fear not peace be unto thee be strong yea be strong
nd when he had spoken unto me  was strengthened and said et my lord speak for thou hast strengthened me
hen said he nowest thou wherefore  come unto thee and now will  return to fight with the prince of ersia and when  am gone forth lo the prince of recia shall come
ut  will shew thee that which is noted in the scripture of truth and there is none that holdeth with me in these things but ichael your prince
lso  in the first year of arius the ede even  stood to confirm and to strengthen him
nd now will  shew thee the truth
ehold there shall stand up yet three kings in ersia and the fourth shall be far richer than they all and by his strength through his riches he shall stir up all against the realm of recia
nd a mighty king shall stand up that shall rule with great dominion and do according to his will
nd when he shall stand up his kingdom shall be broken and shall be divided toward the four winds of heaven and not to his posterity nor according to his dominion which he ruled for his kingdom shall be plucked up even for others beside those
nd the king of the south shall be strong and one of his princes and he shall be strong above him and have dominion his dominion shall be a great dominion
nd in the end of years they shall join themselves together for the kings daughter of the south shall come to the king of the north to make an agreement but she shall not retain the power of the arm neither shall he stand nor his arm but she shall be given up and they that brought her and he that begat her and he that strengthened her in these times
ut out of a branch of her roots shall one stand up in his estate which shall come with an army and shall enter into the fortress of the king of the north and shall deal against them and shall prevail and shall also carry captives into gypt their gods with their princes and with their precious vessels of silver and of gold and he shall continue more years than the king of the north
o the king of the south shall come into his kingdom and shall return into his own land
ut his sons shall be stirred up and shall assemble a multitude of great forces and one shall certainly come and overflow and pass through then shall he return and be stirred up even to his fortress
nd the king of the south shall be moved with choler and shall come forth and fight with him even with the king of the north and he shall set forth a great multitude but the multitude shall be given into his hand
nd when he hath taken away the multitude his heart shall be lifted up and he shall cast down many ten thousands but he shall not be strengthened by it
or the king of the north shall return and shall set forth a multitude greater than the former and shall certainly come after certain years with a great army and with much riches
nd in those times there shall many stand up against the king of the south also the robbers of thy people shall exalt themselves to establish the vision but they shall fall
o the king of the north shall come and cast up a mount and take the most fenced cities and the arms of the south shall not withstand neither his chosen people neither shall there be any strength to withstand
ut he that cometh against him shall do according to his own will and none shall stand before him and he shall stand in the glorious land which by his hand shall be consumed
e shall also set his face to enter with the strength of his whole kingdom and upright ones with him thus shall he do and he shall give him the daughter of women corrupting her but she shall not stand on his side neither be for him
fter this shall he turn his face unto the isles and shall take many but a prince for his own behalf shall cause the reproach offered by him to cease without his own reproach he shall cause it to turn upon him
hen he shall turn his face toward the fort of his own land but he shall stumble and fall and not be found
hen shall stand up in his estate a raiser of taxes in the glory of the kingdom but within few days he shall be destroyed neither in anger nor in battle
nd in his estate shall stand up a vile person to whom they shall not give the honour of the kingdom but he shall come in peaceably and obtain the kingdom by flatteries
nd with the arms of a flood shall they be overflown from before him and shall be broken yea also the prince of the covenant
nd after the league made with him he shall work deceitfully for he shall come up and shall become strong with a small people
e shall enter peaceably even upon the fattest places of the province and he shall do that which his fathers have not done nor his fathers fathers he shall scatter among them the prey and spoil and riches yea and he shall forecast his devices against the strong holds even for a time
nd he shall stir up his power and his courage against the king of the south with a great army and the king of the south shall be stirred up to battle with a very great and mighty army but he shall not stand for they shall forecast devices against him
ea they that feed of the portion of his meat shall destroy him and his army shall overflow and many shall fall down slain
nd both these kings hearts shall be to do mischief and they shall speak lies at one table but it shall not prosper for yet the end shall be at the time appointed
hen shall he return into his land with great riches and his heart shall be against the holy covenant and he shall do exploits and return to his own land
t the time appointed he shall return and come toward the south but it shall not be as the former or as the latter
or the ships of hittim shall come against him therefore he shall be grieved and return and have indignation against the holy covenant so shall he do he shall even return and have intelligence with them that forsake the holy covenant
nd arms shall stand on his part and they shall pollute the sanctuary of strength and shall take away the daily sacrifice and they shall place the abomination that maketh desolate
nd such as do wickedly against the covenant shall he corrupt by flatteries but the people that do know their od shall be strong and do exploits
nd they that understand among the people shall instruct many yet they shall fall by the sword and by flame by captivity and by spoil many days
ow when they shall fall they shall be holpen with a little help but many shall cleave to them with flatteries
nd some of them of understanding shall fall to try them and to purge and to make them white even to the time of the end because it is yet for a time appointed
nd the king shall do according to his will and he shall exalt himself and magnify himself above every god and shall speak marvellous things against the od of gods and shall prosper till the indignation be accomplished for that that is determined shall be done
either shall he regard the od of his fathers nor the desire of women nor regard any god for he shall magnify himself above all
ut in his estate shall he honour the od of forces and a god whom his fathers knew not shall he honour with gold and silver and with precious stones and pleasant things
hus shall he do in the most strong holds with a strange god whom he shall acknowledge and increase with glory and he shall cause them to rule over many and shall divide the land for gain
nd at the time of the end shall the king of the south push at him and the king of the north shall come against him like a whirlwind with chariots and with horsemen and with many ships and he shall enter into the countries and shall overflow and pass over
e shall enter also into the glorious land and many countries shall be overthrown but these shall escape out of his hand even dom and oab and the chief of the children of mmon
e shall stretch forth his hand also upon the countries and the land of gypt shall not escape
ut he shall have power over the treasures of gold and of silver and over all the precious things of gypt and the ibyans and the thiopians shall be at his steps
ut tidings out of the east and out of the north shall trouble him therefore he shall go forth with great fury to destroy and utterly to make away many
nd he shall plant the tabernacles of his palace between the seas in the glorious holy mountain yet he shall come to his end and none shall help him
nd at that time shall ichael stand up the great prince which standeth for the children of thy people and there shall be a time of trouble such as never was since there was a nation even to that same time and at that time thy people shall be delivered every one that shall be found written in the book
nd many of them that sleep in the dust of the earth shall awake some to everlasting life and some to shame and everlasting contempt
nd they that be wise shall shine as the brightness of the firmament and they that turn many to righteousness as the stars for ever and ever
ut thou  aniel shut up the words and seal the book even to the time of the end many shall run to and fro and knowledge shall be increased

nd phraim is as an heifer that is taught and loveth to tread out the corn but  passed over upon her fair neck  will make phraim to ride udah shall plow and acob shall break his clods
ow to yourselves in righteousness reap in mercy break up your fallow ground for it is time to seek the  till he come and rain righteousness upon you
e have plowed wickedness ye have reaped iniquity ye have eaten the fruit of lies because thou didst trust in thy way in the multitude of thy mighty men
herefore shall a tumult arise among thy people and all thy fortresses shall be spoiled as halman spoiled etharbel in the day of battle the mother was dashed in pieces upon her children
o shall ethel do unto you because of your great wickedness in a morning shall the king of srael utterly be cut off
hen srael was a child then  loved him and called my son out of gypt
s they called them so they went from them they sacrificed unto aalim and burned incense to graven images
 taught phraim also to go taking them by their arms but they knew not that  healed them
 drew them with cords of a man with bands of love and  was to them as they that take off the yoke on their jaws and  laid meat unto them
e shall not return into the land of gypt but the ssyrian shall be his king because they refused to return
nd the sword shall abide on his cities and shall consume his branches and devour them because of their own counsels
nd my people are bent to backsliding from me though they called them to the most igh none at all would exalt him
ow shall  give thee up phraim how shall  deliver thee srael how shall  make thee as dmah how shall  set thee as eboim mine heart is turned within me my repentings are kindled together
 will not execute the fierceness of mine anger  will not return to destroy phraim for  am od and not man the oly ne in the midst of thee and  will not enter into the city
hey shall walk after the  he shall roar like a lion when he shall roar then the children shall tremble from the west
hey shall tremble as a bird out of gypt and as a dove out of the land of ssyria and  will place them in their houses saith the 
phraim compasseth me about with lies and the house of srael with deceit but udah yet ruleth with od and is faithful with the saints
phraim feedeth on wind and followeth after the east wind he daily increaseth lies and desolation and they do make a covenant with the ssyrians and oil is carried into gypt
he  hath also a controversy with udah and will punish acob according to his ways according to his doings will he recompense him
e took his brother by the heel in the womb and by his strength he had power with od yea he had power over the angel and prevailed he wept and made supplication unto him he found him in ethel and there he spake with us even the  od of hosts the  is his memorial
herefore turn thou to thy od keep mercy and judgment and wait on thy od continually
e is a merchant the balances of deceit are in his hand he loveth to oppress
nd phraim said et  am become rich  have found me out substance in all my labours they shall find none iniquity in me that were sin
nd  that am the  thy od from the land of gypt will yet make thee to dwell in tabernacles as in the days of the solemn feast
 have also spoken by the prophets and  have multiplied visions and used similitudes by the ministry of the prophets
s there iniquity in ilead surely they are vanity they sacrifice bullocks in ilgal yea their altars are as heaps in the furrows of the fields
nd acob fled into the country of yria and srael served for a wife and for a wife he kept sheep
nd by a prophet the  brought srael out of gypt and by a prophet was he preserved
phraim provoked him to anger most bitterly therefore shall he leave his blood upon him and his reproach shall his ord return unto him
hen phraim spake trembling he exalted himself in srael but when he offended in aal he died
nd now they sin more and more and have made them molten images of their silver and idols according to their own understanding all of it the work of the craftsmen they say of them et the men that sacrifice kiss the calves
herefore they shall be as the morning cloud and as the early dew that passeth away as the chaff that is driven with the whirlwind out of the floor and as the smoke out of the chimney
et  am the  thy od from the land of gypt and thou shalt know no god but me for there is no saviour beside me
 did know thee in the wilderness in the land of great drought
ccording to their pasture so were they filled they were filled and their heart was exalted therefore have they forgotten me
herefore  will be unto them as a lion as a leopard by the way will  observe them  will meet them as a bear that is bereaved of her whelps and will rend the caul of their heart and there will  devour them like a lion the wild beast shall tear them
 srael thou hast destroyed thyself but in me is thine help
 will be thy king where is any other that may save thee in all thy cities and thy judges of whom thou saidst ive me a king and princes
 gave thee a king in mine anger and took him away in my wrath
he iniquity of phraim is bound up his sin is hid
he sorrows of a travailing woman shall come upon him he is an unwise son for he should not stay long in the place of the breaking forth of children
 will ransom them from the power of the grave  will redeem them from death  death  will be thy plagues  grave  will be thy destruction repentance shall be hid from mine eyes
hough he be fruitful among his brethren an east wind shall come the wind of the  shall come up from the wilderness and his spring shall become dry and his fountain shall be dried up he shall spoil the treasure of all pleasant vessels
amaria shall become desolate for she hath rebelled against her od they shall fall by the sword their infants shall be dashed in pieces and their women with child shall be ripped up
 srael return unto the  thy od for thou hast fallen by thine iniquity

sshur shall not save us we will not ride upon horses neither will we say any more to the work of our hands e are our gods for in thee the fatherless findeth mercy
 will heal their backsliding  will love them freely for mine anger is turned away from him
 will be as the dew unto srael he shall grow as the lily and cast forth his roots as ebanon

hey that dwell under his shadow shall return they shall revive as the corn and grow as the vine the scent thereof shall be as the wine of ebanon

ath this been in your days or even in the days of your fathers

hat which the palmerworm hath left hath the locust eaten and that which the locust hath left hath the cankerworm eaten and that which the cankerworm hath left hath the caterpiller eaten
wake ye drunkards and weep and howl all ye drinkers of wine because of the new wine for it is cut off from your mouth
or a nation is come up upon my land strong and without number whose teeth are the teeth of a lion and he hath the cheek teeth of a great lion
e hath laid my vine waste and barked my fig tree he hath made it clean bare and cast it away the branches thereof are made white

low ye the trumpet in ion and sound an alarm in my holy mountain let all the inhabitants of the land tremble for the day of the  cometh for it is nigh at hand a day of darkness and of gloominess a day of clouds and of thick darkness as the morning spread upon the mountains a great people and a strong there hath not been ever the like neither shall be any more after it even to the years of many generations
 fire devoureth before them and behind them a flame burneth the land is as the garden of den before them and behind them a desolate wilderness yea and nothing shall escape them
he appearance of them is as the appearance of horses and as horsemen so shall they run
ike the noise of chariots on the tops of mountains shall they leap like the noise of a flame of fire that devoureth the stubble as a strong people set in battle array
efore their face the people shall be much pained all faces shall gather blackness
hey shall run like mighty men they shall climb the wall like men of war and they shall march every one on his ways and they shall not break their ranks neither shall one thrust another they shall walk every one in his path and when they fall upon the sword they shall not be wounded

hus saith the  or three transgressions of amascus and for four  will not turn away the punishment thereof because they have threshed ilead with threshing instruments of iron but  will send a fire into the house of azael which shall devour the palaces of enhadad

hus saith the  or three transgressions of aza and for four  will not turn away the punishment thereof because they carried away captive the whole captivity to deliver them up to dom but  will send a fire on the wall of aza which shall devour the palaces thereof and  will cut off the inhabitant from shdod and him that holdeth the sceptre from shkelon and  will turn mine hand against kron and the remnant of the hilistines shall perish saith the ord 
hus saith the  or three transgressions of yrus and for four  will not turn away the punishment thereof because they delivered up the whole captivity to dom and remembered not the brotherly covenant but  will send a fire on the wall of yrus which shall devour the palaces thereof
hus saith the  or three transgressions of dom and for four  will not turn away the punishment thereof because he did pursue his brother with the sword and did cast off all pity and his anger did tear perpetually and he kept his wrath for ever but  will send a fire upon eman which shall devour the palaces of ozrah
hus saith the  or three transgressions of the children of mmon and for four  will not turn away the punishment thereof because they have ripped up the women with child of ilead that they might enlarge their border but  will kindle a fire in the wall of abbah and it shall devour the palaces thereof with shouting in the day of battle with a tempest in the day of the whirlwind and their king shall go into captivity he and his princes together saith the 
hus saith the  or three transgressions of oab and for four  will not turn away the punishment thereof because he burned the bones of the king of dom into lime but  will send a fire upon oab and it shall devour the palaces of irioth and oab shall die with tumult with shouting and with the sound of the trumpet and  will cut off the judge from the midst thereof and will slay all the princes thereof with him saith the 
hus saith the  or three transgressions of udah and for four  will not turn away the punishment thereof because they have despised the law of the  and have not kept his commandments and their lies caused them to err after the which their fathers have walked but  will send a fire upon udah and it shall devour the palaces of erusalem
hus saith the  or three transgressions of srael and for four  will not turn away the punishment thereof because they sold the righteous for silver and the poor for a pair of shoes that pant after the dust of the earth on the head of the poor and turn aside the way of the meek and a man and his father will go in unto the same maid to profane my holy name and they lay themselves down upon clothes laid to pledge by every altar and they drink the wine of the condemned in the house of their god

e who turn judgment to wormwood and leave off righteousness in the earth seek him that maketh the seven stars and rion and turneth the shadow of death into the morning and maketh the day dark with night that calleth for the waters of the sea and poureth them out upon the face of the earth he  is his name that strengtheneth the spoiled against the strong so that the spoiled shall come against the fortress
hey hate him that rebuketh in the gate and they abhor him that speaketh uprightly

hen maziah the priest of ethel sent to eroboam king of srael saying mos hath conspired against thee in the midst of the house of srael the land is not able to bear all his words

nd the ord  of hosts is he that toucheth the land and it shall melt and all that dwell therein shall mourn and it shall rise up wholly like a flood and shall be drowned as by the flood of gypt

rt thou better than populous o that was situate among the rivers that had the waters round about it whose rampart was the sea and her wall was from the sea

ll thy strong holds shall be like fig trees with the firstripe figs if they be shaken they shall even fall into the mouth of the eater

hou hast multiplied thy merchants above the stars of heaven the cankerworm spoileth and fleeth away
hy crowned are as the locusts and thy captains as the great grasshoppers which camp in the hedges in the cold day but when the sun ariseth they flee away and their place is not known where they are

he book of the generation of esus hrist the son of avid the son of braham
braham begat saac and saac begat acob and acob begat udas and his brethren and udas begat hares and ara of hamar and hares begat srom and srom begat ram and ram begat minadab and minadab begat aasson and aasson begat almon and almon begat ooz of achab and ooz begat bed of uth and bed begat esse and esse begat avid the king and avid the king begat olomon of her that had been the wife of rias and olomon begat oboam and oboam begat bia and bia begat sa and sa begat osaphat and osaphat begat oram and oram begat zias and zias begat oatham and oatham begat chaz and chaz begat zekias and zekias begat anasses and anasses begat mon and mon begat osias and osias begat echonias and his brethren about the time they were carried away to abylon and after they were brought to abylon echonias begat alathiel and alathiel begat orobabel and orobabel begat biud and biud begat liakim and liakim begat zor and zor begat adoc and adoc begat chim and chim begat liud and liud begat leazar and leazar begat atthan and atthan begat acob and acob begat oseph the husband of ary of whom was born esus who is called hrist
o all the generations from braham to avid are fourteen generations and from avid until the carrying away into abylon are fourteen generations and from the carrying away into abylon unto hrist are fourteen generations
ow the birth of esus hrist was on this wise hen as his mother ary was espoused to oseph before they came together she was found with child of the oly host
hen oseph her husband being a just man and not willing to make her a publick example was minded to put her away privily
ut while he thought on these things behold the angel of the ord appeared unto him in a dream saying oseph thou son of avid fear not to take unto thee ary thy wife for that which is conceived in her is of the oly host
nd she shall bring forth a son and thou shalt call his name  for he shall save his people from their sins
ow all this was done that it might be fulfilled which was spoken of the ord by the prophet saying ehold a virgin shall be with child and shall bring forth a son nd they shall call his name mmanuel which being interpreted is od with us
hen oseph being raised from sleep did as the angel of the ord had bidden him and took unto him his wife and knew her not till she had brought forth her firstborn son and he called his name 
ow when esus was born in ethlehem of ud a in the days of erod the king behold there came wise men from the east to erusalem saying here is he that is born ing of the ews for we have seen his star in the east and are come to worship him
hen erod the king had heard these things he was troubled and all erusalem with him
nd when he had gathered all the chief priests and scribes of the people together he demanded of them where hrist should be born
nd they said unto him n ethlehem of ud a for thus it is written by the prophet nd thou ethlehem in the land of uda rt not the least among the princes of uda or out of thee shall come a overnor hat shall rule my people srael
hen erod when he had privily called the wise men enquired of them diligently what time the star appeared
nd he sent them to ethlehem and said o and search diligently for the young child and when ye have found him bring me word again that  may come and worship him also
hen they had heard the king they departed and lo the star which they saw in the east went before them till it came and stood over where the young child was
hen they saw the star they rejoiced with exceeding great joy
nd when they were come into the house they saw the young child with ary his mother and fell down and worshipped him and when they had opened their treasures they presented unto him gifts gold and frankincense and myrrh
nd being warned of od in a dream that they should not return to erod they departed into their own country another way
nd when they were departed behold the angel of the ord appeareth to oseph in a dream saying rise and take the young child and his mother and flee into gypt and be thou there until  bring thee word for erod will seek the young child to destroy him
hen he arose he took the young child and his mother by night and departed into gypt and was there until the death of erod that it might be fulfilled which was spoken of the ord by the prophet saying ut of gypt have  called my son
hen erod when he saw that he was mocked of the wise men was exceeding wroth and sent forth and slew all the children that were in ethlehem and in all the coasts thereof from two years old and under according to the time which he had diligently enquired of the wise men
hen was fulfilled that which was spoken by eremy the prophet saying n ama was there a voice heard amentation and weeping and great mourning achel weeping for her children nd would not be comforted because they are not
ut when erod was dead behold an angel of the ord appeareth in a dream to oseph in gypt saying rise and take the young child and his mother and go into the land of srael for they are dead which sought the young childs life
nd he arose and took the young child and his mother and came into the land of srael
ut when he heard that rchelaus did reign in ud a in the room of his father erod he was afraid to go thither notwithstanding being warned of od in a dream he turned aside into the parts of alilee and he came and dwelt in a city called azareth that it might be fulfilled which was spoken by the prophets e shall be called a azarene
n those days came ohn the aptist preaching in the wilderness of ud a and saying epent ye for the kingdom of heaven is at hand
or this is he that was spoken of by the prophet saias saying he voice of one crying in the wilderness repare ye the way of the ord ake his paths straight
nd the same ohn had his raiment of camels hair and a leathern girdle about his loins and his meat was locusts and wild honey
hen went out to him erusalem and all ud a and all the region round about ordan and were baptized of him in ordan confessing their sins
ut when he saw many of the harisees and adducees come to his baptism he said unto them  generation of vipers who hath warned you to flee from the wrath to come
ring forth therefore fruits meet for repentance and think not to say within yourselves e have braham to our father for  say unto you that od is able of these stones to raise up children unto braham
nd now also the axe is laid unto the root of the trees therefore every tree which bringeth not forth good fruit is hewn down and cast into the fire
 indeed baptize you with water unto repentance but he that cometh after me is mightier than  whose shoes  am not worthy to bear he shall baptize you with the oly host and with fire whose fan is in his hand and he will throughly purge his floor and gather his wheat into the garner but he will burn up the chaff with unquenchable fire
hen cometh esus from alilee to ordan unto ohn to be baptized of him
ut ohn forbad him saying  have need to be baptized of thee and comest thou to me
nd esus answering said unto him uffer it to be so now for thus it becometh us to fulfil all righteousness
hen he suffered him
nd esus when he was baptized went up straightway out of the water and lo the heavens were opened unto him and he saw the pirit of od descending like a dove and lighting upon him and lo a voice from heaven saying his is my beloved on in whom  am well pleased
hen was esus led up of the pirit into the wilderness to be tempted of the devil
nd when he had fasted forty days and forty nights he was afterward an hungred
nd when the tempter came to him he said f thou be the on of od command that these stones be made bread
ut he answered and said t is written an shall not live by bread alone but by every word that proceedeth out of the mouth of od
hen the devil taketh him up into the holy city and setteth him on a pinnacle of the temple and saith unto him f thou be the on of od cast thyself down for it is written e shall give his angels charge concerning thee nd in their hands they shall bear thee up est at any time thou dash thy foot against a stone
esus said unto him t is written again hou shalt not tempt the ord thy od
gain the devil taketh him up into an exceeding high mountain and sheweth him all the kingdoms of the world and the glory of them and saith unto him ll these things will  give thee if thou wilt fall down and worship me
hen saith esus unto him et thee hence atan for it is written hou shalt worship the ord thy od and him only shalt thou serve
hen the devil leaveth him and behold angels came and ministered unto him
ow when esus had heard that ohn was cast into prison he departed into alilee and leaving azareth he came and dwelt in apernaum which is upon the sea coast in the borders of abulon and ephthalim that it might be fulfilled which was spoken by saias the prophet saying he land of abulon and the land of ephthalim y the way of the sea beyond ordan alilee of the entiles he people which sat in darknessaw great light nd to them which sat in the region and shadow of deathight is sprung up
rom that time esus began to preach and to say epent for the kingdom of heaven is at hand
nd esus walking by the sea of alilee saw two brethren imon called eter and ndrew his brother casting a net into the sea for they were fishers
nd he saith unto them ollow me and  will make you fishers of men
nd they straightway left their nets and followed him
