nd going on from thence he saw other two brethren ames the son of ebedee and ohn his brother in a ship with ebedee their father mending their nets and he called them
nd they immediately left the ship and their father and followed him
nd esus went about all alilee teaching in their synagogues and preaching the gospel of the kingdom and healing all manner of sickness and all manner of disease among the people
nd his fame went throughout all yria and they brought unto him all sick people that were taken with divers diseases and torments and those which were possessed with devils and those which were lunatick and those that had the palsy and he healed them
nd there followed him great multitudes of people from alilee and from ecapolis and from erusalem and from ud a and from beyond ordan
nd seeing the multitudes he went up into a mountain and when he was set his disciples came unto him and he opened his mouth and taught them saying lessed are the poor in spirit for theirs is the kingdom of heaven
lessed are they that mourn for they shall be comforted
lessed are the meek for they shall inherit the earth
lessed are they which do hunger and thirst after righteousness for they shall be filled
lessed are the merciful for they shall obtain mercy
lessed are the pure in heart for they shall see od
lessed are the peacemakers for they shall be called the children of od
lessed are they which are persecuted for righteousness sake for theirs is the kingdom of heaven
lessed are ye when men shall revile you and persecute you and shall say all manner of evil against you falsely for my sake
ejoice and be exceeding glad for great is your reward in heaven for so persecuted they the prophets which were before you
e are the salt of the earth but if the salt have lost his savour wherewith shall it be salted it is thenceforth good for nothing but to be cast out and to be trodden under foot of men
e are the light of the world
 city that is set on an hill cannot be hid
either do men light a candle and put it under a bushel but on a candlestick and it giveth light unto all that are in the house
et your light so shine before men that they may see your good works and glorify your ather which is in heaven
hink not that  am come to destroy the law or the prophets  am not come to destroy but to fulfil
or verily  say unto you ill heaven and earth pass one jot or one tittle shall in no wise pass from the law till all be fulfilled
hosoever therefore shall break one of these least commandments and shall teach men so he shall be called the least in the kingdom of heaven but whosoever shall do and teach them the same shall be called great in the kingdom of heaven
or  say unto you hat except your righteousness shall exceed the righteousness of the scribes and harisees ye shall in no case enter into the kingdom of heaven
e have heard that it was said by them of old time hou shalt not kill and whosoever shall kill shall be in danger of the judgment but  say unto you hat whosoever is angry with his brother without a cause shall be in danger of the judgment and whosoever shall say to his brother aca shall be in danger of the council but whosoever shall say hou fool shall be in danger of hell fire
herefore if thou bring thy gift to the altar and there rememberest that thy brother hath ought against thee leave there thy gift before the altar and go thy way first be reconciled to thy brother and then come and offer thy gift
gree with thine adversary quickly whiles thou art in the way with him lest at any time the adversary deliver thee to the judge and the judge deliver thee to the officer and thou be cast into prison
erily  say unto thee hou shalt by no means come out thence till thou hast paid the uttermost farthing
e have heard that it was said by them of old time hou shalt not commit adultery but  say unto you hat whosoever looketh on a woman to lust after her hath committed adultery with her already in his heart
nd if thy right eye offend thee pluck it out and cast it from thee for it is profitable for thee that one of thy members should perish and not that thy whole body should be cast into hell
nd if thy right hand offend thee cut it off and cast it from thee for it is profitable for thee that one of thy members should perish and not that thy whole body should be cast into hell
t hath been said hosoever shall put away his wife let him give her a writing of divorcement but  say unto you hat whosoever shall put away his wife saving for the cause of fornication causeth her to commit adultery and whosoever shall marry her that is divorced committeth adultery
gain ye have heard that it hath been said by them of old time hou shalt not forswear thyself but shalt perform unto the ord thine oaths but  say unto you wear not at all neither by heaven for it is ods throne nor by the earth for it is his footstool neither by erusalem for it is the city of the great ing
either shalt thou swear by thy head because thou canst not make one hair white or black
ut let your communication be ea yea ay nay for whatsoever is more than these cometh of evil
e have heard that it hath been said n eye for an eye and a tooth for a tooth but  say unto you hat ye resist not evil but whosoever shall smite thee on thy right cheek turn to him the other also
nd if any man will sue thee at the law and take away thy coat let him have thy cloke also
nd whosoever shall compel thee to go a mile go with him twain
ive to him that asketh thee and from him that would borrow of thee turn not thou away
e have heard that it hath been said hou shalt love thy neighbour and hate thine enemy
ut  say unto you ove your enemies bless them that curse you do good to them that hate you and pray for them which despitefully use you and persecute you that ye may be the children of your ather which is in heaven for he maketh his sun to rise on the evil and on the good and sendeth rain on the just and on the unjust
or if ye love them which love you what reward have ye do not even the publicans the same
nd if ye salute your brethren only what do ye more than others do not even the publicans so
e ye therefore perfect even as your ather which is in heaven is perfect
ake heed that ye do not your alms before men to be seen of them otherwise ye have no reward of your ather which is in heaven
herefore when thou doest thine alms do not sound a trumpet before thee as the hypocrites do in the synagogues and in the streets that they may have glory of men
erily  say unto you hey have their reward
ut when thou doest alms let not thy left hand know what thy right hand doeth that thine alms may be in secret and thy ather which seeth in secret himself shall reward thee openly
nd when thou prayest thou shalt not be as the hypocrites are for they love to pray standing in the synagogues and in the corners of the streets that they may be seen of men
erily  say unto you hey have their reward
ut thou when thou prayest enter into thy closet and when thou hast shut thy door pray to thy ather which is in secret and thy ather which seeth in secret shall reward thee openly
ut when ye pray use not vain repetitions as the heathen do for they think that they shall be heard for their much speaking
e not ye therefore like unto them for your ather knoweth what things ye have need of before ye ask him
fter this manner therefore pray ye ur ather which art in heaven allowed be thy name
hy kingdom come
hy will be done in earth as it is in heaven
ive us this day our daily bread
nd forgive us our debts as we forgive our debtors
nd lead us not into temptation but deliver us from evil or thine is the kingdom and the power and the glory for ever
men
or if ye forgive men their trespasses your heavenly ather will also forgive you but if ye forgive not men their trespasses neither will your ather forgive your trespasses
oreover when ye fast be not as the hypocrites of a sad countenance for they disfigure their faces that they may appear unto men to fast
erily  say unto you hey have their reward
ut thou when thou fastest anoint thine head and wash thy face that thou appear not unto men to fast but unto thy ather which is in secret and thy ather which seeth in secret shall reward thee openly
ay not up for yourselves treasures upon earth where moth and rust doth corrupt and where thieves break through and steal but lay up for yourselves treasures in heaven where neither moth nor rust doth corrupt and where thieves do not break through nor steal for where your treasure is there will your heart be also
he light of the body is the eye if therefore thine eye be single thy whole body shall be full of light
ut if thine eye be evil thy whole body shall be full of darkness
f therefore the light that is in thee be darkness how great is that darkness
o man can serve two masters for either he will hate the one and love the other or else he will hold to the one and despise the other
e cannot serve od and mammon
herefore  say unto you ake no thought for your life what ye shall eat or what ye shall drink nor yet for your body what ye shall put on
s not the life more than meat and the body than raiment
ehold the fowls of the air for they sow not neither do they reap nor gather into barns yet your heavenly ather feedeth them
re ye not much better than they
hich of you by taking thought can add one cubit unto his stature
nd why take ye thought for raiment
onsider the lilies of the field how they grow they toil not neither do they spin and yet  say unto you hat even olomon in all his glory was not arrayed like one of these
herefore if od so clothe the grass of the field which to day is and to morrow is cast into the oven shall he not much more clothe you  ye of little faith
herefore take no thought saying hat shall we eat or hat shall we drink or herewithal shall we be clothed
or after all these things do the entiles seek for your heavenly ather knoweth that ye have need of all these things
ut seek ye first the kingdom of od and his righteousness and all these things shall be added unto you
ake therefore no thought for the morrow for the morrow shall take thought for the things of itself
ufficient unto the day is the evil thereof
udge not that ye be not judged
or with what judgment ye judge ye shall be judged and with what measure ye mete it shall be measured to you again
nd why beholdest thou the mote that is in thy brothers eye but considerest not the beam that is in thine own eye
r how wilt thou say to thy brother et me pull out the mote out of thine eye and behold a beam is in thine own eye
hou hypocrite first cast out the beam out of thine own eye and then shalt thou see clearly to cast out the mote out of thy brothers eye
ive not that which is holy unto the dogs neither cast ye your pearls before swine lest they trample them under their feet and turn again and rend you
sk and it shall be given you seek and ye shall find knock and it shall be opened unto you for every one that asketh receiveth and he that seeketh findeth and to him that knocketh it shall be opened
r what man is there of you whom if his son ask bread will he give him a stone
r if he ask a fish will he give him a serpent
f ye then being evil know how to give good gifts unto your children how much more shall your ather which is in heaven give good things to them that ask him
herefore all things whatsoever ye would that men should do to you do ye even so to them for this is the law and the prophets
nter ye in at the strait gate for wide is the gate and broad is the way that leadeth to destruction and many there be which go in thereat because strait is the gate and narrow is the way which leadeth unto life and few there be that find it
eware of false prophets which come to you in sheeps clothing but inwardly they are ravening wolves
e shall know them by their fruits
o men gather grapes of thorns or figs of thistles
ven so every good tree bringeth forth good fruit but a corrupt tree bringeth forth evil fruit
 good tree cannot bring forth evil fruit neither can a corrupt tree bring forth good fruit
very tree that bringeth not forth good fruit is hewn down and cast into the fire
herefore by their fruits ye shall know them
ot every one that saith unto me ord ord shall enter into the kingdom of heaven but he that doeth the will of my ather which is in heaven
any will say to me in that day ord ord have we not prophesied in thy name and in thy name have cast out devils and in thy name done many wonderful works
nd then will  profess unto them  never knew you depart from me ye that work iniquity
herefore whosoever heareth these sayings of mine and doeth them  will liken him unto a wise man which built his house upon a rock and the rain descended and the floods came and the winds blew and beat upon that house and it fell not for it was founded upon a rock
nd every one that heareth these sayings of mine and doeth them not shall be likened unto a foolish man which built his house upon the sand and the rain descended and the floods came and the winds blew and beat upon that house and it fell and great was the fall of it
nd it came to pass when esus had ended these sayings the people were astonished at his doctrine or he taught them as one having authority and not as the scribes
hen he was come down from the mountain great multitudes followed him
nd behold there came a leper and worshipped him saying ord if thou wilt thou canst make me clean
nd esus put forth his hand and touched him saying  will be thou clean
nd immediately his leprosy was cleansed
nd esus saith unto him ee thou tell no man but go thy way shew thyself to the priest and offer the gift that oses commanded for a testimony unto them
nd when esus was entered into apernaum there came unto him a centurion beseeching him and saying ord my servant lieth at home sick of the palsy grievously tormented
nd esus saith unto him  will come and heal him
he centurion answered and said ord  am not worthy that thou shouldest come under my roof but speak the word only and my servant shall be healed
or  am a man under authority having soldiers under me and  say to this man o and he goeth and to another ome and he cometh and to my servant o this and he doeth it
hen esus heard it he marvelled and said to them that followed erily  say unto you  have not found so great faith no not in srael
nd  say unto you hat many shall come from the east and west and shall sit down with braham and saac and acob in the kingdom of heaven
ut the children of the kingdom shall be cast out into outer darkness there shall be weeping and gnashing of teeth
nd esus said unto the centurion o thy way and as thou hast believed so be it done unto thee
nd his servant was healed in the selfsame hour
nd when esus was come into eters house he saw his wifes mother laid and sick of a fever
nd he touched her hand and the fever left her and she arose and ministered unto them
hen the even was come they brought unto him many that were possessed with devils and he cast out the spirits with his word and healed all that were sick that it might be fulfilled which was spoken by saias the prophet saying imself took our infirmities and bare our sicknesses
ow when esus saw great multitudes about him he gave commandment to depart unto the other side
nd a certain scribe came and said unto him aster  will follow thee whithersoever thou goest
nd esus saith unto him he foxes have holes and the birds of the air have nests but the on of man hath not where to lay his head
nd another of his disciples said unto him ord suffer me first to go and bury my father
ut esus said unto him ollow me and let the dead bury their dead
nd when he was entered into a ship his disciples followed him
nd behold there arose a great tempest in the sea insomuch that the ship was covered with the waves but he was asleep
nd his disciples came to him and awoke him saying ord save us we perish
nd he saith unto them hy are ye fearful  ye of little faith
hen he arose and rebuked the winds and the sea and there was a great calm
ut the men marvelled saying hat manner of man is this that even the winds and the sea obey him
nd when he was come to the other side into the country of the ergesenes there met him two possessed with devils coming out of the tombs exceeding fierce so that no man might pass by that way
nd behold they cried out saying hat have we to do with thee esus thou on of od art thou come hither to torment us before the time
nd there was a good way off from them an herd of many swine feeding
o the devils besought him saying f thou cast us out suffer us to go away into the herd of swine
nd he said unto them o
nd when they were come out they went into the herd of swine and behold the whole herd of swine ran violently down a steep place into the sea and perished in the waters
nd they that kept them fled and went their ways into the city and told every thing and what was befallen to the possessed of the devils
nd behold the whole city came out to meet esus and when they saw him they besought him that he would depart out of their coasts
nd he entered into a ship and passed over and came into his own city
nd behold they brought to him a man sick of the palsy lying on a bed and esus seeing their faith said unto the sick of the palsy on be of good cheer thy sins be forgiven thee
nd behold certain of the scribes said within themselves his man blasphemeth
nd esus knowing their thoughts said herefore think ye evil in your hearts
or whether is easier to say hy sins be forgiven thee or to say rise and walk
ut that ye may know that the on of man hath power on earth to forgive sins then saith he to the sick of the palsy  rise take up thy bed and go unto thine house
nd he arose and departed to his house
ut when the multitudes saw it they marvelled and glorified od which had given such power unto men
nd as esus passed forth from thence he saw a man named atthew sitting at the receipt of custom and he saith unto him ollow me
nd he arose and followed him
nd it came to pass as esus sat at meat in the house behold many publicans and sinners came and sat down with him and his disciples
nd when the harisees saw it they said unto his disciples hy eateth your aster with publicans and sinners
ut when esus heard that he said unto them hey that be whole need not a physician but they that are sick
ut go ye and learn what that meaneth  will have mercy and not sacrifice for  am not come to call the righteous but sinners to repentance
hen came to him the disciples of ohn saying hy do we and the harisees fast oft but thy disciples fast not
nd esus said unto them an the children of the bridechamber mourn as long as the bridegroom is with them but the days will come when the bridegroom shall be taken from them and then shall they fast
o man putteth a piece of new cloth unto an old garment for that which is put in to fill it up taketh from the garment and the rent is made worse
either do men put new wine into old bottles else the bottles break and the wine runneth out and the bottles perish but they put new wine into new bottles and both are preserved
hile he spake these things unto them behold there came a certain ruler and worshipped him saying y daughter is even now dead but come and lay thy hand upon her and she shall live
nd esus arose and followed him and so did his disciples
nd behold a woman which was diseased with an issue of blood twelve years came behind him and touched the hem of his garment for she said within herself f  may but touch his garment  shall be whole
ut esus turned him about and when he saw her he said aughter be of good comfort thy faith hath made thee whole
nd the woman was made whole from that hour
nd when esus came into the rulers house and saw the minstrels and the people making a noise he said unto them ive place for the maid is not dead but sleepeth
nd they laughed him to scorn
ut when the people were put forth he went in and took her by the hand and the maid arose
nd the fame hereof went abroad into all that land
nd when esus departed thence two blind men followed him crying and saying hou on of avid have mercy on us
nd when he was come into the house the blind men came to him and esus saith unto them elieve ye that  am able to do this
hey said unto him ea ord
hen touched he their eyes saying ccording to your faith be it unto you
nd their eyes were opened and esus straitly charged them saying ee that no man know it
ut they when they were departed spread abroad his fame in all that country
s they went out behold they brought to him a dumb man possessed with a devil
nd when the devil was cast out the dumb spake and the multitudes marvelled saying t was never so seen in srael
ut the harisees said e casteth out devils through the prince of the devils
nd esus went about all the cities and villages teaching in their synagogues and preaching the gospel of the kingdom and healing every sickness and every disease among the people
ut when he saw the multitudes he was moved with compassion on them because they fainted and were scattered abroad as sheep having no shepherd
hen saith he unto his disciples he harvest truly is plenteous but the labourers are few ray ye therefore the ord of the harvest that he will send forth labourers into his harvest
nd when he had called unto him his twelve disciples he gave them power against unclean spirits to cast them out and to heal all manner of sickness and all manner of disease
ow the names of the twelve apostles are these he first imon who is called eter and ndrew his brother ames the son of ebedee and ohn his brother hilip and artholomew homas and atthew the publican ames the son of lph us and ebb us whose surname was hadd us imon the anaanite and udas scariot who also betrayed him
hese twelve esus sent forth and commanded them saying o not into the way of the entiles and into any city of the amaritans enter ye not but go rather to the lost sheep of the house of srael
nd as ye go preach saying he kingdom of heaven is at hand
eal the sick cleanse the lepers raise the dead cast out devils freely ye have received freely give
rovide neither gold nor silver nor brass in your purses nor scrip for your journey neither two coats neither shoes nor yet staves for the workman is worthy of his meat
nd into whatsoever city or town ye shall enter enquire who in it is worthy and there abide till ye go thence
nd when ye come into an house salute it
nd if the house be worthy let your peace come upon it but if it be not worthy let your peace return to you
nd whosoever shall not receive you nor hear your words when ye depart out of that house or city shake off the dust of your feet
erily  say unto you t shall be more tolerable for the land of odom and omorrha in the day of judgment than for that city
ehold  send you forth as sheep in the midst of wolves be ye therefore wise as serpents and harmless as doves
ut beware of men for they will deliver you up to the councils and they will scourge you in their synagogues and ye shall be brought before governors and kings for my sake for a testimony against them and the entiles
ut when they deliver you up take no thought how or what ye shall speak for it shall be given you in that same hour what ye shall speak
or it is not ye that speak but the pirit of your ather which speaketh in you
nd the brother shall deliver up the brother to death and the father the child and the children shall rise up against their parents and cause them to be put to death
nd ye shall be hated of all men for my names sake but he that endureth to the end shall be saved
ut when they persecute you in this city flee ye into another for verily  say unto you e shall not have gone over the cities of srael till the on of man be come
he disciple is not above his master nor the servant above his lord
t is enough for the disciple that he be as his master and the servant as his lord
f they have called the master of the house eelzebub how much more shall they call them of his household
ear them not therefore for there is nothing covered that shall not be revealed and hid that shall not be known
hat  tell you in darkness that speak ye in light and what ye hear in the ear that preach ye upon the housetops
nd fear not them which kill the body but are not able to kill the soul but rather fear him which is able to destroy both soul and body in hell
re not two sparrows sold for a farthing and one of them shall not fall on the ground without your ather
ut the very hairs of your head are all numbered
ear ye not therefore ye are of more value than many sparrows
hosoever therefore shall confess me before men him will  confess also before my ather which is in heaven
ut whosoever shall deny me before men him will  also deny before my ather which is in heaven
hink not that  am come to send peace on earth  came not to send peace but a sword
or  am come to set a man at variance against his father and the daughter against her mother and the daughter in law against her mother in law
nd a mans foes shall be they of his own household
e that loveth father or mother more than me is not worthy of me and he that loveth son or daughter more than me is not worthy of me
nd he that taketh not his cross and followeth after me is not worthy of me
e that findeth his life shall lose it and he that loseth his life for my sake shall find it
e that receiveth you receiveth me and he that receiveth me receiveth him that sent me
e that receiveth a prophet in the name of a prophet shall receive a prophets reward and he that receiveth a righteous man in the name of a righteous man shall receive a righteous mans reward
nd whosoever shall give to drink unto one of these little ones a cup of cold water only in the name of a disciple verily  say unto you he shall in no wise lose his reward
nd it came to pass when esus had made an end of commanding his twelve disciples he departed thence to teach and to preach in their cities
ow when ohn had heard in the prison the works of hrist he sent two of his disciples and said unto him rt thou he that should come or do we look for another
esus answered and said unto them o and shew ohn again those things which ye do hear and see the blind receive their sight and the lame walk the lepers are cleansed and the deaf hear the dead are raised up and the poor have the gospel preached to them
nd blessed is he whosoever shall not be offended in me
nd as they departed esus began to say unto the multitudes concerning ohn hat went ye out into the wilderness to see
 reed shaken with the wind
ut what went ye out for to see
 man clothed in soft raiment behold they that wear soft clothing are in kings houses
ut what went ye out for to see
 prophet yea  say unto you and more than a prophet
or this is he of whom it is written ehold  send my messenger before thy face hich shall prepare thy way before thee
erily  say unto you mong them that are born of women there hath not risen a greater than ohn the aptist notwithstanding he that is least in the kingdom of heaven is greater than he
nd from the days of ohn the aptist until now the kingdom of heaven suffereth violence and the violent take it by force
or all the prophets and the law prophesied until ohn
nd if ye will receive it this is lias which was for to come
e that hath ears to hear let him hear
ut whereunto shall  liken this generation
t is like unto children sitting in the markets and calling unto their fellows and saying e have piped unto you and ye have not danced we have mourned unto you and ye have not lamented
or ohn came neither eating nor drinking and they say e hath a devil
he on of man came eating and drinking and they say ehold a man gluttonous and a winebibber a friend of publicans and sinners
ut wisdom is justified of her children
hen began he to upbraid the cities wherein most of his mighty works were done because they repented not oe unto thee horazin woe unto thee ethsaida for if the mighty works which were done in you had been done in yre and idon they would have repented long ago in sackcloth and ashes
ut  say unto you t shall be more tolerable for yre and idon at the day of judgment than for you
nd thou apernaum which art exalted unto heaven shalt be brought down to hell for if the mighty works which have been done in thee had been done in odom it would have remained until this day
ut  say unto you hat it shall be more tolerable for the land of odom in the day of judgment than for thee
t that time esus answered and said  thank thee  ather ord of heaven and earth because thou hast hid these things from the wise and prudent and hast revealed them unto babes
ven so ather for so it seemed good in thy sight
ll things are delivered unto me of my ather and no man knoweth the on but the ather neither knoweth any man the ather save the on and he to whomsoever the on will reveal him
ome unto me all ye that labour and are heavy laden and  will give you rest
ake my yoke upon you and learn of me for  am meek and lowly in heart and ye shall find rest unto your souls
or my yoke is easy and my burden is light
t that time esus went on the sabbath day through the corn and his disciples were an hungred and began to pluck the ears of corn and to eat
ut when the harisees saw it they said unto him ehold thy disciples do that which is not lawful to do upon the sabbath day
ut he said unto them ave ye not read what avid did when he was an hungred and they that were with him how he entered into the house of od and did eat the shewbread which was not lawful for him to eat neither for them which were with him but only for the priests
r have ye not read in the law how that on the sabbath days the priests in the temple profane the sabbath and are blameless
ut  say unto you hat in this place is one greater than the temple
ut if ye had known what this meaneth  will have mercy and not sacrifice ye would not have condemned the guiltless
or the on of man is ord even of the sabbath day
nd when he was departed thence he went into their synagogue and behold there was a man which had his hand withered
nd they asked him saying s it lawful to heal on the sabbath days that they might accuse him
nd he said unto them hat man shall there be among you that shall have one sheep and if it fall into a pit on the sabbath day will he not lay hold on it and lift it out
ow much then is a man better than a sheep
herefore it is lawful to do well on the sabbath days
hen saith he to the man tretch forth thine hand
nd he stretched it forth and it was restored whole like as the other
hen the harisees went out and held a council against him how they might destroy him
ut when esus knew it he withdrew himself from thence and great multitudes followed him and he healed them all and charged them that they should not make him known that it might be fulfilled which was spoken by saias the prophet saying ehold my servant whom  have chosen y beloved in whom my soul is well pleased  will put my spirit upon him nd he shall shew judgment to the entiles
e shall not strive nor cry either shall any man hear his voice in the streets
 bruised reed shall he not break nd smoking flax shall he not quench ill he send forth judgment unto victory
nd in his name shall the entiles trust
hen was brought unto him one possessed with a devil blind and dumb and he healed him insomuch that the blind and dumb both spake and saw
nd all the people were amazed and said s not this the son of avid
ut when the harisees heard it they said his fellow doth not cast out devils but by eelzebub the prince of the devils
nd esus knew their thoughts and said unto them very kingdom divided against itself is brought to desolation and every city or house divided against itself shall not stand and if atan cast out atan he is divided against himself how shall then his kingdom stand
nd if  by eelzebub cast out devils by whom do your children cast them out therefore they shall be your judges
ut if  cast out devils by the pirit of od then the kingdom of od is come unto you
r else how can one enter into a strong mans house and spoil his goods except he first bind the strong man and then he will spoil his house
e that is not with me is against me and he that gathereth not with me scattereth abroad
herefore  say unto you ll manner of sin and blasphemy shall be forgiven unto men but the blasphemy against the oly host shall not be forgiven unto men
nd whosoever speaketh a word against the on of man it shall be forgiven him but whosoever speaketh against the oly host it shall not be forgiven him neither in this world neither in the world to come
ither make the tree good and his fruit good or else make the tree corrupt and his fruit corrupt for the tree is known by his fruit
 generation of vipers how can ye being evil speak good things for out of the abundance of the heart the mouth speaketh
 good man out of the good treasure of the heart bringeth forth good things and an evil man out of the evil treasure bringeth forth evil things
ut  say unto you hat every idle word that men shall speak they shall give account thereof in the day of judgment
or by thy words thou shalt be justified and by thy words thou shalt be condemned
hen certain of the scribes and of the harisees answered saying aster we would see a sign from thee
ut he answered and said unto them n evil and adulterous generation seeketh after a sign and there shall no sign be given to it but the sign of the prophet onas for as onas was three days and three nights in the whales belly so shall the on of man be three days and three nights in the heart of the earth
he men of ineveh shall rise in judgment with this generation and shall condemn it because they repented at the preaching of onas and behold a greater than onas is here
he queen of the south shall rise up in the judgment with this generation and shall condemn it for she came from the uttermost parts of the earth to hear the wisdom of olomon and behold a greater than olomon is here
hen the unclean spirit is gone out of a man he walketh through dry places seeking rest and findeth none
hen he saith  will return into my house from whence  came out and when he is come he findeth it empty swept and garnished
hen goeth he and taketh with himself seven other spirits more wicked than himself and they enter in and dwell there and the last state of that man is worse than the first
ven so shall it be also unto this wicked generation
hile he yet talked to the people behold his mother and his brethren stood without desiring to speak with him
hen one said unto him ehold thy mother and thy brethren stand without desiring to speak with thee
ut he answered and said unto him that told him ho is my mother and who are my brethren
nd he stretched forth his hand toward his disciples and said ehold my mother and my brethren
or whosoever shall do the will of my ather which is in heaven the same is my brother and sister and mother
he same day went esus out of the house and sat by the sea side
nd great multitudes were gathered together unto him so that he went into a ship and sat and the whole multitude stood on the shore
nd he spake many things unto them in parables saying ehold a sower went forth to sow and when he sowed some seeds fell by the way side and the fowls came and devoured them up some fell upon stony places where they had not much earth and forthwith they sprung up because they had no deepness of earth and when the sun was up they were scorched and because they had no root they withered away
nd some fell among thorns and the thorns sprung up and choked them but other fell into good ground and brought forth fruit some an hundredfold some sixtyfold some thirtyfold
ho hath ears to hear let him hear
nd the disciples came and said unto him hy speakest thou unto them in parables
e answered and said unto them ecause it is given unto you to know the mysteries of the kingdom of heaven but to them it is not given
or whosoever hath to him shall be given and he shall have more abundance but whosoever hath not from him shall be taken away even that he hath
herefore speak  to them in parables because they seeing see not and hearing they hear not neither do they understand
nd in them is fulfilled the prophecy of saias which saith y hearing ye shall hear and shall not understand nd seeing ye shall see and shall not perceive or this peoples heart is waxed gross nd their ears are dull of hearing nd their eyes they have closed est at any time they should see with their eyes nd hear with their ears nd should understand with their heart nd should be converted and  should heal them
ut blessed are your eyes for they see and your ears for they hear
or verily  say unto you hat many prophets and righteous men have desired to see those things which ye see and have not seen them and to hear those things which ye hear and have not heard them
ear ye therefore the parable of the sower
hen any one heareth the word of the kingdom and understandeth it not then cometh the wicked one and catcheth away that which was sown in his heart
his is he which received seed by the way side
ut he that received the seed into stony places the same is he that heareth the word and anon with joy receiveth it yet hath he not root in himself but dureth for a while for when tribulation or persecution ariseth because of the word by and by he is offended
e also that received seed among the thorns is he that heareth the word and the care of this world and the deceitfulness of riches choke the word and he becometh unfruitful
ut he that received seed into the good ground is he that heareth the word and understandeth it which also beareth fruit and bringeth forth some an hundredfold some sixty some thirty
nother parable put he forth unto them saying he kingdom of heaven is likened unto a man which sowed good seed in his field but while men slept his enemy came and sowed tares among the wheat and went his way
ut when the blade was sprung up and brought forth fruit then appeared the tares also
o the servants of the householder came and said unto him ir didst not thou sow good seed in thy field from whence then hath it tares
e said unto them n enemy hath done this
he servants said unto him ilt thou then that we go and gather them up
ut he said ay lest while ye gather up the tares ye root up also the wheat with them
et both grow together until the harvest and in the time of harvest  will say to the reapers ather ye together first the tares and bind them in bundles to burn them but gather the wheat into my barn
nother parable put he forth unto them saying he kingdom of heaven is like to a grain of mustard seed which a man took and sowed in his field which indeed is the least of all seeds but when it is grown it is the greatest among herbs and becometh a tree so that the birds of the air come and lodge in the branches thereof
nother parable spake he unto them he kingdom of heaven is like unto leaven which a woman took and hid in three measures of meal till the whole was leavened
ll these things spake esus unto the multitude in parables and without a parable spake he not unto them hat it might be fulfilled which was spoken by the prophet saying  will open my mouth in parables  will utter things which have been kept secret from the foundation of the world
hen esus sent the multitude away and went into the house and his disciples came unto him saying eclare unto us the parable of the tares of the field
e answered and said unto them e that soweth the good seed is the on of man the field is the world the good seed are the children of the kingdom but the tares are the children of the wicked one the enemy that sowed them is the devil the harvest is the end of the world and the reapers are the angels
s therefore the tares are gathered and burned in the fire so shall it be in the end of this world
he on of man shall send forth his angels and they shall gather out of his kingdom all things that offend and them which do iniquity and shall cast them into a furnace of fire there shall be wailing and gnashing of teeth
hen shall the righteous shine forth as the sun in the kingdom of their ather
ho hath ears to hear let him hear
gain the kingdom of heaven is like unto treasure hid in a field the which when a man hath found he hideth and for joy thereof goeth and selleth all that he hath and buyeth that field
gain the kingdom of heaven is like unto a merchant man seeking goodly pearls who when he had found one pearl of great price went and sold all that he had and bought it
gain the kingdom of heaven is like unto a net that was cast into the sea and gathered of every kind which when it was full they drew to shore and sat down and gathered the good into vessels but cast the bad away
o shall it be at the end of the world the angels shall come forth and sever the wicked from among the just and shall cast them into the furnace of fire there shall be wailing and gnashing of teeth
esus saith unto them ave ye understood all these things
hey say unto him ea ord
hen said he unto them herefore every scribe which is instructed unto the kingdom of heaven is like unto a man that is an householder which bringeth forth out of his treasure things new and old
nd it came to pass that when esus had finished these parables he departed thence
nd when he was come into his own country he taught them in their synagogue insomuch that they were astonished and said hence hath this man this wisdom and these mighty works
s not this the carpenters son is not his mother called ary and his brethren ames and oses and imon and udas
nd his sisters are they not all with us
hence then hath this man all these things
nd they were offended in him
ut esus said unto them  prophet is not without honour save in his own country and in his own house
nd he did not many mighty works there because of their unbelief
t that time erod the tetrarch heard of the fame of esus and said unto his servants his is ohn the aptist he is risen from the dead and therefore mighty works do shew forth themselves in him
or erod had laid hold on ohn and bound him and put him in prison for erodias sake his brother hilips wife
or ohn said unto him t is not lawful for thee to have her
nd when he would have put him to death he feared the multitude because they counted him as a prophet
ut when erods birthday was kept the daughter of erodias danced before them and pleased erod
hereupon he promised with an oath to give her whatsoever she would ask
nd she being before instructed of her mother said ive me here ohn aptists head in a charger
nd the king was sorry nevertheless for the oaths sake and them which sat with him at meat he commanded it to be given her
nd he sent and beheaded ohn in the prison
nd his head was brought in a charger and given to the damsel and she brought it to her mother
nd his disciples came and took up the body and buried it and went and told esus
hen esus heard of it he departed thence by ship into a desert place apart and when the people had heard thereof they followed him on foot out of the cities
nd esus went forth and saw a great multitude and was moved with compassion toward them and he healed their sick
nd when it was evening his disciples came to him saying his is a desert place and the time is now past send the multitude away that they may go into the villages and buy themselves victuals
ut esus said unto them hey need not depart give ye them to eat
nd they say unto him e have here but five loaves and two fishes
e said ring them hither to me
nd he commanded the multitude to sit down on the grass and took the five loaves and the two fishes and looking up to heaven he blessed and brake and gave the loaves to his disciples and the disciples to the multitude
nd they did all eat and were filled and they took up of the fragments that remained twelve baskets full
nd they that had eaten were about five thousand men beside women and children
nd straightway esus constrained his disciples to get into a ship and to go before him unto the other side while he sent the multitudes away
nd when he had sent the multitudes away he went up into a mountain apart to pray and when the evening was come he was there alone
ut the ship was now in the midst of the sea tossed with waves for the wind was contrary
nd in the fourth watch of the night esus went unto them walking on the sea
nd when the disciples saw him walking on the sea they were troubled saying t is a spirit and they cried out for fear
ut straightway esus spake unto them saying e of good cheer it is  be not afraid
nd eter answered him and said ord if it be thou bid me come unto thee on the water
nd he said ome
nd when eter was come down out of the ship he walked on the water to go to esus
ut when he saw the wind boisterous he was afraid and beginning to sink he cried saying ord save me
nd immediately esus stretched forth his hand and caught him and said unto him  thou of little faith wherefore didst thou doubt
nd when they were come into the ship the wind ceased
hen they that were in the ship came and worshipped him saying f a truth thou art the on of od
nd when they were gone over they came into the land of ennesaret
nd when the men of that place had knowledge of him they sent out into all that country round about and brought unto him all that were diseased and besought him that they might only touch the hem of his garment and as many as touched were made perfectly whole
hen came to esus scribes and harisees which were of erusalem saying hy do thy disciples transgress the tradition of the elders for they wash not their hands when they eat bread
ut he answered and said unto them hy do ye also transgress the commandment of od by your tradition
or od commanded saying onour thy father and mother and e that curseth father or mother let him die the death
ut ye say hosoever shall say to his father or his mother t is a gift by whatsoever thou mightest be profited by me and honour not his father or his mother he shall be free
hus have ye made the commandment of od of none effect by your tradition
e hypocrites well did saias prophesy of you saying his people draweth nigh unto me with their mouth and honoureth me with their lips ut their heart is far from me
ut in vain they do worship me eaching for doctrines the commandments of men
nd he called the multitude and said unto them ear and understand not that which goeth into the mouth defileth a man but that which cometh out of the mouth this defileth a man
hen came his disciples and said unto him nowest thou that the harisees were offended after they heard this saying
ut he answered and said very plant which my heavenly ather hath not planted shall be rooted up
et them alone they be blind leaders of the blind
nd if the blind lead the blind both shall fall into the ditch
hen answered eter and said unto him eclare unto us this parable
nd esus said re ye also yet without understanding
o not ye yet understand that whatsoever entereth in at the mouth goeth into the belly and is cast out into the draught
ut those things which proceed out of the mouth come forth from the heart and they defile the man
or out of the heart proceed evil thoughts murders adulteries fornications thefts false witness blasphemies these are the things which defile a man but to eat with unwashen hands defileth not a man
hen esus went thence and departed into the coasts of yre and idon
nd behold a woman of anaan came out of the same coasts and cried unto him saying ave mercy on me  ord thou on of avid my daughter is grievously vexed with a devil
ut he answered her not a word
nd his disciples came and besought him saying end her away for she crieth after us
ut he answered and said  am not sent but unto the lost sheep of the house of srael
hen came she and worshipped him saying ord help me
ut he answered and said t is not meet to take the childrens bread and to cast it to dogs
nd she said ruth ord yet the dogs eat of the crumbs which fall from their masters table
hen esus answered and said unto her  woman great is thy faith be it unto thee even as thou wilt
nd her daughter was made whole from that very hour
nd esus departed from thence and came nigh unto the sea of alilee and went up into a mountain and sat down there
nd great multitudes came unto him having with them those that were lame blind dumb maimed and many others and cast them down at esus feet and he healed them insomuch that the multitude wondered when they saw the dumb to speak the maimed to be whole the lame to walk and the blind to see and they glorified the od of srael
hen esus called his disciples unto him and said  have compassion on the multitude because they continue with me now three days and have nothing to eat and  will not send them away fasting lest they faint in the way
nd his disciples say unto him hence should we have so much bread in the wilderness as to fill so great a multitude
nd esus saith unto them ow many loaves have ye
nd they said even and a few little fishes
nd he commanded the multitude to sit down on the ground
nd he took the seven loaves and the fishes and gave thanks and brake them and gave to his disciples and the disciples to the multitude
nd they did all eat and were filled and they took up of the broken meat that was left seven baskets full
nd they that did eat were four thousand men beside women and children
nd he sent away the multitude and took ship and came into the coasts of agdala
he harisees also with the adducees came and tempting desired him that he would shew them a sign from heaven
e answered and said unto them hen it is evening ye say t will be fair weather for the sky is red
nd in the morning t will be foul weather to day for the sky is red and lowring
 ye hypocrites ye can discern the face of the sky but can ye not discern the signs of the times
 wicked and adulterous generation seeketh after a sign and there shall no sign be given unto it but the sign of the prophet onas
nd he left them and departed
nd when his disciples were come to the other side they had forgotten to take bread
hen esus said unto them ake heed and beware of the leaven of the harisees and of the adducees
nd they reasoned among themselves saying t is because we have taken no bread
hich when esus perceived he said unto them  ye of little faith why reason ye among yourselves because ye have brought no bread
o ye not yet understand neither remember the five loaves of the five thousand and how many baskets ye took up
either the seven loaves of the four thousand and how many baskets ye took up
ow is it that ye do not understand that  spake it not to you concerning bread that ye should beware of the leaven of the harisees and of the adducees
hen understood they how that he bade them not beware of the leaven of bread but of the doctrine of the harisees and of the adducees
hen esus came into the coasts of  sarea hilippi he asked his disciples saying hom do men say that  the on of man am
nd they said ome say that thou art ohn the aptist some lias and others eremias or one of the prophets
e saith unto them ut whom say ye that  am
nd imon eter answered and said hou art the hrist the on of the living od
nd esus answered and said unto him lessed art thou imon arjona for flesh and blood hath not revealed it unto thee but my ather which is in heaven
nd  say also unto thee hat thou art eter and upon this rock  will build my church and the gates of hell shall not prevail against it
nd  will give unto thee the keys of the kingdom of heaven and whatsoever thou shalt bind on earth shall be bound in heaven and whatsoever thou shalt loose on earth shall be loosed in heaven
hen charged he his disciples that they should tell no man that he was esus the hrist
rom that time forth began esus to shew unto his disciples how that he must go unto erusalem and suffer many things of the elders and chief priests and scribes and be killed and be raised again the third day
hen eter took him and began to rebuke him saying e it far from thee ord this shall not be unto thee
ut he turned and said unto eter et thee behind me atan thou art an offence unto me for thou savourest not the things that be of od but those that be of men
hen said esus unto his disciples f any man will come after me let him deny himself and take up his cross and follow me
or whosoever will save his life shall lose it and whosoever will lose his life for my sake shall find it
or what is a man profited if he shall gain the whole world and lose his own soul or what shall a man give in exchange for his soul
or the on of man shall come in the glory of his ather with his angels and then he shall reward every man according to his works
erily  say unto you here be some standing here which shall not taste of death till they see the on of man coming in his kingdom
nd after six days esus taketh eter ames and ohn his brother and bringeth them up into an high mountain apart and was transfigured before them and his face did shine as the sun and his raiment was white as the light
nd behold there appeared unto them oses and lias talking with him
hen answered eter and said unto esus ord it is good for us to be here if thou wilt let us make here three tabernacles one for thee and one for oses and one for lias
hile he yet spake behold a bright cloud overshadowed them and behold a voice out of the cloud which said his is my beloved on in whom  am well pleased hear ye him
nd when the disciples heard it they fell on their face and were sore afraid
nd esus came and touched them and said rise and be not afraid
nd when they had lifted up their eyes they saw no man save esus only
nd as they came down from the mountain esus charged them saying ell the vision to no man until the on of man be risen again from the dead
nd his disciples asked him saying hy then say the scribes that lias must first come
nd esus answered and said unto them lias truly shall first come and restore all things
ut  say unto you hat lias is come already and they knew him not but have done unto him whatsoever they listed
ikewise shall also the on of man suffer of them
hen the disciples understood that he spake unto them of ohn the aptist
nd when they were come to the multitude there came to him a certain man kneeling down to him and saying ord have mercy on my son for he is lunatick and sore vexed for ofttimes he falleth into the fire and oft into the water
nd  brought him to thy disciples and they could not cure him
hen esus answered and said  faithless and perverse generation how long shall  be with you how long shall  suffer you bring him hither to me
nd esus rebuked the devil and he departed out of him and the child was cured from that very hour
hen came the disciples to esus apart and said hy could not we cast him out
nd esus said unto them ecause of your unbelief for verily  say unto you f ye have faith as a grain of mustard seed ye shall say unto this mountain emove hence to yonder place and it shall remove and nothing shall be impossible unto you
owbeit this kind goeth not out but by prayer and fasting
nd while they abode in alilee esus said unto them he on of man shall be betrayed into the hands of men and they shall kill him and the third day he shall be raised again
nd they were exceeding sorry
nd when they were come to apernaum they that received tribute money came to eter and said oth not your master pay tribute
e saith es
nd when he was come into the house esus prevented him saying hat thinkest thou imon of whom do the kings of the earth take custom or tribute of their own children or of strangers
eter saith unto him f strangers
esus saith unto him hen are the children free
otwithstanding lest we should offend them go thou to the sea and cast an hook and take up the fish that first cometh up and when thou hast opened his mouth thou shalt find a piece of money that take and give unto them for me and thee
t the same time came the disciples unto esus saying ho is the greatest in the kingdom of heaven
nd esus called a little child unto him and set him in the midst of them and said erily  say unto you xcept ye be converted and become as little children ye shall not enter into the kingdom of heaven
hosoever therefore shall humble himself as this little child the same is greatest in the kingdom of heaven
nd whoso shall receive one such little child in my name receiveth me
ut whoso shall offend one of these little ones which believe in me it were better for him that a millstone were hanged about his neck and that he were drowned in the depth of the sea
oe unto the world because of offences for it must needs be that offences come but woe to that man by whom the offence cometh
herefore if thy hand or thy foot offend thee cut them off and cast them from thee it is better for thee to enter into life halt or maimed rather than having two hands or two feet to be cast into everlasting fire
nd if thine eye offend thee pluck it out and cast it from thee it is better for thee to enter into life with one eye rather than having two eyes to be cast into hell fire
ake heed that ye despise not one of these little ones for  say unto you hat in heaven their angels do always behold the face of my ather which is in heaven
or the on of man is come to save that which was lost
ow think ye if a man have an hundred sheep and one of them be gone astray doth he not leave the ninety and nine and goeth into the mountains and seeketh that which is gone astray
nd if so be that he find it verily  say unto you he rejoiceth more of that sheep than of the ninety and nine which went not astray
ven so it is not the will of your ather which is in heaven that one of these little ones should perish
oreover if thy brother shall trespass against thee go and tell him his fault between thee and him alone if he shall hear thee thou hast gained thy brother
ut if he will not hear thee then take with thee one or two more that in the mouth of two or three witnesses every word may be established
nd if he shall neglect to hear them tell it unto the church but if he neglect to hear the church let him be unto thee as an heathen man and a publican
erily  say unto you hatsoever ye shall bind on earth shall be bound in heaven and whatsoever ye shall loose on earth shall be loosed in heaven
gain  say unto you hat if two of you shall agree on earth as touching any thing that they shall ask it shall be done for them of my ather which is in heaven
or where two or three are gathered together in my name there am  in the midst of them
hen came eter to him and said ord how oft shall my brother sin against me and  forgive him till seven times
esus saith unto him  say not unto thee ntil seven times but ntil seventy times seven
herefore is the kingdom of heaven likened unto a certain king which would take account of his servants
nd when he had begun to reckon one was brought unto him which owed him ten thousand talents
ut forasmuch as he had not to pay his lord commanded him to be sold and his wife and children and all that he had and payment to be made
he servant therefore fell down and worshipped him saying ord have patience with me and  will pay thee all
hen the lord of that servant was moved with compassion and loosed him and forgave him the debt
ut the same servant went out and found one of his fellowservants which owed him an hundred pence and he laid hands on him and took him by the throat saying ay me that thou owest
nd his fellowservant fell down at his feet and besought him saying ave patience with me and  will pay thee all
nd he would not but went and cast him into prison till he should pay the debt
o when his fellowservants saw what was done they were very sorry and came and told unto their lord all that was done
hen his lord after that he had called him said unto him  thou wicked servant  forgave thee all that debt because thou desiredst me shouldest not thou also have had compassion on thy fellowservant even as  had pity on thee
nd his lord was wroth and delivered him to the tormentors till he should pay all that was due unto him
o likewise shall my heavenly ather do also unto you if ye from your hearts forgive not every one his brother their trespasses
nd it came to pass that when esus had finished these sayings he departed from alilee and came into the coasts of ud a beyond ordan nd great multitudes followed him and he healed them there
he harisees also came unto him tempting him and saying unto him s it lawful for a man to put away his wife for every cause
nd he answered and said unto them ave ye not read that he which made them at the beginning made them male and female and said or this cause shall a man leave father and mother and shall cleave to his wife and they twain shall be one flesh
herefore they are no more twain but one flesh
hat therefore od hath joined together let not man put asunder
hey say unto him hy did oses then command to give a writing of divorcement and to put her away
e saith unto them oses because of the hardness of your hearts suffered you to put away your wives but from the beginning it was not so
nd  say unto you hosoever shall put away his wife except it be for fornication and shall marry another committeth adultery and whoso marrieth her which is put away doth commit adultery
is disciples say unto him f the case of the man be so with his wife it is not good to marry
ut he said unto them ll men cannot receive this saying save they to whom it is given
or there are some eunuchs which were so born from their mothers womb and there are some eunuchs which were made eunuchs of men and there be eunuchs which have made themselves eunuchs for the kingdom of heavens sake
e that is able to receive it let him receive it
hen were there brought unto him little children that he should put his hands on them and pray and the disciples rebuked them
ut esus said uffer little children and forbid them not to come unto me for of such is the kingdom of heaven
nd he laid his hands on them and departed thence
nd behold one came and said unto him ood aster what good thing shall  do that  may have eternal life
nd he said unto him hy callest thou me good there is none good but one that is od but if thou wilt enter into life keep the commandments
e saith unto him hich
esus said hou shalt do no murder hou shalt not commit adultery hou shalt not steal hou shalt not bear false witness onour thy father and thy mother and hou shalt love thy neighbour as thyself
he young man saith unto him ll these things have  kept from my youth up what lack  yet
esus said unto him f thou wilt be perfect go and sell that thou hast and give to the poor and thou shalt have treasure in heaven and come and follow me
ut when the young man heard that saying he went away sorrowful for he had great possessions
hen said esus unto his disciples erily  say unto you hat a rich man shall hardly enter into the kingdom of heaven
nd again  say unto you t is easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle than for a rich man to enter into the kingdom of od
hen his disciples heard it they were exceedingly amazed saying ho then can be saved
ut esus beheld them and said unto them ith men this is impossible but with od all things are possible
hen answered eter and said unto him ehold we have forsaken all and followed thee what shall we have therefore
nd esus said unto them erily  say unto you hat ye which have followed me in the regeneration when the on of man shall sit in the throne of his glory ye also shall sit upon twelve thrones judging the twelve tribes of srael
nd every one that hath forsaken houses or brethren or sisters or father or mother or wife or children or lands for my names sake shall receive an hundredfold and shall inherit everlasting life
ut many that are first shall be last and the last shall be first
or the kingdom of heaven is like unto a man that is an householder which went out early in the morning to hire labourers into his vineyard
nd when he had agreed with the labourers for a penny a day he sent them into his vineyard
nd he went out about the third hour and saw others standing idle in the marketplace and said unto them o ye also into the vineyard and whatsoever is right  will give you
nd they went their way
gain he went out about the sixth and ninth hour and did likewise
nd about the eleventh hour he went out and found others standing idle and saith unto them hy stand ye here all the day idle
hey say unto him ecause no man hath hired us
e saith unto them o ye also into the vineyard and whatsoever is right that shall ye receive
o when even was come the lord of the vineyard saith unto his steward all the labourers and give them their hire beginning from the last unto the first
nd when they came that were hired about the eleventh hour they received every man a penny
ut when the first came they supposed that they should have received more and they likewise received every man a penny
nd when they had received it they murmured against the goodman of the house saying hese last have wrought but one hour and thou hast made them equal unto us which have borne the burden and heat of the day
ut he answered one of them and said riend  do thee no wrong didst not thou agree with me for a penny
ake that thine is and go thy way  will give unto this last even as unto thee
s it not lawful for me to do what  will with mine own
s thine eye evil because  am good
o the last shall be first and the first last for many be called but few chosen
nd esus going up to erusalem took the twelve disciples apart in the way and said unto them ehold we go up to erusalem and the on of man shall be betrayed unto the chief priests and unto the scribes and they shall condemn him to death and shall deliver him to the entiles to mock and to scourge and to crucify him and the third day he shall rise again
hen came to him the mother of ebedees children with her sons worshipping him and desiring a certain thing of him
nd he said unto her hat wilt thou
he saith unto him rant that these my two sons may sit the one on thy right hand and the other on the left in thy kingdom
ut esus answered and said e know not what ye ask
re ye able to drink of the cup that  shall drink of and to be baptized with the baptism that  am baptized with
hey say unto him e are able
nd he saith unto them e shall drink indeed of my cup and be baptized with the baptism that  am baptized with but to sit on my right hand and on my left is not mine to give but it shall be given to them for whom it is prepared of my ather
nd when the ten heard it they were moved with indignation against the two brethren
ut esus called them unto him and said e know that the princes of the entiles exercise dominion over them and they that are great exercise authority upon them
ut it shall not be so among you but whosoever will be great among you let him be your minister and whosoever will be chief among you let him be your servant even as the on of man came not to be ministered unto but to minister and to give his life a ransom for many
nd as they departed from ericho a great multitude followed him
nd behold two blind men sitting by the way side when they heard that esus passed by cried out saying ave mercy on us  ord thou on of avid
nd the multitude rebuked them because they should hold their peace but they cried the more saying ave mercy on us  ord thou on of avid
nd esus stood still and called them and said hat will ye that  shall do unto you
hey say unto him ord that our eyes may be opened
o esus had compassion on them and touched their eyes and immediately their eyes received sight and they followed him
nd when they drew nigh unto erusalem and were come to ethphage unto the mount of lives then sent esus two disciples saying unto them o into the village over against you and straightway ye shall find an ass tied and a colt with her loose them and bring them unto me
nd if any man say ought unto you ye shall say he ord hath need of them and straightway he will send them
ll this was done that it might be fulfilled which was spoken by the prophet saying ell ye the daughter of ion ehold thy ing cometh unto thee eek and sitting upon an ass nd a colt the foal of an ass
nd the disciples went and did as esus commanded them and brought the ass and the colt and put on them their clothes and they set him thereon
nd a very great multitude spread their garments in the way others cut down branches from the trees and strawed them in the way
nd the multitudes that went before and that followed cried saying osanna to the son of avid lessed is he that cometh in the name of the ord osanna in the highest
nd when he was come into erusalem all the city was moved saying ho is this
nd the multitude said his is esus the prophet of azareth of alilee
nd esus went into the temple of od and cast out all them that sold and bought in the temple and overthrew the tables of the moneychangers and the seats of them that sold doves and said unto them t is written y house shall be called the house of prayer but ye have made it a den of thieves
nd the blind and the lame came to him in the temple and he healed them
nd when the chief priests and scribes saw the wonderful things that he did and the children crying in the temple and saying osanna to the son of avid they were sore displeased and said unto him earest thou what these say
nd esus saith unto them ea have ye never read ut of the mouth of babes and sucklings thou hast perfected praise
nd he left them and went out of the city into ethany and he lodged there
ow in the morning as he returned into the city he hungered
nd when he saw a fig tree in the way he came to it and found nothing thereon but leaves only and said unto it et no fruit grow on thee henceforward for ever
nd presently the fig tree withered away
nd when the disciples saw it they marvelled saying ow soon is the fig tree withered away
esus answered and said unto them erily  say unto you f ye have faith and doubt not ye shall not only do this which is done to the fig tree but also if ye shall say unto this mountain e thou removed and be thou cast into the sea it shall be done
nd all things whatsoever ye shall ask in prayer believing ye shall receive
nd when he was come into the temple the chief priests and the elders of the people came unto him as he was teaching and said y what authority doest thou these things and who gave thee this authority
nd esus answered and said unto them  also will ask you one thing which if ye tell me  in like wise will tell you by what authority  do these things
he baptism of ohn whence was it from heaven or of men
nd they reasoned with themselves saying f we shall say rom heaven he will say unto us hy did ye not then believe him
ut if we shall say f men we fear the people for all hold ohn as a prophet
nd they answered esus and said e cannot tell
nd he said unto them either tell  you by what authority  do these things
ut what think ye
 certain man had two sons and he came to the first and said on go work to day in my vineyard
e answered and said  will not but afterward he repented and went
nd he came to the second and said likewise
nd he answered and said  go sir and went not
hether of them twain did the will of his father
hey say unto him he first
esus saith unto them erily  say unto you hat the publicans and the harlots go into the kingdom of od before you
or ohn came unto you in the way of righteousness and ye believed him not but the publicans and the harlots believed him and ye when ye had seen it repented not afterward that ye might believe him
ear another parable here was a certain householder which planted a vineyard and hedged it round about and digged a winepress in it and built a tower and let it out to husbandmen and went into a far country and when the time of the fruit drew near he sent his servants to the husbandmen that they might receive the fruits of it
nd the husbandmen took his servants and beat one and killed another and stoned another
gain he sent other servants more than the first and they did unto them likewise
ut last of all he sent unto them his son saying hey will reverence my son
ut when the husbandmen saw the son they said among themselves his is the heir come let us kill him and let us seize on his inheritance
nd they caught him and cast him out of the vineyard and slew him
hen the lord therefore of the vineyard cometh what will he do unto those husbandmen
hey say unto him e will miserably destroy those wicked men and will let out his vineyard unto other husbandmen which shall render him the fruits in their seasons
esus saith unto them id ye never read in the scriptures he stone which the builders rejected he same is become the head of the corner his is the ords doing nd it is marvellous in our eyes
herefore say  unto you he kingdom of od shall be taken from you and given to a nation bringing forth the fruits thereof
nd whosoever shall fall on this stone shall be broken but on whomsoever it shall fall it will grind him to powder
nd when the chief priests and harisees had heard his parables they perceived that he spake of them
ut when they sought to lay hands on him they feared the multitude because they took him for a prophet
nd esus answered and spake unto them again by parables and said he kingdom of heaven is like unto a certain king which made a marriage for his son and sent forth his servants to call them that were bidden to the wedding and they would not come
gain he sent forth other servants saying ell them which are bidden ehold  have prepared my dinner my oxen and my fatlings are killed and all things are ready come unto the marriage
ut they made light of it and went their ways one to his farm another to his merchandise and the remnant took his servants and entreated them spitefully and slew them
ut when the king heard thereof he was wroth and he sent forth his armies and destroyed those murderers and burned up their city
hen saith he to his servants he wedding is ready but they which were bidden were not worthy
o ye therefore into the highways and as many as ye shall find bid to the marriage
o those servants went out into the highways and gathered together all as many as they found both bad and good and the wedding was furnished with guests
nd when the king came in to see the guests he saw there a man which had not on a wedding garment and he saith unto him riend how camest thou in hither not having a wedding garment
nd he was speechless
hen said the king to the servants ind him hand and foot and take him away and cast him into outer darkness there shall be weeping and gnashing of teeth
or many are called but few are chosen
hen went the harisees and took counsel how they might entangle him in his talk
nd they sent out unto him their disciples with the erodians saying aster we know that thou art true and teachest the way of od in truth neither carest thou for any man for thou regardest not the person of men
ell us therefore hat thinkest thou
s it lawful to give tribute unto  sar or not
ut esus perceived their wickedness and said hy tempt ye me ye hypocrites
hew me the tribute money
nd they brought unto him a penny
nd he saith unto them hose is this image and superscription
hey say unto him  sars
hen saith he unto them ender therefore unto  sar the things which are  sars and unto od the things that are ods
hen they had heard these words they marvelled and left him and went their way
he same day came to him the adducees which say that there is no resurrection and asked him saying aster oses said f a man die having no children his brother shall marry his wife and raise up seed unto his brother
ow there were with us seven brethren and the first when he had married a wife deceased and having no issue left his wife unto his brother likewise the second also and the third unto the seventh
nd last of all the woman died also
herefore in the resurrection whose wife shall she be of the seven for they all had her
esus answered and said unto them e do err not knowing the scriptures nor the power of od
or in the resurrection they neither marry nor are given in marriage but are as the angels of od in heaven
ut as touching the resurrection of the dead have ye not read that which was spoken unto you by od saying  am the od of braham and the od of saac and the od of acob
od is not the od of the dead but of the living
nd when the multitude heard this they were astonished at his doctrine
ut when the harisees had heard that he had put the adducees to silence they were gathered together
hen one of them which was a lawyer asked him a question tempting him and saying aster which is the great commandment in the law
esus said unto him hou shalt love the ord thy od with all thy heart and with all thy soul and with all thy mind
his is the first and great commandment
nd the second is like unto it hou shalt love thy neighbour as thyself
n these two commandments hang all the law and the prophets
hile the harisees were gathered together esus asked them saying hat think ye of hrist whose son is he
hey say unto him he son of avid
e saith unto them ow then doth avid in spirit call him ord saying he  said unto my ord it thou on my right hand ill  make thine enemies thy footstool
f avid then call him ord how is he his son
nd no man was able to answer him a word neither durst any man from that day forth ask him any more questions
hen spake esus to the multitude and to his disciples saying he scribes and the harisees sit in oses seat all therefore whatsoever they bid you observe that observe and do but do not ye after their works for they say and do not
or they bind heavy burdens and grievous to be borne and lay them on mens shoulders but they themselves will not move them with one of their fingers
ut all their works they do for to be seen of men they make broad their phylacteries and enlarge the borders of their garments and love the uppermost rooms at feasts and the chief seats in the synagogues and greetings in the markets and to be called of men abbi abbi
ut be not ye called abbi for one is your aster even hrist and all ye are brethren
nd call no man your father upon the earth for one is your ather which is in heaven
either be ye called masters for one is your aster even hrist
ut he that is greatest among you shall be your servant
nd whosoever shall exalt himself shall be abased and he that shall humble himself shall be exalted
ut woe unto you scribes and harisees hypocrites for ye shut up the kingdom of heaven against men for ye neither go in yourselves neither suffer ye them that are entering to go in
oe unto you scribes and harisees hypocrites for ye devour widows houses and for a pretence make long prayer therefore ye shall receive the greater damnation
oe unto you scribes and harisees hypocrites for ye compass sea and land to make one proselyte and when he is made ye make him twofold more the child of hell than yourselves
oe unto you ye blind guides which say hosoever shall swear by the temple it is nothing but whosoever shall swear by the gold of the temple he is a debtor
e fools and blind for whether is greater the gold or the temple that sanctifieth the gold
nd hosoever shall swear by the altar it is nothing but whosoever sweareth by the gift that is upon it he is guilty
e fools and blind for whether is greater the gift or the altar that sanctifieth the gift
hoso therefore shall swear by the altar sweareth by it and by all things thereon
nd whoso shall swear by the temple sweareth by it and by him that dwelleth therein
nd he that shall swear by heaven sweareth by the throne of od and by him that sitteth thereon
oe unto you scribes and harisees hypocrites for ye pay tithe of mint and anise and cummin and have omitted the weightier matters of the law judgment mercy and faith these ought ye to have done and not to leave the other undone
e blind guides which strain at a gnat and swallow a camel
oe unto you scribes and harisees hypocrites for ye make clean the outside of the cup and of the platter but within they are full of extortion and excess
hou blind harisee cleanse first that which is within the cup and platter that the outside of them may be clean also
