oe unto you scribes and harisees hypocrites for ye are like unto whited sepulchres which indeed appear beautiful outward but are within full of dead mens bones and of all uncleanness
ven so ye also outwardly appear righteous unto men but within ye are full of hypocrisy and iniquity
oe unto you scribes and harisees hypocrites because ye build the tombs of the prophets and garnish the sepulchres of the righteous and say f we had been in the days of our fathers we would not have been partakers with them in the blood of the prophets
herefore ye be witnesses unto yourselves that ye are the children of them which killed the prophets
ill ye up then the measure of your fathers
e serpents ye generation of vipers how can ye escape the damnation of hell
herefore behold  send unto you prophets and wise men and scribes and some of them ye shall kill and crucify and some of them shall ye scourge in your synagogues and persecute them from city to city that upon you may come all the righteous blood shed upon the earth from the blood of righteous bel unto the blood of acharias son of arachias whom ye slew between the temple and the altar
erily  say unto you ll these things shall come upon this generation
 erusalem erusalem thou that killest the prophets and stonest them which are sent unto thee how often would  have gathered thy children together even as a hen gathereth her chickens under her wings and ye would not
ehold your house is left unto you desolate
or  say unto you e shall not see me henceforth till ye shall say lessed is he that cometh in the name of the ord
nd esus went out and departed from the temple and his disciples came to him for to shew him the buildings of the temple
nd esus said unto them ee ye not all these things verily  say unto you here shall not be left here one stone upon another that shall not be thrown down
nd as he sat upon the mount of lives the disciples came unto him privately saying ell us when shall these things be and what shall be the sign of thy coming and of the end of the world
nd esus answered and said unto them ake heed that no man deceive you
or many shall come in my name saying  am hrist and shall deceive many
nd ye shall hear of wars and rumours of wars see that ye be not troubled for all these things must come to pass but the end is not yet
or nation shall rise against nation and kingdom against kingdom and there shall be famines and pestilences and earthquakes in divers places
ll these are the beginning of sorrows
hen shall they deliver you up to be afflicted and shall kill you and ye shall be hated of all nations for my names sake
nd then shall many be offended and shall betray one another and shall hate one another
nd many false prophets shall rise and shall deceive many
nd because iniquity shall abound the love of many shall wax cold
ut he that shall endure unto the end the same shall be saved
nd this gospel of the kingdom shall be preached in all the world for a witness unto all nations and then shall the end come
hen ye therefore shall see the abomination of desolation spoken of by aniel the prophet stand in the holy place whoso readeth let him understand then let them which be in ud a flee into the mountains let him which is on the house top not come down to take any thing out of his house neither let him which is in the field return back to take his clothes
nd woe unto them that are with child and to them that give suck in those days
ut pray ye that your flight be not in the winter neither on the sabbath day for then shall be great tribulation such as was not since the beginning of the world to this time no nor ever shall be
nd except those days should be shortened there should no flesh be saved but for the elects sake those days shall be shortened
hen if any man shall say unto you o here is hrist or there believe it not
or there shall arise false hrists and false prophets and shall shew great signs and wonders insomuch that if it were possible they shall deceive the very elect
ehold  have told you before
herefore if they shall say unto you ehold he is in the desert go not forth behold he is in the secret chambers believe it not
or as the lightning cometh out of the east and shineth even unto the west so shall also the coming of the on of man be
or wheresoever the carcase is there will the eagles be gathered together
mmediately after the tribulation of those days shall the sun be darkened and the moon shall not give her light and the stars shall fall from heaven and the powers of the heavens shall be shaken and then shall appear the sign of the on of man in heaven and then shall all the tribes of the earth mourn and they shall see the on of man coming in the clouds of heaven with power and great glory
nd he shall send his angels with a great sound of a trumpet and they shall gather together his elect from the four winds from one end of heaven to the other
ow learn a parable of the fig tree hen his branch is yet tender and putteth forth leaves ye know that summer is nigh so likewise ye when ye shall see all these things know that it is near even at the doors
erily  say unto you his generation shall not pass till all these things be fulfilled
eaven and earth shall pass away but my words shall not pass away
ut of that day and hour knoweth no man no not the angels of heaven but my ather only
ut as the days of oe were so shall also the coming of the on of man be
or as in the days that were before the flood they were eating and drinking marrying and giving in marriage until the day that oe entered into the ark and knew not until the flood came and took them all away so shall also the coming of the on of man be
hen shall two be in the field the one shall be taken and the other left
wo women shall be grinding at the mill the one shall be taken and the other left
atch therefore for ye know not what hour your ord doth come
ut know this that if the goodman of the house had known in what watch the thief would come he would have watched and would not have suffered his house to be broken up
herefore be ye also ready for in such an hour as ye think not the on of man cometh
ho then is a faithful and wise servant whom his lord hath made ruler over his household to give them meat in due season
lessed is that servant whom his lord when he cometh shall find so doing
erily  say unto you hat he shall make him ruler over all his goods
ut and if that evil servant shall say in his heart y lord delayeth his coming and shall begin to smite his fellowservants and to eat and drink with the drunken the lord of that servant shall come in a day when he looketh not for him and in an hour that he is not aware of and shall cut him asunder and appoint him his portion with the hypocrites there shall be weeping and gnashing of teeth
hen shall the kingdom of heaven be likened unto ten virgins which took their lamps and went forth to meet the bridegroom
nd five of them were wise and five were foolish
hey that were foolish took their lamps and took no oil with them but the wise took oil in their vessels with their lamps
hile the bridegroom tarried they all slumbered and slept
nd at midnight there was a cry made ehold the bridegroom cometh go ye out to meet him
hen all those virgins arose and trimmed their lamps
nd the foolish said unto the wise ive us of your oil for our lamps are gone out
ut the wise answered saying ot so lest there be not enough for us and you but go ye rather to them that sell and buy for yourselves
nd while they went to buy the bridegroom came and they that were ready went in with him to the marriage and the door was shut
fterward came also the other virgins saying ord ord open to us
ut he answered and said erily  say unto you  know you not
atch therefore for ye know neither the day nor the hour wherein the on of man cometh
or the kingdom of heaven is as a man travelling into a far country who called his own servants and delivered unto them his goods
nd unto one he gave five talents to another two and to another one to every man according to his several ability and straightway took his journey
hen he that had received the five talents went and traded with the same and made them other five talents
nd likewise he that had received two he also gained other two
ut he that had received one went and digged in the earth and hid his lords money
fter a long time the lord of those servants cometh and reckoneth with them
nd so he that had received five talents came and brought other five talents saying ord thou deliveredst unto me five talents behold  have gained beside them five talents more
is lord said unto him ell done thou good and faithful servant thou hast been faithful over a few things  will make thee ruler over many things enter thou into the joy of thy lord
e also that had received two talents came and said ord thou deliveredst unto me two talents behold  have gained two other talents beside them
is lord said unto him ell done good and faithful servant thou hast been faithful over a few things  will make thee ruler over many things enter thou into the joy of thy lord
hen he which had received the one talent came and said ord  knew thee that thou art an hard man reaping where thou hast not sown and gathering where thou hast not strawed and  was afraid and went and hid thy talent in the earth lo there thou hast that is thine
is lord answered and said unto him hou wicked and slothful servant thou knewest that  reap where  sowed not and gather where  have not strawed thou oughtest therefore to have put my money to the exchangers and then at my coming  should have received mine own with usury
ake therefore the talent from him and give it unto him which hath ten talents
or unto every one that hath shall be given and he shall have abundance but from him that hath not shall be taken away even that which he hath
nd cast ye the unprofitable servant into outer darkness there shall be weeping and gnashing of teeth
hen the on of man shall come in his glory and all the holy angels with him then shall he sit upon the throne of his glory and before him shall be gathered all nations and he shall separate them one from another as a shepherd divideth his sheep from the goats and he shall set the sheep on his right hand but the goats on the left
hen shall the ing say unto them on his right hand ome ye blessed of my ather inherit the kingdom prepared for you from the foundation of the world for  was an hungred and ye gave me meat  was thirsty and ye gave me drink  was a stranger and ye took me in naked and ye clothed me  was sick and ye visited me  was in prison and ye came unto me
hen shall the righteous answer him saying ord when saw we thee an hungred and fed thee or thirsty and gave thee drink
hen saw we thee a stranger and took thee in or naked and clothed thee
r when saw we thee sick or in prison and came unto thee
nd the ing shall answer and say unto them erily  say unto you nasmuch as ye have done it unto one of the least of these my brethren ye have done it unto me
hen shall he say also unto them on the left hand epart from me ye cursed into everlasting fire prepared for the devil and his angels for  was an hungred and ye gave me no meat  was thirsty and ye gave me no drink  was a stranger and ye took me not in naked and ye clothed me not sick and in prison and ye visited me not
hen shall they also answer him saying ord when saw we thee an hungred or athirst or a stranger or naked or sick or in prison and did not minister unto thee
hen shall he answer them saying erily  say unto you nasmuch as ye did it not to one of the least of these ye did it not to me
nd these shall go away into everlasting punishment but the righteous into life eternal
nd it came to pass when esus had finished all these sayings he said unto his disciples e know that after two days is the feast of the passover and the on of man is betrayed to be crucified
hen assembled together the chief priests and the scribes and the elders of the people unto the palace of the high priest who was called aiaphas and consulted that they might take esus by subtilty and kill him
ut they said ot on the feast day lest there be an uproar among the people
ow when esus was in ethany in the house of imon the leper there came unto him a woman having an alabaster box of very precious ointment and poured it on his head as he sat at meat
ut when his disciples saw it they had indignation saying o what purpose is this waste
or this ointment might have been sold for much and given to the poor
hen esus understood it he said unto them hy trouble ye the woman for she hath wrought a good work upon me
or ye have the poor always with you but me ye have not always
or in that she hath poured this ointment on my body she did it for my burial
erily  say unto you heresoever this gospel shall be preached in the whole world there shall also this that this woman hath done be told for a memorial of her
hen one of the twelve called udas scariot went unto the chief priests and said unto them hat will ye give me and  will deliver him unto you
nd they covenanted with him for thirty pieces of silver
nd from that time he sought opportunity to betray him
ow the first day of the feast of unleavened bread the disciples came to esus saying unto him here wilt thou that we prepare for thee to eat the passover
nd he said o into the city to such a man and say unto him he aster saith y time is at hand  will keep the passover at thy house with my disciples
nd the disciples did as esus had appointed them and they made ready the passover
ow when the even was come he sat down with the twelve
nd as they did eat he said erily  say unto you that one of you shall betray me
nd they were exceeding sorrowful and began every one of them to say unto him ord is it 
nd he answered and said e that dippeth his hand with me in the dish the same shall betray me
he on of man goeth as it is written of him but woe unto that man by whom the on of man is betrayed it had been good for that man if he had not been born
hen udas which betrayed him answered and said aster is it 
e said unto him hou hast said
nd as they were eating esus took bread and blessed it and brake it and gave it to the disciples and said ake eat this is my body
nd he took the cup and gave thanks and gave it to them saying rink ye all of it for this is my blood of the new testament which is shed for many for the remission of sins
ut  say unto you  will not drink henceforth of this fruit of the vine until that day when  drink it new with you in my athers kingdom
nd when they had sung an hymn they went out into the mount of lives
hen saith esus unto them ll ye shall be offended because of me this night for it is written  will smite the shepherd and the sheep of the flock shall be scattered abroad
ut after  am risen again  will go before you into alilee
eter answered and said unto him hough all men shall be offended because of thee yet will  never be offended
esus said unto him erily  say unto thee hat this night before the cock crow thou shalt deny me thrice
eter said unto him hough  should die with thee yet will  not deny thee
ikewise also said all the disciples
hen cometh esus with them unto a place called ethsemane and saith unto the disciples it ye here while  go and pray yonder
nd he took with him eter and the two sons of ebedee and began to be sorrowful and very heavy
hen saith he unto them y soul is exceeding sorrowful even unto death tarry ye here and watch with me
nd he went a little further and fell on his face and prayed saying  my ather if it be possible let this cup pass from me nevertheless not as  will but as thou wilt
nd he cometh unto the disciples and findeth them asleep and saith unto eter hat could ye not watch with me one hour
atch and pray that ye enter not into temptation the spirit indeed is willing but the flesh is weak
e went away again the second time and prayed saying  my ather if this cup may not pass away from me except  drink it thy will be done
nd he came and found them asleep again for their eyes were heavy
nd he left them and went away again and prayed the third time saying the same words
hen cometh he to his disciples and saith unto them leep on now and take your rest behold the hour is at hand and the on of man is betrayed into the hands of sinners
ise let us be going behold he is at hand that doth betray me
nd while he yet spake lo udas one of the twelve came and with him a great multitude with swords and staves from the chief priests and elders of the people
ow he that betrayed him gave them a sign saying homsoever  shall kiss that same is he hold him fast
nd forthwith he came to esus and said ail master and kissed him
nd esus said unto him riend wherefore art thou come
hen came they and laid hands on esus and took him
nd behold one of them which were with esus stretched out his hand and drew his sword and struck a servant of the high priests and smote off his ear
hen said esus unto him ut up again thy sword into his place for all they that take the sword shall perish with the sword
hinkest thou that  cannot now pray to my ather and he shall presently give me more than twelve legions of angels
ut how then shall the scriptures be fulfilled that thus it must be
n that same hour said esus to the multitudes re ye come out as against a thief with swords and staves for to take me
 sat daily with you teaching in the temple and ye laid no hold on me
ut all this was done that the scriptures of the prophets might be fulfilled
hen all the disciples forsook him and fled
nd they that had laid hold on esus led him away to aiaphas the high priest where the scribes and the elders were assembled
ut eter followed him afar off unto the high priests palace and went in and sat with the servants to see the end
ow the chief priests and elders and all the council sought false witness against esus to put him to death but found none yea though many false witnesses came yet found they none
t the last came two false witnesses and said his fellow said  am able to destroy the temple of od and to build it in three days
nd the high priest arose and said unto him nswerest thou nothing what is it which these witness against thee
ut esus held his peace
nd the high priest answered and said unto him  adjure thee by the living od that thou tell us whether thou be the hrist the on of od
esus saith unto him hou hast said nevertheless  say unto you ereafter shall ye see the on of man sitting on the right hand of power and coming in the clouds of heaven
hen the high priest rent his clothes saying e hath spoken blasphemy what further need have we of witnesses behold now ye have heard his blasphemy
hat think ye
hey answered and said e is guilty of death
hen did they spit in his face and buffeted him and others smote him with the palms of their hands saying rophesy unto us thou hrist ho is he that smote thee
ow eter sat without in the palace and a damsel came unto him saying hou also wast with esus of alilee
ut he denied before them all saying  know not what thou sayest
nd when he was gone out into the porch another maid saw him and said unto them that were there his fellow was also with esus of azareth
nd again he denied with an oath  do not know the man
nd after a while came unto him they that stood by and said to eter urely thou also art one of them for thy speech bewrayeth thee
hen began he to curse and to swear saying  know not the man
nd immediately the cock crew
nd eter remembered the word of esus which said unto him efore the cock crow thou shalt deny me thrice
nd he went out and wept bitterly
hen the morning was come all the chief priests and elders of the people took counsel against esus to put him to death and when they had bound him they led him away and delivered him to ontius ilate the governor
hen udas which had betrayed him when he saw that he was condemned repented himself and brought again the thirty pieces of silver to the chief priests and elders saying  have sinned in that  have betrayed the innocent blood
nd they said hat is that to us see thou to that
nd he cast down the pieces of silver in the temple and departed and went and hanged himself
nd the chief priests took the silver pieces and said t is not lawful for to put them into the treasury because it is the price of blood
nd they took counsel and bought with them the potters field to bury strangers in
herefore that field was called he field of blood unto this day
hen was fulfilled that which was spoken by eremy the prophet saying nd they took the thirty pieces of silver the price of him that was valued whom they of the children of srael did value and gave them for the potters field as the ord appointed me
nd esus stood before the governor and the governor asked him saying rt thou the ing of the ews
nd esus said unto him hou sayest
nd when he was accused of the chief priests and elders he answered nothing
hen said ilate unto him earest thou not how many things they witness against thee
nd he answered him to never a word insomuch that the governor marvelled greatly
ow at that feast the governor was wont to release unto the people a prisoner whom they would
nd they had then a notable prisoner called arabbas
herefore when they were gathered together ilate said unto them hom will ye that  release unto you
arabbas or esus which is called hrist
or he knew that for envy they had delivered him
hen he was set down on the judgment seat his wife sent unto him saying ave thou nothing to do with that just man for  have suffered many things this day in a dream because of him
ut the chief priests and elders persuaded the multitude that they should ask arabbas and destroy esus
he governor answered and said unto them hether of the twain will ye that  release unto you
hey said arabbas
ilate saith unto them hat shall  do then with esus which is called hrist
hey all say unto him et him be crucified
nd the governor said hy what evil hath he done
ut they cried out the more saying et him be crucified
hen ilate saw that he could prevail nothing but that rather a tumult was made he took water and washed his hands before the multitude saying  am innocent of the blood of this just person see ye to it
hen answered all the people and said is blood be on us and on our children
hen released he arabbas unto them and when he had scourged esus he delivered him to be crucified
hen the soldiers of the governor took esus into the common hall and gathered unto him the whole band of soldiers
nd they stripped him and put on him a scarlet robe
nd when they had platted a crown of thorns they put it upon his head and a reed in his right hand and they bowed the knee before him and mocked him saying ail ing of the ews
nd they spit upon him and took the reed and smote him on the head
nd after that they had mocked him they took the robe off from him and put his own raiment on him and led him away to crucify him
nd as they came out they found a man of yrene imon by name him they compelled to bear his cross
nd when they were come unto a place called olgotha that is to say a place of a skull they gave him vinegar to drink mingled with gall and when he had tasted thereof he would not drink
nd they crucified him and parted his garments casting lots that it might be fulfilled which was spoken by the prophet hey parted my garments among them and upon my vesture did they cast lots
nd sitting down they watched him there and set up over his head his accusation written        
hen were there two thieves crucified with him one on the right hand and another on the left
nd they that passed by reviled him wagging their heads and saying hou that destroyest the temple and buildest it in three days save thyself
f thou be the on of od come down from the cross
ikewise also the chief priests mocking him with the scribes and elders said e saved others himself he cannot save
f he be the ing of srael let him now come down from the cross and we will believe him
e trusted in od let him deliver him now if he will have him for he said  am the on of od
he thieves also which were crucified with him cast the same in his teeth
ow from the sixth hour there was darkness over all the land unto the ninth hour
nd about the ninth hour esus cried with a loud voice saying li li lama sabachthani that is to say y od my od why hast thou forsaken me
ome of them that stood there when they heard that said his man calleth for lias
nd straightway one of them ran and took a spunge and filled it with vinegar and put it on a reed and gave him to drink
he rest said et be let us see whether lias will come to save him
esus when he had cried again with a loud voice yielded up the ghost
nd behold the veil of the temple was rent in twain from the top to the bottom and the earth did quake and the rocks rent and the graves were opened and many bodies of the saints which slept arose and came out of the graves after his resurrection and went into the holy city and appeared unto many
ow when the centurion and they that were with him watching esus saw the earthquake and those things that were done they feared greatly saying ruly this was the on of od
nd many women were there beholding afar off which followed esus from alilee ministering unto him among which was ary agdalene and ary the mother of ames and oses and the mother of ebedees children
hen the even was come there came a rich man of rimath a named oseph who also himself was esus disciple he went to ilate and begged the body of esus
hen ilate commanded the body to be delivered
nd when oseph had taken the body he wrapped it in a clean linen cloth and laid it in his own new tomb which he had hewn out in the rock and he rolled a great stone to the door of the sepulchre and departed
nd there was ary agdalene and the other ary sitting over against the sepulchre
ow the next day that followed the day of the preparation the chief priests and harisees came together unto ilate saying ir we remember that that deceiver said while he was yet alive fter three days  will rise again
ommand therefore that the sepulchre be made sure until the third day lest his disciples come by night and steal him away and say unto the people e is risen from the dead so the last error shall be worse than the first
ilate said unto them e have a watch go your way make it as sure as ye can
o they went and made the sepulchre sure sealing the stone and setting a watch
n the end of the sabbath as it began to dawn toward the first day of the week came ary agdalene and the other ary to see the sepulchre
nd behold there was a great earthquake for the angel of the ord descended from heaven and came and rolled back the stone from the door and sat upon it
is countenance was like lightning and his raiment white as snow and for fear of him the keepers did shake and became as dead men
nd the angel answered and said unto the women ear not ye for  know that ye seek esus which was crucified
e is not here for he is risen as he said
ome see the place where the ord lay
nd go quickly and tell his disciples that he is risen from the dead and behold he goeth before you into alilee there shall ye see him lo  have told you
nd they departed quickly from the sepulchre with fear and great joy and did run to bring his disciples word
nd as they went to tell his disciples behold esus met them saying ll hail
nd they came and held him by the feet and worshipped him
hen said esus unto them e not afraid go tell my brethren that they go into alilee and there shall they see me
ow when they were going behold some of the watch came into the city and shewed unto the chief priests all the things that were done
nd when they were assembled with the elders and had taken counsel they gave large money unto the soldiers saying ay ye is disciples came by night and stole him away while we slept
nd if this come to the governors ears we will persuade him and secure you
o they took the money and did as they were taught and this saying is commonly reported among the ews until this day
hen the eleven disciples went away into alilee into a mountain where esus had appointed them
nd when they saw him they worshipped him but some doubted
nd esus came and spake unto them saying ll power is given unto me in heaven and in earth
o ye therefore and teach all nations baptizing them in the name of the ather and of the on and of the oly host teaching them to observe all things whatsoever  have commanded you and lo  am with you alway even unto the end of the world
men
he beginning of the gospel of esus hrist the on of od s it is written in the prophets ehold  send my messenger before thy face hich shall prepare thy way before thee
he voice of one crying in the wilderness repare ye the way of the ord ake his paths straight
ohn did baptize in the wilderness and preach the baptism of repentance for the remission of sins
nd there went out unto him all the land of ud a and they of erusalem and were all baptized of him in the river of ordan confessing their sins
nd ohn was clothed with camels hair and with a girdle of a skin about his loins and he did eat locusts and wild honey and preached saying here cometh one mightier than  after me the latchet of whose shoes  am not worthy to stoop down and unloose
 indeed have baptized you with water but he shall baptize you with the oly host
nd it came to pass in those days that esus came from azareth of alilee and was baptized of ohn in ordan
nd straightway coming up out of the water he saw the heavens opened and the pirit like a dove descending upon him and there came a voice from heaven saying hou art my beloved on in whom  am well pleased
nd immediately the pirit driveth him into the wilderness
nd he was there in the wilderness forty days tempted of atan and was with the wild beasts and the angels ministered unto him
ow after that ohn was put in prison esus came into alilee preaching the gospel of the kingdom of od and saying he time is fulfilled and the kingdom of od is at hand repent ye and believe the gospel
ow as he walked by the sea of alilee he saw imon and ndrew his brother casting a net into the sea for they were fishers
nd esus said unto them ome ye after me and  will make you to become fishers of men
nd straightway they forsook their nets and followed him
nd when he had gone a little farther thence he saw ames the son of ebedee and ohn his brother who also were in the ship mending their nets
nd straightway he called them and they left their father ebedee in the ship with the hired servants and went after him
nd they went into apernaum and straightway on the sabbath day he entered into the synagogue and taught
nd they were astonished at his doctrine for he taught them as one that had authority and not as the scribes
nd there was in their synagogue a man with an unclean spirit and he cried out saying et us alone what have we to do with thee thou esus of azareth art thou come to destroy us
 know thee who thou art the oly ne of od
nd esus rebuked him saying old thy peace and come out of him
nd when the unclean spirit had torn him and cried with a loud voice he came out of him
nd they were all amazed insomuch that they questioned among themselves saying hat thing is this what new doctrine is this for with authority commandeth he even the unclean spirits and they do obey him
nd immediately his fame spread abroad throughout all the region round about alilee
nd forthwith when they were come out of the synagogue they entered into the house of imon and ndrew with ames and ohn
ut imons wifes mother lay sick of a fever and anon they tell him of her
nd he came and took her by the hand and lifted her up and immediately the fever left her and she ministered unto them
nd at even when the sun did set they brought unto him all that were diseased and them that were possessed with devils
nd all the city was gathered together at the door
nd he healed many that were sick of divers diseases and cast out many devils and suffered not the devils to speak because they knew him
nd in the morning rising up a great while before day he went out and departed into a solitary place and there prayed
nd imon and they that were with him followed after him
nd when they had found him they said unto him ll men seek for thee
nd he said unto them et us go into the next towns that  may preach there also for therefore came  forth
nd he preached in their synagogues throughout all alilee and cast out devils
nd there came a leper to him beseeching him and kneeling down to him and saying unto him f thou wilt thou canst make me clean
nd esus moved with compassion put forth his hand and touched him and saith unto him  will be thou clean
nd as soon as he had spoken immediately the leprosy departed from him and he was cleansed
nd he straitly charged him and forthwith sent him away and saith unto him ee thou say nothing to any man but go thy way shew thyself to the priest and offer for thy cleansing those things which oses commanded for a testimony unto them
ut he went out and began to publish it much and to blaze abroad the matter insomuch that esus could no more openly enter into the city but was without in desert places and they came to him from every quarter
nd again he entered into apernaum after some days and it was noised that he was in the house
nd straightway many were gathered together insomuch that there was no room to receive them no not so much as about the door and he preached the word unto them
nd they come unto him bringing one sick of the palsy which was borne of four
nd when they could not come nigh unto him for the press they uncovered the roof where he was and when they had broken it up they let down the bed wherein the sick of the palsy lay
hen esus saw their faith he said unto the sick of the palsy on thy sins be forgiven thee
ut there were certain of the scribes sitting there and reasoning in their hearts hy doth this man thus speak blasphemies who can forgive sins but od only
nd immediately when esus perceived in his spirit that they so reasoned within themselves he said unto them hy reason ye these things in your hearts
hether is it easier to say to the sick of the palsy hy sins be forgiven thee or to say rise and take up thy bed and walk
ut that ye may know that the on of man hath power on earth to forgive sins he saith to the sick of the palsy   say unto thee rise and take up thy bed and go thy way into thine house
nd immediately he arose took up the bed and went forth before them all insomuch that they were all amazed and glorified od saying e never saw it on this fashion
nd he went forth again by the sea side and all the multitude resorted unto him and he taught them
nd as he passed by he saw evi the son of lph us sitting at the receipt of custom and said unto him ollow me
nd he arose and followed him
nd it came to pass that as esus sat at meat in his house many publicans and sinners sat also together with esus and his disciples for there were many and they followed him
nd when the scribes and harisees saw him eat with publicans and sinners they said unto his disciples ow is it that he eateth and drinketh with publicans and sinners
hen esus heard it he saith unto them hey that are whole have no need of the physician but they that are sick  came not to call the righteous but sinners to repentance
nd the disciples of ohn and of the harisees used to fast and they come and say unto him hy do the disciples of ohn and of the harisees fast but thy disciples fast not
nd esus said unto them an the children of the bridechamber fast while the bridegroom is with them as long as they have the bridegroom with them they cannot fast
ut the days will come when the bridegroom shall be taken away from them and then shall they fast in those days
o man also seweth a piece of new cloth on an old garment else the new piece that filled it up taketh away from the old and the rent is made worse
nd no man putteth new wine into old bottles else the new wine doth burst the bottles and the wine is spilled and the bottles will be marred but new wine must be put into new bottles
nd it came to pass that he went through the corn fields on the sabbath day and his disciples began as they went to pluck the ears of corn
nd the harisees said unto him ehold why do they on the sabbath day that which is not lawful
nd he said unto them ave ye never read what avid did when he had need and was an hungred he and they that were with him
ow he went into the house of od in the days of biathar the high priest and did eat the shewbread which is not lawful to eat but for the priests and gave also to them which were with him
nd he said unto them he sabbath was made for man and not man for the sabbath therefore the on of man is ord also of the sabbath
nd he entered again into the synagogue and there was a man there which had a withered hand
nd they watched him whether he would heal him on the sabbath day that they might accuse him
nd he saith unto the man which had the withered hand tand forth
nd he saith unto them s it lawful to do good on the sabbath days or to do evil to save life or to kill
ut they held their peace
nd when he had looked round about on them with anger being grieved for the hardness of their hearts he saith unto the man tretch forth thine hand
nd he stretched it out and his hand was restored whole as the other
nd the harisees went forth and straightway took counsel with the erodians against him how they might destroy him
ut esus withdrew himself with his disciples to the sea and a great multitude from alilee followed him and from ud a and from erusalem and from dum a and from beyond ordan and they about yre and idon a great multitude when they had heard what great things he did came unto him
nd he spake to his disciples that a small ship should wait on him because of the multitude lest they should throng him
or he had healed many insomuch that they pressed upon him for to touch him as many as had plagues
nd unclean spirits when they saw him fell down before him and cried saying hou art the on of od
nd he straitly charged them that they should not make him known
nd he goeth up into a mountain and calleth unto him whom he would and they came unto him
nd he ordained twelve that they should be with him and that he might send them forth to preach and to have power to heal sicknesses and to cast out devils and imon he surnamed eter and ames the son of ebedee and ohn the brother of ames and he surnamed them oanerges which is he sons of thunder and ndrew and hilip and artholomew and atthew and homas and ames the son of lph us and hadd us and imon the anaanite and udas scariot which also betrayed him and they went into an house
nd the multitude cometh together again so that they could not so much as eat bread
nd when his friends heard of it they went out to lay hold on him for they said e is beside himself
nd the scribes which came down from erusalem said e hath eelzebub and by the prince of the devils casteth he out devils
nd he called them unto him and said unto them in parables ow can atan cast out atan
nd if a kingdom be divided against itself that kingdom cannot stand
nd if a house be divided against itself that house cannot stand
nd if atan rise up against himself and be divided he cannot stand but hath an end
o man can enter into a strong mans house and spoil his goods except he will first bind the strong man and then he will spoil his house
erily  say unto you ll sins shall be forgiven unto the sons of men and blasphemies wherewith soever they shall blaspheme but he that shall blaspheme against the oly host hath never forgiveness but is in danger of eternal damnation because they said e hath an unclean spirit
here came then his brethren and his mother and standing without sent unto him calling him
nd the multitude sat about him and they said unto him ehold thy mother and thy brethren without seek for thee
nd he answered them saying ho is my mother or my brethren
nd he looked round about on them which sat about him and said ehold my mother and my brethren
or whosoever shall do the will of od the same is my brother and my sister and mother
nd he began again to teach by the sea side and there was gathered unto him a great multitude so that he entered into a ship and sat in the sea and the whole multitude was by the sea on the land
nd he taught them many things by parables and said unto them in his doctrine earken ehold there went out a sower to sow and it came to pass as he sowed some fell by the way side and the fowls of the air came and devoured it up
nd some fell on stony ground where it had not much earth and immediately it sprang up because it had no depth of earth but when the sun was up it was scorched and because it had no root it withered away
nd some fell among thorns and the thorns grew up and choked it and it yielded no fruit
nd other fell on good ground and did yield fruit that sprang up and increased and brought forth some thirty and some sixty and some an hundred
nd he said unto them e that hath ears to hear let him hear
nd when he was alone they that were about him with the twelve asked of him the parable
nd he said unto them nto you it is given to know the mystery of the kingdom of od but unto them that are without all these things are done in parables that seeing they may see and not perceive and hearing they may hear and not understand lest at any time they should be converted and their sins should be forgiven them
nd he said unto them now ye not this parable and how then will ye know all parables
he sower soweth the word
nd these are they by the way side where the word is sown but when they have heard atan cometh immediately and taketh away the word that was sown in their hearts
nd these are they likewise which are sown on stony ground who when they have heard the word immediately receive it with gladness and have no root in themselves and so endure but for a time afterward when affliction or persecution ariseth for the words sake immediately they are offended
nd these are they which are sown among thorns such as hear the word and the cares of this world and the deceitfulness of riches and the lusts of other things entering in choke the word and it becometh unfruitful
nd these are they which are sown on good ground such as hear the word and receive it and bring forth fruit some thirtyfold some sixty and some an hundred
nd he said unto them s a candle brought to be put under a bushel or under a bed and not to be set on a candlestick
or there is nothing hid which shall not be manifested neither was any thing kept secret but that it should come abroad
f any man have ears to hear let him hear
nd he said unto them ake heed what ye hear with what measure ye mete it shall be measured to you and unto you that hear shall more be given
or he that hath to him shall be given and he that hath not from him shall be taken even that which he hath
nd he said o is the kingdom of od as if a man should cast seed into the ground and should sleep and rise night and day and the seed should spring and grow up he knoweth not how
or the earth bringeth forth fruit of herself first the blade then the ear after that the full corn in the ear
ut when the fruit is brought forth immediately he putteth in the sickle because the harvest is come
nd he said hereunto shall we liken the kingdom of od or with what comparison shall we compare it
t is like a grain of mustard seed which when it is sown in the earth is less than all the seeds that be in the earth but when it is sown it groweth up and becometh greater than all herbs and shooteth out great branches so that the fowls of the air may lodge under the shadow of it
nd with many such parables spake he the word unto them as they were able to hear it
ut without a parable spake he not unto them and when they were alone he expounded all things to his disciples
nd the same day when the even was come he saith unto them et us pass over unto the other side
nd when they had sent away the multitude they took him even as he was in the ship
nd there were also with him other little ships
nd there arose a great storm of wind and the waves beat into the ship so that it was now full
nd he was in the hinder part of the ship asleep on a pillow and they awake him and say unto him aster carest thou not that we perish
nd he arose and rebuked the wind and said unto the sea eace be still
nd the wind ceased and there was a great calm
nd he said unto them hy are ye so fearful how is it that ye have no faith
nd they feared exceedingly and said one to another hat manner of man is this that even the wind and the sea obey him
nd they came over unto the other side of the sea into the country of the adarenes
nd when he was come out of the ship immediately there met him out of the tombs a man with an unclean spirit who had his dwelling among the tombs and no man could bind him no not with chains because that he had been often bound with fetters and chains and the chains had been plucked asunder by him and the fetters broken in pieces neither could any man tame him
nd always night and day he was in the mountains and in the tombs crying and cutting himself with stones
ut when he saw esus afar off he ran and worshipped him and cried with a loud voice and said hat have  to do with thee esus thou on of the most high od
 adjure thee by od that thou torment me not
or he said unto him ome out of the man thou unclean spirit
nd he asked him hat is thy name
nd he answered saying y name is egion for we are many
nd he besought him much that he would not send them away out of the country
ow there was there nigh unto the mountains a great herd of swine feeding
nd all the devils besought him saying end us into the swine that we may enter into them
nd forthwith esus gave them leave
nd the unclean spirits went out and entered into the swine and the herd ran violently down a steep place into the sea they were about two thousand and were choked in the sea
nd they that fed the swine fled and told it in the city and in the country
nd they went out to see what it was that was done
nd they come to esus and see him that was possessed with the devil and had the legion sitting and clothed and in his right mind and they were afraid
nd they that saw it told them how it befell to him that was possessed with the devil and also concerning the swine
nd they began to pray him to depart out of their coasts
nd when he was come into the ship he that had been possessed with the devil prayed him that he might be with him
owbeit esus suffered him not but saith unto him o home to thy friends and tell them how great things the ord hath done for thee and hath had compassion on thee
nd he departed and began to publish in ecapolis how great things esus had done for him and all men did marvel
nd when esus was passed over again by ship unto the other side much people gathered unto him and he was nigh unto the sea
nd behold there cometh one of the rulers of the synagogue airus by name and when he saw him he fell at his feet and besought him greatly saying y little daughter lieth at the point of death  pray thee come and lay thy hands on her that she may be healed and she shall live
nd esus went with him and much people followed him and thronged him
nd a certain woman which had an issue of blood twelve years and had suffered many things of many physicians and had spent all that she had and was nothing bettered but rather grew worse when she had heard of esus came in the press behind and touched his garment
or she said f  may touch but his clothes  shall be whole
nd straightway the fountain of her blood was dried up and she felt in her body that she was healed of that plague
nd esus immediately knowing in himself that virtue had gone out of him turned him about in the press and said ho touched my clothes
nd his disciples said unto him hou seest the multitude thronging thee and sayest thou ho touched me
nd he looked round about to see her that had done this thing
ut the woman fearing and trembling knowing what was done in her came and fell down before him and told him all the truth
nd he said unto her aughter thy faith hath made thee whole go in peace and be whole of thy plague
hile he yet spake there came from the ruler of the synagogues house certain which said hy daughter is dead why troublest thou the aster any further
s soon as esus heard the word that was spoken he saith unto the ruler of the synagogue e not afraid only believe
nd he suffered no man to follow him save eter and ames and ohn the brother of ames
nd he cometh to the house of the ruler of the synagogue and seeth the tumult and them that wept and wailed greatly
nd when he was come in he saith unto them hy make ye this ado and weep the damsel is not dead but sleepeth
nd they laughed him to scorn
ut when he had put them all out he taketh the father and the mother of the damsel and them that were with him and entereth in where the damsel was lying
nd he took the damsel by the hand and said unto her alitha cumi which is being interpreted amsel  say unto thee arise
nd straightway the damsel arose and walked for she was of the age of twelve years
nd they were astonished with a great astonishment
nd he charged them straitly that no man should know it and commanded that something should be given her to eat
nd he went out from thence and came into his own country and his disciples follow him
nd when the sabbath day was come he began to teach in the synagogue and many hearing him were astonished saying rom whence hath this man these things and what wisdom is this which is given unto him that even such mighty works are wrought by his hands
s not this the carpenter the son of ary the brother of ames and oses and of uda and imon and are not his sisters here with us
nd they were offended at him
ut esus said unto them  prophet is not without honour but in his own country and among his own kin and in his own house
nd he could there do no mighty work save that he laid his hands upon a few sick folk and healed them
nd he marvelled because of their unbelief
nd he went round about the villages teaching
nd he called unto him the twelve and began to send them forth by two and two and gave them power over unclean spirits and commanded them that they should take nothing for their journey save a staff only no scrip no bread no money in their purse but be shod with sandals and not put on two coats
nd he said unto them n what place soever ye enter into an house there abide till ye depart from that place
nd whosoever shall not receive you nor hear you when ye depart thence shake off the dust under your feet for a testimony against them
erily  say unto you t shall be more tolerable for odom and omorrha in the day of judgment than for that city
nd they went out and preached that men should repent
nd they cast out many devils and anointed with oil many that were sick and healed them
nd king erod heard of him for his name was spread abroad and he said hat ohn the aptist was risen from the dead and therefore mighty works do shew forth themselves in him
thers said hat it is lias
nd others said hat it is a prophet or as one of the prophets
ut when erod heard thereof he said t is ohn whom  beheaded he is risen from the dead
or erod himself had sent forth and laid hold upon ohn and bound him in prison for erodias sake his brother hilips wife for he had married her
or ohn had said unto erod t is not lawful for thee to have thy brothers wife
herefore erodias had a quarrel against him and would have killed him but she could not for erod feared ohn knowing that he was a just man and an holy and observed him and when he heard him he did many things and heard him gladly
nd when a convenient day was come that erod on his birthday made a supper to his lords high captains and chief estates of alilee and when the daughter of the said erodias came in and danced and pleased erod and them that sat with him the king said unto the damsel sk of me whatsoever thou wilt and  will give it thee
nd he sware unto her hatsoever thou shalt ask of me  will give it thee unto the half of my kingdom
nd she went forth and said unto her mother hat shall  ask
nd she said he head of ohn the aptist
nd she came in straightway with haste unto the king and asked saying  will that thou give me by and by in a charger the head of ohn the aptist
nd the king was exceeding sorry yet for his oaths sake and for their sakes which sat with him he would not reject her
nd immediately the king sent an executioner and commanded his head to be brought and he went and beheaded him in the prison and brought his head in a charger and gave it to the damsel and the damsel gave it to her mother
nd when his disciples heard of it they came and took up his corpse and laid it in a tomb
nd the apostles gathered themselves together unto esus and told him all things both what they had done and what they had taught
nd he said unto them ome ye yourselves apart into a desert place and rest a while for there were many coming and going and they had no leisure so much as to eat
nd they departed into a desert place by ship privately
nd the people saw them departing and many knew him and ran afoot thither out of all cities and outwent them and came together unto him
nd esus when he came out saw much people and was moved with compassion toward them because they were as sheep not having a shepherd and he began to teach them many things
nd when the day was now far spent his disciples came unto him and said his is a desert place and now the time is far passed send them away that they may go into the country round about and into the villages and buy themselves bread for they have nothing to eat
e answered and said unto them ive ye them to eat
nd they say unto him hall we go and buy two hundred pennyworth of bread and give them to eat
e saith unto them ow many loaves have ye go and see
nd when they knew they say ive and two fishes
nd he commanded them to make all sit down by companies upon the green grass
nd they sat down in ranks by hundreds and by fifties
nd when he had taken the five loaves and the two fishes he looked up to heaven and blessed and brake the loaves and gave them to his disciples to set before them and the two fishes divided he among them all
nd they did all eat and were filled
nd they took up twelve baskets full of the fragments and of the fishes
nd they that did eat of the loaves were about five thousand men
nd straightway he constrained his disciples to get into the ship and to go to the other side before unto ethsaida while he sent away the people
nd when he had sent them away he departed into a mountain to pray
nd when even was come the ship was in the midst of the sea and he alone on the land
nd he saw them toiling in rowing for the wind was contrary unto them and about the fourth watch of the night he cometh unto them walking upon the sea and would have passed by them
ut when they saw him walking upon the sea they supposed it had been a spirit and cried out for they all saw him and were troubled
nd immediately he talked with them and saith unto them e of good cheer it is  be not afraid
nd he went up unto them into the ship and the wind ceased and they were sore amazed in themselves beyond measure and wondered
or they considered not the miracle of the loaves for their heart was hardened
nd when they had passed over they came into the land of ennesaret and drew to the shore
nd when they were come out of the ship straightway they knew him and ran through that whole region round about and began to carry about in beds those that were sick where they heard he was
nd whithersoever he entered into villages or cities or country they laid the sick in the streets and besought him that they might touch if it were but the border of his garment and as many as touched him were made whole
hen came together unto him the harisees and certain of the scribes which came from erusalem
nd when they saw some of his disciples eat bread with defiled that is to say with unwashen hands they found fault
or the harisees and all the ews except they wash their hands oft eat not holding the tradition of the elders
nd when they come from the market except they wash they eat not
nd many other things there be which they have received to hold as the washing of cups and pots brasen vessels and of tables
hen the harisees and scribes asked him hy walk not thy disciples according to the tradition of the elders but eat bread with unwashen hands
e answered and said unto them ell hath saias prophesied of you hypocrites as it is written his people honoureth me with their lips ut their heart is far from me
owbeit in vain do they worship me eaching for doctrines the commandments of men
or laying aside the commandment of od ye hold the tradition of men as the washing of pots and cups and many other such like things ye do
nd he said unto them ull well ye reject the commandment of od that ye may keep your own tradition
or oses said onour thy father and thy mother and hoso curseth father or mother let him die the death but ye say f a man shall say to his father or mother t is orban that is to say a gift by whatsoever thou mightest be profited by me he shall be free
nd ye suffer him no more to do ought for his father or his mother making the word of od of none effect through your tradition which ye have delivered and many such like things do ye
nd when he had called all the people unto him he said unto them earken unto me every one of you and understand there is nothing from without a man that entering into him can defile him but the things which come out of him those are they that defile the man
f any man have ears to hear let him hear
nd when he was entered into the house from the people his disciples asked him concerning the parable
nd he saith unto them re ye so without understanding also
o ye not perceive that whatsoever thing from without entereth into the man it cannot defile him because it entereth not into his heart but into the belly and goeth out into the draught purging all meats
nd he said hat which cometh out of the man that defileth the man
or from within out of the heart of men proceed evil thoughts adulteries fornications murders thefts covetousness wickedness deceit lasciviousness an evil eye blasphemy pride foolishness all these evil things come from within and defile the man
nd from thence he arose and went into the borders of yre and idon and entered into an house and would have no man know it but he could not be hid
or a certain woman whose young daughter had an unclean spirit heard of him and came and fell at his feet the woman was a reek a yrophenician by nation and she besought him that he would cast forth the devil out of her daughter
ut esus said unto her et the children first be filled for it is not meet to take the childrens bread and to cast it unto the dogs
nd she answered and said unto him es ord yet the dogs under the table eat of the childrens crumbs
nd he said unto her or this saying go thy way the devil is gone out of thy daughter
nd when she was come to her house she found the devil gone out and her daughter laid upon the bed
nd again departing from the coasts of yre and idon he came unto the sea of alilee through the midst of the coasts of ecapolis
nd they bring unto him one that was deaf and had an impediment in his speech and they beseech him to put his hand upon him
nd he took him aside from the multitude and put his fingers into his ears and he spit and touched his tongue and looking up to heaven he sighed and saith unto him phphatha that is e opened
nd straightway his ears were opened and the string of his tongue was loosed and he spake plain
nd he charged them that they should tell no man but the more he charged them so much the more a great deal they published it and were beyond measure astonished saying e hath done all things well he maketh both the deaf to hear and the dumb to speak
n those days the multitude being very great and having nothing to eat esus called his disciples unto him and saith unto them  have compassion on the multitude because they have now been with me three days and have nothing to eat and if  send them away fasting to their own houses they will faint by the way for divers of them came from far
nd his disciples answered him rom whence can a man satisfy these men with bread here in the wilderness
nd he asked them ow many loaves have ye
nd they said even
nd he commanded the people to sit down on the ground and he took the seven loaves and gave thanks and brake and gave to his disciples to set before them and they did set them before the people
nd they had a few small fishes and he blessed and commanded to set them also before them
o they did eat and were filled and they took up of the broken meat that was left seven baskets
nd they that had eaten were about four thousand and he sent them away
nd straightway he entered into a ship with his disciples and came into the parts of almanutha
nd the harisees came forth and began to question with him seeking of him a sign from heaven tempting him
nd he sighed deeply in his spirit and saith hy doth this generation seek after a sign verily  say unto you here shall no sign be given unto this generation
nd he left them and entering into the ship again departed to the other side
ow the disciples had forgotten to take bread neither had they in the ship with them more than one loaf
nd he charged them saying ake heed beware of the leaven of the harisees and of the leaven of erod
nd they reasoned among themselves saying t is because we have no bread
nd when esus knew it he saith unto them hy reason ye because ye have no bread perceive ye not yet neither understand have ye your heart yet hardened
aving eyes see ye not and having ears hear ye not and do ye not remember
hen  brake the five loaves among five thousand how many baskets full of fragments took ye up
hey say unto him welve
nd when the seven among four thousand how many baskets full of fragments took ye up
nd they said even
nd he said unto them ow is it that ye do not understand
nd he cometh to ethsaida and they bring a blind man unto him and besought him to touch him
nd he took the blind man by the hand and led him out of the town and when he had spit on his eyes and put his hands upon him he asked him if he saw ought
nd he looked up and said  see men as trees walking
fter that he put his hands again upon his eyes and made him look up and he was restored and saw every man clearly
nd he sent him away to his house saying either go into the town nor tell it to any in the town
nd esus went out and his disciples into the towns of  sarea hilippi and by the way he asked his disciples saying unto them hom do men say that  am
nd they answered ohn the aptist but some say lias and others ne of the prophets
nd he saith unto them ut whom say ye that  am
nd eter answereth and saith unto him hou art the hrist
nd he charged them that they should tell no man of him
nd he began to teach them that the on of man must suffer many things and be rejected of the elders and of the chief priests and scribes and be killed and after three days rise again
nd he spake that saying openly
nd eter took him and began to rebuke him
ut when he had turned about and looked on his disciples he rebuked eter saying et thee behind me atan for thou savourest not the things that be of od but the things that be of men
nd when he had called the people unto him with his disciples also he said unto them hosoever will come after me let him deny himself and take up his cross and follow me
or whosoever will save his life shall lose it but whosoever shall lose his life for my sake and the gospels the same shall save it
or what shall it profit a man if he shall gain the whole world and lose his own soul
r what shall a man give in exchange for his soul
hosoever therefore shall be ashamed of me and of my words in this adulterous and sinful generation of him also shall the on of man be ashamed when he cometh in the glory of his ather with the holy angels
nd he said unto them erily  say unto you hat there be some of them that stand here which shall not taste of death till they have seen the kingdom of od come with power
nd after six days esus taketh with him eter and ames and ohn and leadeth them up into an high mountain apart by themselves and he was transfigured before them
nd his raiment became shining exceeding white as snow so as no fuller on earth can white them
nd there appeared unto them lias with oses and they were talking with esus
nd eter answered and said to esus aster it is good for us to be here and let us make three tabernacles one for thee and one for oses and one for lias
or he wist not what to say for they were sore afraid
nd there was a cloud that overshadowed them and a voice came out of the cloud saying his is my beloved on hear him
nd suddenly when they had looked round about they saw no man any more save esus only with themselves
nd as they came down from the mountain he charged them that they should tell no man what things they had seen till the on of man were risen from the dead
nd they kept that saying with themselves questioning one with another what the rising from the dead should mean
nd they asked him saying hy say the scribes that lias must first come
nd he answered and told them lias verily cometh first and restoreth all things and how it is written of the on of man that he must suffer many things and be set at nought
ut  say unto you hat lias is indeed come and they have done unto him whatsoever they listed as it is written of him
nd when he came to his disciples he saw a great multitude about them and the scribes questioning with them
nd straightway all the people when they beheld him were greatly amazed and running to him saluted him
nd he asked the scribes hat question ye with them
nd one of the multitude answered and said aster  have brought unto thee my son which hath a dumb spirit and wheresoever he taketh him he teareth him and he foameth and gnasheth with his teeth and pineth away and  spake to thy disciples that they should cast him out and they could not
e answereth him and saith  faithless generation how long shall  be with you how long shall  suffer you bring him unto me
nd they brought him unto him and when he saw him straightway the spirit tare him and he fell on the ground and wallowed foaming
nd he asked his father ow long is it ago since this came unto him
nd he said f a child
nd ofttimes it hath cast him into the fire and into the waters to destroy him but if thou canst do any thing have compassion on us and help us
esus said unto him f thou canst believe all things are possible to him that believeth
nd straightway the father of the child cried out and said with tears ord  believe help thou mine unbelief
hen esus saw that the people came running together he rebuked the foul spirit saying unto him hou dumb and deaf spirit  charge thee come out of him and enter no more into him
nd the spirit cried and rent him sore and came out of him and he was as one dead insomuch that many said e is dead
ut esus took him by the hand and lifted him up and he arose
nd when he was come into the house his disciples asked him privately hy could not we cast him out
nd he said unto them his kind can come forth by nothing but by prayer and fasting
nd they departed thence and passed through alilee and he would not that any man should know it
or he taught his disciples and said unto them he on of man is delivered into the hands of men and they shall kill him and after that he is killed he shall rise the third day
ut they understood not that saying and were afraid to ask him
nd he came to apernaum and being in the house he asked them hat was it that ye disputed among yourselves by the way
ut they held their peace for by the way they had disputed among themselves who should be the greatest
nd he sat down and called the twelve and saith unto them f any man desire to be first the same shall be last of all and servant of all
nd he took a child and set him in the midst of them and when he had taken him in his arms he said unto them hosoever shall receive one of such children in my name receiveth me and whosoever shall receive me receiveth not me but him that sent me
nd ohn answered him saying aster we saw one casting out devils in thy name and he followeth not us and we forbad him because he followeth not us
ut esus said orbid him not for there is no man which shall do a miracle in my name that can lightly speak evil of me
or he that is not against us is on our part
or whosoever shall give you a cup of water to drink in my name because ye belong to hrist verily  say unto you he shall not lose his reward
nd whosoever shall offend one of these little ones that believe in me it is better for him that a millstone were hanged about his neck and he were cast into the sea
nd if thy hand offend thee cut it off it is better for thee to enter into life maimed than having two hands to go into hell into the fire that never shall be quenched where their worm dieth not and the fire is not quenched
nd if thy foot offend thee cut it off it is better for thee to enter halt into life than having two feet to be cast into hell into the fire that never shall be quenched where their worm dieth not and the fire is not quenched
nd if thine eye offend thee pluck it out it is better for thee to enter into the kingdom of od with one eye than having two eyes to be cast into hell fire where their worm dieth not and the fire is not quenched
or every one shall be salted with fire and every sacrifice shall be salted with salt
alt is good but if the salt have lost his saltness wherewith will ye season it
ave salt in yourselves and have peace one with another
nd he arose from thence and cometh into the coasts of ud a by the farther side of ordan and the people resort unto him again and as he was wont he taught them again
nd the harisees came to him and asked him s it lawful for a man to put away his wife tempting him
nd he answered and said unto them hat did oses command you
nd they said oses suffered to write a bill of divorcement and to put her away
nd esus answered and said unto them or the hardness of your heart he wrote you this precept
ut from the beginning of the creation od made them male and female
or this cause shall a man leave his father and mother and cleave to his wife and they twain shall be one flesh so then they are no more twain but one flesh
hat therefore od hath joined together let not man put asunder
nd in the house his disciples asked him again of the same matter
nd he saith unto them hosoever shall put away his wife and marry another committeth adultery against her
nd if a woman shall put away her husband and be married to another she committeth adultery
nd they brought young children to him that he should touch them and his disciples rebuked those that brought them
ut when esus saw it he was much displeased and said unto them uffer the little children to come unto me and forbid them not for of such is the kingdom of od
erily  say unto you hosoever shall not receive the kingdom of od as a little child he shall not enter therein
nd he took them up in his arms put his hands upon them and blessed them
nd when he was gone forth into the way there came one running and kneeled to him and asked him ood aster what shall  do that  may inherit eternal life
nd esus said unto him hy callest thou me good there is none good but one that is od
hou knowest the commandments o not commit adultery o not kill o not steal o not bear false witness efraud not onour thy father and mother
nd he answered and said unto him aster all these have  observed from my youth
hen esus beholding him loved him and said unto him ne thing thou lackest go thy way sell whatsoever thou hast and give to the poor and thou shalt have treasure in heaven and come take up the cross and follow me
nd he was sad at that saying and went away grieved for he had great possessions
nd esus looked round about and saith unto his disciples ow hardly shall they that have riches enter into the kingdom of od
nd the disciples were astonished at his words
ut esus answereth again and saith unto them hildren how hard is it for them that trust in riches to enter into the kingdom of od
t is easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle than for a rich man to enter into the kingdom of od
nd they were astonished out of measure saying among themselves ho then can be saved
nd esus looking upon them saith ith men it is impossible but not with od for with od all things are possible
hen eter began to say unto him o we have left all and have followed thee
nd esus answered and said erily  say unto you here is no man that hath left house or brethren or sisters or father or mother or wife or children or lands for my sake and the gospels but he shall receive an hundredfold now in this time houses and brethren and sisters and mothers and children and lands with persecutions and in the world to come eternal life
ut many that are first shall be last and the last first
nd they were in the way going up to erusalem and esus went before them and they were amazed and as they followed they were afraid
nd he took again the twelve and began to tell them what things should happen unto him saying ehold we go up to erusalem and the on of man shall be delivered unto the chief priests and unto the scribes and they shall condemn him to death and shall deliver him to the entiles and they shall mock him and shall scourge him and shall spit upon him and shall kill him and the third day he shall rise again
nd ames and ohn the sons of ebedee come unto him saying aster we would that thou shouldest do for us whatsoever we shall desire
nd he said unto them hat would ye that  should do for you
hey said unto him rant unto us that we may sit one on thy right hand and the other on thy left hand in thy glory
ut esus said unto them e know not what ye ask can ye drink of the cup that  drink of and be baptized with the baptism that  am baptized with
nd they said unto him e can
nd esus said unto them e shall indeed drink of the cup that  drink of and with the baptism that  am baptized withal shall ye be baptized but to sit on my right hand and on my left hand is not mine to give but it shall be given to them for whom it is prepared
nd when the ten heard it they began to be much displeased with ames and ohn
ut esus called them to him and saith unto them e know that they which are accounted to rule over the entiles exercise lordship over them and their great ones exercise authority upon them
ut so shall it not be among you but whosoever will be great among you shall be your minister and whosoever of you will be the chiefest shall be servant of all
or even the on of man came not to be ministered unto but to minister and to give his life a ransom for many
nd they came to ericho and as he went out of ericho with his disciples and a great number of people blind artim us the son of im us sat by the highway side begging
nd when he heard that it was esus of azareth he began to cry out and say esus thou on of avid have mercy on me
nd many charged him that he should hold his peace but he cried the more a great deal hou on of avid have mercy on me
nd esus stood still and commanded him to be called
nd they call the blind man saying unto him e of good comfort rise he calleth thee
nd he casting away his garment rose and came to esus
nd esus answered and said unto him hat wilt thou that  should do unto thee
he blind man said unto him ord that  might receive my sight
nd esus said unto him o thy way thy faith hath made thee whole
nd immediately he received his sight and followed esus in the way
nd when they came nigh to erusalem unto ethphage and ethany at the mount of lives he sendeth forth two of his disciples and saith unto them o your way into the village over against you and as soon as ye be entered into it ye shall find a colt tied whereon never man sat loose him and bring him
nd if any man say unto you hy do ye this say ye that the ord hath need of him and straightway he will send him hither
nd they went their way and found the colt tied by the door without in a place where two ways met and they loose him
nd certain of them that stood there said unto them hat do ye loosing the colt
nd they said unto them even as esus had commanded and they let them go
nd they brought the colt to esus and cast their garments on him and he sat upon him
nd many spread their garments in the way and others cut down branches off the trees and strawed them in the way
nd they that went before and they that followed cried saying osanna lessed is he that cometh in the name of the ord lessed be the kingdom of our father avid that cometh in the name of the ord osanna in the highest
nd esus entered into erusalem and into the temple and when he had looked round about upon all things and now the eventide was come he went out unto ethany with the twelve
nd on the morrow when they were come from ethany he was hungry and seeing a fig tree afar off having leaves he came if haply he might find any thing thereon and when he came to it he found nothing but leaves for the time of figs was not yet
nd esus answered and said unto it o man eat fruit of thee hereafter for ever
nd his disciples heard it
nd they come to erusalem and esus went into the temple and began to cast out them that sold and bought in the temple and overthrew the tables of the moneychangers and the seats of them that sold doves and would not suffer that any man should carry any vessel through the temple
nd he taught saying unto them s it not written y house shall be called of all nations the house of prayer but ye have made it a den of thieves
nd the scribes and chief priests heard it and sought how they might destroy him for they feared him because all the people was astonished at his doctrine
nd when even was come he went out of the city
nd in the morning as they passed by they saw the fig tree dried up from the roots
nd eter calling to remembrance saith unto him aster behold the fig tree which thou cursedst is withered away
nd esus answering saith unto them ave faith in od
or verily  say unto you hat whosoever shall say unto this mountain e thou removed and be thou cast into the sea and shall not doubt in his heart but shall believe that those things which he saith shall come to pass he shall have whatsoever he saith
herefore  say unto you hat things soever ye desire when ye pray believe that ye receive them and ye shall have them
nd when ye stand praying forgive if ye have ought against any that your ather also which is in heaven may forgive you your trespasses
ut if ye do not forgive neither will your ather which is in heaven forgive your trespasses
nd they come again to erusalem and as he was walking in the temple there come to him the chief priests and the scribes and the elders and say unto him y what authority doest thou these things and who gave thee this authority to do these things
nd esus answered and said unto them  will also ask of you one question and answer me and  will tell you by what authority  do these things
he baptism of ohn was it from heaven or of men answer me
nd they reasoned with themselves saying f we shall say rom heaven he will say hy then did ye not believe him
ut if we shall say f men they feared the people for all men counted ohn that he was a prophet indeed
nd they answered and said unto esus e cannot tell
nd esus answering saith unto them either do  tell you by what authority  do these things
nd he began to speak unto them by parables
 certain man planted a vineyard and set an hedge about it and digged a place for the winefat and built a tower and let it out to husbandmen and went into a far country
nd at the season he sent to the husbandmen a servant that he might receive from the husbandmen of the fruit of the vineyard
nd they caught him and beat him and sent him away empty
nd again he sent unto them another servant and at him they cast stones and wounded him in the head and sent him away shamefully handled
nd again he sent another and him they killed and many others beating some and killing some
