aving yet therefore one son his wellbeloved he sent him also last unto them saying hey will reverence my son
ut those husbandmen said among themselves his is the heir come let us kill him and the inheritance shall be ours
nd they took him and killed him and cast him out of the vineyard
hat shall therefore the lord of the vineyard do he will come and destroy the husbandmen and will give the vineyard unto others
nd have ye not read this scripture he stone which the builders rejected s become the head of the corner his was the ords doing nd it is marvellous in our eyes
nd they sought to lay hold on him but feared the people for they knew that he had spoken the parable against them and they left him and went their way
nd they send unto him certain of the harisees and of the erodians to catch him in his words
nd when they were come they say unto him aster we know that thou art true and carest for no man for thou regardest not the person of men but teachest the way of od in truth s it lawful to give tribute to  sar or not
hall we give or shall we not give
ut he knowing their hypocrisy said unto them hy tempt ye me bring me a penny that  may see it
nd they brought it
nd he saith unto them hose is this image and superscription
nd they said unto him  sars
nd esus answering said unto them ender to  sar the things that are  sars and to od the things that are ods
nd they marvelled at him
hen come unto him the adducees which say there is no resurrection and they asked him saying aster oses wrote unto us f a mans brother die and leave his wife behind him and leave no children that his brother should take his wife and raise up seed unto his brother
ow there were seven brethren and the first took a wife and dying left no seed
nd the second took her and died neither left he any seed and the third likewise
nd the seven had her and left no seed last of all the woman died also
n the resurrection therefore when they shall rise whose wife shall she be of them for the seven had her to wife
nd esus answering said unto them o ye not therefore err because ye know not the scriptures neither the power of od
or when they shall rise from the dead they neither marry nor are given in marriage but are as the angels which are in heaven
nd as touching the dead that they rise have ye not read in the book of oses how in the bush od spake unto him saying  am the od of braham and the od of saac and the od of acob
e is not the od of the dead but the od of the living ye therefore do greatly err
nd one of the scribes came and having heard them reasoning together and perceiving that he had answered them well asked him hich is the first commandment of all
nd esus answered him he first of all the commandments is  ear  srael he ord our od is one ord and thou shalt love the ord thy od with all thy heart and with all thy soul and with all thy mind and with all thy strength this is the first commandment
nd the second is like namely this hou shalt love thy neighbour as thyself
here is none other commandment greater than these
nd the scribe said unto him ell aster thou hast said the truth for there is one od and there is none other but he and to love him with all the heart and with all the understanding and with all the soul and with all the strength and to love his neighbour as himself is more than all whole burnt offerings and sacrifices
nd when esus saw that he answered discreetly he said unto him hou art not far from the kingdom of od
nd no man after that durst ask him any question
nd esus answered and said while he taught in the temple ow say the scribes that hrist is the son of avid
or avid himself said by the oly host he  said to my ord it thou on my right hand ill  make thine enemies thy footstool
avid therefore himself calleth him ord and whence is he then his son
nd the common people heard him gladly
nd he said unto them in his doctrine eware of the scribes which love to go in long clothing and love salutations in the marketplaces and the chief seats in the synagogues and the uppermost rooms at feasts which devour widows houses and for a pretence make long prayers these shall receive greater damnation
nd esus sat over against the treasury and beheld how the people cast money into the treasury and many that were rich cast in much
nd there came a certain poor widow and she threw in two mites which make a farthing
nd he called unto him his disciples and saith unto them erily  say unto you hat this poor widow hath cast more in than all they which have cast into the treasury for all they did cast in of their abundance but she of her want did cast in all that she had even all her living
nd as he went out of the temple one of his disciples saith unto him aster see what manner of stones and what buildings are here
nd esus answering said unto him eest thou these great buildings there shall not be left one stone upon another that shall not be thrown down
nd as he sat upon the mount of lives over against the temple eter and ames and ohn and ndrew asked him privately ell us when shall these things be and what shall be the sign when all these things shall be fulfilled
nd esus answering them began to say ake heed lest any man deceive you for many shall come in my name saying  am hrist and shall deceive many
nd when ye shall hear of wars and rumours of wars be ye not troubled for such things must needs be but the end shall not be yet
or nation shall rise against nation and kingdom against kingdom and there shall be earthquakes in divers places and there shall be famines and troubles these are the beginnings of sorrows
ut take heed to yourselves for they shall deliver you up to councils and in the synagogues ye shall be beaten and ye shall be brought before rulers and kings for my sake for a testimony against them
nd the gospel must first be published among all nations
ut when they shall lead you and deliver you up take no thought beforehand what ye shall speak neither do ye premeditate but whatsoever shall be given you in that hour that speak ye for it is not ye that speak but the oly host
ow the brother shall betray the brother to death and the father the son and children shall rise up against their parents and shall cause them to be put to death
nd ye shall be hated of all men for my names sake but he that shall endure unto the end the same shall be saved
ut when ye shall see the abomination of desolation spoken of by aniel the prophet standing where it ought not let him that readeth understand  then let them that be in ud a flee to the mountains and let him that is on the housetop not go down into the house neither enter therein to take any thing out of his house and let him that is in the field not turn back again for to take up his garment
ut woe to them that are with child and to them that give suck in those days
nd pray ye that your flight be not in the winter
or in those days shall be affliction such as was not from the beginning of the creation which od created unto this time neither shall be
nd except that the ord had shortened those days no flesh should be saved but for the elects sake whom he hath chosen he hath shortened the days
nd then if any man shall say to you o here is hrist or lo he is there believe him not for false hrists and false prophets shall rise and shall shew signs and wonders to seduce if it were possible even the elect
ut take ye heed behold  have foretold you all things
ut in those days after that tribulation the sun shall be darkened and the moon shall not give her light and the stars of heaven shall fall and the powers that are in heaven shall be shaken
nd then shall they see the on of man coming in the clouds with great power and glory
nd then shall he send his angels and shall gather together his elect from the four winds from the uttermost part of the earth to the uttermost part of heaven
ow learn a parable of the fig tree hen her branch is yet tender and putteth forth leaves ye know that summer is near so ye in like manner when ye shall see these things come to pass know that it is nigh even at the doors
erily  say unto you that this generation shall not pass till all these things be done
eaven and earth shall pass away but my words shall not pass away
ut of that day and that hour knoweth no man no not the angels which are in heaven neither the on but the ather
ake ye heed watch and pray for ye know not when the time is
or the on of man is as a man taking a far journey who left his house and gave authority to his servants and to every man his work and commanded the porter to watch
atch ye therefore for ye know not when the master of the house cometh at even or at midnight or at the cockcrowing or in the morning lest coming suddenly he find you sleeping
nd what  say unto you  say unto all atch
fter two days was the feast of the passover and of unleavened bread and the chief priests and the scribes sought how they might take him by craft and put him to death
ut they said ot on the feast day lest there be an uproar of the people
nd being in ethany in the house of imon the leper as he sat at meat there came a woman having an alabaster box of ointment of spikenard very precious and she brake the box and poured it on his head
nd there were some that had indignation within themselves and said hy was this waste of the ointment made
or it might have been sold for more than three hundred pence and have been given to the poor
nd they murmured against her
nd esus said et her alone why trouble ye her she hath wrought a good work on me
or ye have the poor with you always and whensoever ye will ye may do them good but me ye have not always
he hath done what she could she is come aforehand to anoint my body to the burying
erily  say unto you heresoever this gospel shall be preached throughout the whole world this also that she hath done shall be spoken of for a memorial of her
nd udas scariot one of the twelve went unto the chief priests to betray him unto them
nd when they heard it they were glad and promised to give him money
nd he sought how he might conveniently betray him
nd the first day of unleavened bread when they killed the passover his disciples said unto him here wilt thou that we go and prepare that thou mayest eat the passover
nd he sendeth forth two of his disciples and saith unto them o ye into the city and there shall meet you a man bearing a pitcher of water follow him
nd wheresoever he shall go in say ye to the goodman of the house he aster saith here is the guestchamber where  shall eat the passover with my disciples
nd he will shew you a large upper room furnished and prepared there make ready for us
nd his disciples went forth and came into the city and found as he had said unto them and they made ready the passover
nd in the evening he cometh with the twelve
nd as they sat and did eat esus said erily  say unto you ne of you which eateth with me shall betray me
nd they began to be sorrowful and to say unto him one by one s it  and another said s it 
nd he answered and said unto them t is one of the twelve that dippeth with me in the dish
he on of man indeed goeth as it is written of him but woe to that man by whom the on of man is betrayed good were it for that man if he had never been born
nd as they did eat esus took bread and blessed and brake it and gave to them and said ake eat this is my body
nd he took the cup and when he had given thanks he gave it to them and they all drank of it
nd he said unto them his is my blood of the new testament which is shed for many
erily  say unto you  will drink no more of the fruit of the vine until that day that  drink it new in the kingdom of od
nd when they had sung an hymn they went out into the mount of lives
nd esus saith unto them ll ye shall be offended because of me this night for it is written  will smite the shepherd and the sheep shall be scattered
ut after that  am risen  will go before you into alilee
ut eter said unto him lthough all shall be offended yet will not 
nd esus saith unto him erily  say unto thee hat this day even in this night before the cock crow twice thou shalt deny me thrice
ut he spake the more vehemently f  should die with thee  will not deny thee in any wise
ikewise also said they all
nd they came to a place which was named ethsemane and he saith to his disciples it ye here while  shall pray
nd he taketh with him eter and ames and ohn and began to be sore amazed and to be very heavy and saith unto them y soul is exceeding sorrowful unto death tarry ye here and watch
nd he went forward a little and fell on the ground and prayed that if it were possible the hour might pass from him
nd he said bba ather all things are possible unto thee take away this cup from me nevertheless not what  will but what thou wilt
nd he cometh and findeth them sleeping and saith unto eter imon sleepest thou couldest not thou watch one hour
atch ye and pray lest ye enter into temptation
he spirit truly is ready but the flesh is weak
nd again he went away and prayed and spake the same words
nd when he returned he found them asleep again for their eyes were heavy  neither wist they what to answer him
nd he cometh the third time and saith unto them leep on now and take your rest it is enough the hour is come behold the on of man is betrayed into the hands of sinners
ise up let us go lo he that betrayeth me is at hand
nd immediately while he yet spake cometh udas one of the twelve and with him a great multitude with swords and staves from the chief priests and the scribes and the elders
nd he that betrayed him had given them a token saying homsoever  shall kiss that same is he take him and lead him away safely
nd as soon as he was come he goeth straightway to him and saith aster master and kissed him
nd they laid their hands on him and took him
nd one of them that stood by drew a sword and smote a servant of the high priest and cut off his ear
nd esus answered and said unto them re ye come out as against a thief with swords and with staves to take me
 was daily with you in the temple teaching and ye took me not but the scriptures must be fulfilled
nd they all forsook him and fled
nd there followed him a certain young man having a linen cloth cast about his naked body and the young men laid hold on him and he left the linen cloth and fled from them naked
nd they led esus away to the high priest and with him were assembled all the chief priests and the elders and the scribes
nd eter followed him afar off even into the palace of the high priest and he sat with the servants and warmed himself at the fire
nd the chief priests and all the council sought for witness against esus to put him to death and found none
or many bare false witness against him but their witness agreed not together
nd there arose certain and bare false witness against him saying e heard him say  will destroy this temple that is made with hands and within three days  will build another made without hands
ut neither so did their witness agree together
nd the high priest stood up in the midst and asked esus saying nswerest thou nothing what is it which these witness against thee
ut he held his peace and answered nothing
gain the high priest asked him and said unto him rt thou the hrist the on of the lessed
nd esus said  am and ye shall see the on of man sitting on the right hand of power and coming in the clouds of heaven
hen the high priest rent his clothes and saith hat need we any further witnesses
e have heard the blasphemy what think ye
nd they all condemned him to be guilty of death
nd some began to spit on him and to cover his face and to buffet him and to say unto him rophesy and the servants did strike him with the palms of their hands
nd as eter was beneath in the palace there cometh one of the maids of the high priest and when she saw eter warming himself she looked upon him and said nd thou also wast with esus of azareth
ut he denied saying  know not neither understand  what thou sayest
nd he went out into the porch and the cock crew
nd a maid saw him again and began to say to them that stood by his is one of them
nd he denied it again
nd a little after they that stood by said again to eter urely thou art one of them for thou art a alil an and thy speech agreeth thereto
ut he began to curse and to swear saying  know not this man of whom ye speak
nd the second time the cock crew
nd eter called to mind the word that esus said unto him efore the cock crow twice thou shalt deny me thrice
nd when he thought thereon he wept
nd straightway in the morning the chief priests held a consultation with the elders and scribes and the whole council and bound esus and carried him away and delivered him to ilate
nd ilate asked him rt thou the ing of the ews
nd he answering said unto him hou sayest it 
nd the chief priests accused him of many things but he answered nothing
nd ilate asked him again saying nswerest thou nothing behold how many things they witness against thee
ut esus yet answered nothing so that ilate marvelled
ow at that feast he released unto them one prisoner whomsoever they desired
nd there was one named arabbas which lay bound with them that had made insurrection with him who had committed murder in the insurrection
nd the multitude crying aloud began to desire him to do as he had ever done unto them
ut ilate answered them saying ill ye that  release unto you the ing of the ews
or he knew that the chief priests had delivered him for envy
ut the chief priests moved the people that he should rather release arabbas unto them
nd ilate answered and said again unto them hat will ye then that  shall do unto him whom ye call the ing of the ews
nd they cried out again rucify him
hen ilate said unto them hy what evil hath he done
nd they cried out the more exceedingly rucify him
nd so ilate willing to content the people released arabbas unto them and delivered esus when he had scourged him to be crucified
nd the soldiers led him away into the hall called r torium and they call together the whole band
nd they clothed him with purple and platted a crown of thorns and put it about his head and began to salute him ail ing of the ews
nd they smote him on the head with a reed and did spit upon him and bowing their knees worshipped him
nd when they had mocked him they took off the purple from him and put his own clothes on him and led him out to crucify him
nd they compel one imon a yrenian who passed by coming out of the country the father of lexander and ufus to bear his cross
nd they bring him unto the place olgotha which is being interpreted he place of a skull
nd they gave him to drink wine mingled with myrrh but he received it not
nd when they had crucified him they parted his garments casting lots upon them what every man should take
nd it was the third hour and they crucified him
nd the superscription of his accusation was written over     
nd with him they crucify two thieves the one on his right hand and the other on his left
nd the scripture was fulfilled which saith nd he was numbered with the transgressors
nd they that passed by railed on him wagging their heads and saying h thou that destroyest the temple and buildest it in three days save thyself and come down from the cross
ikewise also the chief priests mocking said among themselves with the scribes e saved others himself he cannot save
et hrist the ing of srael descend now from the cross that we may see and believe
nd they that were crucified with him reviled him
nd when the sixth hour was come there was darkness over the whole land until the ninth hour
nd at the ninth hour esus cried with a loud voice saying loi loi lama sabachthani which is being interpreted y od my od why hast thou forsaken me
nd some of them that stood by when they heard it said ehold he calleth lias
nd one ran and filled a spunge full of vinegar and put it on a reed and gave him to drink saying et alone let us see whether lias will come to take him down
nd esus cried with a loud voice and gave up the ghost
nd the veil of the temple was rent in twain from the top to the bottom
nd when the centurion which stood over against him saw that he so cried out and gave up the ghost he said ruly this man was the on of od
here were also women looking on afar off among whom was ary agdalene and ary the mother of ames the less and of oses and alome who also when he was in alilee followed him and ministered unto him and many other women which came up with him unto erusalem
nd now when the even was come because it was the preparation that is the day before the sabbath oseph of rimath a an honourable counsellor which also waited for the kingdom of od came and went in boldly unto ilate and craved the body of esus
nd ilate marvelled if he were already dead and calling unto him the centurion he asked him whether he had been any while dead
nd when he knew it of the centurion he gave the body to oseph
nd he bought fine linen and took him down and wrapped him in the linen and laid him in a sepulchre which was hewn out of a rock and rolled a stone unto the door of the sepulchre
nd ary agdalene and ary the mother of oses beheld where he was laid
nd when the sabbath was past ary agdalene and ary the mother of ames and alome had bought sweet spices that they might come and anoint him
nd very early in the morning the first day of the week they came unto the sepulchre at the rising of the sun
nd they said among themselves ho shall roll us away the stone from the door of the sepulchre
nd when they looked they saw that the stone was rolled away for it was very great
nd entering into the sepulchre they saw a young man sitting on the right side clothed in a long white garment and they were affrighted
nd he saith unto them e not affrighted e seek esus of azareth which was crucified he is risen he is not here behold the place where they laid him
ut go your way tell his disciples and eter that he goeth before you into alilee there shall ye see him as he said unto you
nd they went out quickly and fled from the sepulchre for they trembled and were amazed neither said they any thing to any man for they were afraid
ow when esus was risen early the first day of the week he appeared first to ary agdalene out of whom he had cast seven devils
nd she went and told them that had been with him as they mourned and wept
nd they when they had heard that he was alive and had been seen of her believed not
fter that he appeared in another form unto two of them as they walked and went into the country
nd they went and told it unto the residue neither believed they them
fterward he appeared unto the eleven as they sat at meat and upbraided them with their unbelief and hardness of heart because they believed not them which had seen him after he was risen
nd he said unto them o ye into all the world and preach the gospel to every creature
e that believeth and is baptized shall be saved but he that believeth not shall be damned
nd these signs shall follow them that believe n my name shall they cast out devils they shall speak with new tongues they shall take up serpents and if they drink any deadly thing it shall not hurt them they shall lay hands on the sick and they shall recover
o then after the ord had spoken unto them he was received up into heaven and sat on the right hand of od
nd they went forth and preached every where the ord working with them and confirming the word with signs following
men
orasmuch as many have taken in hand to set forth in order a declaration of those things which are most surely believed among us even as they delivered them unto us which from the beginning were eyewitnesses and ministers of the word it seemed good to me also having had perfect understanding of all things from the very first to write unto thee in order most excellent heophilus that thou mightest know the certainty of those things wherein thou hast been instructed
here was in the days of erod the king of ud a a certain priest named acharias of the course of bia and his wife was of the daughters of aron and her name was lisabeth
nd they were both righteous before od walking in all the commandments and ordinances of the ord blameless
nd they had no child because that lisabeth was barren and they both were now well stricken in years
nd it came to pass that while he executed the priests office before od in the order of his course according to the custom of the priests office his lot was to burn incense when he went into the temple of the ord
nd the whole multitude of the people were praying without at the time of incense
nd there appeared unto him an angel of the ord standing on the right side of the altar of incense
nd when acharias saw him he was troubled and fear fell upon him
ut the angel said unto him ear not acharias for thy prayer is heard and thy wife lisabeth shall bear thee a son and thou shalt call his name ohn
nd thou shalt have joy and gladness and many shall rejoice at his birth
or he shall be great in the sight of the ord and shall drink neither wine nor strong drink and he shall be filled with the oly host even from his mothers womb
nd many of the children of srael shall he turn to the ord their od
nd he shall go before him in the spirit and power of lias to turn the hearts of the fathers to the children and the disobedient to the wisdom of the just to make ready a people prepared for the ord
nd acharias said unto the angel hereby shall  know this for  am an old man and my wife well stricken in years
nd the angel answering said unto him  am abriel that stand in the presence of od and am sent to speak unto thee and to shew thee these glad tidings
nd behold thou shalt be dumb and not able to speak until the day that these things shall be performed because thou believest not my words which shall be fulfilled in their season
nd the people waited for acharias and marvelled that he tarried so long in the temple
nd when he came out he could not speak unto them and they perceived that he had seen a vision in the temple for he beckoned unto them and remained speechless
nd it came to pass that as soon as the days of his ministration were accomplished he departed to his own house
nd after those days his wife lisabeth conceived and hid herself five months saying hus hath the ord dealt with me in the days wherein he looked on me to take away my reproach among men
nd in the sixth month the angel abriel was sent from od unto a city of alilee named azareth to a virgin espoused to a man whose name was oseph of the house of avid and the virgins name was ary
nd the angel came in unto her and said ail thou that art highly favoured the ord is with thee blessed art thou among women
nd when she saw him she was troubled at his saying and cast in her mind what manner of salutation this should be
nd the angel said unto her ear not ary for thou hast found favour with od
nd behold thou shalt conceive in thy womb and bring forth a son and shalt call his name 
e shall be great and shall be called the on of the ighest and the ord od shall give unto him the throne of his father avid and he shall reign over the house of acob for ever and of his kingdom there shall be no end
hen said ary unto the angel ow shall this be seeing  know not a man
nd the angel answered and said unto her he oly host shall come upon thee and the power of the ighest shall overshadow thee therefore also that holy thing which shall be born of thee shall be called the on of od
nd behold thy cousin lisabeth she hath also conceived a son in her old age and this is the sixth month with her who was called barren
or with od nothing shall be impossible
nd ary said ehold the handmaid of the ord be it unto me according to thy word
nd the angel departed from her
nd ary arose in those days and went into the hill country with haste into a city of uda and entered into the house of acharias and saluted lisabeth
nd it came to pass that when lisabeth heard the salutation of ary the babe leaped in her womb and lisabeth was filled with the oly host and she spake out with a loud voice and said lessed art thou among women and blessed is the fruit of thy womb
nd whence is this to me that the mother of my ord should come to me
or lo as soon as the voice of thy salutation sounded in mine ears the babe leaped in my womb for joy
nd blessed is she that believed for there shall be a performance of those things which were told her from the ord
nd ary said y soul doth magnify the ord nd my spirit hath rejoiced in od my aviour
or he hath regarded the low estate of his handmaiden or behold from henceforth all generations shall call me blessed
or he that is mighty hath done to me great things nd holy is his name
nd his mercy is on them that fear himrom generation to generation
e hath shewed strength with his arm e hath scattered the proud in the imagination of their hearts
e hath put down the mighty from their seats nd exalted them of low degree
e hath filled the hungry with good things nd the rich he hath sent empty away
e hath holpen his servant srael n remembrance of his mercy s he spake to our fathers o braham and to his seed for ever
nd ary abode with her about three months and returned to her own house
ow lisabeths full time came that she should be delivered and she brought forth a son
nd her neighbours and her cousins heard how the ord had shewed great mercy upon her and they rejoiced with her
nd it came to pass that on the eighth day they came to circumcise the child and they called him acharias after the name of his father
nd his mother answered and said ot so but he shall be called ohn
nd they said unto her here is none of thy kindred that is called by this name
nd they made signs to his father how he would have him called
nd he asked for a writing table and wrote saying is name is ohn
nd they marvelled all
nd his mouth was opened immediately and his tongue loosed and he spake and praised od
nd fear came on all that dwelt round about them and all these sayings were noised abroad throughout all the hill country of ud a
nd all they that heard them laid them up in their hearts saying hat manner of child shall this be
nd the hand of the ord was with him
nd his father acharias was filled with the oly host and prophesied saying lessed be the ord od of srael or he hath visited and redeemed his people nd hath raised up an horn of salvation for usn the house of his servant avid s he spake by the mouth of his holy prophets which have been since the world began hat we should be saved from our enemies and from the hand of all that hate us o perform the mercy promised to our fathers nd to remember his holy covenant he oath which he sware to our father braham hat he would grant unto us that we being delivered out of the hand of our enemiesight serve him without fear n holiness and righteousness before him all the days of our life
nd thou child shalt be called the prophet of the ighest or thou shalt go before the face of the ord to prepare his ways o give knowledge of salvation unto his peopley the remission of their sins hrough the tender mercy of our od hereby the dayspring from on high hath visited us o give light to them that sit in darkness and in the shadow of death o guide our feet into the way of peace
nd the child grew and waxed strong in spirit and was in the deserts till the day of his shewing unto srael
nd it came to pass in those days that there went out a decree from  sar ugustus that all the world should be taxed
nd this taxing was first made when yrenius was governor of yria nd all went to be taxed every one into his own city
nd oseph also went up from alilee out of the city of azareth into ud a unto the city of avid which is called ethlehem because he was of the house and lineage of avid to be taxed with ary his espoused wife being great with child
nd so it was that while they were there the days were accomplished that she should be delivered
nd she brought forth her firstborn son and wrapped him in swaddling clothes and laid him in a manger because there was no room for them in the inn
nd there were in the same country shepherds abiding in the field keeping watch over their flock by night
nd lo the angel of the ord came upon them and the glory of the ord shone round about them and they were sore afraid
nd the angel said unto them ear not for behold  bring you good tidings of great joy which shall be to all people
or unto you is born this day in the city of avid a aviour which is hrist the ord
nd this shall be a sign unto you e shall find the babe wrapped in swaddling clothes lying in a manger
nd suddenly there was with the angel a multitude of the heavenly host praising od and saying lory to od in the highest nd on earth peace good will toward men
nd it came to pass as the angels were gone away from them into heaven the shepherds said one to another et us now go even unto ethlehem and see this thing which is come to pass which the ord hath made known unto us
nd they came with haste and found ary and oseph and the babe lying in a manger
nd when they had seen it they made known abroad the saying which was told them concerning this child
nd all they that heard it wondered at those things which were told them by the shepherds
ut ary kept all these things and pondered them in her heart
nd the shepherds returned glorifying and praising od for all the things that they had heard and seen as it was told unto them
nd when eight days were accomplished for the circumcising of the child his name was called  which was so named of the angel before he was conceived in the womb
nd when the days of her purification according to the law of oses were accomplished they brought him to erusalem to present him to the ord as it is written in the law of the ord very male that openeth the womb shall be called holy to the ord and to offer a sacrifice according to that which is said in the law of the ord  pair of turtledoves or two young pigeons
nd behold there was a man in erusalem whose name was imeon and the same man was just and devout waiting for the consolation of srael and the oly host was upon him
nd it was revealed unto him by the oly host that he should not see death before he had seen the ords hrist
nd he came by the pirit into the temple and when the parents brought in the child esus to do for him after the custom of the law then took he him up in his arms and blessed od and said ord now lettest thou thy servant depart in peace ccording to thy word or mine eyes have seen thy salvation hich thou hast prepared before the face of all people  light to lighten the entiles nd the glory of thy people srael
nd oseph and his mother marvelled at those things which were spoken of him
nd imeon blessed them and said unto ary his mother ehold this child is set for the fall and rising again of many in srael and for a sign which shall be spoken against yea a sword shall pierce through thy own soul also  that the thoughts of many hearts may be revealed
nd there was one nna a prophetess the daughter of hanuel of the tribe of ser she was of a great age and had lived with an husband seven years from her virginity and she was a widow of about fourscore and four years which departed not from the temple but served od with fastings and prayers night and day
nd she coming in that instant gave thanks likewise unto the ord and spake of him to all them that looked for redemption in erusalem
nd when they had performed all things according to the law of the ord they returned into alilee to their own city azareth
nd the child grew and waxed strong in spirit filled with wisdom and the grace of od was upon him
ow his parents went to erusalem every year at the feast of the passover
nd when he was twelve years old they went up to erusalem after the custom of the feast
nd when they had fulfilled the days as they returned the child esus tarried behind in erusalem and oseph and his mother knew not of it
ut they supposing him to have been in the company went a days journey and they sought him among their kinsfolk and acquaintance
nd when they found him not they turned back again to erusalem seeking him
nd it came to pass that after three days they found him in the temple sitting in the midst of the doctors both hearing them and asking them questions
nd all that heard him were astonished at his understanding and answers
nd when they saw him they were amazed and his mother said unto him on why hast thou thus dealt with us behold thy father and  have sought thee sorrowing
nd he said unto them ow is it that ye sought me wist ye not that  must be about my athers business
nd they understood not the saying which he spake unto them
nd he went down with them and came to azareth and was subject unto them but his mother kept all these sayings in her heart
nd esus increased in wisdom and stature and in favour with od and man
ow in the fifteenth year of the reign of iberius  sar ontius ilate being governor of ud a and erod being tetrarch of alilee and his brother hilip tetrarch of tur a and of the region of rachonitis and ysanias the tetrarch of bilene nnas and aiaphas being the high priests the word of od came unto ohn the son of acharias in the wilderness
nd he came into all the country about ordan preaching the baptism of repentance for the remission of sins as it is written in the book of the words of saias the prophet saying he voice of one crying in the wilderness repare ye the way of the ord ake his paths straight
very valley shall be filled nd every mountain and hill shall be brought low nd the crooked shall be made straight nd the rough ways shall be made smooth nd all flesh shall see the salvation of od
hen said he to the multitude that came forth to be baptized of him  generation of vipers who hath warned you to flee from the wrath to come
ring forth therefore fruits worthy of repentance and begin not to say within yourselves e have braham to our father for  say unto you hat od is able of these stones to raise up children unto braham
nd now also the axe is laid unto the root of the trees every tree therefore which bringeth not forth good fruit is hewn down and cast into the fire
nd the people asked him saying hat shall we do then
e answereth and saith unto them e that hath two coats let him impart to him that hath none and he that hath meat let him do likewise
hen came also publicans to be baptized and said unto him aster what shall we do
nd he said unto them xact no more than that which is appointed you
nd the soldiers likewise demanded of him saying nd what shall we do
nd he said unto them o violence to no man neither accuse any falsely and be content with your wages
nd as the people were in expectation and all men mused in their hearts of ohn whether he were the hrist or not ohn answered saying unto them all  indeed baptize you with water but one mightier than  cometh the latchet of whose shoes  am not worthy to unloose he shall baptize you with the oly host and with fire whose fan is in his hand and he will throughly purge his floor and will gather the wheat into his garner but the chaff he will burn with fire unquenchable
nd many other things in his exhortation preached he unto the people
ut erod the tetrarch being reproved by him for erodias his brother hilips wife and for all the evils which erod had done added yet this above all that he shut up ohn in prison
ow when all the people were baptized it came to pass that esus also being baptized and praying the heaven was opened and the oly host descended in a bodily shape like a dove upon him and a voice came from heaven which said hou art my beloved on in thee  am well pleased
nd esus himself began to be about thirty years of age being as was supposed the son of oseph which was the son of eli which was the son of atthat which was the son of evi which was the son of elchi which was the son of anna which was the son of oseph which was the son of attathias which was the son of mos which was the son of aum which was the son of sli which was the son of agge which was the son of aath which was the son of attathias which was the son of emei which was the son of oseph which was the son of uda which was the son of oanna which was the son of hesa which was the son of orobabel which was the son of alathiel which was the son of eri which was the son of elchi which was the son of ddi which was the son of osam which was the son of lmodam which was the son of r which was the son of ose which was the son of liezer which was the son of orim which was the son of atthat which was the son of evi which was the son of imeon which was the son of uda which was the son of oseph which was the son of onan which was the son of liakim which was the son of elea which was the son of enan which was the son of attatha which was the son of athan which was the son of avid which was the son of esse which was the son of bed which was the son of ooz which was the son of almon which was the son of aasson which was the son of minadab which was the son of ram which was the son of srom which was the son of hares which was the son of uda which was the son of acob which was the son of saac which was the son of braham which was the son of hara which was the son of achor which was the son of aruch which was the son of agau which was the son of halec which was the son of eber which was the son of ala which was the son of ainan which was the son of rphaxad which was the son of em which was the son of oe which was the son of amech which was the son of athusala which was the son of noch which was the son of ared which was the son of aleleel which was the son of ainan which was the son of nos which was the son of eth which was the son of dam which was the son of od
nd esus being full of the oly host returned from ordan and was led by the pirit into the wilderness being forty days tempted of the devil
nd in those days he did eat nothing and when they were ended he afterward hungered
nd the devil said unto him f thou be the on of od command this stone that it be made bread
nd esus answered him saying t is written hat man shall not live by bread alone but by every word of od
nd the devil taking him up into an high mountain shewed unto him all the kingdoms of the world in a moment of time
nd the devil said unto him ll this power will  give thee and the glory of them for that is delivered unto me and to whomsoever  will  give it
f thou therefore wilt worship me all shall be thine
nd esus answered and said unto him et thee behind me atan for it is written hou shalt worship the ord thy od and him only shalt thou serve
nd he brought him to erusalem and set him on a pinnacle of the temple and said unto him f thou be the on of od cast thyself down from hence for it is written e shall give his angels charge over thee to keep thee nd in their hands they shall bear thee up lest at any time thou dash thy foot against a stone
nd esus answering said unto him t is said hou shalt not tempt the ord thy od
nd when the devil had ended all the temptation he departed from him for a season
nd esus returned in the power of the pirit into alilee and there went out a fame of him through all the region round about
nd he taught in their synagogues being glorified of all
nd he came to azareth where he had been brought up and as his custom was he went into the synagogue on the sabbath day and stood up for to read
nd there was delivered unto him the book of the prophet saias
nd when he had opened the book he found the place where it was written he pirit of the ord is upon me ecause he hath anointed me to preach the gospel to the poor e hath sent me to heal the brokenhearted to preach deliverance to the captives nd recovering of sight to the blind o set at liberty them that are bruised o preach the acceptable year of the ord
nd he closed the book and he gave it again to the minister and sat down
nd the eyes of all them that were in the synagogue were fastened on him
nd he began to say unto them his day is this scripture fulfilled in your ears
nd all bare him witness and wondered at the gracious words which proceeded out of his mouth
nd they said s not this osephs son
nd he said unto them e will surely say unto me this proverb hysician heal thyself whatsoever we have heard done in apernaum do also here in thy country
nd he said erily  say unto you o prophet is accepted in his own country
ut  tell you of a truth many widows were in srael in the days of lias when the heaven was shut up three years and six months when great famine was throughout all the land but unto none of them was lias sent save unto arepta a city of idon unto a woman that was a widow
nd many lepers were in srael in the time of liseus the prophet and none of them was cleansed saving aaman the yrian
nd all they in the synagogue when they heard these things were filled with wrath nd rose up and thrust him out of the city and led him unto the brow of the hill whereon their city was built that they might cast him down headlong
ut he passing through the midst of them went his way and came down to apernaum a city of alilee and taught them on the sabbath days
nd they were astonished at his doctrine for his word was with power
nd in the synagogue there was a man which had a spirit of an unclean devil and cried out with a loud voice saying et us alone what have we to do with thee thou esus of azareth art thou come to destroy us
 know thee who thou art the oly ne of od
nd esus rebuked him saying old thy peace and come out of him
nd when the devil had thrown him in the midst he came out of him and hurt him not
nd they were all amazed and spake among themselves saying hat a word is this for with authority and power he commandeth the unclean spirits and they come out
nd the fame of him went out into every place of the country round about
nd he arose out of the synagogue and entered into imons house
nd imons wifes mother was taken with a great fever and they besought him for her
nd he stood over her and rebuked the fever and it left her and immediately she arose and ministered unto them
ow when the sun was setting all they that had any sick with divers diseases brought them unto him and he laid his hands on every one of them and healed them
nd devils also came out of many crying out and saying hou art hrist the on of od
nd he rebuking them suffered them not to speak for they knew that he was hrist
nd when it was day he departed and went into a desert place and the people sought him and came unto him and stayed him that he should not depart from them
nd he said unto them  must preach the kingdom of od to other cities also for therefore am  sent
nd he preached in the synagogues of alilee
nd it came to pass that as the people pressed upon him to hear the word of od he stood by the lake of ennesaret and saw two ships standing by the lake but the fishermen were gone out of them and were washing their nets
nd he entered into one of the ships which was imons and prayed him that he would thrust out a little from the land
nd he sat down and taught the people out of the ship
ow when he had left speaking he said unto imon aunch out into the deep and let down your nets for a draught
nd imon answering said unto him aster we have toiled all the night and have taken nothing nevertheless at thy word  will let down the net
nd when they had this done they inclosed a great multitude of fishes and their net brake
nd they beckoned unto their partners which were in the other ship that they should come and help them
nd they came and filled both the ships so that they began to sink
hen imon eter saw it he fell down at esus knees saying epart from me for  am a sinful man  ord
or he was astonished and all that were with him at the draught of the fishes which they had taken and so was also ames and ohn the sons of ebedee which were partners with imon
nd esus said unto imon ear not from henceforth thou shalt catch men
nd when they had brought their ships to land they forsook all and followed him
nd it came to pass when he was in a certain city behold a man full of leprosy who seeing esus fell on his face and besought him saying ord if thou wilt thou canst make me clean
nd he put forth his hand and touched him saying  will be thou clean
nd immediately the leprosy departed from him
nd he charged him to tell no man but go and shew thyself to the priest and offer for thy cleansing according as oses commanded for a testimony unto them
ut so much the more went there a fame abroad of him and great multitudes came together to hear and to be healed by him of their infirmities
nd he withdrew himself into the wilderness and prayed
nd it came to pass on a certain day as he was teaching that there were harisees and doctors of the law sitting by which were come out of every town of alilee and ud a and erusalem and the power of the ord was present to heal them
nd behold men brought in a bed a man which was taken with a palsy and they sought means to bring him in and to lay him before him
nd when they could not find by what way they might bring him in because of the multitude they went upon the housetop and let him down through the tiling with his couch into the midst before esus
nd when he saw their faith he said unto him an thy sins are forgiven thee
nd the scribes and the harisees began to reason saying ho is this which speaketh blasphemies
ho can forgive sins but od alone
ut when esus perceived their thoughts he answering said unto them hat reason ye in your hearts
hether is easier to say hy sins be forgiven thee or to say ise up and walk
ut that ye may know that the on of man hath power upon earth to forgive sins he said unto the sick of the palsy   say unto thee rise and take up thy couch and go into thine house
nd immediately he rose up before them and took up that whereon he lay and departed to his own house glorifying od
nd they were all amazed and they glorified od and were filled with fear saying e have seen strange things to day
nd after these things he went forth and saw a publican named evi sitting at the receipt of custom and he said unto him ollow me
nd he left all rose up and followed him
nd evi made him a great feast in his own house and there was a great company of publicans and of others that sat down with them
ut their scribes and harisees murmured against his disciples saying hy do ye eat and drink with publicans and sinners
nd esus answering said unto them hey that are whole need not a physician but they that are sick
 came not to call the righteous but sinners to repentance
nd they said unto him hy do the disciples of ohn fast often and make prayers and likewise the disciples of the harisees but thine eat and drink
nd he said unto them an ye make the children of the bridechamber fast while the bridegroom is with them
ut the days will come when the bridegroom shall be taken away from them and then shall they fast in those days
nd he spake also a parable unto them o man putteth a piece of a new garment upon an old if otherwise then both the new maketh a rent and the piece that was taken out of the new agreeth not with the old
nd no man putteth new wine into old bottles else the new wine will burst the bottles and be spilled and the bottles shall perish
ut new wine must be put into new bottles and both are preserved
o man also having drunk old wine straightway desireth new for he saith he old is better
nd it came to pass on the second sabbath after the first that he went through the corn fields and his disciples plucked the ears of corn and did eat rubbing them in their hands
nd certain of the harisees said unto them hy do ye that which is not lawful to do on the sabbath days
nd esus answering them said ave ye not read so much as this what avid did when himself was an hungred and they which were with him how he went into the house of od and did take and eat the shewbread and gave also to them that were with him which it is not lawful to eat but for the priests alone
nd he said unto them hat the on of man is ord also of the sabbath
nd it came to pass also on another sabbath that he entered into the synagogue and taught and there was a man whose right hand was withered
nd the scribes and harisees watched him whether he would heal on the sabbath day that they might find an accusation against him
ut he knew their thoughts and said to the man which had the withered hand ise up and stand forth in the midst
nd he arose and stood forth
hen said esus unto them  will ask you one thing s it lawful on the sabbath days to do good or to do evil to save life or to destroy it
nd looking round about upon them all he said unto the man tretch forth thy hand
nd he did so and his hand was restored whole as the other
nd they were filled with madness and communed one with another what they might do to esus
nd it came to pass in those days that he went out into a mountain to pray and continued all night in prayer to od
nd when it was day he called unto him his disciples and of them he chose twelve whom also he named apostles imon whom he also named eter  and ndrew his brother ames and ohn hilip and artholomew atthew and homas ames the son of lph us and imon called elotes and udas the brother of ames and udas scariot which also was the traitor
nd he came down with them and stood in the plain and the company of his disciples and a great multitude of people out of all ud a and erusalem and from the sea coast of yre and idon which came to hear him and to be healed of their diseases and they that were vexed with unclean spirits and they were healed
nd the whole multitude sought to touch him for there went virtue out of him and healed them all
nd he lifted up his eyes on his disciples and said lessed be ye poor for yours is the kingdom of od
lessed are ye that hunger now for ye shall be filled
lessed are ye that weep now for ye shall laugh
lessed are ye when men shall hate you and when they shall separate you from their company and shall reproach you and cast out your name as evil for the on of mans sake
ejoice ye in that day and leap for joy for behold your reward is great in heaven for in the like manner did their fathers unto the prophets
ut woe unto you that are rich for ye have received your consolation
oe unto you that are full for ye shall hunger
oe unto you that laugh now for ye shall mourn and weep
oe unto you when all men shall speak well of you for so did their fathers to the false prophets
ut  say unto you which hear ove your enemies do good to them which hate you bless them that curse you and pray for them which despitefully use you
nd unto him that smiteth thee on the one cheek offer also the other and him that taketh away thy cloke forbid not to take thy coat also
ive to every man that asketh of thee and of him that taketh away thy goods ask them not again
nd as ye would that men should do to you do ye also to them likewise
or if ye love them which love you what thank have ye for sinners also love those that love them
nd if ye do good to them which do good to you what thank have ye for sinners also do even the same
nd if ye lend to them of whom ye hope to receive what thank have ye for sinners also lend to sinners to receive as much again
ut love ye your enemies and do good and lend hoping for nothing again and your reward shall be great and ye shall be the children of the ighest for he is kind unto the unthankful and to the evil
e ye therefore merciful as your ather also is merciful
udge not and ye shall not be judged condemn not and ye shall not be condemned forgive and ye shall be forgiven give and it shall be given unto you good measure pressed down and shaken together and running over shall men give into your bosom
or with the same measure that ye mete withal it shall be measured to you again
nd he spake a parable unto them an the blind lead the blind shall they not both fall into the ditch
he disciple is not above his master but every one that is perfect shall be as his master
nd why beholdest thou the mote that is in thy brothers eye but perceivest not the beam that is in thine own eye
ither how canst thou say to thy brother rother let me pull out the mote that is in thine eye when thou thyself beholdest not the beam that is in thine own eye
hou hypocrite cast out first the beam out of thine own eye and then shalt thou see clearly to pull out the mote that is in thy brothers eye
or a good tree bringeth not forth corrupt fruit neither doth a corrupt tree bring forth good fruit
or every tree is known by his own fruit
or of thorns men do not gather figs nor of a bramble bush gather they grapes
 good man out of the good treasure of his heart bringeth forth that which is good and an evil man out of the evil treasure of his heart bringeth forth that which is evil for of the abundance of the heart his mouth speaketh
nd why call ye me ord ord and do not the things which  say
hosoever cometh to me and heareth my sayings and doeth them  will shew you to whom he is like he is like a man which built an house and digged deep and laid the foundation on a rock and when the flood arose the stream beat vehemently upon that house and could not shake it for it was founded upon a rock
ut he that heareth and doeth not is like a man that without a foundation built an house upon the earth against which the stream did beat vehemently and immediately it fell and the ruin of that house was great
ow when he had ended all his sayings in the audience of the people he entered into apernaum
nd a certain centurions servant who was dear unto him was sick and ready to die
nd when he heard of esus he sent unto him the elders of the ews beseeching him that he would come and heal his servant
nd when they came to esus they besought him instantly saying hat he was worthy for whom he should do this for he loveth our nation and he hath built us a synagogue
hen esus went with them
nd when he was now not far from the house the centurion sent friends to him saying unto him ord trouble not thyself for  am not worthy that thou shouldest enter under my roof wherefore neither thought  myself worthy to come unto thee but say in a word and my servant shall be healed
or  also am a man set under authority having under me soldiers and  say unto one o and he goeth and to another ome and he cometh and to my servant o this and he doeth it
hen esus heard these things he marvelled at him and turned him about and said unto the people that followed him  say unto you  have not found so great faith no not in srael
nd they that were sent returning to the house found the servant whole that had been sick
nd it came to pass the day after that he went into a city called ain and many of his disciples went with him and much people
ow when he came nigh to the gate of the city behold there was a dead man carried out the only son of his mother and she was a widow and much people of the city was with her
nd when the ord saw her he had compassion on her and said unto her eep not
nd he came and touched the bier and they that bare him stood still
nd he said oung man  say unto thee rise
nd he that was dead sat up and began to speak
nd he delivered him to his mother
nd there came a fear on all and they glorified od saying hat a great prophet is risen up among us and hat od hath visited his people
nd this rumour of him went forth throughout all ud a and throughout all the region round about
nd the disciples of ohn shewed him of all these things
nd ohn calling unto him two of his disciples sent them to esus saying rt thou he that should come or look we for another
hen the men were come unto him they said ohn aptist hath sent us unto thee saying rt thou he that should come or look we for another
nd in that same hour he cured many of their infirmities and plagues and of evil spirits and unto many that were blind he gave sight
hen esus answering said unto them o your way and tell ohn what things ye have seen and heard how that the blind see the lame walk the lepers are cleansed the deaf hear the dead are raised to the poor the gospel is preached
nd blessed is he whosoever shall not be offended in me
nd when the messengers of ohn were departed he began to speak unto the people concerning ohn hat went ye out into the wilderness for to see
 reed shaken with the wind
ut what went ye out for to see
 man clothed in soft raiment
ehold they which are gorgeously apparelled and live delicately are in kings courts
ut what went ye out for to see
 prophet
ea  say unto you and much more than a prophet
his is he of whom it is written ehold  send my messenger before thy face which shall prepare thy way before thee
or  say unto you mong those that are born of women there is not a greater prophet than ohn the aptist but he that is least in the kingdom of od is greater than he
nd all the people that heard him and the publicans justified od being baptized with the baptism of ohn
ut the harisees and lawyers rejected the counsel of od against themselves being not baptized of him
nd the ord said hereunto then shall  liken the men of this generation and to what are they like
hey are like unto children sitting in the marketplace and calling one to another and saying e have piped unto you and ye have not danced we have mourned to you and ye have not wept
or ohn the aptist came neither eating bread nor drinking wine and ye say e hath a devil
he on of man is come eating and drinking and ye say ehold a gluttonous man and a winebibber a friend of publicans and sinners
ut wisdom is justified of all her children
nd one of the harisees desired him that he would eat with him
nd he went into the harisees house and sat down to meat
nd behold a woman in the city which was a sinner when she knew that esus sat at meat in the harisees house brought an alabaster box of ointment and stood at his feet behind him weeping and began to wash his feet with tears and did wipe them with the hairs of her head and kissed his feet and anointed them with the ointment
ow when the harisee which had bidden him saw it he spake within himself saying his man if he were a prophet would have known who and what manner of woman this is that toucheth him for she is a sinner
nd esus answering said unto him imon  have somewhat to say unto thee
nd he saith aster say on
here was a certain creditor which had two debtors the one owed five hundred pence and the other fifty
nd when they had nothing to pay he frankly forgave them both
ell me therefore which of them will love him most
imon answered and said  suppose that he to whom he forgave most
nd he said unto him hou hast rightly judged
nd he turned to the woman and said unto imon eest thou this woman
 entered into thine house thou gavest me no water for my feet but she hath washed my feet with tears and wiped them with the hairs of her head
hou gavest me no kiss but this woman since the time  came in hath not ceased to kiss my feet
y head with oil thou didst not anoint but this woman hath anointed my feet with ointment
herefore  say unto thee er sins which are many are forgiven for she loved much but to whom little is forgiven the same loveth little
nd he said unto her hy sins are forgiven
nd they that sat at meat with him began to say within themselves ho is this that forgiveth sins also
nd he said to the woman hy faith hath saved thee go in peace
nd it came to pass afterward that he went throughout every city and village preaching and shewing the glad tidings of the kingdom of od and the twelve were with him and certain women which had been healed of evil spirits and infirmities ary called agdalene out of whom went seven devils and oanna the wife of huza erods steward and usanna and many others which ministered unto him of their substance
nd when much people were gathered together and were come to him out of every city he spake by a parable  sower went out to sow his seed and as he sowed some fell by the way side and it was trodden down and the fowls of the air devoured it
nd some fell upon a rock and as soon as it was sprung up it withered away because it lacked moisture
nd some fell among thorns and the thorns sprang up with it and choked it
nd other fell on good ground and sprang up and bare fruit an hundredfold
nd when he had said these things he cried e that hath ears to hear let him hear
nd his disciples asked him saying hat might this parable be
nd he said nto you it is given to know the mysteries of the kingdom of od but to others in parables that seeing they might not see and hearing they might not understand
ow the parable is this he seed is the word of od
hose by the way side are they that hear then cometh the devil and taketh away the word out of their hearts lest they should believe and be saved
hey on the rock are they which when they hear receive the word with joy and these have no root which for a while believe and in time of temptation fall away
nd that which fell among thorns are they which when they have heard go forth and are choked with cares and riches and pleasures of this life and bring no fruit to perfection
ut that on the good ground are they which in an honest and good heart having heard the word keep it and bring forth fruit with patience
o man when he hath lighted a candle covereth it with a vessel or putteth it under a bed but setteth it on a candlestick that they which enter in may see the light
or nothing is secret that shall not be made manifest neither any thing hid that shall not be known and come abroad
ake heed therefore how ye hear for whosoever hath to him shall be given and whosoever hath not from him shall be taken even that which he seemeth to have
hen came to him his mother and his brethren and could not come at him for the press
nd it was told him by certain which said hy mother and thy brethren stand without desiring to see thee
nd he answered and said unto them y mother and my brethren are these which hear the word of od and do it
ow it came to pass on a certain day that he went into a ship with his disciples and he said unto them et us go over unto the other side of the lake
nd they launched forth
ut as they sailed he fell asleep and there came down a storm of wind on the lake and they were filled with water and were in jeopardy
nd they came to him and awoke him saying aster master we perish
hen he arose and rebuked the wind and the raging of the water and they ceased and there was a calm
nd he said unto them here is your faith
nd they being afraid wondered saying one to another hat manner of man is this for he commandeth even the winds and water and they obey him
nd they arrived at the country of the adarenes which is over against alilee
nd when he went forth to land there met him out of the city a certain man which had devils long time and ware no clothes neither abode in any house but in the tombs
hen he saw esus he cried out and fell down before him and with a loud voice said hat have  to do with thee esus thou on of od most high
 beseech thee torment me not
or he had commanded the unclean spirit to come out of the man
or oftentimes it had caught him and he was kept bound with chains and in fetters and he brake the bands and was driven of the devil into the wilderness nd esus asked him saying hat is thy name
nd he said egion because many devils were entered into him
nd they besought him that he would not command them to go out into the deep
nd there was there an herd of many swine feeding on the mountain and they besought him that he would suffer them to enter into them
nd he suffered them
hen went the devils out of the man and entered into the swine and the herd ran violently down a steep place into the lake and were choked
hen they that fed them saw what was done they fled and went and told it in the city and in the country
hen they went out to see what was done and came to esus and found the man out of whom the devils were departed sitting at the feet of esus clothed and in his right mind and they were afraid
hey also which saw it told them by what means he that was possessed of the devils was healed
hen the whole multitude of the country of the adarenes round about besought him to depart from them for they were taken with great fear and he went up into the ship and returned back again
ow the man out of whom the devils were departed besought him that he might be with him but esus sent him away saying eturn to thine own house and shew how great things od hath done unto thee
nd he went his way and published throughout the whole city how great things esus had done unto him
nd it came to pass that when esus was returned the people gladly received him for they were all waiting for him
nd behold there came a man named airus and he was a ruler of the synagogue and he fell down at esus feet and besought him that he would come into his house for he had one only daughter about twelve years of age and she lay a dying
ut as he went the people thronged him
nd a woman having an issue of blood twelve years which had spent all her living upon physicians neither could be healed of any came behind him and touched the border of his garment and immediately her issue of blood stanched
nd esus said ho touched me
hen all denied eter and they that were with him said aster the multitude throng thee and press thee and sayest thou ho touched me
nd esus said omebody hath touched me for  perceive that virtue is gone out of me
nd when the woman saw that she was not hid she came trembling and falling down before him she declared unto him before all the people for what cause she had touched him and how she was healed immediately
nd he said unto her aughter be of good comfort thy faith hath made thee whole go in peace
hile he yet spake there cometh one from the ruler of the synagogues house saying to him hy daughter is dead trouble not the aster
ut when esus heard it he answered him saying ear not believe only and she shall be made whole
nd when he came into the house he suffered no man to go in save eter and ames and ohn and the father and the mother of the maiden
nd all wept and bewailed her but he said eep not she is not dead but sleepeth
nd they laughed him to scorn knowing that she was dead
nd he put them all out and took her by the hand and called saying aid arise
nd her spirit came again and she arose straightway and he commanded to give her meat
nd her parents were astonished but he charged them that they should tell no man what was done
hen he called his twelve disciples together and gave them power and authority over all devils and to cure diseases
nd he sent them to preach the kingdom of od and to heal the sick
nd he said unto them ake nothing for your journey neither staves nor scrip neither bread neither money neither have two coats apiece
nd whatsoever house ye enter into there abide and thence depart
nd whosoever will not receive you when ye go out of that city shake off the very dust from your feet for a testimony against them
nd they departed and went through the towns preaching the gospel and healing every where
ow erod the tetrarch heard of all that was done by him and he was perplexed because that it was said of some that ohn was risen from the dead and of some that lias had appeared and of others that one of the old prophets was risen again
nd erod said ohn have  beheaded but who is this of whom  hear such things
nd he desired to see him
nd the apostles when they were returned told him all that they had done
nd he took them and went aside privately into a desert place belonging to the city called ethsaida
nd the people when they knew it followed him and he received them and spake unto them of the kingdom of od and healed them that had need of healing
nd when the day began to wear away then came the twelve and said unto him end the multitude away that they may go into the towns and country round about and lodge and get victuals for we are here in a desert place
ut he said unto them ive ye them to eat
nd they said e have no more but five loaves and two fishes except we should go and buy meat for all this people
or they were about five thousand men
nd he said to his disciples ake them sit down by fifties in a company
nd they did so and made them all sit down
hen he took the five loaves and the two fishes and looking up to heaven he blessed them and brake and gave to the disciples to set before the multitude
nd they did eat and were all filled and there was taken up of fragments that remained to them twelve baskets
nd it came to pass as he was alone praying his disciples were with him and he asked them saying hom say the people that  am
hey answering said ohn the aptist but some say lias and others say that one of the old prophets is risen again
e said unto them ut whom say ye that  am
eter answering said he hrist of od
nd he straitly charged them and commanded them to tell no man that thing saying he on of man must suffer many things and be rejected of the elders and chief priests and scribes and be slain and be raised the third day
nd he said to them all f any man will come after me let him deny himself and take up his cross daily and follow me
or whosoever will save his life shall lose it but whosoever will lose his life for my sake the same shall save it
or what is a man advantaged if he gain the whole world and lose himself or be cast away
or whosoever shall be ashamed of me and of my words of him shall the on of man be ashamed when he shall come in his own glory and in his athers and of the holy angels
ut  tell you of a truth there be some standing here which shall not taste of death till they see the kingdom of od
nd it came to pass about an eight days after these sayings he took eter and ohn and ames and went up into a mountain to pray
nd as he prayed the fashion of his countenance was altered and his raiment was white and glistering
nd behold there talked with him two men which were oses and lias who appeared in glory and spake of his decease which he should accomplish at erusalem
ut eter and they that were with him were heavy with sleep and when they were awake they saw his glory and the two men that stood with him
nd it came to pass as they departed from him eter said unto esus aster it is good for us to be here and let us make three tabernacles one for thee and one for oses and one for lias not knowing what he said
hile he thus spake there came a cloud and overshadowed them and they feared as they entered into the cloud
nd there came a voice out of the cloud saying his is my beloved on hear him
nd when the voice was past esus was found alone
nd they kept it close and told no man in those days any of those things which they had seen
nd it came to pass that on the next day when they were come down from the hill much people met him
nd behold a man of the company cried out saying aster  beseech thee look upon my son for he is mine only child
nd lo a spirit taketh him and he suddenly crieth out and it teareth him that he foameth again and bruising him hardly departeth from him
nd  besought thy disciples to cast him out and they could not
nd esus answering said  faithless and perverse generation how long shall  be with you and suffer you
ring thy son hither
nd as he was yet a coming the devil threw him down and tare him
nd esus rebuked the unclean spirit and healed the child and delivered him again to his father
nd they were all amazed at the mighty power of od
ut while they wondered every one at all things which esus did he said unto his disciples et these sayings sink down into your ears for the on of man shall be delivered into the hands of men
ut they understood not this saying and it was hid from them that they perceived it not and they feared to ask him of that saying
hen there arose a reasoning among them which of them should be greatest
nd esus perceiving the thought of their heart took a child and set him by him and said unto them hosoever shall receive this child in my name receiveth me and whosoever shall receive me receiveth him that sent me for he that is least among you all the same shall be great
nd ohn answered and said aster we saw one casting out devils in thy name and we forbad him because he followeth not with us
nd esus said unto him orbid him not for he that is not against us is for us
nd it came to pass when the time was come that he should be received up he stedfastly set his face to go to erusalem and sent messengers before his face and they went and entered into a village of the amaritans to make ready for him
nd they did not receive him because his face was as though he would go to erusalem
nd when his disciples ames and ohn saw this they said ord wilt thou that we command fire to come down from heaven and consume them even as lias did
ut he turned and rebuked them and said e know not what manner of spirit ye are of
or the on of man is not come to destroy mens lives but to save them
nd they went to another village
nd it came to pass that as they went in the way a certain man said unto him ord  will follow thee whithersoever thou goest
nd esus said unto him oxes have holes and birds of the air have nests but the on of man hath not where to lay his head
nd he said unto another ollow me
ut he said ord suffer me first to go and bury my father
esus said unto him et the dead bury their dead but go thou and preach the kingdom of od
nd another also said ord  will follow thee but let me first go bid them farewell which are at home at my house
nd esus said unto him o man having put his hand to the plough and looking back is fit for the kingdom of od
fter these things the ord appointed other seventy also and sent them two and two before his face into every city and place whither he himself would come
herefore said he unto them he harvest truly is great but the labourers are few pray ye therefore the ord of the harvest that he would send forth labourers into his harvest
o your ways behold  send you forth as lambs among wolves
arry neither purse nor scrip nor shoes and salute no man by the way
nd into whatsoever house ye enter first say eace be to this house
nd if the son of peace be there your peace shall rest upon it if not it shall turn to you again
nd in the same house remain eating and drinking such things as they give for the labourer is worthy of his hire
o not from house to house
nd into whatsoever city ye enter and they receive you eat such things as are set before you and heal the sick that are therein and say unto them he kingdom of od is come nigh unto you
ut into whatsoever city ye enter and they receive you not go your ways out into the streets of the same and say ven the very dust of your city which cleaveth on us we do wipe off against you notwithstanding be ye sure of this that the kingdom of od is come nigh unto you
ut  say unto you that it shall be more tolerable in that day for odom than for that city
oe unto thee horazin woe unto thee ethsaida for if the mighty works had been done in yre and idon which have been done in you they had a great while ago repented sitting in sackcloth and ashes
ut it shall be more tolerable for yre and idon at the judgment than for you
nd thou apernaum which art exalted to heaven shalt be thrust down to hell
e that heareth you heareth me and he that despiseth you despiseth me and he that despiseth me despiseth him that sent me
nd the seventy returned again with joy saying ord even the devils are subject unto us through thy name
nd he said unto them  beheld atan as lightning fall from heaven
ehold  give unto you power to tread on serpents and scorpions and over all the power of the enemy and nothing shall by any means hurt you
otwithstanding in this rejoice not that the spirits are subject unto you but rather rejoice because your names are written in heaven
n that hour esus rejoiced in spirit and said  thank thee  ather ord of heaven and earth that thou hast hid these things from the wise and prudent and hast revealed them unto babes even so ather for so it seemed good in thy sight
ll things are delivered to me of my ather and no man knoweth who the on is but the ather and who the ather is but the on and he to whom the on will reveal him
