nd he turned him unto his disciples and said privately lessed are the eyes which see the things that ye see for  tell you that many prophets and kings have desired to see those things which ye see and have not seen them and to hear those things which ye hear and have not heard them
nd behold a certain lawyer stood up and tempted him saying aster what shall  do to inherit eternal life
e said unto him hat is written in the law how readest thou
nd he answering said hou shalt love the ord thy od with all thy heart and with all thy soul and with all thy strength and with all thy mind and thy neighbour as thyself
nd he said unto him hou hast answered right this do and thou shalt live
ut he willing to justify himself said unto esus nd who is my neighbour
nd esus answering said  certain man went down from erusalem to ericho and fell among thieves which stripped him of his raiment and wounded him and departed leaving him half dead
nd by chance there came down a certain priest that way and when he saw him he passed by on the other side
nd likewise a evite when he was at the place came and looked on him and passed by on the other side
ut a certain amaritan as he journeyed came where he was and when he saw him he had compassion on him and went to him and bound up his wounds pouring in oil and wine and set him on his own beast and brought him to an inn and took care of him
nd on the morrow when he departed he took out two pence and gave them to the host and said unto him ake care of him and whatsoever thou spendest more when  come again  will repay thee
hich now of these three thinkest thou was neighbour unto him that fell among the thieves
nd he said e that shewed mercy on him
hen said esus unto him o and do thou likewise
ow it came to pass as they went that he entered into a certain village and a certain woman named artha received him into her house
nd she had a sister called ary which also sat at esus feet and heard his word
ut artha was cumbered about much serving and came to him and said ord dost thou not care that my sister hath left me to serve alone bid her therefore that she help me
nd esus answered and said unto her artha artha thou art careful and troubled about many things but one thing is needful and ary hath chosen that good part which shall not be taken away from her
nd it came to pass that as he was praying in a certain place when he ceased one of his disciples said unto him ord teach us to pray as ohn also taught his disciples
nd he said unto them hen ye pray say ur ather which art in heaven allowed be thy name
hy kingdom come
hy will be done as in heaven so in earth
ive us day by day our daily bread
nd forgive us our sins for we also forgive every one that is indebted to us
nd lead us not into temptation but deliver us from evil
nd he said unto them hich of you shall have a friend and shall go unto him at midnight and say unto him riend lend me three loaves for a friend of mine in his journey is come to me and  have nothing to set before him
nd he from within shall answer and say rouble me not the door is now shut and my children are with me in bed  cannot rise and give thee
 say unto you hough he will not rise and give him because he is his friend yet because of his importunity he will rise and give him as many as he needeth
nd  say unto you sk and it shall be given you seek and ye shall find knock and it shall be opened unto you
or every one that asketh receiveth and he that seeketh findeth and to him that knocketh it shall be opened
f a son shall ask bread of any of you that is a father will he give him a stone or if he ask a fish will he for a fish give him a serpent
r if he shall ask an egg will he offer him a scorpion
f ye then being evil know how to give good gifts unto your children how much more shall your heavenly ather give the oly pirit to them that ask him
nd he was casting out a devil and it was dumb
nd it came to pass when the devil was gone out the dumb spake and the people wondered
ut some of them said e casteth out devils through eelzebub the chief of the devils
nd others tempting him sought of him a sign from heaven
ut he knowing their thoughts said unto them very kingdom divided against itself is brought to desolation and a house divided against an house falleth
f atan also be divided against himself how shall his kingdom stand because ye say that  cast out devils through eelzebub
nd if  by eelzebub cast out devils by whom do your sons cast them out therefore shall they be your judges
ut if  with the finger of od cast out devils no doubt the kingdom of od is come upon you
hen a strong man armed keepeth his palace his goods are in peace but when a stronger than he shall come upon him and overcome him he taketh from him all his armour wherein he trusted and divideth his spoils
e that is not with me is against me and he that gathereth not with me scattereth
hen the unclean spirit is gone out of a man he walketh through dry places seeking rest and finding none he saith  will return unto my house whence  came out
nd when he cometh he findeth it swept and garnished
hen goeth he and taketh to him seven other spirits more wicked than himself and they enter in and dwell there and the last state of that man is worse than the first
nd it came to pass as he spake these things a certain woman of the company lifted up her voice and said unto him lessed is the womb that bare thee and the paps which thou hast sucked
ut he said ea rather blessed are they that hear the word of od and keep it
nd when the people were gathered thick together he began to say his is an evil generation they seek a sign and there shall no sign be given it but the sign of onas the prophet
or as onas was a sign unto the inevites so shall also the on of man be to this generation
he queen of the south shall rise up in the judgment with the men of this generation and condemn them for she came from the utmost parts of the earth to hear the wisdom of olomon and behold a greater than olomon is here
he men of ineve shall rise up in the judgment with this generation and shall condemn it for they repented at the preaching of onas and behold a greater than onas is here
o man when he hath lighted a candle putteth it in a secret place neither under a bushel but on a candlestick that they which come in may see the light
he light of the body is the eye therefore when thine eye is single thy whole body also is full of light but when thine eye is evil thy body also is full of darkness
ake heed therefore that the light which is in thee be not darkness
f thy whole body therefore be full of light having no part dark the whole shall be full of light as when the bright shining of a candle doth give thee light
nd as he spake a certain harisee besought him to dine with him and he went in and sat down to meat
nd when the harisee saw it he marvelled that he had not first washed before dinner
nd the ord said unto him ow do ye harisees make clean the outside of the cup and the platter but your inward part is full of ravening and wickedness
e fools did not he that made that which is without make that which is within also
ut rather give alms of such things as ye have and behold all things are clean unto you
ut woe unto you harisees for ye tithe mint and rue and all manner of herbs and pass over judgment and the love of od these ought ye to have done and not to leave the other undone
oe unto you harisees for ye love the uppermost seats in the synagogues and greetings in the markets
oe unto you scribes and harisees hypocrites for ye are as graves which appear not and the men that walk over them are not aware of them
hen answered one of the lawyers and said unto him aster thus saying thou reproachest us also
nd he said oe unto you also ye lawyers for ye lade men with burdens grievous to be borne and ye yourselves touch not the burdens with one of your fingers
oe unto you for ye build the sepulchres of the prophets and your fathers killed them
ruly ye bear witness that ye allow the deeds of your fathers for they indeed killed them and ye build their sepulchres
herefore also said the wisdom of od  will send them prophets and apostles and some of them they shall slay and persecute that the blood of all the prophets which was shed from the foundation of the world may be required of this generation from the blood of bel unto the blood of acharias which perished between the altar and the temple verily  say unto you t shall be required of this generation
oe unto you lawyers for ye have taken away the key of knowledge ye entered not in yourselves and them that were entering in ye hindered
nd as he said these things unto them the scribes and the harisees began to urge him vehemently and to provoke him to speak of many things laying wait for him and seeking to catch something out of his mouth that they might accuse him
n the mean time when there were gathered together an innumerable multitude of people insomuch that they trode one upon another he began to say unto his disciples first of all eware ye of the leaven of the harisees which is hypocrisy
or there is nothing covered that shall not be revealed neither hid that shall not be known
herefore whatsoever ye have spoken in darkness shall be heard in the light and that which ye have spoken in the ear in closets shall be proclaimed upon the housetops
nd  say unto you my friends e not afraid of them that kill the body and after that have no more that they can do
ut  will forewarn you whom ye shall fear ear him which after he hath killed hath power to cast into hell yea  say unto you ear him
re not five sparrows sold for two farthings and not one of them is forgotten before od
ut even the very hairs of your head are all numbered
ear not therefore ye are of more value than many sparrows
lso  say unto you hosoever shall confess me before men him shall the on of man also confess before the angels of od but he that denieth me before men shall be denied before the angels of od
nd whosoever shall speak a word against the on of man it shall be forgiven him but unto him that blasphemeth against the oly host it shall not be forgiven
nd when they bring you unto the synagogues and unto magistrates and powers take ye no thought how or what thing ye shall answer or what ye shall say for the oly host shall teach you in the same hour what ye ought to say
nd one of the company said unto him aster speak to my brother that he divide the inheritance with me
nd he said unto him an who made me a judge or a divider over you
nd he said unto them ake heed and beware of covetousness for a mans life consisteth not in the abundance of the things which he possesseth
nd he spake a parable unto them saying he ground of a certain rich man brought forth plentifully and he thought within himself saying hat shall  do because  have no room where to bestow my fruits
nd he said his will  do  will pull down my barns and build greater and there will  bestow all my fruits and my goods
nd  will say to my soul oul thou hast much goods laid up for many years take thine ease eat drink and be merry
ut od said unto him hou fool this night thy soul shall be required of thee then whose shall those things be which thou hast provided
o is he that layeth up treasure for himself and is not rich toward od
nd he said unto his disciples herefore  say unto you ake no thought for your life what ye shall eat neither for the body what ye shall put on
he life is more than meat and the body is more than raiment
onsider the ravens for they neither sow nor reap which neither have storehouse nor barn and od feedeth them how much more are ye better than the fowls
nd which of you with taking thought can add to his stature one cubit
f ye then be not able to do that thing which is least why take ye thought for the rest
onsider the lilies how they grow they toil not they spin not and yet  say unto you that olomon in all his glory was not arrayed like one of these
f then od so clothe the grass which is to day in the field and to morrow is cast into the oven how much more will he clothe you  ye of little faith
nd seek not ye what ye shall eat or what ye shall drink neither be ye of doubtful mind
or all these things do the nations of the world seek after and your ather knoweth that ye have need of these things
ut rather seek ye the kingdom of od and all these things shall be added unto you
ear not little flock for it is your athers good pleasure to give you the kingdom
ell that ye have and give alms provide yourselves bags which wax not old a treasure in the heavens that faileth not where no thief approacheth neither moth corrupteth
or where your treasure is there will your heart be also
et your loins be girded about and your lights burning and ye yourselves like unto men that wait for their lord when he will return from the wedding that when he cometh and knocketh they may open unto him immediately
lessed are those servants whom the lord when he cometh shall find watching verily  say unto you that he shall gird himself and make them to sit down to meat and will come forth and serve them
nd if he shall come in the second watch or come in the third watch and find them so blessed are those servants
nd this know that if the goodman of the house had known what hour the thief would come he would have watched and not have suffered his house to be broken through
e ye therefore ready also for the on of man cometh at an hour when ye think not
hen eter said unto him ord speakest thou this parable unto us or even to all
nd the ord said ho then is that faithful and wise steward whom his lord shall make ruler over his household to give them their portion of meat in due season
lessed is that servant whom his lord when he cometh shall find so doing
f a truth  say unto you that he will make him ruler over all that he hath
ut and if that servant say in his heart y lord delayeth his coming and shall begin to beat the menservants and maidens and to eat and drink and to be drunken the lord of that servant will come in a day when he looketh not for him and at an hour when he is not aware and will cut him in sunder and will appoint him his portion with the unbelievers
nd that servant which knew his lords will and prepared not himself neither did according to his will shall be beaten with many stripes
ut he that knew not and did commit things worthy of stripes shall be beaten with few stripes
or unto whomsoever much is given of him shall be much required and to whom men have committed much of him they will ask the more
 am come to send fire on the earth and what will  if it be already kindled
ut  have a baptism to be baptized with and how am  straitened till it be accomplished
uppose ye that  am come to give peace on earth
 tell you ay but rather division for from henceforth there shall be five in one house divided three against two and two against three
he father shall be divided against the son and the son against the father the mother against the daughter and the daughter against the mother the mother in law against her daughter in law and the daughter in law against her mother in law
nd he said also to the people hen ye see a cloud rise out of the west straightway ye say here cometh a shower and so it is
nd when ye see the south wind blow ye say here will be heat and it cometh to pass
e hypocrites ye can discern the face of the sky and of the earth but how is it that ye do not discern this time
ea and why even of yourselves judge ye not what is right
hen thou goest with thine adversary to the magistrate as thou art in the way give diligence that thou mayest be delivered from him lest he hale thee to the judge and the judge deliver thee to the officer and the officer cast thee into prison
 tell thee thou shalt not depart thence till thou hast paid the very last mite
here were present at that season some that told him of the alil ans whose blood ilate had mingled with their sacrifices
nd esus answering said unto them uppose ye that these alil ans were sinners above all the alil ans because they suffered such things
 tell you ay but except ye repent ye shall all likewise perish
r those eighteen upon whom the tower in iloam fell and slew them think ye that they were sinners above all men that dwelt in erusalem
 tell you ay but except ye repent ye shall all likewise perish
e spake also this parable  certain man had a fig tree planted in his vineyard and he came and sought fruit thereon and found none
hen said he unto the dresser of his vineyard ehold these three years  come seeking fruit on this fig tree and find none cut it down why cumbereth it the ground
nd he answering said unto him ord let it alone this year also till  shall dig about it and dung it and if it bear fruit well and if not then after that thou shalt cut it down
nd he was teaching in one of the synagogues on the sabbath
nd behold there was a woman which had a spirit of infirmity eighteen years and was bowed together and could in no wise lift up herself
nd when esus saw her he called her to him and said unto her oman thou art loosed from thine infirmity
nd he laid his hands on her and immediately she was made straight and glorified od
nd the ruler of the synagogue answered with indignation because that esus had healed on the sabbath day and said unto the people here are six days in which men ought to work in them therefore come and be healed and not on the sabbath day
he ord then answered him and said hou hypocrite doth not each one of you on the sabbath loose his ox or his ass from the stall and lead him away to watering
nd ought not this woman being a daughter of braham whom atan hath bound lo these eighteen years be loosed from this bond on the sabbath day
nd when he had said these things all his adversaries were ashamed and all the people rejoiced for all the glorious things that were done by him
hen said he nto what is the kingdom of od like and whereunto shall  resemble it
t is like a grain of mustard seed which a man took and cast into his garden and it grew and waxed a great tree and the fowls of the air lodged in the branches of it
nd again he said hereunto shall  liken the kingdom of od
t is like leaven which a woman took and hid in three measures of meal till the whole was leavened
nd he went through the cities and villages teaching and journeying toward erusalem
hen said one unto him ord are there few that be saved
nd he said unto them trive to enter in at the strait gate for many  say unto you will seek to enter in and shall not be able
hen once the master of the house is risen up and hath shut to the door and ye begin to stand without and to knock at the door saying ord ord open unto us and he shall answer and say unto you  know you not whence ye are then shall ye begin to say e have eaten and drunk in thy presence and thou hast taught in our streets
ut he shall say  tell you  know you not whence ye are depart from me all ye workers of iniquity
here shall be weeping and gnashing of teeth when ye shall see braham and saac and acob and all the prophets in the kingdom of od and you yourselves thrust out
nd they shall come from the east and from the west and from the north and from the south and shall sit down in the kingdom of od
nd behold there are last which shall be first and there are first which shall be last
he same day there came certain of the harisees saying unto him et thee out and depart hence for erod will kill thee
nd he said unto them o ye and tell that fox ehold  cast out devils and  do cures to day and to morrow and the third day  shall be perfected
evertheless  must walk to day and to morrow and the day following for it cannot be that a prophet perish out of erusalem
 erusalem erusalem which killest the prophets and stonest them that are sent unto thee how often would  have gathered thy children together as a hen doth gather her brood under her wings and ye would not
ehold your house is left unto you desolate and verily  say unto you e shall not see me until the time come when ye shall say lessed is he that cometh in the name of the ord
nd it came to pass as he went into the house of one of the chief harisees to eat bread on the sabbath day that they watched him
nd behold there was a certain man before him which had the dropsy
nd esus answering spake unto the lawyers and harisees saying s it lawful to heal on the sabbath day
nd they held their peace
nd he took him and healed him and let him go and answered them saying hich of you shall have an ass or an ox fallen into a pit and will not straightway pull him out on the sabbath day
nd they could not answer him again to these things
nd he put forth a parable to those which were bidden when he marked how they chose out the chief rooms saying unto them hen thou art bidden of any man to a wedding sit not down in the highest room lest a more honourable man than thou be bidden of him and he that bade thee and him come and say to thee ive this man place and thou begin with shame to take the lowest room
ut when thou art bidden go and sit down in the lowest room that when he that bade thee cometh he may say unto thee riend go up higher then shalt thou have worship in the presence of them that sit at meat with thee
or whosoever exalteth himself shall be abased and he that humbleth himself shall be exalted
hen said he also to him that bade him hen thou makest a dinner or a supper call not thy friends nor thy brethren neither thy kinsmen nor thy rich neighbours lest they also bid thee again and a recompence be made thee
ut when thou makest a feast call the poor the maimed the lame the blind and thou shalt be blessed for they cannot recompense thee for thou shalt be recompensed at the resurrection of the just
nd when one of them that sat at meat with him heard these things he said unto him lessed is he that shall eat bread in the kingdom of od
hen said he unto him  certain man made a great supper and bade many and sent his servant at supper time to say to them that were bidden ome for all things are now ready
nd they all with one consent began to make excuse
he first said unto him  have bought a piece of ground and  must needs go and see it  pray thee have me excused
nd another said  have bought five yoke of oxen and  go to prove them  pray thee have me excused
nd another said  have married a wife and therefore  cannot come
o that servant came and shewed his lord these things
hen the master of the house being angry said to his servant o out quickly into the streets and lanes of the city and bring in hither the poor and the maimed and the halt and the blind
nd the servant said ord it is done as thou hast commanded and yet there is room
nd the lord said unto the servant o out into the highways and hedges and compel them to come in that my house may be filled
or  say unto you hat none of those men which were bidden shall taste of my supper
nd there went great multitudes with him and he turned and said unto them f any man come to me and hate not his father and mother and wife and children and brethren and sisters yea and his own life also he cannot be my disciple
nd whosoever doth not bear his cross and come after me cannot be my disciple
or which of you intending to build a tower sitteth not down first and counteth the cost whether he have sufficient to finish it
est haply after he hath laid the foundation and is not able to finish it all that behold it begin to mock him saying his man began to build and was not able to finish
r what king going to make war against another king sitteth not down first and consulteth whether he be able with ten thousand to meet him that cometh against him with twenty thousand
r else while the other is yet a great way off he sendeth an ambassage and desireth conditions of peace
o likewise whosoever he be of you that forsaketh not all that he hath he cannot be my disciple
alt is good but if the salt have lost his savour wherewith shall it be seasoned
t is neither fit for the land nor yet for the dunghill but men cast it out
e that hath ears to hear let him hear
hen drew near unto him all the publicans and sinners for to hear him
nd the harisees and scribes murmured saying his man receiveth sinners and eateth with them
nd he spake this parable unto them saying hat man of you having an hundred sheep if he lose one of them doth not leave the ninety and nine in the wilderness and go after that which is lost until he find it
nd when he hath found it he layeth it on his shoulders rejoicing
nd when he cometh home he calleth together his friends and neighbours saying unto them ejoice with me for  have found my sheep which was lost
 say unto you that likewise joy shall be in heaven over one sinner that repenteth more than over ninety and nine just persons which need no repentance
ither what woman having ten pieces of silver if she lose one piece doth not light a candle and sweep the house and seek diligently till she find it
nd when she hath found it she calleth her friends and her neighbours together saying ejoice with me for  have found the piece which  had lost
ikewise  say unto you there is joy in the presence of the angels of od over one sinner that repenteth
nd he said  certain man had two sons and the younger of them said to his father ather give me the portion of goods that falleth to me
nd he divided unto them his living
nd not many days after the younger son gathered all together and took his journey into a far country and there wasted his substance with riotous living
nd when he had spent all there arose a mighty famine in that land and he began to be in want
nd he went and joined himself to a citizen of that country and he sent him into his fields to feed swine
nd he would fain have filled his belly with the husks that the swine did eat and no man gave unto him
nd when he came to himself he said ow many hired servants of my fathers have bread enough and to spare and  perish with hunger
 will arise and go to my father and will say unto him ather  have sinned against heaven and before thee and am no more worthy to be called thy son make me as one of thy hired servants
nd he arose and came to his father
ut when he was yet a great way off his father saw him and had compassion and ran and fell on his neck and kissed him
nd the son said unto him ather  have sinned against heaven and in thy sight and am no more worthy to be called thy son
ut the father said to his servants ring forth the best robe and put it on him and put a ring on his hand and shoes on his feet and bring hither the fatted calf and kill it and let us eat and be merry for this my son was dead and is alive again he was lost and is found
nd they began to be merry
ow his elder son was in the field and as he came and drew nigh to the house he heard musick and dancing
nd he called one of the servants and asked what these things meant
nd he said unto him hy brother is come and thy father hath killed the fatted calf because he hath received him safe and sound
nd he was angry and would not go in therefore came his father out and intreated him
nd he answering said to his father o these many years do  serve thee neither transgressed  at any time thy commandment and yet thou never gavest me a kid that  might make merry with my friends but as soon as this thy son was come which hath devoured thy living with harlots thou hast killed for him the fatted calf
nd he said unto him on thou art ever with me and all that  have is thine
t was meet that we should make merry and be glad for this thy brother was dead and is alive again and was lost and is found
nd he said also unto his disciples here was a certain rich man which had a steward and the same was accused unto him that he had wasted his goods
nd he called him and said unto him ow is it that  hear this of thee give an account of thy stewardship for thou mayest be no longer steward
hen the steward said within himself hat shall  do for my lord taketh away from me the stewardship  cannot dig to beg  am ashamed
 am resolved what to do that when  am put out of the stewardship they may receive me into their houses
o he called every one of his lords debtors unto him and said unto the first ow much owest thou unto my lord
nd he said n hundred measures of oil
nd he said unto him ake thy bill and sit down quickly and write fifty
hen said he to another nd how much owest thou
nd he said n hundred measures of wheat
nd he said unto him ake thy bill and write fourscore
nd the lord commended the unjust steward because he had done wisely for the children of this world are in their generation wiser than the children of light
nd  say unto you ake to yourselves friends of the mammon of unrighteousness that when ye fail they may receive you into everlasting habitations
e that is faithful in that which is least is faithful also in much and he that is unjust in the least is unjust also in much
f therefore ye have not been faithful in the unrighteous mammon who will commit to your trust the true riches
nd if ye have not been faithful in that which is another mans who shall give you that which is your own
o servant can serve two masters for either he will hate the one and love the other or else he will hold to the one and despise the other
e cannot serve od and mammon
nd the harisees also who were covetous heard all these things and they derided him
nd he said unto them e are they which justify yourselves before men but od knoweth your hearts for that which is highly esteemed among men is abomination in the sight of od
he law and the prophets were until ohn since that time the kingdom of od is preached and every man presseth into it
nd it is easier for heaven and earth to pass than one tittle of the law to fail
hosoever putteth away his wife and marrieth another committeth adultery and whosoever marrieth her that is put away from her husband committeth adultery
here was a certain rich man which was clothed in purple and fine linen and fared sumptuously every day and there was a certain beggar named azarus which was laid at his gate full of sores and desiring to be fed with the crumbs which fell from the rich mans table moreover the dogs came and licked his sores
nd it came to pass that the beggar died and was carried by the angels into brahams bosom the rich man also died and was buried and in hell he lift up his eyes being in torments and seeth braham afar off and azarus in his bosom
nd he cried and said ather braham have mercy on me and send azarus that he may dip the tip of his finger in water and cool my tongue for  am tormented in this flame
ut braham said on remember that thou in thy lifetime receivedst thy good things and likewise azarus evil things but now he is comforted and thou art tormented
nd beside all this between us and you there is a great gulf fixed so that they which would pass from hence to you cannot neither can they pass to us that would come from thence
hen he said  pray thee therefore father that thou wouldest send him to my fathers house for  have five brethren that he may testify unto them lest they also come into this place of torment
braham saith unto him hey have oses and the prophets let them hear them
nd he said ay father braham but if one went unto them from the dead they will repent
nd he said unto him f they hear not oses and the prophets neither will they be persuaded though one rose from the dead
hen said he unto the disciples t is impossible but that offences will come but woe unto him through whom they come
t were better for him that a millstone were hanged about his neck and he cast into the sea than that he should offend one of these little ones
ake heed to yourselves f thy brother trespass against thee rebuke him and if he repent forgive him
nd if he trespass against thee seven times in a day and seven times in a day turn again to thee saying  repent thou shalt forgive him
nd the apostles said unto the ord ncrease our faith
nd the ord said f ye had faith as a grain of mustard seed ye might say unto this sycamine tree e thou plucked up by the root and be thou planted in the sea and it should obey you
ut which of you having a servant plowing or feeding cattle will say unto him by and by when he is come from the field o and sit down to meat
nd will not rather say unto him ake ready wherewith  may sup and gird thyself and serve me till  have eaten and drunken and afterward thou shalt eat and drink
oth he thank that servant because he did the things that were commanded him
 trow not
o likewise ye when ye shall have done all those things which are commanded you say e are unprofitable servants we have done that which was our duty to do
nd it came to pass as he went to erusalem that he passed through the midst of amaria and alilee
nd as he entered into a certain village there met him ten men that were lepers which stood afar off and they lifted up their voices and said esus aster have mercy on us
nd when he saw them he said unto them o shew yourselves unto the priests
nd it came to pass that as they went they were cleansed
nd one of them when he saw that he was healed turned back and with a loud voice glorified od and fell down on his face at his feet giving him thanks and he was a amaritan
nd esus answering said ere there not ten cleansed but where are the nine
here are not found that returned to give glory to od save this stranger
nd he said unto him rise go thy way thy faith hath made thee whole
nd when he was demanded of the harisees when the kingdom of od should come he answered them and said he kingdom of od cometh not with observation neither shall they say o here or lo there for behold the kingdom of od is within you
nd he said unto the disciples he days will come when ye shall desire to see one of the days of the on of man and ye shall not see it
nd they shall say to you ee here or see there go not after them nor follow them
or as the lightning that lighteneth out of the one part under heaven shineth unto the other part under heaven so shall also the on of man be in his day
ut first must he suffer many things and be rejected of this generation
nd as it was in the days of oe so shall it be also in the days of the on of man
hey did eat they drank they married wives they were given in marriage until the day that oe entered into the ark and the flood came and destroyed them all
ikewise also as it was in the days of ot they did eat they drank they bought they sold they planted they builded but the same day that ot went out of odom it rained fire and brimstone from heaven and destroyed them all
ven thus shall it be in the day when the on of man is revealed
n that day he which shall be upon the housetop and his stuff in the house let him not come down to take it away and he that is in the field let him likewise not return back
emember ots wife
hosoever shall seek to save his life shall lose it and whosoever shall lose his life shall preserve it
 tell you in that night there shall be two men in one bed the one shall be taken and the other shall be left
wo women shall be grinding together the one shall be taken and the other left
wo men shall be in the field the one shall be taken and the other left
nd they answered and said unto him here ord
nd he said unto them heresoever the body is thither will the eagles be gathered together
nd he spake a parable unto them to this end that men ought always to pray and not to faint saying here was in a city a judge which feared not od neither regarded man and there was a widow in that city and she came unto him saying venge me of mine adversary
nd he would not for a while but afterward he said within himself hough  fear not od nor regard man yet because this widow troubleth me  will avenge her lest by her continual coming she weary me
nd the ord said ear what the unjust judge saith
nd shall not od avenge his own elect which cry day and night unto him though he bear long with them
 tell you that he will avenge them speedily
evertheless when the on of man cometh shall he find faith on the earth
nd he spake this parable unto certain which trusted in themselves that they were righteous and despised others wo men went up into the temple to pray the one a harisee and the other a publican
he harisee stood and prayed thus with himself od  thank thee that  am not as other men are extortioners unjust adulterers or even as this publican
 fast twice in the week  give tithes of all that  possess
nd the publican standing afar off would not lift up so much as his eyes unto heaven but smote upon his breast saying od be merciful to me a sinner
 tell you this man went down to his house justified rather than the other for every one that exalteth himself shall be abased and he that humbleth himself shall be exalted
nd they brought unto him also infants that he would touch them but when his disciples saw it they rebuked them
ut esus called them unto him and said uffer little children to come unto me and forbid them not for of such is the kingdom of od
erily  say unto you hosoever shall not receive the kingdom of od as a little child shall in no wise enter therein
nd a certain ruler asked him saying ood aster what shall  do to inherit eternal life
nd esus said unto him hy callest thou me good none is good save one that is od
hou knowest the commandments o not commit adultery o not kill o not steal o not bear false witness onour thy father and thy mother
nd he said ll these have  kept from my youth up
ow when esus heard these things he said unto him et lackest thou one thing sell all that thou hast and distribute unto the poor and thou shalt have treasure in heaven and come follow me
nd when he heard this he was very sorrowful for he was very rich
nd when esus saw that he was very sorrowful he said ow hardly shall they that have riches enter into the kingdom of od
or it is easier for a camel to go through a needles eye than for a rich man to enter into the kingdom of od
nd they that heard it said ho then can be saved
nd he said he things which are impossible with men are possible with od
hen eter said o we have left all and followed thee
nd he said unto them erily  say unto you here is no man that hath left house or parents or brethren or wife or children for the kingdom of ods sake who shall not receive manifold more in this present time and in the world to come life everlasting
hen he took unto him the twelve and said unto them ehold we go up to erusalem and all things that are written by the prophets concerning the on of man shall be accomplished
or he shall be delivered unto the entiles and shall be mocked and spitefully entreated and spitted on and they shall scourge him and put him to death and the third day he shall rise again
nd they understood none of these things and this saying was hid from them neither knew they the things which were spoken
nd it came to pass that as he was come nigh unto ericho a certain blind man sat by the way side begging and hearing the multitude pass by he asked what it meant
nd they told him that esus of azareth passeth by
nd he cried saying esus thou on of avid have mercy on me
nd they which went before rebuked him that he should hold his peace but he cried so much the more hou on of avid have mercy on me
nd esus stood and commanded him to be brought unto him and when he was come near he asked him saying hat wilt thou that  shall do unto thee
nd he said ord that  may receive my sight
nd esus said unto him eceive thy sight thy faith hath saved thee
nd immediately he received his sight and followed him glorifying od and all the people when they saw it gave praise unto od
nd esus entered and passed through ericho
nd behold there was a man named acch us which was the chief among the publicans and he was rich
nd he sought to see esus who he was and could not for the press because he was little of stature
nd he ran before and climbed up into a sycomore tree to see him for he was to pass that way
nd when esus came to the place he looked up and saw him and said unto him acch us make haste and come down for to day  must abide at thy house
nd he made haste and came down and received him joyfully
nd when they saw it they all murmured saying hat he was gone to be guest with a man that is a sinner
nd acch us stood and said unto the ord ehold ord the half of my goods  give to the poor and if  have taken any thing from any man by false accusation  restore him fourfold
nd esus said unto him his day is salvation come to this house forsomuch as he also is a son of braham
or the on of man is come to seek and to save that which was lost
nd as they heard these things he added and spake a parable because he was nigh to erusalem and because they thought that the kingdom of od should immediately appear
e said therefore  certain nobleman went into a far country to receive for himself a kingdom and to return
nd he called his ten servants and delivered them ten pounds and said unto them ccupy till  come
ut his citizens hated him and sent a message after him saying e will not have this man to reign over us
nd it came to pass that when he was returned having received the kingdom then he commanded these servants to be called unto him to whom he had given the money that he might know how much every man had gained by trading
hen came the first saying ord thy pound hath gained ten pounds
nd he said unto him ell thou good servant because thou hast been faithful in a very little have thou authority over ten cities
nd the second came saying ord thy pound hath gained five pounds
nd he said likewise to him e thou also over five cities
nd another came saying ord behold here is thy pound which  have kept laid up in a napkin for  feared thee because thou art an austere man thou takest up that thou layedst not down and reapest that thou didst not sow
nd he saith unto him ut of thine own mouth will  judge thee thou wicked servant
hou knewest that  was an austere man taking up that  laid not down and reaping that  did not sow wherefore then gavest not thou my money into the bank that at my coming  might have required mine own with usury
nd he said unto them that stood by ake from him the pound and give it to him that hath ten pounds
nd they said unto him ord he hath ten pounds or  say unto you hat unto every one which hath shall be given and from him that hath not even that he hath shall be taken away from him
ut those mine enemies which would not that  should reign over them bring hither and slay them before me
nd when he had thus spoken he went before ascending up to erusalem
nd it came to pass when he was come nigh to ethphage and ethany at the mount called the mount of lives he sent two of his disciples saying o ye into the village over against you in the which at your entering ye shall find a colt tied whereon yet never man sat loose him and bring him hither
nd if any man ask you hy do ye loose him thus shall ye say unto him ecause the ord hath need of him
nd they that were sent went their way and found even as he had said unto them
nd as they were loosing the colt the owners thereof said unto them hy loose ye the colt
nd they said he ord hath need of him
nd they brought him to esus and they cast their garments upon the colt and they set esus thereon
nd as he went they spread their clothes in the way
nd when he was come nigh even now at the descent of the mount of lives the whole multitude of the disciples began to rejoice and praise od with a loud voice for all the mighty works that they had seen saying lessed be the ing that cometh in the name of the ord peace in heaven and glory in the highest
nd some of the harisees from among the multitude said unto him aster rebuke thy disciples
nd he answered and said unto them  tell you that if these should hold their peace the stones would immediately cry out
nd when he was come near he beheld the city and wept over it saying f thou hadst known even thou at least in this thy day the things which belong unto thy peace but now they are hid from thine eyes
or the days shall come upon thee that thine enemies shall cast a trench about thee and compass thee round and keep thee in on every side and shall lay thee even with the ground and thy children within thee and they shall not leave in thee one stone upon another because thou knewest not the time of thy visitation
nd he went into the temple and began to cast out them that sold therein and them that bought saying unto them t is written y house is the house of prayer but ye have made it a den of thieves
nd he taught daily in the temple
ut the chief priests and the scribes and the chief of the people sought to destroy him and could not find what they might do for all the people were very attentive to hear him
nd it came to pass that on one of those days as he taught the people in the temple and preached the gospel the chief priests and the scribes came upon him with the elders and spake unto him saying ell us by what authority doest thou these things or who is he that gave thee this authority
nd he answered and said unto them  will also ask you one thing and answer me he baptism of ohn was it from heaven or of men
nd they reasoned with themselves saying f we shall say rom heaven he will say hy then believed ye him not
ut and if we say f men all the people will stone us for they be persuaded that ohn was a prophet
nd they answered that they could not tell whence it was
nd esus said unto them either tell  you by what authority  do these things
hen began he to speak to the people this parable  certain man planted a vineyard and let it forth to husbandmen and went into a far country for a long time
nd at the season he sent a servant to the husbandmen that they should give him of the fruit of the vineyard but the husbandmen beat him and sent him away empty
nd again he sent another servant and they beat him also and entreated him shamefully and sent him away empty
nd again he sent a third and they wounded him also and cast him out
hen said the lord of the vineyard hat shall  do
 will send my beloved son it may be they will reverence him when they see him
ut when the husbandmen saw him they reasoned among themselves saying his is the heir come let us kill him that the inheritance may be ours
o they cast him out of the vineyard and killed him
hat therefore shall the lord of the vineyard do unto them
e shall come and destroy these husbandmen and shall give the vineyard to others
nd when they heard it they said od forbid
nd he beheld them and said hat is this then that is written he stone which the builders rejected he same is become the head of the corner
hosoever shall fall upon that stone shall be broken but on whomsoever it shall fall it will grind him to powder
nd the chief priests and the scribes the same hour sought to lay hands on him and they feared the people for they perceived that he had spoken this parable against them
nd they watched him and sent forth spies which should feign themselves just men that they might take hold of his words that so they might deliver him unto the power and authority of the governor
nd they asked him saying aster we know that thou sayest and teachest rightly neither acceptest thou the person of any but teachest the way of od truly s it lawful for us to give tribute unto  sar or no
ut he perceived their craftiness and said unto them hy tempt ye me
hew me a penny
hose image and superscription hath it
hey answered and said  sars
nd he said unto them ender therefore unto  sar the things which be  sars and unto od the things which be ods
nd they could not take hold of his words before the people and they marvelled at his answer and held their peace
hen came to him certain of the adducees which deny that there is any resurrection and they asked him saying aster oses wrote unto us f any mans brother die having a wife and he die without children that his brother should take his wife and raise up seed unto his brother
here were therefore seven brethren and the first took a wife and died without children
nd the second took her to wife and he died childless
nd the third took her and in like manner the seven also and they left no children and died
ast of all the woman died also
herefore in the resurrection whose wife of them is she for seven had her to wife
nd esus answering said unto them he children of this world marry and are given in marriage but they which shall be accounted worthy to obtain that world and the resurrection from the dead neither marry nor are given in marriage neither can they die any more for they are equal unto the angels and are the children of od being the children of the resurrection
ow that the dead are raised even oses shewed at the bush when he calleth the ord the od of braham and the od of saac and the od of acob
or he is not a od of the dead but of the living for all live unto him
hen certain of the scribes answering said aster thou hast well said
nd after that they durst not ask him any question at all
nd he said unto them ow say they that hrist is avids son
nd avid himself saith in the book of salms he  said unto my ord it thou on my right hand ill  make thine enemies thy footstool
avid therefore calleth him ord how is he then his son
hen in the audience of all the people he said unto his disciples eware of the scribes which desire to walk in long robes and love greetings in the markets and the highest seats in the synagogues and the chief rooms at feasts which devour widows houses and for a shew make long prayers the same shall receive greater damnation
nd he looked up and saw the rich men casting their gifts into the treasury
nd he saw also a certain poor widow casting in thither two mites
nd he said f a truth  say unto you that this poor widow hath cast in more than they all for all these have of their abundance cast in unto the offerings of od but she of her penury hath cast in all the living that she had
nd as some spake of the temple how it was adorned with goodly stones and gifts he said s for these things which ye behold the days will come in the which there shall not be left one stone upon another that shall not be thrown down
nd they asked him saying aster but when shall these things be and what sign will there be when these things shall come to pass
nd he said ake heed that ye be not deceived for many shall come in my name saying  am hrist and the time draweth near go ye not therefore after them
ut when ye shall hear of wars and commotions be not terrified for these things must first come to pass but the end is not by and by
hen said he unto them ation shall rise against nation and kingdom against kingdom and great earthquakes shall be in divers places and famines and pestilences and fearful sights and great signs shall there be from heaven
ut before all these they shall lay their hands on you and persecute you delivering you up to the synagogues and into prisons being brought before kings and rulers for my names sake
nd it shall turn to you for a testimony
ettle it therefore in your hearts not to meditate before what ye shall answer for  will give you a mouth and wisdom which all your adversaries shall not be able to gainsay nor resist
nd ye shall be betrayed both by parents and brethren and kinsfolks and friends and some of you shall they cause to be put to death
nd ye shall be hated of all men for my names sake
ut there shall not an hair of your head perish
n your patience possess ye your souls
nd when ye shall see erusalem compassed with armies then know that the desolation thereof is nigh
hen let them which are in ud a flee to the mountains and let them which are in the midst of it depart out and let not them that are in the countries enter thereinto
or these be the days of vengeance that all things which are written may be fulfilled
ut woe unto them that are with child and to them that give suck in those days for there shall be great distress in the land and wrath upon this people
nd they shall fall by the edge of the sword and shall be led away captive into all nations and erusalem shall be trodden down of the entiles until the times of the entiles be fulfilled
nd there shall be signs in the sun and in the moon and in the stars and upon the earth distress of nations with perplexity the sea and the waves roaring mens hearts failing them for fear and for looking after those things which are coming on the earth for the powers of heaven shall be shaken
nd then shall they see the on of man coming in a cloud with power and great glory
nd when these things begin to come to pass then look up and lift up your heads for your redemption draweth nigh
nd he spake to them a parable ehold the fig tree and all the trees when they now shoot forth ye see and know of your own selves that summer is now nigh at hand
o likewise ye when ye see these things come to pass know ye that the kingdom of od is nigh at hand
erily  say unto you his generation shall not pass away till all be fulfilled
eaven and earth shall pass away but my words shall not pass away
nd take heed to yourselves lest at any time your hearts be overcharged with surfeiting and drunkenness and cares of this life and so that day come upon you unawares
or as a snare shall it come on all them that dwell on the face of the whole earth
atch ye therefore and pray always that ye may be accounted worthy to escape all these things that shall come to pass and to stand before the on of man
nd in the day time he was teaching in the temple and at night he went out and abode in the mount that is called the mount of lives
nd all the people came early in the morning to him in the temple for to hear him
ow the feast of unleavened bread drew nigh which is called the assover
nd the chief priests and scribes sought how they might kill him for they feared the people
hen entered atan into udas surnamed scariot being of the number of the twelve
nd he went his way and communed with the chief priests and captains how he might betray him unto them
nd they were glad and covenanted to give him money
nd he promised and sought opportunity to betray him unto them in the absence of the multitude
hen came the day of unleavened bread when the passover must be killed
nd he sent eter and ohn saying o and prepare us the passover that we may eat
nd they said unto him here wilt thou that we prepare
nd he said unto them ehold when ye are entered into the city there shall a man meet you bearing a pitcher of water follow him into the house where he entereth in
nd ye shall say unto the goodman of the house he aster saith unto thee here is the guestchamber where  shall eat the passover with my disciples
nd he shall shew you a large upper room furnished there make ready
nd they went and found as he had said unto them and they made ready the passover
nd when the hour was come he sat down and the twelve apostles with him
nd he said unto them ith desire  have desired to eat this passover with you before  suffer for  say unto you  will not any more eat thereof until it be fulfilled in the kingdom of od
nd he took the cup and gave thanks and said ake this and divide it among yourselves for  say unto you  will not drink of the fruit of the vine until the kingdom of od shall come
nd he took bread and gave thanks and brake it and gave unto them saying his is my body which is given for you this do in remembrance of me
ikewise also the cup after supper saying his cup is the new testament in my blood which is shed for you
ut behold the hand of him that betrayeth me is with me on the table
nd truly the on of man goeth as it was determined but woe unto that man by whom he is betrayed
nd they began to enquire among themselves which of them it was that should do this thing
nd there was also a strife among them which of them should be accounted the greatest
nd he said unto them he kings of the entiles exercise lordship over them and they that exercise authority upon them are called benefactors
ut ye shall not be so but he that is greatest among you let him be as the younger and he that is chief as he that doth serve
or whether is greater he that sitteth at meat or he that serveth is not he that sitteth at meat but  am among you as he that serveth
e are they which have continued with me in my temptations
nd  appoint unto you a kingdom as my ather hath appointed unto me that ye may eat and drink at my table in my kingdom and sit on thrones judging the twelve tribes of srael
nd the ord said imon imon behold atan hath desired to have you that he may sift you as wheat but  have prayed for thee that thy faith fail not and when thou art converted strengthen thy brethren
nd he said unto him ord  am ready to go with thee both into prison and to death
nd he said  tell thee eter the cock shall not crow this day before that thou shalt thrice deny that thou knowest me
nd he said unto them hen  sent you without purse and scrip and shoes lacked ye any thing
nd they said othing
hen said he unto them ut now he that hath a purse let him take it and likewise his scrip and he that hath no sword let him sell his garment and buy one
or  say unto you that this that is written must yet be accomplished in me nd he was reckoned among the transgressors for the things concerning me have an end
nd they said ord behold here are two swords
nd he said unto them t is enough
nd he came out and went as he was wont to the mount of lives and his disciples also followed him
nd when he was at the place he said unto them ray that ye enter not into temptation
nd he was withdrawn from them about a stones cast and kneeled down and prayed saying ather if thou be willing remove this cup from me nevertheless not my will but thine be done
nd there appeared an angel unto him from heaven strengthening him
nd being in an agony he prayed more earnestly and his sweat was as it were great drops of blood falling down to the ground
nd when he rose up from prayer and was come to his disciples he found them sleeping for sorrow and said unto them hy sleep ye rise and pray lest ye enter into temptation
nd while he yet spake behold a multitude and he that was called udas one of the twelve went before them and drew near unto esus to kiss him
ut esus said unto him udas betrayest thou the on of man with a kiss
hen they which were about him saw what would follow they said unto him ord shall we smite with the sword
nd one of them smote the servant of the high priest and cut off his right ear
nd esus answered and said uffer ye thus far
nd he touched his ear and healed him
hen esus said unto the chief priests and captains of the temple and the elders which were come to him e ye come out as against a thief with swords and staves
hen  was daily with you in the temple ye stretched forth no hands against me but this is your hour and the power of darkness
hen took they him and led him and brought him into the high priests house
nd eter followed afar off
nd when they had kindled a fire in the midst of the hall and were set down together eter sat down among them
ut a certain maid beheld him as he sat by the fire and earnestly looked upon him and said his man was also with him
nd he denied him saying oman  know him not
nd after a little while another saw him and said hou art also of them
nd eter said an  am not
nd about the space of one hour after another confidently affirmed saying f a truth this fellow also was with him for he is a alil an
nd eter said an  know not what thou sayest
nd immediately while he yet spake the cock crew
nd the ord turned and looked upon eter
nd eter remembered the word of the ord how he had said unto him efore the cock crow thou shalt deny me thrice
nd eter went out and wept bitterly
nd the men that held esus mocked him and smote him
nd when they had blindfolded him they struck him on the face and asked him saying rophesy who is it that smote thee
nd many other things blasphemously spake they against him
nd as soon as it was day the elders of the people and the chief priests and the scribes came together and led him into their council saying rt thou the hrist tell us
nd he said unto them f  tell you ye will not believe and if  also ask you ye will not answer me nor let me go
ereafter shall the on of man sit on the right hand of the power of od
hen said they all rt thou then the on of od
nd he said unto them e say that  am
nd they said hat need we any further witness for we ourselves have heard of his own mouth
nd the whole multitude of them arose and led him unto ilate
nd they began to accuse him saying e found this fellow perverting the nation and forbidding to give tribute to  sar saying that he himself is hrist a ing
nd ilate asked him saying rt thou the ing of the ews
nd he answered him and said hou sayest it
hen said ilate to the chief priests and to the people  find no fault in this man
nd they were the more fierce saying e stirreth up the people teaching throughout all ewry beginning from alilee to this place
hen ilate heard of alilee he asked whether the man were a alil an
nd as soon as he knew that he belonged unto erods jurisdiction he sent him to erod who himself also was at erusalem at that time
nd when erod saw esus he was exceeding glad for he was desirous to see him of a long season because he had heard many things of him and he hoped to have seen some miracle done by him
hen he questioned with him in many words but he answered him nothing
nd the chief priests and scribes stood and vehemently accused him
nd erod with his men of war set him at nought and mocked him and arrayed him in a gorgeous robe and sent him again to ilate
nd the same day ilate and erod were made friends together for before they were at enmity between themselves
nd ilate when he had called together the chief priests and the rulers and the people said unto them e have brought this man unto me as one that perverteth the people and behold  having examined him before you have found no fault in this man touching those things whereof ye accuse him o nor yet erod for  sent you to him and lo nothing worthy of death is done unto him
 will therefore chastise him and release him
or of necessity he must release one unto them at the feast nd they cried out all at once saying way with this man and release unto us arabbas who for a certain sedition made in the city and for murder was cast into prison ilate therefore willing to release esus spake again to them
ut they cried saying rucify him crucify him
nd he said unto them the third time hy what evil hath he done
 have found no cause of death in him  will therefore chastise him and let him go
nd they were instant with loud voices requiring that he might be crucified
nd the voices of them and of the chief priests prevailed
nd ilate gave sentence that it should be as they required
nd he released unto them him that for sedition and murder was cast into prison whom they had desired but he delivered esus to their will
nd as they led him away they laid hold upon one imon a yrenian coming out of the country and on him they laid the cross that he might bear it after esus
nd there followed him a great company of people and of women which also bewailed and lamented him
ut esus turning unto them said aughters of erusalem weep not for me but weep for yourselves and for your children
or behold the days are coming in the which they shall say lessed are the barren and the wombs that never bare and the paps which never gave suck
hen shall they begin to say to the mountains all on us and to the hills over us
or if they do these things in a green tree what shall be done in the dry
nd there were also two other malefactors led with him to be put to death
nd when they were come to the place which is called alvary there they crucified him and the malefactors one on the right hand and the other on the left
hen said esus ather forgive them for they know not what they do
nd they parted his raiment and cast lots
nd the people stood beholding
nd the rulers also with them derided him saying e saved others let him save himself if he be hrist the chosen of od
nd the soldiers also mocked him coming to him and offering him vinegar and saying f thou be the king of the ews save thyself
nd a superscription also was written over him in letters of reek and atin and ebrew       
nd one of the malefactors which were hanged railed on him saying f thou be hrist save thyself and us
ut the other answering rebuked him saying ost not thou fear od seeing thou art in the same condemnation
nd we indeed justly for we receive the due reward of our deeds but this man hath done nothing amiss
nd he said unto esus ord remember me when thou comest into thy kingdom
nd esus said unto him erily  say unto thee o day shalt thou be with me in paradise
nd it was about the sixth hour and there was a darkness over all the earth until the ninth hour
nd the sun was darkened and the veil of the temple was rent in the midst
nd when esus had cried with a loud voice he said ather into thy hands  commend my spirit and having said thus he gave up the ghost
ow when the centurion saw what was done he glorified od saying ertainly this was a righteous man
nd all the people that came together to that sight beholding the things which were done smote their breasts and returned
nd all his acquaintance and the women that followed him from alilee stood afar off beholding these things
nd behold there was a man named oseph a counsellor and he was a good man and a just the same had not consented to the counsel and deed of them he was of rimath a a city of the ews who also himself waited for the kingdom of od
his man went unto ilate and begged the body of esus
nd he took it down and wrapped it in linen and laid it in a sepulchre that was hewn in stone wherein never man before was laid
nd that day was the preparation and the sabbath drew on
nd the women also which came with him from alilee followed after and beheld the sepulchre and how his body was laid
nd they returned and prepared spices and ointments and rested the sabbath day according to the commandment
ow upon the first day of the week very early in the morning they came unto the sepulchre bringing the spices which they had prepared and certain others with them
nd they found the stone rolled away from the sepulchre
nd they entered in and found not the body of the ord esus
nd it came to pass as they were much perplexed thereabout behold two men stood by them in shining garments and as they were afraid and bowed down their faces to the earth they said unto them hy seek ye the living among the dead
e is not here but is risen remember how he spake unto you when he was yet in alilee saying he on of man must be delivered into the hands of sinful men and be crucified and the third day rise again
nd they remembered his words and returned from the sepulchre and told all these things unto the eleven and to all the rest
t was ary agdalene and oanna and ary the mother of ames and other women that were with them which told these things unto the apostles
nd their words seemed to them as idle tales and they believed them not
hen arose eter and ran unto the sepulchre and stooping down he beheld the linen clothes laid by themselves and departed wondering in himself at that which was come to pass
nd behold two of them went that same day to a village called mmaus which was from erusalem about threescore furlongs
nd they talked together of all these things which had happened
nd it came to pass that while they communed together and reasoned esus himself drew near and went with them
ut their eyes were holden that they should not know him
nd he said unto them hat manner of communications are these that ye have one to another as ye walk and are sad
nd the one of them whose name was leopas answering said unto him rt thou only a stranger in erusalem and hast not known the things which are come to pass there in these days
nd he said unto them hat things
nd they said unto him oncerning esus of azareth which was a prophet mighty in deed and word before od and all the people and how the chief priests and our rulers delivered him to be condemned to death and have crucified him
ut we trusted that it had been he which should have redeemed srael and beside all this to day is the third day since these things were done
ea and certain women also of our company made us astonished which were early at the sepulchre and when they found not his body they came saying that they had also seen a vision of angels which said that he was alive
nd certain of them which were with us went to the sepulchre and found it even so as the women had said but him they saw not
hen he said unto them  fools and slow of heart to believe all that the prophets have spoken ought not hrist to have suffered these things and to enter into his glory
nd beginning at oses and all the prophets he expounded unto them in all the scriptures the things concerning himself
nd they drew nigh unto the village whither they went and he made as though he would have gone further
ut they constrained him saying bide with us for it is toward evening and the day is far spent
nd he went in to tarry with them
nd it came to pass as he sat at meat with them he took bread and blessed it and brake and gave to them
nd their eyes were opened and they knew him and he vanished out of their sight
nd they said one to another id not our heart burn within us while he talked with us by the way and while he opened to us the scriptures
nd they rose up the same hour and returned to erusalem and found the eleven gathered together and them that were with them saying he ord is risen indeed and hath appeared to imon
nd they told what things were done in the way and how he was known of them in breaking of bread
nd as they thus spake esus himself stood in the midst of them and saith unto them eace be unto you
ut they were terrified and affrighted and supposed that they had seen a spirit
nd he said unto them hy are ye troubled and why do thoughts arise in your hearts
ehold my hands and my feet that it is  myself handle me and see for a spirit hath not flesh and bones as ye see me have
nd when he had thus spoken he shewed them his hands and his feet
nd while they yet believed not for joy and wondered he said unto them ave ye here any meat
nd they gave him a piece of a broiled fish and of an honeycomb
nd he took it and did eat before them
nd he said unto them hese are the words which  spake unto you while  was yet with you that all things must be fulfilled which were written in the law of oses and in the prophets and in the psalms concerning me
hen opened he their understanding that they might understand the scriptures and said unto them hus it is written and thus it behoved hrist to suffer and to rise from the dead the third day and that repentance and remission of sins should be preached in his name among all nations beginning at erusalem
nd ye are witnesses of these things
nd behold  send the promise of my ather upon you but tarry ye in the city of erusalem until ye be endued with power from on high
nd he led them out as far as to ethany and he lifted up his hands and blessed them
nd it came to pass while he blessed them he was parted from them and carried up into heaven
nd they worshipped him and returned to erusalem with great joy and were continually in the temple praising and blessing od
men
n the beginning was the ord and the ord was with od and the ord was od
he same was in the beginning with od
ll things were made by him and without him was not any thing made that was made
n him was life and the life was the light of men
nd the light shineth in darkness and the darkness comprehended it not
here was a man sent from od whose name was ohn
he same came for a witness to bear witness of the ight that all men through him might believe
e was not that ight but was sent to bear witness of that ight
hat was the true ight which lighteth every man that cometh into the world
e was in the world and the world was made by him and the world knew him not
e came unto his own and his own received him not
ut as many as received him to them gave he power to become the sons of od even to them that believe on his name which were born not of blood nor of the will of the flesh nor of the will of man but of od
nd the ord was made flesh and dwelt among us and we beheld his glory the glory as of the only begotten of the ather  full of grace and truth
ohn bare witness of him and cried saying his was he of whom  spake e that cometh after me is preferred before me for he was before me
nd of his fulness have all we received and grace for grace
or the law was given by oses but grace and truth came by esus hrist
o man hath seen od at any time the only begotten on which is in the bosom of the ather he hath declared him
nd this is the record of ohn when the ews sent priests and evites from erusalem to ask him ho art thou
nd he confessed and denied not but confessed  am not the hrist
nd they asked him hat then
rt thou lias
nd he saith  am not
rt thou that prophet
nd he answered o
hen said they unto him ho art thou that we may give an answer to them that sent us
hat sayest thou of thyself
e said  am the voice of one crying in the wilderness ake straight the way of the ord as said the prophet saias
nd they which were sent were of the harisees
nd they asked him and said unto him hy baptizest thou then if thou be not that hrist nor lias neither that prophet
ohn answered them saying  baptize with water but there standeth one among you whom ye know not he it is who coming after me is preferred before me whose shoes latchet  am not worthy to unloose
hese things were done in ethabara beyond ordan where ohn was baptizing
he next day ohn seeth esus coming unto him and saith ehold the amb of od which taketh away the sin of the world
his is he of whom  said fter me cometh a man which is preferred before me for he was before me
nd  knew him not but that he should be made manifest to srael therefore am  come baptizing with water
nd ohn bare record saying  saw the pirit descending from heaven like a dove and it abode upon him
nd  knew him not but he that sent me to baptize with water the same said unto me pon whom thou shalt see the pirit descending and remaining on him the same is he which baptizeth with the oly host
nd  saw and bare record that this is the on of od
gain the next day after ohn stood and two of his disciples and looking upon esus as he walked he saith ehold the amb of od
nd the two disciples heard him speak and they followed esus
hen esus turned and saw them following and saith unto them hat seek ye
hey said unto him abbi which is to say being interpreted aster  where dwellest thou
e saith unto them ome and see
hey came and saw where he dwelt and abode with him that day for it was about the tenth hour
ne of the two which heard ohn speak and followed him was ndrew imon eters brother
e first findeth his own brother imon and saith unto him e have found the essias which is being interpreted the hrist
nd he brought him to esus
nd when esus beheld him he said hou art imon the son of ona thou shalt be called ephas which is by interpretation  stone
he day following esus would go forth into alilee and findeth hilip and saith unto him ollow me
ow hilip was of ethsaida the city of ndrew and eter
hilip findeth athanael and saith unto him e have found him of whom oses in the law and the prophets did write esus of azareth the son of oseph
nd athanael said unto him an there any good thing come out of azareth
hilip saith unto him ome and see
esus saw athanael coming to him and saith of him ehold an sraelite indeed in whom is no guile
athanael saith unto him hence knowest thou me
esus answered and said unto him efore that hilip called thee when thou wast under the fig tree  saw thee
athanael answered and saith unto him abbi thou art the on of od thou art the ing of srael
esus answered and said unto him ecause  said unto thee  saw thee under the fig tree believest thou thou shalt see greater things than these
nd he saith unto him erily verily  say unto you ereafter ye shall see heaven open and the angels of od ascending and descending upon the on of man
nd the third day there was a marriage in ana of alilee and the mother of esus was there and both esus was called and his disciples to the marriage
nd when they wanted wine the mother of esus saith unto him hey have no wine
esus saith unto her oman what have  to do with thee mine hour is not yet come
is mother saith unto the servants hatsoever he saith unto you do it
nd there were set there six waterpots of stone after the manner of the purifying of the ews containing two or three firkins apiece
esus saith unto them ill the waterpots with water
nd they filled them up to the brim
nd he saith unto them raw out now and bear unto the governor of the feast
nd they bare it
hen the ruler of the feast had tasted the water that was made wine and knew not whence it was but the servants which drew the water knew the governor of the feast called the bridegroom and saith unto him very man at the beginning doth set forth good wine and when men have well drunk then that which is worse but thou hast kept the good wine until now
his beginning of miracles did esus in ana of alilee and manifested forth his glory and his disciples believed on him
fter this he went down to apernaum he and his mother and his brethren and his disciples and they continued there not many days
nd the ews passover was at hand and esus went up to erusalem and found in the temple those that sold oxen and sheep and doves and the changers of money sitting and when he had made a scourge of small cords he drove them all out of the temple and the sheep and the oxen and poured out the changers money and overthrew the tables and said unto them that sold doves ake these things hence make not my athers house an house of merchandise
nd his disciples remembered that it was written he zeal of thine house hath eaten me up
hen answered the ews and said unto him hat sign shewest thou unto us seeing that thou doest these things
esus answered and said unto them estroy this temple and in three days  will raise it up
hen said the ews orty and six years was this temple in building and wilt thou rear it up in three days
ut he spake of the temple of his body
hen therefore he was risen from the dead his disciples remembered that he had said this unto them and they believed the scripture and the word which esus had said
ow when he was in erusalem at the passover in the feast day many believed in his name when they saw the miracles which he did
ut esus did not commit himself unto them because he knew all men and needed not that any should testify of man for he knew what was in man
here was a man of the harisees named icodemus a ruler of the ews the same came to esus by night and said unto him abbi we know that thou art a teacher come from od for no man can do these miracles that thou doest except od be with him
esus answered and said unto him erily verily  say unto thee xcept a man be born again he cannot see the kingdom of od
icodemus saith unto him ow can a man be born when he is old can he enter the second time into his mothers womb and be born
esus answered erily verily  say unto thee xcept a man be born of water and of the pirit he cannot enter into the kingdom of od
hat which is born of the flesh is flesh and that which is born of the pirit is spirit
arvel not that  said unto thee e must be born again
he wind bloweth where it listeth and thou hearest the sound thereof but canst not tell whence it cometh and whither it goeth so is every one that is born of the pirit
icodemus answered and said unto him ow can these things be
esus answered and said unto him rt thou a master of srael and knowest not these things
erily verily  say unto thee e speak that we do know and testify that we have seen and ye receive not our witness
f  have told you earthly things and ye believe not how shall ye believe if  tell you of heavenly things
nd no man hath ascended up to heaven but he that came down from heaven even the on of man which is in heaven
nd as oses lifted up the serpent in the wilderness even so must the on of man be lifted up that whosoever believeth in him should not perish but have eternal life
