or od so loved the world that he gave his only begotten on that whosoever believeth in him should not perish but have everlasting life
or od sent not his on into the world to condemn the world but that the world through him might be saved
e that believeth on him is not condemned but he that believeth not is condemned already because he hath not believed in the name of the only begotten on of od
nd this is the condemnation that light is come into the world and men loved darkness rather than light because their deeds were evil
or every one that doeth evil hateth the light neither cometh to the light lest his deeds should be reproved
ut he that doeth truth cometh to the light that his deeds may be made manifest that they are wrought in od
fter these things came esus and his disciples into the land of ud a and there he tarried with them and baptized
nd ohn also was baptizing in  non near to alim because there was much water there and they came and were baptized
or ohn was not yet cast into prison
hen there arose a question between some of ohns disciples and the ews about purifying
nd they came unto ohn and said unto him abbi he that was with thee beyond ordan to whom thou barest witness behold the same baptizeth and all men come to him
ohn answered and said  man can receive nothing except it be given him from heaven
e yourselves bear me witness that  said  am not the hrist but that  am sent before him
e that hath the bride is the bridegroom but the friend of the bridegroom which standeth and heareth him rejoiceth greatly because of the bridegrooms voice this my joy therefore is fulfilled
e must increase but  must decrease
e that cometh from above is above all he that is of the earth is earthly and speaketh of the earth he that cometh from heaven is above all
nd what he hath seen and heard that he testifieth and no man receiveth his testimony
e that hath received his testimony hath set to his seal that od is true
or he whom od hath sent speaketh the words of od for od giveth not the pirit by measure unto him
he ather loveth the on and hath given all things into his hand
e that believeth on the on hath everlasting life and he that believeth not the on shall not see life but the wrath of od abideth on him
hen therefore the ord knew how the harisees had heard that esus made and baptized more disciples than ohn though esus himself baptized not but his disciples  he left ud a and departed again into alilee
nd he must needs go through amaria
hen cometh he to a city of amaria which is called ychar near to the parcel of ground that acob gave to his son oseph
ow acobs well was there
esus therefore being wearied with his journey sat thus on the well and it was about the sixth hour
here cometh a woman of amaria to draw water esus saith unto her ive me to drink
or his disciples were gone away unto the city to buy meat hen saith the woman of amaria unto him ow is it that thou being a ew askest drink of me which am a woman of amaria for the ews have no dealings with the amaritans
esus answered and said unto her f thou knewest the gift of od and who it is that saith to thee ive me to drink thou wouldest have asked of him and he would have given thee living water
he woman saith unto him ir thou hast nothing to draw with and the well is deep from whence then hast thou that living water
rt thou greater than our father acob which gave us the well and drank thereof himself and his children and his cattle
esus answered and said unto her hosoever drinketh of this water shall thirst again but whosoever drinketh of the water that  shall give him shall never thirst but the water that  shall give him shall be in him a well of water springing up into everlasting life
he woman saith unto him ir give me this water that  thirst not neither come hither to draw
esus saith unto her o call thy husband and come hither
he woman answered and said  have no husband
esus said unto her hou hast well said  have no husband for thou hast had five husbands and he whom thou now hast is not thy husband in that saidst thou truly
he woman saith unto him ir  perceive that thou art a prophet
ur fathers worshipped in this mountain and ye say that in erusalem is the place where men ought to worship
esus saith unto her oman believe me the hour cometh when ye shall neither in this mountain nor yet at erusalem worship the ather
e worship ye know not what we know what we worship for salvation is of the ews
ut the hour cometh and now is when the true worshippers shall worship the ather in spirit and in truth for the ather seeketh such to worship him
od is a pirit and they that worship him must worship him in spirit and in truth
he woman saith unto him  know that essias cometh which is called hrist when he is come he will tell us all things
esus saith unto her  that speak unto thee am he
nd upon this came his disciples and marvelled that he talked with the woman yet no man said hat seekest thou or hy talkest thou with her
he woman then left her waterpot and went her way into the city and saith to the men ome see a man which told me all things that ever  did is not this the hrist
hen they went out of the city and came unto him
n the mean while his disciples prayed him saying aster eat
ut he said unto them  have meat to eat that ye know not of
herefore said the disciples one to another ath any man brought him ought to eat
esus saith unto them y meat is to do the will of him that sent me and to finish his work
ay not ye here are yet four months and then cometh harvest behold  say unto you ift up your eyes and look on the fields for they are white already to harvest
nd he that reapeth receiveth wages and gathereth fruit unto life eternal that both he that soweth and he that reapeth may rejoice together
nd herein is that saying true ne soweth and another reapeth
 sent you to reap that whereon ye bestowed no labour other men laboured and ye are entered into their labours
nd many of the amaritans of that city believed on him for the saying of the woman which testified e told me all that ever  did
o when the amaritans were come unto him they besought him that he would tarry with them and he abode there two days
nd many more believed because of his own word and said unto the woman ow we believe not because of thy saying for we have heard him ourselves and know that this is indeed the hrist the aviour of the world
ow after two days he departed thence and went into alilee
or esus himself testified that a prophet hath no honour in his own country
hen when he was come into alilee the alil ans received him having seen all the things that he did at erusalem at the feast for they also went unto the feast
o esus came again into ana of alilee where he made the water wine
nd there was a certain nobleman whose son was sick at apernaum
hen he heard that esus was come out of ud a into alilee he went unto him and besought him that he would come down and heal his son for he was at the point of death
hen said esus unto him xcept ye see signs and wonders ye will not believe
he nobleman saith unto him ir come down ere my child die
esus saith unto him o thy way thy son liveth
nd the man believed the word that esus had spoken unto him and he went his way
nd as he was now going down his servants met him and told him saying hy son liveth
hen enquired he of them the hour when he began to amend
nd they said unto him esterday at the seventh hour the fever left him
o the father knew that it was at the same hour in the which esus said unto him hy son liveth and himself believed and his whole house
his is again the second miracle that esus did when he was come out of ud a into alilee
fter this there was a feast of the ews and esus went up to erusalem
ow there is at erusalem by the sheep market a pool which is called in the ebrew tongue ethesda having five porches
n these lay a great multitude of impotent folk of blind halt withered waiting for the moving of the water
or an angel went down at a certain season into the pool and troubled the water whosoever then first after the troubling of the water stepped in was made whole of whatsoever disease he had
nd a certain man was there which had an infirmity thirty and eight years
hen esus saw him lie and knew that he had been now a long time in that case he saith unto him ilt thou be made whole
he impotent man answered him ir  have no man when the water is troubled to put me into the pool but while  am coming another steppeth down before me
esus saith unto him ise take up thy bed and walk
nd immediately the man was made whole and took up his bed and walked and on the same day was the sabbath
he ews therefore said unto him that was cured t is the sabbath day it is not lawful for thee to carry thy bed
e answered them e that made me whole the same said unto me ake up thy bed and walk
hen asked they him hat man is that which said unto thee ake up thy bed and walk
nd he that was healed wist not who it was for esus had conveyed himself away a multitude being in that place
fterward esus findeth him in the temple and said unto him ehold thou art made whole sin no more lest a worse thing come unto thee
he man departed and told the ews that it was esus which had made him whole
nd therefore did the ews persecute esus and sought to slay him because he had done these things on the sabbath day
ut esus answered them y ather worketh hitherto and  work
herefore the ews sought the more to kill him because he not only had broken the sabbath but said also that od was his ather making himself equal with od
hen answered esus and said unto them erily verily  say unto you he on can do nothing of himself but what he seeth the ather do for what things soever he doeth these also doeth the on likewise
or the ather loveth the on and sheweth him all things that himself doeth and he will shew him greater works than these that ye may marvel
or as the ather raiseth up the dead and quickeneth them even so the on quickeneth whom he will
or the ather judgeth no man but hath committed all judgment unto the on that all men should honour the on even as they honour the ather
e that honoureth not the on honoureth not the ather which hath sent him
erily verily  say unto you e that heareth my word and believeth on him that sent me hath everlasting life and shall not come into condemnation but is passed from death unto life
erily verily  say unto you he hour is coming and now is when the dead shall hear the voice of the on of od and they that hear shall live
or as the ather hath life in himself so hath he given to the on to have life in himself and hath given him authority to execute judgment also because he is the on of man
arvel not at this for the hour is coming in the which all that are in the graves shall hear his voice and shall come forth they that have done good unto the resurrection of life and they that have done evil unto the resurrection of damnation
 can of mine own self do nothing as  hear  judge and my judgment is just because  seek not mine own will but the will of the ather which hath sent me
f  bear witness of myself my witness is not true
here is another that beareth witness of me and  know that the witness which he witnesseth of me is true
e sent unto ohn and he bare witness unto the truth
ut  receive not testimony from man but these things  say that ye might be saved
e was a burning and a shining light and ye were willing for a season to rejoice in his light
ut  have greater witness than that of ohn for the works which the ather hath given me to finish the same works that  do bear witness of me that the ather hath sent me
nd the ather himself which hath sent me hath borne witness of me
e have neither heard his voice at any time nor seen his shape
nd ye have not his word abiding in you for whom he hath sent him ye believe not
earch the scriptures for in them ye think ye have eternal life and they are they which testify of me
nd ye will not come to me that ye might have life
 receive not honour from men
ut  know you that ye have not the love of od in you
 am come in my athers name and ye receive me not if another shall come in his own name him ye will receive
ow can ye believe which receive honour one of another and seek not the honour that cometh from od only
o not think that  will accuse you to the ather there is one that accuseth you even oses in whom ye trust
or had ye believed oses ye would have believed me for he wrote of me
ut if ye believe not his writings how shall ye believe my words
fter these things esus went over the sea of alilee which is the sea of iberias
nd a great multitude followed him because they saw his miracles which he did on them that were diseased
nd esus went up into a mountain and there he sat with his disciples
nd the passover a feast of the ews was nigh
hen esus then lifted up his eyes and saw a great company come unto him he saith unto hilip hence shall we buy bread that these may eat
nd this he said to prove him for he himself knew what he would do
hilip answered him wo hundred pennyworth of bread is not sufficient for them that every one of them may take a little
ne of his disciples ndrew imon eters brother saith unto him here is a lad here which hath five barley loaves and two small fishes but what are they among so many
nd esus said ake the men sit down
ow there was much grass in the place
o the men sat down in number about five thousand
nd esus took the loaves and when he had given thanks he distributed to the disciples and the disciples to them that were set down and likewise of the fishes as much as they would
hen they were filled he said unto his disciples ather up the fragments that remain that nothing be lost
herefore they gathered them together and filled twelve baskets with the fragments of the five barley loaves which remained over and above unto them that had eaten
hen those men when they had seen the miracle that esus did said his is of a truth that prophet that should come into the world
hen esus therefore perceived that they would come and take him by force to make him a king he departed again into a mountain himself alone
nd when even was now come his disciples went down unto the sea and entered into a ship and went over the sea toward apernaum
nd it was now dark and esus was not come to them
nd the sea arose by reason of a great wind that blew
o when they had rowed about five and twenty or thirty furlongs they see esus walking on the sea and drawing nigh unto the ship and they were afraid
ut he saith unto them t is  be not afraid
hen they willingly received him into the ship and immediately the ship was at the land whither they went
he day following when the people which stood on the other side of the sea saw that there was none other boat there save that one whereinto his disciples were entered and that esus went not with his disciples into the boat but that his disciples were gone away alone howbeit there came other boats from iberias nigh unto the place where they did eat bread after that the ord had given thanks when the people therefore saw that esus was not there neither his disciples they also took shipping and came to apernaum seeking for esus
nd when they had found him on the other side of the sea they said unto him abbi when camest thou hither
esus answered them and said erily verily  say unto you e seek me not because ye saw the miracles but because ye did eat of the loaves and were filled
abour not for the meat which perisheth but for that meat which endureth unto everlasting life which the on of man shall give unto you for him hath od the ather sealed
hen said they unto him hat shall we do that we might work the works of od
esus answered and said unto them his is the work of od that ye believe on him whom he hath sent
hey said therefore unto him hat sign shewest thou then that we may see and believe thee what dost thou work
ur fathers did eat manna in the desert as it is written e gave them bread from heaven to eat
hen esus said unto them erily verily  say unto you oses gave you not that bread from heaven but my ather giveth you the true bread from heaven
or the bread of od is he which cometh down from heaven and giveth life unto the world
hen said they unto him ord evermore give us this bread
nd esus said unto them  am the bread of life he that cometh to me shall never hunger and he that believeth on me shall never thirst
ut  said unto you hat ye also have seen me and believe not
ll that the ather giveth me shall come to me and him that cometh to me  will in no wise cast out
or  came down from heaven not to do mine own will but the will of him that sent me
nd this is the athers will which hath sent me that of all which he hath given me  should lose nothing but should raise it up again at the last day
nd this is the will of him that sent me that every one which seeth the on and believeth on him may have everlasting life and  will raise him up at the last day
he ews then murmured at him because he said  am the bread which came down from heaven
nd they said s not this esus the son of oseph whose father and mother we know how is it then that he saith  came down from heaven
esus therefore answered and said unto them urmur not among yourselves
o man can come to me except the ather which hath sent me draw him and  will raise him up at the last day
t is written in the prophets nd they shall be all taught of od
very man therefore that hath heard and hath learned of the ather cometh unto me
ot that any man hath seen the ather save he which is of od he hath seen the ather
erily verily  say unto you e that believeth on me hath everlasting life
 am that bread of life
our fathers did eat manna in the wilderness and are dead
his is the bread which cometh down from heaven that a man may eat thereof and not die
 am the living bread which came down from heaven if any man eat of this bread he shall live for ever and the bread that  will give is my flesh which  will give for the life of the world
he ews therefore strove among themselves saying ow can this man give us his flesh to eat
hen esus said unto them erily verily  say unto you xcept ye eat the flesh of the on of man and drink his blood ye have no life in you
hoso eateth my flesh and drinketh my blood hath eternal life and  will raise him up at the last day
or my flesh is meat indeed and my blood is drink indeed
e that eateth my flesh and drinketh my blood dwelleth in me and  in him
s the living ather hath sent me and  live by the ather so he that eateth me even he shall live by me
his is that bread which came down from heaven not as your fathers did eat manna and are dead he that eateth of this bread shall live for ever
hese things said he in the synagogue as he taught in apernaum
any therefore of his disciples when they had heard this said his is an hard saying who can hear it
hen esus knew in himself that his disciples murmured at it he said unto them oth this offend you
hat and if ye shall see the on of man ascend up where he was before
t is the spirit that quickeneth the flesh profiteth nothing the words that  speak unto you they are spirit and they are life
ut there are some of you that believe not
or esus knew from the beginning who they were that believed not and who should betray him
nd he said herefore said  unto you that no man can come unto me except it were given unto him of my ather
rom that time many of his disciples went back and walked no more with him
hen said esus unto the twelve ill ye also go away
hen imon eter answered him ord to whom shall we go thou hast the words of eternal life
nd we believe and are sure that thou art that hrist the on of the living od
esus answered them ave not  chosen you twelve and one of you is a devil
e spake of udas scariot the son of imon for he it was that should betray him being one of the twelve
fter these things esus walked in alilee for he would not walk in ewry because the ews sought to kill him
ow the ews feast of tabernacles was at hand
is brethren therefore said unto him epart hence and go into ud a that thy disciples also may see the works that thou doest
or there is no man that doeth any thing in secret and he himself seeketh to be known openly
f thou do these things shew thyself to the world
or neither did his brethren believe in him
hen esus said unto them y time is not yet come but your time is alway ready
he world cannot hate you but me it hateth because  testify of it that the works thereof are evil
o ye up unto this feast  go not up yet unto this feast for my time is not yet full come
hen he had said these words unto them he abode still in alilee
ut when his brethren were gone up then went he also up unto the feast not openly but as it were in secret
hen the ews sought him at the feast and said here is he
nd there was much murmuring among the people concerning him for some said e is a good man others said ay but he deceiveth the people
owbeit no man spake openly of him for fear of the ews
ow about the midst of the feast esus went up into the temple and taught
nd the ews marvelled saying ow knoweth this man letters having never learned
esus answered them and said y doctrine is not mine but his that sent me
f any man will do his will he shall know of the doctrine whether it be of od or whether  speak of myself
e that speaketh of himself seeketh his own glory but he that seeketh his glory that sent him the same is true and no unrighteousness is in him
id not oses give you the law and yet none of you keepeth the law
hy go ye about to kill me
he people answered and said hou hast a devil who goeth about to kill thee
esus answered and said unto them  have done one work and ye all marvel
oses therefore gave unto you circumcision not because it is of oses but of the fathers and ye on the sabbath day circumcise a man
f a man on the sabbath day receive circumcision that the law of oses should not be broken are ye angry at me because  have made a man every whit whole on the sabbath day
udge not according to the appearance but judge righteous judgment
hen said some of them of erusalem s not this he whom they seek to kill
ut lo he speaketh boldly and they say nothing unto him
o the rulers know indeed that this is the very hrist
owbeit we know this man whence he is but when hrist cometh no man knoweth whence he is
hen cried esus in the temple as he taught saying e both know me and ye know whence  am and  am not come of myself but he that sent me is true whom ye know not
ut  know him for  am from him and he hath sent me
hen they sought to take him but no man laid hands on him because his hour was not yet come
nd many of the people believed on him and said hen hrist cometh will he do more miracles than these which this man hath done
he harisees heard that the people murmured such things concerning him and the harisees and the chief priests sent officers to take him
hen said esus unto them et a little while am  with you and then  go unto him that sent me
e shall seek me and shall not find me and where  am thither ye cannot come
hen said the ews among themselves hither will he go that we shall not find him will he go unto the dispersed among the entiles and teach the entiles
hat manner of saying is this that he said e shall seek me and shall not find me and where  am thither ye cannot come
n the last day that great day of the feast esus stood and cried saying f any man thirst let him come unto me and drink
e that believeth on me as the scripture hath said out of his belly shall flow rivers of living water
ut this spake he of the pirit which they that believe on him should receive for the oly host was not yet given because that esus was not yet glorified any of the people therefore when they heard this saying said f a truth this is the rophet
thers said his is the hrist
ut some said hall hrist come out of alilee
ath not the scripture said hat hrist cometh of the seed of avid and out of the town of ethlehem where avid was
o there was a division among the people because of him
nd some of them would have taken him but no man laid hands on him
hen came the officers to the chief priests and harisees and they said unto them hy have ye not brought him
he officers answered ever man spake like this man
hen answered them the harisees re ye also deceived
ave any of the rulers or of the harisees believed on him
ut this people who knoweth not the law are cursed

hey answered and said unto him rt thou also of alilee
earch and look for out of alilee ariseth no prophet
nd every man went unto his own house
esus went unto the mount of lives
nd early in the morning he came again into the temple and all the people came unto him and he sat down and taught them
nd the scribes and harisees brought unto him a woman taken in adultery and when they had set her in the midst they say unto him aster this woman was taken in adultery in the very act
ow oses in the law commanded us that such should be stoned but what sayest thou
his they said tempting him that they might have to accuse him
ut esus stooped down and with his finger wrote on the ground as though he heard them not
o when they continued asking him he lifted up himself and said unto them e that is without sin among you let him first cast a stone at her
nd again he stooped down and wrote on the ground
nd they which heard it being convicted by their own conscience went out one by one beginning at the eldest even unto the last and esus was left alone and the woman standing in the midst
hen esus had lifted up himself and saw none but the woman he said unto her oman where are those thine accusers hath no man condemned thee
he said o man ord
nd esus said unto her either do  condemn thee go and sin no more
hen spake esus again unto them saying  am the light of the world he that followeth me shall not walk in darkness but shall have the light of life
he harisees therefore said unto him hou bearest record of thyself thy record is not true
esus answered and said unto them hough  bear record of myself yet my record is true for  know whence  came and whither  go but ye cannot tell whence  come and whither  go
e judge after the flesh  judge no man
nd yet if  judge my judgment is true for  am not alone but  and the ather that sent me
t is also written in your law that the testimony of two men is true
 am one that bear witness of myself and the ather that sent me beareth witness of me
hen said they unto him here is thy ather
esus answered e neither know me nor my ather if ye had known me ye should have known my ather also
hese words spake esus in the treasury as he taught in the temple and no man laid hands on him for his hour was not yet come
hen said esus again unto them  go my way and ye shall seek me and shall die in your sins whither  go ye cannot come
hen said the ews ill he kill himself because he saith hither  go ye cannot come
nd he said unto them e are from beneath  am from above ye are of this world  am not of this world
 said therefore unto you that ye shall die in your sins for if ye believe not that  am he ye shall die in your sins
hen said they unto him ho art thou
nd esus saith unto them ven the same that  said unto you from the beginning
 have many things to say and to judge of you but he that sent me is true and  speak to the world those things which  have heard of him
hey understood not that he spake to them of the ather
hen said esus unto them hen ye have lifted up the on of man then shall ye know that  am he and that  do nothing of myself but as my ather hath taught me  speak these things
nd he that sent me is with me the ather hath not left me alone for  do always those things that please him
s he spake these words many believed on him
hen said esus to those ews which believed on him f ye continue in my word then are ye my disciples indeed and ye shall know the truth and the truth shall make you free
hey answered him e be brahams seed and were never in bondage to any man how sayest thou e shall be made free
esus answered them erily verily  say unto you hosoever committeth sin is the servant of sin
nd the servant abideth not in the house for ever but the on abideth ever
f the on therefore shall make you free ye shall be free indeed
 know that ye are brahams seed but ye seek to kill me because my word hath no place in you
 speak that which  have seen with my ather and ye do that which ye have seen with your father
hey answered and said unto him braham is our father
esus saith unto them f ye were brahams children ye would do the works of braham
ut now ye seek to kill me a man that hath told you the truth which  have heard of od this did not braham
e do the deeds of your father
hen said they to him e be not born of fornication we have one ather even od
esus said unto them f od were your ather ye would love me for  proceeded forth and came from od neither came  of myself but he sent me
hy do ye not understand my speech even because ye cannot hear my word
e are of your father the devil and the lusts of your father ye will do
e was a murderer from the beginning and abode not in the truth because there is no truth in him
hen he speaketh a lie he speaketh of his own for he is a liar and the father of it
nd because  tell you the truth ye believe me not
hich of you convinceth me of sin
nd if  say the truth why do ye not believe me
e that is of od heareth ods words ye therefore hear them not because ye are not of od
hen answered the ews and said unto him ay we not well that thou art a amaritan and hast a devil
esus answered  have not a devil but  honour my ather and ye do dishonour me
nd  seek not mine own glory there is one that seeketh and judgeth
erily verily  say unto you f a man keep my saying he shall never see death
hen said the ews unto him ow we know that thou hast a devil
braham is dead and the prophets and thou sayest f a man keep my saying he shall never taste of death
rt thou greater than our father braham which is dead and the prophets are dead whom makest thou thyself
esus answered f  honour myself my honour is nothing it is my ather that honoureth me of whom ye say that he is your od yet ye have not known him but  know him and if  should say  know him not  shall be a liar like unto you but  know him and keep his saying
our father braham rejoiced to see my day and he saw it and was glad
hen said the ews unto him hou art not yet fifty years old and hast thou seen braham
esus said unto them erily verily  say unto you efore braham was  am
hen took they up stones to cast at him but esus hid himself and went out of the temple going through the midst of them and so passed by
nd as esus passed by he saw a man which was blind from his birth
nd his disciples asked him saying aster who did sin this man or his parents that he was born blind
esus answered either hath this man sinned nor his parents but that the works of od should be made manifest in him
 must work the works of him that sent me while it is day the night cometh when no man can work
s long as  am in the world  am the light of the world
hen he had thus spoken he spat on the ground and made clay of the spittle and he anointed the eyes of the blind man with the clay and said unto him o wash in the pool of iloam which is by interpretation ent e went his way therefore and washed and came seeing
he neighbours therefore and they which before had seen him that he was blind said s not this he that sat and begged
ome said his is he others said e is like him but he said  am he
herefore said they unto him ow were thine eyes opened
e answered and said  man that is called esus made clay and anointed mine eyes and said unto me o to the pool of iloam and wash and  went and washed and  received sight
hen said they unto him here is he
e said  know not
hey brought to the harisees him that aforetime was blind
nd it was the sabbath day when esus made the clay and opened his eyes
hen again the harisees also asked him how he had received his sight
e said unto them e put clay upon mine eyes and  washed and do see
herefore said some of the harisees his man is not of od because he keepeth not the sabbath day
thers said ow can a man that is a sinner do such miracles
nd there was a division among them
hey say unto the blind man again hat sayest thou of him that he hath opened thine eyes
e said e is a prophet
ut the ews did not believe concerning him that he had been blind and received his sight until they called the parents of him that had received his sight
nd they asked them saying s this your son who ye say was born blind how then doth he now see
is parents answered them and said e know that this is our son and that he was born blind but by what means he now seeth we know not or who hath opened his eyes we know not he is of age ask him he shall speak for himself
hese words spake his parents because they feared the ews for the ews had agreed already that if any man did confess that he was hrist he should be put out of the synagogue
herefore said his parents e is of age ask him
hen again called they the man that was blind and said unto him ive od the praise we know that this man is a sinner
e answered and said hether he be a sinner or no  know not one thing  know that whereas  was blind now  see
hen said they to him again hat did he to thee how opened he thine eyes
e answered them  have told you already and ye did not hear wherefore would ye hear it again will ye also be his disciples
hen they reviled him and said hou art his disciple but we are oses disciples
e know that od spake unto oses as for this fellow we know not from whence he is
he man answered and said unto them hy herein is a marvellous thing that ye know not from whence he is and yet he hath opened mine eyes
ow we know that od heareth not sinners but if any man be a worshipper of od and doeth his will him he heareth
ince the world began was it not heard that any man opened the eyes of one that was born blind
f this man were not of od he could do nothing
hey answered and said unto him hou wast altogether born in sins and dost thou teach us
nd they cast him out
esus heard that they had cast him out and when he had found him he said unto him ost thou believe on the on of od
e answered and said ho is he ord that  might believe on him
nd esus said unto him hou hast both seen him and it is he that talketh with thee
nd he said ord  believe
nd he worshipped him
nd esus said or judgment  am come into this world that they which see not might see and that they which see might be made blind
nd some of the harisees which were with him heard these words and said unto him re we blind also
esus said unto them f ye were blind ye should have no sin but now ye say e see therefore your sin remaineth
erily verily  say unto you e that entereth not by the door into the sheepfold but climbeth up some other way the same is a thief and a robber
ut he that entereth in by the door is the shepherd of the sheep
o him the porter openeth and the sheep hear his voice and he calleth his own sheep by name and leadeth them out
nd when he putteth forth his own sheep he goeth before them and the sheep follow him for they know his voice
nd a stranger will they not follow but will flee from him for they know not the voice of strangers
his parable spake esus unto them but they understood not what things they were which he spake unto them
hen said esus unto them again erily verily  say unto you  am the door of the sheep
ll that ever came before me are thieves and robbers but the sheep did not hear them
 am the door by me if any man enter in he shall be saved and shall go in and out and find pasture
he thief cometh not but for to steal and to kill and to destroy  am come that they might have life and that they might have it more abundantly
 am the good shepherd the good shepherd giveth his life for the sheep
ut he that is an hireling and not the shepherd whose own the sheep are not seeth the wolf coming and leaveth the sheep and fleeth and the wolf catcheth them and scattereth the sheep
he hireling fleeth because he is an hireling and careth not for the sheep
 am the good shepherd and know my sheep and am known of mine
s the ather knoweth me even so know  the ather and  lay down my life for the sheep
nd other sheep  have which are not of this fold them also  must bring and they shall hear my voice and there shall be one fold and one shepherd
herefore doth my ather love me because  lay down my life that  might take it again
o man taketh it from me but  lay it down of myself
 have power to lay it down and  have power to take it again
his commandment have  received of my ather
here was a division therefore again among the ews for these sayings
nd many of them said e hath a devil and is mad why hear ye him
thers said hese are not the words of him that hath a devil
an a devil open the eyes of the blind
nd it was at erusalem the feast of the dedication and it was winter
nd esus walked in the temple in olomons porch
hen came the ews round about him and said unto him ow long dost thou make us to doubt
f thou be the hrist tell us plainly
esus answered them  told you and ye believed not the works that  do in my athers name they bear witness of me
ut ye believe not because ye are not of my sheep as  said unto you
y sheep hear my voice and  know them and they follow me and  give unto them eternal life and they shall never perish neither shall any man pluck them out of my hand
y ather which gave them me is greater than all and no man is able to pluck them out of my athers hand
 and my ather are one
hen the ews took up stones again to stone him
esus answered them any good works have  shewed you from my ather for which of those works do ye stone me
he ews answered him saying or a good work we stone thee not but for blasphemy and because that thou being a man makest thyself od
esus answered them s it not written in your law  said e are gods
f he called them gods unto whom the word of od came and the scripture cannot be broken say ye of him whom the ather hath sanctified and sent into the world hou blasphemest because  said  am the on of od
f  do not the works of my ather believe me not
ut if  do though ye believe not me believe the works that ye may know and believe that the ather is in me and  in him
herefore they sought again to take him but he escaped out of their hand and went away again beyond ordan into the place where ohn at first baptized and there he abode
nd many resorted unto him and said ohn did no miracle but all things that ohn spake of this man were true
nd many believed on him there
ow a certain man was sick named azarus of ethany the town of ary and her sister artha
t was that ary which anointed the ord with ointment and wiped his feet with her hair whose brother azarus was sick herefore his sisters sent unto him saying ord behold he whom thou lovest is sick
hen esus heard that he said his sickness is not unto death but for the glory of od that the on of od might be glorified thereby
ow esus loved artha and her sister and azarus
hen he had heard therefore that he was sick he abode two days still in the same place where he was
hen after that saith he to his disciples et us go into ud a again
is disciples say unto him aster the ews of late sought to stone thee and goest thou thither again
esus answered re there not twelve hours in the day
f any man walk in the day he stumbleth not because he seeth the light of this world
ut if a man walk in the night he stumbleth because there is no light in him
hese things said he and after that he saith unto them ur friend azarus sleepeth but  go that  may awake him out of sleep
hen said his disciples ord if he sleep he shall do well
owbeit esus spake of his death but they thought that he had spoken of taking of rest in sleep
hen said esus unto them plainly azarus is dead
nd  am glad for your sakes that  was not there to the intent ye may believe nevertheless let us go unto him
hen said homas which is called idymus unto his fellowdisciples et us also go that we may die with him
hen when esus came he found that he had lain in the grave four days already
ow ethany was nigh unto erusalem about fifteen furlongs off and many of the ews came to artha and ary to comfort them concerning their brother
hen artha as soon as she heard that esus was coming went and met him but ary sat still in the house
hen said artha unto esus ord if thou hadst been here my brother had not died
ut  know that even now whatsoever thou wilt ask of od od will give it thee
esus saith unto her hy brother shall rise again
artha saith unto him  know that he shall rise again in the resurrection at the last day
esus said unto her  am the resurrection and the life he that believeth in me though he were dead yet shall he live and whosoever liveth and believeth in me shall never die
elievest thou this
he saith unto him ea ord  believe that thou art the hrist the on of od which should come into the world
nd when she had so said she went her way and called ary her sister secretly saying he aster is come and calleth for thee
s soon as she heard that she arose quickly and came unto him
ow esus was not yet come into the town but was in that place where artha met him
he ews then which were with her in the house and comforted her when they saw ary that she rose up hastily and went out followed her saying he goeth unto the grave to weep there
hen when ary was come where esus was and saw him she fell down at his feet saying unto him ord if thou hadst been here my brother had not died
hen esus therefore saw her weeping and the ews also weeping which came with her he groaned in the spirit and was troubled and said here have ye laid him
hey said unto him ord come and see
esus wept
hen said the ews ehold how he loved him
nd some of them said ould not this man which opened the eyes of the blind have caused that even this man should not have died
esus therefore again groaning in himself cometh to the grave
t was a cave and a stone lay upon it
esus said ake ye away the stone
artha the sister of him that was dead saith unto him ord by this time he stinketh for he hath been dead four days
esus saith unto her aid  not unto thee that if thou wouldest believe thou shouldest see the glory of od
hen they took away the stone from the place where the dead was laid
nd esus lifted up his eyes and said ather  thank thee that thou hast heard me
nd  knew that thou hearest me always but because of the people which stand by  said it that they may believe that thou hast sent me
nd when he thus had spoken he cried with a loud voice azarus come forth
nd he that was dead came forth bound hand and foot with graveclothes and his face was bound about with a napkin
esus saith unto them oose him and let him go
hen many of the ews which came to ary and had seen the things which esus did believed on him
ut some of them went their ways to the harisees and told them what things esus had done
hen gathered the chief priests and the harisees a council and said hat do we for this man doeth many miracles
f we let him thus alone all men will believe on him and the omans shall come and take away both our place and nation
nd one of them named aiaphas being the high priest that same year said unto them e know nothing at all nor consider that it is expedient for us that one man should die for the people and that the whole nation perish not
nd this spake he not of himself but being high priest that year he prophesied that esus should die for that nation and not for that nation only but that also he should gather together in one the children of od that were scattered abroad
hen from that day forth they took counsel together for to put him to death
esus therefore walked no more openly among the ews but went thence unto a country near to the wilderness into a city called phraim and there continued with his disciples
nd the ews passover was nigh at hand and many went out of the country up to erusalem before the passover to purify themselves
hen sought they for esus and spake among themselves as they stood in the temple hat think ye that he will not come to the feast
ow both the chief priests and the harisees had given a commandment that if any man knew where he were he should shew it that they might take him
hen esus six days before the passover came to ethany where azarus was which had been dead whom he raised from the dead
here they made him a supper and artha served but azarus was one of them that sat at the table with him
hen took ary a pound of ointment of spikenard very costly and anointed the feet of esus and wiped his feet with her hair and the house was filled with the odour of the ointment
hen saith one of his disciples udas scariot imons son which should betray him hy was not this ointment sold for three hundred pence and given to the poor
his he said not that he cared for the poor but because he was a thief and had the bag and bare what was put therein
hen said esus et her alone against the day of my burying hath she kept this
or the poor always ye have with you but me ye have not always
uch people of the ews therefore knew that he was there and they came not for esus sake only but that they might see azarus also whom he had raised from the dead
ut the chief priests consulted that they might put azarus also to death because that by reason of him many of the ews went away and believed on esus
n the next day much people that were come to the feast when they heard that esus was coming to erusalem took branches of palm trees and went forth to meet him and cried osanna lessed is the ing of srael that cometh in the name of the ord
nd esus when he had found a young ass sat thereon as it is written ear not daughter of ion behold thy ing cometh sitting on an asss colt
hese things understood not his disciples at the first but when esus was glorified then remembered they that these things were written of him and that they had done these things unto him
he people therefore that was with him when he called azarus out of his grave and raised him from the dead bare record
or this cause the people also met him for that they heard that he had done this miracle
he harisees therefore said among themselves erceive ye how ye prevail nothing behold the world is gone after him
nd there were certain reeks among them that came up to worship at the feast the same came therefore to hilip which was of ethsaida of alilee and desired him saying ir we would see esus
hilip cometh and telleth ndrew and again ndrew and hilip tell esus

erily verily  say unto you xcept a corn of wheat fall into the ground and die it abideth alone but if it die it bringeth forth much fruit
e that loveth his life shall lose it and he that hateth his life in this world shall keep it unto life eternal
f any man serve me let him follow me and where  am there shall also my servant be if any man serve me him will my ather honour

ather glorify thy name
hen came there a voice from heaven saying  have both glorified it and will glorify it again

ow is the judgment of this world now shall the prince of this world be cast out

his he said signifying what death he should die

alk while ye have the light lest darkness come upon you for he that walketh in darkness knoweth not whither he goeth

ut though he had done so many miracles before them yet they believed not on him hat the saying of saias the prophet might be fulfilled which he spake ord who hath believed our report

herefore they could not believe because that saias said again e hath blinded their eyes and hardened their heart hat they should not see with their eyes nor understand with their heart nd be converted nd  should heal them
hese things said saias when he saw his glory and spake of him
evertheless among the chief rulers also many believed on him but because of the harisees they did not confess him lest they should be put out of the synagogue for they loved the praise of men more than the praise of od
esus cried and said e that believeth on me believeth not on me but on him that sent me
nd he that seeth me seeth him that sent me

or  have not spoken of myself but the ather which sent me he gave me a commandment what  should say and what  should speak

e came therefore and took the body of esus
nd there came also icodemus which at the first came to esus by night and brought a mixture of myrrh and aloes about an hundred pound weight
hen took they the body of esus and wound it in linen clothes with the spices as the manner of the ews is to bury
ow in the place where he was crucified there was a garden and in the garden a new sepulchre wherein was never man yet laid
here laid they esus therefore because of the ews preparation day for the sepulchre was nigh at hand
he first day of the week cometh ary agdalene early when it was yet dark unto the sepulchre and seeth the stone taken away from the sepulchre
hen she runneth and cometh to imon eter and to the other disciple whom esus loved and saith unto them hey have taken away the ord out of the sepulchre and we know not where they have laid him
eter therefore went forth and that other disciple and came to the sepulchre
o they ran both together and the other disciple did outrun eter and came first to the sepulchre
nd he stooping down and looking in saw the linen clothes lying yet went he not in
hen cometh imon eter following him and went into the sepulchre and seeth the linen clothes lie and the napkin that was about his head not lying with the linen clothes but wrapped together in a place by itself
hen went in also that other disciple which came first to the sepulchre and he saw and believed
or as yet they knew not the scripture that he must rise again from the dead
hen the disciples went away again unto their own home
ut ary stood without at the sepulchre weeping and as she wept she stooped down and looked into the sepulchre and seeth two angels in white sitting the one at the head and the other at the feet where the body of esus had lain
nd they say unto her oman why weepest thou
he saith unto them ecause they have taken away my ord and  know not where they have laid him
nd when she had thus said she turned herself back and saw esus standing and knew not that it was esus
esus saith unto her oman why weepest thou whom seekest thou
he supposing him to be the gardener saith unto him ir if thou have borne him hence tell me where thou hast laid him and  will take him away
esus saith unto her ary
he turned herself and saith unto him abboni which is to say aster
esus saith unto her ouch me not for  am not yet ascended to my ather but go to my brethren and say unto them  ascend unto my ather and your ather and to my od and your od
ary agdalene came and told the disciples that she had seen the ord and that he had spoken these things unto her
hen the same day at evening being the first day of the week when the doors were shut where the disciples were assembled for fear of the ews came esus and stood in the midst and saith unto them eace be unto you
nd when he had so said he shewed unto them his hands and his side
hen were the disciples glad when they saw the ord
hen said esus to them again eace be unto you as my ather hath sent me even so send  you
nd when he had said this he breathed on them and saith unto them eceive ye the oly host whose soever sins ye remit they are remitted unto them and whose soever sins ye retain they are retained
ut homas one of the twelve called idymus was not with them when esus came
he other disciples therefore said unto him e have seen the ord
ut he said unto them xcept  shall see in his hands the print of the nails and put my finger into the print of the nails and thrust my hand into his side  will not believe
nd after eight days again his disciples were within and homas with them then came esus the doors being shut and stood in the midst and said eace be unto you
hen saith he to homas each hither thy finger and behold my hands and reach hither thy hand and thrust it into my side and be not faithless but believing
nd homas answered and said unto him y ord and my od
esus saith unto him homas because thou hast seen me thou hast believed blessed are they that have not seen and yet have believed
nd many other signs truly did esus in the presence of his disciples which are not written in this book but these are written that ye might believe that esus is the hrist the on of od and that believing ye might have life through his name
fter these things esus shewed himself again to the disciples at the sea of iberias and on this wise shewed he himself
here were together imon eter and homas called idymus and athanael of ana in alilee and the sons of ebedee and two other of his disciples
imon eter saith unto them  go a fishing
hey say unto him e also go with thee
hey went forth and entered into a ship immediately and that night they caught nothing
ut when the morning was now come esus stood on the shore but the disciples knew not that it was esus
hen esus saith unto them hildren have ye any meat
hey answered him o
nd he said unto them ast the net on the right side of the ship and ye shall find
hey cast therefore and now they were not able to draw it for the multitude of fishes
herefore that disciple whom esus loved saith unto eter t is the ord
ow when imon eter heard that it was the ord he girt his fishers coat unto him for he was naked  and did cast himself into the sea
nd the other disciples came in a little ship for they were not far from land but as it were two hundred cubits  dragging the net with fishes
s soon then as they were come to land they saw a fire of coals there and fish laid thereon and bread
esus saith unto them ring of the fish which ye have now caught
imon eter went up and drew the net to land full of great fishes an hundred and fifty and three and for all there were so many yet was not the net broken
esus saith unto them ome and dine
nd none of the disciples durst ask him ho art thou knowing that it was the ord
esus then cometh and taketh bread and giveth them and fish likewise
his is now the third time that esus shewed himself to his disciples after that he was risen from the dead
o when they had dined esus saith to imon eter imon son of onas lovest thou me more than these
e saith unto him ea ord thou knowest that  love thee
e saith unto him eed my lambs
e saith to him again the second time imon son of onas lovest thou me
e saith unto him ea ord thou knowest that  love thee
e saith unto him eed my sheep
e saith unto him the third time imon son of onas lovest thou me
eter was grieved because he said unto him the third time ovest thou me
nd he said unto him ord thou knowest all things thou knowest that  love thee
esus saith unto him eed my sheep
erily verily  say unto thee hen thou wast young thou girdedst thyself and walkedst whither thou wouldest but when thou shalt be old thou shalt stretch forth thy hands and another shall gird thee and carry thee whither thou wouldest not
his spake he signifying by what death he should glorify od
nd when he had spoken this he saith unto him ollow me
hen eter turning about seeth the disciple whom esus loved following which also leaned on his breast at supper and said ord which is he that betrayeth thee
eter seeing him saith to esus ord and what shall this man do
esus saith unto him f  will that he tarry till  come what is that to thee follow thou me
hen went this saying abroad among the brethren that that disciple should not die yet esus said not unto him e shall not die but f  will that he tarry till  come what is that to thee
his is the disciple which testifieth of these things and wrote these things and we know that his testimony is true
nd there are also many other things which esus did the which if they should be written every one  suppose that even the world itself could not contain the books that should be written
men
he former treatise have  made  heophilus of all that esus began both to do and teach until the day in which he was taken up after that he through the oly host had given commandments unto the apostles whom he had chosen to whom also he shewed himself alive after his passion by many infallible proofs being seen of them forty days and speaking of the things pertaining to the kingdom of od and being assembled together with them commanded them that they should not depart from erusalem but wait for the promise of the ather which saith he ye have heard of me
or ohn truly baptized with water but ye shall be baptized with the oly host not many days hence
hen they therefore were come together they asked of him saying ord wilt thou at this time restore again the kingdom to srael
nd he said unto them t is not for you to know the times or the seasons which the ather hath put in his own power
ut ye shall receive power after that the oly host is come upon you and ye shall be witnesses unto me both in erusalem and in all ud a and in amaria and unto the uttermost part of the earth
nd when he had spoken these things while they beheld he was taken up and a cloud received him out of their sight
nd while they looked stedfastly toward heaven as he went up behold two men stood by them in white apparel which also said e men of alilee why stand ye gazing up into heaven this same esus which is taken up from you into heaven shall so come in like manner as ye have seen him go into heaven
hen returned they unto erusalem from the mount called livet which is from erusalem a sabbath days journey
nd when they were come in they went up into an upper room where abode both eter and ames and ohn and ndrew hilip and homas artholomew and atthew ames the son of lph us and imon elotes and udas the brother of ames
hese all continued with one accord in prayer and supplication with the women and ary the mother of esus and with his brethren
nd in those days eter stood up in the midst of the disciples and said the number of names together were about an hundred and twenty  en and brethren this scripture must needs have been fulfilled which the oly host by the mouth of avid spake before concerning udas which was guide to them that took esus
or he was numbered with us and had obtained part of this ministry
ow this man purchased a field with the reward of iniquity and falling headlong he burst asunder in the midst and all his bowels gushed out
nd it was known unto all the dwellers at erusalem insomuch as that field is called in their proper tongue celdama that is to say he field of blood
or it is written in the book of salms et his habitation be desolate nd let no man dwell therein andis bishoprick let another take
herefore of these men which have companied with us all the time that the ord esus went in and out among us beginning from the baptism of ohn unto that same day that he was taken up from us must one be ordained to be a witness with us of his resurrection
nd they appointed two oseph called arsabas who was surnamed ustus and atthias
nd they prayed and said hou ord which knowest the hearts of all men shew whether of these two thou hast chosen that he may take part of this ministry and apostleship from which udas by transgression fell that he might go to his own place
nd they gave forth their lots and the lot fell upon atthias and he was numbered with the eleven apostles
nd when the day of entecost was fully come they were all with one accord in one place
nd suddenly there came a sound from heaven as of a rushing mighty wind and it filled all the house where they were sitting
nd there appeared unto them cloven tongues like as of fire and it sat upon each of them
nd they were all filled with the oly host and began to speak with other tongues as the pirit gave them utterance
nd there were dwelling at erusalem ews devout men out of every nation under heaven
ow when this was noised abroad the multitude came together and were confounded because that every man heard them speak in his own language
nd they were all amazed and marvelled saying one to another ehold are not all these which speak alil ans
nd how hear we every man in our own tongue wherein we were born
arthians and edes and lamites and the dwellers in esopotamia and in ud a and appadocia in ontus and sia hrygia and amphylia in gypt and in the parts of ibya about yrene and strangers of ome ews and proselytes retes and rabians we do hear them speak in our tongues the wonderful works of od
nd they were all amazed and were in doubt saying one to another hat meaneth this
thers mocking said hese men are full of new wine
ut eter standing up with the eleven lifted up his voice and said unto them e men of ud a and all ye that dwell at erusalem be this known unto you and hearken to my words or these are not drunken as ye suppose seeing it is but the third hour of the day
ut this is that which was spoken by the prophet oel nd it shall come to pass in the last days saith od  will pour out of my pirit upon all flesh nd your sons and your daughters shall prophesy nd your young men shall see visions nd your old men shall dream dreams nd on my servants and on my handmaidens will pour out in those days of my pirit and they shall prophesy nd  will shew wonders in heaven above nd signs in the earth beneath lood and fire and vapour of smoke he sun shall be turned into darkness nd the moon into blood efore that great and notableay of the ord come nd it shall come to pass that whosoever shall call on the name of the ord shall be saved
e men of srael hear these words esus of azareth a man approved of od among you by miracles and wonders and signs which od did by him in the midst of you as ye yourselves also know him being delivered by the determinate counsel and foreknowledge of od ye have taken and by wicked hands have crucified and slain whom od hath raised up having loosed the pains of death because it was not possible that he should be holden of it
or avid speaketh concerning him  foresaw the ord always before my face or he is on my right hand that  should not be moved herefore did my heart rejoice and my tongue was glad oreover also my flesh shall rest in hope ecause thou wilt not leave my soul in hell either wilt thou suffer thine oly ne to see corruption
hou hast made known to me the ways of life hou shalt make me full of joy with thy countenance
en and brethren let me freely speak unto you of the patriarch avid that he is both dead and buried and his sepulchre is with us unto this day
herefore being a prophet and knowing that od had sworn with an oath to him that of the fruit of his loins according to the flesh he would raise up hrist to sit on his throne he seeing this before spake of the resurrection of hrist that his soul was not left in hell neither his flesh did see corruption
his esus hath od raised up whereof we all are witnesses
herefore being by the right hand of od exalted and having received of the ather the promise of the oly host he hath shed forth this which ye now see and hear
or avid is not ascended into the heavens but he saith himself he  said unto my ord it thou on my right hand ntil  make thy foes thy footstool
herefore let all the house of srael know assuredly that od hath made that same esus whom ye have crucified both ord and hrist
ow when they heard this they were pricked in their heart and said unto eter and to the rest of the apostles en and brethren what shall we do
hen eter said unto them epent and be baptized every one of you in the name of esus hrist for the remission of sins and ye shall receive the gift of the oly host
or the promise is unto you and to your children and to all that are afar off even as many as the ord our od shall call
nd with many other words did he testify and exhort saying ave yourselves from this untoward generation
hen they that gladly received his word were baptized and the same day there were added unto them about three thousand souls
nd they continued stedfastly in the apostles doctrine and fellowship and in breaking of bread and in prayers
nd fear came upon every soul and many wonders and signs were done by the apostles
nd all that believed were together and had all things common and sold their possessions and goods and parted them to all men as every man had need
nd they continuing daily with one accord in the temple and breaking bread from house to house did eat their meat with gladness and singleness of heart praising od and having favour with all the people
nd the ord added to the church daily such as should be saved
ow eter and ohn went up together into the temple at the hour of prayer being the ninth hour
nd a certain man lame from his mothers womb was carried whom they laid daily at the gate of the temple which is called eautiful to ask alms of them that entered into the temple who seeing eter and ohn about to go into the temple asked an alms
nd eter fastening his eyes upon him with ohn said ook on us
nd he gave heed unto them expecting to receive something of them
hen eter said ilver and gold have  none but such as  have give  thee n the name of esus hrist of azareth rise up and walk
nd he took him by the right hand and lifted him up and immediately his feet and ankle bones received strength
nd he leaping up stood and walked and entered with them into the temple walking and leaping and praising od
nd all the people saw him walking and praising od and they knew that it was he which sat for alms at the eautiful gate of the temple and they were filled with wonder and amazement at that which had happened unto him
nd as the lame man which was healed held eter and ohn all the people ran together unto them in the porch that is called olomons greatly wondering
nd when eter saw it he answered unto the people e men of srael why marvel ye at this or why look ye so earnestly on us as though by our own power or holiness we had made this man to walk
he od of braham and of saac and of acob the od of our fathers hath glorified his on esus whom ye delivered up and denied him in the presence of ilate when he was determined to let him go
ut ye denied the oly ne and the ust and desired a murderer to be granted unto you and killed the rince of life whom od hath raised from the dead whereof we are witnesses
nd his name through faith in his name hath made this man strong whom ye see and know yea the faith which is by him hath given him this perfect soundness in the presence of you all
nd now brethren  wot that through ignorance ye did it as did also your rulers
ut those things which od before had shewed by the mouth of all his prophets that hrist should suffer he hath so fulfilled
epent ye therefore and be converted that your sins may be blotted out when the times of refreshing shall come from the presence of the ord and he shall send esus hrist which before was preached unto you whom the heaven must receive until the times of restitution of all things which od hath spoken by the mouth of all his holy prophets since the world began
or oses truly said unto the fathers  prophet shall the ord your od raise up unto you of your brethren like unto me him shall ye hear in all things whatsoever he shall say unto you
nd it shall come to pass that every soul which will not hear that prophet shall be destroyed from among the people
ea and all the prophets from amuel and those that follow after as many as have spoken have likewise foretold of these days
e are the children of the prophets and of the covenant which od made with our fathers saying unto braham nd in thy seed shall all the kindreds of the earth be blessed
nto you first od having raised up his on esus sent him to bless you in turning away every one of you from his iniquities
nd as they spake unto the people the priests and the captain of the temple and the adducees came upon them being grieved that they taught the people and preached through esus the resurrection from the dead
nd they laid hands on them and put them in hold unto the next day for it was now eventide
owbeit many of them which heard the word believed and the number of the men was about five thousand
nd it came to pass on the morrow that their rulers and elders and scribes and nnas the high priest and aiaphas and ohn and lexander and as many as were of the kindred of the high priest were gathered together at erusalem
nd when they had set them in the midst they asked y what power or by what name have ye done this
hen eter filled with the oly host said unto them e rulers of the people and elders of srael if we this day be examined of the good deed done to the impotent man by what means he is made whole be it known unto you all and to all the people of srael that by the name of esus hrist of azareth whom ye crucified whom od raised from the dead even by him doth this man stand here before you whole
his is the stone which was set at nought of you builders which is become the head of the corner
either is there salvation in any other for there is none other name under heaven given among men whereby we must be saved
ow when they saw the boldness of eter and ohn and perceived that they were unlearned and ignorant men they marvelled and they took knowledge of them that they had been with esus
nd beholding the man which was healed standing with them they could say nothing against it
ut when they had commanded them to go aside out of the council they conferred among themselves saying hat shall we do to these men for that indeed a notable miracle hath been done by them is manifest to all them that dwell in erusalem and we cannot deny it
ut that it spread no further among the people let us straitly threaten them that they speak henceforth to no man in this name
nd they called them and commanded them not to speak at all nor teach in the name of esus
ut eter and ohn answered and said unto them hether it be right in the sight of od to hearken unto you more than unto od judge ye
or we cannot but speak the things which we have seen and heard
o when they had further threatened them they let them go finding nothing how they might punish them because of the people for all men glorified od for that which was done
or the man was above forty years old on whom this miracle of healing was shewed
nd being let go they went to their own company and reported all that the chief priests and elders had said unto them
nd when they heard that they lifted up their voice to od with one accord and said ord thou art od which hast made heaven and earth and the sea and all that in them is ho by the mouth of thy servant avid hast said hy did the heathen rage nd the people imagine vain things
he kings of the earth stood up nd the rulers were gathered togethergainst the ord and against his hrist
or of a truth against thy holy child esus whom thou hast anointed both erod and ontius ilate with the entiles and the people of srael were gathered together for to do whatsoever thy hand and thy counsel determined before to be done
nd now ord behold their threatenings and grant unto thy servants that with all boldness they may speak thy word by stretching forth thine hand to heal and that signs and wonders may be done by the name of thy holy child esus
nd when they had prayed the place was shaken where they were assembled together and they were all filled with the oly host and they spake the word of od with boldness
nd the multitude of them that believed were of one heart and of one soul neither said any of them that ought of the things which he possessed was his own but they had all things common
nd with great power gave the apostles witness of the resurrection of the ord esus and great grace was upon them all
either was there any among them that lacked for as many as were possessors of lands or houses sold them and brought the prices of the things that were sold and laid them down at the apostles feet and distribution was made unto every man according as he had need
nd oses who by the apostles was surnamed arnabas which is being interpreted he son of consolation  a evite and of the country of yprus having land sold it and brought the money and laid it at the apostles feet
ut a certain man named nanias with apphira his wife sold a possession and kept back part of the price his wife also being privy to it and brought a certain part and laid it at the apostles feet
ut eter said nanias why hath atan filled thine heart to lie to the oly host and to keep back part of the price of the land
hiles it remained was it not thine own and after it was sold was it not in thine own power why hast thou conceived this thing in thine heart thou hast not lied unto men but unto od
nd nanias hearing these words fell down and gave up the ghost and great fear came on all them that heard these things
nd the young men arose wound him up and carried him out and buried him
nd it was about the space of three hours after when his wife not knowing what was done came in
nd eter answered unto her ell me whether ye sold the land for so much
nd she said ea for so much
hen eter said unto her ow is it that ye have agreed together to tempt the pirit of the ord behold the feet of them which have buried thy husband are at the door and shall carry thee out
hen fell she down straightway at his feet and yielded up the ghost and the young men came in and found her dead and carrying her forth buried her by her husband
nd great fear came upon all the church and upon as many as heard these things
nd by the hands of the apostles were many signs and wonders wrought among the people and they were all with one accord in olomons porch
nd of the rest durst no man join himself to them but the people magnified them
nd believers were the more added to the ord multitudes both of men and women nsomuch that they brought forth the sick into the streets and laid them on beds and couches that at the least the shadow of eter passing by might overshadow some of them
here came also a multitude out of the cities round about unto erusalem bringing sick folks and them which were vexed with unclean spirits and they were healed every one
hen the high priest rose up and all they that were with him which is the sect of the adducees  and were filled with indignation and laid their hands on the apostles and put them in the common prison
ut the angel of the ord by night opened the prison doors and brought them forth and said o stand and speak in the temple to the people all the words of this life
nd when they heard that they entered into the temple early in the morning and taught
ut the high priest came and they that were with him and called the council together and all the senate of the children of srael and sent to the prison to have them brought
ut when the officers came and found them not in the prison they returned and told saying he prison truly found we shut with all safety and the keepers standing without before the doors but when we had opened we found no man within
ow when the high priest and the captain of the temple and the chief priests heard these things they doubted of them whereunto this would grow
hen came one and told them saying ehold the men whom ye put in prison are standing in the temple and teaching the people
hen went the captain with the officers and brought them without violence for they feared the people lest they should have been stoned
nd when they had brought them they set them before the council and the high priest asked them saying id not we straitly command you that ye should not teach in this name and behold ye have filled erusalem with your doctrine and intend to bring this mans blood upon us
hen eter and the other apostles answered and said e ought to obey od rather than men
he od of our fathers raised up esus whom ye slew and hanged on a tree
im hath od exalted with his right hand to be a rince and a aviour for to give repentance to srael and forgiveness of sins
nd we are his witnesses of these things and so is also the oly host whom od hath given to them that obey him
hen they heard that they were cut to the heart and took counsel to slay them
hen stood there up one in the council a harisee named amaliel a doctor of the law had in reputation among all the people and commanded to put the apostles forth a little space and said unto them e men of srael take heed to yourselves what ye intend to do as touching these men
or before these days rose up heudas boasting himself to be somebody to whom a number of men about four hundred joined themselves who was slain and all as many as obeyed him were scattered and brought to nought
fter this man rose up udas of alilee in the days of the taxing and drew away much people after him he also perished and all even as many as obeyed him were dispersed
nd now  say unto you efrain from these men and let them alone for if this counsel or this work be of men it will come to nought but if it be of od ye cannot overthrow it lest haply ye be found even to fight against od
nd to him they agreed and when they had called the apostles and beaten them they commanded that they should not speak in the name of esus and let them go
nd they departed from the presence of the council rejoicing that they were counted worthy to suffer shame for his name
nd daily in the temple and in every house they ceased not to teach and preach esus hrist
nd in those days when the number of the disciples was multiplied there arose a murmuring of the recians against the ebrews because their widows were neglected in the daily ministration
hen the twelve called the multitude of the disciples unto them and said t is not reason that we should leave the word of od and serve tables
herefore brethren look ye out among you seven men of honest report full of the oly host and wisdom whom we may appoint over this business
ut we will give ourselves continually to prayer and to the ministry of the word
nd the saying pleased the whole multitude and they chose tephen a man full of faith and of the oly host and hilip and rochorus and icanor and imon and armenas and icolas a proselyte of ntioch whom they set before the apostles and when they had prayed they laid their hands on them
nd the word of od increased and the number of the disciples multiplied in erusalem greatly and a great company of the priests were obedient to the faith
nd tephen full of faith and power did great wonders and miracles among the people
hen there arose certain of the synagogue which is called the synagogue of the ibertines and yrenians and lexandrians and of them of ilicia and of sia disputing with tephen
nd they were not able to resist the wisdom and the spirit by which he spake
hen they suborned men which said e have heard him speak blasphemous words against oses and against od
nd they stirred up the people and the elders and the scribes and came upon him and caught him and brought him to the council and set up false witnesses which said his man ceaseth not to speak blasphemous words against this holy place and the law for we have heard him say that this esus of azareth shall destroy this place and shall change the customs which oses delivered us
nd all that sat in the council looking stedfastly on him saw his face as it had been the face of an angel
hen said the high priest re these things so
nd he said en brethren and fathers hearken he od of glory appeared unto our father braham when he was in esopotamia before he dwelt in harran and said unto him et thee out of thy country and from thy kindred and come into the land which  shall shew thee
hen came he out of the land of the hald ans and dwelt in harran and from thence when his father was dead he removed him into this land wherein ye now dwell
nd he gave him none inheritance in it no not so much as to set his foot on yet he promised that he would give it to him for a possession and to his seed after him when as yet he had no child
nd od spake on this wise hat his seed should sojourn in a strange land and that they should bring them into bondage and entreat them evil four hundred years
nd the nation to whom they shall be in bondage will  judge said od and after that shall they come forth and serve me in this place
nd he gave him the covenant of circumcision and so braham begat saac and circumcised him the eighth day and saac begat acob and acob begat the twelve patriarchs
