nd the patriarchs moved with envy sold oseph into gypt but od was with him and delivered him out of all his afflictions and gave him favour and wisdom in the sight of haraoh king of gypt and he made him governor over gypt and all his house
ow there came a dearth over all the land of gypt and hanaan and great affliction and our fathers found no sustenance
ut when acob heard that there was corn in gypt he sent out our fathers first
nd at the second time oseph was made known to his brethren and osephs kindred was made known unto haraoh
hen sent oseph and called his father acob to him and all his kindred threescore and fifteen souls
o acob went down into gypt and died he and our fathers and were carried over into ychem and laid in the sepulchre that braham bought for a sum of money of the sons of mmor the father of ychem
ut when the time of the promise drew nigh which od had sworn to braham the people grew and multiplied in gypt ill another king arose which knew not oseph
he same dealt subtilly with our kindred and evil entreated our fathers so that they cast out their young children to the end they might not live
n which time oses was born and was exceeding fair and nourished up in his fathers house three months and when he was cast out haraohs daughter took him up and nourished him for her own son
nd oses was learned in all the wisdom of the gyptians and was mighty in words and in deeds
nd when he was full forty years old it came into his heart to visit his brethren the children of srael
nd seeing one of them suffer wrong he defended him and avenged him that was oppressed and smote the gyptian for he supposed his brethren would have understood how that od by his hand would deliver them but they understood not
nd the next day he shewed himself unto them as they strove and would have set them at one again saying irs ye are brethren why do ye wrong one to another
ut he that did his neighbour wrong thrust him away saying ho made thee a ruler and a judge over us
ilt thou kill me as thou diddest the gyptian yesterday
hen fled oses at this saying and was a stranger in the land of adian where he begat two sons
nd when forty years were expired there appeared to him in the wilderness of mount ina an angel of the ord in a flame of fire in a bush
hen oses saw it he wondered at the sight and as he drew near to behold it the voice of the ord came unto him saying  am the od of thy fathers the od of braham and the od of saac and the od of acob
hen oses trembled and durst not behold
hen said the ord to him ut off thy shoes from thy feet for the place where thou standest is holy ground
 have seen  have seen the affliction of my people which is in gypt and  have heard their groaning and am come down to deliver them
nd now come  will send thee into gypt
his oses whom they refused saying ho made thee a ruler and a judge the same did od send to be a ruler and a deliverer by the hand of the angel which appeared to him in the bush
e brought them out after that he had shewed wonders and signs in the land of gypt and in the ed sea and in the wilderness forty years
his is that oses which said unto the children of srael  prophet shall the ord your od raise up unto you of your brethren like unto me him shall ye hear
his is he that was in the church in the wilderness with the angel which spake to him in the mount ina and with our fathers who received the lively oracles to give unto us to whom our fathers would not obey but thrust him from them and in their hearts turned back again into gypt saying unto aron ake us gods to go before us for as for this oses which brought us out of the land of gypt we wot not what is become of him
nd they made a calf in those days and offered sacrifice unto the idol and rejoiced in the works of their own hands
hen od turned and gave them up to worship the host of heaven as it is written in the book of the prophets  ye house of srael have ye offered to me slain beasts and sacrifices y the space of forty years in the wilderness
ea ye took up the tabernacle of oloch nd the star of your god emphan igures which ye made to worship them nd  will carry you away beyond abylon
ur fathers had the tabernacle of witness in the wilderness as he had appointed speaking unto oses that he should make it according to the fashion that he had seen
hich also our fathers that came after brought in with esus into the possession of the entiles whom od drave out before the face of our fathers unto the days of avid who found favour before od and desired to find a tabernacle for the od of acob
ut olomon built him an house
owbeit the most igh dwelleth not in temples made with hands as saith the prophet eaven is my throne nd earth is my footstool hat house will ye build me saith the ord r what is the place of my rest
ath not my hand made all these things
e stiffnecked and uncircumcised in heart and ears ye do always resist the oly host as your fathers did so do ye
hich of the prophets have not your fathers persecuted and they have slain them which shewed before of the coming of the ust ne of whom ye have been now the betrayers and murderers who have received the law by the disposition of angels and have not kept it
hen they heard these things they were cut to the heart and they gnashed on him with their teeth
ut he being full of the oly host looked up stedfastly into heaven and saw the glory of od and esus standing on the right hand of od and said ehold  see the heavens opened and the on of man standing on the right hand of od
hen they cried out with a loud voice and stopped their ears and ran upon him with one accord and cast him out of the city and stoned him and the witnesses laid down their clothes at a young mans feet whose name was aul
nd they stoned tephen calling upon od and saying ord esus receive my spirit
nd he kneeled down and cried with a loud voice ord lay not this sin to their charge
nd when he had said this he fell asleep
nd aul was consenting unto his death
nd at that time there was a great persecution against the church which was at erusalem and they were all scattered abroad throughout the regions of ud a and amaria except the apostles
nd devout men carried tephen to his burial and made great lamentation over him
s for aul he made havock of the church entering into every house and haling men and women committed them to prison
herefore they that were scattered abroad went every where preaching the word
hen hilip went down to the city of amaria and preached hrist unto them
nd the people with one accord gave heed unto those things which hilip spake hearing and seeing the miracles which he did
or unclean spirits crying with loud voice came out of many that were possessed with them and many taken with palsies and that were lame were healed
nd there was great joy in that city
ut there was a certain man called imon which beforetime in the same city used sorcery and bewitched the people of amaria giving out that himself was some great one to whom they all gave heed from the least to the greatest saying his man is the great power of od
nd to him they had regard because that of long time he had bewitched them with sorceries
ut when they believed hilip preaching the things concerning the kingdom of od and the name of esus hrist they were baptized both men and women
hen imon himself believed also and when he was baptized he continued with hilip and wondered beholding the miracles and signs which were done
ow when the apostles which were at erusalem heard that amaria had received the word of od they sent unto them eter and ohn who when they were come down prayed for them that they might receive the oly host for as yet he was fallen upon none of them only they were baptized in the name of the ord esus hen laid they their hands on them and they received the oly host
nd when imon saw that through laying on of the apostles hands the oly host was given he offered them money saying ive me also this power that on whomsoever  lay hands he may receive the oly host
ut eter said unto him hy money perish with thee because thou hast thought that the gift of od may be purchased with money
hou hast neither part nor lot in this matter for thy heart is not right in the sight of od
epent therefore of this thy wickedness and pray od if perhaps the thought of thine heart may be forgiven thee
or  perceive that thou art in the gall of bitterness and in the bond of iniquity
hen answered imon and said ray ye to the ord for me that none of these things which ye have spoken come upon me
nd they when they had testified and preached the word of the ord returned to erusalem and preached the gospel in many villages of the amaritans
nd the angel of the ord spake unto hilip saying rise and go toward the south unto the way that goeth down from erusalem unto aza which is desert
nd he arose and went and behold a man of thiopia an eunuch of great authority under andace queen of the thiopians who had the charge of all her treasure and had come to erusalem for to worship was returning and sitting in his chariot read saias the prophet
hen the pirit said unto hilip o near and join thyself to this chariot
nd hilip ran thither to him and heard him read the prophet saias and said nderstandest thou what thou readest
nd he said ow can  except some man should guide me
nd he desired hilip that he would come up and sit with him
he place of the scripture which he read was this e was led as a sheep to the slaughter nd like a lamb dumb before his shearer o opened he not his mouth n his humiliation his judgment was taken away nd who shall declare his generation
or his life is taken from the earth
nd the eunuch answered hilip and said  pray thee of whom speaketh the prophet this of himself or of some other man
hen hilip opened his mouth and began at the same scripture and preached unto him esus
nd as they went on their way they came unto a certain water and the eunuch said ee here is water what doth hinder me to be baptized
nd hilip said f thou believest with all thine heart thou mayest
nd he answered and said  believe that esus hrist is the on of od
nd he commanded the chariot to stand still and they went down both into the water both hilip and the eunuch and he baptized him
nd when they were come up out of the water the pirit of the ord caught away hilip that the eunuch saw him no more and he went on his way rejoicing
ut hilip was found at zotus and passing through he preached in all the cities till he came to  sarea
nd aul yet breathing out threatenings and slaughter against the disciples of the ord went unto the high priest and desired of him letters to amascus to the synagogues that if he found any of this way whether they were men or women he might bring them bound unto erusalem
nd as he journeyed he came near amascus and suddenly there shined round about him a light from heaven and he fell to the earth and heard a voice saying unto him aul aul why persecutest thou me
nd he said ho art thou ord
nd the ord said  am esus whom thou persecutest it is hard for thee to kick against the pricks
nd he trembling and astonished said ord what wilt thou have me to do
nd the ord said unto him rise and go into the city and it shall be told thee what thou must do
nd the men which journeyed with him stood speechless hearing a voice but seeing no man
nd aul arose from the earth and when his eyes were opened he saw no man but they led him by the hand and brought him into amascus
nd he was three days without sight and neither did eat nor drink
nd there was a certain disciple at amascus named nanias and to him said the ord in a vision nanias
nd he said ehold  am here ord
nd the ord said unto him rise and go into the street which is called traight and enquire in the house of udas for one called aul of arsus for behold he prayeth and hath seen in a vision a man named nanias coming in and putting his hand on him that he might receive his sight
hen nanias answered ord  have heard by many of this man how much evil he hath done to thy saints at erusalem and here he hath authority from the chief priests to bind all that call on thy name
ut the ord said unto him o thy way for he is a chosen vessel unto me to bear my name before the entiles and kings and the children of srael for  will shew him how great things he must suffer for my names sake
nd nanias went his way and entered into the house and putting his hands on him said rother aul the ord even esus that appeared unto thee in the way as thou camest hath sent me that thou mightest receive thy sight and be filled with the oly host
nd immediately there fell from his eyes as it had been scales and he received sight forthwith and arose and was baptized
nd when he had received meat he was strengthened
hen was aul certain days with the disciples which were at amascus
nd straightway he preached hrist in the synagogues that he is the on of od
ut all that heard him were amazed and said s not this he that destroyed them which called on this name in erusalem and came hither for that intent that he might bring them bound unto the chief priests
ut aul increased the more in strength and confounded the ews which dwelt at amascus proving that this is very hrist
nd after that many days were fulfilled the ews took counsel to kill him but their laying await was known of aul
nd they watched the gates day and night to kill him
hen the disciples took him by night and let him down by the wall in a basket
nd when aul was come to erusalem he assayed to join himself to the disciples but they were all afraid of him and believed not that he was a disciple
ut arnabas took him and brought him to the apostles and declared unto them how he had seen the ord in the way and that he had spoken to him and how he had preached boldly at amascus in the name of esus
nd he was with them coming in and going out at erusalem
nd he spake boldly in the name of the ord esus and disputed against the recians but they went about to slay him
hich when the brethren knew they brought him down to  sarea and sent him forth to arsus
hen had the churches rest throughout all ud a and alilee and amaria and were edified and walking in the fear of the ord and in the comfort of the oly host were multiplied
nd it came to pass as eter passed throughout all quarters he came down also to the saints which dwelt at ydda
nd there he found a certain man named  neas which had kept his bed eight years and was sick of the palsy
nd eter said unto him  neas esus hrist maketh thee whole arise and make thy bed
nd he arose immediately
nd all that dwelt at ydda and aron saw him and turned to the ord
ow there was at oppa a certain disciple named abitha which by interpretation is called orcas this woman was full of good works and almsdeeds which she did
nd it came to pass in those days that she was sick and died whom when they had washed they laid her in an upper chamber
nd forasmuch as ydda was nigh to oppa and the disciples had heard that eter was there they sent unto him two men desiring him that he would not delay to come to them
hen eter arose and went with them
hen he was come they brought him into the upper chamber and all the widows stood by him weeping and shewing the coats and garments which orcas made while she was with them
ut eter put them all forth and kneeled down and prayed and turning him to the body said abitha arise
nd she opened her eyes and when she saw eter she sat up
nd he gave her his hand and lifted her up and when he had called the saints and widows presented her alive
nd it was known throughout all oppa and many believed in the ord
nd it came to pass that he tarried many days in oppa with one imon a tanner
here was a certain man in  sarea called ornelius a centurion of the band called the talian band a devout man and one that feared od with all his house which gave much alms to the people and prayed to od alway
e saw in a vision evidently about the ninth hour of the day an angel of od coming in to him and saying unto him ornelius
nd when he looked on him he was afraid and said hat is it ord
nd he said unto him hy prayers and thine alms are come up for a memorial before od
nd now send men to oppa and call for one imon whose surname is eter he lodgeth with one imon a tanner whose house is by the sea side he shall tell thee what thou oughtest to do
nd when the angel which spake unto ornelius was departed he called two of his household servants and a devout soldier of them that waited on him continually and when he had declared all these things unto them he sent them to oppa
n the morrow as they went on their journey and drew nigh unto the city eter went up upon the housetop to pray about the sixth hour and he became very hungry and would have eaten but while they made ready he fell into a trance and saw heaven opened and a certain vessel descending unto him as it had been a great sheet knit at the four corners and let down to the earth wherein were all manner of fourfooted beasts of the earth and wild beasts and creeping things and fowls of the air
nd there came a voice to him ise eter kill and eat
ut eter said ot so ord for  have never eaten any thing that is common or unclean
nd the voice spake unto him again the second time hat od hath cleansed that call not thou common
his was done thrice and the vessel was received up again into heaven
ow while eter doubted in himself what this vision which he had seen should mean behold the men which were sent from ornelius had made enquiry for imons house and stood before the gate and called and asked whether imon which was surnamed eter were lodged there
hile eter thought on the vision the pirit said unto him ehold three men seek thee
rise therefore and get thee down and go with them doubting nothing for  have sent them
hen eter went down to the men which were sent unto him from ornelius and said ehold  am he whom ye seek what is the cause wherefore ye are come
nd they said ornelius the centurion a just man and one that feareth od and of good report among all the nation of the ews was warned from od by an holy angel to send for thee into his house and to hear words of thee
hen called he them in and lodged them
nd on the morrow eter went away with them and certain brethren from oppa accompanied him
nd the morrow after they entered into  sarea
nd ornelius waited for them and had called together his kinsmen and near friends
nd as eter was coming in ornelius met him and fell down at his feet and worshipped him
ut eter took him up saying tand up  myself also am a man
nd as he talked with him he went in and found many that were come together
nd he said unto them e know how that it is an unlawful thing for a man that is a ew to keep company or come unto one of another nation but od hath shewed me that  should not call any man common or unclean
herefore came  unto you without gainsaying as soon as  was sent for  ask therefore for what intent ye have sent for me
nd ornelius said our days ago  was fasting until this hour and at the ninth hour  prayed in my house and behold a man stood before me in bright clothing and said ornelius thy prayer is heard and thine alms are had in remembrance in the sight of od
end therefore to oppa and call hither imon whose surname is eter he is lodged in the house of one imon a tanner by the sea side who when he cometh shall speak unto thee
mmediately therefore  sent to thee and thou hast well done that thou art come
ow therefore are we all here present before od to hear all things that are commanded thee of od
hen eter opened his mouth and said f a truth  perceive that od is no respecter of persons but in every nation he that feareth him and worketh righteousness is accepted with him
he word which od sent unto the children of srael preaching peace by esus hrist he is ord of all that word  say ye know which was published throughout all ud a and began from alilee after the baptism which ohn preached how od anointed esus of azareth with the oly host and with power who went about doing good and healing all that were oppressed of the devil for od was with him
nd we are witnesses of all things which he did both in the land of the ews and in erusalem whom they slew and hanged on a tree him od raised up the third day and shewed him openly not to all the people but unto witnesses chosen before of od even to us who did eat and drink with him after he rose from the dead
nd he commanded us to preach unto the people and to testify that it is he which was ordained of od to be the udge of quick and dead
o him give all the prophets witness that through his name whosoever believeth in him shall receive remission of sins
hile eter yet spake these words the oly host fell on all them which heard the word
nd they of the circumcision which believed were astonished as many as came with eter because that on the entiles also was poured out the gift of the oly host
or they heard them speak with tongues and magnify od
hen answered eter an any man forbid water that these should not be baptized which have received the oly host as well as we
nd he commanded them to be baptized in the name of the ord
hen prayed they him to tarry certain days
nd the apostles and brethren that were in ud a heard that the entiles had also received the word of od
nd when eter was come up to erusalem they that were of the circumcision contended with him saying hou wentest in to men uncircumcised and didst eat with them
ut eter rehearsed the matter from the beginning and expounded it by order unto them saying  was in the city of oppa praying and in a trance  saw a vision  certain vessel descend as it had been a great sheet let down from heaven by four corners and it came even to me upon the which when  had fastened mine eyes  considered and saw fourfooted beasts of the earth and wild beasts and creeping things and fowls of the air
nd  heard a voice saying unto me rise eter slay and eat
ut  said ot so ord for nothing common or unclean hath at any time entered into my mouth
ut the voice answered me again from heaven hat od hath cleansed that call not thou common
nd this was done three times and all were drawn up again into heaven
nd behold immediately there were three men already come unto the house where  was sent from  sarea unto me
nd the pirit bade me go with them nothing doubting
oreover these six brethren accompanied me and we entered into the mans house and he shewed us how he had seen an angel in his house which stood and said unto him end men to oppa and call for imon whose surname is eter who shall tell thee words whereby thou and all thy house shall be saved
nd as  began to speak the oly host fell on them as on us at the beginning
hen remembered  the word of the ord how that he said ohn indeed baptized with water but ye shall be baptized with the oly host
orasmuch then as od gave them the like gift as he did unto us who believed on the ord esus hrist what was  that  could withstand od
hen they heard these things they held their peace and glorified od saying hen hath od also to the entiles granted repentance unto life
ow they which were scattered abroad upon the persecution that arose about tephen travelled as far as henice and yprus and ntioch preaching the word to none but unto the ews only
nd some of them were men of yprus and yrene which when they were come to ntioch spake unto the recians preaching the ord esus
nd the hand of the ord was with them and a great number believed and turned unto the ord
hen tidings of these things came unto the ears of the church which was in erusalem and they sent forth arnabas that he should go as far as ntioch
ho when he came and had seen the grace of od was glad and exhorted them all that with purpose of heart they would cleave unto the ord
or he was a good man and full of the oly host and of faith and much people was added unto the ord
hen departed arnabas to arsus for to seek aul and when he had found him he brought him unto ntioch
nd it came to pass that a whole year they assembled themselves with the church and taught much people
nd the disciples were called hristians first in ntioch
nd in these days came prophets from erusalem unto ntioch
nd there stood up one of them named gabus and signified by the pirit that there should be great dearth throughout all the world which came to pass in the days of laudius  sar
hen the disciples every man according to his ability determined to send relief unto the brethren which dwelt in ud a hich also they did and sent it to the elders by the hands of arnabas and aul
ow about that time erod the king stretched forth his hands to vex certain of the church
nd he killed ames the brother of ohn with the sword
nd because he saw it pleased the ews he proceeded further to take eter also
hen were the days of unleavened bread nd when he had apprehended him he put him in prison and delivered him to four quaternions of soldiers to keep him intending after aster to bring him forth to the people
eter therefore was kept in prison but prayer was made without ceasing of the church unto od for him
nd when erod would have brought him forth the same night eter was sleeping between two soldiers bound with two chains and the keepers before the door kept the prison
nd behold the angel of the ord came upon him and a light shined in the prison and he smote eter on the side and raised him up saying rise up quickly
nd his chains fell off from his hands
nd the angel said unto him ird thyself and bind on thy sandals
nd so he did
nd he saith unto him ast thy garment about thee and follow me
nd he went out and followed him and wist not that it was true which was done by the angel but thought he saw a vision
hen they were past the first and the second ward they came unto the iron gate that leadeth unto the city which opened to them of his own accord and they went out and passed on through one street and forthwith the angel departed from him
nd when eter was come to himself he said ow  know of a surety that the ord hath sent his angel and hath delivered me out of the hand of erod and from all the expectation of the people of the ews
nd when he had considered the thing he came to the house of ary the mother of ohn whose surname was ark where many were gathered together praying
nd as eter knocked at the door of the gate a damsel came to hearken named hoda
nd when she knew eters voice she opened not the gate for gladness but ran in and told how eter stood before the gate
nd they said unto her hou art mad
ut she constantly affirmed that it was even so
hen said they t is his angel
ut eter continued knocking and when they had opened the door and saw him they were astonished
ut he beckoning unto them with the hand to hold their peace declared unto them how the ord had brought him out of the prison
nd he said o shew these things unto ames and to the brethren
nd he departed and went into another place
ow as soon as it was day there was no small stir among the soldiers what was become of eter
nd when erod had sought for him and found him not he examined the keepers and commanded that they should be put to death
nd he went down from ud a to  sarea and there abode
nd erod was highly displeased with them of yre and idon but they came with one accord to him and having made lastus the kings chamberlain their friend desired peace because their country was nourished by the kings country
nd upon a set day erod arrayed in royal apparel sat upon his throne and made an oration unto them
nd the people gave a shout saying t is the voice of a god and not of a man
nd immediately the angel of the ord smote him because he gave not od the glory and he was eaten of worms and gave up the ghost
ut the word of od grew and multiplied
nd arnabas and aul returned from erusalem when they had fulfilled their ministry and took with them ohn whose surname was ark
ow there were in the church that was at ntioch certain prophets and teachers as arnabas and imeon that was called iger and ucius of yrene and anaen which had been brought up with erod the tetrarch and aul
s they ministered to the ord and fasted the oly host said eparate me arnabas and aul for the work whereunto  have called them
nd when they had fasted and prayed and laid their hands on them they sent them away
o they being sent forth by the oly host departed unto eleucia and from thence they sailed to yprus
nd when they were at alamis they preached the word of od in the synagogues of the ews and they had also ohn to their minister
nd when they had gone through the isle unto aphos they found a certain sorcerer a false prophet a ew whose name was arjesus which was with the deputy of the country ergius aulus a prudent man who called for arnabas and aul and desired to hear the word of od
ut lymas the sorcerer for so is his name by interpretation withstood them seeking to turn away the deputy from the faith
hen aul who also is called aul  filled with the oly host set his eyes on him and said  full of all subtilty and all mischief thou child of the devil thou enemy of all righteousness wilt thou not cease to pervert the right ways of the ord
nd now behold the hand of the ord is upon thee and thou shalt be blind not seeing the sun for a season
nd immediately there fell on him a mist and a darkness and he went about seeking some to lead him by the hand
hen the deputy when he saw what was done believed being astonished at the doctrine of the ord
ow when aul and his company loosed from aphos they came to erga in amphylia and ohn departing from them returned to erusalem
ut when they departed from erga they came to ntioch in isidia and went into the synagogue on the sabbath day and sat down
nd after the reading of the law and the prophets the rulers of the synagogue sent unto them saying e men and brethren if ye have any word of exhortation for the people say on
hen aul stood up and beckoning with his hand said en of srael and ye that fear od give audience
he od of this people of srael chose our fathers and exalted the people when they dwelt as strangers in the land of gypt and with an high arm brought he them out of it
nd about the time of forty years suffered he their manners in the wilderness
nd when he had destroyed seven nations in the land of hanaan he divided their land to them by lot
nd after that he gave unto them judges about the space of four hundred and fifty years until amuel the prophet
nd afterward they desired a king and od gave unto them aul the son of is a man of the tribe of enjamin by the space of forty years
nd when he had removed him he raised up unto them avid to be their king to whom also he gave testimony and said  have found avid the son of esse a man after mine own heart which shall fulfil all my will
f this mans seed hath od according to his promise raised unto srael a aviour esus when ohn had first preached before his coming the baptism of repentance to all the people of srael
nd as ohn fulfilled his course he said hom think ye that  am
 am not he
ut behold there cometh one after me whose shoes of his feet  am not worthy to loose
en and brethren children of the stock of braham and whosoever among you feareth od to you is the word of this salvation sent
or they that dwell at erusalem and their rulers because they knew him not nor yet the voices of the prophets which are read every sabbath day they have fulfilled them in condemning him
nd though they found no cause of death in him yet desired they ilate that he should be slain
nd when they had fulfilled all that was written of him they took him down from the tree and laid him in a sepulchre
ut od raised him from the dead and he was seen many days of them which came up with him from alilee to erusalem who are his witnesses unto the people
nd we declare unto you glad tidings how that the promise which was made unto the fathers od hath fulfilled the same unto us their children in that he hath raised up esus again as it is also written in the second psalm hou art my on this day have  begotten thee
nd as concerning that he raised him up from the dead now no more to return to corruption he said on this wise  will give you the sure mercies of avid
herefore he saith also in another psalm hou shalt not suffer thine oly ne to see corruption
or avid after he had served his own generation by the will of od fell on sleep and was laid unto his fathers and saw corruption but he whom od raised again saw no corruption
e it known unto you therefore men and brethren that through this man is preached unto you the forgiveness of sins and by him all that believe are justified from all things from which ye could not be justified by the law of oses
eware therefore lest that come upon you which is spoken of in the prophets ehold ye despisers and wonder and perish or  work a work in your days  work which ye shall in no wise believe though a man declare it unto you
nd when the ews were gone out of the synagogue the entiles besought that these words might be preached to them the next sabbath
ow when the congregation was broken up many of the ews and religious proselytes followed aul and arnabas who speaking to them persuaded them to continue in the grace of od
nd the next sabbath day came almost the whole city together to hear the word of od
ut when the ews saw the multitudes they were filled with envy and spake against those things which were spoken by aul contradicting and blaspheming
hen aul and arnabas waxed bold and said t was necessary that the word of od should first have been spoken to you but seeing ye put it from you and judge yourselves unworthy of everlasting life lo we turn to the entiles
or so hath the ord commanded us saying  have set thee to be a light of the entiles hat thou shouldest be for salvation unto the ends of the earth
nd when the entiles heard this they were glad and glorified the word of the ord and as many as were ordained to eternal life believed
nd the word of the ord was published throughout all the region
ut the ews stirred up the devout and honourable women and the chief men of the city and raised persecution against aul and arnabas and expelled them out of their coasts
ut they shook off the dust of their feet against them and came unto conium
nd the disciples were filled with joy and with the oly host
nd it came to pass in conium that they went both together into the synagogue of the ews and so spake that a great multitude both of the ews and also of the reeks believed
ut the unbelieving ews stirred up the entiles and made their minds evil affected against the brethren
ong time therefore abode they speaking boldly in the ord which gave testimony unto the word of his grace and granted signs and wonders to be done by their hands
ut the multitude of the city was divided and part held with the ews and part with the apostles
nd when there was an assault made both of the entiles and also of the ews with their rulers to use them despitefully and to stone them they were ware of it and fled unto ystra and erbe cities of ycaonia and unto the region that lieth round about and there they preached the gospel
nd there sat a certain man at ystra impotent in his feet being a cripple from his mothers womb who never had walked the same heard aul speak who stedfastly beholding him and perceiving that he had faith to be healed said with a loud voice tand upright on thy feet
nd he leaped and walked
nd when the people saw what aul had done they lifted up their voices saying in the speech of ycaonia he gods are come down to us in the likeness of men
nd they called arnabas upiter and aul ercurius because he was the chief speaker
hen the priest of upiter which was before their city brought oxen and garlands unto the gates and would have done sacrifice with the people
hich when the apostles arnabas and aul heard of they rent their clothes and ran in among the people crying out and saying irs why do ye these things
e also are men of like passions with you and preach unto you that ye should turn from these vanities unto the living od which made heaven and earth and the sea and all things that are therein who in times past suffered all nations to walk in their own ways
evertheless he left not himself without witness in that he did good and gave us rain from heaven and fruitful seasons filling our hearts with food and gladness
nd with these sayings scarce restrained they the people that they had not done sacrifice unto them
nd there came thither certain ews from ntioch and conium who persuaded the people and having stoned aul drew him out of the city supposing he had been dead
owbeit as the disciples stood round about him he rose up and came into the city and the next day he departed with arnabas to erbe
nd when they had preached the gospel to that city and had taught many they returned again to ystra and to conium and ntioch confirming the souls of the disciples and exhorting them to continue in the faith and that we must through much tribulation enter into the kingdom of od
nd when they had ordained them elders in every church and had prayed with fasting they commended them to the ord on whom they believed
nd after they had passed throughout isidia they came to amphylia
nd when they had preached the word in erga they went down into ttalia and thence sailed to ntioch from whence they had been recommended to the grace of od for the work which they fulfilled
nd when they were come and had gathered the church together they rehearsed all that od had done with them and how he had opened the door of faith unto the entiles
nd there they abode long time with the disciples
nd certain men which came down from ud a taught the brethren and said xcept ye be circumcised after the manner of oses ye cannot be saved
hen therefore aul and arnabas had no small dissension and disputation with them they determined that aul and arnabas and certain other of them should go up to erusalem unto the apostles and elders about this question
nd being brought on their way by the church they passed through henice and amaria declaring the conversion of the entiles and they caused great joy unto all the brethren
nd when they were come to erusalem they were received of the church and of the apostles and elders and they declared all things that od had done with them
ut there rose up certain of the sect of the harisees which believed saying hat it was needful to circumcise them and to command them to keep the law of oses
nd the apostles and elders came together for to consider of this matter
nd when there had been much disputing eter rose up and said unto them en and brethren ye know how that a good while ago od made choice among us that the entiles by my mouth should hear the word of the gospel and believe
nd od which knoweth the hearts bare them witness giving them the oly host even as he did unto us and put no difference between us and them purifying their hearts by faith
ow therefore why tempt ye od to put a yoke upon the neck of the disciples which neither our fathers nor we were able to bear
ut we believe that through the grace of the ord esus hrist we shall be saved even as they
hen all the multitude kept silence and gave audience to arnabas and aul declaring what miracles and wonders od had wrought among the entiles by them
nd after they had held their peace ames answered saying en and brethren hearken unto me imeon hath declared how od at the first did visit the entiles to take out of them a people for his name
nd to this agree the words of the prophets as it is written fter this  will return nd will build again the tabernacle of avid which is fallen down nd  will build again the ruins thereof nd  will set it up hat the residue of men might seek after the ord nd all the entiles upon whom my name is called aith the ord who doeth all these things
nown unto od are all his works from the beginning of the world
herefore my sentence is that we trouble not them which from among the entiles are turned to od but that we write unto them that they abstain from pollutions of idols and from fornication and from things strangled and from blood
or oses of old time hath in every city them that preach him being read in the synagogues every sabbath day
hen pleased it the apostles and elders with the whole church to send chosen men of their own company to ntioch with aul and arnabas namely udas surnamed arsabas and ilas chief men among the brethren and they wrote letters by them after this manner he apostles and elders and brethren send greeting unto the brethren which are of the entiles in ntioch and yria and ilicia forasmuch as we have heard that certain which went out from us have troubled you with words subverting your souls saying e must be circumcised and keep the law to whom we gave no such commandment it seemed good unto us being assembled with one accord to send chosen men unto you with our beloved arnabas and aul men that have hazarded their lives for the name of our ord esus hrist
e have sent therefore udas and ilas who shall also tell you the same things by mouth
or it seemed good to the oly host and to us to lay upon you no greater burden than these necessary things that ye abstain from meats offered to idols and from blood and from things strangled and from fornication from which if ye keep yourselves ye shall do well
are ye well
o when they were dismissed they came to ntioch and when they had gathered the multitude together they delivered the epistle which when they had read they rejoiced for the consolation
nd udas and ilas being prophets also themselves exhorted the brethren with many words and confirmed them
nd after they had tarried there a space they were let go in peace from the brethren unto the apostles
otwithstanding it pleased ilas to abide there still
aul also and arnabas continued in ntioch teaching and preaching the word of the ord with many others also
nd some days after aul said unto arnabas et us go again and visit our brethren in every city where we have preached the word of the ord and see how they do
nd arnabas determined to take with them ohn whose surname was ark
ut aul thought not good to take him with them who departed from them from amphylia and went not with them to the work
nd the contention was so sharp between them that they departed asunder one from the other and so arnabas took ark and sailed unto yprus and aul chose ilas and departed being recommended by the brethren unto the grace of od
nd he went through yria and ilicia confirming the churches
hen came he to erbe and ystra and behold a certain disciple was there named imotheus the son of a certain woman which was a ewess and believed but his father was a reek which was well reported of by the brethren that were at ystra and conium
im would aul have to go forth with him and took and circumcised him because of the ews which were in those quarters for they knew all that his father was a reek
nd as they went through the cities they delivered them the decrees for to keep that were ordained of the apostles and elders which were at erusalem
nd so were the churches established in the faith and increased in number daily
ow when they had gone throughout hrygia and the region of alatia and were forbidden of the oly host to preach the word in sia after they were come to ysia they assayed to go into ithynia but the pirit suffered them not
nd they passing by ysia came down to roas
nd a vision appeared to aul in the night here stood a man of acedonia and prayed him saying ome over into acedonia and help us
nd after he had seen the vision immediately we endeavoured to go into acedonia assuredly gathering that the ord had called us for to preach the gospel unto them
herefore loosing from roas we came with a straight course to amothracia and the next day to eapolis and from thence to hilippi which is the chief city of that part of acedonia and a colony and we were in that city abiding certain days
nd on the sabbath we went out of the city by a river side where prayer was wont to be made and we sat down and spake unto the women which resorted thither
nd a certain woman named ydia a seller of purple of the city of hyatira which worshipped od heard us whose heart the ord opened that she attended unto the things which were spoken of aul
nd when she was baptized and her household she besought us saying f ye have judged me to be faithful to the ord come into my house and abide there
nd she constrained us
nd it came to pass as we went to prayer a certain damsel possessed with a spirit of divination met us which brought her masters much gain by soothsaying he same followed aul and us and cried saying hese men are the servants of the most high od which shew unto us the way of salvation
nd this did she many days
ut aul being grieved turned and said to the spirit  command thee in the name of esus hrist to come out of her
nd he came out the same hour
nd when her masters saw that the hope of their gains was gone they caught aul and ilas and drew them into the marketplace unto the rulers and brought them to the magistrates saying hese men being ews do exceedingly trouble our city and teach customs which are not lawful for us to receive neither to observe being omans
nd the multitude rose up together against them and the magistrates rent off their clothes and commanded to beat them
nd when they had laid many stripes upon them they cast them into prison charging the jailor to keep them safely who having received such a charge thrust them into the inner prison and made their feet fast in the stocks
nd at midnight aul and ilas prayed and sang praises unto od and the prisoners heard them
nd suddenly there was a great earthquake so that the foundations of the prison were shaken and immediately all the doors were opened and every ones bands were loosed
nd the keeper of the prison awaking out of his sleep and seeing the prison doors open he drew out his sword and would have killed himself supposing that the prisoners had been fled
ut aul cried with a loud voice saying o thyself no harm for we are all here
hen he called for a light and sprang in and came trembling and fell down before aul and ilas and brought them out and said irs what must  do to be saved
nd they said elieve on the ord esus hrist and thou shalt be saved and thy house
nd they spake unto him the word of the ord and to all that were in his house
nd he took them the same hour of the night and washed their stripes and was baptized he and all his straightway
nd when he had brought them into his house he set meat before them and rejoiced believing in od with all his house
nd when it was day the magistrates sent the serjeants saying et those men go
nd the keeper of the prison told this saying to aul he magistrates have sent to let you go now therefore depart and go in peace
ut aul said unto them hey have beaten us openly uncondemned being omans and have cast us into prison and now do they thrust us out privily nay verily but let them come themselves and fetch us out
nd the serjeants told these words unto the magistrates and they feared when they heard that they were omans
nd they came and besought them and brought them out and desired them to depart out of the city
nd they went out of the prison and entered into the house of ydia and when they had seen the brethren they comforted them and departed
ow when they had passed through mphipolis and pollonia they came to hessalonica where was a synagogue of the ews and aul as his manner was went in unto them and three sabbath days reasoned with them out of the scriptures opening and alleging that hrist must needs have suffered and risen again from the dead and that this esus whom  preach unto you is hrist
nd some of them believed and consorted with aul and ilas and of the devout reeks a great multitude and of the chief women not a few
ut the ews which believed not moved with envy took unto them certain lewd fellows of the baser sort and gathered a company and set all the city on an uproar and assaulted the house of ason and sought to bring them out to the people
nd when they found them not they drew ason and certain brethren unto the rulers of the city crying hese that have turned the world upside down are come hither also whom ason hath received and these all do contrary to the decrees of  sar saying that there is another king one esus
nd they troubled the people and the rulers of the city when they heard these things
nd when they had taken security of ason and of the other they let them go
nd the brethren immediately sent away aul and ilas by night unto erea who coming thither went into the synagogue of the ews
hese were more noble than those in hessalonica in that they received the word with all readiness of mind and searched the scriptures daily whether those things were so
herefore many of them believed also of honourable women which were reeks and of men not a few
ut when the ews of hessalonica had knowledge that the word of od was preached of aul at erea they came thither also and stirred up the people
nd then immediately the brethren sent away aul to go as it were to the sea but ilas and imotheus abode there still
nd they that conducted aul brought him unto thens and receiving a commandment unto ilas and imotheus for to come to him with all speed they departed
ow while aul waited for them at thens his spirit was stirred in him when he saw the city wholly given to idolatry
herefore disputed he in the synagogue with the ews and with the devout persons and in the market daily with them that met with him
hen certain philosophers of the picureans and of the toicks encountered him
nd some said hat will this babbler say other some e seemeth to be a setter forth of strange gods because he preached unto them esus and the resurrection
nd they took him and brought him unto reopagus saying ay we know what this new doctrine whereof thou speakest is
or thou bringest certain strange things to our ears we would know therefore what these things mean
or all the thenians and strangers which were there spent their time in nothing else but either to tell or to hear some new thing hen aul stood in the midst of ars hill and said e men of thens  perceive that in all things ye are too superstitious
or as  passed by and beheld your devotions  found an altar with this inscription    
hom therefore ye ignorantly worship him declare  unto you
od that made the world and all things therein seeing that he is ord of heaven and earth dwelleth not in temples made with hands neither is worshipped with mens hands as though he needed any thing seeing he giveth to all life and breath and all things and hath made of one blood all nations of men for to dwell on all the face of the earth and hath determined the times before appointed and the bounds of their habitation that they should seek the ord if haply they might feel after him and find him though he be not far from every one of us for in him we live and move and have our being as certain also of your own poets have said or we are also his offspring
orasmuch then as we are the offspring of od we ought not to think that the odhead is like unto gold or silver or stone graven by art and mans device
nd the times of this ignorance od winked at but now commandeth all men every where to repent because he hath appointed a day in the which he will judge the world in righteousness by that man whom he hath ordained whereof he hath given assurance unto all men in that he hath raised him from the dead
nd when they heard of the resurrection of the dead some mocked and others said e will hear thee again of this matter
o aul departed from among them
owbeit certain men clave unto him and believed among the which was ionysius the reopagite and a woman named amaris and others with them
fter these things aul departed from thens and came to orinth and found a certain ew named quila born in ontus lately come from taly with his wife riscilla because that laudius had commanded all ews to depart from ome and came unto them
nd because he was of the same craft he abode with them and wrought for by their occupation they were tentmakers
nd he reasoned in the synagogue every sabbath and persuaded the ews and the reeks
nd when ilas and imotheus were come from acedonia aul was pressed in the spirit and testified to the ews that esus was hrist
nd when they opposed themselves and blasphemed he shook his raiment and said unto them our blood be upon your own heads  am clean from henceforth  will go unto the entiles
nd he departed thence and entered into a certain mans house named ustus one that worshipped od whose house joined hard to the synagogue
nd rispus the chief ruler of the synagogue believed on the ord with all his house and many of the orinthians hearing believed and were baptized
hen spake the ord to aul in the night by a vision e not afraid but speak and hold not thy peace for  am with thee and no man shall set on thee to hurt thee for  have much people in this city
nd he continued there a year and six months teaching the word of od among them
nd when allio was the deputy of chaia the ews made insurrection with one accord against aul and brought him to the judgment seat saying his fellow persuadeth men to worship od contrary to the law
nd when aul was now about to open his mouth allio said unto the ews f it were a matter of wrong or wicked lewdness  ye ews reason would that  should bear with you but if it be a question of words and names and of your law look ye to it for  will be no judge of such matters
nd he drave them from the judgment seat
hen all the reeks took osthenes the chief ruler of the synagogue and beat him before the judgment seat
nd allio cared for none of those things
nd aul after this tarried there yet a good while and then took his leave of the brethren and sailed thence into yria and with him riscilla and quila having shorn his head in enchrea for he had a vow
nd he came to phesus and left them there but he himself entered into the synagogue and reasoned with the ews
hen they desired him to tarry longer time with them he consented not but bade them farewell saying  must by all means keep this feast that cometh in erusalem but  will return again unto you if od will
nd he sailed from phesus
nd when he had landed at  sarea and gone up and saluted the church he went down to ntioch
nd after he had spent some time there he departed and went over all the country of alatia and hrygia in order strengthening all the disciples
nd a certain ew named pollos born at lexandria an eloquent man and mighty in the scriptures came to phesus
his man was instructed in the way of the ord and being fervent in the spirit he spake and taught diligently the things of the ord knowing only the baptism of ohn
nd he began to speak boldly in the synagogue whom when quila and riscilla had heard they took him unto them and expounded unto him the way of od more perfectly
nd when he was disposed to pass into chaia the brethren wrote exhorting the disciples to receive him who when he was come helped them much which had believed through grace for he mightily convinced the ews and that publickly shewing by the scriptures that esus was hrist
nd it came to pass that while pollos was at orinth aul having passed through the upper coasts came to phesus and finding certain disciples he said unto them ave ye received the oly host since ye believed
nd they said unto him e have not so much as heard whether there be any oly host
nd he said unto them nto what then were ye baptized
nd they said nto ohns baptism
hen said aul ohn verily baptized with the baptism of repentance saying unto the people that they should believe on him which should come after him that is on hrist esus
hen they heard this they were baptized in the name of the ord esus
nd when aul had laid his hands upon them the oly host came on them and they spake with tongues and prophesied
nd all the men were about twelve
nd he went into the synagogue and spake boldly for the space of three months disputing and persuading the things concerning the kingdom of od
ut when divers were hardened and believed not but spake evil of that way before the multitude he departed from them and separated the disciples disputing daily in the school of one yrannus
nd this continued by the space of two years so that all they which dwelt in sia heard the word of the ord esus both ews and reeks
nd od wrought special miracles by the hands of aul so that from his body were brought unto the sick handkerchiefs or aprons and the diseases departed from them and the evil spirits went out of them
hen certain of the vagabond ews exorcists took upon them to call over them which had evil spirits the name of the ord esus saying e adjure you by esus whom aul preacheth
nd there were seven sons of one ceva a ew and chief of the priests which did so
nd the evil spirit answered and said esus  know and aul  know but who are ye
nd the man in whom the evil spirit was leaped on them and overcame them and prevailed against them so that they fled out of that house naked and wounded
nd this was known to all the ews and reeks also dwelling at phesus and fear fell on them all and the name of the ord esus was magnified
nd many that believed came and confessed and shewed their deeds
any of them also which used curious arts brought their books together and burned them before all men and they counted the price of them and found it fifty thousand pieces of silver
o mightily grew the word of od and prevailed
fter these things were ended aul purposed in the spirit when he had passed through acedonia and chaia to go to erusalem saying fter  have been there  must also see ome
o he sent into acedonia two of them that ministered unto him imotheus and rastus but he himself stayed in sia for a season
nd the same time there arose no small stir about that way
or a certain man named emetrius a silversmith which made silver shrines for iana brought no small gain unto the craftsmen whom he called together with the workmen of like occupation and said irs ye know that by this craft we have our wealth
oreover ye see and hear that not alone at phesus but almost throughout all sia this aul hath persuaded and turned away much people saying that they be no gods which are made with hands so that not only this our craft is in danger to be set at nought but also that the temple of the great goddess iana should be despised and her magnificence should be destroyed whom all sia and the world worshippeth
nd when they heard these sayings they were full of wrath and cried out saying reat is iana of the phesians
nd the whole city was filled with confusion and having caught aius and ristarchus men of acedonia auls companions in travel they rushed with one accord into the theatre
nd when aul would have entered in unto the people the disciples suffered him not
nd certain of the chief of sia which were his friends sent unto him desiring him that he would not adventure himself into the theatre
ome therefore cried one thing and some another for the assembly was confused and the more part knew not wherefore they were come together
nd they drew lexander out of the multitude the ews putting him forward
nd lexander beckoned with the hand and would have made his defence unto the people
ut when they knew that he was a ew all with one voice about the space of two hours cried out reat is iana of the phesians
nd when the townclerk had appeased the people he said e men of phesus what man is there that knoweth not how that the city of the phesians is a worshipper of the great goddess iana and of the image which fell down from upiter
eeing then that these things cannot be spoken against ye ought to be quiet and to do nothing rashly
or ye have brought hither these men which are neither robbers of churches nor yet blasphemers of your goddess
herefore if emetrius and the craftsmen which are with him have a matter against any man the law is open and there are deputies let them implead one another
ut if ye enquire any thing concerning other matters it shall be determined in a lawful assembly
or we are in danger to be called in question for this days uproar there being no cause whereby we may give an account of this concourse
nd when he had thus spoken he dismissed the assembly
nd after the uproar was ceased aul called unto him the disciples and embraced them and departed for to go into acedonia
nd when he had gone over those parts and had given them much exhortation he came into reece and there abode three months
nd when the ews laid wait for him as he was about to sail into yria he purposed to return through acedonia
nd there accompanied him into sia opater of erea and of the hessalonians ristarchus and ecundus and aius of erbe and imotheus and of sia ychicus and rophimus
hese going before tarried for us at roas
nd we sailed away from hilippi after the days of unleavened bread and came unto them to roas in five days where we abode seven days
nd upon the first day of the week when the disciples came together to break bread aul preached unto them ready to depart on the morrow and continued his speech until midnight
nd there were many lights in the upper chamber where they were gathered together
nd there sat in a window a certain young man named utychus being fallen into a deep sleep and as aul was long preaching he sunk down with sleep and fell down from the third loft and was taken up dead
nd aul went down and fell on him and embracing him said rouble not yourselves for his life is in him
hen he therefore was come up again and had broken bread and eaten and talked a long while even till break of day so he departed
nd they brought the young man alive and were not a little comforted
nd we went before to ship and sailed unto ssos there intending to take in aul for so had he appointed minding himself to go afoot
nd when he met with us at ssos we took him in and came to itylene
nd we sailed thence and came the next day over against hios and the next day we arrived at amos and tarried at rogyllium and the next day we came to iletus
or aul had determined to sail by phesus because he would not spend the time in sia for he hasted if it were possible for him to be at erusalem the day of entecost
nd from iletus he sent to phesus and called the elders of the church
nd when they were come to him he said unto them e know from the first day that  came into sia after what manner  have been with you at all seasons serving the ord with all humility of mind and with many tears and temptations which befell me by the lying in wait of the ews and how  kept back nothing that was profitable unto you but have shewed you and have taught you publickly and from house to house testifying both to the ews and also to the reeks repentance toward od and faith toward our ord esus hrist
nd now behold  go bound in the spirit unto erusalem not knowing the things that shall befall me there save that the oly host witnesseth in every city saying that bonds and afflictions abide me
ut none of these things move me neither count  my life dear unto myself so that  might finish my course with joy and the ministry which  have received of the ord esus to testify the gospel of the grace of od
nd now behold  know that ye all among whom  have gone preaching the kingdom of od shall see my face no more
herefore  take you to record this day that  am pure from the blood of all men
or  have not shunned to declare unto you all the counsel of od
ake heed therefore unto yourselves and to all the flock over the which the oly host hath made you overseers to feed the church of od which he hath purchased with his own blood
or  know this that after my departing shall grievous wolves enter in among you not sparing the flock
lso of your own selves shall men arise speaking perverse things to draw away disciples after them
herefore watch and remember that by the space of three years  ceased not to warn every one night and day with tears
nd now brethren  commend you to od and to the word of his grace which is able to build you up and to give you an inheritance among all them which are sanctified
 have coveted no mans silver or gold or apparel
ea ye yourselves know that these hands have ministered unto my necessities and to them that were with me
 have shewed you all things how that so labouring ye ought to support the weak and to remember the words of the ord esus how he said t is more blessed to give than to receive
nd when he had thus spoken he kneeled down and prayed with them all
nd they all wept sore and fell on auls neck and kissed him sorrowing most of all for the words which he spake that they should see his face no more
nd they accompanied him unto the ship
nd it came to pass that after we were gotten from them and had launched we came with a straight course unto oos and the day following unto hodes and from thence unto atara and finding a ship sailing over unto henicia we went aboard and set forth
ow when we had discovered yprus we left it on the left hand and sailed into yria and landed at yre for there the ship was to unlade her burden
nd finding disciples we tarried there seven days who said to aul through the pirit that he should not go up to erusalem
nd when we had accomplished those days we departed and went our way and they all brought us on our way with wives and children till we were out of the city and we kneeled down on the shore and prayed
nd when we had taken our leave one of another we took ship and they returned home again
nd when we had finished our course from yre we came to tolemais and saluted the brethren and abode with them one day
nd the next day we that were of auls company departed and came unto  sarea and we entered into the house of hilip the evangelist which was one of the seven and abode with him
nd the same man had four daughters virgins which did prophesy
nd as we tarried there many days there came down from ud a a certain prophet named gabus
nd when he was come unto us he took auls girdle and bound his own hands and feet and said hus saith the oly host o shall the ews at erusalem bind the man that owneth this girdle and shall deliver him into the hands of the entiles
nd when we heard these things both we and they of that place besought him not to go up to erusalem
hen aul answered hat mean ye to weep and to break mine heart for  am ready not to be bound only but also to die at erusalem for the name of the ord esus
nd when he would not be persuaded we ceased saying he will of the ord be done
nd after those days we took up our carriages and went up to erusalem
here went with us also certain of the disciples of  sarea and brought with them one nason of yprus an old disciple with whom we should lodge
nd when we were come to erusalem the brethren received us gladly
nd the day following aul went in with us unto ames and all the elders were present
nd when he had saluted them he declared particularly what things od had wrought among the entiles by his ministry
nd when they heard it they glorified the ord and said unto him hou seest brother how many thousands of ews there are which believe and they are all zealous of the law and they are informed of thee that thou teachest all the ews which are among the entiles to forsake oses saying that they ought not to circumcise their children neither to walk after the customs
hat is it therefore the multitude must needs come together for they will hear that thou art come
o therefore this that we say to thee e have four men which have a vow on them them take and purify thyself with them and be at charges with them that they may shave their heads and all may know that those things whereof they were informed concerning thee are nothing but that thou thyself also walkest orderly and keepest the law
s touching the entiles which believe we have written and concluded that they observe no such thing save only that they keep themselves from things offered to idols and from blood and from strangled and from fornication
hen aul took the men and the next day purifying himself with them entered into the temple to signify the accomplishment of the days of purification until that an offering should be offered for every one of them
nd when the seven days were almost ended the ews which were of sia when they saw him in the temple stirred up all the people and laid hands on him crying out en of srael help his is the man that teacheth all men every where against the people and the law and this place and further brought reeks also into the temple and hath polluted this holy place
or they had seen before with him in the city rophimus an phesian whom they supposed that aul had brought into the temple nd all the city was moved and the people ran together and they took aul and drew him out of the temple and forthwith the doors were shut
nd as they went about to kill him tidings came unto the chief captain of the band that all erusalem was in an uproar
ho immediately took soldiers and centurions and ran down unto them and when they saw the chief captain and the soldiers they left beating of aul
hen the chief captain came near and took him and commanded him to be bound with two chains and demanded who he was and what he had done
nd some cried one thing some another among the multitude and when he could not know the certainty for the tumult he commanded him to be carried into the castle
nd when he came upon the stairs so it was that he was borne of the soldiers for the violence of the people
or the multitude of the people followed after crying way with him
nd as aul was to be led into the castle he said unto the chief captain ay  speak unto thee
ho said anst thou speak reek
rt not thou that gyptian which before these days madest an uproar and leddest out into the wilderness four thousand men that were murderers
ut aul said  am a man which am a ew of arsus a city in ilicia a citizen of no mean city and  beseech thee suffer me to speak unto the people
nd when he had given him licence aul stood on the stairs and beckoned with the hand unto the people
nd when there was made a great silence he spake unto them in the ebrew tongue saying 
en brethren and fathers hear ye my defence which  make now unto you
nd when they heard that he spake in the ebrew tongue to them they kept the more silence and he saith   am verily a man which am a ew born in arsus a city in ilicia yet brought up in this city at the feet of amaliel and taught according to the perfect manner of the law of the fathers and was zealous toward od as ye all are this day
nd  persecuted this way unto the death binding and delivering into prisons both men and women
s also the high priest doth bear me witness and all the estate of the elders from whom also  received letters unto the brethren and went to amascus to bring them which were there bound unto erusalem for to be punished
nd it came to pass that as  made my journey and was come nigh unto amascus about noon suddenly there shone from heaven a great light round about me
nd  fell unto the ground and heard a voice saying unto me aul aul why persecutest thou me
nd  answered ho art thou ord
nd he said unto me  am esus of azareth whom thou persecutest
nd they that were with me saw indeed the light and were afraid but they heard not the voice of him that spake to me
nd  said hat shall  do ord
nd the ord said unto me rise and go into amascus and there it shall be told thee of all things which are appointed for thee to do
nd when  could not see for the glory of that light being led by the hand of them that were with me  came into amascus
nd one nanias a devout man according to the law having a good report of all the ews which dwelt there came unto me and stood and said unto me rother aul receive thy sight
nd the same hour  looked up upon him
nd he said he od of our fathers hath chosen thee that thou shouldest know his will and see that ust ne and shouldest hear the voice of his mouth
or thou shalt be his witness unto all men of what thou hast seen and heard
nd now why tarriest thou arise and be baptized and wash away thy sins calling on the name of the ord
nd it came to pass that when  was come again to erusalem even while  prayed in the temple  was in a trance and saw him saying unto me ake haste and get thee quickly out of erusalem for they will not receive thy testimony concerning me
nd  said ord they know that  imprisoned and beat in every synagogue them that believed on thee and when the blood of thy martyr tephen was shed  also was standing by and consenting unto his death and kept the raiment of them that slew him
nd he said unto me epart for  will send thee far hence unto the entiles
nd they gave him audience unto this word and then lifted up their voices and said way with such a fellow from the earth for it is not fit that he should live
nd as they cried out and cast off their clothes and threw dust into the air the chief captain commanded him to be brought into the castle and bade that he should be examined by scourging that he might know wherefore they cried so against him
nd as they bound him with thongs aul said unto the centurion that stood by s it lawful for you to scourge a man that is a oman and uncondemned
hen the centurion heard that he went and told the chief captain saying ake heed what thou doest for this man is a oman
hen the chief captain came and said unto him ell me art thou a oman
e said ea
nd the chief captain answered ith a great sum obtained  this freedom
nd aul said ut  was free born
hen straightway they departed from him which should have examined him and the chief captain also was afraid after he knew that he was a oman and because he had bound him
n the morrow because he would have known the certainty wherefore he was accused of the ews he loosed him from his bands and commanded the chief priests and all their council to appear and brought aul down and set him before them
nd aul earnestly beholding the council said en and brethren  have lived in all good conscience before od until this day
nd the high priest nanias commanded them that stood by him to smite him on the mouth
hen said aul unto him od shall smite thee thou whited wall for sittest thou to judge me after the law and commandest me to be smitten contrary to the law
nd they that stood by said evilest thou ods high priest
hen said aul  wist not brethren that he was the high priest for it is written hou shalt not speak evil of the ruler of thy people
ut when aul perceived that the one part were adducees and the other harisees he cried out in the council en and brethren  am a harisee the son of a harisee of the hope and resurrection of the dead  am called in question
nd when he had so said there arose a dissension between the harisees and the adducees and the multitude was divided
or the adducees say that there is no resurrection neither angel nor spirit but the harisees confess both
nd there arose a great cry and the scribes that were of the harisees part arose and strove saying e find no evil in this man but if a spirit or an angel hath spoken to him let us not fight against od
nd when there arose a great dissension the chief captain fearing lest aul should have been pulled in pieces of them commanded the soldiers to go down and to take him by force from among them and to bring him into the castle
nd the night following the ord stood by him and said e of good cheer aul for as thou hast testified of me in erusalem so must thou bear witness also at ome
nd when it was day certain of the ews banded together and bound themselves under a curse saying that they would neither eat nor drink till they had killed aul
nd they were more than forty which had made this conspiracy
nd they came to the chief priests and elders and said e have bound ourselves under a great curse that we will eat nothing until we have slain aul
ow therefore ye with the council signify to the chief captain that he bring him down unto you to morrow as though ye would enquire something more perfectly concerning him and we or ever he come near are ready to kill him
nd when auls sisters son heard of their lying in wait he went and entered into the castle and told aul
hen aul called one of the centurions unto him and said ring this young man unto the chief captain for he hath a certain thing to tell him
o he took him and brought him to the chief captain and said aul the prisoner called me unto him and prayed me to bring this young man unto thee who hath something to say unto thee
hen the chief captain took him by the hand and went with him aside privately and asked him hat is that thou hast to tell me
nd he said he ews have agreed to desire thee that thou wouldest bring down aul to morrow into the council as though they would enquire somewhat of him more perfectly
ut do not thou yield unto them for there lie in wait for him of them more than forty men which have bound themselves with an oath that they will neither eat nor drink till they have killed him and now are they ready looking for a promise from thee
o the chief captain then let the young man depart and charged him ee thou tell no man that thou hast shewed these things to me
nd he called unto him two centurions saying ake ready two hundred soldiers to go to  sarea and horsemen threescore and ten and spearmen two hundred at the third hour of the night and provide them beasts that they may set aul on and bring him safe unto elix the governor
nd he wrote a letter after this manner laudius ysias unto the most excellent governor elix sendeth greeting
his man was taken of the ews and should have been killed of them then came  with an army and rescued him having understood that he was a oman
nd when  would have known the cause wherefore they accused him  brought him forth into their council whom  perceived to be accused of questions of their law but to have nothing laid to his charge worthy of death or of bonds
nd when it was told me how that the ews laid wait for the man  sent straightway to thee and gave commandment to his accusers also to say before thee what they had against him
arewell
hen the soldiers as it was commanded them took aul and brought him by night to ntipatris
n the morrow they left the horsemen to go with him and returned to the castle who when they came to  sarea and delivered the epistle to the governor presented aul also before him
nd when the governor had read the letter he asked of what province he was
nd when he understood that he was of ilicia  will hear thee said he when thine accusers are also come
nd he commanded him to be kept in erods judgment hall
nd after five days nanias the high priest descended with the elders and with a certain orator named ertullus who informed the governor against aul
nd when he was called forth ertullus began to accuse him saying eeing that by thee we enjoy great quietness and that very worthy deeds are done unto this nation by thy providence we accept it always and in all places most noble elix with all thankfulness
otwithstanding that  be not further tedious unto thee  pray thee that thou wouldest hear us of thy clemency a few words
or we have found this man a pestilent fellow and a mover of sedition among all the ews throughout the world and a ringleader of the sect of the azarenes who also hath gone about to profane the temple whom we took and would have judged according to our law
ut the chief captain ysias came upon us and with great violence took him away out of our hands commanding his accusers to come unto thee by examining of whom thyself mayest take knowledge of all these things whereof we accuse him
nd the ews also assented saying that these things were so
hen aul after that the governor had beckoned unto him to speak answered orasmuch as  know that thou hast been of many years a judge unto this nation  do the more cheerfully answer for myself because that thou mayest understand that there are yet but twelve days since  went up to erusalem for to worship
nd they neither found me in the temple disputing with any man neither raising up the people neither in the synagogues nor in the city neither can they prove the things whereof they now accuse me
