ut this  confess unto thee that after the way which they call heresy so worship  the od of my fathers believing all things which are written in the law and in the prophets and have hope toward od which they themselves also allow that there shall be a resurrection of the dead both of the just and unjust
nd herein do  exercise myself to have always a conscience void of offence toward od and toward men
ow after many years  came to bring alms to my nation and offerings
hereupon certain ews from sia found me purified in the temple neither with multitude nor with tumult
ho ought to have been here before thee and object if they had ought against me
r else let these same here say if they have found any evil doing in me while  stood before the council except it be for this one voice that  cried standing among them ouching the resurrection of the dead  am called in question by you this day
nd when elix heard these things having more perfect knowledge of that way he deferred them and said hen ysias the chief captain shall come down  will know the uttermost of your matter
nd he commanded a centurion to keep aul and to let him have liberty and that he should forbid none of his acquaintance to minister or come unto him
nd after certain days when elix came with his wife rusilla which was a ewess he sent for aul and heard him concerning the faith in hrist
nd as he reasoned of righteousness temperance and judgment to come elix trembled and answered o thy way for this time when  have a convenient season  will call for thee
e hoped also that money should have been given him of aul that he might loose him wherefore he sent for him the oftener and communed with him
ut after two years orcius estus came into elix room and elix willing to shew the ews a pleasure left aul bound
ow when estus was come into the province after three days he ascended from  sarea to erusalem
hen the high priest and the chief of the ews informed him against aul and besought him and desired favour against him that he would send for him to erusalem laying wait in the way to kill him
ut estus answered that aul should be kept at  sarea and that he himself would depart shortly thither
et them therefore said he which among you are able go down with me and accuse this man if there be any wickedness in him
nd when he had tarried among them more than ten days he went down unto  sarea and the next day sitting on the judgment seat commanded aul to be brought
nd when he was come the ews which came down from erusalem stood round about and laid many and grievous complaints against aul which they could not prove
hile he answered for himself either against the law of the ews neither against the temple nor yet against  sar have  offended any thing at all
ut estus willing to do the ews a pleasure answered aul and said ilt thou go up to erusalem and there be judged of these things before me
hen said aul  stand at  sars judgment seat where  ought to be judged to the ews have  done no wrong as thou very well knowest
or if  be an offender or have committed any thing worthy of death  refuse not to die but if there be none of these things whereof these accuse me no man may deliver me unto them
 appeal unto  sar
hen estus when he had conferred with the council answered ast thou appealed unto  sar unto  sar shalt thou go
nd after certain days king grippa and ernice came unto  sarea to salute estus
nd when they had been there many days estus declared auls cause unto the king saying here is a certain man left in bonds by elix about whom when  was at erusalem the chief priests and the elders of the ews informed me desiring to have judgment against him
o whom  answered t is not the manner of the omans to deliver any man to die before that he which is accused have the accusers face to face and have licence to answer for himself concerning the crime laid against him
herefore when they were come hither without any delay on the morrow  sat on the judgment seat and commanded the man to be brought forth
gainst whom when the accusers stood up they brought none accusation of such things as  supposed but had certain questions against him of their own superstition and of one esus which was dead whom aul affirmed to be alive
nd because  doubted of such manner of questions  asked him whether he would go to erusalem and there be judged of these matters
ut when aul had appealed to be reserved unto the hearing of ugustus  commanded him to be kept till  might send him to  sar
hen grippa said unto estus  would also hear the man myself
o morrow said he thou shalt hear him
nd on the morrow when grippa was come and ernice with great pomp and was entered into the place of hearing with the chief captains and principal men of the city at estus commandment aul was brought forth
nd estus said ing grippa and all men which are here present with us ye see this man about whom all the multitude of the ews have dealt with me both at erusalem and also here crying that he ought not to live any longer
ut when  found that he had committed nothing worthy of death and that he himself hath appealed to ugustus  have determined to send him
f whom  have no certain thing to write unto my lord
herefore  have brought him forth before you and specially before thee  king grippa that after examination had  might have somewhat to write
or it seemeth to me unreasonable to send a prisoner and not withal to signify the crimes laid against him
hen grippa said unto aul hou art permitted to speak for thyself
hen aul stretched forth the hand and answered for himself  think myself happy king grippa because  shall answer for myself this day before thee touching all the things whereof  am accused of the ews especially because  know thee to be expert in all customs and questions which are among the ews wherefore  beseech thee to hear me patiently
y manner of life from my youth which was at the first among mine own nation at erusalem know all the ews which knew me from the beginning if they would testify that after the most straitest sect of our religion  lived a harisee
nd now  stand and am judged for the hope of the promise made of od unto our fathers unto which promise our twelve tribes instantly serving od day and night hope to come
or which hopes sake king grippa  am accused of the ews
hy should it be thought a thing incredible with you that od should raise the dead
 verily thought with myself that  ought to do many things contrary to the name of esus of azareth
hich thing  also did in erusalem and many of the saints did  shut up in prison having received authority from the chief priests and when they were put to death  gave my voice against them
nd  punished them oft in every synagogue and compelled them to blaspheme and being exceedingly mad against them  persecuted them even unto strange cities
hereupon as  went to amascus with authority and commission from the chief priests at midday  king  saw in the way a light from heaven above the brightness of the sun shining round about me and them which journeyed with me
nd when we were all fallen to the earth  heard a voice speaking unto me and saying in the ebrew tongue aul aul why persecutest thou me it is hard for thee to kick against the pricks
nd  said ho art thou ord
nd he said  am esus whom thou persecutest
ut rise and stand upon thy feet for  have appeared unto thee for this purpose to make thee a minister and a witness both of these things which thou hast seen and of those things in the which  will appear unto thee delivering thee from the people and from the entiles unto whom now  send thee to open their eyes and to turn them from darkness to light and from the power of atan unto od that they may receive forgiveness of sins and inheritance among them which are sanctified by faith that is in me
hereupon  king grippa  was not disobedient unto the heavenly vision but shewed first unto them of amascus and at erusalem and throughout all the coasts of ud a and then to the entiles that they should repent and turn to od and do works meet for repentance
or these causes the ews caught me in the temple and went about to kill me
aving therefore obtained help of od  continue unto this day witnessing both to small and great saying none other things than those which the prophets and oses did say should come that hrist should suffer and that he should be the first that should rise from the dead and should shew light unto the people and to the entiles
nd as he thus spake for himself estus said with a loud voice aul thou art beside thyself much learning doth make thee mad
ut he said  am not mad most noble estus but speak forth the words of truth and soberness
or the king knoweth of these things before whom also  speak freely for  am persuaded that none of these things are hidden from him for this thing was not done in a corner
ing grippa believest thou the prophets
 know that thou believest
hen grippa said unto aul lmost thou persuadest me to be a hristian
nd aul said  would to od that not only thou but also all that hear me this day were both almost and altogether such as  am except these bonds
nd when he had thus spoken the king rose up and the governor and ernice and they that sat with them and when they were gone aside they talked between themselves saying his man doeth nothing worthy of death or of bonds
hen said grippa unto estus his man might have been set at liberty if he had not appealed unto  sar
nd when it was determined that we should sail into taly they delivered aul and certain other prisoners unto one named ulius a centurion of ugustus band
nd entering into a ship of dramyttium we launched meaning to sail by the coasts of sia one ristarchus a acedonian of hessalonica being with us
nd the next day we touched at idon
nd ulius courteously entreated aul and gave him liberty to go unto his friends to refresh himself
nd when we had launched from thence we sailed under yprus because the winds were contrary
nd when we had sailed over the sea of ilicia and amphylia we came to yra a city of ycia
nd there the centurion found a ship of lexandria sailing into taly and he put us therein
nd when we had sailed slowly many days and scarce were come over against nidus the wind not suffering us we sailed under rete over against almone and hardly passing it came unto a place which is called he fair havens nigh whereunto was the city of asea
ow when much time was spent and when sailing was now dangerous because the fast was now already past aul admonished them and said unto them irs  perceive that this voyage will be with hurt and much damage not only of the lading and ship but also of our lives
evertheless the centurion believed the master and the owner of the ship more than those things which were spoken by aul
nd because the haven was not commodious to winter in the more part advised to depart thence also if by any means they might attain to henice and there to winter which is an haven of rete and lieth toward the south west and north west
nd when the south wind blew softly supposing that they had obtained their purpose loosing thence they sailed close by rete
ut not long after there arose against it a tempestuous wind called uroclydon
nd when the ship was caught and could not bear up into the wind we let her drive
nd running under a certain island which is called lauda we had much work to come by the boat which when they had taken up they used helps undergirding the ship and fearing lest they should fall into the quicksands strake sail and so were driven
nd we being exceedingly tossed with a tempest the next day they lightened the ship and the third day we cast out with our own hands the tackling of the ship
nd when neither sun nor stars in many days appeared and no small tempest lay on us all hope that we should be saved was then taken away
ut after long abstinence aul stood forth in the midst of them and said irs ye should have hearkened unto me and not have loosed from rete and to have gained this harm and loss
nd now  exhort you to be of good cheer for there shall be no loss of any mans life among you but of the ship
or there stood by me this night the angel of od whose  am and whom  serve saying ear not aul thou must be brought before  sar and lo od hath given thee all them that sail with thee
herefore sirs be of good cheer for  believe od that it shall be even as it was told me
owbeit we must be cast upon a certain island
ut when the fourteenth night was come as we were driven up and down in dria about midnight the shipmen deemed that they drew near to some country and sounded and found it twenty fathoms and when they had gone a little further they sounded again and found it fifteen fathoms
hen fearing lest we should have fallen upon rocks they cast four anchors out of the stern and wished for the day
nd as the shipmen were about to flee out of the ship when they had let down the boat into the sea under colour as though they would have cast anchors out of the foreship aul said to the centurion and to the soldiers xcept these abide in the ship ye cannot be saved
hen the soldiers cut off the ropes of the boat and let her fall off
nd while the day was coming on aul besought them all to take meat saying his day is the fourteenth day that ye have tarried and continued fasting having taken nothing
herefore  pray you to take some meat for this is for your health for there shall not an hair fall from the head of any of you
nd when he had thus spoken he took bread and gave thanks to od in presence of them all and when he had broken it he began to eat
hen were they all of good cheer and they also took some meat
nd we were in all in the ship two hundred threescore and sixteen souls
nd when they had eaten enough they lightened the ship and cast out the wheat into the sea
nd when it was day they knew not the land but they discovered a certain creek with a shore into the which they were minded if it were possible to thrust in the ship
nd when they had taken up the anchors they committed themselves unto the sea and loosed the rudder bands and hoised up the mainsail to the wind and made toward shore
nd falling into a place where two seas met they ran the ship aground and the forepart stuck fast and remained unmoveable but the hinder part was broken with the violence of the waves
nd the soldiers counsel was to kill the prisoners lest any of them should swim out and escape
ut the centurion willing to save aul kept them from their purpose and commanded that they which could swim should cast themselves first into the sea and get to land and the rest some on boards and some on broken pieces of the ship
nd so it came to pass that they escaped all safe to land
nd when they were escaped then they knew that the island was called elita
nd the barbarous people shewed us no little kindness for they kindled a fire and received us every one because of the present rain and because of the cold
nd when aul had gathered a bundle of sticks and laid them on the fire there came a viper out of the heat and fastened on his hand
nd when the barbarians saw the venomous beast hang on his hand they said among themselves o doubt this man is a murderer whom though he hath escaped the sea yet vengeance suffereth not to live
nd he shook off the beast into the fire and felt no harm
owbeit they looked when he should have swollen or fallen down dead suddenly but after they had looked a great while and saw no harm come to him they changed their minds and said that he was a god
n the same quarters were possessions of the chief man of the island whose name was ublius who received us and lodged us three days courteously
nd it came to pass that the father of ublius lay sick of a fever and of a bloody flux to whom aul entered in and prayed and laid his hands on him and healed him
o when this was done others also which had diseases in the island came and were healed who also honoured us with many honours and when we departed they laded us with such things as were necessary
nd after three months we departed in a ship of lexandria which had wintered in the isle whose sign was astor and ollux
nd landing at yracuse we tarried there three days
nd from thence we fetched a compass and came to hegium and after one day the south wind blew and we came the next day to uteoli where we found brethren and were desired to tarry with them seven days and so we went toward ome
nd from thence when the brethren heard of us they came to meet us as far as ppii forum and he three taverns whom when aul saw he thanked od and took courage
nd when we came to ome the centurion delivered the prisoners to the captain of the guard but aul was suffered to dwell by himself with a soldier that kept him
nd it came to pass that after three days aul called the chief of the ews together and when they were come together he said unto them en and brethren though  have committed nothing against the people or customs of our fathers yet was  delivered prisoner from erusalem into the hands of the omans
ho when they had examined me would have let me go because there was no cause of death in me
ut when the ews spake against it  was constrained to appeal unto  sar not that  had ought to accuse my nation of
or this cause therefore have  called for you to see you and to speak with you because that for the hope of srael  am bound with this chain
nd they said unto him e neither received letters out of ud a concerning thee neither any of the brethren that came shewed or spake any harm of thee
ut we desire to hear of thee what thou thinkest for as concerning this sect we know that every where it is spoken against
nd when they had appointed him a day there came many to him into his lodging to whom he expounded and testified the kingdom of od persuading them concerning esus both out of the law of oses and out of the prophets from morning till evening
nd some believed the things which were spoken and some believed not
nd when they agreed not among themselves they departed after that aul had spoken one word ell spake the oly host by saias the prophet unto our fathers saying o unto this people and say earing ye shall hear and shall not understand nd seeing ye shall see and not perceive or the heart of this people is waxed gross nd their ears are dull of hearing nd their eyes have they closed est they should see with their eyes nd hear with their ears nd understand with their heart nd should be converted nd  should heal them
e it known therefore unto you that the salvation of od is sent unto the entiles and that they will hear it
nd when he had said these words the ews departed and had great reasoning among themselves
nd aul dwelt two whole years in his own hired house and received all that came in unto him preaching the kingdom of od and teaching those things which concern the ord esus hrist with all confidence no man forbidding him
aul a servant of esus hrist called to be an apostle separated unto the gospel of od which he had promised afore by his prophets in the holy scriptures  concerning his on esus hrist our ord which was made of the seed of avid according to the flesh and declared to be the on of od with power according to the spirit of holiness by the resurrection from the dead by whom we have received grace and apostleship for obedience to the faith among all nations for his name among whom are ye also the called of esus hrist to all that be in ome beloved of od called to be saints race to you and peace from od our ather and the ord esus hrist
irst  thank my od through esus hrist for you all that your faith is spoken of throughout the whole world
or od is my witness whom  serve with my spirit in the gospel of his on that without ceasing  make mention of you always in my prayers making request if by any means now at length  might have a prosperous journey by the will of od to come unto you
or  long to see you that  may impart unto you some spiritual gift to the end ye may be established that is that  may be comforted together with you by the mutual faith both of you and me
ow  would not have you ignorant brethren that oftentimes  purposed to come unto you but was let hitherto  that  might have some fruit among you also even as among other entiles
 am debtor both to the reeks and to the arbarians both to the wise and to the unwise
o as much as in me is  am ready to preach the gospel to you that are at ome also
or  am not ashamed of the gospel of hrist for it is the power of od unto salvation to every one that believeth to the ew first and also to the reek
or therein is the righteousness of od revealed from faith to faith as it is written he just shall live by faith
or the wrath of od is revealed from heaven against all ungodliness and unrighteousness of men who hold the truth in unrighteousness because that which may be known of od is manifest in them for od hath shewed it unto them
or the invisible things of him from the creation of the world are clearly seen being understood by the things that are made even his eternal power and odhead so that they are without excuse because that when they knew od they glorified him not as od neither were thankful but became vain in their imaginations and their foolish heart was darkened
rofessing themselves to be wise they became fools nd changed the glory of the uncorruptible od into an image made like to corruptible man and to birds and fourfooted beasts and creeping things
herefore od also gave them up to uncleanness through the lusts of their own hearts to dishonour their own bodies between themselves who changed the truth of od into a lie and worshipped and served the creature more than the reator who is blessed for ever
men
or this cause od gave them up unto vile affections for even their women did change the natural use into that which is against nature and likewise also the men leaving the natural use of the woman burned in their lust one toward another men with men working that which is unseemly and receiving in themselves that recompence of their error which was meet
nd even as they did not like to retain od in their knowledge od gave them over to a reprobate mind to do those things which are not convenient being filled with all unrighteousness fornication wickedness covetousness maliciousness full of envy murder debate deceit malignity whisperers backbiters haters of od despiteful proud boasters inventors of evil things disobedient to parents without understanding covenantbreakers without natural affection implacable unmerciful who knowing the judgment of od that they which commit such things are worthy of death not only do the same but have pleasure in them that do them
herefore thou art inexcusable  man whosoever thou art that judgest for wherein thou judgest another thou condemnest thyself for thou that judgest doest the same things
ut we are sure that the judgment of od is according to truth against them which commit such things
nd thinkest thou this  man that judgest them which do such things and doest the same that thou shalt escape the judgment of od
r despisest thou the riches of his goodness and forbearance and longsuffering not knowing that the goodness of od leadeth thee to repentance
ut after thy hardness and impenitent heart treasurest up unto thyself wrath against the day of wrath and revelation of the righteous judgment of od ho will render to every man according to his deeds to them who by patient continuance in well doing seek for glory and honour and immortality eternal life but unto them that are contentious and do not obey the truth but obey unrighteousness indignation and wrath tribulation and anguish upon every soul of man that doeth evil of the ew first and also of the entile but glory honour and peace to every man that worketh good to the ew first and also to the entile for there is no respect of persons with od
or as many as have sinned without law shall also perish without law and as many as have sinned in the law shall be judged by the law for not the hearers of the law are just before od but the doers of the law shall be justified
or when the entiles which have not the law do by nature the things contained in the law these having not the law are a law unto themselves which shew the work of the law written in their hearts their conscience also bearing witness and their thoughts the mean while accusing or else excusing one another in the day when od shall judge the secrets of men by esus hrist according to my gospel
ehold thou art called a ew and restest in the law and makest thy boast of od and knowest his will and approvest the things that are more excellent being instructed out of the law and art confident that thou thyself art a guide of the blind a light of them which are in darkness an instructor of the foolish a teacher of babes which hast the form of knowledge and of the truth in the law
hou therefore which teachest another teachest thou not thyself thou that preachest a man should not steal dost thou steal
hou that sayest a man should not commit adultery dost thou commit adultery thou that abhorrest idols dost thou commit sacrilege
hou that makest thy boast of the law through breaking the law dishonourest thou od
or the name of od is blasphemed among the entiles through you as it is written
or circumcision verily profiteth if thou keep the law but if thou be a breaker of the law thy circumcision is made uncircumcision
herefore if the uncircumcision keep the righteousness of the law shall not his uncircumcision be counted for circumcision
nd shall not uncircumcision which is by nature if it fulfil the law judge thee who by the letter and circumcision dost transgress the law
or he is not a ew which is one outwardly neither is that circumcision which is outward in the flesh but he is a ew which is one inwardly and circumcision is that of the heart in the spirit and not in the letter whose praise is not of men but of od
hat advantage then hath the ew or what profit is there of circumcision
uch every way chiefly because that unto them were committed the oracles of od
or what if some did not believe shall their unbelief make the faith of od without effect
od forbid yea let od be true but every man a liar as it is written hat thou mightest be justified in thy sayings nd mightest overcome when thou art judged
ut if our unrighteousness commend the righteousness of od what shall we say
s od unrighteous who taketh vengeance
 speak as a man od forbid for then how shall od judge the world
or if the truth of od hath more abounded through my lie unto his glory why yet am  also judged as a sinner
nd not rather as we be slanderously reported and as some affirm that we say  et us do evil that good may come whose damnation is just
hat then are we better than they
o in no wise for we have before proved both ews and entiles that they are all under sin as it is written here is none righteous no not one here is none that understandeth here is none that seeketh after od
hey are all gone out of the way they are together become unprofitable here is none that doeth good no not one
heir throat is an open sepulchre ith their tongues they have used deceit he poison of asps is under their lips hose mouth is full of cursing and bitterness heir feet are swift to shed blood estruction and misery are in their ways nd the way of peace have they not known here is no fear of od before their eyes
ow we know that what things soever the law saith it saith to them who are under the law that every mouth may be stopped and all the world may become guilty before od
herefore by the deeds of the law there shall no flesh be justified in his sight for by the law is the knowledge of sin
ut now the righteousness of od without the law is manifested being witnessed by the law and the prophets even the righteousness of od which is by faith of esus hrist unto all and upon all them that believe for there is no difference for all have sinned and come short of the glory of od being justified freely by his grace through the redemption that is in hrist esus whom od hath set forth to be a propitiation through faith in his blood to declare his righteousness for the remission of sins that are past through the forbearance of od to declare  say at this time his righteousness that he might be just and the justifier of him which believeth in esus
here is boasting then
t is excluded
y what law of works
ay but by the law of faith
herefore we conclude that a man is justified by faith without the deeds of the law
s he the od of the ews only is he not also of the entiles
es of the entiles also seeing it is one od which shall justify the circumcision by faith and uncircumcision through faith
o we then make void the law through faith
od forbid yea we establish the law
hat shall we say then that braham our father as pertaining to the flesh hath found
or if braham were justified by works he hath whereof to glory but not before od
or what saith the scripture
braham believed od and it was counted unto him for righteousness
ow to him that worketh is the reward not reckoned of grace but of debt
ut to him that worketh not but believeth on him that justifieth the ungodly his faith is counted for righteousness
ven as avid also describeth the blessedness of the man unto whom od imputeth righteousness without works saying lessed are they whose iniquities are forgiven nd whose sins are covered
lessed is the man to whom the ord will not impute sin
ometh this blessedness then upon the circumcision only or upon the uncircumcision also for we say that faith was reckoned to braham for righteousness
ow was it then reckoned when he was in circumcision or in uncircumcision
ot in circumcision but in uncircumcision
nd he received the sign of circumcision a seal of the righteousness of the faith which he had yet being uncircumcised that he might be the father of all them that believe though they be not circumcised that righteousness might be imputed unto them also and the father of circumcision to them who are not of the circumcision only but who also walk in the steps of that faith of our father braham which he had being yet uncircumcised
or the promise that he should be the heir of the world was not to braham or to his seed through the law but through the righteousness of faith
or if they which are of the law be heirs faith is made void and the promise made of none effect because the law worketh wrath for where no law is there is no transgression
herefore it is of faith that it might be by grace to the end the promise might be sure to all the seed not to that only which is of the law but to that also which is of the faith of braham who is the father of us all as it is written  have made thee a father of many nations  before him whom he believed even od who quickeneth the dead and calleth those things which be not as though they were
ho against hope believed in hope that he might become the father of many nations according to that which was spoken o shall thy seed be
nd being not weak in faith he considered not his own body now dead when he was about an hundred years old neither yet the deadness of arahs womb he staggered not at the promise of od through unbelief but was strong in faith giving glory to od and being fully persuaded that what he had promised he was able also to perform
nd therefore it was imputed to him for righteousness
ow it was not written for his sake alone that it was imputed to him but for us also to whom it shall be imputed if we believe on him that raised up esus our ord from the dead who was delivered for our offences and was raised again for our justification
herefore being justified by faith we have peace with od through our ord esus hrist by whom also we have access by faith into this grace wherein we stand and rejoice in hope of the glory of od
nd not only so but we glory in tribulations also knowing that tribulation worketh patience and patience experience and experience hope and hope maketh not ashamed because the love of od is shed abroad in our hearts by the oly host which is given unto us
or when we were yet without strength in due time hrist died for the ungodly
or scarcely for a righteous man will one die yet peradventure for a good man some would even dare to die
ut od commendeth his love toward us in that while we were yet sinners hrist died for us
uch more then being now justified by his blood we shall be saved from wrath through him
or if when we were enemies we were reconciled to od by the death of his on much more being reconciled we shall be saved by his life
nd not only so but we also joy in od through our ord esus hrist by whom we have now received the atonement
herefore as by one man sin entered into the world and death by sin and so death passed upon all men for that all have sinned for until the law sin was in the world but sin is not imputed when there is no law
evertheless death reigned from dam to oses even over them that had not sinned after the similitude of dams transgression who is the figure of him that was to come
ut not as the offence so also is the free gift
or if through the offence of one many be dead much more the grace of od and the gift by grace which is by one man esus hrist hath abounded unto many
nd not as it was by one that sinned so is the gift for the judgment was by one to condemnation but the free gift is of many offences unto justification
or if by one mans offence death reigned by one much more they which receive abundance of grace and of the gift of righteousness shall reign in life by one esus hrist herefore as by the offence of one judgment came upon all men to condemnation even so by the righteousness of one the free gift came upon all men unto justification of life
or as by one mans disobedience many were made sinners so by the obedience of one shall many be made righteous
oreover the law entered that the offence might abound
ut where sin abounded grace did much more abound that as sin hath reigned unto death even so might grace reign through righteousness unto eternal life by esus hrist our ord
hat shall we say then
hall we continue in sin that grace may abound
od forbid
ow shall we that are dead to sin live any longer therein
now ye not that so many of us as were baptized into esus hrist were baptized into his death
herefore we are buried with him by baptism into death that like as hrist was raised up from the dead by the glory of the ather even so we also should walk in newness of life
or if we have been planted together in the likeness of his death we shall be also in the likeness of his resurrection knowing this that our old man is crucified with him that the body of sin might be destroyed that henceforth we should not serve sin
or he that is dead is freed from sin
ow if we be dead with hrist we believe that we shall also live with him knowing that hrist being raised from the dead dieth no more death hath no more dominion over him
or in that he died he died unto sin once but in that he liveth he liveth unto od
ikewise reckon ye also yourselves to be dead indeed unto sin but alive unto od through esus hrist our ord
et not sin therefore reign in your mortal body that ye should obey it in the lusts thereof
either yield ye your members as instruments of unrighteousness unto sin but yield yourselves unto od as those that are alive from the dead and your members as instruments of righteousness unto od
or sin shall not have dominion over you for ye are not under the law but under grace
hat then shall we sin because we are not under the law but under grace
od forbid
now ye not that to whom ye yield yourselves servants to obey his servants ye are to whom ye obey whether of sin unto death or of obedience unto righteousness
ut od be thanked that ye were the servants of sin but ye have obeyed from the heart that form of doctrine which was delivered you
eing then made free from sin ye became the servants of righteousness
 speak after the manner of men because of the infirmity of your flesh for as ye have yielded your members servants to uncleanness and to iniquity unto iniquity even so now yield your members servants to righteousness unto holiness
or when ye were the servants of sin ye were free from righteousness
hat fruit had ye then in those things whereof ye are now ashamed for the end of those things is death
ut now being made free from sin and become servants to od ye have your fruit unto holiness and the end everlasting life
or the wages of sin is death but the gift of od is eternal life through esus hrist our ord
now ye not brethren for  speak to them that know the law  how that the law hath dominion over a man as long as he liveth
or the woman which hath an husband is bound by the law to her husband so long as he liveth but if the husband be dead she is loosed from the law of her husband
o then if while her husband liveth she be married to another man she shall be called an adulteress but if her husband be dead she is free from that law so that she is no adulteress though she be married to another man
herefore my brethren ye also are become dead to the law by the body of hrist that ye should be married to another even to him who is raised from the dead that we should bring forth fruit unto od
or when we were in the flesh the motions of sins which were by the law did work in our members to bring forth fruit unto death
ut now we are delivered from the law that being dead wherein we were held that we should serve in newness of spirit and not in the oldness of the letter
hat shall we say then
s the law sin
od forbid
ay  had not known sin but by the law for  had not known lust except the law had said hou shalt not covet
ut sin taking occasion by the commandment wrought in me all manner of concupiscence
or without the law sin was dead
or  was alive without the law once but when the commandment came sin revived and  died
nd the commandment which was ordained to life  found to be unto death
or sin taking occasion by the commandment deceived me and by it slew me
herefore the law is holy and the commandment holy and just and good
as then that which is good made death unto me
od forbid
ut sin that it might appear sin working death in me by that which is good that sin by the commandment might become exceeding sinful
or we know that the law is spiritual but  am carnal sold under sin
or that which  do  allow not for what  would that do  not but what  hate that do 
f then  do that which  would not  consent unto the law that it is good
ow then it is no more  that do it but sin that dwelleth in me
or  know that in me that is in my flesh  dwelleth no good thing for to will is present with me but how to perform that which is good  find not
or the good that  would  do not but the evil which  would not that  do
ow if  do that  would not it is no more  that do it but sin that dwelleth in me
 find then a law that when  would do good evil is present with me
or  delight in the law of od after the inward man but  see another law in my members warring against the law of my mind and bringing me into captivity to the law of sin which is in my members
 wretched man that  am who shall deliver me from the body of this death
 thank od through esus hrist our ord
o then with the mind  myself serve the law of od but with the flesh the law of sin
here is therefore now no condemnation to them which are in hrist esus who walk not after the flesh but after the pirit
or the law of the pirit of life in hrist esus hath made me free from the law of sin and death
or what the law could not do in that it was weak through the flesh od sending his own on in the likeness of sinful flesh and for sin condemned sin in the flesh that the righteousness of the law might be fulfilled in us who walk not after the flesh but after the pirit
or they that are after the flesh do mind the things of the flesh but they that are after the pirit the things of the pirit
or to be carnally minded is death but to be spiritually minded is life and peace
ecause the carnal mind is enmity against od for it is not subject to the law of od neither indeed can be
o then they that are in the flesh cannot please od
ut ye are not in the flesh but in the pirit if so be that the pirit of od dwell in you
ow if any man have not the pirit of hrist he is none of his
nd if hrist be in you the body is dead because of sin but the pirit is life because of righteousness
ut if the pirit of him that raised up esus from the dead dwell in you he that raised up hrist from the dead shall also quicken your mortal bodies by his pirit that dwelleth in you
herefore brethren we are debtors not to the flesh to live after the flesh
or if ye live after the flesh ye shall die but if ye through the pirit do mortify the deeds of the body ye shall live
or as many as are led by the pirit of od they are the sons of od
or ye have not received the spirit of bondage again to fear but ye have received the pirit of adoption whereby we cry bba ather
he pirit itself beareth witness with our spirit that we are the children of od and if children then heirs heirs of od and jointheirs with hrist if so be that we suffer with him that we may be also glorified together
or  reckon that the sufferings of this present time are not worthy to be compared with the glory which shall be revealed in us
or the earnest expectation of the creature waiteth for the manifestation of the sons of od
or the creature was made subject to vanity not willingly but by reason of him who hath subjected the same in hope because the creature itself also shall be delivered from the bondage of corruption into the glorious liberty of the children of od
or we know that the whole creation groaneth and travaileth in pain together until now
nd not only they but ourselves also which have the firstfruits of the pirit even we ourselves groan within ourselves waiting for the adoption to wit the redemption of our body
or we are saved by hope but hope that is seen is not hope for what a man seeth why doth he yet hope for
ut if we hope for that we see not then do we with patience wait for it
ikewise the pirit also helpeth our infirmities for we know not what we should pray for as we ought but the pirit itself maketh intercession for us with groanings which cannot be uttered
nd he that searcheth the hearts knoweth what is the mind of the pirit because he maketh intercession for the saints according to the will of od
nd we know that all things work together for good to them that love od to them who are the called according to his purpose
or whom he did foreknow he also did predestinate to be conformed to the image of his on that he might be the firstborn among many brethren
oreover whom he did predestinate them he also called and whom he called them he also justified and whom he justified them he also glorified
hat shall we then say to these things
f od be for us who can be against us
e that spared not his own on but delivered him up for us all how shall he not with him also freely give us all things
ho shall lay any thing to the charge of ods elect
t is od that justifieth
ho is he that condemneth
t is hrist that died yea rather that is risen again who is even at the right hand of od who also maketh intercession for us
ho shall separate us from the love of hrist shall tribulation or distress or persecution or famine or nakedness or peril or sword
s it is written or thy sake we are killed all the day long e are accounted as sheep for the slaughter
ay in all these things we are more than conquerors through him that loved us
or  am persuaded that neither death nor life nor angels nor principalities nor powers nor things present nor things to come nor height nor depth nor any other creature shall be able to separate us from the love of od which is in hrist esus our ord
 say the truth in hrist  lie not my conscience also bearing me witness in the oly host that  have great heaviness and continual sorrow in my heart
or  could wish that myself were accursed from hrist for my brethren my kinsmen according to the flesh who are sraelites to whom pertaineth the adoption and the glory and the covenants and the giving of the law and the service of od and the promises whose are the fathers and of whom as concerning the flesh hrist came who is over all od blessed for ever

ot as though the word of od hath taken none effect
or they are not all srael which are of srael neither because they are the seed of braham are they all children but n saac shall thy seed be called

or this is the word of promise t this time will  come and arah shall have a son
nd not only this but when ebecca also had conceived by one even by our father saac for the children being not yet born neither having done any good or evil that the purpose of od according to election might stand not of works but of him that calleth it was said unto her he elder shall serve the younger
s it is written acob have  loved but sau have  hated

or he saith to oses  will have mercy on whom  will have mercy and  will have compassion on whom  will have compassion

herefore hath he mercy on whom he will have mercy and whom he will he hardeneth
hou wilt say then unto me hy doth he yet find fault
or who hath resisted his will
ay but  man who art thou that repliest against od
hall the thing formed say to him that formed it hy hast thou made me thus
ath not the potter power over the clay of the same lump to make one vessel unto honour and another unto dishonour
hat if od willing to shew his wrath and to make his power known endured with much longsuffering the vessels of wrath fitted to destruction and that he might make known the riches of his glory on the vessels of mercy which he had afore prepared unto glory even us whom he hath called not of the ews only but also of the entiles
s he saith also in see  will call them my people which were not my people nd her beloved which was not beloved
nd it shall come to pass that in the place where it was said unto them e are not my people here shall they be called the children of the living od
saias also crieth concerning srael hough the number of the children of srael be as the sand of the sea a remnant shall be saved for he will finish the work and cut it short in righteousness because a short work will the ord make upon the earth
nd as saias said before xcept the ord of abaoth had left us a seed e had been as odoma and been made like unto omorrha
hat shall we say then
hat the entiles which followed not after righteousness have attained to righteousness even the righteousness which is of faith
ut srael which followed after the law of righteousness hath not attained to the law of righteousness
herefore
ecause they sought it not by faith but as it were by the works of the law
or they stumbled at that stumblingstone as it is written ehold  lay in ion a stumblingstone and rock of offence nd whosoever believeth on him shall not be ashamed
rethren my hearts desire and prayer to od for srael is that they might be saved
or  bear them record that they have a zeal of od but not according to knowledge
or they being ignorant of ods righteousness and going about to establish their own righteousness have not submitted themselves unto the righteousness of od
or hrist is the end of the law for righteousness to every one that believeth
or oses describeth the righteousness which is of the law hat the man which doeth those things shall live by them
ut the righteousness which is of faith speaketh on this wise ay not in thine heart ho shall ascend into heaven
that is to bring hrist down from above or ho shall descend into the deep
that is to bring up hrist again from the dead ut what saith it
he word is nigh thee even in thy mouth and in thy heart that is the word of faith which we preach that if thou shalt confess with thy mouth the ord esus and shalt believe in thine heart that od hath raised him from the dead thou shalt be saved
or with the heart man believeth unto righteousness and with the mouth confession is made unto salvation
or the scripture saith hosoever believeth on him shall not be ashamed
or there is no difference between the ew and the reek for the same ord over all is rich unto all that call upon him
or whosoever shall call upon the name of the ord shall be saved
ow then shall they call on him in whom they have not believed and how shall they believe in him of whom they have not heard and how shall they hear without a preacher
nd how shall they preach except they be sent as it is written ow beautiful are the feet of them that preach the gospel of peace and bring glad tidings of good things
ut they have not all obeyed the gospel
or saias saith ord who hath believed our report
o then faith cometh by hearing and hearing by the word of od
ut  say ave they not heard
es verily heir sound went into all the earth nd their words unto the ends of the world
ut  say id not srael know
irst oses saith  will provoke you to jealousy by them that are no people nd by a foolish nation  will anger you
ut saias is very bold and saith  was found of them that sought me not  was made manifest unto them that asked not after me
ut to srael he saith ll day long  have stretched forth my hands unto a disobedient and gainsaying people
 say then ath od cast away his people
od forbid
or  also am an sraelite of the seed of braham of the tribe of enjamin
od hath not cast away his people which he foreknew
ot ye not what the scripture saith of lias how he maketh intercession to od against srael saying ord they have killed thy prophets and digged down thine altars and  am left alone and they seek my life
ut what saith the answer of od unto him
 have reserved to myself seven thousand men who have not bowed the knee to the image of aal
ven so then at this present time also there is a remnant according to the election of grace
nd if by grace then is it no more of works otherwise grace is no more grace
ut if it be of works then is it no more grace otherwise work is no more work
hat then
srael hath not obtained that which he seeketh for but the election hath obtained it and the rest were blinded according as it is written od hath given them the spirit of slumber eyes that they should not see and ears that they should not hear unto this day
nd avid saith et their table be made a snare and a trap nd a stumblingblock and a recompence unto them et their eyes be darkened that they may not see nd bow down their back alway
 say then ave they stumbled that they should fall
od forbid but rather through their fall salvation is come unto the entiles for to provoke them to jealousy
ow if the fall of them be the riches of the world and the diminishing of them the riches of the entiles how much more their fulness
or  speak to you entiles inasmuch as  am the apostle of the entiles  magnify mine office if by any means  may provoke to emulation them which are my flesh and might save some of them
or if the casting away of them be the reconciling of the world what shall the receiving of them be but life from the dead
or if the firstfruit be holy the lump is also holy and if the root be holy so are the branches
nd if some of the branches be broken off and thou being a wild olive tree wert graffed in among them and with them partakest of the root and fatness of the olive tree boast not against the branches
ut if thou boast thou bearest not the root but the root thee
hou wilt say then he branches were broken off that  might be graffed in
ell because of unbelief they were broken off and thou standest by faith
e not highminded but fear for if od spared not the natural branches take heed lest he also spare not thee
ehold therefore the goodness and severity of od on them which fell severity but toward thee goodness if thou continue in his goodness otherwise thou also shalt be cut off
nd they also if they abide not still in unbelief shall be graffed in for od is able to graff them in again
or if thou wert cut out of the olive tree which is wild by nature and wert graffed contrary to nature into a good olive tree how much more shall these which be the natural branches be graffed into their own olive tree
or  would not brethren that ye should be ignorant of this mystery lest ye should be wise in your own conceits that blindness in part is happened to srael until the fulness of the entiles be come in
nd so all srael shall be saved as it is written here shall come out of ion the eliverer nd shall turn away ungodliness from acob or this is my covenant unto them hen  shall take away their sins
s concerning the gospel they are enemies for your sakes but as touching the election they are beloved for the fathers sakes
or the gifts and calling of od are without repentance
or as ye in times past have not believed od yet have now obtained mercy through their unbelief even so have these also now not believed that through your mercy they also may obtain mercy
or od hath concluded them all in unbelief that he might have mercy upon all
 the depth of the riches both of the wisdom and knowledge of od how unsearchable are his judgments and his ways past finding out
or who hath known the mind of the ord or who hath been his counsellor
r who hath first given to him and it shall be recompensed unto him again
or of him and through him and to him are all things to whom be glory for ever
men
 beseech you therefore brethren by the mercies of od that ye present your bodies a living sacrifice holy acceptable unto od which is your reasonable service
nd be not conformed to this world but be ye transformed by the renewing of your mind that ye may prove what is that good and acceptable and perfect will of od
or  say through the grace given unto me to every man that is among you not to think of himself more highly than he ought to think but to think soberly according as od hath dealt to every man the measure of faith
or as we have many members in one body and all members have not the same office so we being many are one body in hrist and every one members one of another
aving then gifts differing according to the grace that is given to us whether prophecy let us prophesy according to the proportion of faith or ministry let us wait on our ministering or he that teacheth on teaching or he that exhorteth on exhortation he that giveth let him do it with simplicity he that ruleth with diligence he that sheweth mercy with cheerfulness
et love be without dissimulation
bhor that which is evil cleave to that which is good
e kindly affectioned one to another with brotherly love in honour preferring one another not slothful in business fervent in spirit serving the ord rejoicing in hope patient in tribulation continuing instant in prayer distributing to the necessity of saints given to hospitality
less them which persecute you bless and curse not
ejoice with them that do rejoice and weep with them that weep
e of the same mind one toward another
ind not high things but condescend to men of low estate
e not wise in your own conceits
ecompense to no man evil for evil
rovide things honest in the sight of all men
f it be possible as much as lieth in you live peaceably with all men
early beloved avenge not yourselves but rather give place unto wrath for it is written engeance is mine  will repay saith the ord
herefore if thine enemy hunger feed him if he thirst give him drink for in so doing thou shalt heap coals of fire on his head
e not overcome of evil but overcome evil with good
et every soul be subject unto the higher powers
or there is no power but of od the powers that be are ordained of od
hosoever therefore resisteth the power resisteth the ordinance of od and they that resist shall receive to themselves damnation
or rulers are not a terror to good works but to the evil
ilt thou then not be afraid of the power do that which is good and thou shalt have praise of the same for he is the minister of od to thee for good
ut if thou do that which is evil be afraid for he beareth not the sword in vain for he is the minister of od a revenger to execute wrath upon him that doeth evil
herefore ye must needs be subject not only for wrath but also for conscience sake
or for this cause pay ye tribute also for they are ods ministers attending continually upon this very thing
ender therefore to all their dues tribute to whom tribute is due custom to whom custom fear to whom fear honour to whom honour
we no man any thing but to love one another for he that loveth another hath fulfilled the law
or this hou shalt not commit adultery hou shalt not kill hou shalt not steal hou shalt not bear false witness hou shalt not covet and if there be any other commandment it is briefly comprehended in this saying namely hou shalt love thy neighbour as thyself
ove worketh no ill to his neighbour therefore love is the fulfilling of the law
nd that knowing the time that now it is high time to awake out of sleep for now is our salvation nearer than when we believed
he night is far spent the day is at hand let us therefore cast off the works of darkness and let us put on the armour of light
et us walk honestly as in the day not in rioting and drunkenness not in chambering and wantonness not in strife and envying
ut put ye on the ord esus hrist and make not provision for the flesh to fulfil the lusts thereof
im that is weak in the faith receive ye but not to doubtful disputations
or one believeth that he may eat all things another who is weak eateth herbs
et not him that eateth despise him that eateth not and let not him which eateth not judge him that eateth for od hath received him
ho art thou that judgest another mans servant to his own master he standeth or falleth
ea he shall be holden up for od is able to make him stand
ne man esteemeth one day above another another esteemeth every day alike
et every man be fully persuaded in his own mind
e that regardeth the day regardeth it unto the ord and he that regardeth not the day to the ord he doth not regard it
e that eateth eateth to the ord for he giveth od thanks and he that eateth not to the ord he eateth not and giveth od thanks
or none of us liveth to himself and no man dieth to himself
or whether we live we live unto the ord and whether we die we die unto the ord whether we live therefore or die we are the ords
or to this end hrist both died and rose and revived that he might be ord both of the dead and living
ut why dost thou judge thy brother or why dost thou set at nought thy brother for we shall all stand before the judgment seat of hrist
or it is written s  live saith the ord every knee shall bow to me nd every tongue shall confess to od
o then every one of us shall give account of himself to od
et us not therefore judge one another any more but judge this rather that no man put a stumblingblock or an occasion to fall in his brothers way
 know and am persuaded by the ord esus that there is nothing unclean of itself but to him that esteemeth any thing to be unclean to him it is unclean
ut if thy brother be grieved with thy meat now walkest thou not charitably
estroy not him with thy meat for whom hrist died
et not then your good be evil spoken of for the kingdom of od is not meat and drink but righteousness and peace and joy in the oly host
or he that in these things serveth hrist is acceptable to od and approved of men
et us therefore follow after the things which make for peace and things wherewith one may edify another
or meat destroy not the work of od
ll things indeed are pure but it is evil for that man who eateth with offence
t is good neither to eat flesh nor to drink wine nor any thing whereby thy brother stumbleth or is offended or is made weak
ast thou faith have it to thyself before od
appy is he that condemneth not himself in that thing which he alloweth
nd he that doubteth is damned if he eat because he eateth not of faith for whatsoever is not of faith is sin
e then that are strong ought to bear the infirmities of the weak and not to please ourselves
et every one of us please his neighbour for his good to edification
or even hrist pleased not himself but as it is written he reproaches of them that reproached thee fell on me
or whatsoever things were written aforetime were written for our learning that we through patience and comfort of the scriptures might have hope
ow the od of patience and consolation grant you to be likeminded one toward another according to hrist esus that ye may with one mind and one mouth glorify od even the ather of our ord esus hrist
herefore receive ye one another as hrist also received us to the glory of od
ow  say that esus hrist was a minister of the circumcision for the truth of od to confirm the promises made unto the fathers and that the entiles might glorify od for his mercy as it is written or this cause  will confess to thee among the entiles nd sing unto thy name
nd again he saith ejoice ye entiles with his people
nd again raise the ord all ye entiles nd laud him all ye people
nd again saias saith here shall be a root of esse nd he that shall rise to reign over the entiles n him shall the entiles trust
ow the od of hope fill you with all joy and peace in believing that ye may abound in hope through the power of the oly host
nd  myself also am persuaded of you my brethren that ye also are full of goodness filled with all knowledge able also to admonish one another
evertheless brethren  have written the more boldly unto you in some sort as putting you in mind because of the grace that is given to me of od that  should be the minister of esus hrist to the entiles ministering the gospel of od that the offering up of the entiles might be acceptable being sanctified by the oly host
 have therefore whereof  may glory through esus hrist in those things which pertain to od
or  will not dare to speak of any of those things which hrist hath not wrought by me to make the entiles obedient by word and deed through mighty signs and wonders by the power of the pirit of od so that from erusalem and round about unto llyricum  have fully preached the gospel of hrist
ea so have  strived to preach the gospel not where hrist was named lest  should build upon another mans foundation ut as it is written o whom he was not spoken of they shall see nd they that have not heard shall understand
or which cause also  have been much hindered from coming to you
ut now having no more place in these parts and having a great desire these many years to come unto you whensoever  take my journey into pain  will come to you for  trust to see you in my journey and to be brought on my way thitherward by you if first  be somewhat filled with your company
ut now  go unto erusalem to minister unto the saints
or it hath pleased them of acedonia and chaia to make a certain contribution for the poor saints which are at erusalem
t hath pleased them verily and their debtors they are
or if the entiles have been made partakers of their spiritual things their duty is also to minister unto them in carnal things
hen therefore  have performed this and have sealed to them this fruit  will come by you into pain
nd  am sure that when  come unto you  shall come in the fulness of the blessing of the gospel of hrist
ow  beseech you brethren for the ord esus hrists sake and for the love of the pirit that ye strive together with me in your prayers to od for me that  may be delivered from them that do not believe in ud a and that my service which  have for erusalem may be accepted of the saints that  may come unto you with joy by the will of od and may with you be refreshed
ow the od of peace be with you all
men
 commend unto you hebe our sister which is a servant of the church which is at enchrea that ye receive her in the ord as becometh saints and that ye assist her in whatsoever business she hath need of you for she hath been a succourer of many and of myself also
reet riscilla and quila my helpers in hrist esus who have for my life laid down their own necks unto whom not only  give thanks but also all the churches of the entiles
ikewise greet the church that is in their house
alute my wellbeloved p netus who is the firstfruits of chaia unto hrist
reet ary who bestowed much labour on us
alute ndronicus and unia my kinsmen and my fellowprisoners who are of note among the apostles who also were in hrist before me
reet mplias my beloved in the ord
alute rbane our helper in hrist and tachys my beloved
alute pelles approved in hrist
alute them which are of ristobulus household
alute erodion my kinsman
reet them that be of the household of arcissus which are in the ord
alute ryphena and ryphosa who labour in the ord
alute the beloved ersis which laboured much in the ord
alute ufus chosen in the ord and his mother and mine
alute syncritus hlegon ermas atrobas ermes and the brethren which are with them
alute hilologus and ulia ereus and his sister and lympas and all the saints which are with them
alute one another with an holy kiss
he churches of hrist salute you
ow  beseech you brethren mark them which cause divisions and offences contrary to the doctrine which ye have learned and avoid them
or they that are such serve not our ord esus hrist but their own belly and by good words and fair speeches deceive the hearts of the simple
or your obedience is come abroad unto all men
 am glad therefore on your behalf but yet  would have you wise unto that which is good and simple concerning evil
nd the od of peace shall bruise atan under your feet shortly
he grace of our ord esus hrist be with you
men
imotheus my workfellow and ucius and ason and osipater my kinsmen salute you
 ertius who wrote this epistle salute you in the ord
aius mine host and of the whole church saluteth you
rastus the chamberlain of the city saluteth you and uartus a brother
he grace of our ord esus hrist be with you all
men
ow to him that is of power to stablish you according to my gospel and the preaching of esus hrist according to the revelation of the mystery which was kept secret since the world began but now is made manifest and by the scriptures of the prophets according to the commandment of the everlasting od made known to all nations for the obedience of faith to od only wise be glory through esus hrist for ever
men
ritten to the omans from orinthus and sent by hebe servant of the church at enchrea
aul called to be an apostle of esus hrist through the will of od and osthenes our brother unto the church of od which is at orinth to them that are sanctified in hrist esus called to be saints with all that in every place call upon the name of esus hrist our ord both theirs and ours grace be unto you and peace from od our ather and from the ord esus hrist
 thank my od always on your behalf for the grace of od which is given you by esus hrist that in every thing ye are enriched by him in all utterance and in all knowledge even as the testimony of hrist was confirmed in you so that ye come behind in no gift waiting for the coming of our ord esus hrist who shall also confirm you unto the end that ye may be blameless in the day of our ord esus hrist
od is faithful by whom ye were called unto the fellowship of his on esus hrist our ord
ow  beseech you brethren by the name of our ord esus hrist that ye all speak the same thing and that there be no divisions among you but that ye be perfectly joined together in the same mind and in the same judgment
or it hath been declared unto me of you my brethren by them which are of the house of hloe that there are contentions among you
ow this  say that every one of you saith  am of aul and  of pollos and  of ephas and  of hrist
s hrist divided was aul crucified for you or were ye baptized in the name of aul
 thank od that  baptized none of you but rispus and aius lest any should say that  had baptized in mine own name
nd  baptized also the household of tephanas besides  know not whether  baptized any other
or hrist sent me not to baptize but to preach the gospel not with wisdom of words lest the cross of hrist should be made of none effect
or the preaching of the cross is to them that perish foolishness but unto us which are saved it is the power of od
or it is written  will destroy the wisdom of the wise nd will bring to nothing the understanding of the prudent
here is the wise where is the scribe where is the disputer of this world hath not od made foolish the wisdom of this world
or after that in the wisdom of od the world by wisdom knew not od it pleased od by the foolishness of preaching to save them that believe
or the ews require a sign and the reeks seek after wisdom but we preach hrist crucified unto the ews a stumblingblock and unto the reeks foolishness but unto them which are called both ews and reeks hrist the power of od and the wisdom of od
ecause the foolishness of od is wiser than men and the weakness of od is stronger than men
or ye see your calling brethren how that not many wise men after the flesh not many mighty not many noble are called but od hath chosen the foolish things of the world to confound the wise and od hath chosen the weak things of the world to confound the things which are mighty and base things of the world and things which are despised hath od chosen yea and things which are not to bring to nought things that are that no flesh should glory in his presence
ut of him are ye in hrist esus who of od is made unto us wisdom and righteousness and sanctification and redemption that according as it is written e that glorieth let him glory in the ord
nd  brethren when  came to you came not with excellency of speech or of wisdom declaring unto you the testimony of od
or  determined not to know any thing among you save esus hrist and him crucified
nd  was with you in weakness and in fear and in much trembling
nd my speech and my preaching was not with enticing words of mans wisdom but in demonstration of the pirit and of power that your faith should not stand in the wisdom of men but in the power of od
owbeit we speak wisdom among them that are perfect yet not the wisdom of this world nor of the princes of this world that come to nought but we speak the wisdom of od in a mystery even the hidden wisdom which od ordained before the world unto our glory which none of the princes of this world knew for had they known it they would not have crucified the ord of glory
ut as it is written ye hath not seen nor ear heard either have entered into the heart of man he things which od hath prepared for them that love him
ut od hath revealed them unto us by his pirit for the pirit searcheth all things yea the deep things of od
or what man knoweth the things of a man save the spirit of man which is in him even so the things of od knoweth no man but the pirit of od
ow we have received not the spirit of the world but the spirit which is of od that we might know the things that are freely given to us of od
hich things also we speak not in the words which mans wisdom teacheth but which the oly host teacheth comparing spiritual things with spiritual
ut the natural man receiveth not the things of the pirit of od for they are foolishness unto him neither can he know them because they are spiritually discerned
ut he that is spiritual judgeth all things yet he himself is judged of no man
or who hath known the mind of the ord that he may instruct him
ut we have the mind of hrist
nd  brethren could not speak unto you as unto spiritual but as unto carnal even as unto babes in hrist
 have fed you with milk and not with meat for hitherto ye were not able to bear it neither yet now are ye able
or ye are yet carnal for whereas there is among you envying and strife and divisions are ye not carnal and walk as men
or while one saith  am of aul and another  am of pollos are ye not carnal
ho then is aul and who is pollos but ministers by whom ye believed even as the ord gave to every man
 have planted pollos watered but od gave the increase
o then neither is he that planteth any thing neither he that watereth but od that giveth the increase
ow he that planteth and he that watereth are one and every man shall receive his own reward according to his own labour
or we are labourers together with od ye are ods husbandry ye are ods building
ccording to the grace of od which is given unto me as a wise masterbuilder  have laid the foundation and another buildeth thereon
ut let every man take heed how he buildeth thereupon
or other foundation can no man lay than that is laid which is esus hrist
ow if any man build upon this foundation gold silver precious stones wood hay stubble every mans work shall be made manifest for the day shall declare it because it shall be revealed by fire and the fire shall try every mans work of what sort it is
f any mans work abide which he hath built thereupon he shall receive a reward
f any mans work shall be burned he shall suffer loss but he himself shall be saved yet so as by fire
now ye not that ye are the temple of od and that the pirit of od dwelleth in you
f any man defile the temple of od him shall od destroy for the temple of od is holy which temple ye are
et no man deceive himself
f any man among you seemeth to be wise in this world let him become a fool that he may be wise
or the wisdom of this world is foolishness with od
or it is written e taketh the wise in their own craftiness
nd again he ord knoweth the thoughts of the wise that they are vain
herefore let no man glory in men
or all things are yours whether aul or pollos or ephas or the world or life or death or things present or things to come all are yours and ye are hrists and hrist is ods
et a man so account of us as of the ministers of hrist and stewards of the mysteries of od
oreover it is required in stewards that a man be found faithful
ut with me it is a very small thing that  should be judged of you or of mans judgment yea  judge not mine own self
or  know nothing by myself yet am  not hereby justified but he that judgeth me is the ord
herefore judge nothing before the time until the ord come who both will bring to light the hidden things of darkness and will make manifest the counsels of the hearts and then shall every man have praise of od
nd these things brethren  have in a figure transferred to myself and to pollos for your sakes that ye might learn in us not to think of men above that which is written that no one of you be puffed up for one against another
or who maketh thee to differ from another and what hast thou that thou didst not receive now if thou didst receive it why dost thou glory as if thou hadst not received it
ow ye are full now ye are rich ye have reigned as kings without us and  would to od ye did reign that we also might reign with you
or  think that od hath set forth us the apostles last as it were appointed to death for we are made a spectacle unto the world and to angels and to men
e are fools for hrists sake but ye are wise in hrist we are weak but ye are strong ye are honourable but we are despised
ven unto this present hour we both hunger and thirst and are naked and are buffeted and have no certain dwellingplace and labour working with our own hands being reviled we bless being persecuted we suffer it being defamed we intreat we are made as the filth of the world and are the offscouring of all things unto this day
 write not these things to shame you but as my beloved sons  warn you
or though ye have ten thousand instructors in hrist yet have ye not many fathers for in hrist esus  have begotten you through the gospel
herefore  beseech you be ye followers of me
or this cause have  sent unto you imotheus who is my beloved son and faithful in the ord who shall bring you into remembrance of my ways which be in hrist as  teach every where in every church
ow some are puffed up as though  would not come to you
ut  will come to you shortly if the ord will and will know not the speech of them which are puffed up but the power
or the kingdom of od is not in word but in power
hat will ye shall  come unto you with a rod or in love and in the spirit of meekness
t is reported commonly that there is fornication among you and such fornication as is not so much as named among the entiles that one should have his fathers wife
nd ye are puffed up and have not rather mourned that he that hath done this deed might be taken away from among you
or  verily as absent in body but present in spirit have judged already as though  were present concerning him that hath so done this deed in the name of our ord esus hrist when ye are gathered together and my spirit with the power of our ord esus hrist to deliver such an one unto atan for the destruction of the flesh that the spirit may be saved in the day of the ord esus
our glorying is not good
now ye not that a little leaven leaveneth the whole lump
urge out therefore the old leaven that ye may be a new lump as ye are unleavened
or even hrist our passover is sacrificed for us therefore let us keep the feast not with old leaven neither with the leaven of malice and wickedness but with the unleavened bread of sincerity and truth
 wrote unto you in an epistle not to company with fornicators yet not altogether with the fornicators of this world or with the covetous or extortioners or with idolaters for then must ye needs go out of the world
ut now  have written unto you not to keep company if any man that is called a brother be a fornicator or covetous or an idolater or a railer or a drunkard or an extortioner with such an one no not to eat
or what have  to do to judge them also that are without do not ye judge them that are within
ut them that are without od judgeth
herefore put away from among yourselves that wicked person
are any of you having a matter against another go to law before the unjust and not before the saints
o ye not know that the saints shall judge the world and if the world shall be judged by you are ye unworthy to judge the smallest matters
now ye not that we shall judge angels how much more things that pertain to this life
f then ye have judgments of things pertaining to this life set them to judge who are least esteemed in the church
 speak to your shame
s it so that there is not a wise man among you no not one that shall be able to judge between his brethren
ut brother goeth to law with brother and that before the unbelievers
ow therefore there is utterly a fault among you because ye go to law one with another
hy do ye not rather take wrong
hy do ye not rather suffer yourselves to be defrauded
ay ye do wrong and defraud and that your brethren
now ye not that the unrighteous shall not inherit the kingdom of od
e not deceived neither fornicators nor idolaters nor adulterers nor effeminate nor abusers of themselves with mankind nor thieves nor covetous nor drunkards nor revilers nor extortioners shall inherit the kingdom of od
nd such were some of you but ye are washed but ye are sanctified but ye are justified in the name of the ord esus and by the pirit of our od
ll things are lawful unto me but all things are not expedient all things are lawful for me but  will not be brought under the power of any
eats for the belly and the belly for meats but od shall destroy both it and them
ow the body is not for fornication but for the ord and the ord for the body
nd od hath both raised up the ord and will also raise up us by his own power
now ye not that your bodies are the members of hrist shall  then take the members of hrist and make them the members of an harlot
od forbid
hat know ye not that he which is joined to an harlot is one body for two saith he shall be one flesh
ut he that is joined unto the ord is one spirit
lee fornication
very sin that a man doeth is without the body but he that committeth fornication sinneth against his own body
hat know ye not that your body is the temple of the oly host which is in you which ye have of od and ye are not your own
or ye are bought with a price therefore glorify od in your body and in your spirit which are ods
ow concerning the things whereof ye wrote unto me t is good for a man not to touch a woman
evertheless to avoid fornication let every man have his own wife and let every woman have her own husband
et the husband render unto the wife due benevolence and likewise also the wife unto the husband
