he wife hath not power of her own body but the husband and likewise also the husband hath not power of his own body but the wife
efraud ye not one the other except it be with consent for a time that ye may give yourselves to fasting and prayer and come together again that atan tempt you not for your incontinency
ut  speak this by permission and not of commandment
or  would that all men were even as  myself
ut every man hath his proper gift of od one after this manner and another after that
 say therefore to the unmarried and widows t is good for them if they abide even as 
ut if they cannot contain let them marry for it is better to marry than to burn
nd unto the married  command yet not  but the ord et not the wife depart from her husband but and if she depart let her remain unmarried or be reconciled to her husband and let not the husband put away his wife
ut to the rest speak  not the ord f any brother hath a wife that believeth not and she be pleased to dwell with him let him not put her away
nd the woman which hath an husband that believeth not and if he be pleased to dwell with her let her not leave him
or the unbelieving husband is sanctified by the wife and the unbelieving wife is sanctified by the husband else were your children unclean but now are they holy
ut if the unbelieving depart let him depart
 brother or a sister is not under bondage in such cases but od hath called us to peace
or what knowest thou  wife whether thou shalt save thy husband or how knowest thou  man whether thou shalt save thy wife
ut as od hath distributed to every man as the ord hath called every one so let him walk
nd so ordain  in all churches
s any man called being circumcised let him not become uncircumcised
s any called in uncircumcision let him not be circumcised
ircumcision is nothing and uncircumcision is nothing but the keeping of the commandments of od
et every man abide in the same calling wherein he was called
rt thou called being a servant care not for it but if thou mayest be made free use it rather
or he that is called in the ord being a servant is the ords freeman likewise also he that is called being free is hrists servant
e are bought with a price be not ye the servants of men
rethren let every man wherein he is called therein abide with od
ow concerning virgins  have no commandment of the ord yet  give my judgment as one that hath obtained mercy of the ord to be faithful
 suppose therefore that this is good for the present distress  say that it is good for a man so to be
rt thou bound unto a wife seek not to be loosed
rt thou loosed from a wife seek not a wife
ut and if thou marry thou hast not sinned and if a virgin marry she hath not sinned
evertheless such shall have trouble in the flesh but  spare you
ut this  say brethren the time is short it remaineth that both they that have wives be as though they had none and they that weep as though they wept not and they that rejoice as though they rejoiced not and they that buy as though they possessed not and they that use this world as not abusing it for the fashion of this world passeth away
ut  would have you without carefulness
e that is unmarried careth for the things that belong to the ord how he may please the ord but he that is married careth for the things that are of the world how he may please his wife
here is difference also between a wife and a virgin
he unmarried woman careth for the things of the ord that she may be holy both in body and in spirit but she that is married careth for the things of the world how she may please her husband
nd this  speak for your own profit not that  may cast a snare upon you but for that which is comely and that ye may attend upon the ord without distraction
ut if any man think that he behaveth himself uncomely toward his virgin if she pass the flower of her age and need so require let him do what he will he sinneth not let them marry
evertheless he that standeth stedfast in his heart having no necessity but hath power over his own will and hath so decreed in his heart that he will keep his virgin doeth well
o then he that giveth her in marriage doeth well but he that giveth her not in marriage doeth better
he wife is bound by the law as long as her husband liveth but if her husband be dead she is at liberty to be married to whom she will only in the ord
ut she is happier if she so abide after my judgment and  think also that  have the pirit of od
ow as touching things offered unto idols we know that we all have knowledge
nowledge puffeth up but charity edifieth
nd if any man think that he knoweth any thing he knoweth nothing yet as he ought to know
ut if any man love od the same is known of him
s concerning therefore the eating of those things that are offered in sacrifice unto idols we know that an idol is nothing in the world and that there is none other od but one
or though there be that are called gods whether in heaven or in earth as there be gods many and lords many  but to us there is but one od the ather of whom are all things and we in him and one ord esus hrist by whom are all things and we by him
owbeit there is not in every man that knowledge for some with conscience of the idol unto this hour eat it as a thing offered unto an idol and their conscience being weak is defiled
ut meat commendeth us not to od for neither if we eat are we the better neither if we eat not are we the worse
ut take heed lest by any means this liberty of yours become a stumblingblock to them that are weak
or if any man see thee which hast knowledge sit at meat in the idols temple shall not the conscience of him which is weak be emboldened to eat those things which are offered to idols and through thy knowledge shall the weak brother perish for whom hrist died
ut when ye sin so against the brethren and wound their weak conscience ye sin against hrist
herefore if meat make my brother to offend  will eat no flesh while the world standeth lest  make my brother to offend
m  not an apostle am  not free have  not seen esus hrist our ord are not ye my work in the ord
f  be not an apostle unto others yet doubtless  am to you for the seal of mine apostleship are ye in the ord
ine answer to them that do examine me is this ave we not power to eat and to drink
ave we not power to lead about a sister a wife as well as other apostles and as the brethren of the ord and ephas
r  only and arnabas have not we power to forbear working
ho goeth a warfare any time at his own charges who planteth a vineyard and eateth not of the fruit thereof or who feedeth a flock and eateth not of the milk of the flock
ay  these things as a man or saith not the law the same also
or it is written in the law of oses hou shalt not muzzle the mouth of the ox that treadeth out the corn
oth od take care for oxen
r saith he it altogether for our sakes
or our sakes no doubt this is written that he that ploweth should plow in hope and that he that thresheth in hope should be partaker of his hope
f we have sown unto you spiritual things is it a great thing if we shall reap your carnal things
f others be partakers of this power over you are not we rather
evertheless we have not used this power but suffer all things lest we should hinder the gospel of hrist
o ye not know that they which minister about holy things live of the things of the temple and they which wait at the altar are partakers with the altar
ven so hath the ord ordained that they which preach the gospel should live of the gospel
ut  have used none of these things neither have  written these things that it should be so done unto me for it were better for me to die than that any man should make my glorying void
or though  preach the gospel  have nothing to glory of for necessity is laid upon me yea woe is unto me if  preach not the gospel
or if  do this thing willingly  have a reward but if against my will a dispensation of the gospel is committed unto me
hat is my reward then
erily that when  preach the gospel  may make the gospel of hrist without charge that  abuse not my power in the gospel
or though  be free from all men yet have  made myself servant unto all that  might gain the more
nd unto the ews  became as a ew that  might gain the ews to them that are under the law as under the law that  might gain them that are under the law to them that are without law as without law being not without law to od but under the law to hrist  that  might gain them that are without law
o the weak became  as weak that  might gain the weak  am made all things to all men that  might by all means save some
nd this  do for the gospels sake that  might be partaker thereof with you
now ye not that they which run in a race run all but one receiveth the prize
o run that ye may obtain
nd every man that striveth for the mastery is temperate in all things
ow they do it to obtain a corruptible crown but we an incorruptible
 therefore so run not as uncertainly so fight  not as one that beateth the air but  keep under my body and bring it into subjection lest that by any means when  have preached to others  myself should be a castaway
oreover brethren  would not that ye should be ignorant how that all our fathers were under the cloud and all passed through the sea and were all baptized unto oses in the cloud and in the sea and did all eat the same spiritual meat and did all drink the same spiritual drink for they drank of that spiritual ock that followed them and that ock was hrist
ut with many of them od was not well pleased for they were overthrown in the wilderness
ow these things were our examples to the intent we should not lust after evil things as they also lusted
either be ye idolaters as were some of them as it is written he people sat down to eat and drink and rose up to play
either let us commit fornication as some of them committed and fell in one day three and twenty thousand
either let us tempt hrist as some of them also tempted and were destroyed of serpents
either murmur ye as some of them also murmured and were destroyed of the destroyer
ow all these things happened unto them for ensamples and they are written for our admonition upon whom the ends of the world are come
herefore let him that thinketh he standeth take heed lest he fall
here hath no temptation taken you but such as is common to man but od is faithful who will not suffer you to be tempted above that ye are able but will with the temptation also make a way to escape that ye may be able to bear it
herefore my dearly beloved flee from idolatry
 speak as to wise men judge ye what  say
he cup of blessing which we bless is it not the communion of the blood of hrist
he bread which we break is it not the communion of the body of hrist
or we being many are one bread and one body for we are all partakers of that one bread
ehold srael after the flesh are not they which eat of the sacrifices partakers of the altar
hat say  then that the idol is any thing or that which is offered in sacrifice to idols is any thing
ut  say that the things which the entiles sacrifice they sacrifice to devils and not to od and  would not that ye should have fellowship with devils
e cannot drink the cup of the ord and the cup of devils ye cannot be partakers of the ords table and of the table of devils
o we provoke the ord to jealousy are we stronger than he
ll things are lawful for me but all things are not expedient all things are lawful for me but all things edify not
et no man seek his own but every man anothers wealth
hatsoever is sold in the shambles that eat asking no question for conscience sake for the earth is the ords and the fulness thereof
f any of them that believe not bid you to a feast and ye be disposed to go whatsoever is set before you eat asking no question for conscience sake
ut if any man say unto you his is offered in sacrifice unto idols eat not for his sake that shewed it and for conscience sake for the earth is the ords and the fulness thereof conscience  say not thine own but of the other for why is my liberty judged of another mans conscience
or if  by grace be a partaker why am  evil spoken of for that for which  give thanks
hether therefore ye eat or drink or whatsoever ye do do all to the glory of od
ive none offence neither to the ews nor to the entiles nor to the church of od even as  please all men in all things not seeking mine own profit but the profit of many that they may be saved
e ye followers of me even as  also am of hrist
ow  praise you brethren that ye remember me in all things and keep the ordinances as  delivered them to you
ut  would have you know that the head of every man is hrist and the head of the woman is the man and the head of hrist is od
very man praying or prophesying having his head covered dishonoureth his head
ut every woman that prayeth or prophesieth with her head uncovered dishonoureth her head for that is even all one as if she were shaven
or if the woman be not covered let her also be shorn but if it be a shame for a woman to be shorn or shaven let her be covered
or a man indeed ought not to cover his head forasmuch as he is the image and glory of od but the woman is the glory of the man
or the man is not of the woman but the woman of the man
either was the man created for the woman but the woman for the man
or this cause ought the woman to have power on her head because of the angels
evertheless neither is the man without the woman neither the woman without the man in the ord
or as the woman is of the man even so is the man also by the woman but all things of od
udge in yourselves is it comely that a woman pray unto od uncovered
oth not even nature itself teach you that if a man have long hair it is a shame unto him
ut if a woman have long hair it is a glory to her for her hair is given her for a covering
ut if any man seem to be contentious we have no such custom neither the churches of od
ow in this that  declare unto you  praise you not that ye come together not for the better but for the worse
or first of all when ye come together in the church  hear that there be divisions among you and  partly believe it
or there must be also heresies among you that they which are approved may be made manifest among you
hen ye come together therefore into one place this is not to eat the ords supper
or in eating every one taketh before other his own supper and one is hungry and another is drunken
hat have ye not houses to eat and to drink in or despise ye the church of od and shame them that have not
hat shall  say to you shall  praise you in this
 praise you not
or  have received of the ord that which also  delivered unto you hat the ord esus the same night in which he was betrayed took bread and when he had given thanks he brake it and said ake eat this is my body which is broken for you this do in remembrance of me
fter the same manner also he took the cup when he had supped saying his cup is the new testament in my blood this do ye as oft as ye drink it in remembrance of me
or as often as ye eat this bread and drink this cup ye do shew the ords death till he come
herefore whosoever shall eat this bread and drink this cup of the ord unworthily shall be guilty of the body and blood of the ord
ut let a man examine himself and so let him eat of that bread and drink of that cup
or he that eateth and drinketh unworthily eateth and drinketh damnation to himself not discerning the ords body
or this cause many are weak and sickly among you and many sleep
or if we would judge ourselves we should not be judged
ut when we are judged we are chastened of the ord that we should not be condemned with the world
herefore my brethren when ye come together to eat tarry one for another
nd if any man hunger let him eat at home that ye come not together unto condemnation
nd the rest will  set in order when  come
ow concerning spiritual gifts brethren  would not have you ignorant
e know that ye were entiles carried away unto these dumb idols even as ye were led
herefore  give you to understand that no man speaking by the pirit of od calleth esus accursed and that no man can say that esus is the ord but by the oly host
ow there are diversities of gifts but the same pirit
nd there are differences of administrations but the same ord
nd there are diversities of operations but it is the same od which worketh all in all
ut the manifestation of the pirit is given to every man to profit withal
or to one is given by the pirit the word of wisdom to another the word of knowledge by the same pirit to another faith by the same pirit to another the gifts of healing by the same pirit to another the working of miracles to another prophecy to another discerning of spirits to another divers kinds of tongues to another the interpretation of tongues but all these worketh that one and the selfsame pirit dividing to every man severally as he will
or as the body is one and hath many members and all the members of that one body being many are one body so also is hrist
or by one pirit are we all baptized into one body whether we be ews or entiles whether we be bond or free and have been all made to drink into one pirit
or the body is not one member but many
f the foot shall say ecause  am not the hand  am not of the body is it therefore not of the body
nd if the ear shall say ecause  am not the eye  am not of the body is it therefore not of the body
f the whole body were an eye where were the hearing
f the whole were hearing where were the smelling
ut now hath od set the members every one of them in the body as it hath pleased him
nd if they were all one member where were the body
ut now are they many members yet but one body
nd the eye cannot say unto the hand  have no need of thee nor again the head to the feet  have no need of you
ay much more those members of the body which seem to be more feeble are necessary and those members of the body which we think to be less honourable upon these we bestow more abundant honour and our uncomely parts have more abundant comeliness
or our comely parts have no need but od hath tempered the body together having given more abundant honour to that part which lacked that there should be no schism in the body but that the members should have the same care one for another
nd whether one member suffer all the members suffer with it or one member be honoured all the members rejoice with it
ow ye are the body of hrist and members in particular
nd od hath set some in the church first apostles secondarily prophets thirdly teachers after that miracles then gifts of healings helps governments diversities of tongues
re all apostles are all prophets are all teachers are all workers of miracles
ave all the gifts of healing do all speak with tongues do all interpret
ut covet earnestly the best gifts and yet shew  unto you a more excellent way
hough  speak with the tongues of men and of angels and have not charity  am become as sounding brass or a tinkling cymbal
nd though  have the gift of prophecy and understand all mysteries and all knowledge and though  have all faith so that  could remove mountains and have not charity  am nothing
nd though  bestow all my goods to feed the poor and though  give my body to be burned and have not charity it profiteth me nothing
harity suffereth long and is kind charity envieth not charity vaunteth not itself is not puffed up doth not behave itself unseemly seeketh not her own is not easily provoked thinketh no evil rejoiceth not in iniquity but rejoiceth in the truth beareth all things believeth all things hopeth all things endureth all things
harity never faileth but whether there be prophecies they shall fail whether there be tongues they shall cease whether there be knowledge it shall vanish away
or we know in part and we prophesy in part
ut when that which is perfect is come then that which is in part shall be done away
hen  was a child  spake as a child  understood as a child  thought as a child but when  became a man  put away childish things
or now we see through a glass darkly but then face to face now  know in part but then shall  know even as also  am known
nd now abideth faith hope charity these three but the greatest of these is charity
ollow after charity and desire spiritual gifts but rather that ye may prophesy
or he that speaketh in an unknown tongue speaketh not unto men but unto od for no man understandeth him howbeit in the spirit he speaketh mysteries
ut he that prophesieth speaketh unto men to edification and exhortation and comfort
e that speaketh in an unknown tongue edifieth himself but he that prophesieth edifieth the church
 would that ye all spake with tongues but rather that ye prophesied for greater is he that prophesieth than he that speaketh with tongues except he interpret that the church may receive edifying
ow brethren if  come unto you speaking with tongues what shall  profit you except  shall speak to you either by revelation or by knowledge or by prophesying or by doctrine
nd even things without life giving sound whether pipe or harp except they give a distinction in the sounds how shall it be known what is piped or harped
or if the trumpet give an uncertain sound who shall prepare himself to the battle
o likewise ye except ye utter by the tongue words easy to be understood how shall it be known what is spoken for ye shall speak into the air
here are it may be so many kinds of voices in the world and none of them is without signification
herefore if  know not the meaning of the voice  shall be unto him that speaketh a barbarian and he that speaketh shall be a barbarian unto me
ven so ye forasmuch as ye are zealous of spiritual gifts seek that ye may excel to the edifying of the church
herefore let him that speaketh in an unknown tongue pray that he may interpret
or if  pray in an unknown tongue my spirit prayeth but my understanding is unfruitful
hat is it then
 will pray with the spirit and  will pray with the understanding also  will sing with the spirit and  will sing with the understanding also
lse when thou shalt bless with the spirit how shall he that occupieth the room of the unlearned say men at thy giving of thanks seeing he understandeth not what thou sayest
or thou verily givest thanks well but the other is not edified
 thank my od  speak with tongues more than ye all yet in the church  had rather speak five words with my understanding that by my voice  might teach others also than ten thousand words in an unknown tongue
rethren be not children in understanding howbeit in malice be ye children but in understanding be men
n the law it is written ith men of other tongues and other lips will  speak unto this people and yet for all that will they not hear me saith the ord
herefore tongues are for a sign not to them that believe but to them that believe not but prophesying serveth not for them that believe not but for them which believe
f therefore the whole church be come together into one place and all speak with tongues and there come in those that are unlearned or unbelievers will they not say that ye are mad
ut if all prophesy and there come in one that believeth not or one unlearned he is convinced of all he is judged of all and thus are the secrets of his heart made manifest and so falling down on his face he will worship od and report that od is in you of a truth
ow is it then brethren when ye come together every one of you hath a psalm hath a doctrine hath a tongue hath a revelation hath an interpretation
et all things be done unto edifying
f any man speak in an unknown tongue let it be by two or at the most by three and that by course and let one interpret
ut if there be no interpreter let him keep silence in the church and let him speak to himself and to od
et the prophets speak two or three and let the other judge
f any thing be revealed to another that sitteth by let the first hold his peace
or ye may all prophesy one by one that all may learn and all may be comforted
nd the spirits of the prophets are subject to the prophets
or od is not the author of confusion but of peace as in all churches of the saints
et your women keep silence in the churches for it is not permitted unto them to speak but they are commanded to be under obedience as also saith the law
nd if they will learn any thing let them ask their husbands at home for it is a shame for women to speak in the church
hat came the word of od out from you or came it unto you only
f any man think himself to be a prophet or spiritual let him acknowledge that the things that  write unto you are the commandments of the ord
ut if any man be ignorant let him be ignorant
herefore brethren covet to prophesy and forbid not to speak with tongues
et all things be done decently and in order
oreover brethren  declare unto you the gospel which  preached unto you which also ye have received and wherein ye stand by which also ye are saved if ye keep in memory what  preached unto you unless ye have believed in vain
or  delivered unto you first of all that which  also received how that hrist died for our sins according to the scriptures and that he was buried and that he rose again the third day according to the scriptures and that he was seen of ephas then of the twelve after that he was seen of above five hundred brethren at once of whom the greater part remain unto this present but some are fallen asleep
fter that he was seen of ames then of all the apostles
nd last of all he was seen of me also as of one born out of due time
or  am the least of the apostles that am not meet to be called an apostle because  persecuted the church of od
ut by the grace of od  am what  am and his grace which was bestowed upon me was not in vain but  laboured more abundantly than they all yet not  but the grace of od which was with me
herefore whether it were  or they so we preach and so ye believed
ow if hrist be preached that he rose from the dead how say some among you that there is no resurrection of the dead
ut if there be no resurrection of the dead then is hrist not risen and if hrist be not risen then is our preaching vain and your faith is also vain
ea and we are found false witnesses of od because we have testified of od that he raised up hrist whom he raised not up if so be that the dead rise not
or if the dead rise not then is not hrist raised and if hrist be not raised your faith is vain ye are yet in your sins
hen they also which are fallen asleep in hrist are perished
f in this life only we have hope in hrist we are of all men most miserable
ut now is hrist risen from the dead and become the firstfruits of them that slept
or since by man came death by man came also the resurrection of the dead
or as in dam all die even so in hrist shall all be made alive
ut every man in his own order hrist the firstfruits afterward they that are hrists at his coming
hen cometh the end when he shall have delivered up the kingdom to od even the ather when he shall have put down all rule and all authority and power
or he must reign till he hath put all enemies under his feet
he last enemy that shall be destroyed is death
or he hath put all things under his feet
ut when he saith all things are put under him it is manifest that he is excepted which did put all things under him
nd when all things shall be subdued unto him then shall the on also himself be subject unto him that put all things under him that od may be all in all
lse what shall they do which are baptized for the dead if the dead rise not at all why are they then baptized for the dead
nd why stand we in jeopardy every hour
 protest by your rejoicing which  have in hrist esus our ord  die daily
f after the manner of men  have fought with beasts at phesus what advantageth it me if the dead rise not let us eat and drink for to morrow we die
e not deceived evil communications corrupt good manners
wake to righteousness and sin not for some have not the knowledge of od  speak this to your shame
ut some man will say ow are the dead raised up and with what body do they come
hou fool that which thou sowest is not quickened except it die and that which thou sowest thou sowest not that body that shall be but bare grain it may chance of wheat or of some other grain but od giveth it a body as it hath pleased him and to every seed his own body
ll flesh is not the same flesh but there is one kind of flesh of men another flesh of beasts another of fishes and another of birds
here are also celestial bodies and bodies terrestrial but the glory of the celestial is one and the glory of the terrestrial is another
here is one glory of the sun and another glory of the moon and another glory of the stars for one star differeth from another star in glory
o also is the resurrection of the dead
t is sown in corruption it is raised in incorruption it is sown in dishonour it is raised in glory it is sown in weakness it is raised in power it is sown a natural body it is raised a spiritual body
here is a natural body and there is a spiritual body
nd so it is written he first man dam was made a living soul the last dam was made a quickening spirit
owbeit that was not first which is spiritual but that which is natural and afterward that which is spiritual
he first man is of the earth earthy the second man is the ord from heaven
s is the earthy such are they also that are earthy and as is the heavenly such are they also that are heavenly
nd as we have borne the image of the earthy we shall also bear the image of the heavenly
ow this  say brethren that flesh and blood cannot inherit the kingdom of od neither doth corruption inherit incorruption
ehold  shew you a mystery e shall not all sleep but we shall all be changed in a moment in the twinkling of an eye at the last trump for the trumpet shall sound and the dead shall be raised incorruptible and we shall be changed
or this corruptible must put on incorruption and this mortal must put on immortality
o when this corruptible shall have put on incorruption and this mortal shall have put on immortality then shall be brought to pass the saying that is written eath is swallowed up in victory
 death where is thy sting
 grave where is thy victory
he sting of death is sin and the strength of sin is the law
ut thanks be to od which giveth us the victory through our ord esus hrist
herefore my beloved brethren be ye stedfast unmoveable always abounding in the work of the ord forasmuch as ye know that your labour is not in vain in the ord
ow concerning the collection for the saints as  have given order to the churches of alatia even so do ye
pon the first day of the week let every one of you lay by him in store as od hath prospered him that there be no gatherings when  come
nd when  come whomsoever ye shall approve by your letters them will  send to bring your liberality unto erusalem
nd if it be meet that  go also they shall go with me
ow  will come unto you when  shall pass through acedonia for  do pass through acedonia
nd it may be that  will abide yea and winter with you that ye may bring me on my journey whithersoever  go
or  will not see you now by the way but  trust to tarry a while with you if the ord permit
ut  will tarry at phesus until entecost
or a great door and effectual is opened unto me and there are many adversaries
ow if imotheus come see that he may be with you without fear for he worketh the work of the ord as  also do
et no man therefore despise him but conduct him forth in peace that he may come unto me for  look for him with the brethren
s touching our brother pollos  greatly desired him to come unto you with the brethren but his will was not at all to come at this time but he will come when he shall have convenient time
atch ye stand fast in the faith quit you like men be strong
et all your things be done with charity
 beseech you brethren ye know the house of tephanas that it is the firstfruits of chaia and that they have addicted themselves to the ministry of the saints  that ye submit yourselves unto such and to every one that helpeth with us and laboureth
 am glad of the coming of tephanas and ortunatus and chaicus for that which was lacking on your part they have supplied
or they have refreshed my spirit and yours therefore acknowledge ye them that are such
he churches of sia salute you
quila and riscilla salute you much in the ord with the church that is in their house
ll the brethren greet you
reet ye one another with an holy kiss
he salutation of me aul with mine own hand
f any man love not the ord esus hrist let him be nathema aranatha
he grace of our ord esus hrist be with you
y love be with you all in hrist esus
men
he first epistle to the orinthians was written from hilippi by tephanas and ortunatus and chaicus and imotheus
aul an apostle of esus hrist by the will of od and imothy our brother unto the church of od which is at orinth with all the saints which are in all chaia grace be to you and peace from od our ather and from the ord esus hrist
lessed be od even the ather of our ord esus hrist the ather of mercies and the od of all comfort who comforteth us in all our tribulation that we may be able to comfort them which are in any trouble by the comfort wherewith we ourselves are comforted of od
or as the sufferings of hrist abound in us so our consolation also aboundeth by hrist
nd whether we be afflicted it is for your consolation and salvation which is effectual in the enduring of the same sufferings which we also suffer or whether we be comforted it is for your consolation and salvation
nd our hope of you is stedfast knowing that as ye are partakers of the sufferings so shall ye be also of the consolation
or we would not brethren have you ignorant of our trouble which came to us in sia that we were pressed out of measure above strength insomuch that we despaired even of life but we had the sentence of death in ourselves that we should not trust in ourselves but in od which raiseth the dead who delivered us from so great a death and doth deliver in whom we trust that he will yet deliver us ye also helping together by prayer for us that for the gift bestowed upon us by the means of many persons thanks may be given by many on our behalf
or our rejoicing is this the testimony of our conscience that in simplicity and godly sincerity not with fleshly wisdom but by the grace of od we have had our conversation in the world and more abundantly to youward
or we write none other things unto you than what ye read or acknowledge and  trust ye shall acknowledge even to the end as also ye have acknowledged us in part that we are your rejoicing even as ye also are ours in the day of the ord esus
nd in this confidence  was minded to come unto you before that ye might have a second benefit and to pass by you into acedonia and to come again out of acedonia unto you and of you to be brought on my way toward ud a
hen  therefore was thus minded did  use lightness or the things that  purpose do  purpose according to the flesh that with me there should be yea yea and nay nay
ut as od is true our word toward you was not yea and nay
or the on of od esus hrist who was preached among you by us even by me and ilvanus and imotheus was not yea and nay but in him was yea
or all the promises of od in him are yea and in him men unto the glory of od by us
ow he which stablisheth us with you in hrist and hath anointed us is od who hath also sealed us and given the earnest of the pirit in our hearts
oreover  call od for a record upon my soul that to spare you  came not as yet unto orinth
ot for that we have dominion over your faith but are helpers of your joy for by faith ye stand
ut  determined this with myself that  would not come again to you in heaviness
or if  make you sorry who is he then that maketh me glad but the same which is made sorry by me
nd  wrote this same unto you lest when  came  should have sorrow from them of whom  ought to rejoice having confidence in you all that my joy is the joy of you all
or out of much affliction and anguish of heart  wrote unto you with many tears not that ye should be grieved but that ye might know the love which  have more abundantly unto you
ut if any have caused grief he hath not grieved me but in part that  may not overcharge you all
ufficient to such a man is this punishment which was inflicted of many
o that contrariwise ye ought rather to forgive him and comfort him lest perhaps such a one should be swallowed up with overmuch sorrow
herefore  beseech you that ye would confirm your love toward him
or to this end also did  write that  might know the proof of you whether ye be obedient in all things
o whom ye forgive any thing  forgive also for if  forgave any thing to whom  forgave it for your sakes forgave  it in the person of hrist lest atan should get an advantage of us for we are not ignorant of his devices
urthermore when  came to roas to preach hrists gospel and a door was opened unto me of the ord  had no rest in my spirit because  found not itus my brother but taking my leave of them  went from thence into acedonia
ow thanks be unto od which always causeth us to triumph in hrist and maketh manifest the savour of his knowledge by us in every place
or we are unto od a sweet savour of hrist in them that are saved and in them that perish to the one we are the savour of death unto death and to the other the savour of life unto life
nd who is sufficient for these things
or we are not as many which corrupt the word of od but as of sincerity but as of od in the sight of od speak we in hrist
o we begin again to commend ourselves or need we as some others epistles of commendation to you or letters of commendation from you
e are our epistle written in our hearts known and read of all men forasmuch as ye are manifestly declared to be the epistle of hrist ministered by us written not with ink but with the pirit of the living od not in tables of stone but in fleshy tables of the heart
nd such trust have we through hrist to odward not that we are sufficient of ourselves to think any thing as of ourselves but our sufficiency is of od who also hath made us able ministers of the new testament not of the letter but of the spirit for the letter killeth but the spirit giveth life
ut if the ministration of death written and engraven in stones was glorious so that the children of srael could not stedfastly behold the face of oses for the glory of his countenance which glory was to be done away how shall not the ministration of the spirit be rather glorious
or if the ministration of condemnation be glory much more doth the ministration of righteousness exceed in glory
or even that which was made glorious had no glory in this respect by reason of the glory that excelleth
or if that which is done away was glorious much more that which remaineth is glorious
eeing then that we have such hope we use great plainness of speech and not as oses which put a vail over his face that the children of srael could not stedfastly look to the end of that which is abolished but their minds were blinded for until this day remaineth the same vail untaken away in the reading of the old testament which vail is done away in hrist
ut even unto this day when oses is read the vail is upon their heart
evertheless when it shall turn to the ord the vail shall be taken away
ow the ord is that pirit and where the pirit of the ord is there is liberty
ut we all with open face beholding as in a glass the glory of the ord are changed into the same image from glory to glory even as by the pirit of the ord
herefore seeing we have this ministry as we have received mercy we faint not but have renounced the hidden things of dishonesty not walking in craftiness nor handling the word of od deceitfully but by manifestation of the truth commending ourselves to every mans conscience in the sight of od
ut if our gospel be hid it is hid to them that are lost in whom the god of this world hath blinded the minds of them which believe not lest the light of the glorious gospel of hrist who is the image of od should shine unto them
or we preach not ourselves but hrist esus the ord and ourselves your servants for esus sake
or od who commanded the light to shine out of darkness hath shined in our hearts to give the light of the knowledge of the glory of od in the face of esus hrist
ut we have this treasure in earthen vessels that the excellency of the power may be of od and not of us
e are troubled on every side yet not distressed we are perplexed but not in despair persecuted but not forsaken cast down but not destroyed always bearing about in the body the dying of the ord esus that the life also of esus might be made manifest in our body
or we which live are alway delivered unto death for esus sake that the life also of esus might be made manifest in our mortal flesh
o then death worketh in us but life in you
e having the same spirit of faith according as it is written  believed and therefore have  spoken we also believe and therefore speak knowing that he which raised up the ord esus shall raise up us also by esus and shall present us with you
or all things are for your sakes that the abundant grace might through the thanksgiving of many redound to the glory of od
or which cause we faint not but though our outward man perish yet the inward man is renewed day by day
or our light affliction which is but for a moment worketh for us a far more exceeding and eternal weight of glory while we look not at the things which are seen but at the things which are not seen for the things which are seen are temporal but the things which are not seen are eternal
or we know that if our earthly house of this tabernacle were dissolved we have a building of od an house not made with hands eternal in the heavens
or in this we groan earnestly desiring to be clothed upon with our house which is from heaven if so be that being clothed we shall not be found naked
or we that are in this tabernacle do groan being burdened not for that we would be unclothed but clothed upon that mortality might be swallowed up of life
ow he that hath wrought us for the selfsame thing is od who also hath given unto us the earnest of the pirit
herefore we are always confident knowing that whilst we are at home in the body we are absent from the ord for we walk by faith not by sight we are confident  say and willing rather to be absent from the body and to be present with the ord
herefore we labour that whether present or absent we may be accepted of him
or we must all appear before the judgment seat of hrist that every one may receive the things done in his body according to that he hath done whether it be good or bad
nowing therefore the terror of the ord we persuade men but we are made manifest unto od and  trust also are made manifest in your consciences
or we commend not ourselves again unto you but give you occasion to glory on our behalf that ye may have somewhat to answer them which glory in appearance and not in heart
or whether we be beside ourselves it is to od or whether we be sober it is for your cause
or the love of hrist constraineth us because we thus judge that if one died for all then were all dead and that he died for all that they which live should not henceforth live unto themselves but unto him which died for them and rose again
herefore henceforth know we no man after the flesh yea though we have known hrist after the flesh yet now henceforth know we him no more
herefore if any man be in hrist he is a new creature old things are passed away behold all things are become new
nd all things are of od who hath reconciled us to himself by esus hrist and hath given to us the ministry of reconciliation to wit that od was in hrist reconciling the world unto himself not imputing their trespasses unto them and hath committed unto us the word of reconciliation
ow then we are ambassadors for hrist as though od did beseech you by us we pray you in hrists stead be ye reconciled to od
or he hath made him to be sin for us who knew no sin that we might be made the righteousness of od in him
e then as workers together with him beseech you also that ye receive not the grace of od in vain
or he saith  have heard thee in a time accepted nd in the day of salvation have  succoured thee behold now is the accepted time behold now is the day of salvation iving no offence in any thing that the ministry be not blamed ut in all things approving ourselves as the ministers of od in much patience in afflictions in necessities in distresses in stripes in imprisonments in tumults in labours in watchings in fastings by pureness by knowledge by longsuffering by kindness by the oly host by love unfeigned by the word of truth by the power of od by the armour of righteousness on the right hand and on the left by honour and dishonour by evil report and good report as deceivers and yet true as unknown and yet well known as dying and behold we live as chastened and not killed as sorrowful yet alway rejoicing as poor yet making many rich as having nothing and yet possessing all things
 ye orinthians our mouth is open unto you our heart is enlarged
e are not straitened in us but ye are straitened in your own bowels
ow for a recompence in the same  speak as unto my children  be ye also enlarged
e ye not unequally yoked together with unbelievers for what fellowship hath righteousness with unrighteousness and what communion hath light with darkness
nd what concord hath hrist with elial or what part hath he that believeth with an infidel
nd what agreement hath the temple of od with idols for ye are the temple of the living od as od hath said  will dwell in them and walk in them and  will be their od and they shall be my people
hereforeome out from among them and be ye separate saith the ord nd touch not the unclean thing nd  will receive you nd will be a ather unto you nd ye shall be my sons and daughters saith the ord lmighty
aving therefore these promises dearly beloved let us cleanse ourselves from all filthiness of the flesh and spirit perfecting holiness in the fear of od
eceive us we have wronged no man we have corrupted no man we have defrauded no man
 speak not this to condemn you for  have said before that ye are in our hearts to die and live with you
reat is my boldness of speech toward you great is my glorying of you  am filled with comfort  am exceeding joyful in all our tribulation
or when we were come into acedonia our flesh had no rest but we were troubled on every side without were fightings within were fears
evertheless od that comforteth those that are cast down comforted us by the coming of itus and not by his coming only but by the consolation wherewith he was comforted in you when he told us your earnest desire your mourning your fervent mind toward me so that  rejoiced the more
or though  made you sorry with a letter  do not repent though  did repent for  perceive that the same epistle hath made you sorry though it were but for a season
ow  rejoice not that ye were made sorry but that ye sorrowed to repentance for ye were made sorry after a godly manner that ye might receive damage by us in nothing
or godly sorrow worketh repentance to salvation not to be repented of but the sorrow of the world worketh death
or behold this selfsame thing that ye sorrowed after a godly sort what carefulness it wrought in you yea what clearing of yourselves yea what indignation yea what fear yea what vehement desire yea what zeal yea what revenge
n all things ye have approved yourselves to be clear in this matter
herefore though  wrote unto you  did it not for his cause that had done the wrong nor for his cause that suffered wrong but that our care for you in the sight of od might appear unto you
herefore we were comforted in your comfort yea and exceedingly the more joyed we for the joy of itus because his spirit was refreshed by you all
or if  have boasted any thing to him of you  am not ashamed but as we spake all things to you in truth even so our boasting which  made before itus is found a truth
nd his inward affection is more abundant toward you whilst he remembereth the obedience of you all how with fear and trembling ye received him
 rejoice therefore that  have confidence in you in all things
oreover brethren we do you to wit of the grace of od bestowed on the churches of acedonia how that in a great trial of affliction the abundance of their joy and their deep poverty abounded unto the riches of their liberality
or to their power  bear record yea and beyond their power they were willing of themselves praying us with much intreaty that we would receive the gift and take upon us the fellowship of the ministering to the saints
nd this they did not as we hoped but first gave their own selves to the ord and unto us by the will of od
nsomuch that we desired itus that as he had begun so he would also finish in you the same grace also
herefore as ye abound in every thing in faith and utterance and knowledge and in all diligence and in your love to us see that ye abound in this grace also
 speak not by commandment but by occasion of the forwardness of others and to prove the sincerity of your love
or ye know the grace of our ord esus hrist that though he was rich yet for your sakes he became poor that ye through his poverty might be rich
nd herein  give my advice for this is expedient for you who have begun before not only to do but also to be forward a year ago
ow therefore perform the doing of it that as there was a readiness to will so there may be a performance also out of that which ye have
or if there be first a willing mind it is accepted according to that a man hath and not according to that he hath not
or  mean not that other men be eased and ye burdened but by an equality that now at this time your abundance may be a supply for their want that their abundance also may be a supply for your want that there may be equality as it is written e that had gathered much had nothing over and he that had gathered little had no lack
ut thanks be to od which put the same earnest care into the heart of itus for you
or indeed he accepted the exhortation but being more forward of his own accord he went unto you
nd we have sent with him the brother whose praise is in the gospel throughout all the churches and not that only but who was also chosen of the churches to travel with us with this grace which is administered by us to the glory of the same ord and declaration of your ready mind avoiding this that no man should blame us in this abundance which is administered by us providing for honest things not only in the sight of the ord but also in the sight of men
nd we have sent with them our brother whom we have oftentimes proved diligent in many things but now much more diligent upon the great confidence which  have in you
hether any do enquire of itus he is my partner and fellowhelper concerning you or our brethren be enquired of they are the messengers of the churches and the glory of hrist
herefore shew ye to them and before the churches the proof of your love and of our boasting on your behalf
or as touching the ministering to the saints it is superfluous for me to write to you for  know the forwardness of your mind for which  boast of you to them of acedonia that chaia was ready a year ago and your zeal hath provoked very many
et have  sent the brethren lest our boasting of you should be in vain in this behalf that as  said ye may be ready lest haply if they of acedonia come with me and find you unprepared we that we say not ye should be ashamed in this same confident boasting
herefore  thought it necessary to exhort the brethren that they would go before unto you and make up beforehand your bounty whereof ye had notice before that the same might be ready as a matter of bounty and not as of covetousness
ut this  say e which soweth sparingly shall reap also sparingly and he which soweth bountifully shall reap also bountifully
very man according as he purposeth in his heart so let him give not grudgingly or of necessity for od loveth a cheerful giver
nd od is able to make all grace abound toward you that ye always having all sufficiency in all things may abound to every good work s it is written e hath dispersed abroad he hath given to the poor is righteousness remaineth for ever
ow he that ministereth seed to the sower both minister bread for your food and multiply your seed sown and increase the fruits of your righteousness being enriched in every thing to all bountifulness which causeth through us thanksgiving to od
or the administration of this service not only supplieth the want of the saints but is abundant also by many thanksgivings unto od whiles by the experiment of this ministration they glorify od for your professed subjection unto the gospel of hrist and for your liberal distribution unto them and unto all men and by their prayer for you which long after you for the exceeding grace of od in you
hanks be unto od for his unspeakable gift
ow  aul myself beseech you by the meekness and gentleness of hrist who in presence am base among you but being absent am bold toward you but  beseech you that  may not be bold when  am present with that confidence wherewith  think to be bold against some which think of us as if we walked according to the flesh
or though we walk in the flesh we do not war after the flesh for the weapons of our warfare are not carnal but mighty through od to the pulling down of strong holds casting down imaginations and every high thing that exalteth itself against the knowledge of od and bringing into captivity every thought to the obedience of hrist and having in a readiness to revenge all disobedience when your obedience is fulfilled
o ye look on things after the outward appearance
f any man trust to himself that he is hrists let him of himself think this again that as he is hrists even so are we hrists
or though  should boast somewhat more of our authority which the ord hath given us for edification and not for your destruction  should not be ashamed that  may not seem as if  would terrify you by letters
or his letters say they are weighty and powerful but his bodily presence is weak and his speech contemptible
et such an one think this that such as we are in word by letters when we are absent such will we be also in deed when we are present
or we dare not make ourselves of the number or compare ourselves with some that commend themselves but they measuring themselves by themselves and comparing themselves among themselves are not wise
ut we will not boast of things without our measure but according to the measure of the rule which od hath distributed to us a measure to reach even unto you
or we stretch not ourselves beyond our measure as though we reached not unto you for we are come as far as to you also in preaching the gospel of hrist not boasting of things without our measure that is of other mens labours but having hope when your faith is increased that we shall be enlarged by you according to our rule abundantly to preach the gospel in the regions beyond you and not to boast in another mans line of things made ready to our hand
ut he that glorieth let him glory in the ord
or not he that commendeth himself is approved but whom the ord commendeth
ould to od ye could bear with me a little in my folly and indeed bear with me
or  am jealous over you with godly jealousy for  have espoused you to one husband that  may present you as a chaste virgin to hrist
ut  fear lest by any means as the serpent beguiled ve through his subtilty so your minds should be corrupted from the simplicity that is in hrist
or if he that cometh preacheth another esus whom we have not preached or if ye receive another spirit which ye have not received or another gospel which ye have not accepted ye might well bear with him
or  suppose  was not a whit behind the very chiefest apostles
ut though  be rude in speech yet not in knowledge but we have been throughly made manifest among you in all things
ave  committed an offence in abasing myself that ye might be exalted because  have preached to you the gospel of od freely
 robbed other churches taking wages of them to do you service
nd when  was present with you and wanted  was chargeable to no man for that which was lacking to me the brethren which came from acedonia supplied and in all things  have kept myself from being burdensome unto you and so will  keep myself
s the truth of hrist is in me no man shall stop me of this boasting in the regions of chaia
herefore because  love you not
od knoweth
ut what  do that  will do that  may cut off occasion from them which desire occasion that wherein they glory they may be found even as we
or such are false apostles deceitful workers transforming themselves into the apostles of hrist
nd no marvel for atan himself is transformed into an angel of light
herefore it is no great thing if his ministers also be transformed as the ministers of righteousness whose end shall be according to their works
 say again et no man think me a fool if otherwise yet as a fool receive me that  may boast myself a little
hat which  speak  speak it not after the ord but as it were foolishly in this confidence of boasting
eeing that many glory after the flesh  will glory also
or ye suffer fools gladly seeing ye yourselves are wise
or ye suffer if a man bring you into bondage if a man devour you if a man take of you if a man exalt himself if a man smite you on the face
 speak as concerning reproach as though we had been weak
owbeit whereinsoever any is bold  speak foolishly   am bold also
re they ebrews so am 
re they sraelites so am 
re they the seed of braham so am 
re they ministers of hrist
 speak as a fool  am more in labours more abundant in stripes above measure in prisons more frequent in deaths oft
f the ews five times received  forty stripes save one
hrice was  beaten with rods once was  stoned thrice  suffered shipwreck a night and a day  have been in the deep in journeyings often in perils of waters in perils of robbers in perils by mine own countrymen in perils by the heathen in perils in the city in perils in the wilderness in perils in the sea in perils among false brethren in weariness and painfulness in watchings often in hunger and thirst in fastings often in cold and nakedness
eside those things that are without that which cometh upon me daily the care of all the churches
ho is weak and  am not weak who is offended and  burn not
f  must needs glory  will glory of the things which concern mine infirmities
he od and ather of our ord esus hrist which is blessed for evermore knoweth that  lie not
n amascus the governor under retas the king kept the city of the amascenes with a garrison desirous to apprehend me and through a window in a basket was  let down by the wall and escaped his hands
t is not expedient for me doubtless to glory
 will come to visions and revelations of the ord
 knew a man in hrist above fourteen years ago whether in the body  cannot tell or whether out of the body  cannot tell od knoweth such an one caught up to the third heaven
nd  knew such a man whether in the body or out of the body  cannot tell od knoweth how that he was caught up into paradise and heard unspeakable words which it is not lawful for a man to utter
f such an one will  glory yet of myself  will not glory but in mine infirmities
or though  would desire to glory  shall not be a fool for  will say the truth but now  forbear lest any man should think of me above that which he seeth me to be or that he heareth of me
nd lest  should be exalted above measure through the abundance of the revelations there was given to me a thorn in the flesh the messenger of atan to buffet me lest  should be exalted above measure
or this thing  besought the ord thrice that it might depart from me
nd he said unto me y grace is sufficient for thee for my strength is made perfect in weakness
ost gladly therefore will  rather glory in my infirmities that the power of hrist may rest upon me
herefore  take pleasure in infirmities in reproaches in necessities in persecutions in distresses for hrists sake for when  am weak then am  strong
 am become a fool in glorying ye have compelled me for  ought to have been commended of you for in nothing am  behind the very chiefest apostles though  be nothing
ruly the signs of an apostle were wrought among you in all patience in signs and wonders and mighty deeds
or what is it wherein ye were inferior to other churches except it be that  myself was not burdensome to you forgive me this wrong
ehold the third time  am ready to come to you and  will not be burdensome to you for  seek not yours but you for the children ought not to lay up for the parents but the parents for the children
nd  will very gladly spend and be spent for you though the more abundantly  love you the less  be loved
ut be it so  did not burden you nevertheless being crafty  caught you with guile
id  make a gain of you by any of them whom  sent unto you
 desired itus and with him  sent a brother
id itus make a gain of you walked we not in the same spirit walked we not in the same steps
gain think ye that we excuse ourselves unto you we speak before od in hrist but we do all things dearly beloved for your edifying
or  fear lest when  come  shall not find you such as  would and that  shall be found unto you such as ye would not lest there be debates envyings wraths strifes backbitings whisperings swellings tumults and lest when  come again my od will humble me among you and that  shall bewail many which have sinned already and have not repented of the uncleanness and fornication and lasciviousness which they have committed
his is the third time  am coming to you
n the mouth of two or three witnesses shall every word be established
 told you before and foretell you as if  were present the second time and being absent now  write to them which heretofore have sinned and to all other that if  come again  will not spare since ye seek a proof of hrist speaking in me which to youward is not weak but is mighty in you
or though he was crucified through weakness yet he liveth by the power of od
or we also are weak in him but we shall live with him by the power of od toward you
xamine yourselves whether ye be in the faith prove your own selves
now ye not your own selves how that esus hrist is in you except ye be reprobates
ut  trust that ye shall know that we are not reprobates
ow  pray to od that ye do no evil not that we should appear approved but that ye should do that which is honest though we be as reprobates
or we can do nothing against the truth but for the truth
or we are glad when we are weak and ye are strong and this also we wish even your perfection
herefore  write these things being absent lest being present  should use sharpness according to the power which the ord hath given me to edification and not to destruction
inally brethren farewell
e perfect be of good comfort be of one mind live in peace and the od of love and peace shall be with you
reet one another with an holy kiss
ll the saints salute you
he grace of the ord esus hrist and the love of od and the communion of the oly host be with you all
men
he second epistle to the orinthians was written from hilippi a city of acedonia by itus and ucas
aul an apostle not of men neither by man but by esus hrist and od the ather who raised him from the dead and all the brethren which are with me unto the churches of alatia grace be to you and peace from od the ather and from our ord esus hrist who gave himself for our sins that he might deliver us from this present evil world according to the will of od and our ather to whom be glory for ever and ever
men
 marvel that ye are so soon removed from him that called you into the grace of hrist unto another gospel which is not another but there be some that trouble you and would pervert the gospel of hrist
ut though we or an angel from heaven preach any other gospel unto you than that which we have preached unto you let him be accursed
s we said before so say  now again f any man preach any other gospel unto you than that ye have received let him be accursed
or do  now persuade men or od or do  seek to please men for if  yet pleased men  should not be the servant of hrist
ut  certify you brethren that the gospel which was preached of me is not after man
or  neither received it of man neither was  taught it but by the revelation of esus hrist
or ye have heard of my conversation in time past in the ews religion how that beyond measure  persecuted the church of od and wasted it and profited in the ews religion above many my equals in mine own nation being more exceedingly zealous of the traditions of my fathers
ut when it pleased od who separated me from my mothers womb and called me by his grace to reveal his on in me that  might preach him among the heathen immediately  conferred not with flesh and blood neither went  up to erusalem to them which were apostles before me but  went into rabia and returned again unto amascus
hen after three years  went up to erusalem to see eter and abode with him fifteen days
ut other of the apostles saw  none save ames the ords brother
ow the things which  write unto you behold before od  lie not
fterwards  came into the regions of yria and ilicia and was unknown by face unto the churches of ud a which were in hrist but they had heard only hat he which persecuted us in times past now preacheth the faith which once he destroyed
nd they glorified od in me
hen fourteen years after  went up again to erusalem with arnabas and took itus with me also
nd  went up by revelation and communicated unto them that gospel which  preach among the entiles but privately to them which were of reputation lest by any means  should run or had run in vain
ut neither itus who was with me being a reek was compelled to be circumcised and that because of false brethren unawares brought in who came in privily to spy out our liberty which we have in hrist esus that they might bring us into bondage to whom we gave place by subjection no not for an hour that the truth of the gospel might continue with you
ut of these who seemed to be somewhat whatsoever they were it maketh no matter to me od accepteth no mans person for they who seemed to be somewhat in conference added nothing to me but contrariwise when they saw that the gospel of the uncircumcision was committed unto me as the gospel of the circumcision was unto eter for he that wrought effectually in eter to the apostleship of the circumcision the same was mighty in me toward the entiles and when ames ephas and ohn who seemed to be pillars perceived the grace that was given unto me they gave to me and arnabas the right hands of fellowship that we should go unto the heathen and they unto the circumcision
nly they would that we should remember the poor the same which  also was forward to do
ut when eter was come to ntioch  withstood him to the face because he was to be blamed
or before that certain came from ames he did eat with the entiles but when they were come he withdrew and separated himself fearing them which were of the circumcision
nd the other ews dissembled likewise with him insomuch that arnabas also was carried away with their dissimulation
ut when  saw that they walked not uprightly according to the truth of the gospel  said unto eter before them all f thou being a ew livest after the manner of entiles and not as do the ews why compellest thou the entiles to live as do the ews
e who are ews by nature and not sinners of the entiles knowing that a man is not justified by the works of the law but by the faith of esus hrist even we have believed in esus hrist that we might be justified by the faith of hrist and not by the works of the law for by the works of the law shall no flesh be justified
ut if while we seek to be justified by hrist we ourselves also are found sinners is therefore hrist the minister of sin
od forbid
or if  build again the things which  destroyed  make myself a transgressor
or  through the law am dead to the law that  might live unto od
 am crucified with hrist nevertheless  live yet not  but hrist liveth in me and the life which  now live in the flesh  live by the faith of the on of od who loved me and gave himself for me
 do not frustrate the grace of od for if righteousness come by the law then hrist is dead in vain
 foolish alatians who hath bewitched you that ye should not obey the truth before whose eyes esus hrist hath been evidently set forth crucified among you
his only would  learn of you eceived ye the pirit by the works of the law or by the hearing of faith
re ye so foolish having begun in the pirit are ye now made perfect by the flesh
ave ye suffered so many things in vain if it be yet in vain
e therefore that ministereth to you the pirit and worketh miracles among you doeth he it by the works of the law or by the hearing of faith
ven as braham believed od and it was accounted to him for righteousness
now ye therefore that they which are of faith the same are the children of braham
nd the scripture foreseeing that od would justify the heathen through faith preached before the gospel unto braham saying n thee shall all nations be blessed
o then they which be of faith are blessed with faithful braham
or as many as are of the works of the law are under the curse for it is written ursed is every one that continueth not in all things which are written in the book of the law to do them
ut that no man is justified by the law in the sight of od it is evident for he just shall live by faith
nd the law is not of faith but he man that doeth them shall live in them
hrist hath redeemed us from the curse of the law being made a curse for us for it is written ursed is every one that hangeth on a tree that the blessing of braham might come on the entiles through esus hrist that we might receive the promise of the pirit through faith
rethren  speak after the manner of men hough it be but a mans covenant yet if it be confirmed no man disannulleth or addeth thereto
ow to braham and his seed were the promises made
e saith not nd to seeds as of many but as of one nd to thy seed which is hrist
nd this  say that the covenant that was confirmed before of od in hrist the law which was four hundred and thirty years after cannot disannul that it should make the promise of none effect
or if the inheritance be of the law it is no more of promise but od gave it to braham by promise
herefore then serveth the law
t was added because of transgressions till the seed should come to whom the promise was made and it was ordained by angels in the hand of a mediator
ow a mediator is not a mediator of one but od is one
s the law then against the promises of od
od forbid for if there had been a law given which could have given life verily righteousness should have been by the law
ut the scripture hath concluded all under sin that the promise by faith of esus hrist might be given to them that believe
ut before faith came we were kept under the law shut up unto the faith which should afterwards be revealed
herefore the law was our schoolmaster to bring us unto hrist that we might be justified by faith
ut after that faith is come we are no longer under a schoolmaster
or ye are all the children of od by faith in hrist esus
or as many of you as have been baptized into hrist have put on hrist
here is neither ew nor reek there is neither bond nor free there is neither male nor female for ye are all one in hrist esus
nd if ye be hrists then are ye brahams seed and heirs according to the promise
ow  say hat the heir as long as he is a child differeth nothing from a servant though he be lord of all but is under tutors and governors until the time appointed of the father
ven so we when we were children were in bondage under the elements of the world but when the fulness of the time was come od sent forth his on made of a woman made under the law to redeem them that were under the law that we might receive the adoption of sons
nd because ye are sons od hath sent forth the pirit of his on into your hearts crying bba ather
herefore thou art no more a servant but a son and if a son then an heir of od through hrist
owbeit then when ye knew not od ye did service unto them which by nature are no gods
ut now after that ye have known od or rather are known of od how turn ye again to the weak and beggarly elements whereunto ye desire again to be in bondage
e observe days and months and times and years
 am afraid of you lest  have bestowed upon you labour in vain
rethren  beseech you be as  am for  am as ye are ye have not injured me at all
e know how through infirmity of the flesh  preached the gospel unto you at the first
nd my temptation which was in my flesh ye despised not nor rejected but received me as an angel of od even as hrist esus
here is then the blessedness ye spake of for  bear you record that if it had been possible ye would have plucked out your own eyes and have given them to me
m  therefore become your enemy because  tell you the truth
hey zealously affect you but not well yea they would exclude you that ye might affect them
ut it is good to be zealously affected always in a good thing and not only when  am present with you
y little children of whom  travail in birth again until hrist be formed in you  desire to be present with you now and to change my voice for  stand in doubt of you
ell me ye that desire to be under the law do ye not hear the law
or it is written that braham had two sons the one by a bondmaid the other by a freewoman
ut he who was of the bondwoman was born after the flesh but he of the freewoman was by promise
hich things are an allegory for these are the two covenants the one from the mount inai which gendereth to bondage which is gar
or this gar is mount inai in rabia and answereth to erusalem which now is and is in bondage with her children
ut erusalem which is above is free which is the mother of us all
or it is written ejoice thou barren that bearest not reak forth and cry thou that travailest not or the desolate hath many more children than she which hath an husband
ow we brethren as saac was are the children of promise
ut as then he that was born after the flesh persecuted him that was born after the pirit even so it is now
evertheless what saith the scripture
ast out the bondwoman and her son for the son of the bondwoman shall not be heir with the son of the freewoman
o then brethren we are not children of the bondwoman but of the free
tand fast therefore in the liberty wherewith hrist hath made us free and be not entangled again with the yoke of bondage
ehold  aul say unto you that if ye be circumcised hrist shall profit you nothing
or  testify again to every man that is circumcised that he is a debtor to do the whole law
hrist is become of no effect unto you whosoever of you are justified by the law ye are fallen from grace
or we through the pirit wait for the hope of righteousness by faith
or in esus hrist neither circumcision availeth any thing nor uncircumcision but faith which worketh by love
e did run well who did hinder you that ye should not obey the truth
his persuasion cometh not of him that calleth you
 little leaven leaveneth the whole lump
 have confidence in you through the ord that ye will be none otherwise minded but he that troubleth you shall bear his judgment whosoever he be
nd  brethren if  yet preach circumcision why do  yet suffer persecution then is the offence of the cross ceased
 would they were even cut off which trouble you
or brethren ye have been called unto liberty only use not liberty for an occasion to the flesh but by love serve one another
or all the law is fulfilled in one word even in this hou shalt love thy neighbour as thyself
ut if ye bite and devour one another take heed that ye be not consumed one of another
his  say then alk in the pirit and ye shall not fulfil the lust of the flesh
or the flesh lusteth against the pirit and the pirit against the flesh and these are contrary the one to the other so that ye cannot do the things that ye would
ut if ye be led of the pirit ye are not under the law
ow the works of the flesh are manifest which are these dultery fornication uncleanness lasciviousness idolatry witchcraft hatred variance emulations wrath strife seditions heresies envyings murders drunkenness revellings and such like of the which  tell you before as  have also told you in time past that they which do such things shall not inherit the kingdom of od
ut the fruit of the pirit is love joy peace longsuffering gentleness goodness faith meekness temperance against such there is no law
nd they that are hrists have crucified the flesh with the affections and lusts
f we live in the pirit let us also walk in the pirit
et us not be desirous of vain glory provoking one another envying one another
rethren if a man be overtaken in a fault ye which are spiritual restore such an one in the spirit of meekness considering thyself lest thou also be tempted
ear ye one anothers burdens and so fulfil the law of hrist
or if a man think himself to be something when he is nothing he deceiveth himself
ut let every man prove his own work and then shall he have rejoicing in himself alone and not in another
or every man shall bear his own burden
et him that is taught in the word communicate unto him that teacheth in all good things
e not deceived od is not mocked for whatsoever a man soweth that shall he also reap
or he that soweth to his flesh shall of the flesh reap corruption but he that soweth to the pirit shall of the pirit reap life everlasting
nd let us not be weary in well doing for in due season we shall reap if we faint not
s we have therefore opportunity let us do good unto all men especially unto them who are of the household of faith
e see how large a letter  have written unto you with mine own hand
s many as desire to make a fair shew in the flesh they constrain you to be circumcised only lest they should suffer persecution for the cross of hrist
or neither they themselves who are circumcised keep the law but desire to have you circumcised that they may glory in your flesh
ut od forbid that  should glory save in the cross of our ord esus hrist by whom the world is crucified unto me and  unto the world
or in hrist esus neither circumcision availeth any thing nor uncircumcision but a new creature
nd as many as walk according to this rule peace be on them and mercy and upon the srael of od
rom henceforth let no man trouble me for  bear in my body the marks of the ord esus
rethren the grace of our ord esus hrist be with your spirit
men
nto the alatians written from ome
aul an apostle of esus hrist by the will of od to the saints which are at phesus and to the faithful in hrist esus grace be to you and peace from od our ather and from the ord esus hrist
lessed be the od and ather of our ord esus hrist who hath blessed us with all spiritual blessings in heavenly places in hrist according as he hath chosen us in him before the foundation of the world that we should be holy and without blame before him in love having predestinated us unto the adoption of children by esus hrist to himself according to the good pleasure of his will to the praise of the glory of his grace wherein he hath made us accepted in the beloved
n whom we have redemption through his blood the forgiveness of sins according to the riches of his grace wherein he hath abounded toward us in all wisdom and prudence having made known unto us the mystery of his will according to his good pleasure which he hath purposed in himself that in the dispensation of the fulness of times he might gather together in one all things in hrist both which are in heaven and which are on earth even in him in whom also we have obtained an inheritance being predestinated according to the purpose of him who worketh all things after the counsel of his own will that we should be to the praise of his glory who first trusted in hrist
