n whom ye also trusted after that ye heard the word of truth the gospel of your salvation in whom also after that ye believed ye were sealed with that holy pirit of promise which is the earnest of our inheritance until the redemption of the purchased possession unto the praise of his glory
herefore  also after  heard of your faith in the ord esus and love unto all the saints cease not to give thanks for you making mention of you in my prayers that the od of our ord esus hrist the ather of glory may give unto you the spirit of wisdom and revelation in the knowledge of him the eyes of your understanding being enlightened that ye may know what is the hope of his calling and what the riches of the glory of his inheritance in the saints and what is the exceeding greatness of his power to usward who believe according to the working of his mighty power which he wrought in hrist when he raised him from the dead and set him at his own right hand in the heavenly places far above all principality and power and might and dominion and every name that is named not only in this world but also in that which is to come and hath put all things under his feet and gave him to be the head over all things to the church which is his body the fulness of him that filleth all in all
nd you hath he quickened who were dead in trespasses and sins wherein in time past ye walked according to the course of this world according to the prince of the power of the air the spirit that now worketh in the children of disobedience among whom also we all had our conversation in times past in the lusts of our flesh fulfilling the desires of the flesh and of the mind and were by nature the children of wrath even as others
ut od who is rich in mercy for his great love wherewith he loved us even when we were dead in sins hath quickened us together with hrist by grace ye are saved and hath raised us up together and made us sit together in heavenly places in hrist esus that in the ages to come he might shew the exceeding riches of his grace in his kindness toward us through hrist esus
or by grace are ye saved through faith and that not of yourselves it is the gift of od not of works lest any man should boast
or we are his workmanship created in hrist esus unto good works which od hath before ordained that we should walk in them
herefore remember that ye being in time past entiles in the flesh who are called ncircumcision by that which is called the ircumcision in the flesh made by hands that at that time ye were without hrist being aliens from the commonwealth of srael and strangers from the covenants of promise having no hope and without od in the world but now in hrist esus ye who sometimes were far off are made nigh by the blood of hrist
or he is our peace who hath made both one and hath broken down the middle wall of partition between us having abolished in his flesh the enmity even the law of commandments contained in ordinances for to make in himself of twain one new man so making peace and that he might reconcile both unto od in one body by the cross having slain the enmity thereby and came and preached peace to you which were afar off and to them that were nigh
or through him we both have access by one pirit unto the ather
ow therefore ye are no more strangers and foreigners but fellowcitizens with the saints and of the household of od and are built upon the foundation of the apostles and prophets esus hrist himself being the chief corner stone in whom all the building fitly framed together groweth unto an holy temple in the ord in whom ye also are builded together for an habitation of od through the pirit
or this cause  aul the prisoner of esus hrist for you entiles if ye have heard of the dispensation of the grace of od which is given me to youward how that by revelation he made known unto me the mystery as  wrote afore in few words whereby when ye read ye may understand my knowledge in the mystery of hrist which in other ages was not made known unto the sons of men as it is now revealed unto his holy apostles and prophets by the pirit that the entiles should be fellowheirs and of the same body and partakers of his promise in hrist by the gospel whereof  was made a minister according to the gift of the grace of od given unto me by the effectual working of his power
nto me who am less than the least of all saints is this grace given that  should preach among the entiles the unsearchable riches of hrist and to make all men see what is the fellowship of the mystery which from the beginning of the world hath been hid in od who created all things by esus hrist to the intent that now unto the principalities and powers in heavenly places might be known by the church the manifold wisdom of od according to the eternal purpose which he purposed in hrist esus our ord in whom we have boldness and access with confidence by the faith of him
herefore  desire that ye faint not at my tribulations for you which is your glory
or this cause  bow my knees unto the ather of our ord esus hrist of whom the whole family in heaven and earth is named that he would grant you according to the riches of his glory to be strengthened with might by his pirit in the inner man that hrist may dwell in your hearts by faith that ye being rooted and grounded in love may be able to comprehend with all saints what is the breadth and length and depth and height and to know the love of hrist which passeth knowledge that ye might be filled with all the fulness of od
ow unto him that is able to do exceeding abundantly above all that we ask or think according to the power that worketh in us unto him be glory in the church by hrist esus throughout all ages world without end
men
 therefore the prisoner of the ord beseech you that ye walk worthy of the vocation wherewith ye are called with all lowliness and meekness with longsuffering forbearing one another in love endeavouring to keep the unity of the pirit in the bond of peace
here is one body and one pirit even as ye are called in one hope of your calling one ord one faith one baptism one od and ather of all who is above all and through all and in you all
ut unto every one of us is given grace according to the measure of the gift of hrist
herefore he saith hen he ascended up on high he led captivity captive nd gave gifts unto men
ow that he ascended what is it but that he also descended first into the lower parts of the earth
e that descended is the same also that ascended up far above all heavens that he might fill all things nd he gave some apostles and some prophets and some evangelists and some pastors and teachers for the perfecting of the saints for the work of the ministry for the edifying of the body of hrist till we all come in the unity of the faith and of the knowledge of the on of od unto a perfect man unto the measure of the stature of the fulness of hrist that we henceforth be no more children tossed to and fro and carried about with every wind of doctrine by the sleight of men and cunning craftiness whereby they lie in wait to deceive but speaking the truth in love may grow up into him in all things which is the head even hrist from whom the whole body fitly joined together and compacted by that which every joint supplieth according to the effectual working in the measure of every part maketh increase of the body unto the edifying of itself in love
his  say therefore and testify in the ord that ye henceforth walk not as other entiles walk in the vanity of their mind having the understanding darkened being alienated from the life of od through the ignorance that is in them because of the blindness of their heart who being past feeling have given themselves over unto lasciviousness to work all uncleanness with greediness
ut ye have not so learned hrist if so be that ye have heard him and have been taught by him as the truth is in esus that ye put off concerning the former conversation the old man which is corrupt according to the deceitful lusts and be renewed in the spirit of your mind and that ye put on the new man which after od is created in righteousness and true holiness
herefore putting away lying speak every man truth with his neighbour for we are members one of another
e ye angry and sin not let not the sun go down upon your wrath neither give place to the devil
et him that stole steal no more but rather let him labour working with his hands the thing which is good that he may have to give to him that needeth
et no corrupt communication proceed out of your mouth but that which is good to the use of edifying that it may minister grace unto the hearers
nd grieve not the holy pirit of od whereby ye are sealed unto the day of redemption
et all bitterness and wrath and anger and clamour and evil speaking be put away from you with all malice and be ye kind one to another tenderhearted forgiving one another even as od for hrists sake hath forgiven you
e ye therefore followers of od as dear children and walk in love as hrist also hath loved us and hath given himself for us an offering and a sacrifice to od for a sweetsmelling savour
ut fornication and all uncleanness or covetousness let it not be once named among you as becometh saints neither filthiness nor foolish talking nor jesting which are not convenient but rather giving of thanks
or this ye know that no whoremonger nor unclean person nor covetous man who is an idolater hath any inheritance in the kingdom of hrist and of od
et no man deceive you with vain words for because of these things cometh the wrath of od upon the children of disobedience
e not ye therefore partakers with them
or ye were sometimes darkness but now are ye light in the ord walk as children of light for the fruit of the pirit is in all goodness and righteousness and truth proving what is acceptable unto the ord
nd have no fellowship with the unfruitful works of darkness but rather reprove them
or it is a shame even to speak of those things which are done of them in secret
ut all things that are reproved are made manifest by the light for whatsoever doth make manifest is light
herefore he saith wake thou that sleepest and arise from the dead and hrist shall give thee light
ee then that ye walk circumspectly not as fools but as wise redeeming the time because the days are evil
herefore be ye not unwise but understanding what the will of the ord is
nd be not drunk with wine wherein is excess but be filled with the pirit speaking to yourselves in psalms and hymns and spiritual songs singing and making melody in your heart to the ord giving thanks always for all things unto od and the ather in the name of our ord esus hrist submitting yourselves one to another in the fear of od
ives submit yourselves unto your own husbands as unto the ord
or the husband is the head of the wife even as hrist is the head of the church and he is the saviour of the body
herefore as the church is subject unto hrist so let the wives be to their own husbands in every thing
usbands love your wives even as hrist also loved the church and gave himself for it that he might sanctify and cleanse it with the washing of water by the word that he might present it to himself a glorious church not having spot or wrinkle or any such thing but that it should be holy and without blemish
o ought men to love their wives as their own bodies
e that loveth his wife loveth himself
or no man ever yet hated his own flesh but nourisheth and cherisheth it even as the ord the church for we are members of his body of his flesh and of his bones
or this cause shall a man leave his father and mother and shall be joined unto his wife and they two shall be one flesh
his is a great mystery but  speak concerning hrist and the church
evertheless let every one of you in particular so love his wife even as himself and the wife see that she reverence her husband
hildren obey your parents in the ord for this is right
onour thy father and mother which is the first commandment with promise that it may be well with thee and thou mayest live long on the earth
nd ye fathers provoke not your children to wrath but bring them up in the nurture and admonition of the ord
ervants be obedient to them that are your masters according to the flesh with fear and trembling in singleness of your heart as unto hrist not with eyeservice as menpleasers but as the servants of hrist doing the will of od from the heart with good will doing service as to the ord and not to men knowing that whatsoever good thing any man doeth the same shall he receive of the ord whether he be bond or free
nd ye masters do the same things unto them forbearing threatening knowing that your aster also is in heaven neither is there respect of persons with him
inally my brethren be strong in the ord and in the power of his might
ut on the whole armour of od that ye may be able to stand against the wiles of the devil
or we wrestle not against flesh and blood but against principalities against powers against the rulers of the darkness of this world against spiritual wickedness in high places
herefore take unto you the whole armour of od that ye may be able to withstand in the evil day and having done all to stand
tand therefore having your loins girt about with truth and having on the breastplate of righteousness and your feet shod with the preparation of the gospel of peace above all taking the shield of faith wherewith ye shall be able to quench all the fiery darts of the wicked
nd take the helmet of salvation and the sword of the pirit which is the word of od praying always with all prayer and supplication in the pirit and watching thereunto with all perseverance and supplication for all saints and for me that utterance may be given unto me that  may open my mouth boldly to make known the mystery of the gospel for which  am an ambassador in bonds that therein  may speak boldly as  ought to speak
ut that ye also may know my affairs and how  do ychicus a beloved brother and faithful minister in the ord shall make known to you all things whom  have sent unto you for the same purpose that ye might know our affairs and that he might comfort your hearts
eace be to the brethren and love with faith from od the ather and the ord esus hrist
race be with all them that love our ord esus hrist in sincerity
men
ritten from ome unto the phesians by ychicus
aul and imotheus the servants of esus hrist to all the saints in hrist esus which are at hilippi with the bishops and deacons grace be unto you and peace from od our ather and from the ord esus hrist
 thank my od upon every remembrance of you always in every prayer of mine for you all making request with joy for your fellowship in the gospel from the first day until now being confident of this very thing that he which hath begun a good work in you will perform it until the day of esus hrist even as it is meet for me to think this of you all because  have you in my heart inasmuch as both in my bonds and in the defence and confirmation of the gospel ye all are partakers of my grace
or od is my record how greatly  long after you all in the bowels of esus hrist
nd this  pray that your love may abound yet more and more in knowledge and in all judgment that ye may approve things that are excellent that ye may be sincere and without offence till the day of hrist being filled with the fruits of righteousness which are by esus hrist unto the glory and praise of od
ut  would ye should understand brethren that the things which happened unto me have fallen out rather unto the furtherance of the gospel so that my bonds in hrist are manifest in all the palace and in all other places and many of the brethren in the ord waxing confident by my bonds are much more bold to speak the word without fear
ome indeed preach hrist even of envy and strife and some also of good will the one preach hrist of contention not sincerely supposing to add affliction to my bonds but the other of love knowing that  am set for the defence of the gospel
hat then notwithstanding every way whether in pretence or in truth hrist is preached and  therein do rejoice yea and will rejoice
or  know that this shall turn to my salvation through your prayer and the supply of the pirit of esus hrist according to my earnest expectation and my hope that in nothing  shall be ashamed but that with all boldness as always so now also hrist shall be magnified in my body whether it be by life or by death
or to me to live is hrist and to die is gain
ut if  live in the flesh this is the fruit of my labour yet what  shall choose  wot not
or  am in a strait betwixt two having a desire to depart and to be with hrist which is far better nevertheless to abide in the flesh is more needful for you
nd having this confidence  know that  shall abide and continue with you all for your furtherance and joy of faith that your rejoicing may be more abundant in esus hrist for me by my coming to you again
nly let your conversation be as it becometh the gospel of hrist that whether  come and see you or else be absent  may hear of your affairs that ye stand fast in one spirit with one mind striving together for the faith of the gospel and in nothing terrified by your adversaries which is to them an evident token of perdition but to you of salvation and that of od
or unto you it is given in the behalf of hrist not only to believe on him but also to suffer for his sake having the same conflict which ye saw in me and now hear to be in me
f there be therefore any consolation in hrist if any comfort of love if any fellowship of the pirit if any bowels and mercies fulfil ye my joy that ye be likeminded having the same love being of one accord of one mind
et nothing be done through strife or vainglory but in lowliness of mind let each esteem other better than themselves
ook not every man on his own things but every man also on the things of others
et this mind be in you which was also in hrist esus who being in the form of od thought it not robbery to be equal with od but made himself of no reputation and took upon him the form of a servant and was made in the likeness of men and being found in fashion as a man he humbled himself and became obedient unto death even the death of the cross
herefore od also hath highly exalted him and given him a name which is above every name that at the name of esus every knee should bow of things in heaven and things in earth and things under the earth and that every tongue should confess that esus hrist is ord to the glory of od the ather
herefore my beloved as ye have always obeyed not as in my presence only but now much more in my absence work out your own salvation with fear and trembling
or it is od which worketh in you both to will and to do of his good pleasure
o all things without murmurings and disputings that ye may be blameless and harmless the sons of od without rebuke in the midst of a crooked and perverse nation among whom ye shine as lights in the world holding forth the word of life that  may rejoice in the day of hrist that  have not run in vain neither laboured in vain
ea and if  be offered upon the sacrifice and service of your faith  joy and rejoice with you all
or the same cause also do ye joy and rejoice with me
ut  trust in the ord esus to send imotheus shortly unto you that  also may be of good comfort when  know your state
or  have no man likeminded who will naturally care for your state
or all seek their own not the things which are esus hrists
ut ye know the proof of him that as a son with the father he hath served with me in the gospel
im therefore  hope to send presently so soon as  shall see how it will go with me
ut  trust in the ord that  also myself shall come shortly
et  supposed it necessary to send to you paphroditus my brother and companion in labour and fellowsoldier but your messenger and he that ministered to my wants
or he longed after you all and was full of heaviness because that ye had heard that he had been sick
or indeed he was sick nigh unto death but od had mercy on him and not on him only but on me also lest  should have sorrow upon sorrow
 sent him therefore the more carefully that when ye see him again ye may rejoice and that  may be the less sorrowful
eceive him therefore in the ord with all gladness and hold such in reputation because for the work of hrist he was nigh unto death not regarding his life to supply your lack of service toward me
inally my brethren rejoice in the ord
o write the same things to you to me indeed is not grievous but for you it is safe
eware of dogs beware of evil workers beware of the concision
or we are the circumcision which worship od in the spirit and rejoice in hrist esus and have no confidence in the flesh
hough  might also have confidence in the flesh
f any other man thinketh that he hath whereof he might trust in the flesh  more circumcised the eighth day of the stock of srael of the tribe of enjamin an ebrew of the ebrews as touching the law a harisee concerning zeal persecuting the church touching the righteousness which is in the law blameless
ut what things were gain to me those  counted loss for hrist
ea doubtless and  count all things but loss for the excellency of the knowledge of hrist esus my ord for whom  have suffered the loss of all things and do count them but dung that  may win hrist and be found in him not having mine own righteousness which is of the law but that which is through the faith of hrist the righteousness which is of od by faith that  may know him and the power of his resurrection and the fellowship of his sufferings being made conformable unto his death if by any means  might attain unto the resurrection of the dead
ot as though  had already attained either were already perfect but  follow after if that  may apprehend that for which also  am apprehended of hrist esus
rethren  count not myself to have apprehended but this one thing  do forgetting those things which are behind and reaching forth unto those things which are before  press toward the mark for the prize of the high calling of od in hrist esus
et us therefore as many as be perfect be thus minded and if in any thing ye be otherwise minded od shall reveal even this unto you
evertheless whereto we have already attained let us walk by the same rule let us mind the same thing
rethren be followers together of me and mark them which walk so as ye have us for an ensample
or many walk of whom  have told you often and now tell you even weeping that they are the enemies of the cross of hrist whose end is destruction whose od is their belly and whose glory is in their shame who mind earthly things or our conversation is in heaven from whence also we look for the aviour the ord esus hrist who shall change our vile body that it may be fashioned like unto his glorious body according to the working whereby he is able even to subdue all things unto himself
herefore my brethren dearly beloved and longed for my joy and crown so stand fast in the ord my dearly beloved
 beseech uodias and beseech yntyche that they be of the same mind in the ord
nd  intreat thee also true yokefellow help those women which laboured with me in the gospel with lement also and with other my fellowlabourers whose names are in the book of life
ejoice in the ord alway and again  say ejoice
et your moderation be known unto all men
he ord is at hand
e careful for nothing but in every thing by prayer and supplication with thanksgiving let your requests be made known unto od
nd the peace of od which passeth all understanding shall keep your hearts and minds through hrist esus
inally brethren whatsoever things are true whatsoever things are honest whatsoever things are just whatsoever things are pure whatsoever things are lovely whatsoever things are of good report if there be any virtue and if there be any praise think on these things
hose things which ye have both learned and received and heard and seen in me do and the od of peace shall be with you
ut  rejoiced in the ord greatly that now at the last your care of me hath flourished again wherein ye were also careful but ye lacked opportunity
ot that  speak in respect of want for  have learned in whatsoever state  am therewith to be content
 know both how to be abased and  know how to abound every where and in all things  am instructed both to be full and to be hungry both to abound and to suffer need
 can do all things through hrist which strengtheneth me
otwithstanding ye have well done that ye did communicate with my affliction
ow ye hilippians know also that in the beginning of the gospel when  departed from acedonia no church communicated with me as concerning giving and receiving but ye only
or even in hessalonica ye sent once and again unto my necessity
ot because  desire a gift but  desire fruit that may abound to your account
ut  have all and abound  am full having received of paphroditus the things which were sent from you an odour of a sweet smell a sacrifice acceptable wellpleasing to od
ut my od shall supply all your need according to his riches in glory by hrist esus
ow unto od and our ather be glory for ever and ever
men
alute every saint in hrist esus
he brethren which are with me greet you
ll the saints salute you chiefly they that are of  sars household
he grace of our ord esus hrist be with you all
men
t was written to the hilippians from ome by paphroditus
aul an apostle of esus hrist by the will of od and imotheus our brother to the saints and faithful brethren in hrist which are at olosse race be unto you and peace from od our ather and the ord esus hrist
e give thanks to od and the ather of our ord esus hrist praying always for you since we heard of your faith in hrist esus and of the love which ye have to all the saints for the hope which is laid up for you in heaven whereof ye heard before in the word of the truth of the gospel which is come unto you as it is in all the world and bringeth forth fruit as it doth also in you since the day ye heard of it and knew the grace of od in truth as ye also learned of paphras our dear fellowservant who is for you a faithful minister of hrist who also declared unto us your love in the pirit
or this cause we also since the day we heard it do not cease to pray for you and to desire that ye might be filled with the knowledge of his will in all wisdom and spiritual understanding that ye might walk worthy of the ord unto all pleasing being fruitful in every good work and increasing in the knowledge of od strengthened with all might according to his glorious power unto all patience and longsuffering with joyfulness giving thanks unto the ather which hath made us meet to be partakers of the inheritance of the saints in light who hath delivered us from the power of darkness and hath translated us into the kingdom of his dear on in whom we have redemption through his blood even the forgiveness of sins ho is the image of the invisible od the firstborn of every creature for by him were all things created that are in heaven and that are in earth visible and invisible whether they be thrones or dominions or principalities or powers all things were created by him and for him and he is before all things and by him all things consist
nd he is the head of the body the church who is the beginning the firstborn from the dead that in all things he might have the preeminence
or it pleased the ather that in him should all fulness dwell and having made peace through the blood of his cross by him to reconcile all things unto himself by him  say whether they be things in earth or things in heaven
nd you that were sometime alienated and enemies in your mind by wicked works yet now hath he reconciled in the body of his flesh through death to present you holy and unblameable and unreproveable in his sight if ye continue in the faith grounded and settled and be not moved away from the hope of the gospel which ye have heard and which was preached to every creature which is under heaven whereof  aul am made a minister who now rejoice in my sufferings for you and fill up that which is behind of the afflictions of hrist in my flesh for his bodys sake which is the church whereof  am made a minister according to the dispensation of od which is given to me for you to fulfil the word of od even the mystery which hath been hid from ages and from generations but now is made manifest to his saints to whom od would make known what is the riches of the glory of this mystery among the entiles which is hrist in you the hope of glory whom we preach warning every man and teaching every man in all wisdom that we may present every man perfect in hrist esus whereunto  also labour striving according to his working which worketh in me mightily
or  would that ye knew what great conflict  have for you and for them at aodicea and for as many as have not seen my face in the flesh that their hearts might be comforted being knit together in love and unto all riches of the full assurance of understanding to the acknowledgement of the mystery of od and of the ather and of hrist in whom are hid all the treasures of wisdom and knowledge
nd this  say lest any man should beguile you with enticing words
or though  be absent in the flesh yet am  with you in the spirit joying and beholding your order and the stedfastness of your faith in hrist
s ye have therefore received hrist esus the ord so walk ye in him rooted and built up in him and stablished in the faith as ye have been taught abounding therein with thanksgiving
eware lest any man spoil you through philosophy and vain deceit after the tradition of men after the rudiments of the world and not after hrist
or in him dwelleth all the fulness of the odhead bodily
nd ye are complete in him which is the head of all principality and power in whom also ye are circumcised with the circumcision made without hands in putting off the body of the sins of the flesh by the circumcision of hrist buried with him in baptism wherein also ye are risen with him through the faith of the operation of od who hath raised him from the dead
nd you being dead in your sins and the uncircumcision of your flesh hath he quickened together with him having forgiven you all trespasses blotting out the handwriting of ordinances that was against us which was contrary to us and took it out of the way nailing it to his cross and having spoiled principalities and powers he made a shew of them openly triumphing over them in it
et no man therefore judge you in meat or in drink or in respect of an holyday or of the new moon or of the sabbath days which are a shadow of things to come but the body is of hrist
et no man beguile you of your reward in a voluntary humility and worshipping of angels intruding into those things which he hath not seen vainly puffed up by his fleshly mind and not holding the ead from which all the body by joints and bands having nourishment ministered and knit together increaseth with the increase of od
herefore if ye be dead with hrist from the rudiments of the world why as though living in the world are ye subject to ordinances touch not taste not handle not which all are to perish with the using after the commandments and doctrines of men
hich things have indeed a shew of wisdom in will worship and humility and neglecting of the body not in any honour to the satisfying of the flesh
f ye then be risen with hrist seek those things which are above where hrist sitteth on the right hand of od
et your affection on things above not on things on the earth
or ye are dead and your life is hid with hrist in od
hen hrist who is our life shall appear then shall ye also appear with him in glory
ortify therefore your members which are upon the earth fornication uncleanness inordinate affection evil concupiscence and covetousness which is idolatry for which things sake the wrath of od cometh on the children of disobedience in the which ye also walked some time when ye lived in them
ut now ye also put off all these anger wrath malice blasphemy filthy communication out of your mouth
ie not one to another seeing that ye have put off the old man with his deeds and have put on the new man which is renewed in knowledge after the image of him that created him where there is neither reek nor ew circumcision nor uncircumcision arbarian cythian bond nor free but hrist is all and in all
ut on therefore as the elect of od holy and beloved bowels of mercies kindness humbleness of mind meekness longsuffering forbearing one another and forgiving one another if any man have a quarrel against any even as hrist forgave you so also do ye
nd above all these things put on charity which is the bond of perfectness
nd let the peace of od rule in your hearts to the which also ye are called in one body and be ye thankful
et the word of hrist dwell in you richly in all wisdom teaching and admonishing one another in psalms and hymns and spiritual songs singing with grace in your hearts to the ord
nd whatsoever ye do in word or deed do all in the name of the ord esus giving thanks to od and the ather by him
ives submit yourselves unto your own husbands as it is fit in the ord
usbands love your wives and be not bitter against them
hildren obey your parents in all things for this is well pleasing unto the ord
athers provoke not your children to anger lest they be discouraged
ervants obey in all things your masters according to the flesh not with eyeservice as menpleasers but in singleness of heart fearing od and whatsoever ye do do it heartily as to the ord and not unto men knowing that of the ord ye shall receive the reward of the inheritance for ye serve the ord hrist
ut he that doeth wrong shall receive for the wrong which he hath done and there is no respect of persons
asters give unto your servants that which is just and equal knowing that ye also have a aster in heaven
ontinue in prayer and watch in the same with thanksgiving withal praying also for us that od would open unto us a door of utterance to speak the mystery of hrist for which  am also in bonds that  may make it manifest as  ought to speak
alk in wisdom toward them that are without redeeming the time
et your speech be alway with grace seasoned with salt that ye may know how ye ought to answer every man
ll my state shall ychicus declare unto you who is a beloved brother and a faithful minister and fellowservant in the ord whom  have sent unto you for the same purpose that he might know your estate and comfort your hearts with nesimus a faithful and beloved brother who is one of you
hey shall make known unto you all things which are done here
ristarchus my fellowprisoner saluteth you and arcus sisters son to arnabas touching whom ye received commandments if he come unto you receive him and esus which is called ustus who are of the circumcision
hese only are my fellowworkers unto the kingdom of od which have been a comfort unto me
paphras who is one of you a servant of hrist saluteth you always labouring fervently for you in prayers that ye may stand perfect and complete in all the will of od
or  bear him record that he hath a great zeal for you and them that are in aodicea and them in ierapolis
uke the beloved physician and emas greet you
alute the brethren which are in aodicea and ymphas and the church which is in his house
nd when this epistle is read among you cause that it be read also in the church of the aodiceans and that ye likewise read the epistle from aodicea
nd say to rchippus ake heed to the ministry which thou hast received in the ord that thou fulfil it
he salutation by the hand of me aul
emember my bonds
race be with you
men
ritten from ome to the olossians by ychicus and nesimus
aul and ilvanus and imotheus unto the church of the hessalonians which is in od the ather and in the ord esus hrist race be unto you and peace from od our ather and the ord esus hrist
e give thanks to od always for you all making mention of you in our prayers remembering without ceasing your work of faith and labour of love and patience of hope in our ord esus hrist in the sight of od and our ather knowing brethren beloved your election of od
or our gospel came not unto you in word only but also in power and in the oly host and in much assurance as ye know what manner of men we were among you for your sake
nd ye became followers of us and of the ord having received the word in much affliction with joy of the oly host so that ye were ensamples to all that believe in acedonia and chaia
or from you sounded out the word of the ord not only in acedonia and chaia but also in every place your faith to odward is spread abroad so that we need not to speak any thing
or they themselves shew of us what manner of entering in we had unto you and how ye turned to od from idols to serve the living and true od and to wait for his on from heaven whom he raised from the dead even esus which delivered us from the wrath to come
or yourselves brethren know our entrance in unto you that it was not in vain but even after that we had suffered before and were shamefully entreated as ye know at hilippi we were bold in our od to speak unto you the gospel of od with much contention
or our exhortation was not of deceit nor of uncleanness nor in guile but as we were allowed of od to be put in trust with the gospel even so we speak not as pleasing men but od which trieth our hearts
or neither at any time used we flattering words as ye know nor a cloke of covetousness od is witness nor of men sought we glory neither of you nor yet of others when we might have been burdensome as the apostles of hrist
ut we were gentle among you even as a nurse cherisheth her children so being affectionately desirous of you we were willing to have imparted unto you not the gospel of od only but also our own souls because ye were dear unto us
or ye remember brethren our labour and travail for labouring night and day because we would not be chargeable unto any of you we preached unto you the gospel of od
e are witnesses and od also how holily and justly and unblameably we behaved ourselves among you that believe as ye know how we exhorted and comforted and charged every one of you as a father doth his children that ye would walk worthy of od who hath called you unto his kingdom and glory
or this cause also thank we od without ceasing because when ye received the word of od which ye heard of us ye received it not as the word of men but as it is in truth the word of od which effectually worketh also in you that believe
or ye brethren became followers of the churches of od which in ud a are in hrist esus for ye also have suffered like things of your own countrymen even as they have of the ews who both killed the ord esus and their own prophets and have persecuted us and they please not od and are contrary to all men forbidding us to speak to the entiles that they might be saved to fill up their sins alway for the wrath is come upon them to the uttermost
ut we brethren being taken from you for a short time in presence not in heart endeavoured the more abundantly to see your face with great desire
herefore we would have come unto you even  aul once and again but atan hindered us
or what is our hope or joy or crown of rejoicing
re not even ye in the presence of our ord esus hrist at his coming
or ye are our glory and joy
herefore when we could no longer forbear we thought it good to be left at thens alone and sent imotheus our brother and minister of od and our fellowlabourer in the gospel of hrist to establish you and to comfort you concerning your faith that no man should be moved by these afflictions for yourselves know that we are appointed thereunto
or verily when we were with you we told you before that we should suffer tribulation even as it came to pass and ye know
or this cause when  could no longer forbear  sent to know your faith lest by some means the tempter have tempted you and our labour be in vain
ut now when imotheus came from you unto us and brought us good tidings of your faith and charity and that ye have good remembrance of us always desiring greatly to see us as we also to see you therefore brethren we were comforted over you in all our affliction and distress by your faith for now we live if ye stand fast in the ord
or what thanks can we render to od again for you for all the joy wherewith we joy for your sakes before our od night and day praying exceedingly that we might see your face and might perfect that which is lacking in your faith
ow od himself and our ather and our ord esus hrist direct our way unto you
nd the ord make you to increase and abound in love one toward another and toward all men even as we do toward you to the end he may stablish your hearts unblameable in holiness before od even our ather at the coming of our ord esus hrist with all his saints
urthermore then we beseech you brethren and exhort you by the ord esus that as ye have received of us how ye ought to walk and to please od so ye would abound more and more
or ye know what commandments we gave you by the ord esus
or this is the will of od even your sanctification that ye should abstain from fornication that every one of you should know how to possess his vessel in sanctification and honour not in the lust of concupiscence even as the entiles which know not od that no man go beyond and defraud his brother in any matter because that the ord is the avenger of all such as we also have forewarned you and testified
or od hath not called us unto uncleanness but unto holiness
e therefore that despiseth despiseth not man but od who hath also given unto us his holy pirit
ut as touching brotherly love ye need not that  write unto you for ye yourselves are taught of od to love one another
nd indeed ye do it toward all the brethren which are in all acedonia but we beseech you brethren that ye increase more and more and that ye study to be quiet and to do your own business and to work with your own hands as we commanded you that ye may walk honestly toward them that are without and that ye may have lack of nothing
ut  would not have you to be ignorant brethren concerning them which are asleep that ye sorrow not even as others which have no hope
or if we believe that esus died and rose again even so them also which sleep in esus will od bring with him
or this we say unto you by the word of the ord that we which are alive and remain unto the coming of the ord shall not prevent them which are asleep
or the ord himself shall descend from heaven with a shout with the voice of the archangel and with the trump of od and the dead in hrist shall rise first then we which are alive and remain shall be caught up together with them in the clouds to meet the ord in the air and so shall we ever be with the ord
herefore comfort one another with these words
ut of the times and the seasons brethren ye have no need that  write unto you
or yourselves know perfectly that the day of the ord so cometh as a thief in the night
or when they shall say eace and safety then sudden destruction cometh upon them as travail upon a woman with child and they shall not escape
ut ye brethren are not in darkness that that day should overtake you as a thief
e are all the children of light and the children of the day we are not of the night nor of darkness
herefore let us not sleep as do others but let us watch and be sober
or they that sleep sleep in the night and they that be drunken are drunken in the night
ut let us who are of the day be sober putting on the breastplate of faith and love and for an helmet the hope of salvation
or od hath not appointed us to wrath but to obtain salvation by our ord esus hrist who died for us that whether we wake or sleep we should live together with him
herefore comfort yourselves together and edify one another even as also ye do
nd we beseech you brethren to know them which labour among you and are over you in the ord and admonish you and to esteem them very highly in love for their works sake
nd be at peace among yourselves
ow we exhort you brethren warn them that are unruly comfort the feebleminded support the weak be patient toward all men
ee that none render evil for evil unto any man but ever follow that which is good both among yourselves and to all men
ejoice evermore
ray without ceasing
n every thing give thanks for this is the will of od in hrist esus concerning you
uench not the pirit
espise not prophesyings
rove all things hold fast that which is good
bstain from all appearance of evil
nd the very od of peace sanctify you wholly and  pray od your whole spirit and soul and body be preserved blameless unto the coming of our ord esus hrist
aithful is he that calleth you who also will do it
rethren pray for us
reet all the brethren with an holy kiss
 charge you by the ord that this epistle be read unto all the holy brethren
he grace of our ord esus hrist be with you
men
he first epistle unto the hessalonians was written from thens
aul and ilvanus and imotheus unto the church of the hessalonians in od our ather and the ord esus hrist grace unto you and peace from od our ather and the ord esus hrist
e are bound to thank od always for you brethren as it is meet because that your faith groweth exceedingly and the charity of every one of you all toward each other aboundeth so that we ourselves glory in you in the churches of od for your patience and faith in all your persecutions and tribulations that ye endure which is a manifest token of the righteous judgment of od that ye may be counted worthy of the kingdom of od for which ye also suffer seeing it is a righteous thing with od to recompense tribulation to them that trouble you and to you who are troubled rest with us when the ord esus shall be revealed from heaven with his mighty angels in flaming fire taking vengeance on them that know not od and that obey not the gospel of our ord esus hrist who shall be punished with everlasting destruction from the presence of the ord and from the glory of his power when he shall come to be glorified in his saints and to be admired in all them that believe because our testimony among you was believed in that day
herefore also we pray always for you that our od would count you worthy of this calling and fulfil all the good pleasure of his goodness and the work of faith with power that the name of our ord esus hrist may be glorified in you and ye in him according to the grace of our od and the ord esus hrist
ow we beseech you brethren by the coming of our ord esus hrist and by our gathering together unto him that ye be not soon shaken in mind or be troubled neither by spirit nor by word nor by letter as from us as that the day of hrist is at hand
et no man deceive you by any means for that day shall not come except there come a falling away first and that man of sin be revealed the son of perdition who opposeth and exalteth himself above all that is called od or that is worshipped so that he as od sitteth in the temple of od shewing himself that he is od
emember ye not that when  was yet with you  told you these things
nd now ye know what withholdeth that he might be revealed in his time
or the mystery of iniquity doth already work only he who now letteth will let until he be taken out of the way
nd then shall that icked be revealed whom the ord shall consume with the spirit of his mouth and shall destroy with the brightness of his coming even him whose coming is after the working of atan with all power and signs and lying wonders and with all deceivableness of unrighteousness in them that perish because they received not the love of the truth that they might be saved
nd for this cause od shall send them strong delusion that they should believe a lie that they all might be damned who believed not the truth but had pleasure in unrighteousness
ut we are bound to give thanks alway to od for you brethren beloved of the ord because od hath from the beginning chosen you to salvation through sanctification of the pirit and belief of the truth whereunto he called you by our gospel to the obtaining of the glory of our ord esus hrist
herefore brethren stand fast and hold the traditions which ye have been taught whether by word or our epistle
ow our ord esus hrist himself and od even our ather which hath loved us and hath given us everlasting consolation and good hope through grace comfort your hearts and stablish you in every good word and work
inally brethren pray for us that the word of the ord may have free course and be glorified even as it is with you and that we may be delivered from unreasonable and wicked men for all men have not faith
ut the ord is faithful who shall stablish you and keep you from evil
nd we have confidence in the ord touching you that ye both do and will do the things which we command you
nd the ord direct your hearts into the love of od and into the patient waiting for hrist
ow we command you brethren in the name of our ord esus hrist that ye withdraw yourselves from every brother that walketh disorderly and not after the tradition which he received of us
or yourselves know how ye ought to follow us for we behaved not ourselves disorderly among you neither did we eat any mans bread for nought but wrought with labour and travail night and day that we might not be chargeable to any of you not because we have not power but to make ourselves an ensample unto you to follow us
or even when we were with you this we commanded you that if any would not work neither should he eat
or we hear that there are some which walk among you disorderly working not at all but are busybodies
ow them that are such we command and exhort by our ord esus hrist that with quietness they work and eat their own bread
ut ye brethren be not weary in well doing
nd if any man obey not our word by this epistle note that man and have no company with him that he may be ashamed
et count him not as an enemy but admonish him as a brother
ow the ord of peace himself give you peace always by all means
he ord be with you all
he salutation of aul with mine own hand which is the token in every epistle so  write
he grace of our ord esus hrist be with you all
men
he second epistle to the hessalonians was written from thens
aul an apostle of esus hrist by the commandment of od our aviour and ord esus hrist which is our hope unto imothy my own son in the faith race mercy and peace from od our ather and esus hrist our ord
s  besought thee to abide still at phesus when  went into acedonia that thou mightest charge some that they teach no other doctrine neither give heed to fables and endless genealogies which minister questions rather than godly edifying which is in faith so do
ow the end of the commandment is charity out of a pure heart and of a good conscience and of faith unfeigned from which some having swerved have turned aside unto vain jangling desiring to be teachers of the law understanding neither what they say nor whereof they affirm
ut we know that the law is good if a man use it lawfully knowing this that the law is not made for a righteous man but for the lawless and disobedient for the ungodly and for sinners for unholy and profane for murderers of fathers and murderers of mothers for manslayers for whoremongers for them that defile themselves with mankind for menstealers for liars for perjured persons and if there be any other thing that is contrary to sound doctrine according to the glorious gospel of the blessed od which was committed to my trust
nd  thank hrist esus our ord who hath enabled me for that he counted me faithful putting me into the ministry who was before a blasphemer and a persecutor and injurious but  obtained mercy because  did it ignorantly in unbelief
nd the grace of our ord was exceeding abundant with faith and love which is in hrist esus
his is a faithful saying and worthy of all acceptation that hrist esus came into the world to save sinners of whom  am chief
owbeit for this cause  obtained mercy that in me first esus hrist might shew forth all longsuffering for a pattern to them which should hereafter believe on him to life everlasting
ow unto the ing eternal immortal invisible the only wise od be honour and glory for ever and ever
men
his charge  commit unto thee son imothy according to the prophecies which went before on thee that thou by them mightest war a good warfare holding faith and a good conscience which some having put away concerning faith have made shipwreck of whom is ymen us and lexander whom  have delivered unto atan that they may learn not to blaspheme
 exhort therefore that first of all supplications prayers intercessions and giving of thanks be made for all men for kings and for all that are in authority that we may lead a quiet and peaceable life in all godliness and honesty
or this is good and acceptable in the sight of od our aviour who will have all men to be saved and to come unto the knowledge of the truth
or there is one od and one mediator between od and men the man hrist esus who gave himself a ransom for all to be testified in due time
hereunto  am ordained a preacher and an apostle  speak the truth in hrist and lie not a teacher of the entiles in faith and verity
 will therefore that men pray every where lifting up holy hands without wrath and doubting
n like manner also that women adorn themselves in modest apparel with shamefacedness and sobriety not with broided hair or gold or pearls or costly array but which becometh women professing godliness with good works
et the woman learn in silence with all subjection
ut  suffer not a woman to teach nor to usurp authority over the man but to be in silence
or dam was first formed then ve
nd dam was not deceived but the woman being deceived was in the transgression
otwithstanding she shall be saved in childbearing if they continue in faith and charity and holiness with sobriety
his is a true saying f a man desire the office of a bishop he desireth a good work
 bishop then must be blameless the husband of one wife vigilant sober of good behaviour given to hospitality apt to teach not given to wine no striker not greedy of filthy lucre but patient not a brawler not covetous one that ruleth well his own house having his children in subjection with all gravity for if a man know not how to rule his own house how shall he take care of the church of od ot a novice lest being lifted up with pride he fall into the condemnation of the devil
oreover he must have a good report of them which are without lest he fall into reproach and the snare of the devil
ikewise must the deacons be grave not doubletongued not given to much wine not greedy of filthy lucre holding the mystery of the faith in a pure conscience
nd let these also first be proved then let them use the office of a deacon being found blameless
ven so must their wives be grave not slanderers sober faithful in all things
et the deacons be the husbands of one wife ruling their children and their own houses well
or they that have used the office of a deacon well purchase to themselves a good degree and great boldness in the faith which is in hrist esus
hese things write  unto thee hoping to come unto thee shortly ut if  tarry long that thou mayest know how thou oughtest to behave thyself in the house of od which is the church of the living od the pillar and ground of the truth
nd without controversy great is the mystery of godliness od was manifest in the flesh justified in the pirit seen of angels preached unto the entiles believed on in the world received up into glory
ow the pirit speaketh expressly that in the latter times some shall depart from the faith giving heed to seducing spirits and doctrines of devils speaking lies in hypocrisy having their conscience seared with a hot iron forbidding to marry and commanding to abstain from meats which od hath created to be received with thanksgiving of them which believe and know the truth
or every creature of od is good and nothing to be refused if it be received with thanksgiving for it is sanctified by the word of od and prayer
f thou put the brethren in remembrance of these things thou shalt be a good minister of esus hrist nourished up in the words of faith and of good doctrine whereunto thou hast attained
ut refuse profane and old wives fables and exercise thyself rather unto godliness
or bodily exercise profiteth little but godliness is profitable unto all things having promise of the life that now is and of that which is to come
his is a faithful saying and worthy of all acceptation
or therefore we both labour and suffer reproach because we trust in the living od who is the aviour of all men specially of those that believe
hese things command and teach
et no man despise thy youth but be thou an example of the believers in word in conversation in charity in spirit in faith in purity
ill  come give attendance to reading to exhortation to doctrine
eglect not the gift that is in thee which was given thee by prophecy with the laying on of the hands of the presbytery
editate upon these things give thyself wholly to them that thy profiting may appear to all
ake heed unto thyself and unto the doctrine continue in them for in doing this thou shalt both save thyself and them that hear thee
ebuke not an elder but intreat him as a father and the younger men as brethren the elder women as mothers the younger as sisters with all purity
onour widows that are widows indeed
ut if any widow have children or nephews let them learn first to shew piety at home and to requite their parents for that is good and acceptable before od
ow she that is a widow indeed and desolate trusteth in od and continueth in supplications and prayers night and day
ut she that liveth in pleasure is dead while she liveth
nd these things give in charge that they may be blameless
ut if any provide not for his own and specially for those of his own house he hath denied the faith and is worse than an infidel
et not a widow be taken into the number under threescore years old having been the wife of one man well reported of for good works if she have brought up children if she have lodged strangers if she have washed the saints feet if she have relieved the afflicted if she have diligently followed every good work
ut the younger widows refuse for when they have begun to wax wanton against hrist they will marry having damnation because they have cast off their first faith
nd withal they learn to be idle wandering about from house to house and not only idle but tattlers also and busybodies speaking things which they ought not
 will therefore that the younger women marry bear children guide the house give none occasion to the adversary to speak reproachfully
or some are already turned aside after atan
f any man or woman that believeth have widows let them relieve them and let not the church be charged that it may relieve them that are widows indeed
et the elders that rule well be counted worthy of double honour especially they who labour in the word and doctrine
or the scripture saith hou shalt not muzzle the ox that treadeth out the corn
nd he labourer is worthy of his reward
gainst an elder receive not an accusation but before two or three witnesses
hem that sin rebuke before all that others also may fear
 charge thee before od and the ord esus hrist and the elect angels that thou observe these things without preferring one before another doing nothing by partiality
ay hands suddenly on no man neither be partaker of other mens sins keep thyself pure
rink no longer water but use a little wine for thy stomachs sake and thine often infirmities
ome mens sins are open beforehand going before to judgment and some men they follow after
ikewise also the good works of some are manifest beforehand and they that are otherwise cannot be hid
et as many servants as are under the yoke count their own masters worthy of all honour that the name of od and his doctrine be not blasphemed
nd they that have believing masters let them not despise them because they are brethren but rather do them service because they are faithful and beloved partakers of the benefit
hese things teach and exhort
f any man teach otherwise and consent not to wholesome words even the words of our ord esus hrist and to the doctrine which is according to godliness he is proud knowing nothing but doting about questions and strifes of words whereof cometh envy strife railings evil surmisings perverse disputings of men of corrupt minds and destitute of the truth supposing that gain is godliness from such withdraw thyself
ut godliness with contentment is great gain
or we brought nothing into this world and it is certain we can carry nothing out
nd having food and raiment let us be therewith content
ut they that will be rich fall into temptation and a snare and into many foolish and hurtful lusts which drown men in destruction and perdition
or the love of money is the root of all evil which while some coveted after they have erred from the faith and pierced themselves through with many sorrows
ut thou  man of od flee these things and follow after righteousness godliness faith love patience meekness
ight the good fight of faith lay hold on eternal life whereunto thou art also called and hast professed a good profession before many witnesses
 give thee charge in the sight of od who quickeneth all things and before hrist esus who before ontius ilate witnessed a good confession that thou keep this commandment without spot unrebukeable until the appearing of our ord esus hrist which in his times he shall shew who is the blessed and only otentate the ing of kings and ord of lords who only hath immortality dwelling in the light which no man can approach unto whom no man hath seen nor can see to whom be honour and power everlasting
men
harge them that are rich in this world that they be not highminded nor trust in uncertain riches but in the living od who giveth us richly all things to enjoy that they do good that they be rich in good works ready to distribute willing to communicate laying up in store for themselves a good foundation against the time to come that they may lay hold on eternal life
 imothy keep that which is committed to thy trust avoiding profane and vain babblings and oppositions of science falsely so called which some professing have erred concerning the faith
race be with thee
men
he first to imothy was written from aodicea which is the chiefest city of hrygia acatiana
aul an apostle of esus hrist by the will of od according to the promise of life which is in hrist esus o imothy my dearly beloved son race mercy and peace from od the ather and hrist esus our ord
 thank od whom  serve from my forefathers with pure conscience that without ceasing  have remembrance of thee in my prayers night and day greatly desiring to see thee being mindful of thy tears that  may be filled with joy when  call to remembrance the unfeigned faith that is in thee which dwelt first in thy grandmother ois and thy mother unice and  am persuaded that in thee also
herefore  put thee in remembrance that thou stir up the gift of od which is in thee by the putting on of my hands
or od hath not given us the spirit of fear but of power and of love and of a sound mind
e not thou therefore ashamed of the testimony of our ord nor of me his prisoner but be thou partaker of the afflictions of the gospel according to the power of od who hath saved us and called us with an holy calling not according to our works but according to his own purpose and grace which was given us in hrist esus before the world began but is now made manifest by the appearing of our aviour esus hrist who hath abolished death and hath brought life and immortality to light through the gospel whereunto  am appointed a preacher and an apostle and a teacher of the entiles
or the which cause  also suffer these things nevertheless  am not ashamed for  know whom  have believed and am persuaded that he is able to keep that which  have committed unto him against that day
old fast the form of sound words which thou hast heard of me in faith and love which is in hrist esus
hat good thing which was committed unto thee keep by the oly host which dwelleth in us
his thou knowest that all they which are in sia be turned away from me of whom are hygellus and ermogenes
he ord give mercy unto the house of nesiphorus for he oft refreshed me and was not ashamed of my chain but when he was in ome he sought me out very diligently and found me
he ord grant unto him that he may find mercy of the ord in that day and in how many things he ministered unto me at phesus thou knowest very well
hou therefore my son be strong in the grace that is in hrist esus
nd the things that thou hast heard of me among many witnesses the same commit thou to faithful men who shall be able to teach others also
hou therefore endure hardness as a good soldier of esus hrist
o man that warreth entangleth himself with the affairs of this life that he may please him who hath chosen him to be a soldier
nd if a man also strive for masteries yet is he not crowned except he strive lawfully
he husbandman that laboureth must be first partaker of the fruits
onsider what  say and the ord give thee understanding in all things
emember that esus hrist of the seed of avid was raised from the dead according to my gospel wherein  suffer trouble as an evil doer even unto bonds but the word of od is not bound
herefore  endure all things for the elects sakes that they may also obtain the salvation which is in hrist esus with eternal glory
t is a faithful saying or if we be dead with him we shall also live with him if we suffer we shall also reign with him if we deny him he also will deny us if we believe not yet he abideth faithful he cannot deny himself
f these things put them in remembrance charging them before the ord that they strive not about words to no profit but to the subverting of the hearers
tudy to shew thyself approved unto od a workman that needeth not to be ashamed rightly dividing the word of truth
ut shun profane and vain babblings for they will increase unto more ungodliness
nd their word will eat as doth a canker of whom is ymen us and hiletus who concerning the truth have erred saying that the resurrection is past already and overthrow the faith of some
evertheless the foundation of od standeth sure having this seal he ord knoweth them that are his
nd et every one that nameth the name of hrist depart from iniquity
ut in a great house there are not only vessels of gold and of silver but also of wood and of earth and some to honour and some to dishonour
f a man therefore purge himself from these he shall be a vessel unto honour sanctified and meet for the masters use and prepared unto every good work
lee also youthful lusts but follow righteousness faith charity peace with them that call on the ord out of a pure heart
ut foolish and unlearned questions avoid knowing that they do gender strifes
nd the servant of the ord must not strive but be gentle unto all men apt to teach patient in meekness instructing those that oppose themselves if od peradventure will give them repentance to the acknowledging of the truth and that they may recover themselves out of the snare of the devil who are taken captive by him at his will
his know also that in the last days perilous times shall come
or men shall be lovers of their own selves covetous boasters proud blasphemers disobedient to parents unthankful unholy without natural affection trucebreakers false accusers incontinent fierce despisers of those that are good traitors heady highminded lovers of pleasures more than lovers of od having a form of godliness but denying the power thereof from such turn away
or of this sort are they which creep into houses and lead captive silly women laden with sins led away with divers lusts ever learning and never able to come to the knowledge of the truth
ow as annes and ambres withstood oses so do these also resist the truth men of corrupt minds reprobate concerning the faith
ut they shall proceed no further for their folly shall be manifest unto all men as theirs also was
ut thou hast fully known my doctrine manner of life purpose faith longsuffering charity patience persecutions afflictions which came unto me at ntioch at conium at ystra what persecutions  endured but out of them all the ord delivered me
ea and all that will live godly in hrist esus shall suffer persecution
ut evil men and seducers shall wax worse and worse deceiving and being deceived
ut continue thou in the things which thou hast learned and hast been assured of knowing of whom thou hast learned them and that from a child thou hast known the holy scriptures which are able to make thee wise unto salvation through faith which is in hrist esus
ll scripture is given by inspiration of od and is profitable for doctrine for reproof for correction for instruction in righteousness that the man of od may be perfect throughly furnished unto all good works
 charge thee therefore before od and the ord esus hrist who shall judge the quick and the dead at his appearing and his kingdom preach the word be instant in season out of season reprove rebuke exhort with all longsuffering and doctrine
or the time will come when they will not endure sound doctrine but after their own lusts shall they heap to themselves teachers having itching ears and they shall turn away their ears from the truth and shall be turned unto fables
ut watch thou in all things endure afflictions do the work of an evangelist make full proof of thy ministry
or  am now ready to be offered and the time of my departure is at hand
 have fought a good fight  have finished my course  have kept the faith henceforth there is laid up for me a crown of righteousness which the ord the righteous judge shall give me at that day and not to me only but unto all them also that love his appearing
o thy diligence to come shortly unto me for emas hath forsaken me having loved this present world and is departed unto hessalonica rescens to alatia itus unto almatia
nly uke is with me
ake ark and bring him with thee for he is profitable to me for the ministry
nd ychicus have  sent to phesus
he cloke that  left at roas with arpus when thou comest bring with thee and the books but especially the parchments
lexander the coppersmith did me much evil the ord reward him according to his works of whom be thou ware also for he hath greatly withstood our words
t my first answer no man stood with me but all men forsook me  pray od that it may not be laid to their charge
otwithstanding the ord stood with me and strengthened me that by me the preaching might be fully known and that all the entiles might hear and  was delivered out of the mouth of the lion
nd the ord shall deliver me from every evil work and will preserve me unto his heavenly kingdom to whom be glory for ever and ever
men
alute risca and quila and the household of nesiphorus
rastus abode at orinth but rophimus have  left at iletum sick
o thy diligence to come before winter
ubulus greeteth thee and udens and inus and laudia and all the brethren
he ord esus hrist be with thy spirit
race be with you
men
he second epistle unto imotheus ordained the first bishop of the church of the phesians was written from ome when aul was brought before ero the second time
aul a servant of od and an apostle of esus hrist according to the faith of ods elect and the acknowledging of the truth which is after godliness in hope of eternal life which od that cannot lie promised before the world began but hath in due times manifested his word through preaching which is committed unto me according to the commandment of od our aviour to itus mine own son after the common faith race mercy and peace from od the ather and the ord esus hrist our aviour
or this cause left  thee in rete that thou shouldest set in order the things that are wanting and ordain elders in every city as  had appointed thee if any be blameless the husband of one wife having faithful children not accused of riot or unruly
or a bishop must be blameless as the steward of od not selfwilled not soon angry not given to wine no striker not given to filthy lucre but a lover of hospitality a lover of good men sober just holy temperate holding fast the faithful word as he hath been taught that he may be able by sound doctrine both to exhort and to convince the gainsayers
or there are many unruly and vain talkers and deceivers specially they of the circumcision whose mouths must be stopped who subvert whole houses teaching things which they ought not for filthy lucres sake
ne of themselves even a prophet of their own said he retians are alway liars evil beasts slow bellies
his witness is true
herefore rebuke them sharply that they may be sound in the faith not giving heed to ewish fables and commandments of men that turn from the truth
nto the pure all things are pure but unto them that are defiled and unbelieving is nothing pure but even their mind and conscience is defiled
hey profess that they know od but in works they deny him being abominable and disobedient and unto every good work reprobate
ut speak thou the things which become sound doctrine that the aged men be sober grave temperate sound in faith in charity in patience
he aged women likewise that they be in behaviour as becometh holiness not false accusers not given to much wine teachers of good things that they may teach the young women to be sober to love their husbands to love their children to be discreet chaste keepers at home good obedient to their own husbands that the word of od be not blasphemed
oung men likewise exhort to be sober minded
n all things shewing thyself a pattern of good works in doctrine shewing uncorruptness gravity sincerity sound speech that cannot be condemned that he that is of the contrary part may be ashamed having no evil thing to say of you
xhort servants to be obedient unto their own masters and to please them well in all things not answering again not purloining but shewing all good fidelity that they may adorn the doctrine of od our aviour in all things
or the grace of od that bringeth salvation hath appeared to all men teaching us that denying ungodliness and worldly lusts we should live soberly righteously and godly in this present world looking for that blessed hope and the glorious appearing of the great od and our aviour esus hrist who gave himself for us that he might redeem us from all iniquity and purify unto himself a peculiar people zealous of good works
hese things speak and exhort and rebuke with all authority
et no man despise thee
ut them in mind to be subject to principalities and powers to obey magistrates to be ready to every good work to speak evil of no man to be no brawlers but gentle shewing all meekness unto all men
or we ourselves also were sometimes foolish disobedient deceived serving divers lusts and pleasures living in malice and envy hateful and hating one another
ut after that the kindness and love of od our aviour toward man appeared not by works of righteousness which we have done but according to his mercy he saved us by the washing of regeneration and renewing of the oly host which he shed on us abundantly through esus hrist our aviour that being justified by his grace we should be made heirs according to the hope of eternal life
his is a faithful saying and these things  will that thou affirm constantly that they which have believed in od might be careful to maintain good works
hese things are good and profitable unto men
ut avoid foolish questions and genealogies and contentions and strivings about the law for they are unprofitable and vain
 man that is an heretick after the first and second admonition reject knowing that he that is such is subverted and sinneth being condemned of himself
hen  shall send rtemas unto thee or ychicus be diligent to come unto me to icopolis for  have determined there to winter
ring enas the lawyer and pollos on their journey diligently that nothing be wanting unto them
nd let ours also learn to maintain good works for necessary uses that they be not unfruitful
ll that are with me salute thee
reet them that love us in the faith
race be with you all
men
t was written to itus ordained the first bishop of the church of the retians from icopolis of acedonia
aul a prisoner of esus hrist and imothy our brother unto hilemon our dearly beloved and fellowlabourer and to our beloved pphia and rchippus our fellowsoldier and to the church in thy house grace to you and peace from od our ather and the ord esus hrist
 thank my od making mention of thee always in my prayers hearing of thy love and faith which thou hast toward the ord esus and toward all saints that the communication of thy faith may become effectual by the acknowledging of every good thing which is in you in hrist esus
or we have great joy and consolation in thy love because the bowels of the saints are refreshed by thee brother
herefore though  might be much bold in hrist to enjoin thee that which is convenient yet for loves sake  rather beseech thee being such an one as aul the aged and now also a prisoner of esus hrist
 beseech thee for my son nesimus whom  have begotten in my bonds which in time past was to thee unprofitable but now profitable to thee and to me whom  have sent again thou therefore receive him that is mine own bowels whom  would have retained with me that in thy stead he might have ministered unto me in the bonds of the gospel but without thy mind would  do nothing that thy benefit should not be as it were of necessity but willingly
or perhaps he therefore departed for a season that thou shouldest receive him for ever not now as a servant but above a servant a brother beloved specially to me but how much more unto thee both in the flesh and in the ord
f thou count me therefore a partner receive him as myself
f he hath wronged thee or oweth thee ought put that on mine account  aul have written it with mine own hand  will repay it albeit  do not say to thee how thou owest unto me even thine own self besides
ea brother let me have joy of thee in the ord refresh my bowels in the ord
aving confidence in thy obedience  wrote unto thee knowing that thou wilt also do more than  say
ut withal prepare me also a lodging for  trust that through your prayers  shall be given unto you
here salute thee paphras my fellowprisoner in hrist esus arcus ristarchus emas ucas my fellowlabourers
he grace of our ord esus hrist be with your spirit
men
ritten from ome to hilemon by nesimus a servant
od who at sundry times and in divers manners spake in time past unto the fathers by the prophets hath in these last days spoken unto us by his on whom he hath appointed heir of all things by whom also he made the worlds who being the brightness of his glory and the express image of his person and upholding all things by the word of his power when he had by himself purged our sins sat down on the right hand of the ajesty on high being made so much better than the angels as he hath by inheritance obtained a more excellent name than they
or unto which of the angels said he at any time hou art my on his day have  begotten thee
nd again  will be to him a ather nd he shall be to me a on
nd again when he bringeth in the firstbegotten into the world he saith nd let all the angels of od worship him
nd of the angels he saith ho maketh his angels spirits nd his ministers a flame of fire
ut unto the on he saith hy throne  od is for ever and ever  sceptre of righteousness is the sceptre of thy kingdom
hou hast loved righteousness and hated iniquity herefore od even thy od hath anointed theeith the oil of gladness above thy fellows
nd hou ord in the beginning hast laid the foundation of the earth nd the heavens are the works of thine hands hey shall perish but thou remainest nd they all shall wax old as doth a garment nd as a vesture shalt thou fold them up nd they shall be changed ut thou art the same nd thy years shall not fail
ut to which of the angels said he at any time it on my right hand ntil  make thine enemies thy footstool
