hosoever transgresseth and abideth not in the doctrine of hrist hath not od
e that abideth in the doctrine of hrist he hath both the ather and the on
f there come any unto you and bring not this doctrine receive him not into your house neither bid him od speed for he that biddeth him od speed is partaker of his evil deeds
aving many things to write unto you  would not write with paper and ink but  trust to come unto you and speak face to face that our joy may be full
he children of thy elect sister greet thee
men
he elder unto the wellbeloved aius whom  love in the truth
eloved  wish above all things that thou mayest prosper and be in health even as thy soul prospereth
or  rejoiced greatly when the brethren came and testified of the truth that is in thee even as thou walkest in the truth
 have no greater joy than to hear that my children walk in truth
eloved thou doest faithfully whatsoever thou doest to the brethren and to strangers which have borne witness of thy charity before the church whom if thou bring forward on their journey after a godly sort thou shalt do well because that for his names sake they went forth taking nothing of the entiles
e therefore ought to receive such that we might be fellowhelpers to the truth
 wrote unto the church but iotrephes who loveth to have the preeminence among them receiveth us not
herefore if  come  will remember his deeds which he doeth prating against us with malicious words and not content therewith neither doth he himself receive the brethren and forbiddeth them that would and casteth them out of the church
eloved follow not that which is evil but that which is good
e that doeth good is of od but he that doeth evil hath not seen od
emetrius hath good report of all men and of the truth itself yea and we also bear record and ye know that our record is true
 had many things to write but  will not with ink and pen write unto thee but  trust  shall shortly see thee and we shall speak face to face
eace be to thee
ur friends salute thee
reet the friends by name
ude the servant of esus hrist and brother of ames to them that are sanctified by od the ather and preserved in esus hrist and called mercy unto you and peace and love be multiplied
eloved when  gave all diligence to write unto you of the common salvation it was needful for me to write unto you and exhort you that ye should earnestly contend for the faith which was once delivered unto the saints
or there are certain men crept in unawares who were before of old ordained to this condemnation ungodly men turning the grace of our od into lasciviousness and denying the only ord od and our ord esus hrist
 will therefore put you in remembrance though ye once knew this how that the ord having saved the people out of the land of gypt afterward destroyed them that believed not
nd the angels which kept not their first estate but left their own habitation he hath reserved in everlasting chains under darkness unto the judgment of the great day
ven as odom and omorrha and the cities about them in like manner giving themselves over to fornication and going after strange flesh are set forth for an example suffering the vengeance of eternal fire
ikewise also these filthy dreamers defile the flesh despise dominion and speak evil of dignities
et ichael the archangel when contending with the devil he disputed about the body of oses durst not bring against him a railing accusation but said he ord rebuke thee
ut these speak evil of those things which they know not but what they know naturally as brute beasts in those things they corrupt themselves
oe unto them for they have gone in the way of ain and ran greedily after the error of alaam for reward and perished in the gainsaying of ore
hese are spots in your feasts of charity when they feast with you feeding themselves without fear clouds they are without water carried about of winds trees whose fruit withereth without fruit twice dead plucked up by the roots raging waves of the sea foaming out their own shame wandering stars to whom is reserved the blackness of darkness for ever
nd noch also the seventh from dam prophesied of these saying ehold the ord cometh with ten thousands of his saints to execute judgment upon all and to convince all that are ungodly among them of all their ungodly deeds which they have ungodly committed and of all their hard speeches which ungodly sinners have spoken against him
hese are murmurers complainers walking after their own lusts and their mouth speaketh great swelling words having mens persons in admiration because of advantage
ut beloved remember ye the words which were spoken before of the apostles of our ord esus hrist how that they told you there should be mockers in the last time who should walk after their own ungodly lusts
hese be they who separate themselves sensual having not the pirit
ut ye beloved building up yourselves on your most holy faith praying in the oly host keep yourselves in the love of od looking for the mercy of our ord esus hrist unto eternal life
nd of some have compassion making a difference and others save with fear pulling them out of the fire hating even the garment spotted by the flesh
ow unto him that is able to keep you from falling and to present you faultless before the presence of his glory with exceeding joy to the only wise od our aviour be glory and majesty dominion and power both now and ever
men
he evelation of esus hrist which od gave unto him to shew unto his servants things which must shortly come to pass and he sent and signified it by his angel unto his servant ohn who bare record of the word of od and of the testimony of esus hrist and of all things that he saw
lessed is he that readeth and they that hear the words of this prophecy and keep those things which are written therein for the time is at hand
ohn to the seven churches which are in sia race be unto you and peace from him which is and which was and which is to come and from the seven pirits which are before his throne and from esus hrist who is the faithful witness and the first begotten of the dead and the prince of the kings of the earth
nto him that loved us and washed us from our sins in his own blood and hath made us kings and priests unto od and his ather to him be glory and dominion for ever and ever
men
ehold he cometh with clouds and every eye shall see him and they also which pierced him and all kindreds of the earth shall wail because of him
ven so men
 am lpha and mega the beginning and the ending saith the ord which is and which was and which is to come the lmighty
 ohn who also am your brother and companion in tribulation and in the kingdom and patience of esus hrist was in the isle that is called atmos for the word of od and for the testimony of esus hrist
 was in the pirit on the ords day and heard behind me a great voice as of a trumpet saying  am lpha and mega the first and the last and hat thou seest write in a book and send it unto the seven churches which are in sia unto phesus and unto myrna and unto ergamos and unto hyatira and unto ardis and unto hiladelphia and unto aodicea
nd  turned to see the voice that spake with me
nd being turned  saw seven golden candlesticks and in the midst of the seven candlesticks one like unto the on of man clothed with a garment down to the foot and girt about the paps with a golden girdle
is head and his hairs were white like wool as white as snow and his eyes were as a flame of fire and his feet like unto fine brass as if they burned in a furnace and his voice as the sound of many waters
nd he had in his right hand seven stars and out of his mouth went a sharp twoedged sword and his countenance was as the sun shineth in his strength
nd when  saw him  fell at his feet as dead
nd he laid his right hand upon me saying unto me ear not  am the first and the last  am he that liveth and was dead and behold  am alive for evermore men and have the keys of hell and of death
rite the things which thou hast seen and the things which are and the things which shall be hereafter the mystery of the seven stars which thou sawest in my right hand and the seven golden candlesticks
he seven stars are the angels of the seven churches and the seven candlesticks which thou sawest are the seven churches
nto the angel of the church of phesus write hese things saith he that holdeth the seven stars in his right hand who walketh in the midst of the seven golden candlesticks  know thy works and thy labour and thy patience and how thou canst not bear them which are evil and thou hast tried them which say they are apostles and are not and hast found them liars and hast borne and hast patience and for my names sake hast laboured and hast not fainted
evertheless  have somewhat against thee because thou hast left thy first love
emember therefore from whence thou art fallen and repent and do the first works or else  will come unto thee quickly and will remove thy candlestick out of his place except thou repent
ut this thou hast that thou hatest the deeds of the icolaitanes which  also hate
e that hath an ear let him hear what the pirit saith unto the churches o him that overcometh will  give to eat of the tree of life which is in the midst of the paradise of od
nd unto the angel of the church in myrna write hese things saith the first and the last which was dead and is alive  know thy works and tribulation and poverty but thou art rich and  know the blasphemy of them which say they are ews and are not but are the synagogue of atan
ear none of those things which thou shalt suffer behold the devil shall cast some of you into prison that ye may be tried and ye shall have tribulation ten days be thou faithful unto death and  will give thee a crown of life
e that hath an ear let him hear what the pirit saith unto the churches e that overcometh shall not be hurt of the second death
nd to the angel of the church in ergamos write hese things saith he which hath the sharp sword with two edges  know thy works and where thou dwellest even where atans seat is and thou holdest fast my name and hast not denied my faith even in those days wherein ntipas was my faithful martyr who was slain among you where atan dwelleth
ut  have a few things against thee because thou hast there them that hold the doctrine of alaam who taught alac to cast a stumblingblock before the children of srael to eat things sacrificed unto idols and to commit fornication
o hast thou also them that hold the doctrine of the icolaitanes which thing  hate
epent or else  will come unto thee quickly and will fight against them with the sword of my mouth
e that hath an ear let him hear what the pirit saith unto the churches o him that overcometh will  give to eat of the hidden manna and will give him a white stone and in the stone a new name written which no man knoweth saving he that receiveth it
nd unto the angel of the church in hyatira write hese things saith the on of od who hath his eyes like unto a flame of fire and his feet are like fine brass  know thy works and charity and service and faith and thy patience and thy works and the last to be more than the first
otwithstanding  have a few things against thee because thou sufferest that woman ezebel which calleth herself a prophetess to teach and to seduce my servants to commit fornication and to eat things sacrificed unto idols
nd  gave her space to repent of her fornication and she repented not
ehold  will cast her into a bed and them that commit adultery with her into great tribulation except they repent of their deeds
nd  will kill her children with death and all the churches shall know that  am he which searcheth the reins and hearts and  will give unto every one of you according to your works
ut unto you  say and unto the rest in hyatira as many as have not this doctrine and which have not known the depths of atan as they speak  will put upon you none other burden
ut that which ye have already hold fast till  come
nd he that overcometh and keepeth my works unto the end to him will  give power over the nations and he shall rule them with a rod of iron as the vessels of a potter shall they be broken to shivers even as  received of my ather
nd  will give him the morning star
e that hath an ear let him hear what the pirit saith unto the churches
nd unto the angel of the church in ardis write hese things saith he that hath the seven pirits of od and the seven stars  know thy works that thou hast a name that thou livest and art dead
e watchful and strengthen the things which remain that are ready to die for  have not found thy works perfect before od
emember therefore how thou hast received and heard and hold fast and repent
f therefore thou shalt not watch  will come on thee as a thief and thou shalt not know what hour  will come upon thee
hou hast a few names even in ardis which have not defiled their garments and they shall walk with me in white for they are worthy
e that overcometh the same shall be clothed in white raiment and  will not blot out his name out of the book of life but  will confess his name before my ather and before his angels
e that hath an ear let him hear what the pirit saith unto the churches
nd to the angel of the church in hiladelphia write hese things saith he that is holy he that is true he that hath the key of avid he that openeth and no man shutteth and shutteth and no man openeth  know thy works behold  have set before thee an open door and no man can shut it for thou hast a little strength and hast kept my word and hast not denied my name
ehold  will make them of the synagogue of atan which say they are ews and are not but do lie behold  will make them to come and worship before thy feet and to know that  have loved thee
ecause thou hast kept the word of my patience  also will keep thee from the hour of temptation which shall come upon all the world to try them that dwell upon the earth
ehold  come quickly hold that fast which thou hast that no man take thy crown
im that overcometh will  make a pillar in the temple of my od and he shall go no more out and  will write upon him the name of my od and the name of the city of my od which is new erusalem which cometh down out of heaven from my od and  will write upon him my new name
e that hath an ear let him hear what the pirit saith unto the churches
nd unto the angel of the church of the aodiceans write hese things saith the men the faithful and true witness the beginning of the creation of od  know thy works that thou art neither cold nor hot  would thou wert cold or hot
o then because thou art lukewarm and neither cold nor hot  will spue thee out of my mouth
ecause thou sayest  am rich and increased with goods and have need of nothing and knowest not that thou art wretched and miserable and poor and blind and naked  counsel thee to buy of me gold tried in the fire that thou mayest be rich and white raiment that thou mayest be clothed and that the shame of thy nakedness do not appear and anoint thine eyes with eyesalve that thou mayest see
s many as  love  rebuke and chasten be zealous therefore and repent
ehold  stand at the door and knock if any man hear my voice and open the door  will come in to him and will sup with him and he with me
o him that overcometh will  grant to sit with me in my throne even as  also overcame and am set down with my ather in his throne
e that hath an ear let him hear what the pirit saith unto the churches
fter this  looked and behold a door was opened in heaven and the first voice which  heard was as it were of a trumpet talking with me which said ome up hither and  will shew thee things which must be hereafter
nd immediately  was in the spirit and behold a throne was set in heaven and one sat on the throne
nd he that sat was to look upon like a jasper and a sardine stone and there was a rainbow round about the throne in sight like unto an emerald
nd round about the throne were four and twenty seats and upon the seats  saw four and twenty elders sitting clothed in white raiment and they had on their heads crowns of gold
nd out of the throne proceeded lightnings and thunderings and voices and there were seven lamps of fire burning before the throne which are the seven pirits of od
nd before the throne there was a sea of glass like unto crystal and in the midst of the throne and round about the throne were four beasts full of eyes before and behind
nd the first beast was like a lion and the second beast like a calf and the third beast had a face as a man and the fourth beast was like a flying eagle
nd the four beasts had each of them six wings about him and they were full of eyes within and they rest not day and night saying oly holy holy ord od lmighty which was and is and is to come
nd when those beasts give glory and honour and thanks to him that sat on the throne who liveth for ever and ever the four and twenty elders fall down before him that sat on the throne and worship him that liveth for ever and ever and cast their crowns before the throne saying hou art worthy  ord to receive glory and honour and power for thou hast created all things and for thy pleasure they are and were created
nd  saw in the right hand of him that sat on the throne a book written within and on the backside sealed with seven seals
nd  saw a strong angel proclaiming with a loud voice ho is worthy to open the book and to loose the seals thereof
nd no man in heaven nor in earth neither under the earth was able to open the book neither to look thereon
nd  wept much because no man was found worthy to open and to read the book neither to look thereon
nd one of the elders saith unto me eep not behold the ion of the tribe of uda the oot of avid hath prevailed to open the book and to loose the seven seals thereof
nd  beheld and lo in the midst of the throne and of the four beasts and in the midst of the elders stood a amb as it had been slain having seven horns and seven eyes which are the seven pirits of od sent forth into all the earth
nd he came and took the book out of the right hand of him that sat upon the throne
nd when he had taken the book the four beasts and four and twenty elders fell down before the amb having every one of them harps and golden vials full of odours which are the prayers of saints
nd they sung a new song saying hou art worthy to take the book and to open the seals thereof for thou wast slain and hast redeemed us to od by thy blood out of every kindred and tongue and people and nation and hast made us unto our od kings and priests and we shall reign on the earth
nd  beheld and  heard the voice of many angels round about the throne and the beasts and the elders and the number of them was ten thousand times ten thousand and thousands of thousands saying with a loud voice orthy is the amb that was slain to receive power and riches and wisdom and strength and honour and glory and blessing
nd every creature which is in heaven and on the earth and under the earth and such as are in the sea and all that are in them heard  saying lessing and honour and glory and power be unto him that sitteth upon the throne and unto the amb for ever and ever
nd the four beasts said men
nd the four and twenty elders fell down and worshipped him that liveth for ever and ever
nd  saw when the amb opened one of the seals and  heard as it were the noise of thunder one of the four beasts saying ome and see
nd  saw and behold a white horse and he that sat on him had a bow and a crown was given unto him and he went forth conquering and to conquer
nd when he had opened the second seal  heard the second beast say ome and see
nd there went out another horse that was red and power was given to him that sat thereon to take peace from the earth and that they should kill one another and there was given unto him a great sword
nd when he had opened the third seal  heard the third beast say ome and see
nd  beheld and lo a black horse and he that sat on him had a pair of balances in his hand
nd  heard a voice in the midst of the four beasts say  measure of wheat for a penny and three measures of barley for a penny and see thou hurt not the oil and the wine
nd when he had opened the fourth seal  heard the voice of the fourth beast say ome and see
nd  looked and behold a pale horse and his name that sat on him was eath and ell followed with him
nd power was given unto them over the fourth part of the earth to kill with sword and with hunger and with death and with the beasts of the earth
nd when he had opened the fifth seal  saw under the altar the souls of them that were slain for the word of od and for the testimony which they held and they cried with a loud voice saying ow long  ord holy and true dost thou not judge and avenge our blood on them that dwell on the earth
nd white robes were given unto every one of them and it was said unto them that they should rest yet for a little season until their fellowservants also and their brethren that should be killed as they were should be fulfilled
nd  beheld when he had opened the sixth seal and lo there was a great earthquake and the sun became black as sackcloth of hair and the moon became as blood and the stars of heaven fell unto the earth even as a fig tree casteth her untimely figs when she is shaken of a mighty wind
nd the heaven departed as a scroll when it is rolled together and every mountain and island were moved out of their places
nd the kings of the earth and the great men and the rich men and the chief captains and the mighty men and every bondman and every free man hid themselves in the dens and in the rocks of the mountains and said to the mountains and rocks all on us and hide us from the face of him that sitteth on the throne and from the wrath of the amb or the great day of his wrath is come and who shall be able to stand
nd after these things  saw four angels standing on the four corners of the earth holding the four winds of the earth that the wind should not blow on the earth nor on the sea nor on any tree
nd  saw another angel ascending from the east having the seal of the living od and he cried with a loud voice to the four angels to whom it was given to hurt the earth and the sea saying urt not the earth neither the sea nor the trees till we have sealed the servants of our od in their foreheads
nd  heard the number of them which were sealed and there were sealed an hundred and forty and four thousand of all the tribes of the children of srael

nd all the angels stood round about the throne and about the elders and the four beasts and fell before the throne on their faces and worshipped od saying men lessing and glory and wisdom and thanksgiving and honour and power and might be unto our od for ever and ever

nd one of the elders answered saying unto me hat are these which are arrayed in white robes and whence came they
nd  said unto him ir thou knowest
nd he said to me hese are they which came out of great tribulation and have washed their robes and made them white in the blood of the amb

hey shall hunger no more neither thirst any more neither shall the sun light on them nor any heat
or the amb which is in the midst of the throne shall feed them and shall lead them unto living fountains of waters and od shall wipe away all tears from their eyes
nd when he had opened the seventh seal there was silence in heaven about the space of half an hour
nd  saw the seven angels which stood before od and to them were given seven trumpets
nd another angel came and stood at the altar having a golden censer and there was given unto him much incense that he should offer it with the prayers of all saints upon the golden altar which was before the throne
nd the smoke of the incense which came with the prayers of the saints ascended up before od out of the angels hand
nd the angel took the censer and filled it with fire of the altar and cast it into the earth and there were voices and thunderings and lightnings and an earthquake
nd the seven angels which had the seven trumpets prepared themselves to sound
he first angel sounded and there followed hail and fire mingled with blood and they were cast upon the earth and the third part of trees was burnt up and all green grass was burnt up
nd the second angel sounded and as it were a great mountain burning with fire was cast into the sea and the third part of the sea became blood and the third part of the creatures which were in the sea and had life died and the third part of the ships were destroyed
nd the third angel sounded and there fell a great star from heaven burning as it were a lamp and it fell upon the third part of the rivers and upon the fountains of waters and the name of the star is called ormwood and the third part of the waters became wormwood and many men died of the waters because they were made bitter
nd the fourth angel sounded and the third part of the sun was smitten and the third part of the moon and the third part of the stars so as the third part of them was darkened and the day shone not for a third part of it and the night likewise
nd  beheld and heard an angel flying through the midst of heaven saying with a loud voice oe woe woe to the inhabiters of the earth by reason of the other voices of the trumpet of the three angels which are yet to sound
nd the fifth angel sounded and  saw a star fall from heaven unto the earth and to him was given the key of the bottomless pit
nd he opened the bottomless pit and there arose a smoke out of the pit as the smoke of a great furnace and the sun and the air were darkened by reason of the smoke of the pit
nd there came out of the smoke locusts upon the earth and unto them was given power as the scorpions of the earth have power
nd it was commanded them that they should not hurt the grass of the earth neither any green thing neither any tree but only those men which have not the seal of od in their foreheads
nd to them it was given that they should not kill them but that they should be tormented five months and their torment was as the torment of a scorpion when he striketh a man
nd in those days shall men seek death and shall not find it and shall desire to die and death shall flee from them
nd the shapes of the locusts were like unto horses prepared unto battle and on their heads were as it were crowns like gold and their faces were as the faces of men
nd they had hair as the hair of women and their teeth were as the teeth of lions
nd they had breastplates as it were breastplates of iron and the sound of their wings was as the sound of chariots of many horses running to battle
nd they had tails like unto scorpions and there were stings in their tails and their power was to hurt men five months
nd they had a king over them which is the angel of the bottomless pit whose name in the ebrew tongue is baddon but in the reek tongue hath his name pollyon
ne woe is past and behold there come two woes more hereafter
nd the sixth angel sounded and  heard a voice from the four horns of the golden altar which is before od saying to the sixth angel which had the trumpet oose the four angels which are bound in the great river uphrates
nd the four angels were loosed which were prepared for an hour and a day and a month and a year for to slay the third part of men
nd the number of the army of the horsemen were two hundred thousand thousand and  heard the number of them
nd thus  saw the horses in the vision and them that sat on them having breastplates of fire and of jacinth and brimstone and the heads of the horses were as the heads of lions and out of their mouths issued fire and smoke and brimstone
y these three was the third part of men killed by the fire and by the smoke and by the brimstone which issued out of their mouths
or their power is in their mouth and in their tails for their tails were like unto serpents and had heads and with them they do hurt
nd the rest of the men which were not killed by these plagues yet repented not of the works of their hands that they should not worship devils and idols of gold and silver and brass and stone and of wood which neither can see nor hear nor walk neither repented they of their murders nor of their sorceries nor of their fornication nor of their thefts
nd  saw another mighty angel come down from heaven clothed with a cloud and a rainbow was upon his head and his face was as it were the sun and his feet as pillars of fire and he had in his hand a little book open and he set his right foot upon the sea and his left foot on the earth and cried with a loud voice as when a lion roareth and when he had cried seven thunders uttered their voices
nd when the seven thunders had uttered their voices  was about to write and  heard a voice from heaven saying unto me eal up those things which the seven thunders uttered and write them not
nd the angel which  saw stand upon the sea and upon the earth lifted up his hand to heaven and sware by him that liveth for ever and ever who created heaven and the things that therein are and the earth and the things that therein are and the sea and the things which are therein that there should be time no longer but in the days of the voice of the seventh angel when he shall begin to sound the mystery of od should be finished as he hath declared to his servants the prophets
nd the voice which  heard from heaven spake unto me again and said o and take the little book which is open in the hand of the angel which standeth upon the sea and upon the earth
nd  went unto the angel and said unto him ive me the little book

nd he said unto me hou must prophesy again before many peoples and nations and tongues and kings

ut the court which is without the temple leave out and measure it not for it is given unto the entiles and the holy city shall they tread under foot forty and two months

hese are the two olive trees and the two candlesticks standing before the od of the earth
nd if any man will hurt them fire proceedeth out of their mouth and devoureth their enemies and if any man will hurt them he must in this manner be killed
hese have power to shut heaven that it rain not in the days of their prophecy and have power over waters to turn them to blood and to smite the earth with all plagues as often as they will
nd when they shall have finished their testimony the beast that ascendeth out of the bottomless pit shall make war against them and shall overcome them and kill them
nd their dead bodies shall lie in the street of the great city which spiritually is called odom and gypt where also our ord was crucified
nd they of the people and kindreds and tongues and nations shall see their dead bodies three days and an half and shall not suffer their dead bodies to be put in graves
nd they that dwell upon the earth shall rejoice over them and make merry and shall send gifts one to another because these two prophets tormented them that dwelt on the earth
nd after three days and an half the spirit of life from od entered into them and they stood upon their feet and great fear fell upon them which saw them
nd they heard a great voice from heaven saying unto them ome up hither
nd they ascended up to heaven in a cloud and their enemies beheld them
nd the same hour was there a great earthquake and the tenth part of the city fell and in the earthquake were slain of men seven thousand and the remnant were affrighted and gave glory to the od of heaven

nd the temple of od was opened in heaven and there was seen in his temple the ark of his testament and there were lightnings and voices and thunderings and an earthquake and great hail
nd there appeared a great wonder in heaven a woman clothed with the sun and the moon under her feet and upon her head a crown of twelve stars and she being with child cried travailing in birth and pained to be delivered
nd there appeared another wonder in heaven and behold a great red dragon having seven heads and ten horns and seven crowns upon his heads
nd his tail drew the third part of the stars of heaven and did cast them to the earth and the dragon stood before the woman which was ready to be delivered for to devour her child as soon as it was born
nd she brought forth a man child who was to rule all nations with a rod of iron and her child was caught up unto od and to his throne
nd the woman fled into the wilderness where she hath a place prepared of od that they should feed her there a thousand two hundred and threescore days
nd there was war in heaven ichael and his angels fought against the dragon and the dragon fought and his angels and prevailed not neither was their place found any more in heaven
nd the great dragon was cast out that old serpent called the evil and atan which deceiveth the whole world he was cast out into the earth and his angels were cast out with him

nd they overcame him by the blood of the amb and by the word of their testimony and they loved not their lives unto the death
herefore rejoice ye heavens and ye that dwell in them

nd when the dragon saw that he was cast unto the earth he persecuted the woman which brought forth the man child
nd to the woman were given two wings of a great eagle that she might fly into the wilderness into her place where she is nourished for a time and times and half a time from the face of the serpent
nd the serpent cast out of his mouth water as a flood after the woman that he might cause her to be carried away of the flood
nd the earth helped the woman and the earth opened her mouth and swallowed up the flood which the dragon cast out of his mouth
nd the dragon was wroth with the woman and went to make war with the remnant of her seed which keep the commandments of od and have the testimony of esus hrist
nd  stood upon the sand of the sea and saw a beast rise up out of the sea having seven heads and ten horns and upon his horns ten crowns and upon his heads the name of blasphemy
nd the beast which  saw was like unto a leopard and his feet were as the feet of a bear and his mouth as the mouth of a lion and the dragon gave him his power and his seat and great authority
nd  saw one of his heads as it were wounded to death and his deadly wound was healed and all the world wondered after the beast
nd they worshipped the dragon which gave power unto the beast and they worshipped the beast saying ho is like unto the beast who is able to make war with him
nd there was given unto him a mouth speaking great things and blasphemies and power was given unto him to continue forty and two months
nd he opened his mouth in blasphemy against od to blaspheme his name and his tabernacle and them that dwell in heaven
nd it was given unto him to make war with the saints and to overcome them and power was given him over all kindreds and tongues and nations
nd all that dwell upon the earth shall worship him whose names are not written in the book of life of the amb slain from the foundation of the world
f any man have an ear let him hear
e that leadeth into captivity shall go into captivity he that killeth with the sword must be killed with the sword
ere is the patience and the faith of the saints
nd  beheld another beast coming up out of the earth and he had two horns like a lamb and he spake as a dragon
nd he exerciseth all the power of the first beast before him and causeth the earth and them which dwell therein to worship the first beast whose deadly wound was healed
nd he doeth great wonders so that he maketh fire come down from heaven on the earth in the sight of men and deceiveth them that dwell on the earth by the means of those miracles which he had power to do in the sight of the beast saying to them that dwell on the earth that they should make an image to the beast which had the wound by a sword and did live
nd he had power to give life unto the image of the beast that the image of the beast should both speak and cause that as many as would not worship the image of the beast should be killed
nd he causeth all both small and great rich and poor free and bond to receive a mark in their right hand or in their foreheads and that no man might buy or sell save he that had the mark or the name of the beast or the number of his name
ere is wisdom
et him that hath understanding count the number of the beast for it is the number of a man and his number is ix hundred threescore and six
nd  looked and lo a amb stood on the mount ion and with him an hundred forty and four thousand having his athers name written in their foreheads
nd  heard a voice from heaven as the voice of many waters and as the voice of a great thunder and  heard the voice of harpers harping with their harps and they sung as it were a new song before the throne and before the four beasts and the elders and no man could learn that song but the hundred and forty and four thousand which were redeemed from the earth
hese are they which were not defiled with women for they are virgins
hese are they which follow the amb whithersoever he goeth
hese were redeemed from among men being the firstfruits unto od and to the amb
nd in their mouth was found no guile for they are without fault before the throne of od
nd  saw another angel fly in the midst of heaven having the everlasting gospel to preach unto them that dwell on the earth and to every nation and kindred and tongue and people saying with a loud voice ear od and give glory to him for the hour of his judgment is come and worship him that made heaven and earth and the sea and the fountains of waters
nd there followed another angel saying abylon is fallen is fallen that great city because she made all nations drink of the wine of the wrath of her fornication
nd the third angel followed them saying with a loud voice f any man worship the beast and his image and receive his mark in his forehead or in his hand the same shall drink of the wine of the wrath of od which is poured out without mixture into the cup of his indignation and he shall be tormented with fire and brimstone in the presence of the holy angels and in the presence of the amb and the smoke of their torment ascendeth up for ever and ever and they have no rest day nor night who worship the beast and his image and whosoever receiveth the mark of his name
ere is the patience of the saints here are they that keep the commandments of od and the faith of esus

nd he that sat on the cloud thrust in his sickle on the earth and the earth was reaped
nd another angel came out of the temple which is in heaven he also having a sharp sickle

nd the angel thrust in his sickle into the earth and gathered the vine of the earth and cast it into the great winepress of the wrath of od
nd the winepress was trodden without the city and blood came out of the winepress even unto the horse bridles by the space of a thousand and six hundred furlongs
nd  saw another sign in heaven great and marvellous seven angels having the seven last plagues for in them is filled up the wrath of od

nd they sing the song of oses the servant of od and the song of the amb saying reat and marvellous are thy works ord od lmighty just and true are thy ways thou ing of saints
ho shall not fear thee  ord and glorify thy name for thou only art holy for all nations shall come and worship before thee for thy judgments are made manifest
nd after that  looked and behold the temple of the tabernacle of the testimony in heaven was opened and the seven angels came out of the temple having the seven plagues clothed in pure and white linen and having their breasts girded with golden girdles
nd one of the four beasts gave unto the seven angels seven golden vials full of the wrath of od who liveth for ever and ever
nd the temple was filled with smoke from the glory of od and from his power and no man was able to enter into the temple till the seven plagues of the seven angels were fulfilled
nd  heard a great voice out of the temple saying to the seven angels o your ways and pour out the vials of the wrath of od upon the earth
nd the first went and poured out his vial upon the earth and there fell a noisome and grievous sore upon the men which had the mark of the beast and upon them which worshipped his image
nd the second angel poured out his vial upon the sea and it became as the blood of a dead man and every living soul died in the sea
nd the third angel poured out his vial upon the rivers and fountains of waters and they became blood
nd  heard the angel of the waters say hou art righteous  ord which art and wast and shalt be because thou hast judged thus
or they have shed the blood of saints and prophets and thou hast given them blood to drink for they are worthy
nd  heard another out of the altar say ven so ord od lmighty true and righteous are thy judgments
nd the fourth angel poured out his vial upon the sun and power was given unto him to scorch men with fire
nd men were scorched with great heat and blasphemed the name of od which hath power over these plagues and they repented not to give him glory
nd the fifth angel poured out his vial upon the seat of the beast and his kingdom was full of darkness and they gnawed their tongues for pain and blasphemed the od of heaven because of their pains and their sores and repented not of their deeds
nd the sixth angel poured out his vial upon the great river uphrates and the water thereof was dried up that the way of the kings of the east might be prepared
nd  saw three unclean spirits like frogs come out of the mouth of the dragon and out of the mouth of the beast and out of the mouth of the false prophet
or they are the spirits of devils working miracles which go forth unto the kings of the earth and of the whole world to gather them to the battle of that great day of od lmighty
ehold  come as a thief
lessed is he that watcheth and keepeth his garments lest he walk naked and they see his shame
nd he gathered them together into a place called in the ebrew tongue rmageddon
nd the seventh angel poured out his vial into the air and there came a great voice out of the temple of heaven from the throne saying t is done
nd there were voices and thunders and lightnings and there was a great earthquake such as was not since men were upon the earth so mighty an earthquake and so great
nd the great city was divided into three parts and the cities of the nations fell and great abylon came in remembrance before od to give unto her the cup of the wine of the fierceness of his wrath
nd every island fled away and the mountains were not found
nd there fell upon men a great hail out of heaven every stone about the weight of a talent and men blasphemed od because of the plague of the hail for the plague thereof was exceeding great
nd there came one of the seven angels which had the seven vials and talked with me saying unto me ome hither  will shew unto thee the judgment of the great whore that sitteth upon many waters with whom the kings of the earth have committed fornication and the inhabitants of the earth have been made drunk with the wine of her fornication
o he carried me away in the spirit into the wilderness and  saw a woman sit upon a scarlet coloured beast full of names of blasphemy having seven heads and ten horns
nd the woman was arrayed in purple and scarlet colour and decked with gold and precious stones and pearls having a golden cup in her hand full of abominations and filthiness of her fornication and upon her forehead was a name written             
nd  saw the woman drunken with the blood of the saints and with the blood of the martyrs of esus and when  saw her  wondered with great admiration
nd the angel said unto me herefore didst thou marvel
 will tell thee the mystery of the woman and of the beast that carrieth her which hath the seven heads and ten horns
he beast that thou sawest was and is not and shall ascend out of the bottomless pit and go into perdition and they that dwell on the earth shall wonder whose names were not written in the book of life from the foundation of the world when they behold the beast that was and is not and yet is
nd here is the mind which hath wisdom
he seven heads are seven mountains on which the woman sitteth
nd there are seven kings five are fallen and one is and the other is not yet come and when he cometh he must continue a short space
nd the beast that was and is not even he is the eighth and is of the seven and goeth into perdition
nd the ten horns which thou sawest are ten kings which have received no kingdom as yet but receive power as kings one hour with the beast
hese have one mind and shall give their power and strength unto the beast
hese shall make war with the amb and the amb shall overcome them for he is ord of lords and ing of kings and they that are with him are called and chosen and faithful
nd he saith unto me he waters which thou sawest where the whore sitteth are peoples and multitudes and nations and tongues
nd the ten horns which thou sawest upon the beast these shall hate the whore and shall make her desolate and naked and shall eat her flesh and burn her with fire
or od hath put in their hearts to fulfil his will and to agree and give their kingdom unto the beast until the words of od shall be fulfilled
nd the woman which thou sawest is that great city which reigneth over the kings of the earth
nd after these things  saw another angel come down from heaven having great power and the earth was lightened with his glory
nd he cried mightily with a strong voice saying abylon the great is fallen is fallen and is become the habitation of devils and the hold of every foul spirit and a cage of every unclean and hateful bird
or all nations have drunk of the wine of the wrath of her fornication and the kings of the earth have committed fornication with her and the merchants of the earth are waxed rich through the abundance of her delicacies
nd  heard another voice from heaven saying ome out of her my people that ye be not partakers of her sins and that ye receive not of her plagues
or her sins have reached unto heaven and od hath remembered her iniquities
eward her even as she rewarded you and double unto her double according to her works in the cup which she hath filled fill to her double
ow much she hath glorified herself and lived deliciously so much torment and sorrow give her for she saith in her heart  sit a queen and am no widow and shall see no sorrow
herefore shall her plagues come in one day death and mourning and famine and she shall be utterly burned with fire for strong is the ord od who judgeth her
nd the kings of the earth who have committed fornication and lived deliciously with her shall bewail her and lament for her when they shall see the smoke of her burning standing afar off for the fear of her torment saying las alas that great city abylon that mighty city for in one hour is thy judgment come
nd the merchants of the earth shall weep and mourn over her for no man buyeth their merchandise any more the merchandise of gold and silver and precious stones and of pearls and fine linen and purple and silk and scarlet and all thyine wood and all manner vessels of ivory and all manner vessels of most precious wood and of brass and iron and marble and cinnamon and odours and ointments and frankincense and wine and oil and fine flour and wheat and beasts and sheep and horses and chariots and slaves and souls of men
nd the fruits that thy soul lusted after are departed from thee and all things which were dainty and goodly are departed from thee and thou shalt find them no more at all
he merchants of these things which were made rich by her shall stand afar off for the fear of her torment weeping and wailing and saying las alas that great city that was clothed in fine linen and purple and scarlet and decked with gold and precious stones and pearls
or in one hour so great riches is come to nought
nd every shipmaster and all the company in ships and sailors and as many as trade by sea stood afar off and cried when they saw the smoke of her burning saying hat city is like unto this great city
nd they cast dust on their heads and cried weeping and wailing saying las alas that great city wherein were made rich all that had ships in the sea by reason of her costliness for in one hour is she made desolate
ejoice over her thou heaven and ye holy apostles and prophets for od hath avenged you on her
nd a mighty angel took up a stone like a great millstone and cast it into the sea saying hus with violence shall that great city abylon be thrown down and shall be found no more at all
nd the voice of harpers and musicians and of pipers and trumpeters shall be heard no more at all in thee and no craftsman of whatsoever craft he be shall be found any more in thee and the sound of a millstone shall be heard no more at all in thee and the light of a candle shall shine no more at all in thee and the voice of the bridegroom and of the bride shall be heard no more at all in thee for thy merchants were the great men of the earth for by thy sorceries were all nations deceived
nd in her was found the blood of prophets and of saints and of all that were slain upon the earth
nd after these things  heard a great voice of much people in heaven saying lleluia alvation and glory and honour and power unto the ord our od for true and righteous are his judgments for he hath judged the great whore which did corrupt the earth with her fornication and hath avenged the blood of his servants at her hand
nd again they said lleluia
nd her smoke rose up for ever and ever
nd the four and twenty elders and the four beasts fell down and worshipped od that sat on the throne saying men lleluia
nd a voice came out of the throne saying raise our od all ye his servants and ye that fear him both small and great
nd  heard as it were the voice of a great multitude and as the voice of many waters and as the voice of mighty thunderings saying lleluia for the ord od omnipotent reigneth
et us be glad and rejoice and give honour to him for the marriage of the amb is come and his wife hath made herself ready
nd to her was granted that she should be arrayed in fine linen clean and white for the fine linen is the righteousness of saints
nd he saith unto me rite lessed are they which are called unto the marriage supper of the amb
nd he saith unto me hese are the true sayings of od
nd  fell at his feet to worship him
nd he said unto me ee thou do it not  am thy fellowservant and of thy brethren that have the testimony of esus worship od for the testimony of esus is the spirit of prophecy
nd  saw heaven opened and behold a white horse and he that sat upon him was called aithful and rue and in righteousness he doth judge and make war
is eyes were as a flame of fire and on his head were many crowns and he had a name written that no man knew but he himself
nd he was clothed with a vesture dipped in blood and his name is called he ord of od
nd the armies which were in heaven followed him upon white horses clothed in fine linen white and clean
nd out of his mouth goeth a sharp sword that with it he should smite the nations and he shall rule them with a rod of iron and he treadeth the winepress of the fierceness and wrath of lmighty od
nd he hath on his vesture and on his thigh a name written     ord  
nd  saw an angel standing in the sun and he cried with a loud voice saying to all the fowls that fly in the midst of heaven ome and gather yourselves together unto the supper of the great od that ye may eat the flesh of kings and the flesh of captains and the flesh of mighty men and the flesh of horses and of them that sit on them and the flesh of all men both free and bond both small and great
nd  saw the beast and the kings of the earth and their armies gathered together to make war against him that sat on the horse and against his army
nd the beast was taken and with him the false prophet that wrought miracles before him with which he deceived them that had received the mark of the beast and them that worshipped his image
hese both were cast alive into a lake of fire burning with brimstone
nd the remnant were slain with the sword of him that sat upon the horse which sword proceeded out of his mouth and all the fowls were filled with their flesh
nd  saw an angel come down from heaven having the key of the bottomless pit and a great chain in his hand
nd he laid hold on the dragon that old serpent which is the evil and atan and bound him a thousand years and cast him into the bottomless pit and shut him up and set a seal upon him that he should deceive the nations no more till the thousand years should be fulfilled and after that he must be loosed a little season
nd  saw thrones and they sat upon them and judgment was given unto them and  saw the souls of them that were beheaded for the witness of esus and for the word of od and which had not worshipped the beast neither his image neither had received his mark upon their foreheads or in their hands and they lived and reigned with hrist a thousand years
ut the rest of the dead lived not again until the thousand years were finished
his is the first resurrection
lessed and holy is he that hath part in the first resurrection on such the second death hath no power but they shall be priests of od and of hrist and shall reign with him a thousand years
nd when the thousand years are expired atan shall be loosed out of his prison and shall go out to deceive the nations which are in the four quarters of the earth og and agog to gather them together to battle the number of whom is as the sand of the sea
nd they went up on the breadth of the earth and compassed the camp of the saints about and the beloved city and fire came down from od out of heaven and devoured them
nd the devil that deceived them was cast into the lake of fire and brimstone where the beast and the false prophet are and shall be tormented day and night for ever and ever
nd  saw a great white throne and him that sat on it from whose face the earth and the heaven fled away and there was found no place for them
nd  saw the dead small and great stand before od and the books were opened and another book was opened which is the book of life and the dead were judged out of those things which were written in the books according to their works
nd the sea gave up the dead which were in it and death and hell delivered up the dead which were in them and they were judged every man according to their works
nd death and hell were cast into the lake of fire
his is the second death
nd whosoever was not found written in the book of life was cast into the lake of fire
nd  saw a new heaven and a new earth for the first heaven and the first earth were passed away and there was no more sea
nd  ohn saw the holy city new erusalem coming down from od out of heaven prepared as a bride adorned for her husband
nd  heard a great voice out of heaven saying ehold the tabernacle of od is with men and he will dwell with them and they shall be his people and od himself shall be with them and be their od
nd od shall wipe away all tears from their eyes and there shall be no more death neither sorrow nor crying neither shall there be any more pain for the former things are passed away
nd he that sat upon the throne said ehold  make all things new
nd he said unto me rite for these words are true and faithful
nd he said unto me t is done
 am lpha and mega the beginning and the end
 will give unto him that is athirst of the fountain of the water of life freely
e that overcometh shall inherit all things and  will be his od and he shall be my son
ut the fearful and unbelieving and the abominable and murderers and whoremongers and sorcerers and idolaters and all liars shall have their part in the lake which burneth with fire and brimstone which is the second death
nd there came unto me one of the seven angels which had the seven vials full of the seven last plagues and talked with me saying ome hither  will shew thee the bride the ambs wife
nd he carried me away in the spirit to a great and high mountain and shewed me that great city the holy erusalem descending out of heaven from od having the glory of od and her light was like unto a stone most precious even like a jasper stone clear as crystal and had a wall great and high and had twelve gates and at the gates twelve angels and names written thereon which are the names of the twelve tribes of the children of srael on the east three gates on the north three gates on the south three gates and on the west three gates
nd the wall of the city had twelve foundations and in them the names of the twelve apostles of the amb
nd he that talked with me had a golden reed to measure the city and the gates thereof and the wall thereof
nd the city lieth foursquare and the length is as large as the breadth and he measured the city with the reed twelve thousand furlongs
he length and the breadth and the height of it are equal
nd he measured the wall thereof an hundred and forty and four cubits according to the measure of a man that is of the angel
nd the building of the wall of it was of jasper and the city was pure gold like unto clear glass
nd the foundations of the wall of the city were garnished with all manner of precious stones
he first foundation was jasper the second sapphire the third a chalcedony the fourth an emerald the fifth sardonyx the sixth sardius the seventh chrysolyte the eighth beryl the ninth a topaz the tenth a chrysoprasus the eleventh a jacinth the twelfth an amethyst
nd the twelve gates were twelve pearls every several gate was of one pearl and the street of the city was pure gold as it were transparent glass
nd  saw no temple therein for the ord od lmighty and the amb are the temple of it
nd the city had no need of the sun neither of the moon to shine in it for the glory of od did lighten it and the amb is the light thereof
nd the nations of them which are saved shall walk in the light of it and the kings of the earth do bring their glory and honour into it
nd the gates of it shall not be shut at all by day for there shall be no night there
nd they shall bring the glory and honour of the nations into it
nd there shall in no wise enter into it any thing that defileth neither whatsoever worketh abomination or maketh a lie but they which are written in the ambs book of life
nd he shewed me a pure river of water of life clear as crystal proceeding out of the throne of od and of the amb
n the midst of the street of it and on either side of the river was there the tree of life which bare twelve manner of fruits and yielded her fruit every month and the leaves of the tree were for the healing of the nations
nd there shall be no more curse but the throne of od and of the amb shall be in it and his servants shall serve him and they shall see his face and his name shall be in their foreheads
nd there shall be no night there and they need no candle neither light of the sun for the ord od giveth them light and they shall reign for ever and ever
nd he said unto me hese sayings are faithful and true and the ord od of the holy prophets sent his angel to shew unto his servants the things which must shortly be done
ehold  come quickly blessed is he that keepeth the sayings of the prophecy of this book
nd  ohn saw these things and heard them
nd when  had heard and seen  fell down to worship before the feet of the angel which shewed me these things
hen saith he unto me ee thou do it not for  am thy fellowservant and of thy brethren the prophets and of them which keep the sayings of this book worship od
nd he saith unto me eal not the sayings of the prophecy of this book for the time is at hand
e that is unjust let him be unjust still and he which is filthy let him be filthy still and he that is righteous let him be righteous still and he that is holy let him be holy still
nd behold  come quickly and my reward is with me to give every man according as his work shall be
 am lpha and mega the beginning and the end the first and the last
lessed are they that do his commandments that they may have right to the tree of life and may enter in through the gates into the city
or without are dogs and sorcerers and whoremongers and murderers and idolaters and whosoever loveth and maketh a lie
 esus have sent mine angel to testify unto you these things in the churches
 am the root and the offspring of avid and the bright and morning star
nd the pirit and the bride say ome
nd let him that heareth say ome
nd let him that is athirst come
nd whosoever will let him take the water of life freely
or  testify unto every man that heareth the words of the prophecy of this book f any man shall add unto these things od shall add unto him the plagues that are written in this book and if any man shall take away from the words of the book of this prophecy od shall take away his part out of the book of life and out of the holy city and from the things which are written in this book
e which testifieth these things saith urely  come quickly
men
ven so come ord esus
he grace of our ord esus hrist be with you all
